--- CHANGELOG.md (Markdown, 103 lines, ~1,530 tokens) ---
   1 | # Changelog
   2 | 
   3 | All notable changes to Pathstone are documented in this file.
   4 | 
   5 | The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.1.0/),
   6 | and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).
   7 | 
   8 | ## [Unreleased]
   9 | 
  10 | ### Added
  11 | - WorldDevKit epic (#90) — standalone world editor application (14 sub-issues tracked)
  12 | - Prompt structure optimization for provider-side caching (#89)
  13 | 
  14 | ## [0.3.0] — 2025-01-31
  15 | 
  16 | ### Added
  17 | - Full combat state machine: initiative, rounds, death saves (#88, #63)
  18 | - Tool suite expanded to 33 tools: move_player, give_item, spawn_item_on_ground,
  19 |   equip_player, update_quest, set_flag, get_world_state, get_npc_state, award_xp,
  20 |   roll_attack, deal_damage, apply_status, create_item_template, create_npc_template,
  21 |   create_building_template, start_combat, end_combat, next_turn, death_save,
  22 |   set_weather, get_weather, adjust_reputation, get_factions, get_lore,
  23 |   craft_item, search_location, set_market_price, get_market_price (#8, #88)
  24 | - Travel UI with clickable exits + random encounters (#1, #14)
  25 | - Character creation flow with race/class/background selection (#2)
  26 | - GM streaming narration via SSE (#5)
  27 | - Token billing UI: per-turn + per-session usage widget (#6)
  28 | - Crash logs + global exception handler (#16)
  29 | - Anti-loop detection in GM tool calls (#24)
  30 | - Unit tests: 269+ tests across 8 test files (#15)
  31 | - Tooltips on all interactive controls (#50)
  32 | - Save migration: v1→v2 backfills Item.Weight from template (#17)
  33 | - Default world expanded: 6→14 locations, 2 new quests (#42)
  34 | - Client reconnect on disconnect with 3-attempt backoff (#7)
  35 | - WorldBuilder pause/resume with state persistence (#19)
  36 | - Local model support: Ollama/llama.cpp presets + documentation (#27)
  37 | - Quest rewards claiming UI (#70)
  38 | - Onboarding / first-run tutorial (#73)
  39 | - Save browser improvements: sort, search, multi-select delete, metadata (#74)
  40 | - Multiplayer lobby polish: ready toggles, start game, share address (#77)
  41 | - Chunked world generation: regions generated on demand via travel (#20)
  42 | - Custom ruleset per world: AI designs attribute names for non-standard themes (#21)
  43 | - Pet-agent custom task UI: editable delegation list (#22)
  44 | - Re-build existing world: partial/full rebuild from save screen (#23)
  45 | - Weather system: set_weather/get_weather tools, GM context, UI display (#34)
  46 | - Day/night cycle: time-of-day effects on encounters + perception (#35)
  47 | - Faction system: reputation, alignment, GM tools, UI badges (#36)
  48 | - Lore database: get_lore tool for canonical world lore (#43)
  49 | - Integration tests for multiplayer: 6 loopback tests with StubAiClient (#57)
  50 | - CI/CD pipeline: build+test on 3 platforms, release on tag (#58)
  51 | - Content expansion: 116 items, 47 NPCs, 33 buildings (#39, #40, #41)
  52 | - Themes: light/dark toggle + 6 accent colors (#47)
  53 | - Settings dialog: 4-tab layout (AI/Display/Multiplayer/Advanced) (#78)
  54 | - Stack management: split/merge stackable items (#64)
  55 | - Animations: screen transitions, panel fades, button hover (#46)
  56 | - Save compression: gzip world.json/log.json/state.json (#80)
  57 | - Backup saves: auto-rotate, max 5, 30-day retention (#81)
  58 | - Save sharing: export/import .pathstone-world files (#33)
  59 | - Economy simulation: market price modifiers per location (#37)
  60 | - Crafting system: CraftingRecipe + craft_item tool (#65)
  61 | - Container/looting: search_location tool (#67)
  62 | - AI worlds created from scratch: no standard template references
  63 | - Player kick/ban UI for host (#30)
  64 | - Spectator mode: spectators can't submit actions (#31)
  65 | - Late joiner sync: host sends last 20 log entries (#32)
  66 | - Save schema migration tests (#72)
  67 | - Achievement system: 8 milestones with toast notifications (#86)
  68 | - Keyboard shortcuts: Enter/Esc/Ctrl+S/Ctrl+L/1-4/F1 (#51)
  69 | - Item rarity colors in inventory UI (#66)
  70 | - Windows installer: NSIS, self-contained single-file (#4)
  71 | - Context window management: history summarization (#25)
  72 | - Multi-model support: per-role model overrides (#26)
  73 | - Hot-reload prompts for development (#82)
  74 | 
  75 | ### Changed
  76 | - Profile/Settings split: nickname edited inline on main menu, settings screen
  77 |   is AI-only (#78 update)
  78 | - GM system prompt restructured for provider-side prompt caching: static prefix
  79 |   (system + narrator + tools-guide + world lore) + dynamic tail (world state)
  80 | - ContentRegistry expanded from 26→116 items, 12→47 NPCs, 8→33 buildings
  81 | - DefaultWorld expanded from 6→14 locations
  82 | 
  83 | ### Removed
  84 | - Voice chat (#84) — not applicable to a text RPG
  85 | - Sounds (#44) and music (#45) — silence is the intended audio design
  86 | - Demo mode (#61) — scripted GM can't convey the AI-driven experience
  87 | - Daily challenge (#87) — requires central server, contradicts P2P architecture
  88 | - Code signing (#56) — certificates cost money, project is free/non-commercial
  89 | - AI response caching (#83) — obsolete; modern providers cache automatically
  90 | 
  91 | ## [0.2.0] — 2025-01-15
  92 | 
  93 | ### Added
  94 | - Desktop rewrite: C# / .NET 8 / Avalonia UI
  95 | - Core layers: Common, Rules, World, AI, Saves, Profile, Multiplayer
  96 | - 5 GM tools: roll_dice, get_player_state, get_location, spawn_npc, advance_time
  97 | - WorldBuilder: 3-stage pipeline (planner → committer → narrator)
  98 | - Multiplayer: in-process WebSocket host + ClientWebSocket client
  99 | - Save system: file-based with atomic writes
 100 | - Profile system: single local profile, no auth
 101 | - 4 side panels: Character, Inventory, Quests, World
 102 | - Inventory actions: use/equip/unequip/drop

--- README.md (Markdown, 827 lines, ~10,015 tokens) ---
   1 | # Pathstone
   2 | 
   3 | ![CI](https://github.com/GardenXsa/Pathstone/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)
   4 | 
   5 | **A desktop multiplayer narrative role-playing game with an AI Game Master.**
   6 | 
   7 | Pathstone is a single-binary desktop application (Windows / macOS / Linux) that
   8 | runs a procedural narrative TTRPG session. One player hosts the world in-process;
   9 | others connect over a direct WebSocket. An OpenAI-compatible LLM acts as the Game
  10 | Master: it resolves player actions, narrates outcomes, and mutates the world state
  11 | through a tool-call loop. The world is generated from a free-form brief via a
  12 | three-stage AI pipeline (planner, deterministic committer, narrator), with
  13 | optional pet-agent delegations for richer detail.
  14 | 
  15 | The project is a complete rewrite of an earlier Next.js / TypeScript prototype.
  16 | The web stack (HTTP server, JWT auth, socket.io, browser client) has been
  17 | replaced with a native desktop architecture: C# / .NET 8 / Avalonia UI, raw
  18 | `System.Net.WebSockets`, file-based profile identity, and an in-process host.
  19 | 
  20 | ---
  21 | 
  22 | ## Table of contents
  23 | 
  24 | 1. [Status](#status)
  25 | 2. [Technology stack](#technology-stack)

  44 | 14. [Roadmap](#roadmap)
  45 | 15. [Contributing](#contributing)
  46 | 16. [License](#license)
  47 | 
  48 | ---
  49 | 
  50 | ## Status
  51 | 
  52 | Alpha. The foundation is in place and the application boots, loads a world,
  53 | runs a Game Master turn, and persists state. The feature surface is incomplete;
  54 | see the [Roadmap](#roadmap) and the open issues tracker for what remains.
  55 | 
  56 | What works today:
  57 | 
  58 | - Local profile (single identity per install, no auth).
  59 | - Main menu with new-game, load, host, join, profile screens.
  60 | - AI world-build pipeline: planner produces a `WorldPlan`, deterministic
  61 |   committer materializes it into a live `World`, narrator writes the opening
  62 |   narration. Optional pet-agent delegations enrich the world.
  63 | - Single-player game loop: submit action, Game Master resolves via tool calls,
  64 |   narrative appended to log, world state updated, save persisted.
  65 | - Multiplayer: host runs an in-process WebSocket server; clients connect by
  66 |   IP:port. Action queue, chat, narrative streaming, state synchronization.
  67 | - Four tabbed side panels: Character, Inventory, Quests, World. Inventory
  68 |   actions (use / equip / unequip / drop) mutate the world and persist.
  69 | 
  70 | What is not yet implemented: travel UI, character creation flow, combat
  71 | system, sounds, packaging, localization, and a long tail of polish items.
  72 | 
  73 | ---
  74 | 
  75 | ## Technology stack
  76 | 
  77 | | Concern              | Choice                                            |
  78 | |----------------------|---------------------------------------------------|
  79 | | Framework            | .NET 8 (LTS)                                      |
  80 | | Language             | C# 12                                             |
  81 | | UI toolkit           | Avalonia UI 12.0.5 (XAML, MVVM, cross-platform)   |
  82 | | MVVM                 | CommunityToolkit.Mvvm 8.3 (source generators)    |
  83 | | DI                   | Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection 8.0      |
  84 | | Persistence          | System.Text.Json + Microsoft.Data.Sqlite 8.0.10   |
  85 | | Markdown             | Markdig 0.37 (narrative rendering)                |
  86 | | Real-time transport  | `System.Net.WebSockets` (HttpListener + ClientWebSocket) |
  87 | | HTTP client          | `HttpClient` (static shared instance)             |
  88 | | AI provider          | OpenAI-compatible HTTP `/v1/chat/completions`     |
  89 | 
  90 | No web server. No browser. No JWT, cookies, or socket.io. No external WebSocket
  91 | libraries. The host is a background task inside the desktop process.
  92 | 
  93 | ---
  94 | 
  95 | ## Solution layout
  96 | 
  97 | ```
  98 | Pathstone/
  99 | ├── MyGame.sln
 100 | ├── .gitignore
 101 | ├── README.md
 102 | └── src/
 103 |     ├── MyGame.Core/                  # Engine, AI, persistence, multiplayer (no UI)
 104 |     │   ├── Common/                   # EntityId, Rng, EventBus, Result, Version
 105 |     │   ├── Rules/                    # D20, Check
 106 |     │   ├── World/                    # World, Calendar, Ruleset, entities, content
 107 |     │   │   ├── Entities/             # Player, Npc, Location, Building, Item, Quest
 108 |     │   │   └── Content/              # ContentRegistry + embedded data.json
 109 |     │   ├── AI/                       # AiClient, Messages, PromptLoader, Tools
 110 |     │   │   ├── Agents/               # GameMaster, StartSceneAgent, PetAgent, WorldBuilderOrchestrator
 111 |     │   │   ├── Prompts/              # 12 embedded .md prompt templates
 112 |     │   │   └── Tools/                # ToolRegistry + 5 built-in tools
 113 |     │   ├── Saves/                    # SaveManager, SaveMeta, LogEntry, CharacterSheet
 114 |     │   ├── Profile/                  # Profile, ProfileStore, Settings, SettingsStore
 115 |     │   └── Multiplayer/              # HostServer, GameClient, HostSession, ClientSession
 116 |     │       └── Protocol/             # NetMessage + 20 message records
 117 |     └── MyGame.Desktop/               # Avalonia UI
 118 |         ├── Services/                 # ServiceHost (DI)
 119 |         ├── ViewModels/               # Main, MainMenu, Profile, HostGame, JoinGame, Game,
 120 |         │   ├── Panels/               #   CharacterPanel, InventoryPanel, QuestPanel, WorldPanel
 121 |         │   └── ...
 122 |         └── Views/                    # XAML + code-behind for each ViewModel
 123 |             └── Panels/               #   4 panel views
 124 | ```
 125 | 
 126 | The Core project has zero UI dependencies and is unit-testable in isolation.
 127 | The Desktop project depends on Core and never contains game logic.
 128 | 
 129 | ---
 130 | 
 131 | ## Architecture overview
 132 | 
 133 | Pathstone is built around three principles.
 134 | 
 135 | **1. The engine is pure and deterministic.** `MyGame.Core.World.World` is the
 136 | single authoritative state container. All mutations go through its methods
 137 | (`SpawnPlayer`, `SpawnNpc`, `AddLocation`, `SpawnItemOnGround`, ...). The RNG
 138 | is seedable and serializable (`Rng.State` / `Rng.FromState`), so a saved game
 139 | resumes exactly where it left off, bit-for-bit. The AI never mutates the world
 140 | directly; it calls tools, and the tools mutate the world.
 141 | 
 142 | **2. The host is the application.** There is no separate server process. When
 143 | a player clicks "Host game", the desktop process starts an
 144 | `HttpListener`-backed WebSocket server on an OS-assigned port. The host's
 145 | GameMaster engine runs in-process. Clients connect to the host's IP:port over a
 146 | raw WebSocket. When the host closes the application, the world is saved and
 147 | the session pauses. This is the desktop idiom (Don't Starve Together, Baldur's
 148 | Gate 3), not the web-server idiom.
 149 | 
 150 | **3. Identity is local.** A single `Profile` record lives in
 151 | `%APPDATA%/Pathstone/profile.json`. No JWT, no cookies, no auto-created guest
 152 | users. The phantom-user bug class that plagued the earlier web prototype is
 153 | structurally impossible: there is no server-side session store to get out of
 154 | sync with the client.
 155 | 
 156 | ### Data flow
 157 | 
 158 | ```
 159 |                        ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
 160 |                        │              MyGame.Desktop                  │
 161 |                        │  (Avalonia UI + ViewModels + Services)       │
 162 |                        └────────────┬────────────────────────┬────────┘
 163 |                                     │ commands / events      │ WebSocket
 164 |                                     ▼                        ▼
 165 |                        ┌─────────────────────┐  ┌──────────────────────┐
 166 |                        │   MyGame.Core       │  │   MyGame.Core         │
 167 |                        │   (engine + AI)     │  │   Multiplayer         │
 168 |                        │                     │  │                       │
 169 |                        │  World ◄──tools── AI│  │  HostServer           │
 170 |                        │   ▲                 │  │  GameClient           │
 171 |                        │   │ SaveManager      │  │  HostSession          │
 172 |                        │   │ ProfileStore     │  │  ClientSession        │
 173 |                        │   ▼                 │  │  ActionQueue          │
 174 |                        │  AppData/Pathstone/ │  │  Protocol (20 msgs)   │
 175 |                        └─────────────────────┘  └──────────────────────┘
 176 |                                                           │
 177 |                                                           ▼
 178 |                                                  remote clients
 179 | ```
 180 | 
 181 | In single-player mode the multiplayer layer is dormant. In host mode the
 182 | HostServer runs inside the same process as the GameMaster and broadcasts state
 183 | changes to connected clients. In client mode the local GameMaster is absent;
 184 | all action resolution happens on the host and arrives as `NarrativeFinal` /
 185 | `StateUpdate` messages.
 186 | 
 187 | ---
 188 | 
 189 | ## Core layers
 190 | 
 191 | ### Common
 192 | 
 193 | Foundation types used everywhere. No dependencies on other layers.
 194 | 
 195 | - **`EntityId`** — a `readonly record struct` wrapping a cuid-style string.
 196 |   Generated via `EntityId.NewId()` (timestamp base36 + random base36). Sortable,
 197 |   lexicographically comparable, JSON-convertible as a bare string.
 198 | - **`Rng`** — deterministic PRNG (PCG32, 64-bit state). `Next()`,
 199 |   `NextInt(min, max)`, `Pick<T>`, `Shuffle<T>`, `Fork()`. State round-trips
 200 |   through `State` / `FromState(long)` so saves reproduce exactly.
 201 | - **`EventBus`** — generic in-process pub/sub. `Subscribe<T>` returns an
 202 |   `IDisposable`; `Publish<T>` dispatches with exceptions caught per handler.
 203 | - **`Result<T>`** — `readonly record struct` for error handling without
 204 |   exceptions for flow control.
 205 | - **`Version`** — semantic version constant + parse / compare helpers.
 206 | 
 207 | ### Rules
 208 | 
 209 | - **`D20`** — `Roll`, `RollWithModifier`, `RollStat` (4d6 drop lowest),
 210 |   `Advantage`, `Disadvantage`.
 211 | - **`Check`** — `RollCheck(rng, modifier, dc)` returns a `CheckResult` with
 212 |   `CriticalSuccess` (natural 20) / `CriticalFailure` (natural 1) flags.
 213 |   `DifficultyClass` enum: Easy(5) / Medium(10) / Hard(15) / VeryHard(20) /
 214 |   NearlyImpossible(25).
 215 | 
 216 | ### World
 217 | 
 218 | The aggregate root and all entity types.
 219 | 
 220 | - **`World`** — holds entity collections (Players, Npcs, Items, Buildings,
 221 |   Locations, Quests), the `Rng`, `Clock` (`GameTime`), `Ruleset`, `Registries`
 222 |   (content), and a free-form `Flags` metadata bag. Serializes to JSON via
 223 |   `ToJson()` / `FromJson()`. RNG state round-trips for deterministic resumes.
 224 | - **`Calendar` / `GameTime`** — in-game clock (Day / Hour / Minute) with
 225 |   `Advance(int minutes)` and localization-aware formatting.
 226 | - **`Ruleset`** — game configuration record (turn model, token bill mode,
 227 |   visibility, transport mode) + `Rulesets.DefaultDnd` factory.
 228 | - **`EntityFactory`** — factory methods for `CreatePlayer`, `CreateNpcFromTemplate`,
 229 |   `InstantiateItem`, `CreateBuilding`, with attribute derivation and AC recompute.
 230 | - **`ContentRegistry`** — registers `ItemTemplate` / `NpcTemplate` /
 231 |   `BuildingTemplate`. Loads from an embedded `data.json` (26 items, 12 NPCs,
 232 |   8 buildings by default).
 233 | - **`DefaultWorld`** — factory for a hand-authored starting scene ("Долина
 234 |   Туманов") used by the "New Game" button when AI generation is off.
 235 | - **`WorldBuilderCommitter`** — deterministic plan-to-world mutator. Six
 236 |   internal sub-stages: custom templates, locations (with bidirectional exit
 237 |   wiring), population, buildings, content (starter gear + player), title.
 238 |   Fault-tolerant (broken entries are skipped and counted) and idempotent
 239 |   (re-commit matches by location name, no duplicates).
 240 | 
 241 | ### AI
 242 | 
 243 | - **`AiSettings`** — record: `BaseUrl`, `ApiKey`, `Model`, `Temperature`,
 244 |   `MaxTokens`.
 245 | - **`AiClient`** — OpenAI-compatible HTTP client. `ChatAsync`,
 246 |   `ChatWithToolsAsync`, `StreamChatAsync` (SSE `data:` line parsing). Static
 247 |   shared `HttpClient`. Retry once on 429 with 2s backoff; typed `AiException`
 248 |   on 401/403/5xx. Parses token usage into the response.
 249 | - **`Messages`** — `ChatMessage` (Role / Content / ToolCalls / ToolCallId /
 250 |   Name), `ToolCall`, `ToolDefinition`, `ChatResponse`. Factory helpers
 251 |   (`System`, `User`, `Assistant`, `AssistantWithTools`, `ToolResult`).
 252 | - **`PromptLoader`** — reads embedded `.md` prompt resources. `Get(name)`,
 253 |   `Render(name, vars)` with `{{var}}` substitution, `Exists(name)`.
 254 | - **`ToolRegistry`** — registers tool definitions + handlers. Lenient JSON arg
 255 |   parsing (handles double-escaped, malformed). Five built-in tools:
 256 |   `roll_dice`, `get_player_state`, `get_location`, `spawn_npc`, `advance_time`.
 257 | - **`GameMaster`** — the main agent. Builds messages, calls
 258 |   `ChatWithToolsAsync`, executes tool calls in a loop until no more, returns
 259 |   `NarrativeResult` (narration text, token usage, applied tool calls).
 260 | - **`StartSceneAgent`** — single AI call producing an atmospheric opening
 261 |   scene description (no tool loop).
 262 | - **`PetAgent`** — delegated sub-agent. Own message history, own iteration cap,
 263 |   `pet_done` signal tool. Used by the world-builder orchestrator for mass
 264 |   tasks ("spawn 10 NPCs", "create 5 items").
 265 | - **`WorldBuilderOrchestrator`** — three-stage pipeline: planner (1 AI call
 266 |   produces `WorldPlan` JSON), deterministic committer (no AI, mutates World
 267 |   from plan), narrator (1 AI call writes opening narration). Optional pet
 268 |   delegations run between committer and narrator. Progress reported via
 269 |   `IProgress<WorldBuildProgress>`.
 270 | 
 271 | ### Saves
 272 | 
 273 | - **`SaveManager`** — file-based. Each save is a directory under
 274 |   `%APPDATA%/Pathstone/saves/{saveId}/` containing `meta.json`, `world.json`,
 275 |   `log.json`, `state.json`. Atomic writes (`.tmp` then `File.Move`).
 276 |   `CreateSave`, `LoadAll`, `SaveAll`, `ListSaves`, `DeleteSave`.
 277 | - **`SaveMeta`** — id, name, ownerId, characterName, worldTitle, buildStatus,
 278 |   engineVersion, timestamps, turn count.
 279 | - **`LogEntry`** — narrative / action / system / tool log entries with
 280 |   metadata bag.
 281 | - **`CharacterSheet`** — standalone exportable character record (not tied to a
 282 |   World). For the BG3-style "your character travels with you between hosts"
 283 |   feature. `CharacterSheetStore` manages files under
 284 |   `%APPDATA%/Pathstone/characters/`.
 285 | 
 286 | ### Profile
 287 | 
 288 | Replaces the earlier JWT / cookie / auto-create-user auth entirely.
 289 | 
 290 | - **`Profile`** — record: `Guid Id`, `string Nickname`, timestamps.
 291 |   Nickname validation (2-20 chars, Latin / Cyrillic / digits / spaces /
 292 |   hyphens / underscores).
 293 | - **`ProfileStore`** — single local profile. `GetOrCreate()` loads from disk
 294 |   or creates with a random nickname (Russian prefixes + 4-char suffix).
 295 |   `Rename(string)`. Lives at `%APPDATA%/Pathstone/profile.json`.
 296 | - **`Settings`** — combines `AiSettings` + UI prefs (last server, autosave
 297 |   interval, stream narrative flag, language, volume, animations).
 298 | - **`SettingsStore`** — load / save / update with `Changed` event.
 299 | 
 300 | ### Multiplayer
 301 | 
 302 | Raw `System.Net.WebSockets`. No socket.io. No external libraries.
 303 | 
 304 | - **`HostServer`** — `HttpListener`-backed WebSocket server on an OS-assigned
 305 |   port (port 0 → OS picks free port). Per-connection `SemaphoreSlim` for
 306 |   concurrent-send safety. Auto-adds new connections as members.
 307 | - **`GameClient`** — `ClientWebSocket` outgoing connection. Handshake
 308 |   (`HelloMsg` → `WelcomeMsg`), background read loop, event-based dispatch.
 309 | - **`HostSession`** — glues `HostServer` + `GameMaster` + `SaveManager` +
 310 |   `ActionQueue`. Player action arrives → enqueue → GM processes (streaming
 311 |   narrative) → broadcast deltas → save state → broadcast state update.
 312 | - **`ClientSession`** — glues `GameClient` + local state cache. Incoming
 313 |   narrative deltas append to local log; state updates patch the local World
 314 |   view; outgoing actions send to host.
 315 | - **`ActionQueue`** — thread-safe pending-action queue. `Enqueue`,
 316 |   `Cancel(actionId)`, `DrainAll()`.
 317 | - **`MemberInfo`** — connection id, profile id, nickname, role
 318 |   (Host/Player/Spectator), status (Pending/Ready/Playing/Disconnected).
 319 | - **`Protocol`** — 20 message records, polymorphic JSON via
 320 |   `[JsonPolymorphic]` + `[JsonDerivedType]` with a `"kind"` discriminator.
 321 |   Categories: handshake (Hello/Welcome/Reject), lobby (MemberJoined/Left,
 322 |   Ready, Chat, StatusChanged), game (ActionQueued/Resolving, NarrativeDelta,
 323 |   NarrativeFinal, StateUpdate, TurnEnd, ActionCancel/Cancelled), system
 324 |   (Error, Kicked, Ping/Pong).
 325 | 
 326 | ---
 327 | 
 328 | ## Desktop application
 329 | 
 330 | Avalonia 12 with the FluentTheme (dark variant). MVVM via
 331 | CommunityToolkit.Mvvm source generators (`[ObservableProperty]`,
 332 | `[RelayCommand]`).
 333 | 
 334 | ### Screens
 335 | 
 336 | 1. **Main menu** — six buttons: New game (DefaultWorld), Create world (AI
 337 |    pipeline), Host game, Join game, Load save, Profile. Shows current
 338 |    nickname.
 339 | 2. **Profile** — edit nickname + AI settings (BaseUrl, ApiKey, Model,
 340 |    Temperature, MaxTokens).
 341 | 3. **Host game** — game name + max players + optional AI world-build
 342 |    checkbox + brief.
 343 | 4. **Join game** — host IP + port.
 344 | 5. **World brief** — free-form brief TextBox + 3 presets (dark fantasy /
 345 |    cyberpunk / post-apoc) + advanced-generation checkbox (pet-agent
 346 |    delegations).
 347 | 6. **World build** — progress dialog with live percent, stage label, detail,
 348 |    cancel button, "Continue" on success.
 349 | 7. **Game** — three-column layout: left tabbed panels (Character / Inventory /
 350 |    Quests / World) for single-player, members + chat for multiplayer; center
 351 |    narrative log + action input; right pending-actions queue (multiplayer
 352 |    only).
 353 | 
 354 | ### Side panels
 355 | 
 356 | - **CharacterPanel** — identity, attributes, resources, equipped gear,
 357 |   proficient skills, currency.
 358 | - **InventoryPanel** — carried items with use/equip/drop buttons, equipped
 359 |   items with unequip, carrying-capacity bar. Actions raise
 360 |   `ItemActionRequested` events handled by `GameViewModel` (use → apply
 361 |   consumable healing + effects; equip → swap slot; unequip → return to bag;
 362 |   drop → spawn on ground at current location).
 363 | - **QuestPanel** — active / completed / failed quests with objectives and
 364 |   rewards.
 365 | - **WorldPanel** — world title + clock + turn, current location (description,
 366 |   exits, inhabitants, buildings, ground items), all-locations map with
 367 |   visited / discovered markers.
 368 | 
 369 | All four panels refresh from the live `World` after every GM turn or state
 370 | update via `RefreshFromWorld()`.
 371 | 
 372 | ---
 373 | 
 374 | ## Build and run
 375 | 
 376 | ### Prerequisites
 377 | 
 378 | - .NET 8 SDK (8.0.4xx or newer)
 379 | - Avalonia workloads are not required (the desktop project uses the default
 380 |   Microsoft.NET.Sdk)
 381 | 
 382 | ### Build
 383 | 
 384 | ```bash
 385 | dotnet build MyGame.sln
 386 | ```
 387 | 
 388 | Expected output: `0 Warning(s) 0 Error(s)`.
 389 | 
 390 | ### Run
 391 | 
 392 | ```bash
 393 | dotnet run --project src/MyGame.Desktop
 394 | ```
 395 | 
 396 | The application starts at the main menu. First visit: open Profile, set a
 397 | nickname and AI settings (an OpenAI-compatible API key is required for any AI
 398 | feature: world-build, GM turns, narration).
 399 | 
 400 | ### Headless / CI smoke test
 401 | 
 402 | ```bash
 403 | xvfb-run -a dotnet run --project src/MyGame.Desktop
 404 | ```
 405 | 
 406 | The application initializes the window, renders the main menu, and idles. There
 407 | is no automated UI test harness yet (see issue tracker).
 408 | 
 409 | ### Packaging for Windows (issue #4)
 410 | 
 411 | A self-contained single-file Windows x64 build + NSIS installer is the
 412 | supported distribution path. The publish profile
 413 | (`src/MyGame.Desktop/Properties/PublishProfiles/win-x64.pubxml`) bundles the
 414 | .NET 8 runtime into the .exe, so end users don't need .NET installed.
 415 | 
 416 | #### Prerequisites
 417 | 
 418 | - .NET 8 SDK (build only)
 419 | - [NSIS 3.x](https://nsis.sourceforge.io/) (installer only — `makensis` must
 420 |   be on `PATH`)
 421 | 
 422 | #### Build the publish output
 423 | 
 424 | From the repository root (the `desktop-app/` directory):
 425 | 
 426 | ```bash
 427 | # Linux / macOS:
 428 | ./scripts/build-windows.sh
 429 | 
 430 | # Windows PowerShell:
 431 | .\scripts\build-windows.ps1
 432 | ```
 433 | 
 434 | Both produce `publish/win-x64/MyGame.Desktop.exe` plus the native Avalonia
 435 | libraries extracted alongside (single-file with
 436 | `IncludeNativeLibrariesForSelfExtract=true` — faster cold-start than
 437 | embedding them in the bundle).
 438 | 
 439 | #### Build the installer
 440 | 
 441 | ```bash
 442 | makensis installer/pathstone.nsi
 443 | ```
 444 | 
 445 | Produces `installer/Pathstone-Setup-0.2.0.exe`. The installer:
 446 | 
 447 | - installs to `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Pathstone` (per-user, no admin / UAC prompt);
 448 | - creates Start Menu shortcuts (Pathstone + Uninstall);
 449 | - optionally creates a Desktop shortcut;
 450 | - registers `.pathstone-world` and `.pathstone-char` file associations
 451 |   (the app doesn't yet parse file-arg launch — registered for future use);
 452 | - writes an Add/Remove Programs entry with display version, publisher,
 453 |   install location, and estimated size;
 454 | - ships an uninstaller that removes the install directory, shortcuts, and
 455 |   registry entries (saves and settings live under `%APPDATA%\Pathstone` and
 456 |   are preserved).
 457 | 
 458 | The version string is `MyGame.Core.Common.Version.Current` (currently
 459 | `0.2.0`); bump both the C# constant and `!define VERSION` at the top of
 460 | `installer/pathstone.nsi` in lockstep when releasing.
 461 | 
 462 | > **Note (closed #56):** the installer is unsigned. Windows SmartScreen will
 463 | > show a warning on first run — users click "More info" → "Run anyway".
 464 | > Code-signing requires a certificate (EV or OV) and is tracked separately.
 465 | 
 466 | ---
 467 | 
 468 | ## Configuration
 469 | 
 470 | All configuration lives under `%APPDATA%/Pathstone/` (Windows),
 471 | `~/.config/Pathstone/` (Linux), or `~/Library/Application Support/Pathstone/`
 472 | (macOS). The folder contains:
 473 | 
 474 | | File             | Purpose                                  |
 475 | |------------------|------------------------------------------|
 476 | | `profile.json`   | Local profile (id + nickname)            |
 477 | | `settings.json`  | UI prefs + AI settings                   |
 478 | | `saves/`         | One directory per save (4 JSON files)    |
 479 | | `characters/`    | Exported portable character sheets       |
 480 | 
 481 | The AI settings support any OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Set `BaseUrl` to the
 482 | provider's API root (e.g. `https://api.openai.com/v1`,
 483 | `http://localhost:11434/v1` for Ollama). The `ApiKey` is sent as a Bearer
 484 | token; leave null for providers that don't require auth.
 485 | 
 486 | ---
 487 | 
 488 | ## Local AI setup (issue #27)
 489 | 
 490 | Pathstone works with any OpenAI-compatible provider, including local model
 491 | servers like [Ollama](https://ollama.ai) and [llama.cpp](https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp).
 492 | This lets you run the entire game offline — no API key, no per-token billing.
 493 | Trade-off: local models are slower than cloud ones, and smaller context
 494 | windows mean the GM's history summarization triggers more often.
 495 | 
 496 | ### Ollama (recommended for first-time local users)
 497 | 
 498 | 1. **Install Ollama** — download the installer for your platform from
 499 |    <https://ollama.ai> (Windows / macOS / Linux). On Linux you can also
 500 |    `curl -fsSL https://ollama.ai/install.sh | sh`.
 501 | 2. **Pull a model that supports tool calling** — Pathstone's GM uses
 502 |    function-calling (the `tools` field), so you need a model with a
 503 |    tool-call template. Verified options:
 504 |    ```bash
 505 |    ollama pull llama3.1:8b       # 4.7 GB, good balance
 506 |    ollama pull mistral:7b        # 4.1 GB, slightly weaker tools
 507 |    ollama pull qwen2.5:7b        # 4.7 GB, strong on Russian
 508 |    ```
 509 |    Older models (llama2, llama3.0, phi-2) silently ignore the `tools`
 510 |    field and the GM loop won't progress — stick to llama3.1+ / mistral /
 511 |    qwen2 / qwen2.5.
 512 | 3. **Configure Pathstone** — open the app, go to **Настройки**, click
 513 |    the **«Ollama (локально)»** preset (or fill the fields manually):
 514 |    - Base URL: `http://localhost:11434/v1`
 515 |    - Model: `llama3.1:8b` (or whichever you pulled)
 516 |    - API Key: leave empty (Ollama doesn't require auth; the `Authorization`
 517 |      header is skipped when the key is blank)
 518 | 4. **Set context window** — Ollama's default context is 4k tokens, which
 519 |    the GM blows through in ~6 turns. In **Настройки → Контекстное окно**
 520 |    set `Max context tokens` to `8000` (or higher if your machine has the
 521 |    RAM). The GM will summarize older history when the live context
 522 |    crosses 80% of this threshold.
 523 | 
 524 | ### llama.cpp server (advanced)
 525 | 
 526 | For more control over the model (quantization, custom GGUF files, KV-cache
 527 | tuning), run a [llama.cpp server](https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp/tree/master/tools/server):
 528 | 
 529 | ```bash
 530 | # Build llama.cpp, then start the server with an OpenAI-compatible API:
 531 | ./server -m model.gguf --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8080
 532 | ```
 533 | 
 534 | In Pathstone's settings click **«llama.cpp»** preset:
 535 | - Base URL: `http://localhost:8080/v1`
 536 | - Model: `local-model` (llama.cpp doesn't care about the model name; any
 537 |   non-empty string works)
 538 | - API Key: leave empty
 539 | 
 540 | ### Trade-offs vs. cloud models
 541 | 
 542 | | Property        | Local (Ollama / llama.cpp)    | Cloud (OpenAI / DeepSeek)   |
 543 | |-----------------|-------------------------------|-----------------------------|
 544 | | Latency         | 5–30 s/turn on consumer HW    | 1–5 s/turn                  |
 545 | | Cost            | Free (electricity + RAM)      | $0.01–0.10/turn             |
 546 | | Privacy         | Full — no data leaves machine | Provider logs requests      |
 547 | | Context window  | 4k–8k typical                 | 16k–128k                    |
 548 | | Tool calling    | llama3.1+ / mistral / qwen2   | All current models          |
 549 | | Quality         | Lower (7B params)             | Higher (175B+ params)       |
 550 | 
 551 | For a smooth first experience, start with a cloud provider (OpenAI or
 552 | DeepSeek), then switch to local once you've confirmed the app works.
 553 | 
 554 | ### Troubleshooting
 555 | 
 556 | - **401 Unauthorized on Ollama**: you left an old API key in the settings.
 557 |   Click the «Ollama (локально)» preset (which clears the key) or manually
 558 |   blank the **API Key** field.
 559 | - **GM doesn't progress, narrates but never calls tools**: your model
 560 |   doesn't support tool calling. Pull `llama3.1:8b` (or another tool-capable
 561 |   model) and update the **Model** field.
 562 | - **Stream cuts off mid-narration**: context window too small. Increase
 563 |   **Max context tokens** to 8000+ in Настройки.
 564 | - **`connection refused` on local server**: Ollama isn't running. Start
 565 |   it with `ollama serve` (or just launch the Ollama desktop app — it
 566 |   auto-starts the server).
 567 | 
 568 | ---
 569 | 
 570 | ## AI pipeline
 571 | 
 572 | The world-build pipeline is the most complex AI flow. It runs three stages,
 573 | optionally four.
 574 | 
 575 | ### Stage 1: Planner (1 AI call)
 576 | 
 577 | Loads the `world-planner.md` prompt, fills `{{WORLD_BRIEF}}`,
 578 | `{{WORLD_STATE}}`, `{{ITEM_TEMPLATES}}`, `{{NPC_TEMPLATES}}`,
 579 | `{{BUILDING_TEMPLATES}}`, and asks the model to produce a `WorldPlan` as a
 580 | fenced JSON block. The plan is a richly-typed record: title, theme, setting,
 581 | atmosphere, locations (with terrain/danger/role/connections), NPCs, buildings,
 582 | custom templates, starter gear, opening hook, plus optional lore (cosmology,
 583 | history, regions, cultures, economy, magic system, factions).
 584 | 
 585 | ### Stage 2: Committer (no AI)
 586 | 
 587 | `WorldBuilderCommitter` mutates the live `World` from the plan in six
 588 | sub-stages: custom templates → locations (with bidirectional exit wiring) →
 589 | population → buildings → content (starter gear + player) → title. Each
 590 | sub-stage is fault-tolerant (broken entries skipped + counted) and idempotent
 591 | (re-commit matches by name, no duplicates).
 592 | 
 593 | ### Stage 2b: Pet-agent delegations (optional, N AI calls)
 594 | 
 595 | If the caller provided `PetDelegation` records, the orchestrator runs each as
 596 | a separate `PetAgent` with its own LLM conversation + tool loop. Each
 597 | delegation has a label, a task description, optional per-pet AI settings, and
 598 | an iteration cap. The pet agent has full tool access (spawn_npc, give_item,
 599 | etc.) and signals completion via the `pet_done` tool. Failures are non-fatal;
 600 | one delegation crashing does not block others.
 601 | 
 602 | ### Stage 3: Narrator (1 AI call)
 603 | 
 604 | Loads the `world-narrator.md` prompt, fills `{{WORLD_PLAN}}` (readable
 605 | summary) and `{{WORLD_STATE}}` (live world snapshot after the committer ran —
 606 | real location descriptions, real NPC names). Asks the model for 2 short
 607 | paragraphs of atmospheric opening narration. The narration is saved as the
 608 | first `LogEntry` on the save, so it appears immediately when the player enters
 609 | the game.
 610 | 
 611 | ### Cost
 612 | 
 613 | Without pet delegations: 2 AI calls per world build, ~30-90 seconds.
 614 | With the default 2 pet delegations: 4 AI calls, ~60-150 seconds.
 615 | 
 616 | ---
 617 | 
 618 | ## Multiplayer protocol
 619 | 
 620 | All messages inherit from `NetMessage` and use polymorphic JSON serialization
 621 | via `[JsonPolymorphic]` + `[JsonDerivedType]` attributes with a `"kind"`
 622 | discriminator field. The wire format is camelCase UTF-8 JSON.
 623 | 
 624 | ### Handshake
 625 | 
 626 | 1. Client opens WebSocket to `ws://{host}:{port}/`.
 627 | 2. Client sends `HelloMsg` with protocol version + profile nickname + profile id.
 628 | 3. Host validates nickname uniqueness, assigns a connection id, sends
 629 |    `WelcomeMsg` with the party state snapshot + the client's role.
 630 | 4. Host broadcasts `MemberJoinedMsg` to all other connections.
 631 | 
 632 | ### Lobby events
 633 | 
 634 | `MemberJoinedMsg`, `MemberLeftMsg`, `MemberReadyMsg`, `ChatMsg`,
 635 | `StatusChangedMsg` (party status: Lobby / Worldbuilding / CharacterCreation /
 636 | Playing).
 637 | 
 638 | ### Game events
 639 | 
 640 | - Client → Host: `ActionQueuedMsg` (player submits an action), `ActionCancelMsg`.
 641 | - Host → All: `ActionResolvingMsg` (host starts resolving a batch),
 642 |   `NarrativeDeltaMsg` (streamed text chunk), `NarrativeFinalMsg` (full text +
 643 |   tool events), `StateUpdateMsg` (world snapshot), `TurnEndMsg`.
 644 | - Host → One: `ErrorMsg`, `KickedMsg`.
 645 | 
 646 | ### System
 647 | 
 648 | `PingMsg` / `PongMsg` for keepalive. The client fires `Disconnected` on socket
 649 | close; the UI shows a reconnect prompt. Reconnect logic is not yet
 650 | implemented (see issue tracker).
 651 | 
 652 | ---
 653 | 
 654 | ## Save file format
 655 | 
 656 | Each save is a directory `%APPDATA%/Pathstone/saves/save_{Guid:N}/` containing
 657 | four JSON files.
 658 | 
 659 | ### `meta.json`
 660 | 
 661 | ```json
 662 | {
 663 |   "id": "save_abc123...",
 664 |   "name": "Тёмные Шпили Велариса",
 665 |   "ownerId": "profile-guid",
 666 |   "partyId": null,
 667 |   "characterName": "Странник",
 668 |   "characterLevel": 1,
 669 |   "worldTitle": "Тёмные Шпили Велариса",
 670 |   "turn": 7,
 671 |   "buildStatus": "done",
 672 |   "engineVersion": "0.2.0",
 673 |   "createdAt": "2025-01-01T12:00:00Z",
 674 |   "updatedAt": "2025-01-01T14:30:00Z"
 675 | }
 676 | ```
 677 | 
 678 | ### `world.json`
 679 | 
 680 | The full `World` state: all entity collections, RNG state, clock, ruleset,
 681 | content registry, flags. Serialized via `World.ToJson()` with camelCase
 682 | policy and `UnsafeRelaxedJsonEscaping` (Cyrillic writes as raw UTF-8, not
 683 | `\uXXXX` escapes). Round-trips through `World.FromJson()` bit-for-bit; RNG
 684 | state reproduces exactly.
 685 | 
 686 | ### `log.json`
 687 | 
 688 | Array of `LogEntry` records (narrative / action / system / tool entries with
 689 | timestamps and metadata).
 690 | 
 691 | ### `state.json`
 692 | 
 693 | Runtime state: turn number, current player entity id, token usage totals,
 694 | build state. A denormalized subset of `world.json` for fast UI boot without
 695 | loading the full world.
 696 | 
 697 | ### Atomic writes
 698 | 
 699 | Each file is written to `{file}.tmp` then `File.Move`d to its final path.
 700 | `meta.json` is written last, so a crash mid-save leaves a loadable (stale-meta)
 701 | state rather than a corrupted one.
 702 | 
 703 | ---
 704 | 
 705 | ## Project conventions
 706 | 
 707 | ### Naming
 708 | 
 709 | - PascalCase for types, methods, properties.
 710 | - camelCase for local variables and parameters.
 711 | - `_camelCase` for private fields.
 712 | 
 713 | ### Namespaces
 714 | 
 715 | - `MyGame.Core.Common`, `MyGame.Core.Rules`, `MyGame.Core.World`,
 716 |   `MyGame.Core.World.Entities`, `MyGame.Core.World.Content`,
 717 |   `MyGame.Core.AI`, `MyGame.Core.AI.Agents`, `MyGame.Core.AI.Prompts`,
 718 |   `MyGame.Core.AI.Tools`, `MyGame.Core.Saves`, `MyGame.Core.Profile`,
 719 |   `MyGame.Core.Multiplayer`, `MyGame.Core.Multiplayer.Protocol`.
 720 | - `MyGame.Desktop.Services`, `MyGame.Desktop.ViewModels`,
 721 |   `MyGame.Desktop.ViewModels.Panels`, `MyGame.Desktop.Views`,
 722 |   `MyGame.Desktop.Views.Panels`.
 723 | 
 724 | ### Async
 725 | 
 726 | - `Task<T>` and `CancellationToken` on all public async methods.
 727 | - `ConfigureAwait(false)` in Core (no synchronization context).
 728 | - UI thread marshaling via `Avalonia.Threading.Dispatcher.UIThread.Post` in
 729 |   Desktop event handlers.
 730 | 
 731 | ### JSON
 732 | 
 733 | - `System.Text.Json` throughout.
 734 | - camelCase wire format for AI responses and save files.
 735 | - `PropertyNameCaseInsensitive = true` on deserialization.
 736 | - `UnsafeRelaxedJsonEscaping` for human-readable Cyrillic in saves.
 737 | 
 738 | ### Mutability
 739 | 
 740 | - Records for immutable data (entity snapshots, message types, plan records).
 741 | - Classes for stateful services (`World`, `GameMaster`, `SaveManager`,
 742 |   `HostServer`).
 743 | - Entities are mutable classes (HP changes, location shifts, inventory
 744 |   mutations happen in place).
 745 | 
 746 | ### Error handling
 747 | 
 748 | - `Result<T>` for flow-control errors in Core (file not found, parse failure).
 749 | - Exceptions for programmer errors (null args, invalid state).
 750 | - `AiException` for HTTP / parse failures from the LLM provider.
 751 | - All tool handlers wrapped in try/catch; failures become `ToolResult` with
 752 |   `IsError = true` and feed back to the model.
 753 | 
 754 | ### Thread safety
 755 | 
 756 | - `ConcurrentDictionary` for connection registries.
 757 | - `SemaphoreSlim` per WebSocket (concurrent sends corrupt the wire).
 758 | - Plain `lock` for the action queue (`DrainAll` wants bulk removal).
 759 | - `EventBus` dispatches outside the lock to allow re-entry.
 760 | 
 761 | ---
 762 | 
 763 | ## Roadmap
 764 | 
 765 | The roadmap is tracked in the [GitHub issues](https://github.com/GardenXsa/Pathstone/issues).
 766 | Issues are labeled by layer (`core`, `ui`, `ai`, `mp`, `saves`, `engine`,
 767 | `content`, `audio`, `packaging`, `tooling`, `polish`) and by priority
 768 | (`mvp`, `mvp-blocker`, `stretch`).
 769 | 
 770 | High-level themes, in rough priority order:
 771 | 
 772 | 1. **MVP completion** — travel UI, character creation, combat, basic packaging.
 773 |    These are the `mvp-blocker` issues.
 774 | 2. **AI robustness** — streaming narration, context window management,
 775 |    anti-loop detection, multi-model support, local models.
 776 | 3. **Multiplayer polish** — reconnect, host migration, UPnP, late-joiner sync.
 777 | 4. **Content depth** — more templates, faction system, economy, procedural
 778 |    dungeons, weather.
 779 | 5. **Polish** — sounds, music, animations, themes, localization,
 780 |    accessibility.
 781 | 6. **Packaging** — Windows installer, macOS bundle, Linux AppImage, auto-update,
 782 |    code signing.
 783 | 7. **Stretch** — voice chat, mod support, demo mode, documentation site.
 784 | 
 785 | ---
 786 | 
 787 | ## Contributing
 788 | 
 789 | The project is in active alpha development. The architecture is settled; the
 790 | work is feature implementation. If you want to contribute:
 791 | 
 792 | 1. Pick an issue labeled `good first issue` or `mvp` from the tracker.
 793 | 2. Fork the repository and create a feature branch.
 794 | 3. Follow the conventions above. Build must pass with 0 warnings, 0 errors.
 795 | 4. Open a pull request referencing the issue.
 796 | 
 797 | For architectural questions, open a discussion or an issue labeled `question`.
 798 | 
 799 | ### Development environment
 800 | 
 801 | - .NET 8 SDK
 802 | - Any C#-aware editor (Visual Studio, Rider, VS Code with C# Dev Kit)
 803 | - Linux/macOS developers: `xvfb-run` for headless smoke testing
 804 | 
 805 | ### Running tests
 806 | 
 807 | There is no test project yet. Unit tests for Core (engine rules, save
 808 | round-trip, AI client with a mock HTTP handler) are tracked in the issue
 809 | tracker.
 810 | 
 811 | ---
 812 | 
 813 | ## License
 814 | 
 815 | To be determined. The current alpha is source-available on GitHub. A
 816 | permissive license (MIT or Apache 2.0) is likely once the MVP ships.
 817 | 
 818 | ---
 819 | 
 820 | ## Acknowledgements
 821 | 
 822 | Pathstone is a rewrite of an earlier prototype. The architecture was
 823 | reconsidered from scratch after the prototype's web-first assumptions (Next.js
 824 | API server, JWT auth, socket.io, browser client) proved to be a poor fit for
 825 | a desktop TTRPG application. The rewrite keeps the game design and the AI
 826 | prompt corpus; it replaces everything else.

--- docs/README.md (Markdown, 31 lines, ~234 tokens) ---
   1 | # Pathstone Documentation
   2 | 
   3 | This directory contains the configuration for generating API documentation
   4 | using [docfx](https://dotnet.github.io/docfx/).
   5 | 
   6 | ## Building the docs
   7 | 
   8 | ```bash
   9 | # Install docfx as a global tool (one-time)
  10 | dotnet tool install -g docfx
  11 | 
  12 | # Generate + serve locally
  13 | docfx docfx.json --serve
  14 | 
  15 | # Or just generate (output in _site/)
  16 | docfx docfx.json
  17 | ```
  18 | 
  19 | ## Structure
  20 | 
  21 | - `docfx.json` — docfx configuration (at repo root)
  22 | - `README.md` — the main landing page (docfx picks this up as index.html)
  23 | - `api/` — auto-generated API reference (from XML doc comments)
  24 | - `CHANGELOG.md` — included as a content page
  25 | 
  26 | ## Hosting on GitHub Pages
  27 | 
  28 | 1. Run `docfx docfx.json` to generate `_site/`.
  29 | 2. Copy `_site/` contents to a `gh-pages` branch.
  30 | 3. Enable GitHub Pages in repo settings → Pages → Source: `gh-pages` branch.

--- scripts/build-linux.sh (Shell, 17 lines, ~165 tokens) ---
   1 | #!/usr/bin/env bash
   2 | # Build Pathstone for Linux (issue #53).
   3 | # Produces a self-contained single-file binary at publish/linux-x64/.
   4 | set -euo pipefail
   5 | cd "$(dirname "$0")/.."
   6 | echo "Building Pathstone for Linux (linux-x64)..."
   7 | dotnet publish src/MyGame.Desktop/MyGame.Desktop.csproj \
   8 |   -c Release \
   9 |   -r linux-x64 \
  10 |   --self-contained true \
  11 |   -p:PublishSingleFile=true \
  12 |   -p:IncludeNativeLibrariesForSelfExtract=true \
  13 |   -p:EnableCompressionInSingleFile=true \
  14 |   -o publish/linux-x64
  15 | chmod +x publish/linux-x64/MyGame.Desktop
  16 | echo "Done. Output: publish/linux-x64/MyGame.Desktop"

   6 | cd "$(dirname "$0")/.."
   7 | echo "Building Pathstone for macOS (osx-x64)..."
   8 | dotnet publish src/MyGame.Desktop/MyGame.Desktop.csproj \
   9 |   -c Release \
