--- scripts/build-windows.ps1 (PowerShell, 52 lines, ~483 tokens) ---
   1 | # WIN-INSTALLER (issue #4): PowerShell equivalent of
   2 | # build-windows.sh for Windows developers. Produces a self-contained
   3 | # single-file win-x64 build in `publish\win-x64\`.
   4 | #
   5 | # Usage (from the `desktop-app\` directory):
   6 | #     .\scripts\build-windows.ps1
   7 | #
   8 | # Prerequisites:
   9 | #   * .NET 8 SDK (dotnet --version >= 8.0).
  10 | #
  11 | # The build is unsigned (see closed #56 — the installer will trigger
  12 | # a SmartScreen warning on first run).
  13 | 
  14 | [CmdletBinding()]
  15 | param()
  16 | 
  17 | $ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
  18 | 
  19 | # Resolve repo root relative to this script (lives in
  20 | # desktop-app\scripts\).
  21 | $ScriptDir = Split-Path -Parent $MyInvocation.MyCommand.Path
  22 | $RepoRoot  = Split-Path -Parent $ScriptDir
  23 | 
  24 | $Project = Join-Path $RepoRoot 'src\MyGame.Desktop\MyGame.Desktop.csproj'
  25 | $OutDir  = Join-Path $RepoRoot 'publish\win-x64'
  26 | 
  27 | Write-Host "==> Publishing Pathstone for win-x64 (self-contained, single-file)..." -ForegroundColor Cyan
  28 | Write-Host "    Project: $Project"
  29 | Write-Host "    Output:  $OutDir"
  30 | Write-Host ""
  31 | 
  32 | dotnet publish $Project `
  33 |     -c Release `
  34 |     -r win-x64 `
  35 |     --self-contained true `
  36 |     -p:PublishSingleFile=true `
  37 |     -p:IncludeNativeLibrariesForSelfExtract=true `
  38 |     -p:EnableCompressionInSingleFile=true `
  39 |     -o $OutDir
  40 | 
  41 | if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) {
  42 |     throw "dotnet publish failed with exit code $LASTEXITCODE"
  43 | }
  44 | 
  45 | Write-Host ""
  46 | Write-Host "==> Publish complete." -ForegroundColor Green
  47 | Write-Host "    Entry point: $OutDir\MyGame.Desktop.exe"
  48 | Write-Host ""
  49 | Write-Host "Next step: build the installer with NSIS:" -ForegroundColor Cyan
  50 | Write-Host "    makensis $(Join-Path $RepoRoot 'installer\pathstone.nsi')"
  51 | Write-Host "    (produces $(Join-Path $RepoRoot 'installer\Pathstone-Setup-0.2.0.exe'))"

--- scripts/build-windows.sh (Shell, 52 lines, ~505 tokens) ---
   1 | #!/usr/bin/env bash
   2 | # WIN-INSTALLER (issue #4): build a self-contained single-file
   3 | # Windows x64 binary of Pathstone. Output goes to
   4 | # `publish/win-x64/` (containing `MyGame.Desktop.exe` + the native
   5 | # Avalonia libraries). The NSIS installer script
   6 | # (`installer/pathstone.nsi`) packages this folder into a Windows
   7 | # installer .exe.
   8 | #
   9 | # Usage (from the repository root, i.e. the `desktop-app/` dir):
  10 | #     ./scripts/build-windows.sh
  11 | #
  12 | # Prerequisites:
  13 | #   * .NET 8 SDK (`dotnet --version` ≥ 8.0).
  14 | #   * No Windows-only toolchain required — `dotnet publish` can
  15 | #     cross-publish to win-x64 from Linux/macOS.
  16 | #
  17 | # The build is unsigned (see closed #56 — the installer will trigger
  18 | # a SmartScreen warning on first run; users click "More info" →
  19 | # "Run anyway").
  20 | 
  21 | set -euo pipefail
  22 | 
  23 | # Resolve the repo root (the `desktop-app/` directory) relative to
  24 | # this script. The script lives in `desktop-app/scripts/`.
  25 | SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
  26 | REPO_ROOT="$(cd "${SCRIPT_DIR}/.." && pwd)"
  27 | 
  28 | PROJECT="${REPO_ROOT}/src/MyGame.Desktop/MyGame.Desktop.csproj"
  29 | OUT_DIR="${REPO_ROOT}/publish/win-x64"
  30 | 
  31 | echo "==> Publishing Pathstone for win-x64 (self-contained, single-file)…"
  32 | echo "    Project: ${PROJECT}"
  33 | echo "    Output:  ${OUT_DIR}"
  34 | echo
  35 | 
  36 | dotnet publish "${PROJECT}" \
  37 |     -c Release \
  38 |     -r win-x64 \
  39 |     --self-contained true \
  40 |     -p:PublishSingleFile=true \
  41 |     -p:IncludeNativeLibrariesForSelfExtract=true \
  42 |     -p:EnableCompressionInSingleFile=true \
  43 |     -o "${OUT_DIR}"
  44 | 
  45 | echo
  46 | echo "==> Publish complete."
  47 | echo "    Entry point: ${OUT_DIR}/MyGame.Desktop.exe"
  48 | echo
  49 | echo "Next step: build the installer with NSIS:"
  50 | echo "    makensis ${REPO_ROOT}/installer/pathstone.nsi"
  51 | echo "    (produces ${REPO_ROOT}/installer/Pathstone-Setup-0.2.0.exe)"

--- scripts/package-linux-appimage.sh (Shell, 77 lines, ~715 tokens) ---
   1 | #!/usr/bin/env bash
   2 | # Package Pathstone as an AppImage for Linux (issue #53).
   3 | # Requires: scripts/build-linux.sh has been run (publish/linux-x64/ exists).
   4 | # Requires: appimagetool (https://github.com/AppImage/AppImageKit).
   5 | # Produces: publish/Pathstone-x86_64.AppImage
   6 | set -euo pipefail
   7 | cd "$(dirname "$0")/.."
   8 | 
   9 | APPDIR="publish/Pathstone.AppDir"
  10 | 
  11 | if [ ! -d "publish/linux-x64" ]; then
  12 |   echo "Error: publish/linux-x64/ not found. Run scripts/build-linux.sh first."
  13 |   exit 1
  14 | fi
  15 | 
  16 | echo "Creating AppDir structure..."
  17 | rm -rf "$APPDIR"
  18 | mkdir -p "$APPDIR/usr/bin" "$APPDIR/usr/share/icons/hicolor/256x256/apps"
  19 | 
  20 | # Copy binary.
  21 | cp -r publish/linux-x64/* "$APPDIR/usr/bin/"
  22 | chmod +x "$APPDIR/usr/bin/MyGame.Desktop"
  23 | 
  24 | # .desktop file.
  25 | cat > "$APPDIR/pathstone.desktop" << 'DESKTOP'
  26 | [Desktop Entry]
  27 | Name=Pathstone
  28 | Comment=Desktop multiplayer narrative RPG with AI Game Master
  29 | Exec=MyGame.Desktop
  30 | Icon=pathstone
  31 | Terminal=false
  32 | Type=Application
  33 | Categories=Game;RolePlaying;
  34 | DESKTOP
  35 | 
  36 | # AppRun script.
  37 | cat > "$APPDIR/AppRun" << 'APPRUN'
  38 | #!/usr/bin/env bash
  39 | SELF="$(readlink -f "$0")"
  40 | HERE="$(dirname "$SELF")"
  41 | exec "$HERE/usr/bin/MyGame.Desktop" "$@"
  42 | APPRUN
  43 | chmod +x "$APPDIR/AppRun"
  44 | 
  45 | # Placeholder icon (a 1x1 PNG if no real icon exists — replace with real art).
  46 | if [ -f "public/logo.svg" ]; then
  47 |   # Try to convert SVG to PNG if rsvg-convert is available.
  48 |   if command -v rsvg-convert &>/dev/null; then
  49 |     rsvg-convert -w 256 -h 256 public/logo.svg -o "$APPDIR/pathstone.png"
  50 |   else
  51 |     # Fallback: create a simple 256x256 dark blue PNG.
  52 |     python3 -c "
  53 | import struct, zlib
  54 | def png(w,h,data):
  55 |   def chunk(t,d): return struct.pack('>I',len(d))+t+d+struct.pack('>I',zlib.crc32(t+d)&0xffffffff)
  56 |   sig=b'\x89PNG\r\n\x1a\n'
  57 |   ihdr=struct.pack('>IIBBBBB',w,h,8,2,0,0,0)
  58 |   raw=b''
  59 |   for y in range(h): raw+=b'\x00'+data
  60 |   idat=zlib.compress(raw)
  61 |   return sig+chunk(b'IHDR',ihdr)+chunk(b'IDAT',idat)+chunk(b'IEND',b'')
  62 | open('$APPDIR/pathstone.png','wb').write(png(256,256,b'\x0f\x11\x15'*256))
  63 | " 2>/dev/null || true
  64 |   fi
  65 | fi
  66 | cp "$APPDIR/pathstone.png" "$APPDIR/usr/share/icons/hicolor/256x256/apps/pathstone.png" 2>/dev/null || true
  67 | 
  68 | # Build AppImage.
  69 | if command -v appimagetool &>/dev/null; then
  70 |   appimagetool "$APPDIR" "publish/Pathstone-x86_64.AppImage"
  71 |   echo "Done: publish/Pathstone-x86_64.AppImage"
  72 | else
  73 |   echo "appimagetool not found. AppDir created at: $APPDIR"
  74 |   echo "Install appimagetool from https://github.com/AppImage/AppImageKit"
  75 |   echo "Then run: appimagetool $APPDIR publish/Pathstone-x86_64.AppImage"
  76 | fi

--- scripts/package-macos.sh (Shell, 59 lines, ~498 tokens) ---
   1 | #!/usr/bin/env bash
   2 | # Package Pathstone into a macOS .app bundle (issue #52).
   3 | # Requires: scripts/build-macos.sh has been run (publish/osx-x64/ exists).
   4 | # Produces: publish/Pathstone.app/
   5 | set -euo pipefail
   6 | cd "$(dirname "$0")/.."
   7 | 
   8 | APP_DIR="publish/Pathstone.app"
   9 | CONTENTS="$APP_DIR/Contents"
  10 | MACOS_DIR="$CONTENTS/MacOS"
  11 | RESOURCES_DIR="$CONTENTS/Resources"
  12 | 
  13 | if [ ! -d "publish/osx-x64" ]; then
  14 |   echo "Error: publish/osx-x64/ not found. Run scripts/build-macos.sh first."
  15 |   exit 1
  16 | }
  17 | 
  18 | echo "Creating .app bundle structure..."
  19 | rm -rf "$APP_DIR"
  20 | mkdir -p "$MACOS_DIR" "$RESOURCES_DIR"
  21 | 
  22 | # Copy the self-contained binary + native libs.
  23 | cp -r publish/osx-x64/* "$MACOS_DIR/"
  24 | 
  25 | # Info.plist — macOS app metadata.
  26 | cat > "$CONTENTS/Info.plist" << 'PLIST'
  27 | <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
  28 | <!DOCTYPE plist PUBLIC "-//Apple//DTD PLIST 1.0//EN" "http://www.apple.com/DTDs/PropertyList-1.0.dtd">
  29 | <plist version="1.0">
  30 | <dict>
  31 |   <key>CFBundleName</key>
  32 |   <string>Pathstone</string>
  33 |   <key>CFBundleDisplayName</key>
  34 |   <string>Pathstone</string>
  35 |   <key>CFBundleIdentifier</key>
  36 |   <string>com.gardenxsa.pathstone</string>
  37 |   <key>CFBundleVersion</key>
  38 |   <string>0.3.0</string>
  39 |   <key>CFBundleShortVersionString</key>
  40 |   <string>0.3.0</string>
  41 |   <key>CFBundlePackageType</key>
  42 |   <string>APPL</string>
  43 |   <key>CFBundleExecutable</key>
  44 |   <string>MyGame.Desktop</string>
  45 |   <key>LSMinimumSystemVersion</key>
  46 |   <string>10.15</string>
  47 |   <key>NSHighResolutionCapable</key>
  48 |   <true/>
  49 | </dict>
  50 | </plist>
  51 | PLIST
  52 | 
  53 | # Make the executable actually executable.
  54 | chmod +x "$MACOS_DIR/MyGame.Desktop"
  55 | 
  56 | echo "Done: $APP_DIR"
  57 | echo "Note: This bundle is unsigned. macOS Gatekeeper will warn on first launch."
  58 | echo "      Users can right-click → Open to bypass, or run: xattr -cr $APP_DIR"

--- src/MyGame.Core/AI/Agents/GameMaster.cs (C#, 1193 lines, ~15,753 tokens) ---
   1 | using System.Diagnostics;
   2 | using System.Text;
   3 | using System.Text.Json;
   4 | using MyGame.Core.AI.Prompts;
   5 | using MyGame.Core.AI.Tools;
   6 | using MyGame.Core.Logging;
   7 | using MyGame.Core.World;
   8 | using MyGame.Core.World.Entities;
   9 | 
  10 | 
  11 | namespace MyGame.Core.AI.Agents;
  12 | 
  13 | /// <summary>
  14 | /// Result of one GameMaster turn. Port-side record (the TS source returned
  15 | /// a bare <c>string</c> narration; we wrap it to also surface token usage
  16 | /// and applied tool calls for the UI).
  17 | /// </summary>
  18 | public sealed record NarrativeResult
  19 | {
  20 |     /// <summary>
  21 |     /// Concatenated assistant narration across all iterations of the
  22 |     /// tool-call loop. May be empty if the model only emitted tool calls.
  23 |     /// </summary>
  24 |     public required string NarrativeText { get; init; }
  25 | 
  26 |     /// <summary>Total prompt tokens billed across all iterations.</summary>
  27 |     public int PromptTokens { get; init; }
  28 | 
  29 |     /// <summary>Total completion tokens billed across all iterations.</summary>
  30 |     public int CompletionTokens { get; init; }
  31 | 
  32 |     /// <summary>Total tokens billed across all iterations.</summary>
  33 |     public int TotalTokens { get; init; }
  34 | 
  35 |     /// <summary>How many iterations the tool-call loop ran.</summary>
  36 |     public int Iterations { get; init; }
  37 | 
  38 |     /// <summary>Final <c>finish_reason</c> from the provider.</summary>
  39 |     public string? FinishReason { get; init; }
  40 | 
  41 |     /// <summary>
  42 |     /// Tool calls the GM made this turn (name, raw args JSON, result text,
  43 |     /// whether it errored). Useful for the UI's tool-call feed.
  44 |     /// </summary>
  45 |     public IReadOnlyList<AppliedToolCall> ToolCalls { get; init; } = Array.Empty<AppliedToolCall>();
  46 | 
  47 |     /// <summary>True if the loop terminated via an AI exception (auth, network, etc.).</summary>
  48 |     public bool Failed { get; init; }
  49 | 
  50 |     /// <summary>Error message when <see cref="Failed"/> is true.</summary>
  51 |     public string? Error { get; init; }
  52 | }
  53 | 
  54 | /// <summary>
  55 | /// One tool call the GM executed during a turn. Port-side record for the
  56 | /// UI's tool-call feed.
  57 | /// </summary>
  58 | public sealed record AppliedToolCall
  59 | {
  60 |     public required string Name { get; init; }
  61 |     public required string ArgsJson { get; init; }
  62 |     public required string Result { get; init; }
  63 |     public bool IsError { get; init; }
  64 | }
  65 | 
  66 | /// <summary>
  67 | /// AI Game Master agent. Port of <c>ai/agents/gameMaster.ts</c> (core loop
  68 | /// only — features tied to the Next.js server are skipped, see worklog).
  69 | ///
  70 | /// Flow:
  71 | ///  <list type="number">
  72 | ///  <item>Build messages: [system prompt, recent history, player action].</item>
  73 | ///  <item>Call <see cref="AiClient.ChatWithToolsAsync"/> with the registered tools.</item>
  74 | ///  <item>If the response carries tool calls, execute each via
  75 | ///    <see cref="ToolRegistry.ExecuteAsync"/>, append the assistant message
  76 | ///    + tool result messages to the working history, and loop back to (2).</item>
  77 | ///  <item>If no tool calls, the loop terminates; return the concatenated
  78 | ///    narration + token usage + applied tool calls as a
  79 | ///    <see cref="NarrativeResult"/>.</item>
  80 | ///  </list>
  81 | ///
  82 | /// <b>Skips vs. TS source</b> (documented in the worklog):
  83 | ///  <list type="bullet">
  84 | ///  <item>The <c>{{time:+Xm}}</c> hidden-block stream filter — not needed
  85 | ///    here because we don't stream and the model can call
  86 | ///    <c>advance_time</c> explicitly.</item>
  87 | ///  <item>The <c>end_turn</c> tool — the minimal tool set doesn't include
  88 | ///    it; the loop simply terminates when the model stops calling tools.</item>
  89 | ///  <item>Length-pacing nudges and loop-stall nudges — the agent will be
  90 | ///    wired into a future "soft nudge" mechanism; for now we cap at
  91 | ///    <see cref="MaxIterations"/> and return what we have.</item>
  92 | ///  <item>Conversation persistence (<c>world.appendConversation</c>) — the
  93 | ///    spec says to skip persistence (SaveManager is a different layer).
  94 | ///    History is kept in-memory on this <see cref="GameMaster"/> instance.</item>
  95 | ///  <item>Streaming — the loop uses the non-streaming
  96 | ///    <see cref="AiClient.ChatWithToolsAsync"/> for simplicity. A future
  97 | ///    task can swap to streaming for live UI feedback.</item>
  98 | ///  </list>
  99 | /// </summary>
 100 | public sealed class GameMaster
 101 | {
 102 |     /// <summary>
 103 |     /// Default per-turn iteration cap. Matches the TS source's default
 104 |     /// (<c>maxIterations || 8</c>). Each iteration = one
 105 |     /// <see cref="AiClient.ChatWithToolsAsync"/> call; a turn with N tool
 106 |     /// calls typically takes N+1 iterations (one per tool batch + a final
 107 |     /// narration-only round).
 108 |     /// </summary>
 109 |     public const int DefaultMaxIterations = 8;
 110 | 
 111 |     /// <summary>
 112 |     /// When the in-memory conversation history exceeds this many messages,
 113 |     /// the oldest half is sent to the summarizer and replaced with a
 114 |     /// short text summary (issue #25). 30 messages ≈ 5–10 turns of
 115 |     /// back-and-forth (each turn = one user action + one assistant
 116 |     /// narration, occasionally split by tool calls). The threshold is
 117 |     /// deliberately conservative: we'd rather summarise a bit early than
 118 |     /// blow the context window mid-turn.
 119 |     /// </summary>
 120 |     public const int SummarizeAfterMessages = 30;
 121 | 
 122 |     /// <summary>
 123 |     /// Hard cap on completion tokens for the summarization call itself.
 124 |     /// The summary is meant to be a short recap (~300 words ≈ 400 tokens),
 125 |     /// so 500 leaves headroom for the model's preamble / formatting
 126 |     /// without burning the full <see cref="AiSettings.MaxTokens"/> budget.
 127 |     /// </summary>
 128 |     public const int SummaryMaxTokens = 500;
 129 | 
 130 |     /// <summary>
 131 |     /// Default cap on the total estimated prompt size (system + summary +
 132 |     /// history + world state), in tokens. When the estimate exceeds 80%
 133 |     /// of this value, summarization is triggered EARLY — before the
 134 |     /// <see cref="SummarizeAfterMessages"/> threshold is reached. 12000 is
 135 |     /// conservative for the typical 16k-context model class (gpt-4o-mini,
 136 |     /// deepseek-chat, llama3.1:8b); larger-context models can override
 137 |     /// this via <see cref="Profile.Settings.MaxContextTokens"/>.
 138 |     /// </summary>
 139 |     public const int DefaultMaxContextTokens = 12000;
 140 | 
 141 |     private readonly IAiClient _ai;
 142 |     private readonly AiSettings? _aiSettings;
 143 |     private readonly MyGame.Core.World.World _world;
 144 |     private readonly PromptLoader _prompts;
 145 |     private readonly ToolRegistry _tools;
 146 |     private readonly int _maxIterations;
 147 |     private readonly int _maxContextTokens;
 148 |     private readonly List<ChatMessage> _history = new();
 149 | 
 150 |     /// <summary>
 151 |     /// Running text summary of the older (already-summarised) portion of
 152 |     /// the conversation. Null until the first summarization pass completes
 153 |     /// (issue #25). Persisted on <see cref="Saves.SaveMeta.HistorySummary"/>
 154 |     /// so the GM doesn't lose context across save/load — see
 155 |     /// <see cref="HistorySummary"/>.
 156 |     /// </summary>
 157 |     private string? _summary;
 158 | 
 159 |     /// <summary>
 160 |     /// Per-turn tool-call loop detector. Reset at the start of each
 161 |     /// <see cref="ProcessActionAsync"/> call; consulted after each tool
 162 |     /// call to inject a Russian-language nudge into the working history
 163 |     /// when the GM is repeating itself.
 164 |     /// </summary>
 165 |     private readonly LoopDetector _loopDetector = new();
 166 | 
 167 |     /// <summary>
 168 |     /// Create a GameMaster bound to the given AI client, world, prompt
 169 |     /// loader, and tool registry. The same instance should be reused
 170 |     /// across turns so the in-memory conversation history accumulates
 171 |     /// (call <see cref="ResetHistory"/> to clear it, e.g. on save load).
 172 |     /// </summary>
 173 |     /// <param name="ai">Base AI client used for all GM calls. When
 174 |     /// <paramref name="aiSettings"/> is provided, a role-specific client
 175 |     /// is derived via <see cref="IAiClient.WithModel"/> for the
 176 |     /// <see cref="AiRole.GM"/> model override (issue #26).</param>
 177 |     /// <param name="aiSettings">Optional AI settings — used to derive a
 178 |     /// role-specific model (issue #26) and read per-role overrides. When
 179 |     /// null, the base <paramref name="ai"/> client is used as-is.</param>
 180 |     /// <param name="maxContextTokens">Optional override for the
 181 |     /// context-window threshold used by the early-summarization trigger
 182 |     /// (issue #25). Defaults to <see cref="DefaultMaxContextTokens"/>.</param>
 183 |     public GameMaster(
 184 |         IAiClient ai,
 185 |         MyGame.Core.World.World world,
 186 |         PromptLoader prompts,
 187 |         ToolRegistry tools,
 188 |         int? maxIterations = null,
 189 |         AiSettings? aiSettings = null,
 190 |         int? maxContextTokens = null)
 191 |     {
 192 |         _ai = ai ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(ai));
 193 |         _world = world ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(world));
 194 |         _prompts = prompts ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(prompts));
 195 |         _tools = tools ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(tools));
 196 |         _maxIterations = Math.Max(1, Math.Min(50, maxIterations ?? DefaultMaxIterations));
 197 |         _aiSettings = aiSettings;
 198 |         _maxContextTokens = Math.Max(1024, maxContextTokens ?? DefaultMaxContextTokens);
 199 |     }
 200 | 
 201 |     /// <summary>
 202 |     /// In-memory conversation history (excluding the system prompt, which
 203 |     /// is rebuilt each turn). Trimmed to the last
 204 |     /// <see cref="MessagesConstants.MaxConversationMessages"/> messages.
 205 |     /// Older history may also be summarized into <see cref="HistorySummary"/>
 206 |     /// when it exceeds <see cref="SummarizeAfterMessages"/> or when the
 207 |     /// estimated prompt size approaches the context-window threshold
 208 |     /// (issue #25).
 209 |     /// </summary>
 210 |     public IReadOnlyList<ChatMessage> History => _history;
 211 | 
 212 |     /// <summary>
 213 |     /// Text summary of the older (already-summarised) portion of the
 214 |     /// conversation, or null when no summarization has happened yet (issue #25).
 215 |     ///
 216 |     /// <para>
 217 |     /// Persisted on <see cref="Saves.SaveMeta.HistorySummary"/> so the GM
 218 |     /// doesn't lose context across save/load: on save, the host copies
 219 |     /// this property into <c>meta.HistorySummary</c>; on load, the host
 220 |     /// restores it by assigning back here. The summary is then prepended
 221 |     /// to the working message list as a user message
 222 |     /// (<c>## Предыдущие события (изложение)</c>) so the model sees the
 223 |     /// compressed history before the recent turns.
 224 |     /// </para>
 225 |     /// </summary>
 226 |     public string? HistorySummary
 227 |     {
 228 |         get => _summary;
 229 |         set => _summary = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(value) ? null : value;
 230 |     }
 231 | 
 232 |     /// <summary>Clear the in-memory conversation history.</summary>
 233 |     public void ResetHistory() => _history.Clear();
 234 | 
 235 |     /// <summary>
 236 |     /// Process one player action: build the prompt, run the tool-call loop,
 237 |     /// and return the final narration + token usage + applied tool calls.
 238 |     /// </summary>
 239 |     public Task<NarrativeResult> ProcessActionAsync(
 240 |         string playerAction,
 241 |         CancellationToken ct = default)
 242 |         => ProcessActionBatchAsync(new[] { playerAction }, narrativeDelta: null, ct);
 243 | 
 244 |     /// <summary>
 245 |     /// Process one player action with optional streaming narrative deltas.
 246 |     /// </summary>
 247 |     public Task<NarrativeResult> ProcessActionAsync(
 248 |         string playerAction,
 249 |         IProgress<string>? narrativeDelta,
 250 |         CancellationToken ct = default)
 251 |         => ProcessActionBatchAsync(new[] { playerAction }, narrativeDelta, ct);
 252 | 
 253 |     /// <summary>
 254 |     /// Process a batch of player actions in a single GM turn. This is the
 255 |     /// "action_queue" turn model: all players act, the GM resolves the
 256 |     /// actions together in one narration + tool-call loop.
 257 |     ///
 258 |     /// <list type="bullet">
 259 |     ///   <item><c>actions.Count == 0</c> → returns immediately with an
 260 |     ///     empty result (no-op).</item>
 261 |     ///   <item><c>actions.Count == 1</c> → identical to the legacy
 262 |     ///     single-action <see cref="ProcessActionAsync(string, CancellationToken)"/>
 263 |     ///     call (the player action becomes the lone user message).</item>
 264 |     ///   <item><c>actions.Count &gt; 1</c> → builds a single combined user
 265 |     ///     message listing the numbered actions and runs the GM once. The
 266 |     ///     GM narrates the resolution of ALL actions in one scene.</item>
 267 |     /// </list>
 268 |     ///
 269 |     /// <para><b>Prompt structure (issue #89):</b> the system prompt is
 270 |     /// built from <c>system.md</c> (rules) + <c>narrator.md</c> (style
 271 |     /// guide) + <c>tools-guide.md</c> (tool reference) + a static
 272 |     /// world-lore block (title, theme, setting, atmosphere, starting
 273 |     /// hook). All of these change ONLY on world rebuild, so the system
 274 |     /// prompt is byte-identical across turns within a session — which
 275 |     /// lets the AI provider cache it server-side. The dynamic per-turn
 276 |     /// world state (player, location, NPCs, time, last action) is sent
 277 |     /// as a SEPARATE user message right before the player action,
 278 |     /// labeled <c>## ТЕКУЩЕЕ СОСТОЯНИЕ МИРА</c>, so it doesn't
 279 |     /// invalidate the cached system prefix.</para>
 280 |     /// </summary>
 281 |     /// <param name="actions">Player action texts. Order is preserved in
 282 |     /// the combined prompt (1-indexed list).</param>
 283 |     /// <param name="narrativeDelta">Optional streaming callback. See
 284 |     /// <see cref="ProcessActionAsync(string, IProgress{string}, CancellationToken)"/>.</param>
 285 |     /// <param name="ct">Cancellation token.</param>
 286 |     public async Task<NarrativeResult> ProcessActionBatchAsync(
 287 |         IReadOnlyList<string> actions,
 288 |         IProgress<string>? narrativeDelta = null,
 289 |         CancellationToken ct = default)
 290 |     {
 291 |         if (actions is null || actions.Count == 0)
 292 |         {
 293 |             return new NarrativeResult { NarrativeText = string.Empty };
 294 |         }
 295 | 
 296 |         // Issue #71: log the GM turn start.
 297 |         try { GameLogger.Instance?.Info($"[GameMaster] Turn {_world.Turn + 1}: {actions.Count} action(s)"); }
 298 |         catch { /* logger not initialized */ }
 299 | 
 300 |         var playerAction = actions.Count == 1
 301 |             ? actions[0]
 302 |             : BuildBatchActionMessage(actions);
 303 | 
 304 |         if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(playerAction))
 305 |             throw new ArgumentException("Action text is required.", nameof(actions));
 306 | 
 307 |         // Reset the loop detector for this turn — signatures don't carry
 308 |         // across turns.
 309 |         _loopDetector.Reset();
 310 | 
 311 |         // Derive a role-specific client for the GM role (issue #26). When
 312 |         // _aiSettings is null OR no GMModel override is set, WithModel
 313 |         // returns the same client (no allocation). Otherwise it produces a
 314 |         // thin wrapper that shares the base client's HttpClient.
 315 |         var gmAi = _aiSettings is null ? _ai : _ai.WithModel(_aiSettings.GetModelForRole(AiRole.GM));
 316 | 
 317 |         // Build the STATIC system prompt (system.md + narrator.md +
 318 |         // tools-guide.md + STATIC_WORLD_LORE). Identical across turns
 319 |         // within a session → eligible for provider-side prompt caching.
 320 |         var systemPrompt = BuildSystemPrompt();
 321 | 
 322 |         // Verification hook (issue #89): when MYGAME_VERIFY_STATIC_PROMPT=1,
 323 |         // assert that the system prompt is byte-identical to the previous
 324 |         // turn's. If it differs, the cache is being defeated — log a
 325 |         // warning so the dev sees it. (No-op in production unless the
 326 |         // env var is set.)
 327 |         VerifyStaticSystemPrompt(systemPrompt);
 328 | 
 329 |         // Dynamic per-turn world state (player, location, NPCs, time,
 330 |         // last action). Goes in a separate user message labeled clearly
 331 |         // as context, NOT as a player command. This keeps the cached
 332 |         // system prefix untouched.
 333 |         var worldStateBlock = "## ТЕКУЩЕЕ СОСТОЯНИЕ МИРА\n" + BuildWorldStateBlock();
 334 | 
 335 |         // Context-window management (issue #25): if the in-memory history
 336 |         // exceeds the summarization threshold, OR the estimated total
 337 |         // prompt size approaches the context-window limit, fold the oldest
 338 |         // half of the history into a short text summary. The summary is
 339 |         // stored in _summary and prepended to the working message list as
 340 |         // a user message. Failures are non-fatal — the turn continues
 341 |         // with the unsummarized history (we'll try again next turn).
 342 |         await MaybeSummarizeAsync(gmAi, systemPrompt, worldStateBlock, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 343 | 
 344 |         var toolDefs = _tools.Definitions.ToList();
 345 |         var working = new List<ChatMessage>();
 346 |         working.Add(ChatMessage.System(systemPrompt));
 347 |         // If a summary from a prior summarization pass exists, surface it
 348 |         // as a user message right after the system prompt — the model
 349 |         // sees the compressed history before the recent turns. Marked as
 350 |         // context (not a player command) via the heading.
 351 |         if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(_summary))
 352 |         {
 353 |             working.Add(ChatMessage.User(
 354 |                 "## Предыдущие события (изложение)\n" + _summary));
 355 |         }
 356 |         // Trim history to the most recent N messages so the prompt doesn't
 357 |         // blow past the provider's context window on long sessions.
 358 |         var trimmedHistory = _history.Count > MessagesConstants.MaxConversationMessages
 359 |             ? _history.Skip(_history.Count - MessagesConstants.MaxConversationMessages).ToList()
 360 |             : _history;
 361 |         working.AddRange(trimmedHistory);
 362 |         working.Add(ChatMessage.User(worldStateBlock));
 363 |         // firstNewIdx marks where the per-turn messages start. Everything
 364 |         // from here onward is new this turn and gets persisted to
 365 |         // _history (the world-state context-refresh message is NOT
 366 |         // persisted — it's per-turn and would otherwise leave stale
 367 |         // snapshots in history).
 368 |         var firstNewIdx = working.Count;
 369 |         working.Add(ChatMessage.User(playerAction));
 370 | 
 371 |         var narration = new StringBuilder();
 372 |         var appliedCalls = new List<AppliedToolCall>();
 373 |         int promptTokens = 0, completionTokens = 0, totalTokens = 0;
 374 |         string? finishReason = null;
 375 |         int iteration = 0;
 376 | 
 377 |         try
 378 |         {
 379 |             while (iteration < _maxIterations)
 380 |             {
 381 |                 iteration++;
 382 |                 ct.ThrowIfCancellationRequested();
 383 | 
 384 |                 // Streaming iteration: forward content deltas to the
 385 |                 // callback as they arrive; accumulate the full
 386 |                 // ChatResponse (content + tool_calls + finish_reason +
 387 |                 // token usage) in the holder for processing below.
 388 |                 var holder = new StreamChatResult();
 389 |                 await foreach (var delta in gmAi.StreamChatWithToolsAsync(
 390 |                     working, toolDefs, holder, ct).ConfigureAwait(false))
 391 |                 {
 392 |                     narrativeDelta?.Report(delta);
 393 |                 }
 394 |                 var response = holder.Response ?? new ChatResponse();
 395 | 
 396 |                 // ChatResponse now carries token counts directly (per task
 397 |                 // 3-c-1 spec) — no separate Usage sub-record.
 398 |                 promptTokens += response.PromptTokens;
 399 |                 completionTokens += response.CompletionTokens;
 400 |                 totalTokens += response.PromptTokens + response.CompletionTokens;
 401 |                 finishReason = response.FinishReason;
 402 | 
 403 |                 if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(response.Content))
 404 |                     narration.Append(response.Content);
 405 | 
 406 |                 // No tool calls — the turn is done.
 407 |                 if (response.ToolCalls is null || response.ToolCalls.Count == 0)
 408 |                 {
 409 |                     // Append the assistant's content-only message to the
 410 |                     // working history so the next turn sees it.
 411 |                     working.Add(ChatMessage.Assistant(response.Content));
 412 |                     break;
 413 |                 }
 414 | 
 415 |                 // Append the assistant message WITH tool_calls so the
 416 |                 // provider can correlate the tool results with this batch.
 417 |                 working.Add(new ChatMessage
 418 |                 {
 419 |                     Role = ChatRole.Assistant,
 420 |                     Content = response.Content,
 421 |                     ToolCalls = response.ToolCalls.ToList(),
 422 |                 });
 423 | 
 424 |                 // Anti-loop: record each tool call's signature and inject
 425 |                 // a nudge if a repetition pattern is detected. At most one
 426 |                 // nudge per iteration (so we don't spam the model with
 427 |                 // multiple nudges for one batch of tool calls).
 428 |                 string? nudgeThisIteration = null;
 429 |                 foreach (var tc in response.ToolCalls)
 430 |                 {
 431 |                     var nudge = _loopDetector.Record(tc.Name, tc.Arguments);
 432 |                     if (nudge is not null && nudgeThisIteration is null)
 433 |                         nudgeThisIteration = nudge.Text;
 434 |                 }
 435 |                 if (nudgeThisIteration is not null)
 436 |                     working.Add(ChatMessage.System(nudgeThisIteration));
 437 | 
 438 |                 // Execute each tool call in order. The provider expects a
 439 |                 // tool-result message per call, with tool_call_id matching.
 440 |                 foreach (var tc in response.ToolCalls)
 441 |                 {
 442 |                     var result = await _tools.ExecuteAsync(tc.Id, tc.Name, tc.Arguments, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 443 |                     appliedCalls.Add(new AppliedToolCall
 444 |                     {
 445 |                         Name = tc.Name,
 446 |                         ArgsJson = tc.Arguments,
 447 |                         Result = result.Content,
 448 |                         IsError = result.IsError,
 449 |                     });
 450 |                     working.Add(ChatMessage.ToolResult(tc.Id, result.Content));
 451 |                 }
 452 | 
 453 |                 // Loop continues — the model will see the tool results and
 454 |                 // either call more tools or produce a final narration.
 455 |             }
 456 | 
 457 |             // Persist the new messages from this turn into the in-memory
 458 |             // history (excluding the system prompt, which is rebuilt each
 459 |             // turn, AND excluding the per-turn world-state user message,
 460 |             // which would leave stale state snapshots in history). The
 461 |             // first new message is the player action at firstNewIdx.
 462 |             var newMessages = working
 463 |                 .Skip(firstNewIdx)
 464 |                 .ToList();
 465 |             _history.AddRange(newMessages);
 466 |             TrimHistory();
 467 | 
 468 |             return new NarrativeResult
 469 |             {
 470 |                 NarrativeText = narration.ToString(),
 471 |                 PromptTokens = promptTokens,
 472 |                 CompletionTokens = completionTokens,
 473 |                 TotalTokens = totalTokens,
 474 |                 Iterations = iteration,
 475 |                 FinishReason = finishReason,
 476 |                 ToolCalls = appliedCalls,
 477 |             };
 478 |         }
 479 |         catch (AiException ex)
 480 |         {
 481 |             // Even on failure, persist whatever messages we accumulated so
 482 |             // the next turn can continue from where we left off (the TS
 483 |             // source did the same). Persist from the player action
 484 |             // (firstNewIdx) onwards — the per-turn world-state user
 485 |             // message is excluded to avoid stale snapshots in history.
 486 |             if (firstNewIdx < working.Count)
 487 |             {
 488 |                 _history.AddRange(working.Skip(firstNewIdx));
 489 |                 TrimHistory();
 490 |             }
 491 | 
 492 |             return new NarrativeResult
 493 |             {
 494 |                 NarrativeText = narration.ToString(),
 495 |                 PromptTokens = promptTokens,
 496 |                 CompletionTokens = completionTokens,
 497 |                 TotalTokens = totalTokens,
 498 |                 Iterations = iteration,
 499 |                 FinishReason = finishReason,
 500 |                 ToolCalls = appliedCalls,
 501 |                 Failed = true,
 502 |                 Error = ex.Message,
 503 |             };
 504 |         }
 505 |     }
 506 | 
 507 |     private void TrimHistory()
 508 |     {
 509 |         if (_history.Count > MessagesConstants.MaxConversationMessages)
 510 |             _history.RemoveRange(0, _history.Count - MessagesConstants.MaxConversationMessages);
 511 |     }
 512 | 
 513 |     // ── Context-window management (issue #25) ──────────────────────────
 514 | 
 515 |     /// <summary>
 516 |     /// Decide whether to summarize the oldest portion of the conversation
 517 |     /// history, and if so, do it. Two triggers (either fires):
 518 |     ///
 519 |     /// <list type="bullet">
 520 |     ///   <item><b>Message-count trigger:</b> <c>_history.Count &gt;</c>
 521 |     ///     <see cref="SummarizeAfterMessages"/>. 30 messages ≈ 5–10 turns;
 522 |     ///     past that, the older turns are increasingly stale and worth
 523 |     ///     compressing.</item>
 524 |     ///   <item><b>Token-estimate trigger:</b> the estimated total prompt
 525 |     ///     size (system + summary + history + world state) exceeds 80% of
 526 |     ///     <see cref="_maxContextTokens"/>. This catches long narrations
 527 |     ///     (single turns can be 1000+ tokens) before they blow the
 528 |     ///     provider's context window.</item>
 529 |     /// </list>
 530 |     ///
 531 |     /// <para>
 532 |     /// When triggered, the OLDEST HALF of <c>_history</c> is sent to
 533 |     /// <see cref="SummarizeHistoryAsync"/>. If a summary already exists
 534 |     /// (from a prior pass), it's prepended to the messages being
 535 |     /// summarized so the new summary is cumulative ("previous summary +
 536 |     /// new events") rather than a fresh recap each time. The summarized
 537 |     /// messages are then removed from <c>_history</c> and the new summary
 538 |     /// is stored in <see cref="_summary"/>.
 539 |     /// </para>
 540 |     ///
 541 |     /// <para>
 542 |     /// <b>Failure handling:</b> if the summarization AI call throws an
 543 |     /// <see cref="AiException"/> (network, auth, etc.), the turn proceeds
 544 |     /// with the unsummarized history — the GM continues, the user sees
 545 |     /// their action resolved, and we'll try again next turn. This is
 546 |     /// preferable to bricking the game because the summarizer hit a rate
 547 |     /// limit. The exception is logged to <c>Trace</c>.
 548 |     /// </para>
 549 |     /// </summary>
 550 |     private async Task MaybeSummarizeAsync(
 551 |         IAiClient gmAi,
 552 |         string systemPrompt,
 553 |         string worldStateBlock,
 554 |         CancellationToken ct)
 555 |     {
 556 |         if (_history.Count == 0) return;
 557 | 
 558 |         // Token-estimate trigger: total prompt size approaching 80% of the
 559 |         // context-window budget. The estimate is rough (4 chars ≈ 1 token,
 560 |         // see EstimateTokens) but adequate for triggering — we'd rather
 561 |         // summarize a touch early than blow the context window.
 562 |         var estimatedTokens = EstimateTokens(systemPrompt)
 563 |             + EstimateTokens(_summary)
 564 |             + EstimateTokens(worldStateBlock);
 565 |         foreach (var m in _history)
 566 |             estimatedTokens += EstimateTokens(m.Content);
 567 |         var tokenTrigger = estimatedTokens > (_maxContextTokens * 0.8);
 568 | 
 569 |         var countTrigger = _history.Count > SummarizeAfterMessages;
 570 |         if (!countTrigger && !tokenTrigger)
 571 |             return;
 572 | 
 573 |         // Summarize the oldest half (at least 1 message). The newer half
 574 |         // stays in _history verbatim so the most recent turns retain their
 575 |         // full detail (the model needs precise recall of the last few
 576 |         // actions to narrate coherently).
 577 |         var summarizeCount = Math.Max(1, _history.Count / 2);
 578 |         var toSummarize = _history.Take(summarizeCount).ToList();
 579 |         if (toSummarize.Count == 0) return;
 580 | 
 581 |         try
 582 |         {
 583 |             var newSummary = await SummarizeHistoryAsync(gmAi, toSummarize, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 584 |             if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(newSummary))
 585 |             {
 586 |                 // Remove the summarized messages from _history. The newer
 587 |                 // half (which we kept) is now at the front.
 588 |                 _history.RemoveRange(0, summarizeCount);
 589 |                 _summary = newSummary;
 590 |             }
 591 |         }
 592 |         catch (OperationCanceledException) { throw; }
 593 |         catch (AiException ex)
 594 |         {
 595 |             // Non-fatal — log and continue with the unsummarized history.
 596 |             // We'll try again next turn (the trigger will re-fire).
 597 |             Trace.WriteLine(
 598 |                 $"[GameMaster] summarization failed (non-fatal): {ex.Kind}: {ex.Message}. " +
 599 |                 $"History will be sent unsummarized this turn.");
 600 |         }
 601 |     }
 602 | 
 603 |     /// <summary>
 604 |     /// Call the AI to summarize a slice of the conversation history into a
 605 |     /// short text recap (~300 words, ~400 tokens). Russian-language system
 606 |     /// prompt per the spec (issue #25). When <paramref name="oldMessages"/>
 607 |     /// is empty, returns an empty string (no AI call). When a summary
 608 |     /// already exists (see <see cref="_summary"/>), it's prepended to the
 609 |     /// user content so the new summary is cumulative — the model sees
 610 |     /// "previous summary + new events" and produces a unified recap.
 611 |     ///
 612 |     /// <para>
 613 |     /// The summarization call uses the GM role's AI client (issue #26:
 614 |     /// multi-model support) — it's the same agent that produced the
 615 |     /// history, so it has the right context-window budget and temperature.
 616 |     /// No tools are passed (plain chat completion). The
 617 |     /// <see cref="SummaryMaxTokens"/> cap is enforced via a derived
 618 |     /// <see cref="AiSettings"/> so the model doesn't burn the full GM
 619 |     /// completion budget on the recap.
 620 |     /// </para>
 621 |     /// </summary>
 622 |     private async Task<string> SummarizeHistoryAsync(
 623 |         IAiClient gmAi,
 624 |         IReadOnlyList<ChatMessage> oldMessages,
 625 |         CancellationToken ct)
 626 |     {
 627 |         if (oldMessages is null || oldMessages.Count == 0)
 628 |             return string.Empty;
 629 | 
 630 |         const string systemContent =
 631 |             "Ты — ассистент, который кратко излагает историю игровой сессии. " +
 632 |             "Сожми следующие сообщения в краткое изложение (~300 слов), сохранив " +
 633 |             "ключевые события, решения, имена NPC, локации, и текущие цели. На русском.";
 634 | 
 635 |         // Concatenate the old messages as a readable transcript. Each
 636 |         // message is prefixed with its role so the summarizer can tell
 637 |         // player actions (user) from GM narration (assistant) from tool
 638 |         // results (tool).
 639 |         var sb = new StringBuilder();
 640 |         if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(_summary))
 641 |         {
 642 |             // Cumulative summary: prepend the existing summary so the model
 643 |             // produces a unified recap ("previous summary + new events").
 644 |             sb.AppendLine("Предыдущее изложение:");
 645 |             sb.AppendLine(_summary);
 646 |             sb.AppendLine();
 647 |             sb.AppendLine("Новые события:");
 648 |         }
 649 |         foreach (var m in oldMessages)
 650 |         {
 651 |             var role = m.Role switch
 652 |             {
 653 |                 ChatRole.System => "система",
 654 |                 ChatRole.User => "игрок",
 655 |                 ChatRole.Assistant => "GM",
 656 |                 ChatRole.Tool => "инструмент",
 657 |                 _ => m.Role.ToString(),
 658 |             };
 659 |             var content = m.Content ?? string.Empty;
 660 |             if (m.Role == ChatRole.Assistant && m.ToolCalls is { Count: > 0 })
 661 |             {
 662 |                 // Surface tool-call requests compactly so the summarizer
 663 |                 // sees the model's intent even when content is null.
 664 |                 var calls = string.Join(", ", m.ToolCalls.Select(tc => $"{tc.Name}({tc.Arguments})"));
 665 |                 content = string.IsNullOrEmpty(content)
 666 |                     ? $"[инструменты: {calls}]"
 667 |                     : $"{content} [инструменты: {calls}]";
 668 |             }
 669 |             sb.Append($"[{role}] ");
 670 |             sb.AppendLine(content);
 671 |         }
 672 | 
 673 |         // Derive a one-off client with the summarization token cap so we
 674 |         // don't burn the full GM MaxTokens budget on the recap. WithModel
 675 |         // returns the same instance when the model is unchanged; the
 676 |         // fresh AiSettings below lowers MaxTokens for this call only.
 677 |         // We construct via the public (AiSettings) ctor which uses the
 678 |         // shared static HttpClient — the summarization call doesn't need
 679 |         // a custom handler / socket pool.
 680 |         var summarySettings = gmAi.Settings with { MaxTokens = SummaryMaxTokens };
 681 |         var summaryClient = new AiClient(summarySettings);
 682 |         var messages = new List<ChatMessage>
 683 |         {
 684 |             ChatMessage.System(systemContent),
 685 |             ChatMessage.User(sb.ToString()),
 686 |         };
 687 |         var response = await summaryClient.ChatAsync(messages, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 688 |         return (response.Content ?? string.Empty).Trim();
 689 |     }
 690 | 
 691 |     /// <summary>
 692 |     /// Rough token-count estimate: 4 characters ≈ 1 token. Adequate for
 693 |     /// triggering summarization — we only need a reasonable upper bound,
 694 |     /// not an exact count (the actual tokenizer is provider-specific and
 695 |     /// not worth the dependency for this purpose). Returns 0 for null/
 696 |     /// empty input. Exposed as <c>public static</c> so tests can verify
 697 |     /// the math without constructing a GameMaster, and because future
 698 |     /// agents (PetAgent, StartSceneAgent) may want to reuse the estimate.
 699 |     /// </summary>
 700 |     public static int EstimateTokens(string? text)
 701 |     {
 702 |         if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(text)) return 0;
 703 |         // (length + 3) / 4 rounds up so a 1-char string is 1 token (not 0).
 704 |         return (text!.Length + 3) / 4;
 705 |     }
 706 | 
 707 |     /// <summary>
 708 |     /// Build the combined user-message text for a batch of player actions.
 709 |     /// Renders as a numbered Russian list so the GM knows multiple players
 710 |     /// are acting in one turn and resolves them in one narration. Example:
 711 |     /// <code>
 712 |     /// ## Действия игроков в этом ходу:
 713 |     ///
 714 |     /// 1. атакую гоблина мечом
 715 |     /// 2. кастую огненный шар
 716 |     /// </code>
 717 |     /// </summary>
 718 |     private static string BuildBatchActionMessage(IReadOnlyList<string> actions)
 719 |     {
 720 |         var sb = new StringBuilder();
 721 |         sb.AppendLine("## Действия игроков в этом ходу:");
 722 |         sb.AppendLine();
 723 |         for (int i = 0; i < actions.Count; i++)
 724 |         {
 725 |             sb.AppendLine($"{i + 1}. {actions[i]}");
 726 |         }
 727 |         return sb.ToString();
 728 |     }
 729 | 
 730 |     /// <summary>
 731 |     /// Build the STATIC system prompt: <c>system.md</c> (rules) +
 732 |     /// <c>narrator.md</c> (style guide) + <c>tools-guide.md</c> (tool
 733 |     /// reference) + a static world-lore block. All components change only
 734 |     /// on world rebuild, NOT per-turn — so the system prompt is
 735 |     /// byte-identical across turns within a session, which lets the AI
 736 |     /// provider cache it server-side (issue #89).
 737 |     ///
 738 |     /// <para>
 739 |     /// The <c>system.md</c> template's <c>{{WORLD_STATE}}</c> placeholder
 740 |     /// was removed in favor of a separate per-turn user message (see
 741 |     /// <see cref="ProcessActionBatchAsync"/>). The
 742 |     /// <c>{{STATIC_WORLD_LORE}}</c> placeholder is filled here with world
 743 |     /// title + theme + setting + atmosphere + starting hook (read from
 744 |     /// <see cref="World.World.Flags"/>). The
 745 |     /// <c>{{ITEM_TEMPLATES}}</c> / <c>{{NPC_TEMPLATES}}</c> /
 746 |     /// <c>{{BUILDING_TEMPLATES}}</c> placeholders are filled with the
 747 |     /// current registry contents (which only change on world rebuild).
 748 |     /// </para>
 749 |     /// </summary>
 750 |     private string BuildSystemPrompt()
 751 |     {
 752 |         var staticLore = BuildStaticWorldLoreBlock();
 753 |         var itemTpls = string.Join(", ", _world.Registries.Items.All().Select(t => t.Id).OrderBy(s => s).Take(30));
 754 |         var npcTpls = string.Join(", ", _world.Registries.Npcs.All().Select(t => t.Id).OrderBy(s => s).Take(30));
 755 |         var bldTpls = string.Join(", ", _world.Registries.Buildings.All().Select(t => t.Id).OrderBy(s => s).Take(30));
 756 | 
 757 |         var vars = new Dictionary<string, string>
 758 |         {
 759 |             ["STATIC_WORLD_LORE"] = staticLore,
 760 |             ["ITEM_TEMPLATES"] = itemTpls,
 761 |             ["NPC_TEMPLATES"] = npcTpls,
 762 |             ["BUILDING_TEMPLATES"] = bldTpls,
 763 |         };
 764 | 
 765 |         var system = _prompts.Render("system", vars);
 766 |         var narrator = _prompts.Get("narrator");
 767 |         var toolsGuide = _prompts.Exists("tools-guide") ? _prompts.Get("tools-guide") : string.Empty;
 768 |         return system + "\n\n---\n\n" + narrator + "\n\n---\n\n" + toolsGuide;
 769 |     }
 770 | 
 771 |     /// <summary>
 772 |     /// Render the static world-lore block from <see cref="World.World.Flags"/>.
 773 |     /// Pulls worldTitle / worldTheme / worldSetting / worldAtmosphere /
 774 |     /// startingHook — these are set by the world-builder and change only
 775 |     /// on world rebuild, so the rendered block is byte-identical across
 776 |     /// turns within a session (cached by the provider along with the rest
 777 |     /// of the system prompt).
 778 |     /// </summary>
 779 |     private string BuildStaticWorldLoreBlock()
 780 |     {
 781 |         var sb = new StringBuilder();
 782 |         var title = TryGetFlagString(_world.Flags, "worldTitle");
 783 |         var theme = TryGetFlagString(_world.Flags, "worldTheme");
 784 |         var setting = TryGetFlagString(_world.Flags, "worldSetting");
 785 |         var atmosphere = TryGetFlagString(_world.Flags, "worldAtmosphere");
 786 |         var hook = TryGetFlagString(_world.Flags, "startingHook");
 787 | 
 788 |         if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(title))
 789 |             sb.AppendLine($"- Название мира: {title}");
 790 |         if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(theme))
 791 |             sb.AppendLine($"- Тема: {theme}");
 792 |         if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(setting))
 793 |             sb.AppendLine($"- Сеттинг: {setting}");
 794 |         if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(atmosphere))
 795 |             sb.AppendLine($"- Атмосфера: {atmosphere}");
 796 |         if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(hook))
 797 |             sb.AppendLine($"- Стартовый крючок: {hook}");
 798 | 
 799 |         if (sb.Length == 0)
 800 |             sb.AppendLine("(лор мира ещё не задан — используй дефолтные предположения тёмного фэнтези).");
 801 | 
 802 |         return sb.ToString();
 803 |     }
 804 | 
 805 |     /// <summary>
 806 |     /// Verification hook for issue #89. When the environment variable
 807 |     /// <c>MYGAME_VERIFY_STATIC_PROMPT</c> is set to <c>"1"</c>, asserts
 808 |     /// that the system prompt is byte-identical to the previous turn's.
 809 |     /// If it differs, logs a warning (the prompt-cache is being defeated).
 810 |     /// No-op in production unless the env var is set.
 811 |     /// </summary>
 812 |     private void VerifyStaticSystemPrompt(string systemPrompt)
 813 |     {
 814 |         if (!s_verifyStaticPrompt) return;
 815 | 
 816 |         if (_lastSystemPrompt is not null && !string.Equals(_lastSystemPrompt, systemPrompt, StringComparison.Ordinal))
 817 |         {
 818 |             Trace.WriteLine("[GameMaster] WARNING: system prompt differs from the previous turn — " +
 819 |                             "provider-side prompt caching is defeated. " +
 820 |                             "Static prefix should not change between turns of the same world.");
 821 |         }
 822 |         _lastSystemPrompt = systemPrompt;
 823 |     }
 824 | 
 825 |     private string? _lastSystemPrompt;
 826 |     private static readonly bool s_verifyStaticPrompt =
 827 |         Environment.GetEnvironmentVariable("MYGAME_VERIFY_STATIC_PROMPT") == "1";
 828 | 
 829 |     /// <summary>
 830 |     /// Render a minimal world-state block. The full TS version
 831 |     /// (<c>buildWorldStateBlock</c> in <c>prompts/index.ts</c>) produced a
 832 |     /// rich block with attributes, resources, equipment, inventory, log
 833 |     /// tail, all-locations overview, world lore, etc. We ship a smaller
 834 |     /// version here that covers the essentials: player identity, current
 835 |     /// location, NPCs/buildings present, all-locations overview. A later
 836 |     /// task can port the full rendering.
 837 |     /// </summary>
 838 |     private string BuildWorldStateBlock()
 839 |     {
 840 |         var sb = new StringBuilder();
 841 |         var p = _world.ActivePlayer ?? _world.Players.FirstOrDefault();
 842 |         if (p is null)
 843 |         {
 844 |             sb.AppendLine("## Персонаж игрока\n(игрок ещё не создан)");
 845 |             return sb.ToString();
 846 |         }
 847 | 
 848 |         // World title (set by the world-builder via Flags["worldTitle"]).
 849 |         var worldTitle = TryGetFlagString(_world.Flags, "worldTitle");
 850 |         if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(worldTitle))
 851 |         {
 852 |             sb.AppendLine($"## Мир: {worldTitle}");
 853 |             sb.AppendLine();
 854 |         }
 855 | 
 856 |         var loc = _world.GetLocation(p.LocationId);
 857 | 
 858 |         sb.AppendLine("## Персонаж игрока");
 859 |         sb.AppendLine($"- Имя: {p.Name} | Раса: {p.Race ?? "—"} | Класс: {p.Class ?? "—"} | Уровень: {p.Level ?? 1}");
 860 |         if (p.Attributes.Count > 0)
 861 |         {
 862 |             // Issue #21 (custom ruleset): show custom attribute display
 863 |             // names from the ruleset's AttributeNames overlay (e.g.
 864 |             // "Cool" instead of "СИЛ" for a cyberpunk world) so the GM
 865 |             // knows what to call them. Falls back to the bare key when
 866 |             // no overlay is set (DefaultDnd behavior).
 867 |             var attrs = string.Join(", ", p.Attributes.OrderBy(kv => kv.Key).Select(kv =>
 868 |                 $"{Rulesets.GetAttributeName(_world.Ruleset, kv.Key)}={kv.Value}"));
 869 |             sb.AppendLine($"- Характеристики: {attrs}");
 870 |         }
 871 |         if (p.Resources.Count > 0)
 872 |         {
 873 |             // Same overlay treatment for resource pool names.
 874 |             var res = string.Join(", ", p.Resources.OrderBy(kv => kv.Key).Select(kv =>
 875 |                 $"{Rulesets.GetResourceName(_world.Ruleset, kv.Key)}={kv.Value}"));
 876 |             sb.AppendLine($"- Ресурсы: {res}");
 877 |         }
 878 |         if (p.Equipped.Count > 0)
 879 |             sb.AppendLine($"- Экипировка: {string.Join(", ", p.Equipped.Select(kv => $"{kv.Key}: {kv.Value.Name}"))}");
 880 |         if (p.Inventory.Items.Count > 0)
 881 |             sb.AppendLine($"- Инвентарь: {string.Join(", ", p.Inventory.Items.Select(i => $"{i.Name} ×{i.Quantity}"))}");
 882 |         sb.AppendLine($"- Валюта: {p.Inventory.Currency}");
 883 | 
 884 |         // Issue #21 — surface the custom skill list (if the ruleset
 885 |         // designer set one) so the GM knows what skills exist in this
 886 |         // world. Hidden when no overlay is set (DefaultDnd — the GM
 887 |         // already knows the D&D skill list).
 888 |         if (_world.Ruleset.Skills is { Count: > 0 } skills)
 889 |         {
 890 |             sb.AppendLine($"- Навыки мира: {string.Join(", ", skills)}");
 891 |         }
 892 | 
 893 |         // Active status effects on the player (name + duration).
 894 |         if (p.Effects is { Count: > 0 })
 895 |         {
 896 |             var effs = string.Join(", ", p.Effects.Select(e =>
 897 |             {
 898 |                 var dur = e.Duration < 0
 899 |                     ? "постоянно"
 900 |                     : $"{e.Duration} ход.";
 901 |                 return $"{e.Name} ({dur})";
 902 |             }));
 903 |             sb.AppendLine($"- Эффекты: {effs}");
 904 |         }
 905 | 
 906 |         // Active quests (up to 5): name + completed/total + next incomplete.
 907 |         var activeQuests = _world.Quests
 908 |             .Where(q => q.Status == QuestStatus.Active)
 909 |             .Take(5)
 910 |             .ToList();
 911 |         if (activeQuests.Count > 0)
 912 |         {
 913 |             sb.AppendLine();
 914 |             sb.AppendLine("## Активные квесты");
 915 |             foreach (var q in activeQuests)
 916 |             {
 917 |                 var done = q.Objectives.Count(o => o.Done);
 918 |                 var total = q.Objectives.Count;
 919 |                 var next = q.Objectives.FirstOrDefault(o => !o.Done);
 920 |                 var nextLabel = next is not null && !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(next.Description)
 921 |                     ? $" → далее: {next.Description}"
 922 |                     : "";
 923 |                 sb.AppendLine($"- {q.Name} [{done}/{total}]{nextLabel}");
 924 |             }
 925 |         }
 926 | 
 927 |         sb.AppendLine();
 928 |         sb.AppendLine("## Время в мире");
 929 |         // Issue #35: include the time-of-day label so the model knows
 930 |         // whether it's day or night (drives Perception disadvantage +
 931 |         // random-encounter rate). Rendered as
 932 |         // "День 3, 14:00 (день)" / "День 3, 22:00 (ночь)".
 933 |         var todLabel = GameTime.GetTimeOfDayLabel(_world.Clock.Hour);
 934 |         sb.AppendLine($"- {_world.Clock} ({todLabel})");
 935 | 
 936 |         // ENGINE-DEPTH (issue #35): at night, surface Perception
 937 |         // disadvantage + increased danger so the model applies them
 938 |         // consistently without the player having to remind it.
 939 |         if (GameTime.IsNight(_world.Clock.Hour))
 940 |         {
 941 |             sb.AppendLine("- Сейчас ночь: disadvantage на Perception (без тёмного зрения), некоторые NPC только ночью, повышенная опасность.");
 942 |         }
 943 | 
 944 |         // ENGINE-DEPTH (issue #34): weather context. Only emitted when
 945 |         // the weather subsystem is active (CurrentWeather is set). Tells
 946 |         // the model the current weather + its mechanical effects so the
 947 |         // model can apply them consistently. Effects per weather type:
 948 |         //  - rain/storm/snow: travel takes +30 min (call advance_time
 949 |         //    with the larger amount when narrating travel).
 950 |         //  - rain/fog: random-encounter chance is higher (the system
 951 |         //    already bumps Danger*10% → Danger*15% in that case).
 952 |         //  - fog: disadvantage on Perception.
 953 |         if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(_world.CurrentWeather))
 954 |         {
 955 |             sb.AppendLine();
 956 |             sb.AppendLine("## Погода");
 957 |             var weatherLabel = TryGetWeatherLabel(_world.CurrentWeather);
 958 |             var effects = DescribeWeatherEffects(_world.CurrentWeather);
 959 |             var forecast = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(_world.WeatherForecast)
 960 |                 ? ""
 961 |                 : $" {_world.WeatherForecast}.";
 962 |             sb.AppendLine($"- Погода: {weatherLabel}.{forecast} Влияние: {effects}.");
 963 |         }
 964 | 
 965 |         // ENGINE-DEPTH (issue #36): faction reputation summary. Tells the
 966 |         // model which factions are friendly/hostile toward the player so
 967 |         // NPC dialogue and encounter outcomes are consistent. Only
 968 |         // emitted when the world actually has factions.
 969 |         if (_world.Factions.Count > 0)
 970 |         {
 971 |             sb.AppendLine();
 972 |             sb.AppendLine("## Фракции");
 973 |             foreach (var f in _world.Factions)
 974 |             {
 975 |                 sb.AppendLine($"- {f.Name} [{f.Alignment}, реп. {f.Reputation}]");
 976 |             }
 977 |         }
 978 | 
 979 |         // ENGINE-DEPTH (issue #43): lore availability hint. Tells the
 980 |         // model which canonical lore topics exist so it can call get_lore
 981 |         // instead of hallucinating facts. Only emitted when the world has
 982 |         // a populated lore DB. The actual lore content is fetched on
 983 |         // demand via the tool (kept out of the per-turn context to keep
 984 |         // the prompt small).
 985 |         if (_world.Lore is { Entries.Count: > 0 } lore)
 986 |         {
 987 |             sb.AppendLine();
 988 |             sb.AppendLine("## Доступный лор");
 989 |             var topicsRu = lore.Topics.Select(t => t switch
 990 |             {
 991 |                 "deities" => "божества",
 992 |                 "history" => "история",
 993 |                 "magic" => "магия",
 994 |                 "cultures" => "культуры",
 995 |                 "economy" => "экономика",
 996 |                 "current events" => "текущие события",
 997 |                 var other => other,
 998 |             });
 999 |             sb.AppendLine($"- Доступный лор: {string.Join(", ", topicsRu)}. Используй get_lore для деталей.");
1000 |         }
1001 | 
1002 |         // COMBAT-DEATH: surface the structured combat state to the GM so
1003 |         // it knows whose turn it is and doesn't act out of order. When
1004 |         // combat is inactive (the default), this block is omitted — the
1005 |         // GM is freeform-narrating. When active, we list the round, the
1006 |         // current actor (whose turn it is), and the full initiative
1007 |         // order with the active combatant marked.
1008 |         if (_world.Combat is { Active: true } combat && combat.TurnOrder.Count > 0)
1009 |         {
1010 |             sb.AppendLine();
1011 |             sb.AppendLine("## БОЙ");
1012 |             sb.AppendLine($"- Раунд: {combat.Round}");
1013 |             var currentIdx = Math.Clamp(combat.CurrentActorIndex, 0, combat.TurnOrder.Count - 1);
1014 |             var currentName = combat.TurnOrder[currentIdx].Name;
1015 |             sb.AppendLine($"- Сейчас ход: {currentName}");
1016 |             // Initiative order with markers: "►" for the current actor,
1017 |             // "✓" for those who already acted this round, "·" otherwise.
1018 |             var order = string.Join(", ", combat.TurnOrder.Select((c, i) =>
1019 |             {
1020 |                 var marker = i == currentIdx ? "►" : c.HasActedThisRound ? "✓" : "·";
1021 |                 return $"{marker}{c.Name} ({c.Initiative})";
1022 |             }));
1023 |             sb.AppendLine($"- Инициатива: {order}");
1024 |             sb.AppendLine("- Соблюдай очерёдность. Действуй только за текущего бойца; для передачи хода вызывай next_turn.");
1025 |         }
1026 | 
1027 |         // COMBAT-DEATH: when the player is at 0 HP, surface the death-
1028 |         // save tally so the GM knows to call death_save (and eventually
1029 |         // narrate the death / stabilisation). The "deathSaves" flag is
1030 |         // "S,F" (0-3 each); we read it leniently.
1031 |         if (p.Resources.TryGetValue("hp", out var playerHp) && playerHp <= 0 && p.IsAlive)
1032 |         {
1033 |             var (suc, fail) = ReadDeathSaves(_world.Flags);
1034 |             sb.AppendLine();
1035 |             sb.AppendLine("## Спасброски от смерти");
1036 |             sb.AppendLine($"- Игрок на 0 HP! Успехи: {suc}/3, Провалы: {fail}/3.");
1037 |             sb.AppendLine("- Каждый ход вызывай death_save, пока игрок не стабилизируется (3 успеха) или не умрёт (3 провала).");
1038 |         }
1039 |         else if (!p.IsAlive)
1040 |         {
1041 |             sb.AppendLine();
1042 |             sb.AppendLine("## ВНИМАНИЕ");
1043 |             sb.AppendLine("- Игрок мёртв. Не вызывай инструменты, меняющие мир; просто наррируй финал и заверши сцену.");
1044 |         }
1045 | 
1046 |         sb.AppendLine();
1047 |         sb.AppendLine($"## Текущая локация: {loc?.Name ?? "—"}");
1048 |         if (loc is not null)
1049 |         {
1050 |             sb.AppendLine($"- Описание: {loc.Description ?? "—"}");
1051 |             sb.AppendLine($"- Местность: {loc.Terrain} | Опасность: {loc.Danger}/10");
1052 |             if (loc.Exits.Count > 0)
1053 |             {
1054 |                 var exits = string.Join(", ", loc.Exits.Select(e =>
1055 |                 {
1056 |                     var to = _world.GetLocation(e.To)?.Name ?? e.To.ToString();
1057 |                     return $"{e.Direction} → {to}";
1058 |                 }));
1059 |                 sb.AppendLine($"- Выходы: {exits}");
1060 |             }
1061 |             if (loc.Npcs.Count > 0)
1062 |             {
1063 |                 // Enriched: name + race/class + level + disposition.
1064 |                 var npcs = string.Join(", ", loc.Npcs
1065 |                     .Select(id => _world.GetNpc(id))
1066 |                     .Where(n => n is not null)
1067 |                     .Select(n =>
1068 |                     {
1069 |                         var npc = n!;
1070 |                         var disp = TryGetFlagString(npc.Flags, "disposition");
1071 |                         if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(disp))
1072 |                             disp = npc.Disposition ?? "neutral";
1073 |                         var rc = string.IsNullOrEmpty(npc.Race) && string.IsNullOrEmpty(npc.Class)
1074 |                             ? ""
1075 |                             : $" {npc.Race ?? "—"}/{npc.Class ?? "—"}";
1076 |                         var lvl = npc.Level is int l ? $" ур.{l}" : "";
1077 |                         return $"{npc.Name}{rc}{lvl} ({disp})";
1078 |                     }));
1079 |                 sb.AppendLine($"- Обитатели: {npcs}");
1080 |             }
1081 |             if (loc.Buildings.Count > 0)
1082 |             {
1083 |                 var blds = string.Join(", ", loc.Buildings
1084 |                     .Select(id => _world.GetBuilding(id))
1085 |                     .Where(b => b is not null)
1086 |                     .Select(b => b!.Name));
1087 |                 sb.AppendLine($"- Здания: {blds}");
1088 |             }
1089 |         }
1090 | 
1091 |         sb.AppendLine();
1092 |         sb.AppendLine("## Все локации мира");
1093 |         if (_world.Locations.Count == 0)
1094 |         {
1095 |             sb.AppendLine("- (локаций пока нет)");
1096 |         }
1097 |         else
1098 |         {
1099 |             foreach (var l in _world.Locations.Take(30))
1100 |             {
1101 |                 sb.AppendLine($"- {l.Name} (ID: {l.Id}, terrain: {l.Terrain}, danger: {l.Danger})");
1102 |             }
1103 |         }
1104 | 
1105 |         // Last player action — continuity hint pulled from the in-memory
1106 |         // conversation history (the World itself doesn't store a log).
1107 |         var lastUser = _history.LastOrDefault(m => m.Role == ChatRole.User);
1108 |         if (lastUser?.Content is string la && !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(la))
1109 |         {
1110 |             const int MaxLen = 240;
1111 |             var trimmed = la.Length > MaxLen ? la.Substring(0, MaxLen) + "…" : la;
1112 |             sb.AppendLine();
1113 |             sb.AppendLine("## Последнее действие игрока");
1114 |             sb.AppendLine($"- {trimmed}");
1115 |         }
1116 | 
1117 |         return sb.ToString();
1118 |     }
1119 | 
1120 |     /// <summary>
1121 |     /// Best-effort string extraction from a <see cref="Entity.Flags"/> /
1122 |     /// <see cref="World.Flags"/> entry. Values may be either plain strings
1123 |     /// (set in-memory) or <see cref="System.Text.Json.JsonElement"/> values
1124 |     /// (after a save round-trip). Returns null when missing or non-string.
1125 |     /// </summary>
1126 |     private static string? TryGetFlagString(System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary<string, object>? flags, string key)
1127 |     {
1128 |         if (flags is null) return null;
1129 |         if (!flags.TryGetValue(key, out var v) || v is null) return null;
1130 |         var s = v.ToString();
1131 |         return string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(s) ? null : s;
1132 |     }
1133 | 
1134 |     /// <summary>
1135 |     /// Read the <c>"deathSaves"</c> world flag (<c>"S,F"</c>) into a
1136 |     /// tuple of (successes, failures), each clamped to [0, 3]. Missing
1137 |     /// / malformed → (0, 0). Used by the system-prompt world-state block
1138 |     /// to surface the death-save tally to the GM.
1139 |     /// </summary>
1140 |     private static (int Successes, int Failures) ReadDeathSaves(
1141 |         System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary<string, object>? flags)
1142 |     {
1143 |         var s = TryGetFlagString(flags, "deathSaves");
1144 |         if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(s)) return (0, 0);
1145 |         var parts = s.Split(',', StringSplitOptions.TrimEntries);
1146 |         if (parts.Length != 2) return (0, 0);
1147 |         if (!int.TryParse(parts[0], out var suc)) suc = 0;
1148 |         if (!int.TryParse(parts[1], out var fail)) fail = 0;
1149 |         return (Math.Clamp(suc, 0, 3), Math.Clamp(fail, 0, 3));
1150 |     }
1151 | 
1152 |     /// <summary>
1153 |     /// Map a weather tag to its RU display label (issue #34). Mirrors
1154 |     /// <c>SetWeatherTool.WeatherLabels</c> so the GM context renders the
1155 |     /// same string the UI does. Returns the raw tag if unknown (defensive
1156 |     /// — shouldn't happen since set_weather validates the input).
1157 |     /// </summary>
1158 |     private static string TryGetWeatherLabel(string? weather) => weather switch
1159 |     {
1160 |         "clear" => "🌤 Ясно",
1161 |         "rain" => "🌧 Дождь",
1162 |         "storm" => "⛈ Гроза",
1163 |         "fog" => "🌫 Туман",
1164 |         "snow" => "❄ Снег",
1165 |         "overcast" => "☁ Облачно",
1166 |         _ => weather ?? "—",
1167 |     };
1168 | 
1169 |     /// <summary>
1170 |     /// Describe the mechanical effects of a weather tag (issue #34).
1171 |     /// Returns a short RU phrase the GM uses to apply the rules
1172 |     /// consistently. Effects per weather type:
1173 |     ///  <list type="bullet">
1174 |     ///  <item><c>rain</c> / <c>storm</c> / <c>snow</c>: travel +30 min,
1175 |     ///    increased encounter chance (rain only).</item>
1176 |     ///  <item><c>fog</c>: disadvantage Perception, increased encounter
1177 |     ///    chance.</item>
1178 |     ///  <item><c>overcast</c>: no mechanical effect (flavor only).</item>
1179 |     ///  <item><c>clear</c>: no mechanical effect.</item>
1180 |     ///  </list>
1181 |     /// </summary>
1182 |     private static string DescribeWeatherEffects(string? weather) => weather switch
1183 |     {
1184 |         "rain" => "путешествие +30 мин, повышенный шанс встреч",
1185 |         "storm" => "путешествие +30 мин, опасные условия",
1186 |         "snow" => "путешествие +30 мин, сложная местность",
1187 |         "fog" => "disadvantage на Perception, повышенный шанс встреч",
1188 |         "overcast" => "без механического эффекта",
1189 |         "clear" => "без механического эффекта",
1190 |         _ => "без механического эффекта",
1191 |     };
1192 | }

--- src/MyGame.Core/AI/Agents/LoopDetector.cs (C#, 133 lines, ~1,499 tokens) ---
   1 | namespace MyGame.Core.AI.Agents;
   2 | 
   3 | /// <summary>
   4 | /// A nudge injected into the working conversation history when the
   5 | /// <see cref="LoopDetector"/> flags the GM as repeating itself. Null when
   6 | /// no nudge is needed (the call was clean / within bounds).
   7 | /// </summary>
   8 | public sealed record LoopNudge(string Text);
   9 | 
  10 | /// <summary>
  11 | /// Per-turn tool-call loop detector. Records the signature
  12 | /// (<c>(toolName, argsHash)</c>) of each tool call the GM makes inside one
  13 | /// <see cref="GameMaster.ProcessActionAsync"/> turn and flags when the GM
  14 | /// is stuck in a repetition pattern.
  15 | ///
  16 | /// <para>
  17 | /// Two rules are checked on every <see cref="Record"/> call:
  18 | /// </para>
  19 | /// <list type="number">
  20 | ///   <item><b>Direct repeat</b> — the same <c>(toolName, argsHash)</c>
  21 | ///     appears 3+ times in the last 5 tool calls. The GM is hammering the
  22 | ///     same tool with the same arguments.</item>
  23 | ///   <item><b>Ping-pong</b> — the last 4 tool calls form an alternating
  24 | ///     A, B, A, B pattern (two distinct tools, each appearing twice in
  25 | ///     alternation).</item>
  26 | /// </list>
  27 | ///
  28 | /// <para>
  29 | /// When a rule fires, a <see cref="LoopNudge"/> is returned with a
  30 | /// Russian-language system message reminding the GM to vary its approach.
  31 | /// At most <see cref="MaxNudges"/> nudges are emitted per turn — after
  32 | /// that the detector gives up and lets <see cref="GameMaster._maxIterations"/>
  33 | /// cap the loop. (The spec: "If it doesn't [self-correct] after 2 nudges,
  34 | /// let the iteration cap handle it.")
  35 | /// </para>
  36 | ///
  37 | /// <para>
  38 | /// The args hash is a plain <see cref="string.GetHashCode()"/> of the raw
  39 | /// JSON args string — that's enough for in-memory equality detection
  40 | /// within one turn (we're not persisting or comparing across turns/processes).
  41 | /// </para>
  42 | ///
  43 | /// <para>
  44 | /// This helper is NOT thread-safe — one instance is owned by a single
  45 | /// <see cref="GameMaster"/> and reset per turn.
  46 | /// </para>
  47 | /// </summary>
  48 | public sealed class LoopDetector
  49 | {
  50 |     /// <summary>
  51 |     /// Maximum nudges emitted per turn before we stop trying (the iteration
  52 |     /// cap then handles termination). Per spec: "If it doesn't [self-correct]
  53 |     /// after 2 nudges, let the iteration cap handle it."
  54 |     /// </summary>
  55 |     public const int MaxNudges = 2;
  56 | 
  57 |     // Sliding window of (toolName, argsHash) signatures, oldest-first.
  58 |     private readonly List<(string ToolName, int ArgsHash)> _history = new();
  59 |     private int _nudgeCount;
  60 | 
  61 |     /// <summary>
  62 |     /// Reset the detector for a new turn. Called by
  63 |     /// <see cref="GameMaster.ProcessActionAsync"/> at the start of each
  64 |     /// invocation so detection is per-turn only.
  65 |     /// </summary>
  66 |     public void Reset()
  67 |     {
  68 |         _history.Clear();
  69 |         _nudgeCount = 0;
  70 |     }
  71 | 
  72 |     /// <summary>
  73 |     /// Record one tool call and return a <see cref="LoopNudge"/> if a
  74 |     /// repetition pattern was detected. Returns null when the call was
  75 |     /// clean (no pattern detected, or the per-turn nudge cap has been
  76 |     /// reached and we're letting the iteration cap handle termination).
  77 |     /// </summary>
  78 |     /// <param name="toolName">Tool name (e.g. <c>"move_player"</c>).</param>
  79 |     /// <param name="argsJson">Raw JSON-args string from the model. Used
  80 |     /// only for its hash (so structural differences like key ordering
  81 |     /// count as different calls — same as the underlying model output).</param>
  82 |     public LoopNudge? Record(string toolName, string? argsJson)
  83 |     {
  84 |         if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(toolName)) return null;
  85 | 
  86 |         var argsHash = argsJson?.GetHashCode(StringComparison.Ordinal) ?? 0;
  87 |         _history.Add((toolName, argsHash));
  88 | 
  89 |         // Nudge cap reached — let the iteration cap handle termination.
  90 |         if (_nudgeCount >= MaxNudges) return null;
  91 | 
  92 |         // Rule 1: direct repeat — same (toolName, argsHash) 3+ times in
  93 |         // the last 5 calls.
  94 |         if (_history.Count >= 3)
  95 |         {
  96 |             var window = _history.Skip(Math.Max(0, _history.Count - 5)).ToList();
  97 |             var counts = window
  98 |                 .GroupBy(x => x)
  99 |                 .Select(g => (Sig: g.Key, Count: g.Count()))
 100 |                 .ToList();
 101 |             var topRepeat = counts.MaxBy(c => c.Count);
 102 |             if (topRepeat.Count >= 3)
 103 |             {
 104 |                 _nudgeCount++;
 105 |                 return new LoopNudge(
 106 |                     $"[SYSTEM] Ты повторяешь одно и то же действие " +
 107 |                     $"({topRepeat.Sig.ToolName} с теми же аргументами). " +
 108 |                     $"Попробуй другой подход или заверши наррацию без инструментов.");
 109 |             }
 110 |         }
 111 | 
 112 |         // Rule 2: ping-pong — last 4 calls form A, B, A, B.
 113 |         if (_history.Count >= 4)
 114 |         {
 115 |             var n = _history.Count;
 116 |             var a = _history[n - 4];
 117 |             var b = _history[n - 3];
 118 |             var c = _history[n - 2];
 119 |             var d = _history[n - 1];
 120 |             if (a.Equals(c) && b.Equals(d) && !a.Equals(b))
 121 |             {
 122 |                 _nudgeCount++;
 123 |                 return new LoopNudge(
 124 |                     $"[SYSTEM] Ты чередуешь одни и те же два действия " +
 125 |                     $"({a.ToolName} и {b.ToolName}). " +
 126 |                     $"Попробуй другой подход или заверши наррацию без инструментов.");
 127 |             }
 128 |         }
 129 | 
 130 |         return null;
 131 |     }
 132 | }

   1 | using System.Text.Json;

  55 | 
  56 | /// <summary>
  57 | /// Pet agent — a delegated sub-agent that runs a focused task with its own
  58 | /// LLM conversation and tool-call loop. Port of
  59 | /// <c>ai/agents/petAgent.ts</c>.
  60 | ///
  61 | /// <para>
  62 | /// The pet agent is used by the world-builder orchestrator (or any other
  63 | /// supervisor) to delegate a well-scoped sub-task ("spawn 5 bandits in the
  64 | /// forest", "design a custom item template for the artifact") to a
