  65 | /// secondary LLM call. It has its own message history, its own iteration
  66 | /// cap, and reports back via a <c>pet_done</c> tool call (or a fallback
  67 | /// summary if it runs out of iterations).
  68 | /// </para>
  69 | ///
  70 | /// <para>
  71 | /// Unlike the TS source, the desktop MVP does NOT support nested pet
  72 | /// agents (a pet agent cannot delegate to another pet agent). The
  73 | /// <c>pet_done</c> tool is the only pet-specific tool; all other tools
  74 | /// come from the shared <see cref="ToolRegistry"/>.
  75 | /// </para>
  76 | /// </summary>
  77 | public sealed class PetAgent
  78 | {
  79 |     /// <summary>Default per-run iteration cap. The TS source used 8.</summary>
  80 |     public const int DefaultMaxIterations = 6;
  81 | 
  82 |     private readonly AiClient _mainAi;
  83 |     private readonly AiSettings? _aiSettings;
  84 |     private readonly MyGame.Core.World.World _world;
  85 |     private readonly ToolRegistry _tools;
  86 |     private readonly PetAgentConfig _config;
  87 |     private readonly int _maxIterations;
  88 | 
  89 |     // Pet-specific tool: signals task completion. Registered on a private
  90 |     // registry snapshot so it doesn't pollute the main tool list.
  91 |     private static readonly ToolDefinition PetDoneTool = new()
  92 |     {
  93 |         Name = "pet_done",
  94 |         Description = "Завершить задачу pet-агента. Аргумент: summary — краткое описание результата.",
  95 |         ParametersJson = """
  96 |         {
  97 |           "type": "object",
  98 |           "properties": {
  99 |             "summary": { "type": "string", "description": "Краткое описание выполненной работы." }
 100 |           },
 101 |           "required": ["summary"]
 102 |         }
 103 |         """,
 104 |     };
 105 | 
 106 |     /// <summary>Create a pet agent bound to the given AI client, world, tool registry, and config.</summary>
 107 |     /// <param name="mainAi">Base AI client. When <paramref name="aiSettings"/>
 108 |     /// is provided AND the pet's config doesn't carry its own
 109 |     /// <see cref="PetAgentConfig.Settings"/>, a role-specific client is
 110 |     /// derived via <see cref="AiClient.WithModel"/> for the
 111 |     /// <see cref="AiRole.Pet"/> model override (issue #26).</param>
 112 |     /// <param name="aiSettings">Optional AI settings for the PetModel
 113 |     /// override. When null, the base <paramref name="mainAi"/> client is
 114 |     /// used as-is (unless the config has its own Settings).</param>
 115 |     public PetAgent(
 116 |         AiClient mainAi,
 117 |         MyGame.Core.World.World world,
 118 |         ToolRegistry tools,
 119 |         PetAgentConfig config,
 120 |         int? maxIterations = null,
 121 |         AiSettings? aiSettings = null)
 122 |     {
 123 |         _mainAi = mainAi ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(mainAi));
 124 |         _world = world ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(world));
 125 |         _tools = tools ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(tools));
 126 |         _config = config ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(config));
 127 |         _maxIterations = Math.Max(1, Math.Min(20, maxIterations ?? DefaultMaxIterations));
 128 |         _aiSettings = aiSettings;
 129 |     }
 130 | 
 131 |     /// <summary>
 132 |     /// Run the pet agent on the given task. Returns when the agent calls
 133 |     /// <c>pet_done</c> or hits the iteration cap. Events are raised via
 134 |     /// <paramref name="onEvent"/> (if provided).
 135 |     /// </summary>
 136 |     public async Task<PetAgentResult> RunAsync(
 137 |         string task,
 138 |         Action<PetAgentEvent>? onEvent = null,
 139 |         CancellationToken ct = default)
 140 |     {
 141 |         if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(task))
 142 |             return new PetAgentResult
 143 |             {
 144 |                 Summary = "Пустая задача — pet-агент не запущен.",
 145 |                 Iterations = 0,
 146 |                 Success = false,
 147 |                 Error = "empty task",
 148 |             };
 149 | 
 150 |         // Pick the AI client to use:
 151 |         //   1. If the pet config has its own Settings (per-pet override),
 152 |         //      use a fresh client from those settings (highest priority).
 153 |         //   2. Else if the orchestrator passed shared settings (with a
 154 |         //      possible PetModel override), derive a role-specific client
 155 |         //      from the main AI client (issue #26).
 156 |         //   3. Else fall back to the main AI client as-is.
 157 |         AiClient ai;
 158 |         if (_config.Settings is not null)
 159 |             ai = new AiClient(_config.Settings);
 160 |         else if (_aiSettings is not null)
 161 |             ai = _mainAi.WithModel(_aiSettings.GetModelForRole(AiRole.Pet));
 162 |         else
 163 |             ai = _mainAi;
 164 | 
 165 |         var systemPrompt =
 166 |             "Ты — делегированный pet-агент внутри конвейера построения мира. Работай на русском. " +
 167 |             "Выполни конкретную задачу от главного агента. Можешь использовать обычные инструменты мира, " +
 168 |             "но НЕ можешь делегировать другим pet-агентам. " +
 169 |             "По завершении вызови pet_done с кратким summary. " +
 170 |             "НЕ вызывай finish_stage, end_worldbuilding, end_turn, commit_world_plan, set_world_meta — это инструменты главного агента.";
 171 | 
 172 |         var messages = new List<ChatMessage>
 173 |         {
 174 |             ChatMessage.System(systemPrompt),
 175 |             ChatMessage.User(task),
 176 |         };
 177 | 
 178 |         onEvent?.Invoke(new PetAgentEvent
 179 |         {
 180 |             AgentId = _config.Id,
 181 |             AgentName = _config.Name,
 182 |             Kind = PetEventKind.Start,
 183 |             Text = task,
 184 |         });
 185 | 
 186 |         // Build the tool list: main registry + pet_done.
 187 |         var toolDefs = _tools.Definitions.ToList();
 188 |         toolDefs.Add(PetDoneTool);
 189 | 
 190 |         string fullText = string.Empty;
 191 |         string summary = string.Empty;
 192 | 
 193 |         try
 194 |         {
 195 |             for (int iteration = 0; iteration < _maxIterations; iteration++)
 196 |             {
 197 |                 ct.ThrowIfCancellationRequested();
 198 | 
 199 |                 var response = await ai.ChatWithToolsAsync(messages, toolDefs, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 200 | 
 201 |                 if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(response.Content))
 202 |                 {
 203 |                     fullText += response.Content;
 204 |                     onEvent?.Invoke(new PetAgentEvent
 205 |                     {
 206 |                         AgentId = _config.Id,
 207 |                         AgentName = _config.Name,
 208 |                         Kind = PetEventKind.Text,
 209 |                         Text = response.Content,
 210 |                     });
 211 |                 }
 212 | 
 213 |                 if (response.ToolCalls is null || response.ToolCalls.Count == 0)
 214 |                 {
 215 |                     // Nudge: ask the agent to either continue or call pet_done.
 216 |                     messages.Add(ChatMessage.Assistant(response.Content));
 217 |                     messages.Add(ChatMessage.User(
 218 |                         "Если задача завершена, вызови pet_done с кратким summary. " +
 219 |                         "Иначе продолжай инструментами."));
 220 |                     continue;
 221 |                 }
 222 | 
 223 |                 // Append assistant message with tool_calls.
 224 |                 messages.Add(ChatMessage.AssistantWithTools(response.ToolCalls, response.Content));
 225 | 
 226 |                 // Execute each tool.
 227 |                 bool done = false;
 228 |                 foreach (var tc in response.ToolCalls)
 229 |                 {
 230 |                     var result = await _tools.ExecuteAsync(tc.Id, tc.Name, tc.Arguments ?? "{}", ct)
 231 |                         .ConfigureAwait(false);
 232 | 
 233 |                     // Special-case pet_done — extract the summary and short-circuit.
 234 |                     if (tc.Name == "pet_done")
 235 |                     {
 236 |                         summary = ExtractSummary(tc.Arguments) ?? result.Content;
 237 |                         onEvent?.Invoke(new PetAgentEvent
 238 |                         {
 239 |                             AgentId = _config.Id,
 240 |                             AgentName = _config.Name,
 241 |                             Kind = PetEventKind.Tool,
 242 |                             ToolName = tc.Name,
 243 |                             ArgsJson = tc.Arguments,
 244 |                             Result = result.Content,
 245 |                         });
 246 |                         onEvent?.Invoke(new PetAgentEvent
 247 |                         {
 248 |                             AgentId = _config.Id,
 249 |                             AgentName = _config.Name,
 250 |                             Kind = PetEventKind.Done,
 251 |                             Text = summary,
 252 |                         });
 253 |                         done = true;
 254 |                         break;
 255 |                     }
 256 | 
 257 |                     onEvent?.Invoke(new PetAgentEvent
 258 |                     {
 259 |                         AgentId = _config.Id,
 260 |                         AgentName = _config.Name,
 261 |                         Kind = PetEventKind.Tool,
 262 |                         ToolName = tc.Name,
 263 |                         ArgsJson = tc.Arguments,
 264 |                         Result = result.Content,
 265 |                     });
 266 | 
 267 |                     messages.Add(ChatMessage.ToolResult(tc.Id, result.Content));
 268 |                 }
 269 | 
 270 |                 if (done)
 271 |                 {
 272 |                     return new PetAgentResult
 273 |                     {
 274 |                         Summary = summary,
 275 |                         Iterations = iteration + 1,
 276 |                         Success = true,
 277 |                     };
 278 |                 }
 279 |             }
 280 | 
 281 |             // Hit the iteration cap — return a fallback summary.
 282 |             var fallback = !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(summary)
 283 |                 ? summary
 284 |                 : (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(fullText)
 285 |                     ? fullText.Trim()
 286 |                     : "Pet-агент остановился без summary.");
 287 |             onEvent?.Invoke(new PetAgentEvent
 288 |             {
 289 |                 AgentId = _config.Id,
 290 |                 AgentName = _config.Name,
 291 |                 Kind = PetEventKind.Done,
 292 |                 Text = fallback,
 293 |             });
 294 |             return new PetAgentResult
 295 |             {
 296 |                 Summary = fallback,
 297 |                 Iterations = _maxIterations,
 298 |                 Success = false,
 299 |                 Error = "iteration cap reached",
 300 |             };
 301 |         }
 302 |         catch (OperationCanceledException)
 303 |         {
 304 |             throw;
 305 |         }
 306 |         catch (AiException ex)
 307 |         {
 308 |             onEvent?.Invoke(new PetAgentEvent
 309 |             {
 310 |                 AgentId = _config.Id,
 311 |                 AgentName = _config.Name,
 312 |                 Kind = PetEventKind.Error,
 313 |                 Text = ex.Message,
 314 |             });
 315 |             return new PetAgentResult
 316 |             {
 317 |                 Summary = $"Pet-агент упал: {ex.Message}",
 318 |                 Iterations = 0,
 319 |                 Success = false,
 320 |                 Error = ex.Message,
 321 |             };
 322 |         }
 323 |         catch (Exception ex)
 324 |         {
 325 |             onEvent?.Invoke(new PetAgentEvent
 326 |             {
 327 |                 AgentId = _config.Id,
 328 |                 AgentName = _config.Name,
 329 |                 Kind = PetEventKind.Error,
 330 |                 Text = ex.Message,
 331 |             });
 332 |             return new PetAgentResult
 333 |             {
 334 |                 Summary = $"Pet-агент упал: {ex.Message}",
 335 |                 Iterations = 0,
 336 |                 Success = false,
 337 |                 Error = ex.Message,
 338 |             };
 339 |         }
 340 |     }
 341 | 
 342 |     /// <summary>
 343 |     /// Extract the <c>summary</c> field from the pet_done tool's arguments
 344 |     /// JSON. Defensive — returns null on any parse failure.
 345 |     /// </summary>
 346 |     private static string? ExtractSummary(string? argsJson)
 347 |     {
 348 |         if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(argsJson)) return null;
 349 |         try
 350 |         {
 351 |             using var doc = JsonDocument.Parse(argsJson);
 352 |             return doc.RootElement.TryGetProperty("summary", out var s) ? s.GetString() : null;
 353 |         }
 354 |         catch
 355 |         {
 356 |             return null;
 357 |         }
 358 |     }
 359 | }

--- src/MyGame.Core/AI/Agents/PetDelegation.cs (C#, 53 lines, ~581 tokens) ---
   1 | namespace MyGame.Core.AI.Agents;
   2 | 
   3 | /// <summary>
   4 | /// One delegated sub-task for the world-builder's optional pet-agent
   5 | /// phase. The orchestrator runs each delegation as a separate
   6 | /// <see cref="PetAgent"/> with its own LLM conversation + tool loop,
   7 | /// after the deterministic committer stage and before the narrator.
   8 | ///
   9 | /// <para>
  10 | /// Use cases: mass NPC spawning ("засели 10 разбойников в лесу"), batch
  11 | /// custom-item creation ("создай 5 артефактов с разными свойствами"),
  12 | /// flavour population ("расставь маркеры лора по 8 локациям"). The pet
  13 | /// agent has full tool access (spawn_npc, give_item, etc.) and reports
  14 | /// back via <c>pet_done</c>.
  15 | /// </para>
  16 | ///
  17 | /// <para>
  18 | /// Delegations are OPTIONAL — if none are provided, the orchestrator
  19 | /// skips the pet phase entirely (the default). This keeps the simple
  20 | /// 3-stage pipeline (planner → commit → narrate) as the default; pet
  21 | /// delegation is an opt-in for users who want richer worlds.
  22 | /// </para>
  23 | /// </summary>
  24 | public sealed record PetDelegation
  25 | {
  26 |     /// <summary>
  27 |     /// Display name shown in the progress UI ("Pet: заселение леса").
  28 |     /// </summary>
  29 |     public required string Label { get; init; }
  30 | 
  31 |     /// <summary>
  32 |     /// The task description handed to the pet agent. Should be concrete
  33 |     /// and self-contained — the pet agent doesn't see the orchestrator's
  34 |     /// other context. Example: "Засели 8 разбойников (templateId:
  35 |     /// npc_bandit) в локации «Северный Лес». Дай каждому имя и
  36 |     /// описание. Заверши вызовом pet_done."
  37 |     /// </summary>
  38 |     public required string Task { get; init; }
  39 | 
  40 |     /// <summary>
  41 |     /// Optional per-pet AI settings override. If null, the orchestrator's
  42 |     /// main <see cref="AiClient"/> settings are used.
  43 |     /// </summary>
  44 |     public AiSettings? Settings { get; init; }
  45 | 
  46 |     /// <summary>
  47 |     /// Optional iteration cap for this delegation. If null, the pet
  48 |     /// agent's default (<see cref="PetAgent.DefaultMaxIterations"/>) is
  49 |     /// used.
  50 |     /// </summary>
  51 |     public int? MaxIterations { get; init; }
  52 | }

--- src/MyGame.Core/AI/Agents/StartSceneAgent.cs (C#, 165 lines, ~1,788 tokens) ---
   1 | using System.Text;
   2 | using MyGame.Core.AI.Prompts;
   3 | using MyGame.Core.AI.Tools;
   4 | 
   5 | 
   6 | namespace MyGame.Core.AI.Agents;
   7 | 
   8 | /// <summary>
   9 | /// Result of <see cref="StartSceneAgent.RunAsync"/>.
  10 | /// </summary>
  11 | public sealed record StartSceneResult
  12 | {
  13 |     /// <summary>
  14 |     /// Atmospheric opening narration to show the player as the first entry
  15 |     /// in the story feed.
  16 |     /// </summary>
  17 |     public required string SceneDescription { get; init; }
  18 | 
  19 |     /// <summary>Number of LLM iterations the agent ran.</summary>
  20 |     public int Iterations { get; init; }
  21 | 
  22 |     /// <summary>True if the agent completed normally; false if it hit an error or the iteration cap.</summary>
  23 |     public bool Success { get; init; }
  24 | 
  25 |     /// <summary>Error message when <see cref="Success"/> is false.</summary>
  26 |     public string? Error { get; init; }
  27 | }
  28 | 
  29 | /// <summary>
  30 | /// Start-Scene Agent. Port of <c>ai/agents/startSceneAgent.ts</c>.
  31 | ///
  32 | /// In the TS source this agent used a tool-call loop to move the player to
  33 | /// a starting location, grant starter gear, and spawn nearby NPCs before
  34 | /// handing off to the GM. The C# port is SIMPLER — it builds a prompt
  35 | /// from world context, asks the model for an atmospheric opening-scene
  36 | /// description (no tools), and returns the text. The actual scene setup
  37 | /// (player placement, starter gear) is expected to be done by the caller
  38 | /// (e.g. the Desktop UI's "New Game" flow) using the World API directly,
  39 | /// which is more reliable than driving it through an LLM tool loop.
  40 | ///
  41 | /// Rationale: the TS source's tool-driven start-scene was a frequent
  42 | /// source of bugs (the model would forget to <c>move_player</c> with
  43 | /// <c>force:true</c>, or skip <c>equip_player</c> after <c>give_item</c>).
  44 | /// The desktop rewrite ships deterministic scene setup + AI-generated
  45 | /// narration instead.
  46 | /// </summary>
  47 | public sealed class StartSceneAgent
  48 | {
  49 |     /// <summary>Default per-run iteration cap. The TS source used 12.</summary>
  50 |     public const int DefaultMaxIterations = 4;
  51 | 
  52 |     private readonly AiClient _ai;
  53 |     private readonly MyGame.Core.World.World _world;
  54 |     private readonly PromptLoader _prompts;
  55 |     private readonly int _maxIterations;
  56 | 
  57 |     /// <summary>Create a start-scene agent bound to the given AI client, world, and prompt loader.</summary>
  58 |     public StartSceneAgent(AiClient ai, MyGame.Core.World.World world, PromptLoader prompts, int? maxIterations = null)
  59 |     {
  60 |         _ai = ai ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(ai));
  61 |         _world = world ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(world));
  62 |         _prompts = prompts ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(prompts));
  63 |         _maxIterations = Math.Max(1, Math.Min(20, maxIterations ?? DefaultMaxIterations));
  64 |     }
  65 | 
  66 |     /// <summary>
  67 |     /// Generate the opening scene description. Calls the AI once (no tool
  68 |     /// loop) with the world-start-scene prompt, world state, and a hint
  69 |     /// derived from the active player's identity. Returns the model's
  70 |     /// response as <see cref="StartSceneResult.SceneDescription"/>.
  71 |     /// </summary>
  72 |     public async Task<StartSceneResult> RunAsync(CancellationToken ct = default)
  73 |     {
  74 |         try
  75 |         {
  76 |             var systemPrompt = BuildSystemPrompt();
  77 |             var messages = new List<ChatMessage>
  78 |             {
  79 |                 ChatMessage.System(systemPrompt),
  80 |                 ChatMessage.User(
  81 |                     "Напиши 2–3 коротких абзаца вступительной наррации для стартовой сцены. " +
  82 |                     "Без звёздочек, без заголовков, без «Что будешь делать?». Просто художественный текст."),
  83 |             };
  84 | 
  85 |             var response = await _ai.ChatAsync(messages, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
  86 |             var text = response.Content?.Trim() ?? string.Empty;
  87 |             return new StartSceneResult
  88 |             {
  89 |                 SceneDescription = text,
  90 |                 Iterations = 1,
  91 |                 Success = !string.IsNullOrEmpty(text),
  92 |                 Error = string.IsNullOrEmpty(text) ? "AI вернул пустую наррацию." : null,
  93 |             };
  94 |         }
  95 |         catch (AiException ex)
  96 |         {
  97 |             return new StartSceneResult
  98 |             {
  99 |                 SceneDescription = string.Empty,
 100 |                 Iterations = 0,
 101 |                 Success = false,
 102 |                 Error = ex.Message,
 103 |             };
 104 |         }
 105 |     }
 106 | 
 107 |     private string BuildSystemPrompt()
 108 |     {
 109 |         var template = _prompts.Get("world-start-scene");
 110 |         var stateBlock = BuildWorldStateBlock();
 111 |         var vars = new Dictionary<string, string>
 112 |         {
 113 |             ["WORLD_STATE"] = stateBlock,
 114 |         };
 115 |         return PromptLoader.Substitute(template, vars);
 116 |     }
 117 | 
 118 |     private string BuildWorldStateBlock()
 119 |     {
 120 |         var sb = new StringBuilder();
 121 |         var p = _world.ActivePlayer ?? _world.Players.FirstOrDefault();
 122 |         if (p is not null)
 123 |         {
 124 |             sb.AppendLine("# ПЕРСОНАЖ ИГРОКА");
 125 |             sb.AppendLine($"Имя: {p.Name}");
 126 |             sb.AppendLine($"Раса: {p.Race ?? "—"}");
 127 |             sb.AppendLine($"Класс/роль: {p.Class ?? "—"}");
 128 |             sb.AppendLine($"Предыстория: {p.Background ?? "—"}");
 129 |             var loc = _world.GetLocation(p.LocationId);
 130 |             sb.AppendLine($"Текущая локация: {loc?.Name ?? "—"} ({p.LocationId})");
 131 |             sb.AppendLine();
 132 |         }
 133 | 
 134 |         sb.AppendLine("# ТЕКУЩЕЕ СОСТОЯНИЕ МИРА");
 135 |         sb.AppendLine("Локации (первые 30):");
 136 |         if (_world.Locations.Count == 0)
 137 |         {
 138 |             sb.AppendLine("  (пока нет)");
 139 |         }
 140 |         else
 141 |         {
 142 |             foreach (var l in _world.Locations.Take(30))
 143 |                 sb.AppendLine($"  • {l.Name} ({l.Id}, terrain={l.Terrain})");
 144 |         }
 145 | 
 146 |         sb.AppendLine();
 147 |         sb.AppendLine("NPC (первые 15):");
 148 |         if (_world.Npcs.Count == 0)
 149 |         {
 150 |             sb.AppendLine("  (пока нет)");
 151 |         }
 152 |         else
 153 |         {
 154 |             foreach (var n in _world.Npcs.Take(15))
 155 |                 sb.AppendLine($"  • {n.Name} ({n.Id}, loc={n.LocationId})");
 156 |         }
 157 | 
 158 |         sb.AppendLine();
 159 |         sb.AppendLine("# ЗАДАЧА");
 160 |         sb.AppendLine("Напиши атмосферное вступление для стартовой сцены. Без инструментов, без звёздочек. " +
 161 |                       "2–3 коротких абзаца на русском. Не задавай вопросов игроку.");
 162 |         return sb.ToString();
 163 |     }
 164 | }

--- src/MyGame.Core/AI/Agents/WorldBuilderOrchestrator.cs (C#, 2042 lines, ~26,217 tokens) ---
   1 | using System.Diagnostics;
   2 | using System.Text;
   3 | using System.Text.Json;
   4 | using System.Text.Json.Nodes;
   5 | using MyGame.Core.AI.Prompts;
   6 | using MyGame.Core.AI.Tools;
   7 | using MyGame.Core.Common;
   8 | using MyGame.Core.Saves;
   9 | using MyGame.Core.World;
  10 | 
  11 | // 'World' is both a namespace (MyGame.Core.World) and a type
  12 | // (MyGame.Core.World.World). Alias to disambiguate the type reference.
  13 | using GameWorld = MyGame.Core.World.World;
  14 | 
  15 | namespace MyGame.Core.AI.Agents;
  16 | 
  17 | /// <summary>
  18 | /// Result of <see cref="WorldBuilderOrchestrator.RunAsync"/>.
  19 | /// </summary>
  20 | public enum WorldBuilderResultKind
  21 | {
  22 |     /// <summary>The build completed; the world is playable.</summary>
  23 |     Complete,
  24 | 
  25 |     /// <summary>The build was cancelled mid-flight.</summary>
  26 |     Cancelled,
  27 | 
  28 |     /// <summary>The build failed (AI error, timeout, etc.).</summary>
  29 |     Failed,
  30 | }
  31 | 
  32 | /// <summary>
  33 | /// Snapshot of the orchestrator's progress through the world-build
  34 | /// pipeline, used by the pause/resume feature (issue #19).
  35 | ///
  36 | /// <para>
  37 | /// The orchestrator publishes a fresh <see cref="WorldBuilderState"/>
  38 | /// after each stage / sub-stage via <see cref="WorldBuilderOrchestrator.SaveState"/>,
  39 | /// and a caller can restore one via <see cref="WorldBuilderOrchestrator.LoadState"/>
  40 | /// so a resumed run continues from where the previous run left off
  41 | /// (rather than starting over from the planning stage).
  42 | /// </para>
  43 | ///
  44 | /// <para>
  45 | /// The fields capture:
  46 | /// <list type="bullet">
  47 | ///   <item><see cref="Stage"/> — a stable string identifier for the
  48 | ///     current point in the pipeline (<c>"planning"</c>,
  49 | ///     <c>"planning_done"</c>, <c>"ruleset"</c>, <c>"committer"</c>,
  50 | ///     <c>"committer_done"</c>, <c>"pets"</c>, <c>"pets_done"</c>,
  51 | ///     <c>"narration"</c>, <c>"done"</c>). On <see cref="WorldBuilderOrchestrator.LoadState"/>,
  52 | ///     the orchestrator skips any stage whose <c>*_done</c> marker
  53 | ///     precedes or matches <see cref="Stage"/>.</item>
  54 | ///   <item><see cref="Plan"/> — the committed <see cref="WorldPlan"/>
  55 | ///     from the planner stage. Null before planning has run; non-null
  56 | ///     afterwards so a resumed run can skip the planner AI call.</item>
  57 | ///   <item><see cref="Messages"/> — the orchestrator builds messages
  58 | ///     fresh each run (no per-stage conversation history accumulates
  59 | ///     between planner + narrator), so this list is kept for API
  60 | ///     symmetry with the spec but is typically empty. Pet-agent
  61 | ///     conversations live on their own <c>PetAgent</c> instances and
  62 | ///     are not serialised here.</item>
  63 | ///   <item><see cref="Iteration"/> — the pet-delegation index (0-based)
  64 | ///     of the next delegation to run. Resumed runs skip already-completed
  65 | ///     delegations 0..<see cref="Iteration"/>-1.</item>
  66 | /// </list>
  67 | /// </para>
  68 | /// </summary>
  69 | public sealed record WorldBuilderState
  70 | {
  71 |     /// <summary>
  72 |     /// Stable identifier for the orchestrator's current point in the
  73 |     /// pipeline. See the class-level doc for the enumeration of values.
  74 |     /// </summary>
  75 |     public required string Stage { get; init; } = "planning";
  76 | 
  77 |     /// <summary>
  78 |     /// The committed <see cref="WorldPlan"/> (null before planning has
  79 |     /// run). Restored via <see cref="WorldBuilderOrchestrator.LoadState"/>
  80 |     /// so a resumed run can skip the planner AI call.
  81 |     /// </summary>
  82 |     public WorldPlan? Plan { get; init; }
  83 | 
  84 |     /// <summary>
  85 |     /// Conversation history (kept for API symmetry with the spec; the
  86 |     /// orchestrator builds messages fresh each run, so this is typically
  87 |     /// empty). Pet-agent conversations are not serialised here.
  88 |     /// </summary>
  89 |     public List<ChatMessage> Messages { get; init; } = new();
  90 | 
  91 |     /// <summary>
  92 |     /// 0-based index of the next pet-delegation to run. Resumed runs
  93 |     /// skip already-completed delegations 0..<c>Iteration-1</c>.
  94 |     /// </summary>
  95 |     public int Iteration { get; init; }
  96 | }
  97 | 
  98 | /// <summary>
  99 | /// Result of <see cref="WorldBuilderOrchestrator.RunAsync"/>.
 100 | /// </summary>
 101 | public sealed record WorldBuilderResult
 102 | {
 103 |     public required WorldBuilderResultKind Kind { get; init; }
 104 | 
 105 |     /// <summary>Summary text (success message or error).</summary>
 106 |     public string? Summary { get; init; }
 107 | 
 108 |     /// <summary>Final plan committed by the planner, if any.</summary>
 109 |     public WorldPlan? Plan { get; init; }
 110 | 
 111 |     /// <summary>Opening narration produced by the narrator stage (if any).</summary>
 112 |     public string? OpeningNarration { get; init; }
 113 | 
 114 |     /// <summary>Stats from the deterministic committer stage (if reached).</summary>
 115 |     public CommitStats? Stats { get; init; }
 116 | 
 117 |     /// <summary>
 118 |     /// True when the run paused mid-flight and returned without
 119 |     /// completing the pipeline. Set by <see cref="WorldBuilderOrchestrator.Pause"/>
 120 |     /// + the orchestrator's pause-checkpoints; the host should treat
 121 |     /// this as "the build is paused, not failed" and offer a resume.
 122 |     /// </summary>
 123 |     public bool Paused { get; init; }
 124 | }
 125 | 
 126 | /// <summary>
 127 | /// World-Builder Orchestrator. Port of
 128 | /// <c>ai/agents/worldBuilderOrchestrator.ts</c>, rewritten for the desktop
 129 | /// MVP as a three-phase pipeline:
 130 | ///
 131 | /// <list type="number">
 132 | /// <item><b>Planner</b> — one AI call with the <c>world-planner.md</c>
 133 | ///   prompt produces a <see cref="WorldPlan"/> (JSON in the response
 134 | ///   content). This is the only creative call that designs the world.</item>
 135 | /// <item><b>Committer</b> — <see cref="WorldBuilderCommitter"/> mutates the
 136 | ///   live <see cref="MyGame.Core.World.World"/> deterministically from the
 137 | ///   plan: registers custom templates, creates locations + wires exits,
 138 | ///   spawns NPCs / buildings, grants starter gear, sets the world title.
 139 | ///   No AI calls — pure data transformation. Fault-tolerant: broken
 140 | ///   entries are skipped and counted in <see cref="CommitStats.Errors"/>.</item>
 141 | /// <item><b>Narrator</b> — one AI call with the <c>world-narrator.md</c>
 142 | ///   prompt produces the atmospheric opening narration (2 short
 143 | ///   paragraphs) shown to the player when they enter the game. The
 144 | ///   narration is also stored as the first <see cref="LogEntry"/> on the
 145 | ///   world log.</item>
 146 | /// </list>
 147 | ///
 148 | /// <para>
 149 | /// <b>Pause / resume (issue #19):</b> the orchestrator exposes
 150 | /// <see cref="Pause"/> / <see cref="Resume"/> and checks
 151 | /// <see cref="_paused"/> between every stage + sub-stage (the polling
 152 | /// loop yields the thread every 100ms so the UI stays responsive). The
 153 | /// host can snapshot progress via <see cref="SaveState"/> and restore it
 154 | /// via <see cref="LoadState"/> so a resumed run skips already-completed
 155 | /// stages (planning → committer → pets → narration).
 156 | /// </para>
 157 | /// </summary>
 158 | public sealed class WorldBuilderOrchestrator
 159 | {
 160 |     /// <summary>Default iteration cap for the planner call.</summary>
 161 |     public const int DefaultPlannerIterations = 4;
 162 | 
 163 |     private readonly AiClient _ai;
 164 |     private readonly AiSettings? _aiSettings;
 165 |     private readonly MyGame.Core.World.World _world;
 166 |     private readonly PromptLoader _prompts;
 167 |     private readonly ToolRegistry _tools;
 168 |     private readonly int _plannerIterations;
 169 |     private readonly List<PetDelegation> _petDelegations;
 170 | 
 171 |     // ─── Pause/resume state (issue #19) ──────────────────────────────
 172 |     //
 173 |     // _paused is volatile so writes from the UI thread (Pause() /
 174 |     // Resume()) are visible to the background task running RunAsync.
 175 |     // The pause-checkpoints in RunAsync poll this flag in a 100ms loop
 176 |     // (WaitIfPausedAsync) that also checks the CancellationToken so a
 177 |     // cancel during pause propagates immediately.
 178 |     //
 179 |     // _currentStage / _currentPlan / _currentIteration are mutated by
 180 |     // RunAsync as it crosses stage boundaries; SaveState() snapshots
 181 |     // them into a WorldBuilderState record. LoadState() restores them
 182 |     // AND sets _loadedStage so RunAsync knows which stages to skip on
 183 |     // the next run.
 184 |     private volatile bool _paused;
 185 |     private string _currentStage = "planning";
 186 |     private WorldPlan? _currentPlan;
 187 |     private int _currentIteration;
 188 |     private string? _loadedStage; // when non-null, skip stages ≤ this
 189 | 
 190 |     /// <summary>Create an orchestrator bound to the given AI client, world, prompt loader, and tool registry.</summary>
 191 |     /// <param name="ai">Base AI client. When <paramref name="aiSettings"/>
 192 |     /// is provided, role-specific clients are derived via
 193 |     /// <see cref="AiClient.WithModel"/> for the planner + narrator stages
 194 |     /// (issue #26).</param>
 195 |     /// <param name="aiSettings">Optional AI settings for per-role model
 196 |     /// overrides (<see cref="AiSettings.PlannerModel"/>,
 197 |     /// <see cref="AiSettings.NarratorModel"/>, <see cref="AiSettings.PetModel"/>).
 198 |     /// When null, the base <paramref name="ai"/> client is used as-is.</param>
 199 |     public WorldBuilderOrchestrator(
 200 |         AiClient ai,
 201 |         MyGame.Core.World.World world,
 202 |         PromptLoader prompts,
 203 |         ToolRegistry tools,
 204 |         int? plannerIterations = null,
 205 |         IReadOnlyCollection<PetDelegation>? petDelegations = null,
 206 |         AiSettings? aiSettings = null)
 207 |     {
 208 |         _ai = ai ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(ai));
 209 |         _world = world ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(world));
 210 |         _prompts = prompts ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(prompts));
 211 |         _tools = tools ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(tools));
 212 |         _plannerIterations = Math.Max(1, Math.Min(20, plannerIterations ?? DefaultPlannerIterations));
 213 |         _petDelegations = petDelegations?.ToList() ?? new();
 214 |         _aiSettings = aiSettings;
 215 |     }
 216 | 
 217 |     // ─── Pause / resume API (issue #19) ──────────────────────────────
 218 | 
 219 |     /// <summary>
 220 |     /// True while the orchestrator is paused (the background task is
 221 |     /// blocked in <see cref="WaitIfPausedAsync"/>, polling every 100ms
 222 |     /// for either a resume or a cancel). Volatile so the read in the
 223 |     /// polling loop observes writes from the UI thread.
 224 |     /// </summary>
 225 |     public bool Paused => _paused;
 226 | 
 227 |     /// <summary>
 228 |     /// Pause the orchestrator at the next pause-checkpoint. The
 229 |     /// currently-running AI call (if any) continues to completion —
 230 |     /// pause takes effect BETWEEN stages, not mid-API-call. Safe to
 231 |     /// call from the UI thread while <see cref="RunAsync"/> runs on a
 232 |     /// background task.
 233 |     /// </summary>
 234 |     public void Pause() => _paused = true;
 235 | 
 236 |     /// <summary>
 237 |     /// Resume the orchestrator from a paused state. The polling loop in
 238 |     /// <see cref="WaitIfPausedAsync"/> exits within ~100ms and the next
 239 |     /// stage begins. No-op if not paused.
 240 |     /// </summary>
 241 |     public void Resume() => _paused = false;
 242 | 
 243 |     /// <summary>
 244 |     /// Snapshot the orchestrator's current progress into a
 245 |     /// <see cref="WorldBuilderState"/>. Safe to call from any thread;
 246 |     /// captures the current <c>_currentStage</c> / <c>_currentPlan</c>
 247 |     /// / <c>_currentIteration</c>. The returned record is safe to JSON-
 248 |     /// serialise + reload in a later process via
 249 |     /// <see cref="LoadState"/>.
 250 |     /// </summary>
 251 |     public WorldBuilderState SaveState() => new()
 252 |     {
 253 |         Stage = _currentStage,
 254 |         Plan = _currentPlan,
 255 |         Messages = new List<ChatMessage>(),
 256 |         Iteration = _currentIteration,
 257 |     };
 258 | 
 259 |     /// <summary>
 260 |     /// Restore orchestrator progress from a previously-saved
 261 |     /// <see cref="WorldBuilderState"/>. The next <see cref="RunAsync"/>
 262 |     /// call skips any stage whose <c>*_done</c> marker precedes or
 263 |     /// matches <see cref="WorldBuilderState.Stage"/>; the planner AI
 264 |     /// call is skipped when <see cref="WorldBuilderState.Plan"/> is
 265 |     /// non-null; the first <see cref="WorldBuilderState.Iteration"/>
 266 |     /// pet-delegations are skipped.
 267 |     /// </summary>
 268 |     /// <param name="state">Saved state. Must not be null.</param>
 269 |     public void LoadState(WorldBuilderState state)
 270 |     {
 271 |         if (state is null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(state));
 272 |         _currentStage = state.Stage;
 273 |         _currentPlan = state.Plan;
 274 |         _currentIteration = state.Iteration;
 275 |         _loadedStage = state.Stage;
 276 |     }
 277 | 
 278 |     /// <summary>
 279 |     /// Block the calling background task while <see cref="_paused"/> is
 280 |     /// true. Polls every 100ms so a resume takes effect quickly. The
 281 |     /// cancellation token is checked on every iteration so a cancel
 282 |     /// during pause propagates immediately (rather than after the next
 283 |     /// stage starts).
 284 |     /// </summary>
 285 |     private async Task WaitIfPausedAsync(CancellationToken ct)
 286 |     {
 287 |         while (_paused)
 288 |         {
 289 |             ct.ThrowIfCancellationRequested();
 290 |             try
 291 |             {
 292 |                 await Task.Delay(100, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 293 |             }
 294 |             catch (OperationCanceledException) when (ct.IsCancellationRequested)
 295 |             {
 296 |                 throw;
 297 |             }
 298 |             // Task.Delay can throw OperationCanceledException when the
 299 |             // token fires during the delay — propagate. Any other
 300 |             // exception is unexpected; let it bubble.
 301 |         }
 302 |     }
 303 | 
 304 |     /// <summary>
 305 |     /// Determine whether a stage has already been completed (according
 306 |     /// to the loaded-state marker) and should be skipped on a resumed
 307 |     /// run. The ordering is:
 308 |     /// <code>
 309 |     /// planning &lt; planning_done &lt; ruleset &lt; committer &lt;
 310 |     /// committer_done &lt; pets &lt; pets_done &lt; narration &lt; done
 311 |     /// </code>
 312 |     /// A null loaded-stage means "fresh run" → nothing is skipped.
 313 |     /// </summary>
 314 |     private bool IsStageAlreadyDone(string stageMarker, string loadedStage)
 315 |     {
 316 |         if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(loadedStage)) return false;
 317 |         return StageOrder(stageMarker) <= StageOrder(loadedStage);
 318 |     }
 319 | 
 320 |     /// <summary>
 321 |     /// Map a stage name to its position in the pipeline order. Unknown
 322 |     /// stages get int.MaxValue so they're never considered "already
 323 |     /// done" relative to a known marker (defensive — keeps a corrupt
 324 |     /// state file from skipping the entire pipeline).
 325 |     /// </summary>
 326 |     private static int StageOrder(string? stage) => stage switch
 327 |     {
 328 |         "planning"        => 1,
 329 |         "planning_done"   => 2,
 330 |         "ruleset"         => 3,
 331 |         "committer"       => 4,
 332 |         "committer_done"  => 5,
 333 |         "pets"            => 6,
 334 |         "pets_done"       => 7,
 335 |         "narration"       => 8,
 336 |         "done"            => 9,
 337 |         _                 => int.MaxValue,
 338 |     };
 339 | 
 340 |     /// <summary>
 341 |     /// Run the world-build pipeline. Reports progress via
 342 |     /// <paramref name="progress"/>; respects <paramref name="ct"/> for
 343 |     /// cancellation. Returns a <see cref="WorldBuilderResult"/> with the
 344 |     /// final plan (if planning succeeded) and a summary.
 345 |     ///
 346 |     /// <para>
 347 |     /// <b>Pause / resume (issue #19):</b> between every stage + sub-stage
 348 |     /// the orchestrator calls <see cref="WaitIfPausedAsync"/> which blocks
 349 |     /// while <see cref="Paused"/> is true. A loaded state (set via
 350 |     /// <see cref="LoadState"/>) causes already-completed stages to be
 351 |     /// skipped so a resumed run continues from where the previous run
 352 |     /// left off. The pet-delegation loop also skips the first
 353 |     /// <see cref="WorldBuilderState.Iteration"/> delegations.
 354 |     /// </para>
 355 |     /// </summary>
 356 |     public async Task<WorldBuilderResult> RunAsync(
 357 |         WorldPlanRequest request,
 358 |         IProgress<WorldBuildProgress>? progress = null,
 359 |         CancellationToken ct = default)
 360 |     {
 361 |         if (request is null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(request));
 362 | 
 363 |         // The loaded stage marker (null for a fresh run). Used by
 364 |         // IsStageAlreadyDone to skip stages that were already completed
 365 |         // in a previous run.
 366 |         var loadedStage = _loadedStage;
 367 | 
 368 |         // ── Stage 1: planning ─────────────────────────────────────────────
 369 |         WorldPlan? plan = _currentPlan;
 370 |         if (!IsStageAlreadyDone("planning_done", loadedStage ?? ""))
 371 |         {
 372 |             _currentStage = "planning";
 373 |             progress?.Report(new WorldBuildProgress
 374 |             {
 375 |                 Stage = "planning",
 376 |                 Status = ProgressStatus.Active,
 377 |                 Label = "Планировщик анализирует бриф",
 378 |                 Percent = 5,
 379 |             });
 380 | 
 381 |             try
 382 |             {
 383 |                 plan = await RunPlannerAsync(request, progress, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 384 |                 _currentPlan = plan;
 385 |                 _currentStage = "planning_done";
 386 |                 progress?.Report(new WorldBuildProgress
 387 |                 {
 388 |                     Stage = "planning",
 389 |                     Status = ProgressStatus.Done,
 390 |                     Label = "План мира зафиксирован",
 391 |                     Detail = $"«{plan.Title}» — {plan.Locations.Count} локаций, {plan.Npcs.Count} NPC",
 392 |                     Percent = 15,
 393 |                 });
 394 |             }
 395 |             catch (OperationCanceledException)
 396 |             {
 397 |                 progress?.Report(new WorldBuildProgress
 398 |                 {
 399 |                     Stage = "planning",
 400 |                     Status = ProgressStatus.Error,
 401 |                     Label = "Отменено",
 402 |                     Percent = 0,
 403 |                 });
 404 |                 return new WorldBuilderResult
 405 |                 {
 406 |                     Kind = WorldBuilderResultKind.Cancelled,
 407 |                     Summary = "Build cancelled during planning.",
 408 |                 };
 409 |             }
 410 |             catch (AiException ex)
 411 |             {
 412 |                 progress?.Report(new WorldBuildProgress
 413 |                 {
 414 |                     Stage = "planning",
 415 |                     Status = ProgressStatus.Error,
 416 |                     Label = "Ошибка планирования",
 417 |                     Detail = ex.Message,
 418 |                     Percent = 0,
 419 |                 });
 420 |                 return new WorldBuilderResult
 421 |                 {
 422 |                     Kind = WorldBuilderResultKind.Failed,
 423 |                     Summary = "Planning failed: " + ex.Message,
 424 |                 };
 425 |             }
 426 |         }
 427 |         else
 428 |         {
 429 |             // Resumed run: planning was already done — reuse the saved
 430 |             // plan + publish a Done tick so the UI shows the right state.
 431 |             plan = _currentPlan ?? throw new InvalidOperationException(
 432 |                 "Loaded state marked planning_done but no plan was restored.");
 433 |             progress?.Report(new WorldBuildProgress
 434 |             {
 435 |                 Stage = "planning",
 436 |                 Status = ProgressStatus.Done,
 437 |                 Label = "План мира восстановлен",
 438 |                 Detail = $"«{plan.Title}» — {plan.Locations.Count} локаций, {plan.Npcs.Count} NPC",
 439 |                 Percent = 15,
 440 |             });
 441 |         }
 442 | 
 443 |         // Pause-checkpoint after planning.
 444 |         await WaitIfPausedAsync(ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 445 | 
 446 |         // ── Stage 2: ruleset ──────────────────────────────────────────────
 447 |         // Issue #21: design a custom ruleset overlay (attribute display
 448 |         // names + resource pool names + skill list) for non-fantasy
 449 |         // themes. For standard fantasy (the default) we keep DefaultDnd.
 450 |         // The AI call is opt-in: it only fires when the plan's theme
 451 |         // contains one of the non-fantasy genre keywords. Failures here
 452 |         // are non-fatal — the world is still playable with the default
 453 |         // ruleset; we just log the error.
 454 |         _currentStage = "ruleset";
 455 |         progress?.Report(new WorldBuildProgress
 456 |         {
 457 |             Stage = "ruleset",
 458 |             Status = ProgressStatus.Active,
 459 |             Label = "Применение правил мира",
 460 |             Percent = 22,
 461 |         });
 462 |         await Task.Yield();
 463 |         ct.ThrowIfCancellationRequested();
 464 |         if (IsNonFantasyTheme(plan.Theme))
 465 |         {
 466 |             try
 467 |             {
 468 |                 var overlay = await RunRulesetDesignerAsync(plan, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 469 |                 if (overlay is not null)
 470 |                 {
 471 |                     _world.Ruleset = _world.Ruleset with
 472 |                     {
 473 |                         AttributeNames = overlay.AttributeNames,
 474 |                         ResourcePools = overlay.ResourcePools,
 475 |                         Skills = overlay.Skills,
 476 |                     };
 477 |                     progress?.Report(new WorldBuildProgress
 478 |                     {
 479 |                         Stage = "ruleset",
 480 |                         Status = ProgressStatus.Done,
 481 |                         Label = "Правила мира: кастомная система",
 482 |                         Detail = DescribeRulesetOverlay(overlay),
 483 |                         Percent = 25,
 484 |                     });
 485 |                 }
 486 |                 else
 487 |                 {
 488 |                     progress?.Report(new WorldBuildProgress
 489 |                     {
 490 |                         Stage = "ruleset",
 491 |                         Status = ProgressStatus.Done,
 492 |                         Label = "Правила установлены (DnD 5e по умолчанию)",
 493 |                         Detail = "AI вернул пустой ruleset — использованы стандартные правила.",
 494 |                         Percent = 25,
 495 |                     });
 496 |                 }
 497 |             }
 498 |             catch (OperationCanceledException) { throw; }
 499 |             catch (AiException ex)
 500 |             {
 501 |                 // Non-fatal: fall back to DefaultDnd.
 502 |                 progress?.Report(new WorldBuildProgress
 503 |                 {
 504 |                     Stage = "ruleset",
 505 |                     Status = ProgressStatus.Error,
 506 |                     Label = "Не удалось спроектировать ruleset — использованы стандартные правила",
 507 |                     Detail = ex.Message,
 508 |                     Percent = 25,
 509 |                 });
 510 |             }
 511 |         }
 512 |         else
 513 |         {
 514 |             progress?.Report(new WorldBuildProgress
 515 |             {
 516 |                 Stage = "ruleset",
 517 |                 Status = ProgressStatus.Done,
 518 |                 Label = "Правила установлены (DnD 5e по умолчанию)",
 519 |                 Percent = 25,
 520 |             });
 521 |         }
 522 | 
 523 |         // Pause-checkpoint after ruleset.
 524 |         await WaitIfPausedAsync(ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 525 | 
 526 |         // ── Stage 3-6: deterministic commit ───────────────────────────────
 527 |         // One committer instance runs all four sub-stages (templates,
 528 |         // locations, population, buildings, content, title) in order. We
 529 |         // publish a progress tick before each sub-stage so the UI shows
 530 |         // the build progressing even though the work itself is
 531 |         // synchronous and fast (no AI calls).
 532 |         //
 533 |         // PAUSE/RESUME: the committer mutates the live World in place
 534 |         // and is idempotent (re-commit matches by name, no duplicates).
 535 |         // On a resumed run we always re-run the committer against the
 536 |         // (possibly fresh) World passed in by the host — this is cheap
 537 |         // (no AI calls) and ensures the live World reflects the plan
 538 |         // even if the host spun up a new World instance for the resume.
 539 |         _currentStage = "committer";
 540 |         var committer = new WorldBuilderCommitter(_world);
 541 |         var stats = new CommitStats();
 542 | 
 543 |         // 3a: custom templates (registered so later stages can reference
 544 |         // them by id).
 545 |         progress?.Report(new WorldBuildProgress
 546 |         {
 547 |             Stage = "templates",
 548 |             Status = ProgressStatus.Active,
 549 |             Label = "Регистрация кастомных шаблонов",
 550 |             Percent = 30,
 551 |         });
 552 |         committer.CommitCustomTemplates(plan, stats);
 553 |         progress?.Report(new WorldBuildProgress
 554 |         {
 555 |             Stage = "templates",
 556 |             Status = ProgressStatus.Done,
 557 |             Label = $"Шаблоны: {stats.CustomItems} предм. / {stats.CustomNpcs} NPC / {stats.CustomBuildings} зд.",
 558 |             Percent = 33,
 559 |         });
 560 | 
 561 |         // 3b: locations.
 562 |         progress?.Report(new WorldBuildProgress
 563 |         {
 564 |             Stage = "locations",
 565 |             Status = ProgressStatus.Active,
 566 |             Label = "Каркас локаций",
 567 |             Percent = 38,
 568 |         });
 569 |         committer.CommitLocations(plan, stats);
 570 |         progress?.Report(new WorldBuildProgress
 571 |         {
 572 |             Stage = "locations",
 573 |             Status = ProgressStatus.Done,
 574 |             Label = $"Создано локаций: {stats.Locations}",
 575 |             Percent = 45,
 576 |         });
 577 | 
 578 |         // 4: population.
 579 |         progress?.Report(new WorldBuildProgress
 580 |         {
 581 |             Stage = "population",
 582 |             Status = ProgressStatus.Active,
 583 |             Label = "Заселение мира",
 584 |             Percent = 50,
 585 |         });
 586 |         committer.CommitPopulation(plan, stats);
 587 |         progress?.Report(new WorldBuildProgress
 588 |         {
 589 |             Stage = "population",
 590 |             Status = ProgressStatus.Done,
 591 |             Label = $"Заселено NPC: {stats.Npcs}",
 592 |             Percent = 58,
 593 |         });
 594 | 
 595 |         // 5: buildings.
 596 |         progress?.Report(new WorldBuildProgress
 597 |         {
 598 |             Stage = "buildings",
 599 |             Status = ProgressStatus.Active,
 600 |             Label = "Возведение зданий",
 601 |             Percent = 62,
 602 |         });
 603 |         committer.CommitBuildings(plan, stats);
 604 |         progress?.Report(new WorldBuildProgress
 605 |         {
 606 |             Stage = "buildings",
 607 |             Status = ProgressStatus.Done,
 608 |             Label = $"Построено зданий: {stats.Buildings}",
 609 |             Percent = 70,
 610 |         });
 611 | 
 612 |         // 6: content (starter gear + player).
 613 |         progress?.Report(new WorldBuildProgress
 614 |         {
 615 |             Stage = "content",
 616 |             Status = ProgressStatus.Active,
 617 |             Label = "Наполнение предметами",
 618 |             Percent = 73,
 619 |         });
 620 |         committer.CommitContent(plan, stats);
 621 |         committer.CommitTitle(plan);
 622 |         progress?.Report(new WorldBuildProgress
 623 |         {
 624 |             Stage = "content",
 625 |             Status = ProgressStatus.Done,
 626 |             Label = $"Стартовых предметов: {stats.StarterItems}",
 627 |             Percent = 80,
 628 |         });
 629 | 
 630 |         _currentStage = "committer_done";
 631 | 
 632 |         // Pause-checkpoint after the committer (before pets).
 633 |         await WaitIfPausedAsync(ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 634 | 
 635 |         // ── Stage 6b: pet-agent delegations (optional) ──────────────────
 636 |         // If the caller provided any PetDelegations, run each as a
 637 |         // separate PetAgent with its own LLM conversation + tool loop.
 638 |         // The pet agent can spawn NPCs, create items, set flags — full
 639 |         // tool access. Failures are non-fatal (the world is already
 640 |         // playable from the committer stage); we log the error and move
 641 |         // on to the next delegation.
 642 |         //
 643 |         // PAUSE/RESUME: _currentIteration holds the index of the next
 644 |         // delegation to run (0 for a fresh run; restored to a non-zero
 645 |         // value by LoadState on a resume). Delegations 0..Iteration-1
 646 |         // are skipped.
 647 |         if (_petDelegations.Count > 0)
 648 |         {
 649 |             _currentStage = "pets";
 650 |             var petSummaries = new List<string>();
 651 |             int petTotal = _petDelegations.Count;
 652 |             // Skip already-completed delegations on a resumed run.
 653 |             int petIndex = _currentIteration;
 654 |             for (; petIndex < _petDelegations.Count; petIndex++)
 655 |             {
 656 |                 var del = _petDelegations[petIndex];
 657 |                 // Pause-checkpoint BEFORE each pet delegation. The
 658 |                 // current iteration marker is updated before the call so
 659 |                 // SaveState() during pause picks the right "next to run"
 660 |                 // index.
 661 |                 _currentIteration = petIndex;
 662 |                 await WaitIfPausedAsync(ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 663 | 
 664 |                 int petPercent = 80 + (int)((8.0 * (petIndex + 1)) / petTotal); // 80→88
 665 |                 progress?.Report(new WorldBuildProgress
 666 |                 {
 667 |                     Stage = "pet",
 668 |                     Status = ProgressStatus.Active,
 669 |                     Label = $"Pet-агент: {del.Label}",
 670 |                     Detail = $"Делегация {petIndex + 1}/{petTotal}",
 671 |                     Percent = petPercent,
 672 |                 });
 673 | 
 674 |                 try
 675 |                 {
 676 |                     var petConfig = new PetAgentConfig
 677 |                     {
 678 |                         Id = $"pet_{petIndex + 1}",
 679 |                         Name = del.Label,
 680 |                         Settings = del.Settings,
 681 |                     };
 682 |                     // Pass the orchestrator's AI settings so the PetAgent
 683 |                     // can derive a PetModel-override client (issue #26).
 684 |                     // When _aiSettings is null OR PetModel is unset, the
 685 |                     // pet agent falls back to the base _ai client.
 686 |                     var pet = new PetAgent(_ai, _world, _tools, petConfig, del.MaxIterations, _aiSettings);
 687 |                     var result = await pet.RunAsync(del.Task, ct: ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 688 |                     petSummaries.Add($"{del.Label}: {result.Summary}");
 689 |                     progress?.Report(new WorldBuildProgress
 690 |                     {
 691 |                         Stage = "pet",
 692 |                         Status = ProgressStatus.Done,
 693 |                         Label = $"Pet: {del.Label} — готово",
 694 |                         Detail = result.Success ? result.Summary : $"частично ({result.Error})",
 695 |                         Percent = petPercent,
 696 |                     });
 697 |                 }
 698 |                 catch (OperationCanceledException) { throw; }
 699 |                 catch (Exception ex)
 700 |                 {
 701 |                     stats.Errors.Add($"pet «{del.Label}»: {ex.Message}");
 702 |                     progress?.Report(new WorldBuildProgress
 703 |                     {
 704 |                         Stage = "pet",
 705 |                         Status = ProgressStatus.Error,
 706 |                         Label = $"Pet: {del.Label} — ошибка",
 707 |                         Detail = ex.Message,
 708 |                         Percent = petPercent,
 709 |                     });
 710 |                 }
 711 |             }
 712 | 
 713 |             // All delegations completed (or skipped on resume) — advance
 714 |             // the iteration counter past the end so a subsequent
 715 |             // SaveState doesn't try to re-run them.
 716 |             _currentIteration = petIndex;
 717 |             _currentStage = "pets_done";
 718 | 
 719 |             // Stash the pet summaries on the result so the UI can show
 720 |             // what each delegation accomplished.
 721 |             if (petSummaries.Count > 0)
 722 |             {
 723 |                 stats.Errors.Insert(0, "Pet-делегации:\n" + string.Join("\n", petSummaries));
 724 |             }
 725 |         }
 726 | 
 727 |         // Pause-checkpoint after pets (before narration).
 728 |         await WaitIfPausedAsync(ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 729 | 
 730 |         // ── Stage 7: narration ─────────────────────────────────────────────
 731 |         // One AI call with the world-narrator prompt. Produces the
 732 |         // atmospheric opening narration shown to the player when they
 733 |         // enter the game. Failures here are non-fatal — the world is
 734 |         // already playable; we just fall back to a plain hook line.
 735 |         _currentStage = "narration";
 736 |         string? narration = null;
 737 |         try
 738 |         {
 739 |             narration = await RunNarratorAsync(plan, progress, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 740 |             progress?.Report(new WorldBuildProgress
 741 |             {
 742 |                 Stage = "narration",
 743 |                 Status = ProgressStatus.Done,
 744 |                 Label = "Наррация готова",
 745 |                 Percent = 97,
 746 |             });
 747 |         }
 748 |         catch (OperationCanceledException)
 749 |         {
 750 |             progress?.Report(new WorldBuildProgress
 751 |             {
 752 |                 Stage = "narration",
 753 |                 Status = ProgressStatus.Error,
 754 |                 Label = "Отменено",
 755 |                 Percent = 80,
 756 |             });
 757 |             return new WorldBuilderResult
 758 |             {
 759 |                 Kind = WorldBuilderResultKind.Cancelled,
 760 |                 Summary = "Build cancelled during narration.",
 761 |                 Plan = plan,
 762 |                 Stats = stats,
 763 |             };
 764 |         }
 765 |         catch (AiException ex)
 766 |         {
 767 |             // Non-fatal: the world is playable; just log the error.
 768 |             progress?.Report(new WorldBuildProgress
 769 |             {
 770 |                 Stage = "narration",
 771 |                 Status = ProgressStatus.Error,
 772 |                 Label = "Наррация не удалась (мир играбелен)",
 773 |                 Detail = ex.Message,
 774 |                 Percent = 95,
 775 |             });
 776 |         }
 777 | 
 778 |         _currentStage = "done";
 779 |         progress?.Report(new WorldBuildProgress
 780 |         {
 781 |             Stage = "done",
 782 |             Status = ProgressStatus.Done,
 783 |             Label = $"Мир «{plan.Title}» готов",
 784 |             Detail = stats.Summary() + (stats.Errors.Count > 0
 785 |                 ? $" | Первая ошибка: {stats.Errors[0]}"
 786 |                 : ""),
 787 |             Percent = 100,
 788 |         });
 789 | 
 790 |         // Clear the loaded-stage marker so a subsequent RunAsync (without
 791 |         // a fresh LoadState) starts over from planning.
 792 |         _loadedStage = null;
 793 | 
 794 |         return new WorldBuilderResult
 795 |         {
 796 |             Kind = WorldBuilderResultKind.Complete,
 797 |             Summary = $"Мир «{plan.Title}» построен. {stats.Summary()}",
 798 |             Plan = plan,
 799 |             OpeningNarration = narration,
 800 |             Stats = stats,
 801 |         };
 802 |     }
 803 | 
 804 |     // ─── Chunked generation: on-demand region fill (issue #20) ──────────
 805 | 
 806 |     /// <summary>
 807 |     /// Generate a cold region on-demand. Used by the travel handler when
 808 |     /// the player crosses into a region that wasn't detailed by the
 809 |     /// initial planner pass (chunked mode). Reuses the original
 810 |     /// <see cref="WorldPlan"/> (stashed on the save's
 811 |     /// <see cref="SaveMeta.OriginalPlanJson"/>) so the new region matches
 812 |     /// the world's established lore; asks the AI to detail the region's
 813 |     /// locations, NPCs, and buildings; commits via
 814 |     /// <see cref="WorldBuilderCommitter"/> (idempotent — won't duplicate
 815 |     /// existing locations); marks the region GenStatus="ready" in
 816 |     /// <see cref="World.Flags"/>.
 817 |     /// </summary>
 818 |     /// <param name="regionName">Name of the cold region to generate
 819 |     /// (must match a <see cref="PlanRegion.Name"/> in the original plan).</param>
 820 |     /// <param name="originalPlan">The original <see cref="WorldPlan"/>
 821 |     /// from the initial build (reloaded from
 822 |     /// <see cref="SaveMeta.OriginalPlanJson"/> by the caller). Null
 823 |     /// triggers a graceful no-op return — the travel handler logs the
 824 |     /// skip.</param>
 825 |     /// <param name="progress">Optional progress callback (the travel UI
 826 |     /// shows a "Генерация региона…" overlay while this runs).</param>
 827 |     /// <param name="ct">Cancellation token (travel overlay Cancel button).</param>
 828 |     /// <returns>A <see cref="RegionGenerationResult"/> with the count of
 829 |     /// new locations / NPCs / buildings committed and any errors. The
 830 |     /// <see cref="RegionGenerationResult.Success"/> flag is false on AI
 831 |     /// failure or when the region name doesn't match a cold region in
 832 |     /// the plan.</returns>
 833 |     public async Task<RegionGenerationResult> GenerateRegionAsync(
 834 |         string regionName,
 835 |         WorldPlan? originalPlan,
 836 |         IProgress<WorldBuildProgress>? progress = null,
 837 |         CancellationToken ct = default)
 838 |     {
 839 |         if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(regionName))
 840 |             return new RegionGenerationResult { Success = false, Error = "empty region name" };
 841 |         if (originalPlan is null)
 842 |             return new RegionGenerationResult { Success = false, Error = "no original plan available" };
 843 | 
 844 |         // Find the region in the original plan. If it's already marked
 845 |         // "ready", this is a no-op (idempotent — the travel handler may
 846 |         // double-call after a race).
 847 |         var region = (originalPlan.Regions ?? new())
 848 |             .FirstOrDefault(r => string.Equals(r.Name, regionName, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase));
 849 |         if (region is null)
 850 |             return new RegionGenerationResult { Success = false, Error = $"region «{regionName}» not found in plan" };
 851 |         if (string.Equals(region.GenStatus, "ready", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
 852 |             return new RegionGenerationResult { Success = true, AlreadyReady = true, Summary = "region already ready" };
 853 | 
 854 |         // Check World.Flags for an existing "ready" marker — the region
 855 |         // may have been generated by a previous call in this session.
 856 |         if (_world.Flags is not null
 857 |             && _world.Flags.TryGetValue("readyRegions", out var rr)
 858 |             && rr is System.Text.Json.JsonElement je && je.ValueKind == System.Text.Json.JsonValueKind.Array)
 859 |         {
 860 |             var existingReady = je.EnumerateArray()
 861 |                 .Select(e => e.GetString())
 862 |                 .Where(s => !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(s))
 863 |                 .ToList();
 864 |             if (existingReady.Any(n => string.Equals(n, regionName, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)))
 865 |                 return new RegionGenerationResult { Success = true, AlreadyReady = true, Summary = "region already ready" };
 866 |         }
 867 | 
 868 |         progress?.Report(new WorldBuildProgress
 869 |         {
 870 |             Stage = "region",
 871 |             Status = ProgressStatus.Active,
 872 |             Label = $"Генерация региона «{regionName}»",
 873 |             Detail = "Запрос к планировщику…",
 874 |             Percent = 30,
 875 |         });
 876 | 
 877 |         // Build the focused planner prompt. We give the AI:
 878 |         //   - the world title + theme + setting + atmosphere
 879 |         //   - the cold region's high-level description from the plan
 880 |         //   - the names of existing locations on the boundary (so the
 881 |         //     AI can connect new locations to them)
 882 |         var worldTitle = originalPlan.Title;
 883 |         var theme = originalPlan.Theme;
 884 |         var boundaryLocs = _world.Locations
 885 |             .Where(l => l.Discovered)
 886 |             .Select(l => l.Name)
 887 |             .Take(20)
 888 |             .ToList();
 889 |         var boundaryBlock = boundaryLocs.Count > 0
 890 |             ? string.Join(", ", boundaryLocs)
 891 |             : "(нет видимых пограничных локаций — соедини с любой существующей)";
 892 | 
 893 |         var systemPrompt =
 894 |             "Ты — Продолжатель Мира. Игрок подошёл к границе нового региона в уже существующем мире. " +
 895 |             "Сгенерируй локации, NPC и здания для этого региона и соедини их с пограничными локациями. " +
 896 |             "Работай на русском. Верни ТОЛЬКО JSON-объект PartialWorldPlan в блоке ```json ... ```. " +
 897 |             "Без другого текста.\n\n" +
 898 |             "Формат JSON:\n" +
 899 |             "```json\n" +
 900 |             "{\n" +
 901 |             "  \"locations\": [{\"name\":\"...\",\"terrain\":\"...\",\"danger\":N,\"role\":\"settlement\", " +
 902 |             "\"description\":\"...\",\"connections\":[\"<имя существующей пограничной локации>\"], " +
 903 |             "\"directionFromHub\":\"<направление>\",\"region\":\"<имя региона>\"}],\n" +
 904 |             "  \"npcs\": [{\"name\":\"...\",\"template\":\"<customNpcTemplate.Id или стандартный>\"," +
 905 |             "\"location\":\"<имя локации из locations>\",\"role\":\"...\",\"disposition\":\"...\"," +
 906 |             "\"behavior\":\"...\",\"level\":N,\"notes\":\"...\"}],\n" +
 907 |             "  \"buildings\": [{\"template\":\"<customBuildingTemplate.Id или стандартный>\"," +
 908 |             "\"location\":\"<имя локации>\",\"nameOverride\":\"...\"}],\n" +
 909 |             "  \"customNpcTemplates\": [...],\n" +
 910 |             "  \"customItemTemplates\": [...],\n" +
 911 |             "  \"customBuildingTemplates\": [...]\n" +
 912 |             "}\n" +
 913 |             "```\n" +
 914 |             "Все новые локации должны иметь field region равным имени генерируемого региона. " +
 915 |             "В connections указывай ИМЕНА существующих локаций (из списка границы) — иначе выход не подключится.";
 916 | 
 917 |         var userPrompt =
 918 |             $"Сгенерируй локации, NPC, здания для региона «{regionName}» мира «{worldTitle}».\n" +
 919 |             $"Тема мира: {theme}. Сеттинг: {originalPlan.Setting}. Атмосфера: {originalPlan.Atmosphere}.\n" +
 920 |             $"Описание региона из плана: тип={region.Type}, климат={region.Climate}, " +
 921 |             $"население={region.Population}, экономика={region.Economy}, политика={region.Politics}, " +
 922 |             $"культура={region.Culture}.\n" +
 923 |             $"Существующие локации на границе: {boundaryBlock}.\n" +
 924 |             $"Соедини новые локации с границей (минимум один выход к существующей локации).\n" +
 925 |             "Верни JSON-объект PartialWorldPlan в блоке ```json ... ```.";
 926 | 
 927 |         var messages = new List<ChatMessage>
 928 |         {
 929 |             ChatMessage.System(systemPrompt),
 930 |             ChatMessage.User(userPrompt),
 931 |         };
 932 | 
 933 |         var plannerAi = _aiSettings is null ? _ai : _ai.WithModel(_aiSettings.GetModelForRole(AiRole.Planner));
 934 |         ChatResponse response;
 935 |         try
 936 |         {
 937 |             response = await plannerAi.ChatAsync(messages, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 938 |         }
 939 |         catch (AiException ex)
 940 |         {
 941 |             return new RegionGenerationResult { Success = false, Error = $"planner AI: {ex.Message}" };
 942 |         }
 943 | 
 944 |         var json = ExtractJsonBlock(response.Content ?? string.Empty);
 945 |         if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(json))
 946 |             return new RegionGenerationResult { Success = false, Error = "planner returned no JSON block" };
 947 | 
 948 |         // Parse the partial plan. We deserialize into a WorldPlan (the
 949 |         // partial plan only fills Locations/Npcs/Buildings/Custom* fields;
 950 |         // the required Title/Theme/Setting/Atmosphere/StartingHook fields
 951 |         // are missing, so we use a permissive parse + manual fill).
 952 |         WorldPlan? partial;
 953 |         try
 954 |         {
 955 |             var opts = new System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions
 956 |             {
 957 |                 PropertyNamingPolicy = System.Text.Json.JsonNamingPolicy.CamelCase,
 958 |                 PropertyNameCaseInsensitive = true,
 959 |             };
 960 |             // Wrap the partial JSON in a full-plan shell so the required
 961 |             // fields are present. The wrapper's Title/Theme/etc. come from
 962 |             // the original plan; the partial's Locations/Npcs/Buildings
 963 |             // override the wrapper's empty lists via a merge step.
 964 |             using var doc = System.Text.Json.JsonDocument.Parse(json);
 965 |             var merged = new JsonObject();
 966 |             merged["title"] = originalPlan.Title;
 967 |             merged["theme"] = originalPlan.Theme;
 968 |             merged["setting"] = originalPlan.Setting;
 969 |             merged["atmosphere"] = originalPlan.Atmosphere;
 970 |             merged["startingHook"] = originalPlan.StartingHook;
 971 |             merged["generationMode"] = "chunked";
 972 |             foreach (var prop in doc.RootElement.EnumerateObject())
 973 |             {
 974 |                 merged[prop.Name] = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<JsonNode>(prop.Value.GetRawText());
 975 |             }
 976 |             partial = System.Text.Json.JsonSerializer.Deserialize<WorldPlan>(merged, opts);
 977 |             if (partial is null)
 978 |                 return new RegionGenerationResult { Success = false, Error = "partial plan deserialized to null" };
 979 |         }
 980 |         catch (System.Text.Json.JsonException ex)
 981 |         {
 982 |             return new RegionGenerationResult { Success = false, Error = $"partial plan JSON malformed: {ex.Message}" };
 983 |         }
 984 | 
 985 |         // Tag all parsed locations with the target region (defensive —
 986 |         // the planner should already set region=regionName, but LLMs
 987 |         // don't always comply).
 988 |         partial = partial with
 989 |         {
 990 |             Locations = (partial.Locations ?? new())
 991 |                 .Select(pl => string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(pl.Region)
 992 |                     ? pl with { Region = regionName }
 993 |                     : pl)
 994 |                 .ToList(),
 995 |         };
 996 | 
 997 |         progress?.Report(new WorldBuildProgress
 998 |         {
 999 |             Stage = "region",
1000 |             Status = ProgressStatus.Active,
1001 |             Label = $"Генерация региона «{regionName}»",
1002 |             Detail = $"Получено {partial.Locations.Count} локаций, {partial.Npcs.Count} NPC, {partial.Buildings.Count} зданий. Коммит…",
1003 |             Percent = 70,
1004 |         });
1005 | 
1006 |         // Commit via the WorldBuilderCommitter. It's idempotent — won't
1007 |         // duplicate locations/NPCs that already exist by name. We pass
1008 |         // the partial plan with GenerationMode="chunked" so the
1009 |         // committer's CommitLocations correctly filters (the region is
1010 |         // not in coldRegions anymore since we'll mark it ready after
1011 |         // commit; the committer's filter is: skip if Region is in
1012 |         // coldRegions. Since regionName was in coldRegions, the filter
1013 |         // would WRONGLY skip the new locations. So we clear
1014 |         // coldRegions/readyRegions on the world BEFORE committing, then
1015 |         // re-set them with regionName moved to readyRegions.)
1016 |         if (_world.Flags is not null && _world.Flags.ContainsKey("coldRegions"))
1017 |         {
1018 |             var cold = ReadFlagStringList(_world.Flags, "coldRegions");
1019 |             cold = cold.Where(n => !string.Equals(n, regionName, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)).ToList();
1020 |             _world.Flags["coldRegions"] = cold;
1021 |         }
1022 | 
1023 |         var committer = new WorldBuilderCommitter(_world);
1024 |         var stats = new CommitStats();
1025 |         // Re-set GenerationMode to null on the partial plan so the
1026 |         // committer treats ALL locations as committable (we've already
1027 |         // removed regionName from coldRegions; the partial plan's other
1028 |         // regions don't matter here).
1029 |         var commitPlan = partial with { GenerationMode = null };
1030 |         committer.CommitCustomTemplates(commitPlan, stats);
1031 |         committer.CommitLocations(commitPlan, stats);
1032 |         committer.CommitPopulation(commitPlan, stats);
1033 |         committer.CommitBuildings(commitPlan, stats);
1034 | 
1035 |         // Mark the region ready in World.Flags (move it to readyRegions).
1036 |         if (_world.Flags is null) _world.Flags = new();
1037 |         var readyList = ReadFlagStringList(_world.Flags, "readyRegions");
1038 |         if (!readyList.Any(n => string.Equals(n, regionName, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)))
1039 |             readyList.Add(regionName);
1040 |         _world.Flags["readyRegions"] = readyList;
1041 | 
1042 |         progress?.Report(new WorldBuildProgress
1043 |         {
1044 |             Stage = "region",
1045 |             Status = ProgressStatus.Done,
1046 |             Label = $"Регион «{regionName}» готов",
1047 |             Detail = stats.Summary(),
1048 |             Percent = 100,
1049 |         });
1050 | 
1051 |         return new RegionGenerationResult
1052 |         {
1053 |             Success = true,
1054 |             LocationsAdded = stats.Locations,
1055 |             NpcsAdded = stats.Npcs,
1056 |             BuildingsAdded = stats.Buildings,
1057 |             Errors = stats.Errors,
1058 |             Summary = stats.Summary(),
1059 |         };
1060 |     }
1061 | 
1062 |     /// <summary>
1063 |     /// Result of <see cref="GenerateRegionAsync"/>. Returned to the
1064 |     /// travel handler so it can log what was added (and surface any
1065 |     /// errors to the player via the in-game log).
1066 |     /// </summary>
1067 |     public sealed record RegionGenerationResult
1068 |     {
1069 |         public bool Success { get; init; }
1070 |         public bool AlreadyReady { get; init; }
1071 |         public int LocationsAdded { get; init; }
1072 |         public int NpcsAdded { get; init; }
1073 |         public int BuildingsAdded { get; init; }
1074 |         public List<string> Errors { get; init; } = new();
1075 |         public string? Error { get; init; }
1076 |         public string? Summary { get; init; }
1077 |     }
1078 | 
1079 |     /// <summary>
1080 |     /// Read a <c>World.Flags</c> string-list entry into a fresh
1081 |     /// <see cref="List{T}"/>. Tolerates values stored as either a
1082 |     /// <see cref="string"/> (single-item, treated as a one-element list)
1083 |     /// or a <see cref="System.Text.Json.JsonElement"/> array (the form
1084 |     /// produced by JSON serialization). Returns an empty list when the
1085 |     /// key is missing or the value shape is unrecognized.
1086 |     /// </summary>
1087 |     private static List<string> ReadFlagStringList(
1088 |         System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary<string, object>? flags, string key)
1089 |     {
1090 |         if (flags is null) return new();
1091 |         if (!flags.TryGetValue(key, out var v) || v is null) return new();
1092 |         switch (v)
1093 |         {
1094 |             case string s:
1095 |                 return string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(s) ? new() : new() { s };
1096 |             case System.Collections.IEnumerable e when v is not string:
1097 |                 var result = new List<string>();
1098 |                 foreach (var item in e)
1099 |                 {
1100 |                     if (item is string ss && !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(ss)) result.Add(ss);
1101 |                     else if (item is System.Text.Json.JsonElement je && je.ValueKind == System.Text.Json.JsonValueKind.String)
1102 |                     {
1103 |                         var sv = je.GetString();
1104 |                         if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(sv)) result.Add(sv);
1105 |                     }
1106 |                 }
1107 |                 return result;
1108 |             default:
1109 |                 return new();
1110 |         }
1111 |     }
1112 | 
1113 |     // ─── Rebuild existing world (issue #23) ──────────────────────────────
1114 | 
1115 |     /// <summary>
1116 |     /// Rebuild options for <see cref="RebuildAsync"/>. Each flag selects
1117 |     /// a category to regenerate. <see cref="FullRebuild"/> is the only
1118 |     /// destructive option — it discards all existing entities and
