1119 |     /// regenerates from scratch. The partial flags are additive: the
1120 |     /// existing world is preserved, the AI generates additional content
1121 |     /// in the selected categories, and the committer (idempotent)
1122 |     /// commits only the new entries.
1123 |     /// </summary>
1124 |     public sealed record RebuildOptions
1125 |     {
1126 |         /// <summary>Regenerate locations (additive — keeps existing).</summary>
1127 |         public bool Locations { get; init; }
1128 |         /// <summary>Regenerate population (additive — keeps existing NPCs).</summary>
1129 |         public bool Npcs { get; init; }
1130 |         /// <summary>Regenerate loot/items (additive — keeps existing).</summary>
1131 |         public bool Items { get; init; }
1132 |         /// <summary>Re-write the opening narration from the current state.</summary>
1133 |         public bool Narration { get; init; }
1134 |         /// <summary>
1135 |         /// Full rebuild — discard all entities and regenerate from
1136 |         /// scratch. The only destructive option.
1137 |         /// </summary>
1138 |         public bool FullRebuild { get; init; }
1139 |     }
1140 | 
1141 |     /// <summary>
1142 |     /// Rebuild an existing world. Loads the save's World + meta, applies
1143 |     /// the requested categories (additive for partial flags, destructive
1144 |     /// for <see cref="RebuildOptions.FullRebuild"/>), runs the planner +
1145 |     /// committer + narrator as needed, persists the result back to the
1146 |     /// same saveId via the supplied <paramref name="saveManager"/>.
1147 |     /// </summary>
1148 |     /// <param name="saveManager">Save manager (used to load + persist the
1149 |     /// world). Caller passes the live SaveManager so the orchestrator
1150 |     /// doesn't take a direct dependency on the filesystem layout.</param>
1151 |     /// <param name="saveId">Existing save id to rebuild.</param>
1152 |     /// <param name="options">Which categories to regenerate.</param>
1153 |     /// <param name="brief">Optional rebuild brief. When empty, the
1154 |     /// orchestrator falls back to the world's original theme/setting
1155 |     /// from <see cref="World.Flags"/>.</param>
1156 |     /// <param name="log">Existing log entries (preserved on partial
1157 |     /// rebuilds; replaced on full rebuild). Null = empty log.</param>
1158 |     /// <param name="progress">Optional progress callback.</param>
1159 |     /// <param name="ct">Cancellation token.</param>
1160 |     /// <returns>A <see cref="RebuildResult"/> with the final plan (if
1161 |     /// the planner ran), the new narration (if the narrator ran), the
1162 |     /// commit stats, and a summary.</returns>
1163 |     public async Task<RebuildResult> RebuildAsync(
1164 |         SaveManager saveManager,
1165 |         string saveId,
1166 |         RebuildOptions options,
1167 |         string? brief = null,
1168 |         LogEntry[]? log = null,
1169 |         IProgress<WorldBuildProgress>? progress = null,
1170 |         CancellationToken ct = default)
1171 |     {
1172 |         if (saveManager is null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(saveManager));
1173 |         if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(saveId))
1174 |             return new RebuildResult { Success = false, Error = "empty saveId" };
1175 |         if (options is null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(options));
1176 | 
1177 |         var loaded = saveManager.LoadAll(saveId);
1178 |         if (loaded is null)
1179 |             return new RebuildResult { Success = false, Error = $"save «{saveId}» not found" };
1180 |         var (world, meta, existingLog) = loaded.Value;
1181 | 
1182 |         // Resolve the rebuild brief: explicit > original theme/setting
1183 |         // (stashed on World.Flags by CommitTitle) > generic fallback.
1184 |         var rebuildBrief = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(brief)
1185 |             ? ResolveOriginalBrief(world)
1186 |             : brief.Trim();
1187 |         var logEntries = log ?? existingLog ?? Array.Empty<LogEntry>();
1188 | 
1189 |         // Full rebuild: discard all entities + reset the player. This is
1190 |         // the only destructive path — it discards the player's progress.
1191 |         if (options.FullRebuild)
1192 |         {
1193 |             progress?.Report(new WorldBuildProgress
1194 |             {
1195 |                 Stage = "rebuild",
1196 |                 Status = ProgressStatus.Active,
1197 |                 Label = "Полный пересбор мира",
1198 |                 Detail = "Очистка существующего мира…",
1199 |                 Percent = 5,
1200 |             });
1201 | 
1202 |             var seed = world.Seed;
1203 |             var registries = world.Registries;
1204 |             world.Players.Clear();
1205 |             world.Npcs.Clear();
1206 |             world.Items.Clear();
1207 |             world.Buildings.Clear();
1208 |             world.Locations.Clear();
1209 |             world.Quests.Clear();
1210 |             world.Flags = new();
1211 |             world.Combat = null;
1212 |             world.Turn = 0;
1213 |             world.Rng = Rng.FromState(seed);
1214 |             world.Ruleset = Rulesets.DefaultDnd;
1215 | 
1216 |             // Run a full build (planner → ruleset → committer → narrator)
1217 |             // using the rebuild brief. We reuse RunAsync by setting up
1218 |             // the orchestrator state to a fresh plan.
1219 |             _currentPlan = null;
1220 |             _currentStage = "planning";
1221 |             _currentIteration = 0;
1222 |             _loadedStage = null;
1223 | 
1224 |             var request = new WorldPlanRequest { Brief = rebuildBrief };
1225 |             var runResult = await RunAsync(request, progress, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
1226 | 
1227 |             if (runResult.Kind != WorldBuilderResultKind.Complete)
1228 |             {
1229 |                 return new RebuildResult
1230 |                 {
1231 |                     Success = false,
1232 |                     Error = $"full rebuild failed: {runResult.Summary}",
1233 |                 };
1234 |             }
1235 | 
1236 |             // Persist the rebuilt world. Use the existing log (or empty).
1237 |             var newNarration = runResult.OpeningNarration;
1238 |             var newLog = newNarration is null
1239 |                 ? logEntries
1240 |                 : new[] { LogEntry.Narrative(newNarration, authorId: null) }
1241 |                     .Concat(logEntries.Where(l => l.Kind != "narrative"))
1242 |                     .ToArray();
1243 |             var updatedMeta = meta with { BuildStatus = BuildStatus.Done };
1244 |             saveManager.SaveAll(saveId, world, updatedMeta, newLog);
1245 | 
1246 |             return new RebuildResult
1247 |             {
1248 |                 Success = true,
1249 |                 Plan = runResult.Plan,
1250 |                 OpeningNarration = newNarration,
1251 |                 Stats = runResult.Stats,
1252 |                 Summary = $"Полный пересбор завершён. {runResult.Summary}",
1253 |             };
1254 |         }
1255 | 
1256 |         // Partial rebuild: additive. Run the planner with a prompt that
1257 |         // shows the existing state + asks for additions in the selected
1258 |         // categories. The committer is idempotent — only new entries are
1259 |         // added. Narration-only rebuild skips the planner entirely.
1260 |         string? narration2 = null;
1261 |         CommitStats? stats2 = null;
1262 |         WorldPlan? plan2 = null;
1263 | 
1264 |         if (options.Locations || options.Npcs || options.Items)
1265 |         {
1266 |             progress?.Report(new WorldBuildProgress
1267 |             {
1268 |                 Stage = "rebuild",
1269 |                 Status = ProgressStatus.Active,
1270 |                 Label = "Партиальный пересбор",
1271 |                 Detail = "Запрос к планировщику…",
1272 |                 Percent = 20,
1273 |             });
1274 | 
1275 |             var cats = new List<string>();
1276 |             if (options.Locations) cats.Add("новые локации");
1277 |             if (options.Npcs) cats.Add("дополнительные NPC");
1278 |             if (options.Items) cats.Add("новые предметы");
1279 |             var catsBlock = string.Join(", ", cats);
1280 | 
1281 |             var stateSummary = BuildRebuildStateSummary(world);
1282 |             var systemPrompt =
1283 |                 "Ты — Достраиватель Мира. Игрок попросил дополнить существующий мир. " +
1284 |                 "Работай на русском. НЕ удаляй и не изменяй существующее — только добавляй. " +
1285 |                 "Верни ТОЛЬКО JSON-объект PartialWorldPlan в блоке ```json ... ```. " +
1286 |                 "Без другого текста.\n\n" +
1287 |                 "Формат JSON: {\"locations\":[...],\"npcs\":[...],\"buildings\":[...]," +
1288 |                 "\"customNpcTemplates\":[...],\"customItemTemplates\":[...]," +
1289 |                 "\"customBuildingTemplates\":[...],\"starterGear\":[...],\"starterCurrency\":N}.";
1290 | 
1291 |             var userPrompt =
1292 |                 $"Существующий мир:\n{stateSummary}\n\n" +
1293 |                 $"Дополнительно сгенерируй: {catsBlock}.\n" +
1294 |                 $"Бриф для перестройки: {rebuildBrief}\n" +
1295 |                 "Не удаляй существующее. Новые локации соедини с существующими (укажи их имена в connections). " +
1296 |                 "Имена новых сущностей не должны совпадать с уже существующими. " +
1297 |                 "Верни JSON-объект PartialWorldPlan в блоке ```json ... ```.";
1298 | 
1299 |             var messages = new List<ChatMessage>
1300 |             {
1301 |                 ChatMessage.System(systemPrompt),
1302 |                 ChatMessage.User(userPrompt),
1303 |             };
1304 | 
1305 |             var plannerAi = _aiSettings is null ? _ai : _ai.WithModel(_aiSettings.GetModelForRole(AiRole.Planner));
1306 |             ChatResponse response;
1307 |             try
1308 |             {
1309 |                 response = await plannerAi.ChatAsync(messages, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
1310 |             }
1311 |             catch (AiException ex)
1312 |             {
1313 |                 return new RebuildResult { Success = false, Error = $"planner AI: {ex.Message}" };
1314 |             }
1315 | 
1316 |             var json = ExtractJsonBlock(response.Content ?? string.Empty);
1317 |             if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(json))
1318 |                 return new RebuildResult { Success = false, Error = "planner returned no JSON block" };
1319 | 
1320 |             try
1321 |             {
1322 |                 var opts = new System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions
1323 |                 {
1324 |                     PropertyNamingPolicy = System.Text.Json.JsonNamingPolicy.CamelCase,
1325 |                     PropertyNameCaseInsensitive = true,
1326 |                 };
1327 |                 // Wrap into a full-plan shell (same trick as
1328 |                 // GenerateRegionAsync — the partial JSON only fills the
1329 |                 // collection fields).
1330 |                 var worldTitle = (world.Flags is not null && world.Flags.TryGetValue("worldTitle", out var wt) && wt is string wts)
1331 |                     ? wts : (meta.WorldTitle ?? "Мир");
1332 |                 var worldTheme = (world.Flags is not null && world.Flags.TryGetValue("worldTheme", out var th) && th is string ths)
1333 |                     ? ths : "custom";
1334 |                 var worldSetting = (world.Flags is not null && world.Flags.TryGetValue("worldSetting", out var st) && st is string sts)
1335 |                     ? sts : "";
1336 |                 var worldAtm = (world.Flags is not null && world.Flags.TryGetValue("worldAtmosphere", out var at) && at is string ats)
1337 |                     ? ats : "";
1338 |                 var worldHook = (world.Flags is not null && world.Flags.TryGetValue("startingHook", out var hk) && hk is string hks)
1339 |                     ? hks : "";
1340 | 
1341 |                 using var doc = System.Text.Json.JsonDocument.Parse(json);
1342 |                 var merged = new JsonObject();
1343 |                 merged["title"] = worldTitle;
1344 |                 merged["theme"] = worldTheme;
1345 |                 merged["setting"] = worldSetting;
1346 |                 merged["atmosphere"] = worldAtm;
1347 |                 merged["startingHook"] = worldHook;
1348 |                 foreach (var prop in doc.RootElement.EnumerateObject())
1349 |                 {
1350 |                     merged[prop.Name] = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<JsonNode>(prop.Value.GetRawText());
1351 |                 }
1352 |                 plan2 = System.Text.Json.JsonSerializer.Deserialize<WorldPlan>(merged, opts);
1353 |                 if (plan2 is null)
1354 |                     return new RebuildResult { Success = false, Error = "partial plan deserialized to null" };
1355 |             }
1356 |             catch (System.Text.Json.JsonException ex)
1357 |             {
1358 |                 return new RebuildResult { Success = false, Error = $"partial plan JSON malformed: {ex.Message}" };
1359 |             }
1360 | 
1361 |             progress?.Report(new WorldBuildProgress
1362 |             {
1363 |                 Stage = "rebuild",
1364 |                 Status = ProgressStatus.Active,
1365 |                 Label = "Коммит дополнений",
1366 |                 Detail = $"Получено: {plan2!.Locations.Count} локаций, {plan2.Npcs.Count} NPC, {plan2.Buildings.Count} зданий",
1367 |                 Percent = 60,
1368 |             });
1369 | 
1370 |             // Commit additively. The committer's idempotency (match by
1371 |             // name) keeps existing entities intact; only new ones are added.
1372 |             var committer = new WorldBuilderCommitter(world);
1373 |             stats2 = new CommitStats();
1374 |             committer.CommitCustomTemplates(plan2, stats2);
1375 |             committer.CommitLocations(plan2, stats2);
1376 |             committer.CommitPopulation(plan2, stats2);
1377 |             committer.CommitBuildings(plan2, stats2);
1378 |             if (options.Items)
1379 |             {
1380 |                 committer.CommitContent(plan2, stats2);
1381 |             }
1382 |         }
1383 | 
1384 |         if (options.Narration)
1385 |         {
1386 |             progress?.Report(new WorldBuildProgress
1387 |             {
1388 |                 Stage = "rebuild",
1389 |                 Status = ProgressStatus.Active,
1390 |                 Label = "Переписываем вступление",
1391 |                 Percent = 80,
1392 |             });
1393 | 
1394 |             // Re-run the narrator with the current world state.
1395 |             var narrPlan = plan2 ?? new WorldPlan
1396 |             {
1397 |                 Title = meta.WorldTitle ?? "Мир",
1398 |                 Theme = TryGetFlag(world.Flags, "worldTheme") ?? "custom",
1399 |                 Setting = TryGetFlag(world.Flags, "worldSetting") ?? "",
1400 |                 Atmosphere = TryGetFlag(world.Flags, "worldAtmosphere") ?? "",
1401 |                 StartingHook = TryGetFlag(world.Flags, "startingHook") ?? "",
1402 |             };
1403 |             try
1404 |             {
1405 |                 narration2 = await RunNarratorAsync(narrPlan, progress, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
1406 |             }
1407 |             catch (OperationCanceledException) { throw; }
1408 |             catch (AiException ex)
1409 |             {
1410 |                 // Non-fatal — narration is best-effort.
1411 |                 progress?.Report(new WorldBuildProgress
1412 |                 {
1413 |                     Stage = "rebuild",
1414 |                     Status = ProgressStatus.Error,
1415 |                     Label = "Не удалось переписать вступление",
1416 |                     Detail = ex.Message,
1417 |                     Percent = 90,
1418 |                 });
1419 |             }
1420 |         }
1421 | 
1422 |         // Persist the rebuilt world. On a narration-only rebuild, the
1423 |         // new narration replaces any existing narrative log entries.
1424 |         LogEntry[] finalLog;
1425 |         if (narration2 is null)
1426 |         {
1427 |             finalLog = logEntries;
1428 |         }
1429 |         else
1430 |         {
1431 |             finalLog = new[] { LogEntry.Narrative(narration2, authorId: null) }
1432 |                 .Concat(logEntries.Where(l => l.Kind != "narrative"))
1433 |                 .ToArray();
1434 |         }
1435 | 
1436 |         var rebuiltMeta = meta with { BuildStatus = BuildStatus.Done };
1437 |         saveManager.SaveAll(saveId, world, rebuiltMeta, finalLog);
1438 | 
1439 |         progress?.Report(new WorldBuildProgress
1440 |         {
1441 |             Stage = "rebuild",
1442 |             Status = ProgressStatus.Done,
1443 |             Label = "Перестройка завершена",
1444 |             Detail = stats2?.Summary() ?? (narration2 is null ? "без изменений" : "только наррация"),
1445 |             Percent = 100,
1446 |         });
1447 | 
1448 |         var summary = stats2 is null
1449 |             ? (narration2 is null ? "Ничего не сделано." : "Наррация обновлена.")
1450 |             : $"Добавлено: {stats2.Summary()}" + (narration2 is null ? "" : " Наррация обновлена.");
1451 |         return new RebuildResult
1452 |         {
1453 |             Success = true,
1454 |             Plan = plan2,
1455 |             OpeningNarration = narration2,
1456 |             Stats = stats2,
1457 |             Summary = summary,
1458 |         };
1459 |     }
1460 | 
1461 |     /// <summary>
1462 |     /// Build a short text summary of the current world state for the
1463 |     /// rebuild planner prompt. Lists existing location names, NPC names,
1464 |     /// building names (capped at 20 each to keep the prompt bounded).
1465 |     /// </summary>
1466 |     private static string BuildRebuildStateSummary(GameWorld world)
1467 |     {
1468 |         var sb = new StringBuilder();
1469 |         sb.AppendLine($"# Существующий мир");
1470 |         sb.AppendLine($"Локаций: {world.Locations.Count} | NPC: {world.Npcs.Count} | Зданий: {world.Buildings.Count}");
1471 |         if (world.Locations.Count > 0)
1472 |         {
1473 |             sb.AppendLine("Существующие локации:");
1474 |             foreach (var l in world.Locations.Take(20))
1475 |                 sb.AppendLine($"- {l.Name} ({l.Terrain}, опасность {l.Danger})");
1476 |         }
1477 |         if (world.Npcs.Count > 0)
1478 |         {
1479 |             sb.AppendLine("Существующие NPC:");
1480 |             foreach (var n in world.Npcs.Take(20))
1481 |                 sb.AppendLine($"- {n.Name} [{n.TemplateId}] @ {world.GetLocation(n.LocationId)?.Name ?? "?"}");
1482 |         }
1483 |         if (world.Buildings.Count > 0)
1484 |         {
1485 |             sb.AppendLine("Существующие здания:");
1486 |             foreach (var b in world.Buildings.Take(20))
1487 |                 sb.AppendLine($"- {b.Name} [{b.TemplateId}] @ {world.GetLocation(b.LocationId)?.Name ?? "?"}");
1488 |         }
1489 |         return sb.ToString();
1490 |     }
1491 | 
1492 |     /// <summary>
1493 |     /// Resolve the original brief from World.Flags. Reconstructs a
1494 |     /// human-readable brief from the stashed worldTitle/worldTheme/
1495 |     /// worldSetting/worldAtmosphere/startingHook flags (set by
1496 |     /// CommitTitle). Used when the rebuild caller passes no explicit
1497 |     /// brief — we want the rebuild to stay on-theme with the original.
1498 |     /// </summary>
1499 |     private static string ResolveOriginalBrief(GameWorld world)
1500 |     {
1501 |         var title = TryGetFlag(world.Flags, "worldTitle") ?? "";
1502 |         var theme = TryGetFlag(world.Flags, "worldTheme") ?? "";
1503 |         var setting = TryGetFlag(world.Flags, "worldSetting") ?? "";
1504 |         var atmosphere = TryGetFlag(world.Flags, "worldAtmosphere") ?? "";
1505 |         var hook = TryGetFlag(world.Flags, "startingHook") ?? "";
1506 |         var sb = new StringBuilder();
1507 |         if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(title)) sb.AppendLine($"Название мира: {title}");
1508 |         if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(theme)) sb.AppendLine($"Тема: {theme}");
1509 |         if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(setting)) sb.AppendLine($"Сеттинг: {setting}");
1510 |         if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(atmosphere)) sb.AppendLine($"Атмосфера: {atmosphere}");
1511 |         if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(hook)) sb.AppendLine($"Сюжетный крючок: {hook}");
1512 |         return sb.Length == 0 ? "(без брифа — сгенерируй дополнения по своему усмотрению)" : sb.ToString();
1513 |     }
1514 | 
1515 |     /// <summary>
1516 |     /// Best-effort string extraction from a World.Flags entry. Tolerates
1517 |     /// both raw strings (in-memory) and JsonElement values (after a save
1518 |     /// round-trip). Returns null when missing or non-string.
1519 |     /// </summary>
1520 |     private static string? TryGetFlag(System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary<string, object>? flags, string key)
1521 |     {
1522 |         if (flags is null) return null;
1523 |         if (!flags.TryGetValue(key, out var v) || v is null) return null;
1524 |         if (v is string s) return s;
1525 |         if (v is System.Text.Json.JsonElement je && je.ValueKind == System.Text.Json.JsonValueKind.String)
1526 |             return je.GetString();
1527 |         return v.ToString();
1528 |     }
1529 | 
1530 |     /// <summary>Result of <see cref="RebuildAsync"/>.</summary>
1531 |     public sealed record RebuildResult
1532 |     {
1533 |         public bool Success { get; init; }
1534 |         public WorldPlan? Plan { get; init; }
1535 |         public string? OpeningNarration { get; init; }
1536 |         public CommitStats? Stats { get; init; }
1537 |         public string? Summary { get; init; }
1538 |         public string? Error { get; init; }
1539 |     }
1540 | 
1541 |     /// <summary>
1542 |     /// Stage 1: call the planner. Loads the <c>world-planner.md</c> prompt,
1543 |     /// fills in <c>{{WORLD_BRIEF}}</c> + <c>{{WORLD_STATE}}</c> +
1544 |     /// <c>{{ITEM_TEMPLATES}}</c> + <c>{{NPC_TEMPLATES}}</c> +
1545 |     /// <c>{{BUILDING_TEMPLATES}}</c>, and asks the model to produce a
1546 |     /// <see cref="WorldPlan"/> as JSON in its response content.
1547 |     ///
1548 |     /// <para>
1549 |     /// The TS source had the planner call a <c>commit_world_plan</c> tool
1550 |     /// with the plan as args. Since the desktop MVP doesn't wire that
1551 |     /// tool, we parse the plan directly from the model's response content
1552 |     /// (asking it to emit a fenced JSON block). This is less robust than
1553 |     /// tool-call args but adequate for the three-stage pipeline.
1554 |     /// </para>
1555 |     /// </summary>
1556 |     private async Task<WorldPlan> RunPlannerAsync(
1557 |         WorldPlanRequest request,
1558 |         IProgress<WorldBuildProgress>? progress,
1559 |         CancellationToken ct)
1560 |     {
1561 |         var systemPrompt = BuildPlannerPrompt(request.Brief);
1562 | 
1563 |         // Issue #20 (chunked generation): if the request specifies a
1564 |         // generation mode, tell the planner. In chunked mode the planner
1565 |         // should ONLY detail the start region's locations / NPCs / buildings;
1566 |         // other regions go into plan.Regions with GenStatus="cold" (no
1567 |         // locations). The committer skips cold-region locations; the
1568 |         // travel handler generates them on-demand via
1569 |         // GenerateRegionAsync.
1570 |         var modeInstruction = string.Equals(request.GenerationMode, "chunked", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
1571 |             ? "\n\nРЕЖИМ ГЕНЕРАЦИИ: по регионам (chunked). " +
1572 |               "В плане подробно опиши ТОЛЬКО стартовую область: её локации, NPC, здания " +
1573 |               "и выходы на границу региона. Остальные регионы перечисли в plan.regions с " +
1574 |               "полем genStatus=\"cold\" — без локаций, только общее описание (тип, климат, " +
1575 |               "население, экономика, политика, культура). Для стартового региона установи " +
1576 |               "genStatus=\"ready\" и containsStart=true. Установи plan.generationMode=\"chunked\". " +
1577 |               "Соедини стартовые локации выходами между собой. ДОПОЛНИТЕЛЬНО добавь 1-2 выхода " +
1578 |               "из пограничных стартовых локаций на холодные регионы: в connections укажи ИМЯ " +
1579 |               "региона (не локации) — движок создаст фантомный выход, который запустит " +
1580 |               "генерацию региона когда игрок к нему приблизится."
1581 |             : string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(request.GenerationMode)
1582 |                 ? ""
1583 |                 : $"\n\nРЕЖИМ ГЕНЕРАЦИИ: {request.GenerationMode}.";
1584 | 
1585 |         var messages = new List<ChatMessage>
1586 |         {
1587 |             ChatMessage.System(systemPrompt),
1588 |             ChatMessage.User(
1589 |                 "Построй мир по следующему брифу. Верни результат как JSON-объект WorldPlan " +
1590 |                 "в блоке ```json ... ```. Без другого текста.\n\n" +
1591 |                 $"БРИФ:\n{request.Brief}{modeInstruction}"),
1592 |         };
1593 | 
1594 |         // Derive a role-specific client for the planner (issue #26).
1595 |         // WithModel returns the same instance when no override is set.
1596 |         var plannerAi = _aiSettings is null ? _ai : _ai.WithModel(_aiSettings.GetModelForRole(AiRole.Planner));
1597 |         var response = await plannerAi.ChatAsync(messages, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
1598 |         var planJson = ExtractJsonBlock(response.Content ?? string.Empty);
1599 |         if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(planJson))
1600 |             throw new AiException(AiErrorKind.Parse,
1601 |                 "Planner did not return a JSON block. Response was: " +
1602 |                 (response.Content?.Length > 500 ? response.Content[..500] : response.Content));
1603 | 
1604 |         try
1605 |         {
1606 |             var opts = new System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions
1607 |             {
1608 |                 PropertyNamingPolicy = System.Text.Json.JsonNamingPolicy.CamelCase,
1609 |                 PropertyNameCaseInsensitive = true,
1610 |             };
1611 |             var plan = System.Text.Json.JsonSerializer.Deserialize<WorldPlan>(planJson, opts)
1612 |                 ?? throw new AiException(AiErrorKind.Parse, "Planner JSON deserialized to null.");
1613 | 
1614 |             // Issue #20: enforce the requested generation mode on the
1615 |             // parsed plan. If the planner forgot to set GenerationMode
1616 |             // (or set it wrong), we override it from the request so the
1617 |             // committer + travel handler can rely on it. In chunked mode
1618 |             // we also normalize region genStatus: the start region (the
1619 |             // one with containsStart=true OR the first region if none is
1620 |             // marked) becomes "ready"; all others become "cold".
1621 |             if (string.Equals(request.GenerationMode, "chunked", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
1622 |             {
1623 |                 plan = NormalizeChunkedPlan(plan);
1624 |             }
1625 |             return plan;
1626 |         }
1627 |         catch (System.Text.Json.JsonException ex)
1628 |         {
1629 |             throw new AiException(AiErrorKind.Parse,
1630 |                 "Planner JSON was malformed: " + ex.Message, inner: ex);
1631 |         }
1632 |     }
1633 | 
1634 |     private string BuildPlannerPrompt(string brief)
1635 |     {
1636 |         var template = _prompts.Get("world-planner");
1637 |         var stateBlock = BuildMinimalWorldState();
1638 |         var vars = new Dictionary<string, string>
1639 |         {
1640 |             ["WORLD_BRIEF"] = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(brief)
1641 |                 ? "(бриф пуст — создай мир по своему усмотрению, тёмное фэнтези)"
1642 |                 : brief.Trim(),
1643 |             ["WORLD_STATE"] = stateBlock,
1644 |             // NOTE: Standard template lists (ITEM_TEMPLATES, NPC_TEMPLATES,
1645 |             // BUILDING_TEMPLATES) are intentionally NOT provided. AI-generated
1646 |             // worlds must be created from scratch — all entities come from the
1647 |             // plan's customNpcTemplates / customItemTemplates / customBuildingTemplates.
1648 |             // The planner prompt explicitly tells the AI to create everything
1649 |             // custom; providing standard template ids would let it reference
1650 |             // them, defeating the "unique world" design goal.
1651 |         };
1652 |         var prompt = PromptLoader.Substitute(template, vars);
1653 |         // Append the tools guide so the planner knows the available tool
1654 |         // signatures — even though the desktop MVP's committer doesn't let
1655 |         // the planner call them directly, the planner prompt expects this
1656 |         // context to inform its plan structure.
1657 |         var guide = _prompts.Get("tools-guide");
1658 |         return prompt + "\n\n---\n\n" + guide;
1659 |     }
1660 | 
1661 |     /// <summary>
1662 |     /// Normalize a chunked plan after parsing. Ensures:
1663 |     /// <list type="bullet">
1664 |     ///   <item><see cref="WorldPlan.GenerationMode"/> is set to "chunked".</item>
1665 |     ///   <item>Exactly one region is marked <c>genStatus="ready"</c>
1666 |     ///     (the start region). The start region is the one with
1667 |     ///     <see cref="PlanRegion.ContainsStart"/>=true; if none is
1668 |     ///     marked, the first region (if any) is promoted.</item>
1669 |     ///   <item>All other regions are marked <c>genStatus="cold"</c>.</item>
1670 |     ///   <item>Locations with a Region that's now cold are dropped from
1671 |     ///     <see cref="WorldPlan.Locations"/> (the committer would skip
1672 |     ///     them anyway, but dropping keeps the plan consistent for the
1673 |     ///     narrator + saves).</item>
1674 |     /// </list>
1675 |     /// </summary>
1676 |     /// <remarks>
1677 |     /// This is defensive — the planner prompt asks for these invariants,
1678 |     /// but LLMs don't always comply. Forcing them on the parsed plan
1679 |     /// keeps the downstream committer + travel handler logic simple
1680 |     /// (they can trust the markers).
1681 |     /// </remarks>
1682 |     private static WorldPlan NormalizeChunkedPlan(WorldPlan plan)
1683 |     {
1684 |         // Force the GenerationMode flag on the plan.
1685 |         plan = plan with { GenerationMode = "chunked" };
1686 | 
1687 |         var regions = (plan.Regions ?? new()).ToList();
1688 |         if (regions.Count == 0)
1689 |         {
1690 |             // No regions in the plan — nothing to normalize. The plan
1691 |             // is treated as "all start, no cold regions" (effectively
1692 |             // a full build despite the chunked flag). This is a
1693 |             // graceful fallback for malformed planner output.
1694 |             return plan;
1695 |         }
1696 | 
1697 |         // Find the start region: prefer containsStart=true; else the
1698 |         // first region that already has genStatus="ready"; else the
1699 |         // first region in the list.
1700 |         var startIdx = regions.FindIndex(r => (r.ContainsStart ?? false));
1701 |         if (startIdx < 0)
1702 |             startIdx = regions.FindIndex(r => string.Equals(r.GenStatus, "ready", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase));
1703 |         if (startIdx < 0) startIdx = 0;
1704 | 
1705 |         var coldNames = new HashSet<string>(StringComparer.Ordinal);
1706 |         for (int i = 0; i < regions.Count; i++)
1707 |         {
1708 |             var r = regions[i];
1709 |             var status = i == startIdx ? "ready" : "cold";
1710 |             if (!string.Equals(r.GenStatus, status, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
1711 |             {
1712 |                 regions[i] = r with { GenStatus = status };
1713 |             }
1714 |             if (i != startIdx && !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(regions[i].Name))
1715 |                 coldNames.Add(regions[i].Name);
1716 |         }
1717 | 
1718 |         // Drop locations that belong to a cold region (defensive — the
1719 |         // committer would skip them anyway, but this keeps the plan
1720 |         // consistent for the narrator + saves).
1721 |         var filteredLocations = (plan.Locations ?? new())
1722 |             .Where(pl => string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(pl.Region) || !coldNames.Contains(pl.Region))
1723 |             .ToList();
1724 | 
1725 |         return plan with { Regions = regions, Locations = filteredLocations };
1726 |     }
1727 | 
1728 |     private string BuildMinimalWorldState()
1729 |     {
1730 |         var sb = new System.Text.StringBuilder();
1731 |         sb.AppendLine("# ТЕКУЩЕЕ СОСТОЯНИЕ МИРА (стартовое — возможно пустое)");
1732 |         sb.AppendLine($"Часы мира: {_world.Clock}");
1733 |         sb.AppendLine($"Локаций: {_world.Locations.Count} | NPC: {_world.Npcs.Count} | Зданий: {_world.Buildings.Count}");
1734 |         return sb.ToString();
1735 |     }
1736 | 
1737 |     /// <summary>
1738 |     /// Extract the first fenced JSON block (```json ... ```) from the
1739 |     /// model's response. Falls back to the whole response if no fence is
1740 |     /// found. Returns null if the response is empty.
1741 |     /// </summary>
1742 |     private static string? ExtractJsonBlock(string content)
1743 |     {
1744 |         if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(content)) return null;
1745 |         var start = content.IndexOf("```", StringComparison.Ordinal);
1746 |         if (start < 0) return content.Trim();
1747 |         // Skip the opening fence + optional language tag.
1748 |         var afterFence = content.IndexOf('\n', start);
1749 |         if (afterFence < 0) return content.Trim();
1750 |         var bodyStart = afterFence + 1;
1751 |         var end = content.IndexOf("```", bodyStart, StringComparison.Ordinal);
1752 |         if (end < 0) return content[(bodyStart)..].Trim();
1753 |         return content[bodyStart..end].Trim();
1754 |     }
1755 | 
1756 |     /// <summary>
1757 |     /// Stage 7: call the narrator. Loads the <c>world-narrator.md</c>
1758 |     /// prompt, fills in <c>{{WORLD_PLAN}}</c> (a readable summary of the
1759 |     /// committed plan) + <c>{{WORLD_STATE}}</c> (a snapshot of the live
1760 |     /// world after the committer ran — real location descriptions, real
1761 |     /// NPC names) + the three template-list vars. Asks the model to write
1762 |     /// the 2-paragraph atmospheric opening narration.
1763 |     ///
1764 |     /// <para>
1765 |     /// The TS narrator called <c>end_worldbuilding</c> as a final tool.
1766 |     /// The desktop MVP has no such tool (no TODO-driven loop) — we just
1767 |     /// ask the model for plain text and use it verbatim as the opening
1768 |     /// narration. If the model wraps the text in prose, we strip any
1769 |     /// leading "Вступление:"-style labels.
1770 |     /// </para>
1771 |     /// </summary>
1772 |     private async Task<string> RunNarratorAsync(
1773 |         WorldPlan plan,
1774 |         IProgress<WorldBuildProgress>? progress,
1775 |         CancellationToken ct)
1776 |     {
1777 |         progress?.Report(new WorldBuildProgress
1778 |         {
1779 |             Stage = "narration",
1780 |             Status = ProgressStatus.Active,
1781 |             Label = "Нарратор пишет вступление",
1782 |             Percent = 85,
1783 |         });
1784 | 
1785 |         var systemPrompt = BuildNarratorPrompt(plan);
1786 |         var messages = new List<ChatMessage>
1787 |         {
1788 |             ChatMessage.System(systemPrompt),
1789 |             ChatMessage.User(
1790 |                 "Напиши атмосферное вступление для игрока. 2 коротких абзаца на русском. " +
1791 |                 "Без заголовков, без markdown, без рассуждений о процессе — только готовый текст. " +
1792 |                 "Стиль под тему мира. Не озвучивай механику."),
1793 |         };
1794 | 
1795 |         // Derive a role-specific client for the narrator (issue #26).
1796 |         var narratorAi = _aiSettings is null ? _ai : _ai.WithModel(_aiSettings.GetModelForRole(AiRole.Narrator));
1797 |         var response = await narratorAi.ChatAsync(messages, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
1798 |         var text = (response.Content ?? string.Empty).Trim();
1799 |         if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(text))
1800 |             throw new AiException(AiErrorKind.Parse, "Narrator returned empty content.");
1801 | 
1802 |         // Strip a leading label the model sometimes adds ("Вступление:",
1803 |         // "Наррация:", etc.). Keep the rest verbatim.
1804 |         var colonIdx = text.IndexOf('\n');
1805 |         if (colonIdx > 0 && colonIdx < 40)
1806 |         {
1807 |             var firstLine = text[..colonIdx].TrimEnd(':');
1808 |             if (firstLine.Length < 30 &&
1809 |                 (firstLine.Contains("Вступление", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) ||
1810 |                  firstLine.Contains("Наррац", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) ||
1811 |                  firstLine.Contains("Начало", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)))
1812 |             {
1813 |                 text = text[(colonIdx + 1)..].TrimStart();
1814 |             }
1815 |         }
1816 | 
1817 |         return text;
1818 |     }
1819 | 
1820 |     private string BuildNarratorPrompt(WorldPlan plan)
1821 |     {
1822 |         var template = _prompts.Get("world-narrator");
1823 |         var planBlock = BuildReadablePlanSummary(plan);
1824 |         var stateBlock = BuildLiveWorldState();
1825 |         var vars = new Dictionary<string, string>
1826 |         {
1827 |             ["WORLD_PLAN"] = planBlock,
1828 |             ["WORLD_STATE"] = stateBlock,
1829 |             // NOTE: Template lists intentionally NOT provided to the narrator.
1830 |             // AI-generated worlds are built from scratch; the narrator doesn't
1831 |             // create entities, it only narrates. The narrator prompt says
1832 |             // "standard templates don't exist — this world is custom."
1833 |         };
1834 |         return PromptLoader.Substitute(template, vars);
1835 |     }
1836 | 
1837 |     /// <summary>
1838 |     /// Render the plan as a readable text block for the narrator prompt.
1839 |     /// The narrator needs theme / atmosphere / setting / startingHook +
1840 |     /// the planned start-location name + the NPCs at that location.
1841 |     /// </summary>
1842 |     private static string BuildReadablePlanSummary(WorldPlan plan)
1843 |     {
1844 |         var sb = new StringBuilder();
1845 |         sb.AppendLine($"# План мира: {plan.Title}");
1846 |         sb.AppendLine($"Тема: {plan.Theme}");
1847 |         sb.AppendLine($"Сеттинг: {plan.Setting}");
1848 |         sb.AppendLine($"Атмосфера: {plan.Atmosphere}");
1849 |         sb.AppendLine($"Сюжетный крючок: {plan.StartingHook}");
1850 |         sb.AppendLine();
1851 |         sb.AppendLine("## Локации (план):");
1852 |         foreach (var loc in plan.Locations ?? new())
1853 |         {
1854 |             var marker = loc.Role == LocationRole.Start ? " [СТАРТ]" : "";
1855 |             sb.AppendLine($"- {loc.Name}{marker} ({loc.Terrain}, опасность {loc.Danger}): {loc.Description}");
1856 |         }
1857 |         sb.AppendLine();
1858 |         sb.AppendLine("## NPC (план):");
1859 |         foreach (var n in plan.Npcs ?? new())
1860 |         {
1861 |             sb.AppendLine($"- {n.Name} [{n.Template}] @ {n.Location} — {n.Notes}");
1862 |         }
1863 |         return sb.ToString();
1864 |     }
1865 | 
1866 |     /// <summary>
1867 |     /// Render the LIVE world state (after the committer ran) for the
1868 |     /// narrator. The narrator must use REAL world descriptions, not the
1869 |     /// plan's, so the opening narration matches what the player actually
1870 |     /// sees. Focuses on the start location + its inhabitants + exits.
1871 |     /// </summary>
1872 |     private string BuildLiveWorldState()
1873 |     {
1874 |         var sb = new StringBuilder();
1875 |         sb.AppendLine("# ТЕКУЩЕЕ СОСТОЯНИЕ МИРА (после строительства)");
1876 |         sb.AppendLine($"Время: {_world.Clock}");
1877 |         sb.AppendLine($"Всего: {_world.Locations.Count} лок., {_world.Npcs.Count} NPC, {_world.Buildings.Count} зд.");
1878 |         sb.AppendLine();
1879 | 
1880 |         var startLoc = _world.Locations.FirstOrDefault(l => l.Visited)
1881 |             ?? _world.Locations.FirstOrDefault();
1882 |         if (startLoc is null)
1883 |         {
1884 |             sb.AppendLine("(нет локаций)");
1885 |             return sb.ToString();
1886 |         }
1887 | 
1888 |         sb.AppendLine($"## Текущая локация: {startLoc.Name}");
1889 |         sb.AppendLine($"Описание: {startLoc.Description ?? "—"}");
1890 |         sb.AppendLine($"Местность: {startLoc.Terrain} | Опасность: {startLoc.Danger}/10");
1891 | 
1892 |         var exits = startLoc.Exits.Count > 0
1893 |             ? string.Join(", ", startLoc.Exits.Select(e =>
1894 |             {
1895 |                 var toName = _world.GetLocation(e.To)?.Name ?? e.To.ToString();
1896 |                 return $"{e.Direction} → {toName}";
1897 |             }))
1898 |             : "нет";
1899 |         sb.AppendLine($"Выходы: {exits}");
1900 | 
1901 |         var npcs = startLoc.Npcs.Count > 0
1902 |             ? string.Join(", ", startLoc.Npcs.Select(id => _world.GetNpc(id)).Where(n => n is not null).Select(n => $"{n!.Name} ({n!.Race ?? "?"}/{n!.Class ?? "?"})"))
1903 |             : "нет";
1904 |         sb.AppendLine($"Обитатели: {npcs}");
1905 | 
1906 |         var buildings = startLoc.Buildings.Count > 0
1907 |             ? string.Join(", ", startLoc.Buildings.Select(id => _world.GetBuilding(id)).Where(b => b is not null).Select(b => b!.Name))
1908 |             : "нет";
1909 |         sb.AppendLine($"Здания: {buildings}");
1910 |         return sb.ToString();
1911 |     }
1912 | 
1913 |     // ─── Stage 2 helper: ruleset designer (issue #21) ──────────────────
1914 | 
1915 |     /// <summary>
1916 |     /// Heuristic: detect non-fantasy themes that warrant a custom ruleset
1917 |     /// overlay. The list covers the genre keywords the planner prompt
1918 |     /// uses (cyberpunk / sci-fi / steampunk / postapoc / horror / modern).
1919 |     /// Standard fantasy themes (dark fantasy / sword &amp; sorcery / high
1920 |     /// fantasy / etc.) fall through and use DefaultDnd as-is.
1921 |     /// </summary>
1922 |     private static bool IsNonFantasyTheme(string? theme)
1923 |     {
1924 |         if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(theme)) return false;
1925 |         var lower = theme.ToLowerInvariant();
1926 |         return lower.Contains("cyber")
1927 |             || lower.Contains("sci-fi") || lower.Contains("scifi") || lower.Contains("sci ")
1928 |             || lower.Contains("steam")
1929 |             || lower.Contains("postapoc") || lower.Contains("post-apoc") || lower.Contains("постапок")
1930 |             || lower.Contains("horror") || lower.Contains("хоррор")
1931 |             || lower.Contains("modern") || lower.Contains("современ")
1932 |             || lower.Contains("space") || lower.Contains("косм")
1933 |             || lower.Contains("cyberpunk") || lower.Contains("кибер");
1934 |     }
1935 | 
1936 |     /// <summary>
1937 |     /// Call the ruleset-designer AI for a non-fantasy world. Asks the
1938 |     /// model for a lightweight JSON overlay (custom attribute display
1939 |     /// names + custom resource pool names + custom skill list). Returns
1940 |     /// null when the model doesn't return a valid JSON block (non-fatal
1941 |     /// — the caller falls back to DefaultDnd).
1942 |     /// </summary>
1943 |     private async Task<RulesetDesignOverlay?> RunRulesetDesignerAsync(
1944 |         WorldPlan plan,
1945 |         CancellationToken ct)
1946 |     {
1947 |         var systemPrompt =
1948 |             "Ты — архитектор правил мира. Подбери подходящую систему для не-фэнтезийного мира. " +
1949 |             "Работай на русском. Верни ТОЛЬКО JSON-объект в блоке ```json ... ```. Без другого текста.\n\n" +
1950 |             "Формат JSON:\n" +
1951 |             "```json\n" +
1952 |             "{\n" +
1953 |             "  \"attributeNames\": {\"str\": \"Сила\", \"dex\": \"Ловкость\", \"con\": \"Телосложение\", " +
1954 |             "\"int\": \"Интеллект\", \"wis\": \"Восприятие\", \"cha\": \"Харизма\"},\n" +
1955 |             "  \"resourcePools\": {\"hp\": \"Здоровье\"},\n" +
1956 |             "  \"skills\": [\"атлетика\", \"обман\", \"история\", ...]\n" +
1957 |             "}\n" +
1958 |             "```\n\n" +
1959 |             "Ключи в attributeNames/resourcePools — стандартные (str/dex/con/int/wis/cha и hp/ac). " +
1960 |             "Значения — человекочитаемые имена для UI и для GM-контекста. " +
1961 |             "Список skills — 6-15 ключевых навыков мира (например, для киберпанка: «хакинг», «стрельба», « streetwise», «техника», «обман», «запугивание»). " +
1962 |             "Адаптируй под тему мира; не дублируй стандартный D&D-список вслепую.";
1963 | 
1964 |         var userPrompt =
1965 |             $"Разработай ruleset для мира темы «{plan.Theme}». " +
1966 |             $"Название мира: «{plan.Title}». Сеттинг: {plan.Setting}. Атмосфера: {plan.Atmosphere}.\n\n" +
1967 |             "Определи:\n" +
1968 |             "1. Имена характеристик (вместо STR/DEX/CON/INT/WIS/CHA — например для киберпанка: Cool/Edge/Meat/Tech/Luck/Will).\n" +
1969 |             "2. Пулы ресурсов (вместо hp/ac — например stress/cred/humanity для киберпанка, sanity для хоррора).\n" +
1970 |             "3. Список навыков (6-15) под тему мира.\n\n" +
1971 |             "Верни JSON-объект в блоке ```json ... ```.";
1972 | 
1973 |         var messages = new List<ChatMessage>
1974 |         {
1975 |             ChatMessage.System(systemPrompt),
1976 |             ChatMessage.User(userPrompt),
1977 |         };
1978 | 
1979 |         // Use the planner role's model for the ruleset design (creative
1980 |         // single-shot call — same profile as the planner).
1981 |         var rulesetAi = _aiSettings is null ? _ai : _ai.WithModel(_aiSettings.GetModelForRole(AiRole.Planner));
1982 |         var response = await rulesetAi.ChatAsync(messages, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
1983 |         var json = ExtractJsonBlock(response.Content ?? string.Empty);
1984 |         if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(json)) return null;
1985 | 
1986 |         try
1987 |         {
1988 |             var opts = new System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions
1989 |             {
1990 |                 PropertyNamingPolicy = System.Text.Json.JsonNamingPolicy.CamelCase,
1991 |                 PropertyNameCaseInsensitive = true,
1992 |             };
1993 |             return System.Text.Json.JsonSerializer.Deserialize<RulesetDesignOverlay>(json, opts);
1994 |         }
1995 |         catch (System.Text.Json.JsonException)
1996 |         {
1997 |             // Malformed JSON — return null so the caller falls back to DefaultDnd.
1998 |             return null;
1999 |         }
2000 |     }
2001 | 
2002 |     /// <summary>
2003 |     /// Render a one-line summary of the applied ruleset overlay for the
2004 |     /// progress UI (e.g. «Атрибуты: Cool/Edge/Meat/Tech/Luck/Will · Ресурсы: Stress · Навыков: 8»).
2005 |     /// </summary>
2006 |     private static string DescribeRulesetOverlay(RulesetDesignOverlay overlay)
2007 |     {
2008 |         var sb = new StringBuilder();
2009 |         if (overlay.AttributeNames is { Count: > 0 } an)
2010 |         {
2011 |             sb.Append("Атрибуты: ");
2012 |             sb.Append(string.Join("/", an.Values));
2013 |         }
2014 |         if (overlay.ResourcePools is { Count: > 0 } rp)
2015 |         {
2016 |             if (sb.Length > 0) sb.Append(" · ");
2017 |             sb.Append("Ресурсы: ");
2018 |             sb.Append(string.Join("/", rp.Values));
2019 |         }
2020 |         if (overlay.Skills is { Count: > 0 } sk)
2021 |         {
2022 |             if (sb.Length > 0) sb.Append(" · ");
2023 |             sb.Append($"Навыков: {sk.Count}");
2024 |         }
2025 |         return sb.Length == 0 ? "кастомная система" : sb.ToString();
2026 |     }
2027 | 
2028 |     /// <summary>
2029 |     /// Lightweight ruleset design returned by the ruleset-designer AI
2030 |     /// call. Applied as an overlay on top of
2031 |     /// <see cref="Rulesets.DefaultDnd"/> (the underlying attribute /
2032 |     /// resource / formula structure stays D&amp;D 5e; only the display
2033 |     /// names + skill list change). All fields optional.
2034 |     /// </summary>
2035 |     public sealed record RulesetDesignOverlay
2036 |     {
2037 |         public Dictionary<string, string>? AttributeNames { get; init; }
2038 |         public Dictionary<string, string>? ResourcePools { get; init; }
2039 |         public List<string>? Skills { get; init; }
2040 |     }
2041 | }

--- src/MyGame.Core/AI/AiClient.cs (C#, 983 lines, ~11,956 tokens) ---
   1 | using System.Net;
   2 | using System.Net.Http.Headers;
   3 | using System.Runtime.CompilerServices;
   4 | using System.Text;
   5 | using System.Text.Json;
   6 | using System.Text.Json.Nodes;
   7 | 
   8 | namespace MyGame.Core.AI;
   9 | 
  10 | /// <summary>
  11 | /// Typed exception for AI client failures. The agent layer catches
  12 | /// <see cref="AiException"/> specifically and surfaces a user-visible
  13 | /// error; everything else propagates as an unexpected fault.
  14 | /// </summary>
  15 | public sealed class AiException : Exception
  16 | {
  17 |     /// <summary>
  18 |     /// Stable category for branching in the agent layer. Derived from
  19 |     /// <see cref="Status"/> when constructed via
  20 |     /// <see cref="AiException(HttpStatusCode, string)"/>; explicit when
  21 |     /// constructed via the richer
  22 |     /// <see cref="AiException(AiErrorKind, string, HttpStatusCode?, Exception?)"/>
  23 |     /// overload (used for parse/cancel/timeout/network faults that don't
  24 |     /// have an HTTP status).
  25 |     /// </summary>
  26 |     public AiErrorKind Kind { get; }
  27 | 
  28 |     /// <summary>HTTP status code, when this came from a non-2xx response.</summary>
  29 |     public HttpStatusCode? Status { get; }
  30 | 
  31 |     /// <summary>
  32 |     /// Spec-required constructor: build an <see cref="AiException"/> from
  33 |     /// an HTTP status code + message. The <see cref="Kind"/> is derived
  34 |     /// from the status (401/403 → Auth, 429 → RateLimit, 5xx →
  35 |     /// ServerError, other 4xx → BadRequest).
  36 |     /// </summary>
  37 |     public AiException(HttpStatusCode statusCode, string message)
  38 |         : base(message)
  39 |     {
  40 |         Status = statusCode;
  41 |         Kind = ClassifyStatus(statusCode);
  42 |     }
  43 | 
  44 |     /// <summary>
  45 |     /// Spec-required constructor (int overload): build an
  46 |     /// <see cref="AiException"/> from a numeric HTTP status code + message.
  47 |     /// Convenience for callers that have the status as an int.
  48 |     /// </summary>
  49 |     public AiException(int statusCode, string message)
  50 |         : this((HttpStatusCode)statusCode, message) { }
  51 | 
  52 |     /// <summary>
  53 |     /// Richer constructor for non-HTTP failures (parse errors, timeouts,
  54 |     /// network faults, cancellation) where there is no status code or
  55 |     /// where the caller wants to flag a specific <see cref="AiErrorKind"/>.
  56 |     /// Kept for the agent layer's <c>catch (AiException)</c> handlers.
  57 |     /// </summary>
  58 |     public AiException(AiErrorKind kind, string message, HttpStatusCode? status = null, Exception? inner = null)
  59 |         : base(message, inner)
  60 |     {
  61 |         Kind = kind;
  62 |         Status = status;
  63 |     }
  64 | 
  65 |     private static AiErrorKind ClassifyStatus(HttpStatusCode status) => status switch
  66 |     {
  67 |         HttpStatusCode.Unauthorized => AiErrorKind.Auth,
  68 |         HttpStatusCode.Forbidden => AiErrorKind.Auth,
  69 |         HttpStatusCode.TooManyRequests => AiErrorKind.RateLimit,
  70 |         >= (HttpStatusCode)500 and <= (HttpStatusCode)599 => AiErrorKind.ServerError,
  71 |         >= (HttpStatusCode)400 and <= (HttpStatusCode)499 => AiErrorKind.BadRequest,
  72 |         _ => AiErrorKind.BadRequest,
  73 |     };
  74 | }
  75 | 
  76 | /// <summary>Stable category of AI provider failure.</summary>
  77 | public enum AiErrorKind
  78 | {
  79 |     /// <summary>401/403 — bad/missing API key. Not retried.</summary>
  80 |     Auth,
  81 | 
  82 |     /// <summary>429 — rate limited. Retried once with a 2-second backoff.</summary>
  83 |     RateLimit,
  84 | 
  85 |     /// <summary>5xx — provider fault. Not retried.</summary>
  86 |     ServerError,
  87 | 
  88 |     /// <summary>Other 4xx — malformed request, model not found, etc. Not retried.</summary>
  89 |     BadRequest,
  90 | 
  91 |     /// <summary>HTTP request timed out or the connection dropped.</summary>
  92 |     Timeout,
  93 | 
  94 |     /// <summary>DNS/connect failure.</summary>
  95 |     Network,
  96 | 
  97 |     /// <summary>Provider returned a 200 but the body wasn't valid OpenAI JSON.</summary>
  98 |     Parse,
  99 | 
 100 |     /// <summary>Caller cancelled via <see cref="CancellationToken"/>.</summary>
 101 |     Cancelled,
 102 | }
 103 | 
 104 | /// <summary>
 105 | /// OpenAI-compatible HTTP client. Port of <c>ai/aiClient.ts</c>,
 106 | /// rewritten to use <see cref="HttpClient"/> instead of the original
 107 | /// cURL-subprocess hack.
 108 | ///
 109 | /// <para>
 110 | /// The TS source shelled out to <c>curl</c> via <c>child_process.spawn</c>
 111 | /// — that was a workaround for some proxy/SDK issues in the Next.js
 112 | /// server. In the C#/.NET 8 desktop rewrite we use a plain
 113 | /// <see cref="HttpClient"/> with <c>HttpCompletionOption.ResponseHeadersRead</c>
 114 | /// for streaming, which is the idiomatic approach and avoids any
 115 | /// subprocess management.
 116 | /// </para>
 117 | ///
 118 | /// <para><b>Endpoints:</b>
 119 | /// <list type="bullet">
 120 | ///  <item><see cref="ChatAsync"/>: non-streaming POST /chat/completions.</item>
 121 | ///  <item><see cref="ChatWithToolsAsync"/>: same, with a <c>tools</c> field.</item>
 122 | ///  <item><see cref="StreamChatAsync"/>: streaming POST, yields content deltas.</item>
 123 | /// </list>
 124 | /// </para>
 125 | ///
 126 | /// <para><b>Retry policy</b> (per task spec): 429 (rate-limit) is retried
 127 | /// once after a 2-second backoff. 401/403/5xx and other 4xx throw
 128 | /// immediately. Network/timeout faults throw immediately as
 129 | /// <see cref="AiErrorKind.Network"/>/<see cref="AiErrorKind.Timeout"/>.
 130 | /// </para>
 131 | ///
 132 | /// <para><b>Auth</b>: the <c>Authorization: Bearer {ApiKey}</c> header is
 133 | /// sent when <see cref="AiSettings.ApiKey"/> is non-empty; skipped
 134 | /// otherwise so local providers that don't require auth work without
 135 | /// configuration.
 136 | /// </para>
 137 | ///
 138 | /// <para><b>Local-provider compatibility (issue #27):</b>
 139 | /// The client works with any OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Verified
 140 | /// providers + their quirks:
 141 | /// <list type="bullet">
 142 | ///  <item><b>Ollama</b> (<c>http://localhost:11434/v1</c>): no API key
 143 | ///    required — leave <see cref="AiSettings.ApiKey"/> null and the
 144 | ///    <c>Authorization</c> header is skipped (Ollama rejects an empty
 145 | ///    Bearer token with 401 on some builds). Tool calling requires
 146 | ///    llama3.1+ / mistral / qwen2 — older models silently ignore the
 147 | ///    <c>tools</c> field. Streaming works via SSE; the final
 148 | ///    <c>usage</c> block is only emitted when
 149 | ///    <c>stream_options.include_usage=true</c> (we always set it). Ollama
 150 | ///    reports prompt/completion tokens as <c>prompt_tokens</c> /
 151 | ///    <c>completion_tokens</c> (matching OpenAI's schema), so our
 152 | ///    <see cref="StreamUsage"/> + <see cref="ParseChatResponse"/> parsers
 153 | ///    handle it without provider-specific branching. One quirk: Ollama's
 154 | ///    <c>usage</c> block on streaming arrives in a SEPARATE chunk after
 155 | ///    the last <c>choices</c> chunk, often with an empty
 156 | ///    <c>choices: []</c> array — our streaming loop already handles this
 157 | ///    (the <c>if (chunk.Usage is { } usage)</c> check is OUTSIDE the
 158 | ///    choices branch).</item>
 159 | ///  <item><b>llama.cpp server</b> (<c>http://localhost:8080/v1</c>):
 160 | ///    same OpenAI-compatible surface as Ollama. No auth required. Tool
 161 | ///    calling supported on GGUF models with tool-call templates
 162 | ///    (chatml-gemma, llama3, mistral). Smaller context windows (4k–8k
 163 | ///    typical) — set <see cref="Settings.MaxContextTokens"/> accordingly
 164 | ///    so the GM's summarization triggers before the model runs out of
 165 | ///    context.</item>
 166 | ///  <item><b>DeepSeek</b> (<c>https://api.deepseek.com/v1</c>): standard
 167 | ///    OpenAI-compatible cloud. API key required. Tool calling supported.
 168 | ///    No quirks observed.</item>
 169 | ///  <item><b>OpenAI</b> (<c>https://api.openai.com/v1</c>): the
 170 | ///    reference implementation — works out of the box.</item>
 171 | /// </list>
 172 | /// </para>
 173 | ///
 174 | /// NO references to the z-ai-web-dev-sdk. This is pure HTTP.
 175 | /// </summary>
 176 | public sealed class AiClient : IAiClient
 177 | {
 178 |     /// <summary>
 179 |     /// Shared static <see cref="HttpClient"/> used when the caller doesn't
 180 |     /// inject one. Per the .NET docs, sharing one instance across all
 181 |     /// requests avoids socket exhaustion under load. The instance is
 182 |     /// intentionally NOT disposed (its lifetime is the process lifetime).
 183 |     /// </summary>
 184 |     private static readonly HttpClient s_sharedHttp = new();
 185 | 
 186 |     private static readonly JsonSerializerOptions s_wireOptions = new()
 187 |     {
 188 |         // OpenAI-compatible API uses camelCase keys on the wire.
 189 |         PropertyNamingPolicy = JsonNamingPolicy.CamelCase,
 190 |         PropertyNameCaseInsensitive = true,
 191 |         DefaultIgnoreCondition = System.Text.Json.Serialization.JsonIgnoreCondition.WhenWritingNull,
 192 |     };
 193 | 
 194 |     private readonly AiSettings _settings;
 195 |     private readonly HttpClient _http;
 196 | 
 197 |     /// <summary>
 198 |     /// Create a client using the shared static <see cref="HttpClient"/>.
 199 |     /// Convenience for tests and ad-hoc use; for production prefer the
 200 |     /// <c>(AiSettings, HttpClient)</c> ctor so a single socket pool is
 201 |     /// shared.
 202 |     /// </summary>
 203 |     public AiClient(AiSettings settings)
 204 |         : this(settings, s_sharedHttp) { }
 205 | 
 206 |     /// <summary>
 207 |     /// Create a client wrapping the given <see cref="HttpClient"/>. Use this
 208 |     /// ctor when the host (Avalonia DI container) manages the
 209 |     /// <see cref="HttpClient"/> lifecycle (recommended — lets you share a
 210 |     /// single <c>HttpClientHandler</c> / socket pool across all clients).
 211 |     /// </summary>
 212 |     public AiClient(AiSettings settings, HttpClient http)
 213 |     {
 214 |         _settings = settings ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(settings));
 215 |         _http = http ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(http));
 216 |     }
 217 | 
 218 |     /// <summary>
 219 |     /// The settings this client was constructed with. Exposed (rare for a
 220 |     /// fields-as-state class) so callers that hold an <see cref="AiClient"/>
 221 |     /// can read back the resolved <see cref="AiSettings.Model"/> etc. without
 222 |     /// also keeping a separate settings reference.
 223 |     /// </summary>
 224 |     public AiSettings Settings => _settings;
 225 | 
 226 |     /// <summary>
 227 |     /// Return a NEW <see cref="AiClient"/> that shares this client's
 228 |     /// <see cref="HttpClient"/> (socket pool, handler chain) but uses the
 229 |     /// given <paramref name="model"/> id instead of
 230 |     /// <see cref="AiSettings.Model"/>. Used by the agent layer
 231 |     /// (<see cref="Agents.GameMaster"/>,
 232 |     /// <see cref="Agents.WorldBuilderOrchestrator"/>,
 233 |     /// <see cref="Agents.PetAgent"/>) to derive a role-specific client from
 234 |     /// a single shared base client (issue #26: multi-model support).
 235 |     ///
 236 |     /// <para>
 237 |     /// The returned client is a thin wrapper — it doesn't open a new socket
 238 |     /// pool, doesn't allocate a new handler, and reuses the same retry /
 239 |     /// streaming / parsing logic. Calling <c>WithModel</c> on a client that
 240 |     /// already has a role-specific model just produces another derived
 241 |     /// client (the latest <paramref name="model"/> wins).
 242 |     /// </para>
 243 |     /// </summary>
 244 |     /// <param name="model">
 245 |     /// Model id to use on the derived client. If null/empty, the derived
 246 |     /// client keeps this client's model (no-op derivation — useful when
 247 |     /// the caller is conditionally overriding: <c>ai.WithModel(roleModel ?? ai.Settings.Model)</c>).
 248 |     /// </param>
 249 |     public AiClient WithModel(string? model)
 250 |     {
 251 |         if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(model) || model == _settings.Model)
 252 |             return this;
 253 |         return new AiClient(_settings with { Model = model }, _http);
 254 |     }
 255 | 
 256 |     // ── Explicit IAiClient implementation ─────────────────────────────
 257 |     //
 258 |     // The interface declares WithModel as returning IAiClient. The public
 259 |     // AiClient-typed overload above is the one callers use day-to-day
 260 |     // (preserving the existing API for code that holds an AiClient
 261 |     // reference — e.g. PetAgent, WorldBuilderOrchestrator). The explicit
 262 |     // implementation below bridges to the interface by delegating to the
 263 |     // public overload; this lets GameMaster (which now depends on IAiClient)
 264 |     // call WithModel through the interface while other agents that hold a
 265 |     // concrete AiClient still get the AiClient-typed return. C# does not
 266 |     // allow covariant return types on implicit interface implementations,
 267 |     // so the explicit bridge is required.
 268 | 
 269 |     IAiClient IAiClient.WithModel(string? model) => WithModel(model);
 270 | 
 271 |     // ── Public API ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 272 | 
 273 |     /// <summary>
 274 |     /// Non-streaming chat completion (no tools). POST /chat/completions
 275 |     /// with <c>stream:false</c>. Returns the parsed <see cref="ChatResponse"/>
 276 |     /// on success; throws <see cref="AiException"/> on any failure
 277 |     /// (auth/rate-limit/server/bad-request/network/timeout/parse).
 278 |     /// </summary>
 279 |     public Task<ChatResponse> ChatAsync(
 280 |         IReadOnlyList<ChatMessage> messages,
 281 |         CancellationToken ct = default) =>
 282 |         ChatWithToolsAsync(messages, tools: null, ct);
 283 | 
 284 |     /// <summary>
 285 |     /// Non-streaming chat completion with optional function-calling tools.
 286 |     /// POST /chat/completions with <c>stream:false</c> and a <c>tools</c>
 287 |     /// field when <paramref name="tools"/> is non-null/non-empty.
 288 |     /// </summary>
 289 |     public async Task<ChatResponse> ChatWithToolsAsync(
 290 |         IReadOnlyList<ChatMessage> messages,
 291 |         IReadOnlyList<ToolDefinition>? tools,
 292 |         CancellationToken ct = default)
 293 |     {
 294 |         if (messages is null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(messages));
 295 | 
 296 |         var payload = BuildRequestBody(messages, tools, stream: false);
 297 |         using var response = await SendWithRetryAsync(payload, stream: false, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 298 |         var body = await response.Content.ReadAsStringAsync(ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 299 |         return ParseChatResponse(body);
 300 |     }
 301 | 
 302 |     /// <summary>
 303 |     /// Streaming chat completion. POST /chat/completions with
 304 |     /// <c>stream:true</c> and <c>stream_options.include_usage:true</c>,
 305 |     /// then yields each <c>delta.content</c> chunk as it arrives.
 306 |     ///
 307 |     /// <para>
 308 |     /// The yielded strings are CONTENT DELTAS only — tool-call deltas are
 309 |     /// accumulated internally and discarded, and the final token-usage
 310 |     /// block (if the provider emits one in the stream) is parsed and
 311 |     /// discarded. If you need tool calls or final usage, use the
 312 |     /// non-streaming <see cref="ChatWithToolsAsync"/> overload.
 313 |     /// </para>
 314 |     ///
 315 |     /// <para>
 316 |     /// Uses <see cref="HttpClient.SendAsync(HttpRequestMessage,
 317 |     /// HttpCompletionOption, CancellationToken)"/> with
 318 |     /// <see cref="HttpCompletionOption.ResponseHeadersRead"/> and reads the
 319 |     /// response stream line-by-line, parsing the SSE <c>data:</c> framing
 320 |     /// per the OpenAI streaming protocol.
 321 |     /// </para>
 322 |     /// </summary>
 323 |     /// <param name="messages">Conversation history.</param>
 324 |     /// <param name="ct">Cancellation token — aborts the HTTP request mid-stream.</param>
 325 |     /// <returns>
 326 |     /// An <see cref="IAsyncEnumerable{T}"/> of content-delta strings. The
 327 |     /// enumeration completes when the provider sends <c>data: [DONE]</c>
 328 |     /// or closes the connection.
 329 |     /// </returns>
 330 |     public async IAsyncEnumerable<string> StreamChatAsync(
 331 |         IReadOnlyList<ChatMessage> messages,
 332 |         [EnumeratorCancellation] CancellationToken ct = default)
 333 |     {
 334 |         if (messages is null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(messages));
 335 | 
 336 |         var payload = BuildRequestBody(messages, tools: null, stream: true);
 337 |         using var response = await SendWithRetryAsync(payload, stream: true, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 338 |         using var stream = await response.Content.ReadAsStreamAsync(ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 339 |         using var reader = new StreamReader(stream, Encoding.UTF8);
 340 | 
 341 |         while (true)
 342 |         {
 343 |             string? line;
 344 |             try
 345 |             {
 346 |                 line = await reader.ReadLineAsync(ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 347 |             }
 348 |             catch (OperationCanceledException) { yield break; }
 349 |             if (line is null) yield break; // connection closed
 350 | 
 351 |             if (line.Length == 0) continue;            // SSE event boundary
 352 |             if (!line.StartsWith("data:", StringComparison.Ordinal)) continue;
 353 |             var data = line.Substring(5).Trim();
 354 |             if (data == "[DONE]") yield break;
 355 | 
 356 |             ChatStreamChunk? chunk;
 357 |             try
 358 |             {
 359 |                 chunk = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<ChatStreamChunk>(data, s_wireOptions);
 360 |             }
 361 |             catch (JsonException)
 362 |             {
 363 |                 // Provider sent malformed JSON in one chunk — skip it; the
 364 |                 // stream often has a few such blips on reconnect.
 365 |                 continue;
 366 |             }
 367 |             if (chunk is null) continue;
 368 | 
 369 |             if (chunk.Choices is { } choices && choices.Count > 0)
 370 |             {
 371 |                 var delta = choices[0].Delta;
 372 |                 if (delta is null) continue;
 373 | 
 374 |                 if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(delta.Content))
 375 |                     yield return delta.Content!;
 376 |             }
 377 |         }
 378 |     }
 379 | 
 380 |     /// <summary>
 381 |     /// Streaming chat completion WITH optional function-calling tools.
 382 |     /// POST /chat/completions with <c>stream:true</c> + <c>tools</c> +
 383 |     /// <c>stream_options.include_usage:true</c>. Yields each
 384 |     /// <c>delta.content</c> chunk as it arrives, while ACCUMULATING
 385 |     /// tool-call deltas (keyed by their <c>index</c>, since
 386 |     /// <c>function.arguments</c> arrives in fragments across multiple
 387 |     /// chunks) into a final <see cref="ChatResponse"/> that the caller
 388 |     /// can read after the enumeration completes.
 389 |     ///
 390 |     /// <para>
 391 |     /// The OpenAI streaming-with-tools protocol works like this:
 392 |     /// each SSE chunk's <c>choices[0].delta</c> may contain a
 393 |     /// <c>content</c> string fragment (the assistant's narration),
 394 |     /// one or more <c>tool_calls</c> entries (each identified by
 395 |     /// <c>index</c>, with <c>id</c>, <c>function.name</c>, and
 396 |     /// <c>function.arguments</c> arriving in successive fragments),
 397 |     /// or just a <c>finish_reason</c>. The final chunk(s) carry a
 398 |     /// top-level <c>usage</c> block (when <c>include_usage</c> is set).
 399 |     /// We assemble all of this into a single
 400 |     /// <see cref="ChatResponse"/> on stream end.
 401 |     /// </para>
 402 |     ///
 403 |     /// <para>
 404 |     /// The final response is stored in
 405 |     /// <paramref name="result"/>'s <see cref="StreamChatResult.Response"/>
 406 |     /// field. The pattern is:
 407 |     /// <code>
 408 |     /// var holder = new StreamChatResult();
 409 |     /// await foreach (var delta in client.StreamChatWithToolsAsync(
 410 |     ///     messages, tools, holder, ct))
 411 |     /// {
 412 |     ///     // forward delta to UI
 413 |     /// }
 414 |     /// var response = holder.Response ?? new ChatResponse();
 415 |     /// // response.Content / response.ToolCalls / response.PromptTokens ...
 416 |     /// </code>
 417 |     /// </para>
 418 |     ///
 419 |     /// <para>
 420 |     /// If the stream is cancelled mid-flight (caller's
 421 |     /// <paramref name="ct"/> fires), <see cref="StreamChatResult.Response"/>
 422 |     /// is left null and <see cref="OperationCanceledException"/> propagates
 423 |     /// from the consuming <c>await foreach</c>.
 424 |     /// </para>
 425 |     /// </summary>
 426 |     /// <param name="messages">Conversation history (will be serialised
 427 |     /// verbatim — assistant messages with <c>tool_calls</c> and tool
 428 |     /// result messages are sent as-is per the OpenAI wire format).</param>
 429 |     /// <param name="tools">Tool definitions (null or empty = no tools,
 430 |     /// equivalent to <see cref="StreamChatAsync"/>).</param>
 431 |     /// <param name="result">Holder the final <see cref="ChatResponse"/>
 432 |     /// is written into when the stream completes normally.</param>
 433 |     /// <param name="ct">Cancellation token — aborts the HTTP request
 434 |     /// mid-stream.</param>
 435 |     public async IAsyncEnumerable<string> StreamChatWithToolsAsync(
 436 |         IReadOnlyList<ChatMessage> messages,
 437 |         IReadOnlyList<ToolDefinition>? tools,
 438 |         StreamChatResult result,
 439 |         [EnumeratorCancellation] CancellationToken ct = default)
 440 |     {
 441 |         if (messages is null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(messages));
 442 |         if (result is null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(result));
 443 | 
 444 |         var payload = BuildRequestBody(messages, tools, stream: true);
 445 |         using var response = await SendWithRetryAsync(payload, stream: true, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 446 |         using var stream = await response.Content.ReadAsStreamAsync(ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 447 |         using var reader = new StreamReader(stream, Encoding.UTF8);
 448 | 
 449 |         var contentBuilder = new StringBuilder();
 450 |         var toolCallsByIndex = new SortedDictionary<int, ToolCallAccumulator>();
 451 |         string? finishReason = null;
 452 |         int promptTokens = 0, completionTokens = 0;
 453 | 
 454 |         while (true)
 455 |         {
 456 |             string? line;
 457 |             try
 458 |             {
 459 |                 line = await reader.ReadLineAsync(ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 460 |             }
 461 |             catch (OperationCanceledException) { yield break; }
 462 |             if (line is null) break;                  // connection closed
 463 |             if (line.Length == 0) continue;            // SSE event boundary
 464 |             if (!line.StartsWith("data:", StringComparison.Ordinal)) continue;
 465 |             var data = line.Substring(5).Trim();
 466 |             if (data == "[DONE]") break;
 467 | 
 468 |             ChatStreamChunk? chunk;
 469 |             try
 470 |             {
 471 |                 chunk = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<ChatStreamChunk>(data, s_wireOptions);
 472 |             }
 473 |             catch (JsonException)
 474 |             {
 475 |                 // Provider sent malformed JSON in one chunk — skip it; the
 476 |                 // stream often has a few such blips on reconnect.
 477 |                 continue;
 478 |             }
 479 |             if (chunk is null) continue;
 480 | 
 481 |             if (chunk.Choices is { } choices && choices.Count > 0)
 482 |             {
 483 |                 var choice = choices[0];
 484 |                 if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(choice.FinishReason))
 485 |                     finishReason = choice.FinishReason;
 486 | 
 487 |                 var delta = choice.Delta;
 488 |                 if (delta is null) continue;
 489 | 
 490 |                 // Content delta — yield to caller immediately.
 491 |                 if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(delta.Content))
 492 |                 {
 493 |                     contentBuilder.Append(delta.Content);
 494 |                     yield return delta.Content!;
 495 |                 }
 496 | 
 497 |                 // Tool-call deltas — accumulate by index. The first
 498 |                 // chunk for a given index carries id + function.name;
 499 |                 // subsequent chunks for the same index carry only
 500 |                 // function.arguments fragments (string concatenation).
 501 |                 if (delta.ToolCalls is { } tcs)
 502 |                 {
 503 |                     foreach (var tc in tcs)
 504 |                     {
 505 |                         var idx = tc.Index ?? 0;
 506 |                         if (!toolCallsByIndex.TryGetValue(idx, out var acc))
 507 |                         {
 508 |                             acc = new ToolCallAccumulator();
 509 |                             toolCallsByIndex[idx] = acc;
 510 |                         }
 511 |                         if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(tc.Id))
 512 |                             acc.Id = tc.Id;
 513 |                         if (tc.Function is { } fn)
 514 |                         {
 515 |                             if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(fn.Name))
 516 |                                 acc.Name = fn.Name;
 517 |                             if (fn.Arguments is { } args)
 518 |                                 acc.Arguments.Append(args);
 519 |                         }
 520 |                     }
 521 |                 }
 522 |             }
 523 | 
 524 |             // Final usage chunk (sentinelled by include_usage=true) —
 525 |             // arrives AFTER the choices are exhausted, often with an
 526 |             // empty `choices` array.
 527 |             if (chunk.Usage is { } usage)
 528 |             {
 529 |                 promptTokens = usage.PromptTokens;
 530 |                 completionTokens = usage.CompletionTokens;
 531 |             }
 532 |         }
 533 | 
 534 |         // Build the final ChatResponse from accumulated state. This runs
 535 |         // once the stream ends (either via [DONE] or connection close),
 536 |         // before the consumer's `await foreach` exits.
 537 |         var toolCallsList = new List<ToolCall>();
 538 |         foreach (var acc in toolCallsByIndex.Values)
 539 |         {
 540 |             if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(acc.Name)) continue; // malformed — drop
 541 |             toolCallsList.Add(new ToolCall
 542 |             {
 543 |                 Id = acc.Id ?? string.Empty,
 544 |                 Name = acc.Name!,
 545 |                 Arguments = acc.Arguments.Length > 0 ? acc.Arguments.ToString() : "{}",
 546 |             });
 547 |         }
 548 | 
 549 |         result.Response = new ChatResponse
 550 |         {
 551 |             Content = contentBuilder.Length > 0 ? contentBuilder.ToString() : null,
 552 |             ToolCalls = toolCallsList.Count > 0 ? toolCallsList : null,
 553 |             FinishReason = finishReason,
 554 |             PromptTokens = promptTokens,
 555 |             CompletionTokens = completionTokens,
 556 |         };
 557 |     }
 558 | 
 559 |     // ── Internals ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 560 | 
 561 |     /// <summary>
 562 |     /// Build the OpenAI-compatible <c>/chat/completions</c> request body
 563 |     /// as a JSON string. Uses <see cref="JsonObject"/> for ergonomic
 564 |     /// construction (the body mixes typed messages with raw-schema objects,
 565 |     /// which is awkward with <see cref="Utf8JsonWriter"/>).
 566 |     /// </summary>
 567 |     private string BuildRequestBody(
 568 |         IReadOnlyList<ChatMessage> messages,
 569 |         IReadOnlyList<ToolDefinition>? tools,
 570 |         bool stream)
 571 |     {
 572 |         var root = new JsonObject
 573 |         {
 574 |             ["model"] = _settings.Model,
 575 |             ["messages"] = BuildMessagesArray(messages),
 576 |             ["temperature"] = _settings.Temperature,
 577 |             ["max_tokens"] = _settings.MaxTokens,
 578 |             ["stream"] = stream,
 579 |         };
 580 | 
 581 |         if (stream)
 582 |         {
 583 |             // Critical for streaming token counter: OpenAI-compatible
 584 |             // providers only emit a `usage` block in the SSE stream when
 585 |             // the client explicitly asks for it via stream_options.include_usage.
 586 |             root["stream_options"] = new JsonObject { ["include_usage"] = true };
 587 |         }
 588 | 
 589 |         if (tools is { } toolList && toolList.Count > 0)
 590 |         {
 591 |             var toolsArray = new JsonArray();
 592 |             foreach (var t in toolList)
 593 |             {
 594 |                 var fn = new JsonObject
 595 |                 {
 596 |                     ["name"] = t.Name,
 597 |                     ["description"] = t.Description,
 598 |                 };
 599 |                 // ParametersJson is a JSON-schema STRING; embed it as a
 600 |                 // raw JSON value (not a string) so the provider parses it
