 601 |                 // as an object. If the schema is empty/invalid, fall back
 602 |                 // to an empty object.
 603 |                 JsonNode? schemaNode;
 604 |                 try
 605 |                 {
 606 |                     schemaNode = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(t.ParametersJson)
 607 |                         ? new JsonObject()
 608 |                         : JsonNode.Parse(t.ParametersJson) ?? new JsonObject();
 609 |                 }
 610 |                 catch (JsonException)
 611 |                 {
 612 |                     schemaNode = new JsonObject();
 613 |                 }
 614 |                 fn["parameters"] = schemaNode;
 615 |                 toolsArray.Add(new JsonObject
 616 |                 {
 617 |                     ["type"] = "function",
 618 |                     ["function"] = fn,
 619 |                 });
 620 |             }
 621 |             root["tools"] = toolsArray;
 622 |         }
 623 | 
 624 |         return root.ToJsonString(s_wireOptions);
 625 |     }
 626 | 
 627 |     private static JsonArray BuildMessagesArray(IReadOnlyList<ChatMessage> messages)
 628 |     {
 629 |         var arr = new JsonArray();
 630 |         foreach (var m in messages)
 631 |         {
 632 |             var obj = new JsonObject
 633 |             {
 634 |                 ["role"] = m.Role switch
 635 |                 {
 636 |                     ChatRole.System => "system",
 637 |                     ChatRole.User => "user",
 638 |                     ChatRole.Assistant => "assistant",
 639 |                     ChatRole.Tool => "tool",
 640 |                     _ => "user",
 641 |                 },
 642 |                 ["content"] = m.Content,
 643 |             };
 644 | 
 645 |             if (m.ToolCalls is { } tcs && tcs.Count > 0)
 646 |             {
 647 |                 var tcsArr = new JsonArray();
 648 |                 foreach (var tc in tcs)
 649 |                 {
 650 |                     tcsArr.Add(new JsonObject
 651 |                     {
 652 |                         ["id"] = tc.Id,
 653 |                         ["type"] = "function",
 654 |                         ["function"] = new JsonObject
 655 |                         {
 656 |                             ["name"] = tc.Name,
 657 |                             ["arguments"] = tc.Arguments,
 658 |                         },
 659 |                     });
 660 |                 }
 661 |                 obj["tool_calls"] = tcsArr;
 662 |             }
 663 | 
 664 |             if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(m.ToolCallId))
 665 |                 obj["tool_call_id"] = m.ToolCallId;
 666 | 
 667 |             if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(m.Name))
 668 |                 obj["name"] = m.Name;
 669 | 
 670 |             arr.Add(obj);
 671 |         }
 672 |         return arr;
 673 |     }
 674 | 
 675 |     /// <summary>
 676 |     /// Send one HTTP request with the spec-mandated retry policy:
 677 |     /// 429 (TooManyRequests) is retried ONCE after a 2-second backoff;
 678 |     /// 401/403/5xx/other-4xx throw immediately; network/timeout faults
 679 |     /// throw immediately. Cancellation propagates as
 680 |     /// <see cref="AiErrorKind.Cancelled"/>.
 681 |     /// </summary>
 682 |     private async Task<HttpResponseMessage> SendWithRetryAsync(
 683 |         string payload, bool stream, CancellationToken ct)
 684 |     {
 685 |         var url = ResolveCompletionUrl(_settings.BaseUrl);
 686 | 
 687 |         // Two attempts: initial (attempt=0) + one retry on 429 (attempt=1).
 688 |         for (int attempt = 0; attempt < 2; attempt++)
 689 |         {
 690 |             ct.ThrowIfCancellationRequested();
 691 | 
 692 |             using var req = new HttpRequestMessage(HttpMethod.Post, url);
 693 |             req.Headers.Accept.Clear();
 694 |             req.Headers.Accept.Add(new MediaTypeWithQualityHeaderValue(
 695 |                 stream ? "text/event-stream" : "application/json"));
 696 |             // Per the task spec: skip the Authorization header entirely
 697 |             // when ApiKey is null/empty so local providers that don't
 698 |             // require auth work without configuration.
 699 |             if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(_settings.ApiKey))
 700 |                 req.Headers.Authorization = new AuthenticationHeaderValue("Bearer", _settings.ApiKey);
 701 |             req.Content = new StringContent(payload, Encoding.UTF8, "application/json");
 702 | 
 703 |             HttpResponseMessage response;
 704 |             try
 705 |             {
 706 |                 response = stream
 707 |                     ? await _http.SendAsync(req, HttpCompletionOption.ResponseHeadersRead, ct).ConfigureAwait(false)
 708 |                     : await _http.SendAsync(req, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 709 |             }
 710 |             catch (OperationCanceledException) when (ct.IsCancellationRequested)
 711 |             {
 712 |                 throw new AiException(AiErrorKind.Cancelled, "Request cancelled by caller.");
 713 |             }
 714 |             catch (TaskCanceledException ex)
 715 |             {
 716 |                 // HttpClient.Timeout surfaces as TaskCanceledException — treat as timeout.
 717 |                 throw new AiException(AiErrorKind.Timeout, "AI request timed out.", inner: ex);
 718 |             }
 719 |             catch (HttpRequestException ex)
 720 |             {
 721 |                 throw new AiException(AiErrorKind.Network, "Network error contacting AI provider: " + ex.Message, inner: ex);
 722 |             }
 723 | 
 724 |             // 2xx — return the response. The caller owns disposing it.
 725 |             if (response.IsSuccessStatusCode) return response;
 726 | 
 727 |             // Non-2xx — read the body for the error message, then decide on retry.
 728 |             var body = await response.Content.ReadAsStringAsync(ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 729 |             var statusCode = response.StatusCode;
 730 |             var message = ExtractErrorMessage(body) ?? $"HTTP {(int)statusCode} {response.ReasonPhrase}";
 731 |             response.Dispose();
 732 | 
 733 |             // 429 — retry once after a 2-second backoff (per spec).
 734 |             if (statusCode == HttpStatusCode.TooManyRequests && attempt == 0)
 735 |             {
 736 |                 try
 737 |                 {
 738 |                     await Task.Delay(TimeSpan.FromSeconds(2), ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 739 |                 }
 740 |                 catch (OperationCanceledException) when (ct.IsCancellationRequested)
 741 |                 {
 742 |                     throw new AiException(AiErrorKind.Cancelled, "Request cancelled during 429 retry backoff.");
 743 |                 }
 744 |                 continue;
 745 |             }
 746 | 
 747 |             // Everything else (401/403/5xx/other-4xx) — throw immediately.
 748 |             throw new AiException(statusCode, message);
 749 |         }
 750 | 
 751 |         // Unreachable: the loop either returns a 2xx response or throws.
 752 |         // But the compiler can't prove that, so provide a sentinel.
 753 |         throw new AiException(AiErrorKind.RateLimit, "AI provider returned 429 after the 2-second retry.");
 754 |     }
 755 | 
 756 |     /// <summary>
 757 |     /// OpenAI's error JSON is <c>{ "error": { "message": "..." } }</c>.
 758 |     /// Some providers (LLMost) return <c>{ "error": "string" }</c> or just
 759 |     /// a bare string. Try all shapes; return null on no match.
 760 |     /// </summary>
 761 |     private static string? ExtractErrorMessage(string body)
 762 |     {
 763 |         if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(body)) return null;
 764 |         try
 765 |         {
 766 |             using var doc = JsonDocument.Parse(body);
 767 |             var root = doc.RootElement;
 768 |             if (root.ValueKind == JsonValueKind.Object)
 769 |             {
 770 |                 if (root.TryGetProperty("error", out var err))
 771 |                 {
 772 |                     if (err.ValueKind == JsonValueKind.String) return err.GetString();
 773 |                     if (err.ValueKind == JsonValueKind.Object && err.TryGetProperty("message", out var msg))
 774 |                         return msg.GetString();
 775 |                 }
 776 |                 if (root.TryGetProperty("message", out var msgEl) && msgEl.ValueKind == JsonValueKind.String)
 777 |                     return msgEl.GetString();
 778 |             }
 779 |             return body.Length > 500 ? body[..500] : body;
 780 |         }
 781 |         catch (JsonException)
 782 |         {
 783 |             return body.Length > 500 ? body[..500] : body;
 784 |         }
 785 |     }
 786 | 
 787 |     /// <summary>
 788 |     /// Build the canonical <c>/chat/completions</c> URL from a base URL.
 789 |     /// Trims trailing slashes and appends <c>/chat/completions</c> only if
 790 |     /// the base URL doesn't already end with it. Port of
 791 |     /// <c>completionUrl</c> from <c>aiClient.ts</c>.
 792 |     /// </summary>
 793 |     public static string ResolveCompletionUrl(string baseUrl)
 794 |     {
 795 |         if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(baseUrl))
 796 |             throw new ArgumentException("BaseUrl is required.", nameof(baseUrl));
 797 |         var url = baseUrl.TrimEnd('/');
 798 |         if (!url.EndsWith("/chat/completions", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
 799 |             url += "/chat/completions";
 800 |         return url;
 801 |     }
 802 | 
 803 |     /// <summary>
 804 |     /// Parse a non-streaming response body into a <see cref="ChatResponse"/>.
 805 |     /// Defensive: malformed tool_calls are silently dropped (the tool
 806 |     /// registry will handle the missing call gracefully by returning an
 807 |     /// error result). Token usage is parsed from the <c>usage</c> block
 808 |     /// into <see cref="ChatResponse.PromptTokens"/> /
 809 |     /// <see cref="ChatResponse.CompletionTokens"/> directly per the task
 810 |     /// spec (no separate <c>Usage</c> sub-record).
 811 |     /// </summary>
 812 |     private static ChatResponse ParseChatResponse(string body)
 813 |     {
 814 |         if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(body))
 815 |             throw new AiException(AiErrorKind.Parse, "AI provider returned an empty response body.");
 816 | 
 817 |         JsonDocument doc;
 818 |         try
 819 |         {
 820 |             doc = JsonDocument.Parse(body);
 821 |         }
 822 |         catch (JsonException ex)
 823 |         {
 824 |             throw new AiException(AiErrorKind.Parse, "AI provider returned malformed JSON: " + ex.Message, inner: ex);
 825 |         }
 826 | 
 827 |         using (doc)
 828 |         {
 829 |             var root = doc.RootElement;
 830 |             if (root.ValueKind != JsonValueKind.Object)
 831 |                 throw new AiException(AiErrorKind.Parse, "AI response root is not a JSON object.");
 832 | 
 833 |             // Check for top-level error.
 834 |             if (root.TryGetProperty("error", out var errEl))
 835 |             {
 836 |                 var msg = errEl.ValueKind == JsonValueKind.String
 837 |                     ? errEl.GetString()
 838 |                     : (errEl.TryGetProperty("message", out var m) ? m.GetString() : "Unknown provider error");
 839 |                 throw new AiException(AiErrorKind.BadRequest, msg ?? "Unknown provider error.");
 840 |             }
 841 | 
 842 |             string? content = null;
 843 |             List<ToolCall>? toolCalls = null;
 844 |             string? finishReason = null;
 845 | 
 846 |             if (root.TryGetProperty("choices", out var choicesEl) && choicesEl.ValueKind == JsonValueKind.Array)
 847 |             {
 848 |                 foreach (var choice in choicesEl.EnumerateArray())
 849 |                 {
 850 |                     if (choice.TryGetProperty("finish_reason", out var fr) && fr.ValueKind == JsonValueKind.String)
 851 |                         finishReason = fr.GetString();
 852 |                     if (!choice.TryGetProperty("message", out var msgEl)) continue;
 853 | 
 854 |                     if (msgEl.TryGetProperty("content", out var c) && c.ValueKind == JsonValueKind.String)
 855 |                         content = c.GetString();
 856 | 
 857 |                     if (msgEl.TryGetProperty("tool_calls", out var tcsEl) && tcsEl.ValueKind == JsonValueKind.Array)
 858 |                     {
 859 |                         toolCalls ??= new List<ToolCall>();
 860 |                         foreach (var tc in tcsEl.EnumerateArray())
 861 |                         {
 862 |                             try
 863 |                             {
 864 |                                 var id = tc.TryGetProperty("id", out var idEl) ? idEl.GetString() ?? string.Empty : string.Empty;
 865 |                                 if (!tc.TryGetProperty("function", out var fnEl)) continue;
 866 |                                 var name = fnEl.TryGetProperty("name", out var nameEl) ? nameEl.GetString() ?? string.Empty : string.Empty;
 867 |                                 var args = fnEl.TryGetProperty("arguments", out var argsEl)
 868 |                                     ? (argsEl.ValueKind == JsonValueKind.String ? argsEl.GetString() ?? "{}" : argsEl.GetRawText())
 869 |                                     : "{}";
 870 |                                 toolCalls.Add(new ToolCall { Id = id, Name = name, Arguments = args });
 871 |                             }
 872 |                             catch (Exception)
 873 |                             {
 874 |                                 // Drop a single malformed tool call rather than failing the whole response.
 875 |                             }
 876 |                         }
 877 |                     }
 878 |                     break; // we only read the first choice
 879 |                 }
 880 |             }
 881 | 
 882 |             // Parse the usage block: usage.prompt_tokens / usage.completion_tokens.
 883 |             // Defensive — some providers (notably Anthropic-compat proxies)
 884 |             // emit slightly different field names; we fall back to 0 on any
 885 |             // missing/unparseable field. The spec only requires the two
 886 |             // direct fields on ChatResponse; total_tokens and cached_tokens
 887 |             // (which the prior implementation carried) are intentionally
 888 |             // dropped to keep the public surface lean.
 889 |             int promptTokens = 0;
 890 |             int completionTokens = 0;
 891 |             if (root.TryGetProperty("usage", out var uEl) && uEl.ValueKind == JsonValueKind.Object)
 892 |             {
 893 |                 if (uEl.TryGetProperty("prompt_tokens", out var pt) && pt.TryGetInt32(out var p))
 894 |                     promptTokens = p;
 895 |                 if (uEl.TryGetProperty("completion_tokens", out var ct2) && ct2.TryGetInt32(out var c2))
 896 |                     completionTokens = c2;
 897 |             }
 898 | 
 899 |             return new ChatResponse
 900 |             {
 901 |                 Content = content,
 902 |                 ToolCalls = toolCalls,
 903 |                 FinishReason = finishReason,
 904 |                 PromptTokens = promptTokens,
 905 |                 CompletionTokens = completionTokens,
 906 |             };
 907 |         }
 908 |     }
 909 | 
 910 |     // ── Streaming chunk DTO (used for JSON deserialization) ─────────────────
 911 | 
 912 |     private sealed class ChatStreamChunk
 913 |     {
 914 |         public List<StreamChoice>? Choices { get; set; }
 915 |         public StreamUsage? Usage { get; set; }
 916 |     }
 917 | 
 918 |     private sealed class StreamChoice
 919 |     {
 920 |         public StreamDelta? Delta { get; set; }
 921 |         public string? FinishReason { get; set; }
 922 |     }
 923 | 
 924 |     private sealed class StreamDelta
 925 |     {
 926 |         public string? Content { get; set; }
 927 |         public List<StreamToolCall>? ToolCalls { get; set; }
 928 |     }
 929 | 
 930 |     private sealed class StreamToolCall
 931 |     {
 932 |         public int? Index { get; set; }
 933 |         public string? Id { get; set; }
 934 |         public StreamFunction? Function { get; set; }
 935 |     }
 936 | 
 937 |     private sealed class StreamFunction
 938 |     {
 939 |         public string? Name { get; set; }
 940 |         public string? Arguments { get; set; }
 941 |     }
 942 | 
 943 |     /// <summary>
 944 |     /// Streaming usage block (parsed but intentionally discarded — the
 945 |     /// streaming API yields only content deltas to the caller, per spec).
 946 |     /// </summary>
 947 |     private sealed class StreamUsage
 948 |     {
 949 |         public int PromptTokens { get; set; }
 950 |         public int CompletionTokens { get; set; }
 951 |         public int TotalTokens { get; set; }
 952 |     }
 953 | 
 954 |     /// <summary>
 955 |     /// Mutable accumulator for one tool call as it arrives across multiple
 956 |     /// SSE chunks. The <c>function.arguments</c> field is fragmentary in
 957 |     /// streaming — each chunk contributes a partial string that we
 958 |     /// concatenate. The <c>id</c> and <c>function.name</c> arrive once on
 959 |     /// the first chunk for this index.
 960 |     /// </summary>
 961 |     private sealed class ToolCallAccumulator
 962 |     {
 963 |         public string? Id { get; set; }
 964 |         public string? Name { get; set; }
 965 |         public StringBuilder Arguments { get; } = new();
 966 |     }
 967 | }
 968 | 
 969 | /// <summary>
 970 | /// Holder for the final <see cref="ChatResponse"/> produced by
 971 | /// <see cref="AiClient.StreamChatWithToolsAsync"/>. Allocate one, pass it
 972 | /// into the streaming call, then read <see cref="Response"/> after the
 973 | /// <c>IAsyncEnumerable&lt;string&gt;</c> is exhausted.
 974 | /// </summary>
 975 | public sealed class StreamChatResult
 976 | {
 977 |     /// <summary>
 978 |     /// The final ChatResponse (Content + ToolCalls + FinishReason + token
 979 |     /// usage). Null if the stream was cancelled mid-flight or threw.
 980 |     /// </summary>
 981 |     public ChatResponse? Response { get; set; }
 982 | }

   1 | namespace MyGame.Core.AI;
   2 | 
   3 | /// <summary>
   4 | /// Identifies an AI agent role within the pipeline. Used by
   5 | /// <see cref="AiSettings.GetModelForRole"/> to pick a role-specific
   6 | /// model override (or fall back to <see cref="AiSettings.Model"/>).
   7 | /// </summary>
   8 | public enum AiRole
   9 | {
  10 |     /// <summary>
  11 |     /// World-builder planner. Designs the world structure from the brief
  12 |     /// (locations, NPCs, buildings, theme, atmosphere). One-shot AI call
  13 |     /// per world build.
  14 |     /// </summary>
  15 |     Planner,
  16 | 
  17 |     /// <summary>
  18 |     /// In-session Game Master. Runs the tool-call loop that narrates the
  19 |     /// player's actions and mutates the world. Long-running, multi-turn.
  20 |     /// </summary>
  21 |     GM,
  22 | 
  23 |     /// <summary>
  24 |     /// World-builder narrator. Writes the atmospheric opening narration
  25 |     /// after the deterministic committer has populated the world.
  26 |     /// </summary>
  27 |     Narrator,
  28 | 
  29 |     /// <summary>
  30 |     /// Pet agent. A delegated sub-agent for focused sub-tasks during
  31 |     /// world-build (e.g. "spawn 5 bandits in the forest"). Has its own
  32 |     /// tool-call loop, shorter iteration cap.
  33 |     /// </summary>
  34 |     Pet,
  35 | }
  36 | 
  37 | /// <summary>
  38 | /// Connection + sampling settings for the OpenAI-compatible AI provider.
  39 | ///
  40 | /// Port-side record (no direct TS counterpart — the TS source kept these
  41 | /// fields on <c>lib/settings.ts</c>'s <c>AISettings</c> interface, which
  42 | /// mixed UI/server concerns with pure connection data). This C# record is
  43 | /// the pure transport layer: it carries only what
  44 | /// <see cref="AiClient.ChatAsync"/> needs to format a request, and it
  45 | /// serialises plainly (System.Text.Json defaults) so a settings file on
  46 | /// disk round-trips losslessly.
  47 | ///
  48 | /// <para>
  49 | /// There is NO reference to the z-ai-web-dev-sdk here. The desktop rewrite
  50 | /// talks to any OpenAI-compatible provider (OpenAI, LLMost, DeepSeek,
  51 | /// local LM Studio, etc.) over plain HTTP — see <see cref="AiClient"/>.
  52 | /// </para>
  53 | ///
  54 | /// <para>
  55 | /// <b>Multi-model support (issue #26):</b> the four optional role-specific
  56 | /// model fields (<see cref="PlannerModel"/>, <see cref="GMModel"/>,
  57 | /// <see cref="NarratorModel"/>, <see cref="PetModel"/>) let the user pick
  58 | /// different models for each agent role. When a role-specific field is
  59 | /// null/empty, <see cref="GetModelForRole"/> falls back to
  60 | /// <see cref="Model"/> (the main one). This is useful for mixing a strong
  61 | /// model for the planner (creative) with a cheaper model for the GM
  62 | /// (frequent) — without spinning up separate provider accounts.
  63 | /// </para>
  64 | /// </summary>
  65 | public sealed record AiSettings
  66 | {
  67 |     /// <summary>
  68 |     /// Base URL of the OpenAI-compatible API, WITHOUT the trailing
  69 |     /// <c>/chat/completions</c> path. Defaults to OpenAI's public endpoint.
  70 |     /// <see cref="AiClient"/> appends <c>/chat/completions</c> itself, so a
  71 |     /// base URL of <c>https://api.openai.com/v1</c> resolves to
  72 |     /// <c>https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions</c>.
  73 |     /// </summary>
  74 |     public string BaseUrl { get; init; } = "https://api.openai.com/v1";
  75 | 
  76 |     /// <summary>
  77 |     /// Bearer token sent in the <c>Authorization</c> header. Null/empty
  78 |     /// means anonymous (only valid for local providers that don't enforce
  79 |     /// auth). The <see cref="AiClient"/> skips the <c>Authorization</c>
  80 |     /// header when ApiKey is null so local servers don't reject the request,
  81 |     /// and throws a typed <see cref="AiException"/> if the provider returns
  82 |     /// 401/403.
  83 |     /// </summary>
  84 |     public string? ApiKey { get; init; }
  85 | 
  86 |     /// <summary>
  87 |     /// Model id passed in the request body (<c>gpt-4o-mini</c>,
  88 |     /// <c>deepseek-chat</c>, <c>llama3.1:8b</c>, …). Defaults to OpenAI's
  89 |     /// cheapest current model — the desktop MVP targets low cost-per-turn.
  90 |     ///
  91 |     /// <para>
  92 |     /// This is the <b>default</b> model used for any role that doesn't
  93 |     /// have a role-specific override set. Per-role overrides live in
  94 |     /// <see cref="PlannerModel"/> / <see cref="GMModel"/> /
  95 |     /// <see cref="NarratorModel"/> / <see cref="PetModel"/> (issue #26).
  96 |     /// </para>
  97 |     /// </summary>
  98 |     public string Model { get; init; } = "gpt-4o-mini";
  99 | 
 100 |     /// <summary>
 101 |     /// Sampling temperature, 0.0 = greedy, 2.0 = max chaos. Default 0.7 is
 102 |     /// the OpenAI-recommended starting point for creative narrative.
 103 |     /// </summary>
 104 |     public double Temperature { get; init; } = 0.7;
 105 | 
 106 |     /// <summary>
 107 |     /// Hard cap on completion tokens per request. Default 2000 is enough
 108 |     /// for a couple of paragraphs of narration + a tool-call round. The
 109 |     /// world-builder orchestrator uses a higher cap (configured at the
 110 |     /// call site, not here — this record is the shared default).
 111 |     /// </summary>
 112 |     public int MaxTokens { get; init; } = 2000;
 113 | 
 114 |     // ─── Per-role model overrides (issue #26) ───────────────────────────
 115 |     //
 116 |     // Each is an OPTIONAL model id; when null/empty, GetModelForRole
 117 |     // returns the main <see cref="Model"/>. Set a role-specific override
 118 |     // to use a different model for that role (e.g. a stronger model for
 119 |     // the planner, a cheaper one for the GM).
 120 |     //
 121 |     // The fields are init-only (the record is immutable) — update via
 122 |     // `with { ... }`. They serialise plainly so existing settings.json
 123 |     // files load with nulls for missing fields (backward-compatible).
 124 | 
 125 |     /// <summary>
 126 |     /// Model id for the world-builder <see cref="AiRole.Planner"/>. When
 127 |     /// null/empty, falls back to <see cref="Model"/>. Use this to use a
 128 |     /// stronger creative model for world-planning (one-shot per build).
 129 |     /// </summary>
 130 |     public string? PlannerModel { get; init; }
 131 | 
 132 |     /// <summary>
 133 |     /// Model id for the in-session <see cref="AiRole.GM"/>. When
 134 |     /// null/empty, falls back to <see cref="Model"/>. Use this to use a
 135 |     /// cheaper / faster model for the GM's frequent tool-call loop.
 136 |     /// </summary>
 137 |     public string? GMModel { get; init; }
 138 | 
 139 |     /// <summary>
 140 |     /// Model id for the world-builder <see cref="AiRole.Narrator"/>.
 141 |     /// When null/empty, falls back to <see cref="Model"/>. Use this to
 142 |     /// pick a more literary model for the atmospheric opening narration.
 143 |     /// </summary>
 144 |     public string? NarratorModel { get; init; }
 145 | 
 146 |     /// <summary>
 147 |     /// Model id for <see cref="AiRole.Pet"/> (delegated sub-agents during
 148 |     /// world-build). When null/empty, falls back to <see cref="Model"/>.
 149 |     /// </summary>
 150 |     public string? PetModel { get; init; }
 151 | 
 152 |     /// <summary>
 153 |     /// Resolve the model id to use for the given role. Returns the
 154 |     /// role-specific override when set (non-null and non-empty after
 155 |     /// trimming); otherwise falls back to <see cref="Model"/>.
 156 |     ///
 157 |     /// <para>
 158 |     /// Used by <see cref="AiClient.WithModel"/> + the agent constructors
 159 |     /// to derive a role-specific client at the start of each run. The
 160 |     /// fallback chain is: role override (if set) → main Model.
 161 |     /// </para>
 162 |     /// </summary>
 163 |     public string GetModelForRole(AiRole role) => role switch
 164 |     {
 165 |         AiRole.Planner  => string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(PlannerModel)  ? Model : PlannerModel!.Trim(),
 166 |         AiRole.GM       => string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(GMModel)       ? Model : GMModel!.Trim(),
 167 |         AiRole.Narrator => string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(NarratorModel) ? Model : NarratorModel!.Trim(),
 168 |         AiRole.Pet      => string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(PetModel)      ? Model : PetModel!.Trim(),
 169 |         _ => Model,
 170 |     };
 171 | }

--- src/MyGame.Core/AI/IAiClient.cs (C#, 130 lines, ~1,592 tokens) ---
   1 | namespace MyGame.Core.AI;
   2 | 
   3 | /// <summary>
   4 | /// Abstraction over an OpenAI-compatible chat-completions client.
   5 | /// Implemented by <see cref="AiClient"/> (production) and by test stubs
   6 | /// (e.g. <c>StubAiClient</c> in MyGame.Tests) that return canned
   7 | /// <see cref="ChatResponse"/> objects without making any HTTP call.
   8 | ///
   9 | /// <para>
  10 | /// Introduced for issue #57 (multiplayer integration tests) so the
  11 | /// <see cref="Agents.GameMaster"/> can be constructed in tests with a
  12 | /// stub client that returns deterministic narration, letting the
  13 | /// end-to-end host-server + game-client protocol be exercised without
  14 | /// contacting a real LLM provider.
  15 | /// </para>
  16 | ///
  17 | /// <para>
  18 | /// <b>Members:</b>
  19 | /// <list type="bullet">
  20 | ///   <item><see cref="Settings"/> — the <see cref="AiSettings"/> the
  21 | ///     client was constructed with. Agents read this to derive
  22 | ///     role-specific clients + per-call token caps (e.g. the GM's
  23 | ///     summarization call derives a one-off client with a lower
  24 | ///     MaxTokens).</item>
  25 | ///   <item><see cref="WithModel"/> — return a client that shares this
  26 | ///     client's HTTP/socket pool (in the production implementation) but
  27 | ///     uses the given model id. Used by the agents to derive a
  28 | ///     role-specific client for a single run. Test stubs return
  29 | ///     <c>this</c> (model overrides are irrelevant when the response is
  30 | ///     canned).</item>
  31 | ///   <item><see cref="ChatAsync"/> — non-streaming chat completion, no
  32 | ///     tools.</item>
  33 | ///   <item><see cref="ChatWithToolsAsync"/> — non-streaming chat
  34 | ///     completion with optional function-calling tools.</item>
  35 | ///   <item><see cref="StreamChatAsync"/> — streaming chat completion
  36 | ///     (content deltas only).</item>
  37 | ///   <item><see cref="StreamChatWithToolsAsync"/> — streaming chat
  38 | ///     completion with tools, accumulating tool-call deltas into the
  39 | ///     supplied <see cref="StreamChatResult"/>.</item>
  40 | /// </list>
  41 | /// </para>
  42 | ///
  43 | /// <para>
  44 | /// <b>Implementation notes for test stubs:</b>
  45 | /// <list type="bullet">
  46 | ///   <item><see cref="WithModel"/> may return <c>this</c> — the stub's
  47 | ///     <see cref="Settings"/> + canned-response queue don't depend on
  48 | ///     the model id.</item>
  49 | ///   <item><see cref="StreamChatWithToolsAsync"/> should yield the
  50 | ///     canned content as a single delta and populate the
  51 | ///     <see cref="StreamChatResult.Response"/> with the full
  52 | ///     <see cref="ChatResponse"/> (content + token counts) so the
  53 | ///     agent's loop terminates immediately on iteration 1 (no tool
  54 | ///     calls → loop exits).</item>
  55 | ///   <item>Token counts in the canned <see cref="ChatResponse"/> are
  56 | ///     informational — tests can set them to any value for assertion
  57 | ///     purposes.</item>
  58 | /// </list>
  59 | /// </para>
  60 | /// </summary>
  61 | public interface IAiClient
  62 | {
  63 |     /// <summary>
  64 |     /// The settings this client was constructed with. Exposed so callers
  65 |     /// that hold an <see cref="IAiClient"/> can read back the resolved
  66 |     /// <see cref="AiSettings.Model"/> etc. without keeping a separate
  67 |     /// settings reference.
  68 |     /// </summary>
  69 |     AiSettings Settings { get; }
  70 | 
  71 |     /// <summary>
  72 |     /// Return a client that uses the given model id instead of
  73 |     /// <see cref="AiSettings.Model"/>. Implementations should share the
  74 |     /// underlying HTTP/socket pool when one exists (production
  75 |     /// <see cref="AiClient"/>); test stubs may simply return
  76 |     /// <c>this</c>.
  77 |     /// <para>
  78 |     /// When <paramref name="model"/> is null/empty/whitespace OR equals
  79 |     /// the current model, implementations should return the same
  80 |     /// instance (no allocation). This makes the pattern
  81 |     /// <c>ai.WithModel(roleOverride ?? ai.Settings.Model)</c> a no-op
  82 |     /// when no override is set.
  83 |     /// </para>
  84 |     /// </summary>
  85 |     IAiClient WithModel(string? model);
  86 | 
  87 |     /// <summary>
  88 |     /// Non-streaming chat completion (no tools). Equivalent to calling
  89 |     /// <see cref="ChatWithToolsAsync"/> with <c>tools: null</c>.
  90 |     /// </summary>
  91 |     Task<ChatResponse> ChatAsync(
  92 |         IReadOnlyList<ChatMessage> messages,
  93 |         CancellationToken ct = default);
  94 | 
  95 |     /// <summary>
  96 |     /// Non-streaming chat completion with optional function-calling
  97 |     /// tools. Returns the parsed <see cref="ChatResponse"/> on success;
  98 |     /// throws <see cref="AiException"/> on any failure.
  99 |     /// </summary>
 100 |     Task<ChatResponse> ChatWithToolsAsync(
 101 |         IReadOnlyList<ChatMessage> messages,
 102 |         IReadOnlyList<ToolDefinition>? tools,
 103 |         CancellationToken ct = default);
 104 | 
 105 |     /// <summary>
 106 |     /// Streaming chat completion. Yields each <c>delta.content</c>
 107 |     /// chunk as it arrives. Tool-call deltas are accumulated internally
 108 |     /// and discarded; for tool-call support use
 109 |     /// <see cref="StreamChatWithToolsAsync"/>.
 110 |     /// </summary>
 111 |     IAsyncEnumerable<string> StreamChatAsync(
 112 |         IReadOnlyList<ChatMessage> messages,
 113 |         CancellationToken ct = default);
 114 | 
 115 |     /// <summary>
 116 |     /// Streaming chat completion WITH optional function-calling tools.
 117 |     /// Yields each <c>delta.content</c> chunk as it arrives while
 118 |     /// accumulating tool-call deltas (keyed by their <c>index</c>) into
 119 |     /// the supplied <paramref name="result"/> holder. The final
 120 |     /// <see cref="ChatResponse"/> is written to
 121 |     /// <see cref="StreamChatResult.Response"/> when the stream
 122 |     /// completes.
 123 |     /// </summary>
 124 |     IAsyncEnumerable<string> StreamChatWithToolsAsync(
 125 |         IReadOnlyList<ChatMessage> messages,
 126 |         IReadOnlyList<ToolDefinition>? tools,
 127 |         StreamChatResult result,
 128 |         CancellationToken ct = default);
 129 | }

   1 | using System.Text.Json.Serialization;
   2 | 
   3 | namespace MyGame.Core.AI;
   4 | 
   5 | /// <summary>
   6 | /// Role of a <see cref="ChatMessage"/> in the OpenAI chat-completions
   7 | /// protocol. Port of the string-union <c>'system' | 'user' | 'assistant' |
   8 | /// 'tool'</c> from <c>ai/messages.ts</c>.
   9 | /// </summary>
  10 | public enum ChatRole
  11 | {
  12 |     /// <summary>Director / system instructions — shapes model behavior.</summary>
  13 |     [JsonPropertyName("system")] System = 0,
  14 | 
  15 |     /// <summary>Player / human input.</summary>
  16 |     [JsonPropertyName("user")] User = 1,
  17 | 
  18 |     /// <summary>Model output (narration + optional tool_calls).</summary>
  19 |     [JsonPropertyName("assistant")] Assistant = 2,
  20 | 
  21 |     /// <summary>Result of a previous tool_call, fed back to the model.</summary>
  22 |     [JsonPropertyName("tool")] Tool = 3,
  23 | }
  24 | 
  25 | /// <summary>
  26 | /// One tool-call request issued by the model. Port of the
  27 | /// <c>tool_calls[].function</c> sub-shape from <c>ai/messages.ts</c>.
  28 | /// </summary>
  29 | public sealed record ToolCall
  30 | {
  31 |     /// <summary>
  32 |     /// Stable id the provider assigns so the next <c>role:tool</c> message
  33 |     /// can reference this call by <see cref="ChatMessage.ToolCallId"/>.
  34 |     /// </summary>
  35 |     public required string Id { get; init; }
  36 | 
  37 |     /// <summary>Tool name (matches <see cref="ToolDefinition.Name"/>).</summary>
  38 |     public required string Name { get; init; }
  39 | 
  40 |     /// <summary>
  41 |     /// Raw JSON-string arguments the model produced. MUST be parsed
  42 |     /// defensively — the model can emit malformed JSON, in which case the
  43 |     /// tool registry wraps the parse error in a tool-result with
  44 |     /// <c>IsError</c>=true instead of throwing.
  45 |     /// </summary>
  46 |     public string Arguments { get; init; } = "{}";
  47 | }
  48 | 
  49 | /// <summary>
  50 | /// One chat message — structurally identical to the OpenAI
  51 | /// <c>/chat/completions</c> message shape. Port of <c>ChatMessage</c> from
  52 | /// <c>ai/messages.ts</c>.
  53 | ///
  54 | /// <para>
  55 | /// Carries the four wire fields (<c>role</c>, <c>content</c>,
  56 | /// <c>tool_calls</c>, <c>tool_call_id</c>) plus the optional <c>name</c>
  57 | /// field OpenAI accepts on any message (used to differentiate multiple
  58 | /// authors of the same role, e.g. two players in a multiplayer session).
  59 | /// </para>
  60 | /// </summary>
  61 | public sealed record ChatMessage
  62 | {
  63 |     /// <summary>Sender role.</summary>
  64 |     [JsonPropertyName("role")]
  65 |     public required ChatRole Role { get; init; }
  66 | 
  67 |     /// <summary>
  68 |     /// Text content. Nullable because assistant messages that carry tool
  69 |     /// calls often have null content, and tool messages have their result
  70 |     /// in this field as a string.
  71 |     /// </summary>
  72 |     [JsonPropertyName("content")]
  73 |     public string? Content { get; init; }
  74 | 
  75 |     /// <summary>
  76 |     /// Tool calls issued by the model. Only set on assistant messages.
  77 |     /// Null on other roles.
  78 |     /// </summary>
  79 |     [JsonPropertyName("tool_calls")]
  80 |     [JsonIgnore(Condition = JsonIgnoreCondition.WhenWritingNull)]
  81 |     public IReadOnlyList<ToolCall>? ToolCalls { get; init; }
  82 | 
  83 |     /// <summary>
  84 |     /// Reference to the tool call this message answers. Only set on
  85 |     /// <see cref="ChatRole.Tool"/> messages.
  86 |     /// </summary>
  87 |     [JsonPropertyName("tool_call_id")]
  88 |     [JsonIgnore(Condition = JsonIgnoreCondition.WhenWritingNull)]
  89 |     public string? ToolCallId { get; init; }
  90 | 
  91 |     /// <summary>
  92 |     /// Optional author name (OpenAI accepts this on any role; used to
  93 |     /// differentiate multiple authors of the same role, e.g. multiple
  94 |     /// players in a multiplayer session).
  95 |     /// </summary>
  96 |     [JsonPropertyName("name")]
  97 |     [JsonIgnore(Condition = JsonIgnoreCondition.WhenWritingNull)]
  98 |     public string? Name { get; init; }
  99 | 
 100 |     // ── Factory helpers for the agent layer ───────────────────────────────

 104 |     {
 105 |         Role = ChatRole.System,
 106 |         Content = content,
 107 |     };
 108 | 
 109 |     /// <summary>Build a <see cref="ChatRole.User"/> message.</summary>

 111 |     {
 112 |         Role = ChatRole.User,
 113 |         Content = content,
 114 |     };

 120 |         Content = content,
 121 |         Name = name,
 122 |     };

 126 |     {
 127 |         Role = ChatRole.Assistant,
 128 |         Content = content,
 129 |     };

 131 |     /// <summary>

 145 |     {
 146 |         Role = ChatRole.Tool,
 147 |         ToolCallId = toolCallId,
 148 |         Content = content,
 149 |     };

 152 | /// <summary>
 153 | /// OpenAI-compatible function-tool definition. Port of <c>ToolDef</c> from
 154 | /// <c>ai/tools/index.ts</c>.
 155 | ///
 156 | /// <para>
 157 | /// The <see cref="ParametersJson"/> field is a JSON-schema STRING (not a
 158 | /// parsed object) so the registry can hold tools defined by hand-written
 159 | /// string literals without forcing each call site to construct a
 160 | /// <c>JsonElement</c>. The <see cref="AiClient"/> parses the string when
 161 | /// serializing the request body and embeds it as a raw JSON value.
 162 | /// </para>
 163 | /// </summary>
 164 | public sealed record ToolDefinition
 165 | {
 166 |     /// <summary>Tool name (matches what the model emits in <c>tool_calls[].function.name</c>).</summary>
 167 |     public required string Name { get; init; }
 168 | 
 169 |     /// <summary>Human-readable description shown to the model.</summary>
 170 |     public required string Description { get; init; }
 171 | 
 172 |     /// <summary>
 173 |     /// JSON schema for the tool's arguments, as a string. Empty/whitespace
 174 |     /// means <c>"{}"</c> (no parameters).
 175 |     /// </summary>
 176 |     public string ParametersJson { get; init; } = "{}";
 177 | }
 178 | 
 179 | /// <summary>
 180 | /// Normalised response from one chat-completion call. Port of the
 181 | /// <c>StreamResult</c> shape from <c>aiClient.ts</c> (renamed because the
 182 | /// C# client doesn't expose a separate streaming-result type — the streaming
 183 | /// API yields <see cref="string"/> deltas directly).
 184 | /// </summary>
 185 | public sealed record ChatResponse
 186 | {
 187 |     /// <summary>
 188 |     /// Concatenated assistant content. May be null/empty if the model only
 189 |     /// emitted tool calls this round.
 190 |     /// </summary>
 191 |     public string? Content { get; init; }
 192 | 
 193 |     /// <summary>
 194 |     /// Tool calls the model issued (parsed from
 195 |     /// <c>choices[0].message.tool_calls</c>). Null when the model didn't
 196 |     /// call any tools.
 197 |     /// </summary>
 198 |     public IReadOnlyList<ToolCall>? ToolCalls { get; init; }
 199 | 
 200 |     /// <summary>
 201 |     /// Provider's <c>finish_reason</c> for the choice
 202 |     /// (<c>stop</c>, <c>tool_calls</c>, <c>length</c>, <c>content_filter</c>).
 203 |     /// Null when not present (some providers omit it on errors).
 204 |     /// </summary>
 205 |     public string? FinishReason { get; init; }
 206 | 
 207 |     /// <summary>
 208 |     /// Prompt (input) token count parsed from the provider's
 209 |     /// <c>usage.prompt_tokens</c> field. 0 when the provider didn't
 210 |     /// return a usage block.
 211 |     /// </summary>
 212 |     public int PromptTokens { get; init; }
 213 | 
 214 |     /// <summary>
 215 |     /// Completion (output) token count parsed from the provider's
 216 |     /// <c>usage.completion_tokens</c> field. 0 when the provider didn't
 217 |     /// return a usage block.
 218 |     /// </summary>
 219 |     public int CompletionTokens { get; init; }
 220 | }
