--- src/MyGame.Core/Logging/GameLogger.cs (C#, 89 lines, ~744 tokens) ---
   1 | using System;
   2 | using System.Collections.Generic;
   3 | using System.IO;
   4 | using System.Linq;
   5 | using System.Text;
   6 | using System.Threading;
   7 | 
   8 | namespace MyGame.Core.Logging;
   9 | 
  10 | /// <summary>
  11 | /// Structured logger writing to %APPDATA%/MyGame/logs/game-{date}.log.
  12 | /// Issue #71. Levels: Trace, Debug, Info, Warning, Error.
  13 | /// Thread-safe via lock. File is flushed on every write (safe for crashes).
  14 | /// </summary>
  15 | public sealed class GameLogger
  16 | {
  17 |     public enum Level
  18 |     {
  19 |         Trace = 0,
  20 |         Debug = 1,
  21 |         Info = 2,
  22 |         Warning = 3,
  23 |         Error = 4,
  24 |     }
  25 | 
  26 |     private static GameLogger? _instance;
  27 |     private static readonly object _initLock = new();
  28 | 
  29 |     public static GameLogger Instance => _instance ?? throw new InvalidOperationException("GameLogger not initialized. Call Initialize first.");
  30 | 
  31 |     public Level MinLevel { get; set; } = Level.Info;
  32 | 
  33 |     private readonly string _logDir;
  34 |     private readonly object _writeLock = new();
  35 |     private readonly Queue<(Level Level, string Message, DateTimeOffset Ts)> _recent = new();
  36 |     private const int MaxRecent = 100;
  37 | 
  38 |     private GameLogger(string logDir)
  39 |     {
  40 |         _logDir = logDir;
  41 |         Directory.CreateDirectory(_logDir);
  42 |     }
  43 | 
  44 |     public static void Initialize(string logDir, Level minLevel = Level.Info)
  45 |     {
  46 |         lock (_initLock)
  47 |         {
  48 |             _instance = new GameLogger(logDir) { MinLevel = minLevel };
  49 |         }
  50 |     }
  51 | 
  52 |     public void Log(Level level, string message)
  53 |     {
  54 |         if (level < MinLevel) return;
  55 |         var ts = DateTimeOffset.UtcNow;
  56 |         var line = $"[{ts:yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss.fff}] [{level,-5}] {message}";
  57 | 
  58 |         lock (_writeLock)
  59 |         {
  60 |             _recent.Enqueue((level, message, ts));
  61 |             while (_recent.Count > MaxRecent) _recent.Dequeue();
  62 | 
  63 |             try
  64 |             {
  65 |                 var file = Path.Combine(_logDir, $"game-{ts:yyyyMMdd}.log");
  66 |                 File.AppendAllText(file, line + "\n");
  67 |             }
  68 |             catch { /* don't crash on log failure */ }
  69 |         }
  70 |     }
  71 | 
  72 |     public void Trace(string msg) => Log(Level.Trace, msg);
  73 |     public void Debug(string msg) => Log(Level.Debug, msg);
  74 |     public void Info(string msg) => Log(Level.Info, msg);
  75 |     public void Warning(string msg) => Log(Level.Warning, msg);
  76 |     public void Error(string msg) => Log(Level.Error, msg);
  77 | 
  78 |     public IReadOnlyList<string> GetRecent(int count = 50)
  79 |     {
  80 |         lock (_writeLock)
  81 |         {
  82 |             return _recent
  83 |                 .TakeLast(count)
  84 |                 .Select(e => $"[{e.Ts:HH:mm:ss}] [{e.Level,-5}] {e.Message}")
  85 |                 .ToList();
  86 |         }
  87 |     }
  88 | }

--- src/MyGame.Core/Multiplayer/ActionQueue.cs (C#, 238 lines, ~2,520 tokens) ---
   1 | using System.Collections.Concurrent;
   2 | using System.Diagnostics;
   3 | 
   4 | namespace MyGame.Core.Multiplayer;
   5 | 
   6 | // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   7 | //  PlayerAction
   8 | // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   9 | 
  10 | /// <summary>
  11 | /// One player action pending GM resolution. Port of <c>QueuedAction</c>
  12 | /// from <c>lib/action-queue.ts</c>, simplified for the desktop rewrite:
  13 | ///
  14 | /// <list type="bullet">
  15 | ///   <item>Drops <c>saveId</c> — the desktop host has exactly one active
  16 | ///     save per <see cref="HostServer"/>, so the save id is implicit.</item>
  17 | ///   <item>Drops <c>playerEntityId</c> — entity-id resolution is the
  18 | ///     host's job, not the queue's. The host's GameMaster will look up
  19 | ///     the active player by <see cref="PlayerId"/> (which is the
  20 | ///     member's <see cref="MemberInfo.ConnectionId"/>) at turn-processing
  21 | ///     time.</item>
  22 | ///   <item>Drops <c>resolving</c>/<c>resolved</c> boolean flags — the
  23 | ///     queue is "pending only"; once drained, actions leave the queue
  24 | ///     entirely. This matches the spec's <c>DrainAll()</c> contract
  25 | ///     (drain = remove-and-return) and avoids the half-state the TS
  26 | ///     source had (resolving-but-not-resolved actions lingering in the
  27 | ///     queue).</item>
  28 | /// </list>
  29 | ///
  30 | /// The action id (<see cref="Id"/>) is a string (Guid's "N" format) so
  31 | /// the wire messages <see cref="Protocol.ActionQueuedMsg"/> /
  32 | /// <see cref="Protocol.ActionCancelMsg"/> can carry it as a bare string.
  33 | /// </summary>
  34 | public sealed record PlayerAction
  35 | {
  36 |     /// <summary>
  37 |     /// Unique id (Guid's "N" format, 32-char lowercase hex). Generated
  38 |     /// by the client when they submit the action and echoed back by the
  39 |     /// host. Used as the cancellation key.
  40 |     /// </summary>
  41 |     public required string Id { get; init; }
  42 | 
  43 |     /// <summary>
  44 |     /// The member's <see cref="MemberInfo.ConnectionId"/>. Lets the GM
  45 |     /// correlate the action with the submitting player.
  46 |     /// </summary>
  47 |     public required Guid PlayerId { get; init; }
  48 | 
  49 |     /// <summary>
  50 |     /// The member's <see cref="MemberInfo.Nickname"/>. Denormalised so
  51 |     /// the GM's system prompt can show "Иван: attacks the goblin"
  52 |     /// without an extra lookup.
  53 |     /// </summary>
  54 |     public required string PlayerNickname { get; init; }
  55 | 
  56 |     /// <summary>The free-text action the player typed (already trimmed
  57 |     /// + capped at 4000 chars by the host).</summary>
  58 |     public required string Text { get; init; }
  59 | 
  60 |     /// <summary>UTC moment the action was submitted.</summary>
  61 |     public required DateTimeOffset SubmittedAt { get; init; }
  62 | }
  63 | 
  64 | // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  65 | //  ActionQueue
  66 | // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  67 | 
  68 | /// <summary>
  69 | /// Thread-safe queue of pending player actions. Port of
  70 | /// <c>ActionQueueManager</c> from <c>lib/action-queue.ts</c>.
  71 | ///
  72 | /// <para>
  73 | /// The TS source kept a per-<c>saveId</c> map of queues plus a per-save
  74 | /// <c>EventEmitter</c>. The desktop port collapses this to a single
  75 | /// in-memory queue per <see cref="HostServer"/> instance (one save per
  76 | /// host — see <see cref="PlayerAction"/> notes) and replaces the
  77 | /// EventEmitter with C# events.
  78 | /// </para>
  79 | ///
  80 | /// <para><b>Concurrency:</b> the spec suggests <c>Channel&lt;T&gt;</c> or
  81 | /// <c>BlockingCollection</c>. Both are optimised for the producer/
  82 | /// consumer streaming pattern (one-at-a-time <c>Take</c>), but the
  83 | /// <see cref="DrainAll"/> contract wants <em>bulk</em> removal of all
  84 | /// pending items in one call, which Channel doesn't support cleanly
  85 | /// (you'd have to loop <c>TryRead</c> until empty). A plain
  86 | /// <c>List&lt;T&gt;</c> guarded by a <c>lock</c> is simpler, correct,
  87 | /// and the right tool for this access pattern. Documented as a small
  88 | /// deviation in the worklog.</para>
  89 | ///
  90 | /// <para><b>Events:</b> the queue raises <see cref="Enqueued"/> and
  91 | /// <see cref="Cancelled"/> for the HostSession to react (e.g. wake up
  92 | /// the GM turn loop). Subscribers are invoked under the queue's lock
  93 | /// (so the queue is consistent at event time), but exceptions in
  94 | /// subscribers are caught + traced (one bad subscriber can't break the
  95 | /// queue).</para>
  96 | /// </summary>
  97 | public sealed class ActionQueue
  98 | {
  99 |     private readonly List<PlayerAction> _pending = new();
 100 |     private readonly object _lock = new();
 101 | 
 102 |     /// <summary>
 103 |     /// Raised after an action is enqueued. The handler runs under the
 104 |     /// queue's lock (so <see cref="Count"/> is consistent at event
 105 |     /// time) but exception-safe (a thrown handler is caught + traced).
 106 |     /// </summary>
 107 |     public event Action<PlayerAction>? Enqueued;
 108 | 
 109 |     /// <summary>
 110 |     /// Raised after an action is cancelled. Passes the action id that
 111 |     /// was cancelled (or null if the id wasn't found). Same lock +
 112 |     /// exception-safety contract as <see cref="Enqueued"/>.
 113 |     /// </summary>
 114 |     public event Action<string?>? Cancelled;
 115 | 
 116 |     /// <summary>
 117 |     /// Add an action to the queue. Returns the same <see cref="PlayerAction"/>
 118 |     /// (the caller already constructed it; this method doesn't mutate it).
 119 |     /// </summary>
 120 |     public PlayerAction Enqueue(PlayerAction action)
 121 |     {
 122 |         if (action is null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(action));
 123 | 
 124 |         lock (_lock)
 125 |         {
 126 |             _pending.Add(action);
 127 |             Raise(Enqueued, action);
 128 |         }
 129 |         return action;
 130 |     }
 131 | 
 132 |     /// <summary>
 133 |     /// Cancel (remove) an action by id. Returns true if the action was
 134 |     /// found and removed; false if it wasn't in the queue (already
 135 |     /// drained, already cancelled, or never existed).
 136 |     /// </summary>
 137 |     public bool Cancel(string actionId)
 138 |     {
 139 |         if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(actionId)) return false;
 140 | 
 141 |         lock (_lock)
 142 |         {
 143 |             var idx = _pending.FindIndex(a => a.Id == actionId);
 144 |             if (idx < 0)
 145 |             {
 146 |                 Raise(Cancelled, null);
 147 |                 return false;
 148 |             }
 149 |             _pending.RemoveAt(idx);
 150 |             Raise(Cancelled, actionId);
 151 |             return true;
 152 |         }
 153 |     }
 154 | 
 155 |     /// <summary>
 156 |     /// Remove and return ALL pending actions. The returned list is a
 157 |     /// snapshot — mutating it has no effect on the queue (which is now
 158 |     /// empty). Returns an empty list if no actions are pending.
 159 |     /// </summary>
 160 |     public IReadOnlyList<PlayerAction> DrainAll()
 161 |     {
 162 |         lock (_lock)
 163 |         {
 164 |             if (_pending.Count == 0) return Array.Empty<PlayerAction>();
 165 |             var snapshot = _pending.ToArray();
 166 |             _pending.Clear();
 167 |             return snapshot;
 168 |         }
 169 |     }
 170 | 
 171 |     /// <summary>Peek at the current pending actions without removing
 172 |     /// them. Returns a snapshot — safe to enumerate outside the lock.</summary>
 173 |     public IReadOnlyList<PlayerAction> PeekAll()
 174 |     {
 175 |         lock (_lock)
 176 |         {
 177 |             return _pending.ToArray();
 178 |         }
 179 |     }
 180 | 
 181 |     /// <summary>Current number of pending actions.</summary>
 182 |     public int Count
 183 |     {
 184 |         get
 185 |         {
 186 |             lock (_lock) return _pending.Count;
 187 |         }
 188 |     }
 189 | 
 190 |     /// <summary>
 191 |     /// True if at least one pending action is in the queue. Slightly
 192 |     /// cheaper than <see cref="Count"/> > 0 because it doesn't need to
 193 |     /// take the lock just to check emptiness (the lock is still taken
 194 |     /// for the read, but the comparison is simpler).
 195 |     /// </summary>
 196 |     public bool HasPending => Count > 0;
 197 | 
 198 |     /// <summary>Clear the queue (e.g. on game end).</summary>
 199 |     public void Clear()
 200 |     {
 201 |         lock (_lock)
 202 |         {
 203 |             _pending.Clear();
 204 |         }
 205 |     }
 206 | 
 207 |     // ── Event helper ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
 208 | 
 209 |     /// <summary>
 210 |     /// Raise an event under the lock, catching any subscriber exception
 211 |     /// so a single bad handler can't corrupt the queue or break other
 212 |     /// subscribers. Mirrors the EventBus exception-safety contract.
 213 |     /// Works for both reference and value types, and for nullable
 214 |     /// annotations (the <c>T?</c> annotation is erased at runtime, so a
 215 |     /// single generic method covers all cases — including the
 216 |     /// <see cref="Cancelled"/> event which passes null when an unknown
 217 |     /// id is cancelled).
 218 |     /// </summary>
 219 |     private static void Raise<T>(Action<T>? handler, T args)
 220 |     {
 221 |         if (handler is null) return;
 222 |         // Snapshot the invocation list so a handler that unsubscribes
 223 |         // mid-dispatch doesn't cause CollectionModified.
 224 |         var delegates = handler.GetInvocationList();
 225 |         foreach (var d in delegates)
 226 |         {
 227 |             try
 228 |             {
 229 |                 ((Action<T>)d)(args);
 230 |             }
 231 |             catch (Exception ex)
 232 |             {
 233 |                 Trace.WriteLine($"[ActionQueue] subscriber threw: {ex}");
 234 |             }
 235 |         }
 236 |     }
 237 | }

--- src/MyGame.Core/Multiplayer/ClientSession.cs (C#, 524 lines, ~5,627 tokens) ---
   1 | using System.Diagnostics;
   2 | using System.Text;
   3 | using MyGame.Core.Multiplayer.Protocol;

   9 | namespace MyGame.Core.Multiplayer;
  10 | 
  11 | // Same World namespace/type alias trick as HostSession.cs.
  12 | using GameWorld = MyGame.Core.World.World;
  13 | 
  14 | /// <summary>
  15 | /// High-level orchestrator for the JOIN-side of multiplayer. Glues
  16 | /// together a <see cref="GameClient"/> (transport) with a local state
  17 | /// cache (world snapshot, narrative buffer, log) so the UI layer has a
  18 | /// single object to subscribe to.
  19 | ///
  20 | /// <para>
  21 | /// The client session is the join-side mirror of <see cref="HostSession"/>:
  22 | /// the same event surface (member join/leave, chat, action queued,
  23 | /// narrative delta/final, state update, turn end) but with the data
  24 | /// flowing FROM the host instead of being generated locally. Outgoing
  25 | /// player actions go through <see cref="SubmitActionAsync"/> which
  26 | /// generates an action id locally + sends <see cref="ActionQueuedMsg"/>
  27 | /// to the host (the host re-broadcasts to everyone, including this
  28 | /// client, so the local UI sees the action appear in the queue via the
  29 | /// echoed <see cref="GameClient.ActionQueued"/> event).
  30 | /// </para>
  31 | ///
  32 | /// <para><b>Local state cache:</b> the session keeps:
  33 | /// <list type="bullet">
  34 | ///   <item><see cref="LocalWorld"/> — the latest World received via
  35 | ///     <see cref="StateUpdateMsg"/>. Deserialised once per update (the
  36 | ///     host sends a full snapshot; diff support is a future feature).</item>
  37 | ///   <item><see cref="NarrativeBuffer"/> — the accumulated narrative
  38 | ///     text from <see cref="NarrativeDeltaMsg"/>s. Replaced wholesale
  39 | ///     when a <see cref="NarrativeFinalMsg"/> arrives (the final is the
  40 | ///     canonical full text — any deltas before it were streaming).</item>
  41 | ///   <item><see cref="Log"/> — a list of <see cref="LogEntry"/> records
  42 | ///     mirroring what the host is persisting. Built locally from
  43 | ///     narrative-final + action-queued + tool events. Lets the UI render
  44 | ///     the log without needing the save file.</item>
  45 | ///   <item><see cref="PendingActions"/> — actions submitted by this
  46 | ///     client that haven't been resolved yet. Tracked locally so the UI
  47 | ///     can show "your action is pending" + support cancel.</item>
  48 | /// </list>
  49 | /// </para>
  50 | /// </summary>
  51 | public sealed class ClientSession
  52 | {
  53 |     // ─── Config ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  54 | 
  55 |     private readonly Profile.Profile _profile;
  56 |     private readonly GameClient _client;
  57 | 
  58 |     // ─── Runtime state ───────────────────────────────────────────────
  59 |

  61 |     private GameWorld? _localWorld;
  62 |     private string _narrativeBuffer = string.Empty;
  63 |     private readonly List<LogEntry> _log = new();
  64 |     private readonly Dictionary<string, PlayerAction> _pendingActions = new();
  65 |     private int _turn;
  66 |     private PartyStatus _status = PartyStatus.Lobby;
  67 |     private string? _saveId;
  68 | 
  69 |     /// <summary>True after <see cref="ConnectAsync"/> succeeds.</summary>
  70 |     public bool IsConnected => _client.IsConnected;
  71 | 
  72 |     /// <summary>The connection id assigned by the host.</summary>
  73 |     public Guid ConnectionId => _client.ConnectionId;
  74 | 
  75 |     /// <summary>The role assigned by the host.</summary>
  76 |     public MemberRole Role => _client.Role;
  77 | 
  78 |     /// <summary>Create a client session for the given local profile.</summary>
  79 |     public ClientSession(Profile.Profile profile)
  80 |     {
  81 |         _profile = profile ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(profile));
  82 |         _client = new GameClient(profile);
  83 | 
  84 |         // Wire GameClient events to our internal handlers. Each handler
  85 |         // updates the local state cache and raises the corresponding
  86 |         // session event (so the UI subscribes to one object).
  87 |         _client.Welcomed += OnWelcomed;
  88 |         _client.MemberJoined += m => RaiseEvent(MemberJoined, m);
  89 |         _client.MemberLeft += m => RaiseEvent(MemberLeft, m);
  90 |         _client.MemberReady += m => RaiseEvent(MemberReady, m);
  91 |         _client.StatusChanged += OnStatusChanged;
  92 |         _client.ChatReceived += c => RaiseEvent(ChatReceived, c);
  93 |         _client.ActionQueued += OnActionQueued;
  94 |         _client.ActionCancelled += OnActionCancelled;
  95 |         _client.ActionResolving += OnActionResolving;
  96 |         _client.NarrativeDelta += OnNarrativeDelta;
  97 |         _client.NarrativeFinal += OnNarrativeFinal;
  98 |         _client.StateUpdate += OnStateUpdate;
  99 |         _client.TurnEnd += OnTurnEnd;
 100 |         _client.Error += e => RaiseEvent(Error, e);
 101 |         _client.Kicked += k => RaiseEvent(Kicked, k);
 102 |         _client.LogSynced += log => RaiseEvent(LogSynced, log);
 103 |         _client.Disconnected += info => RaiseEvent(Disconnected, info);
 104 |     }

 108 |     /// <summary>
 109 |     /// The latest World received from the host. Null until the first
 110 |     /// <see cref="StateUpdateMsg"/> arrives (typically after the first
 111 |     /// GM turn). The UI should null-check before rendering.
 112 |     /// </summary>
 113 |     public GameWorld? LocalWorld
 114 |     {
 115 |         get { lock (_stateLock) return _localWorld; }
 116 |     }

 118 |     /// <summary>

 121 |     /// </summary>

 123 |     {
 124 |         get { lock (_stateLock) return _narrativeBuffer; }

 127 |     /// <summary>
 128 |     /// Local narrative + action + tool log. Built from incoming
 129 |     /// messages — NOT loaded from a save file (the joining client
 130 |     /// doesn't have the host's save). Lets the UI render the log from
 131 |     /// session start; for the full history, the client would need to
 132 |     /// request the save file separately (future feature).
 133 |     /// </summary>
 134 |     public IReadOnlyList<LogEntry> Log

 143 |     /// cancel.
 144 |     /// </summary>
 145 |     public IReadOnlyList<PlayerAction> PendingActions
 146 |     {

 148 |     }

 150 |     /// <summary>Current turn number (from the last
 151 |     /// <see cref="TurnEndMsg"/> or <see cref="StateUpdateMsg"/>).</summary>

 154 |         get { lock (_stateLock) return _turn; }
 155 |     }

 157 |     /// <summary>Current party status (from the last
 158 |     /// <see cref="StatusChangedMsg"/> or <see cref="WelcomeMsg"/>).</summary>
 159 |     public PartyStatus Status
 160 |     {
 161 |         get { lock (_stateLock) return _status; }
 162 |     }

 164 |     /// <summary>Save id the party is playing (from the
 165 |     /// <see cref="WelcomeMsg"/> or <see cref="StatusChangedMsg"/>).</summary>

 169 |     }

 171 |     // ─── Events (UI surface) ─────────────────────────────────────────

 173 |     /// <summary>Raised when the handshake completes. Carries the
 174 |     /// WelcomeMsg (which contains the party snapshot + assigned role).</summary>

 185 |

 189 |     /// <summary>Chat message received.</summary>
 190 |     public event Action<ChatMsg>? ChatReceived;
 191 | 
 192 |     /// <summary>An action was queued (by anyone — including this
 193 |     /// client, echoed back by the host).</summary>
 194 |     public event Action<ActionQueuedMsg>? ActionQueued;
 195 | 
 196 |     /// <summary>An action was cancelled.</summary>
 197 |     public event Action<ActionCancelledMsg>? ActionCancelled;
 198 | 
 199 |     /// <summary>The host started resolving a batch of actions.</summary>
 200 |     public event Action<ActionResolvingMsg>? ActionResolving;
 201 | 
 202 |     /// <summary>Streaming narrative delta. Append to UI narration.</summary>
 203 |     public event Action<NarrativeDeltaMsg>? NarrativeDelta;
 204 | 
 205 |     /// <summary>Final narration for the turn (full text + tool events).</summary>

 223 |     /// <summary>WebSocket connection dropped. Carries the close reason
 224 |     /// (may be empty) and an <see cref="DisconnectedInfo.Intentional"/>
 225 |     /// flag distinguishing user/host-initiated disconnects (Leave button,
 226 |     /// kick) from network drops. The UI uses the flag to decide whether
 227 |     /// to show a reconnect overlay.</summary>

 230 |     // ─── Connect / disconnect ────────────────────────────────────────
 231 | 
 232 |     /// <summary>
 233 |     /// Connect to a host. Performs the HelloMsg → WelcomeMsg handshake.
 234 |     /// Returns the WelcomeMsg on success; throws on reject/timeout.
 235 |     /// </summary>
 236 |     public async Task<WelcomeMsg> ConnectAsync(string host, int port, CancellationToken ct = default)
 237 |     {
 238 |         var welcome = await _client.ConnectAsync(host, port, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 239 |         // OnWelcomed (the event handler) runs the state-cache update.
 240 |         return welcome;
 241 |     }
 242 | 
 243 |     /// <summary>Graceful disconnect. Marks the disconnect as intentional
 244 |     /// (the user clicked Leave) so the <see cref="Disconnected"/> event
 245 |     /// suppresses the reconnect overlay.</summary>
 246 |     public Task DisconnectAsync() => _client.DisconnectAsync();
 247 | 
 248 |     /// <summary>
 249 |     /// Re-connect to the same host/port as the last successful
 250 |     /// <see cref="ConnectAsync"/>. Performs the HelloMsg → WelcomeMsg
 251 |     /// handshake again. On success, raises <see cref="Welcomed"/> with
 252 |     /// the fresh WelcomeMsg (which carries a fresh party snapshot —
 253 |     /// the host re-sends members, save id, turn, etc.).
 254 |     ///
 255 |     /// <para>
 256 |     /// On failure (host still unreachable, handshake timeout, reject),
 257 |     /// the method throws — the caller (GameViewModel) catches it and
 258 |     /// retries up to 3 times with 2-second backoff before giving up and
 259 |     /// showing the "Не удалось переподключиться" exit-only overlay.
 260 |     /// </para>
 261 |     /// </summary>
 262 |     public async Task<WelcomeMsg> ReconnectAsync(CancellationToken ct = default)
 263 |     {
 264 |         var welcome = await _client.ReconnectAsync(ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 265 |         // OnWelcomed (the GameClient event handler) runs the state-cache
 266 |         // update + raises the session-level Welcomed event, so the UI
 267 |         // refreshes its members list + party status from the fresh
 268 |         // snapshot.
 269 |         return welcome;
 270 |     }
 271 | 
 272 |     // ─── Outgoing player actions / chat ──────────────────────────────
 273 | 
 274 |     /// <summary>
 275 |     /// Submit an action on behalf of the local player. Generates an
 276 |     /// action id locally, sends <see cref="ActionQueuedMsg"/> to the
 277 |     /// host (which re-broadcasts to everyone including this client),
 278 |     /// and tracks the action in <see cref="PendingActions"/> for
 279 |     /// cancel support.
 280 |     /// </summary>
 281 |     public async Task SubmitActionAsync(string text, CancellationToken ct = default)
 282 |     {
 283 |         if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(text))
 284 |             throw new ArgumentException("Action text cannot be empty.", nameof(text));
 285 | 
 286 |         var actionId = NewActionId();
 287 |         var trimmed = text.Trim();
 288 |         if (trimmed.Length > 4000) trimmed = trimmed[..4000];
 289 | 
 290 |         var ts = DateTimeOffset.UtcNow;
 291 |         var action = new PlayerAction
 292 |         {
 293 |             Id = actionId,
 294 |             PlayerId = _client.ConnectionId,
 295 |             PlayerNickname = _profile.Nickname,
 296 |             Text = trimmed,
 297 |             SubmittedAt = ts,
 298 |         };
 299 | 
 300 |         // Track locally so we can cancel later + show as "pending" in UI.
 301 |         lock (_stateLock) _pendingActions[actionId] = action;
 302 | 
 303 |         var msg = new ActionQueuedMsg
 304 |         {
 305 |             ActionId = actionId,
 306 |             FromId = _client.ConnectionId,
 307 |             FromNickname = _profile.Nickname,
 308 |             Text = trimmed,
 309 |             Ts = ts,
 310 |         };
 311 |         await _client.SendAsync(msg, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 312 |     }
 313 | 
 314 |     /// <summary>
 315 |     /// Cancel a pending action by id. Sends
 316 |     /// <see cref="ActionCancelMsg"/> to the host. Returns true if the
 317 |     /// action was in the local pending list (the host's actual cancel
 318 |     /// confirmation comes back via <see cref="ActionCancelledMsg"/>
 319 |     /// and is handled in <see cref="OnActionCancelled"/>).
 320 |     /// </summary>
 321 |     public async Task<bool> CancelActionAsync(string actionId, CancellationToken ct = default)
 322 |     {
 323 |         if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(actionId)) return false;
 324 | 
 325 |         bool wasPending;
 326 |         lock (_stateLock) wasPending = _pendingActions.ContainsKey(actionId);
 327 |         if (!wasPending) return false;
 328 | 
 329 |         await _client.SendAsync(new ActionCancelMsg { ActionId = actionId }, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 330 |         return true;
 331 |     }
 332 | 
 333 |     /// <summary>Send a lobby chat message.</summary>
 334 |     public async Task SendChatAsync(string text, CancellationToken ct = default)
 335 |     {
 336 |         if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(text)) return;
 337 |         var trimmed = text.Trim();
 338 |         if (trimmed.Length > 1000) trimmed = trimmed[..1000];
 339 | 
 340 |         var msg = new ChatMsg
 341 |         {
 342 |             // The host rewrites fromId + fromNickname from the socket
 343 |             // identity, so these fields are technically ignored — but we
 344 |             // send them anyway for symmetry (and in case a future host
 345 |             // trusts the client).
 346 |             FromId = _client.ConnectionId,
 347 |             FromNickname = _profile.Nickname,
 348 |             Text = trimmed,
 349 |             Ts = DateTimeOffset.UtcNow,
 350 |         };
 351 |         await _client.SendAsync(msg, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 352 |     }
 353 | 
 354 |     /// <summary>
 355 |     /// Toggle this client's ready state in the lobby. Sends
 356 |     /// <see cref="MemberReadyMsg"/> to the host (which re-broadcasts).
 357 |     /// </summary>
 358 |     public Task SetReadyAsync(bool ready, CancellationToken ct = default) =>
 359 |         _client.SendAsync(new MemberReadyMsg
 360 |         {
 361 |             ConnectionId = _client.ConnectionId,
 362 |             Ready = ready,
 363 |         }, ct);
 364 | 
 365 |     // ─── Event handlers (update local cache + raise session events) ──
 366 | 
 367 |     private void OnWelcomed(WelcomeMsg w)
 368 |     {
 369 |         lock (_stateLock)
 370 |         {
 371 |             _status = w.Party.Status;
 372 |             _saveId = w.Party.SaveId;
 373 |             _turn = w.Party.Turn;
 374 |         }
 375 |         RaiseEvent(Welcomed, w);
 376 |     }
 377 | 
 378 |     private void OnStatusChanged(StatusChangedMsg s)
 379 |     {
 380 |         lock (_stateLock)
 381 |         {
 382 |             _status = s.Status;
 383 |             if (s.SaveId is not null) _saveId = s.SaveId;
 384 |             if (s.Turn > 0) _turn = s.Turn;
 385 |         }
 386 |         RaiseEvent(StatusChanged, s);
 387 |     }
 388 | 
 389 |     private void OnActionQueued(ActionQueuedMsg msg)
 390 |     {
 391 |         // Append to local log (so the UI's log view shows actions from
 392 |         // other players too).
 393 |         lock (_stateLog)
 394 |         {
 395 |             _log.Add(LogEntry.Action(
 396 |                 $"{msg.FromNickname}: {msg.Text}",
 397 |                 authorId: msg.FromId.ToString()));
 398 |         }
 399 | 
 400 |         // If this is our own action echoed back, it's already in
 401 |         // PendingActions — no need to re-add. (We could double-check
 402 |         // the actionId matches and update the SubmittedAt, but it's
 403 |         // already correct from the local SubmitActionAsync.)
 404 | 
 405 |         RaiseEvent(ActionQueued, msg);
 406 |     }
 407 | 
 408 |     private void OnActionCancelled(ActionCancelledMsg msg)
 409 |     {
 410 |         lock (_stateLock)
 411 |         {
 412 |             _pendingActions.Remove(msg.ActionId);
 413 |         }
 414 |         RaiseEvent(ActionCancelled, msg);
 415 |     }
 416 | 
 417 |     private void OnActionResolving(ActionResolvingMsg msg)
 418 |     {
 419 |         // The host has picked up these actions for resolution — remove
 420 |         // them from our local pending list (they're no longer
 421 |         // cancellable).
 422 |         lock (_stateLock)
 423 |         {
 424 |             foreach (var id in msg.ActionIds)
 425 |             {
 426 |                 _pendingActions.Remove(id);
 427 |             }
 428 |         }
 429 |         RaiseEvent(ActionResolving, msg);
 430 |     }
 431 | 
 432 |     private void OnNarrativeDelta(NarrativeDeltaMsg msg)
 433 |     {
 434 |         lock (_stateLock)
 435 |         {
 436 |             _narrativeBuffer += msg.TextDelta;
 437 |         }
 438 |         RaiseEvent(NarrativeDelta, msg);
 439 |     }
 440 | 
 441 |     private void OnNarrativeFinal(NarrativeFinalMsg msg)
 442 |     {
 443 |         lock (_stateLock)
 444 |         {
 445 |             // The final message is the canonical full text — replace
 446 |             // the buffer (in case any deltas were lost or out of order).
 447 |             _narrativeBuffer = msg.FullText;
 448 |             // Append to log.
 449 |             _log.Add(LogEntry.Narrative(msg.FullText));
 450 |             // Append tool events.
 451 |             foreach (var tc in msg.ToolEvents)
 452 |             {
 453 |                 _log.Add(LogEntry.Tool(
 454 |                     $"{tc.Name}: {tc.Result}",
 455 |                     metadata: new Dictionary<string, object>
 456 |                     {
 457 |                         ["name"] = tc.Name,
 458 |                         ["args"] = tc.ArgsJson,
 459 |                         ["isError"] = tc.IsError,
 460 |                     }));
 461 |             }
 462 |         }
 463 |         RaiseEvent(NarrativeFinal, msg);
 464 |     }
 465 | 
 466 |     private void OnStateUpdate(StateUpdateMsg msg)
 467 |     {
 468 |         // Deserialise the world snapshot. Use the default content
 469 |         // registry (same as SaveManager.LoadWorld does when no
 470 |         // registry is injected).
 471 |         GameWorld? newWorld = null;
 472 |         try
 473 |         {
 474 |             if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(msg.WorldJson))
 475 |             {
 476 |                 newWorld = GameWorld.FromJson(msg.WorldJson, ContentRegistry.LoadDefault());
 477 |             }
 478 |         }
 479 |         catch (Exception ex)
 480 |         {
 481 |             Trace.WriteLine($"[ClientSession] Failed to deserialise world snapshot: {ex}");
 482 |         }
 483 | 
 484 |         lock (_stateLock)
 485 |         {
 486 |             if (newWorld is not null) _localWorld = newWorld;
 487 |             _turn = msg.Turn;
 488 |         }
 489 |         RaiseEvent(StateUpdate, msg);
 490 |     }
 491 | 
 492 |     private void OnTurnEnd(TurnEndMsg msg)
 493 |     {
 494 |         lock (_stateLock)
 495 |         {
 496 |             _turn = msg.TurnNumber;
 497 |         }
 498 |         RaiseEvent(TurnEnd, msg);
 499 |     }
 500 | 
 501 |     // ─── Helpers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 502 | 
 503 |     private static string NewActionId() => Guid.NewGuid().ToString("N");
 504 | 
 505 |     /// <summary>
 506 |     /// Alias used inside OnActionQueued to take the same lock as the
 507 |     /// other state mutations. (C# doesn't let us refer to <c>_stateLock</c>
 508 |     /// from a field initializer, so we use a property that returns it.)
 509 |     /// </summary>
 510 |     private object _stateLog => _stateLock;
 511 | 
 512 |     /// <summary>Exception-safe event raise.</summary>
 513 |     private static void RaiseEvent<T>(Action<T>? handler, T args)
 514 |     {
 515 |         if (handler is null) return;
 516 |         var delegates = handler.GetInvocationList();
 517 |         foreach (var d in delegates)
 518 |         {
 519 |             try { ((Action<T>)d)(args); }
 520 |             catch (Exception ex) { Trace.WriteLine($"[ClientSession] event subscriber threw: {ex}"); }
 521 |         }
 522 |     }
 523 | }

--- src/MyGame.Core/Multiplayer/GameClient.cs (C#, 612 lines, ~7,197 tokens) ---
   1 | using System.Diagnostics;
   2 | using System.Net.WebSockets;
   3 | using System.Text;
   4 | using MyGame.Core.Multiplayer.Protocol;
   5 | using MyGame.Core.Profile;
   6 | 
   7 | namespace MyGame.Core.Multiplayer;
   8 | 
   9 | /// <summary>
  10 | /// Outgoing WebSocket client. Replaces the TS source's
  11 | /// <c>use-websocket.ts</c> (socket.io-client React hook) with a plain
  12 | /// <see cref="ClientWebSocket"/> connection to a host's
  13 | /// <c>ws://&lt;host&gt;:&lt;port&gt;/</c> endpoint.
  14 | ///
  15 | /// <para>
  16 | /// When the user clicks "Join Game", the UI constructs a
  17 | /// <see cref="GameClient"/> with the local <c>Profile</c>, calls
  18 | /// <see cref="ConnectAsync"/> with the host's IP + port, and the client
  19 | /// performs the HelloMsg → WelcomeMsg handshake. Subsequent incoming
  20 | /// messages are dispatched to typed events the UI can subscribe to.
  21 | /// </para>
  22 | ///
  23 | /// <para><b>Architecture notes (deviations from the TS source):</b>
  24 | /// <list type="bullet">
  25 | ///   <item>NO socket.io-client. Raw <see cref="ClientWebSocket"/> carrying
  26 | ///     JSON-serialised <see cref="NetMessage"/>s.</item>
  27 | ///   <item>NO React hook. The client is a plain class with C# events. The
  28 | ///     Avalonia UI layer wires up an <c>IObservable</c> adapter or calls
  29 | ///     <c>Dispatcher.UIThread.Post</c> from the event handlers.</item>
  30 | ///   <item>Manual reconnection via <see cref="ReconnectAsync"/>. When the
  31 | ///     connection drops unexpectedly, the <see cref="Disconnected"/> event
  32 | ///     fires with <see cref="DisconnectedInfo.Intentional"/> = false; the
  33 | ///     UI is responsible for showing a "Reconnect?" dialog and calling
  34 | ///     <see cref="ReconnectAsync"/> on user accept. (The TS source had
  35 | ///     auto-reconnect via socket.io; the C# port makes it user-driven so
  36 | ///     the player can choose to bail to menu instead.)</item>
  37 | ///   <item>NO JWT / cookies. The client sends a <see cref="HelloMsg"/>
  38 | ///     with the local <c>Profile</c> identity in the first frame after
  39 | ///     the WebSocket upgrade.</item>
  40 | /// </list>
  41 | /// </para>
  42 | ///
  43 | /// <para><b>Thread safety:</b> sends are serialised by a per-client
  44 | /// <see cref="SemaphoreSlim"/> (because <see cref="WebSocket.SendAsync"/>
  45 | /// is NOT safe for concurrent callers on the same instance). The read
  46 | /// loop runs on a ThreadPool task; event handlers are invoked on that
  47 | /// same task (UI subscribers must marshal to the UI thread).</para>
  48 | /// </summary>
  49 | public sealed class GameClient
  50 | {
  51 |     // ─── Config ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  52 | 
  53 |     private readonly Profile.Profile _profile;
  54 | 
  55 |     // ─── Runtime state ───────────────────────────────────────────────
  56 | 
  57 |     private ClientWebSocket? _ws;
  58 |     private Task? _readLoopTask;
  59 |     private readonly SemaphoreSlim _sendSemaphore = new(1, 1);
  60 |     // Non-readonly: ReconnectAsync may swap this for a fresh CTS when the
  61 |     // previous one was cancelled (e.g. by DisconnectAsync).
  62 |     private CancellationTokenSource _ownCts = new();
  63 | 
  64 |     // Host + port from the last successful ConnectAsync. Stored so
  65 |     // ReconnectAsync can re-run the handshake without the caller having
  66 |     // to pass them again. Null/0 until the first successful connect.
  67 |     private string? _host;
  68 |     private int _port;
  69 | 
  70 |     // Set to true by DisconnectAsync (the user clicked Leave) or by the
  71 |     // Kicked handler (the host kicked us). The read loop reads this when
  72 |     // raising Disconnected so the UI knows whether to show a reconnect
  73 |     // overlay (network drop) or not (intentional leave / kick). Reset to
  74 |     // false at the start of every ConnectAsync / ReconnectAsync call.
  75 |     private volatile bool _intentionalDisconnect;
  76 | 
  77 |     /// <summary>
  78 |     /// The connection id assigned by the host in <see cref="WelcomeMsg"/>.
  79 |     /// Valid only after <see cref="Welcomed"/> fires. Use this as the
  80 |     /// <c>fromId</c> on outgoing <see cref="ChatMsg"/> /
  81 |     /// <see cref="ActionQueuedMsg"/> (although the host rewrites fromId
  82 |     /// from the socket identity anyway, sending the correct id lets the
  83 |     /// host skip the rewrite as an optimisation).
  84 |     /// </summary>
  85 |     public Guid ConnectionId { get; private set; }
  86 | 
  87 |     /// <summary>
  88 |     /// The role the host assigned in <see cref="WelcomeMsg"/>.
  89 |     /// Always <see cref="MemberRole.Player"/> or
  90 |     /// <see cref="MemberRole.Spectator"/> (the host is never a client).
  91 |     /// </summary>
  92 |     public MemberRole Role { get; private set; } = MemberRole.Player;
  93 | 
  94 |     /// <summary>True after <see cref="ConnectAsync"/> succeeds (the
  95 |     /// handshake completed and the read loop is running).</summary>
  96 |     public bool IsConnected => _ws is not null
  97 |         && _ws.State == WebSocketState.Open
  98 |         && ConnectionId != Guid.Empty;
  99 | 
 100 |     /// <summary>
 101 |     /// Create a client for the given local profile. Does NOT connect —
 102 |     /// call <see cref="ConnectAsync"/> to open the WebSocket.
 103 |     /// </summary>
 104 |     public GameClient(Profile.Profile profile)
 105 |     {
 106 |         _profile = profile ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(profile));
 107 |     }
 108 | 
 109 |     // ─── Events ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 110 | 
 111 |     /// <summary>Raised when the <see cref="WelcomeMsg"/> arrives and the
 112 |     /// handshake completes. <see cref="ConnectionId"/> and
 113 |     /// <see cref="Role"/> are set before this event fires.</summary>
 114 |     public event Action<WelcomeMsg>? Welcomed;
 115 | 
 116 |     /// <summary>Raised when another member joins the party (host or
 117 |     /// another client).</summary>
 118 |     public event Action<MemberJoinedMsg>? MemberJoined;
 119 | 
 120 |     /// <summary>Raised when a member leaves (disconnect or kick).</summary>
 121 |     public event Action<MemberLeftMsg>? MemberLeft;
 122 | 
 123 |     /// <summary>Raised when a member toggles their ready state.</summary>
 124 |     public event Action<MemberReadyMsg>? MemberReady;
 125 | 
 126 |     /// <summary>Raised when the party status changes (Lobby →
 127 |     /// Worldbuilding → …).</summary>
 128 |     public event Action<StatusChangedMsg>? StatusChanged;
 129 | 
 130 |     /// <summary>Raised when a chat message arrives (from anyone — the
 131 |     /// host's own chat is included).</summary>
 132 |     public event Action<ChatMsg>? ChatReceived;
 133 | 
 134 |     /// <summary>Raised when an action is queued (by anyone — including
 135 |     /// the local client's own action, echoed back by the host).</summary>
 136 |     public event Action<ActionQueuedMsg>? ActionQueued;
 137 | 
 138 |     /// <summary>Raised when an action is cancelled.</summary>
 139 |     public event Action<ActionCancelledMsg>? ActionCancelled;
 140 | 
 141 |     /// <summary>Raised when the host starts resolving a batch of actions.</summary>
 142 |     public event Action<ActionResolvingMsg>? ActionResolving;
 143 | 
 144 |     /// <summary>Raised on each streaming narrative text chunk. Append to
 145 |     /// the UI's narration buffer.</summary>
 146 |     public event Action<NarrativeDeltaMsg>? NarrativeDelta;
 147 | 
 148 |     /// <summary>Raised at the end of a GM turn with the full narration
 149 |     /// text + the tool calls made.</summary>
 150 |     public event Action<NarrativeFinalMsg>? NarrativeFinal;
 151 | 
 152 |     /// <summary>Raised when the host broadcasts a world state update
 153 |     /// (after each GM turn).</summary>
 154 |     public event Action<StateUpdateMsg>? StateUpdate;
 155 | 
 156 |     /// <summary>Raised when a GM turn ends.</summary>
 157 |     public event Action<TurnEndMsg>? TurnEnd;
 158 | 
 159 |     /// <summary>Raised when the host sends an error message.</summary>
 160 |     public event Action<ErrorMsg>? Error;
 161 | 
 162 |     /// <summary>Raised when the host kicks this client. The connection
 163 |     /// is closed immediately after this event fires.</summary>
 164 |     public event Action<KickedMsg>? Kicked;
 165 | 
 166 |     /// <summary>Issue #32: received log history from host (late joiner sync).</summary>
 167 |     public event Action<LogSyncMsg>? LogSynced;
 168 | 
 169 |     /// <summary>
 170 |     /// Raised when the WebSocket connection drops (network failure, host
 171 |     /// shutdown, graceful close, kick, or user-initiated leave). The
 172 |     /// payload is a <see cref="DisconnectedInfo"/> record carrying the
 173 |     /// close reason and an <see cref="DisconnectedInfo.Intentional"/>
 174 |     /// flag — true when the disconnect was user-initiated (Leave button)
 175 |     /// or host-initiated (kick), false when it was a network drop.
 176 |     /// </summary>
 177 |     public event Action<DisconnectedInfo>? Disconnected;
 178 | 
 179 |     // ─── Connect / disconnect ────────────────────────────────────────
 180 | 
 181 |     /// <summary>
 182 |     /// Open a WebSocket to <c>ws://&lt;host&gt;:&lt;port&gt;/</c>, send
 183 |     /// the <see cref="HelloMsg"/> handshake, and wait for the
 184 |     /// <see cref="WelcomeMsg"/> reply. Returns the WelcomeMsg on success;
 185 |     /// throws <see cref="InvalidOperationException"/> on reject or timeout.
 186 |     /// </summary>
 187 |     /// <param name="host">Host IP or hostname (e.g. "192.168.1.10").</param>
 188 |     /// <param name="port">Host's TCP port.</param>
 189 |     /// <param name="ct">Cancellation token (cancelling aborts the
 190 |     /// connect attempt + handshake).</param>
 191 |     public async Task<WelcomeMsg> ConnectAsync(string host, int port, CancellationToken ct)
 192 |     {
 193 |         if (IsConnected)
 194 |             throw new InvalidOperationException("GameClient is already connected.");
 195 |         if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(host))
 196 |             throw new ArgumentException("host is required.", nameof(host));
 197 |         if (port <= 0 || port > 65535)
 198 |             throw new ArgumentOutOfRangeException(nameof(port));
 199 | 
 200 |         // Store host/port for ReconnectAsync. Reset the intentional flag
 201 |         // — a fresh connect is by definition not a disconnect.
 202 |         _host = host;
 203 |         _port = port;
 204 |         _intentionalDisconnect = false;
 205 | 
 206 |         // Link the caller's CT with our own (so DisconnectAsync can
 207 |         // cancel an in-flight connect too).
 208 |         using var linked = CancellationTokenSource.CreateLinkedTokenSource(ct, _ownCts.Token);
 209 | 
 210 |         var ws = new ClientWebSocket();
 211 |         try
 212 |         {
 213 |             await ws.ConnectAsync(new Uri($"ws://{host}:{port}/"), linked.Token).ConfigureAwait(false);
 214 |         }
 215 |         catch (Exception ex)
 216 |         {
 217 |             ws.Dispose();
 218 |             throw new InvalidOperationException($"Failed to connect to ws://{host}:{port}/: {ex.Message}", ex);
 219 |         }
 220 | 
 221 |         _ws = ws;
 222 | 
 223 |         // Send HelloMsg as the first frame.
 224 |         var hello = new HelloMsg
 225 |         {
 226 |             ProtocolVersion = Common.Version.Current,
 227 |             Nickname = _profile.Nickname,
 228 |             ProfileId = _profile.Id,
 229 |         };
 230 |         await SendAsyncInternalAsync(hello, linked.Token).ConfigureAwait(false);
 231 | 
 232 |         // Wait for WelcomeMsg (or RejectMsg). The handshake has a
 233 |         // 15-second timeout so a dead host doesn't hang the UI forever.
 234 |         using var handshakeCts = new CancellationTokenSource(TimeSpan.FromSeconds(15));
 235 |         using var handshakeLinked = CancellationTokenSource.CreateLinkedTokenSource(linked.Token, handshakeCts.Token);
 236 | 
 237 |         WelcomeMsg? welcome = null;
 238 |         RejectMsg? reject = null;
 239 | 
 240 |         // Read messages synchronously until we get Welcome or Reject.
 241 |         // (The full read loop starts after the handshake.)
 242 |         try
 243 |         {
 244 |             while (welcome is null && reject is null)
 245 |             {
 246 |                 var (json, closed) = await ReceiveMessageAsync(ws, handshakeLinked.Token).ConfigureAwait(false);
 247 |                 if (closed)
 248 |                 {
 249 |                     throw new InvalidOperationException("Connection closed during handshake.");
 250 |                 }
 251 | 
 252 |                 var msg = MultiplayerJson.Deserialize(json);
 253 |                 switch (msg)
 254 |                 {
 255 |                     case WelcomeMsg w:
 256 |                         welcome = w;
 257 |                         break;
 258 |                     case RejectMsg r:
 259 |                         reject = r;
 260 |                         break;
 261 |                     // Ignore other messages during handshake (shouldn't happen
 262 |                     // but be tolerant — a misbehaving host might send a
 263 |                     // MemberJoinedMsg before Welcome).
 264 |                     default:
 265 |                         Trace.WriteLine($"[GameClient] Ignored pre-handshake message: {msg?.GetType().Name ?? "null"}");
 266 |                         break;
 267 |                 }
 268 |             }
 269 |         }
 270 |         catch (OperationCanceledException) when (handshakeCts.IsCancellationRequested)
 271 |         {
 272 |             await CloseAsyncInternalAsync(ws, WebSocketCloseStatus.NormalClosure, "handshake timeout").ConfigureAwait(false);
 273 |             throw new InvalidOperationException("Handshake timed out waiting for Welcome.");
 274 |         }
 275 | 
 276 |         if (reject is not null)
 277 |         {
 278 |             await CloseAsyncInternalAsync(ws, WebSocketCloseStatus.NormalClosure, "rejected").ConfigureAwait(false);
 279 |             throw new InvalidOperationException($"Host rejected connection: {reject.Reason}");
 280 |         }
 281 | 
 282 |         // Handshake succeeded — set the connection id + role, fire the
 283 |         // Welcomed event, and start the read loop.
 284 |         ConnectionId = welcome!.ConnectionId;
 285 |         Role = welcome.Role;
 286 | 
 287 |         _readLoopTask = Task.Run(() => ReadLoopAsync(_ownCts.Token));
 288 | 
 289 |         RaiseEvent(Welcomed, welcome);
 290 |         return welcome;
 291 |     }
 292 | 
 293 |     /// <summary>
 294 |     /// Send a message to the host. Serialised by the per-client
 295 |     /// <see cref="_sendSemaphore"/> so concurrent sends don't corrupt the
 296 |     /// WebSocket.
 297 |     /// </summary>
 298 |     public async Task SendAsync(NetMessage msg, CancellationToken ct)
 299 |     {
 300 |         var ws = _ws;
 301 |         if (ws is null || ws.State != WebSocketState.Open)
 302 |             throw new InvalidOperationException("GameClient is not connected.");
 303 | 
 304 |         await SendAsyncInternalAsync(msg, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 305 |     }
 306 | 
 307 |     /// <summary>
 308 |     /// Graceful disconnect: send a Close frame, wait for the read loop to
 309 |     /// finish, dispose the WebSocket. Safe to call multiple times. Marks
 310 |     /// the disconnect as intentional (<see cref="_intentionalDisconnect"/>)
 311 |     /// so the <see cref="Disconnected"/> event (if it fires — see the
 312 |     /// read-loop finally block) carries <see cref="DisconnectedInfo.Intentional"/>
 313 |     /// = true, telling the UI NOT to show the reconnect overlay.
 314 |     /// </summary>
 315 |     public async Task DisconnectAsync()
 316 |     {
 317 |         _intentionalDisconnect = true;
 318 |         _ownCts.Cancel();
 319 | 
 320 |         var ws = Interlocked.Exchange(ref _ws, null);
 321 |         if (ws is not null)
 322 |         {
 323 |             await CloseAsyncInternalAsync(ws, WebSocketCloseStatus.NormalClosure, "client disconnect").ConfigureAwait(false);
 324 |             ws.Dispose();
 325 |         }
 326 | 
 327 |         if (_readLoopTask is not null)
 328 |         {
 329 |             try { await _readLoopTask.ConfigureAwait(false); } catch { /* ignore */ }
 330 |             _readLoopTask = null;
 331 |         }
 332 |     }
 333 | 
 334 |     /// <summary>
 335 |     /// Re-connect to the same host/port as the last successful
 336 |     /// <see cref="ConnectAsync"/>. Performs the same HelloMsg →
 337 |     /// WelcomeMsg handshake. Reuses the stored host + port (set on the
 338 |     /// first successful connect); throws if no previous connect was
 339 |     /// made. Returns the fresh WelcomeMsg on success.
 340 |     ///
 341 |     /// <para>
 342 |     /// Cleans up the previous (now-dead) socket + read loop before
 343 |     /// re-connecting. The intentional-disconnect flag is reset to false
 344 |     /// (a reconnect attempt is by definition not intentional — if this
 345 |     /// one also drops, the UI should show the overlay again).
 346 |     /// </para>
 347 |     /// </summary>
 348 |     /// <exception cref="InvalidOperationException">
 349 |     /// No previous host/port stored (ConnectAsync was never called).
 350 |     /// </exception>
 351 |     public async Task<WelcomeMsg> ReconnectAsync(CancellationToken ct)
 352 |     {
 353 |         if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(_host) || _port <= 0)
 354 |             throw new InvalidOperationException("No previous host/port to reconnect to.");
 355 | 
 356 |         // Reset the intentional flag — this is a fresh attempt.
 357 |         _intentionalDisconnect = false;
 358 | 
 359 |         // Clean up the previous (now-dead) socket + read loop. The read
 360 |         // loop should already have exited (the network drop broke the
 361 |         // receive), but defensively await it to be sure no stale task
 362 |         // is left running.
 363 |         var oldWs = Interlocked.Exchange(ref _ws, null);
 364 |         if (oldWs is not null)
 365 |         {
 366 |             try { oldWs.Dispose(); } catch { /* ignore */ }
 367 |         }
 368 |         if (_readLoopTask is not null)
 369 |         {
 370 |             try { await _readLoopTask.ConfigureAwait(false); } catch { /* ignore */ }
 371 |             _readLoopTask = null;
 372 |         }
 373 | 
 374 |         // If the previous CTS was cancelled (e.g. by DisconnectAsync),
 375 |         // replace it with a fresh one so the new read loop can run.
 376 |         // (ConnectAsync links _ownCts.Token into its own linked CTS, so
 377 |         // a cancelled _ownCts would break the new handshake.)
 378 |         if (_ownCts.IsCancellationRequested)
 379 |         {
 380 |             _ownCts.Dispose();
 381 |             _ownCts = new CancellationTokenSource();
 382 |         }
 383 | 
 384 |         // Reset handshake-derived state so IsConnected returns false
 385 |         // until the new Welcome arrives.
 386 |         ConnectionId = Guid.Empty;
 387 |         Role = MemberRole.Player;
 388 | 
 389 |         return await ConnectAsync(_host, _port, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 390 |     }
 391 | 
 392 |     // ─── Read loop ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
 393 | 
 394 |     private async Task ReadLoopAsync(CancellationToken ct)
 395 |     {
 396 |         var ws = _ws;
 397 |         if (ws is null) return;
 398 | 
 399 |         try
 400 |         {
 401 |             while (!ct.IsCancellationRequested && ws.State == WebSocketState.Open)
 402 |             {
 403 |                 string json;
 404 |                 bool closed;
 405 |                 try
 406 |                 {
 407 |                     (json, closed) = await ReceiveMessageAsync(ws, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 408 |                 }
 409 |                 catch (OperationCanceledException) when (ct.IsCancellationRequested)
 410 |                 {
 411 |                     break;
 412 |                 }
 413 |                 catch (WebSocketException ex)
 414 |                 {
 415 |                     Trace.WriteLine($"[GameClient] Read failed: {ex.Message}");
 416 |                     RaiseEvent(Disconnected, new DisconnectedInfo(ex.Message, _intentionalDisconnect));
 417 |                     break;
 418 |                 }
 419 | 
 420 |                 if (closed)
 421 |                 {
 422 |                     RaiseEvent(Disconnected, new DisconnectedInfo("server closed connection", _intentionalDisconnect));
 423 |                     break;
 424 |                 }
 425 | 
 426 |                 NetMessage? msg;
 427 |                 try
 428 |                 {
 429 |                     msg = MultiplayerJson.Deserialize(json);
 430 |                 }
 431 |                 catch (Exception ex)
 432 |                 {
 433 |                     Trace.WriteLine($"[GameClient] Failed to parse message: {ex.Message}");
 434 |                     continue;
 435 |                 }
 436 | 
 437 |                 if (msg is null) continue;
 438 | 
 439 |                 try
 440 |                 {
 441 |                     DispatchIncoming(msg);
 442 |                 }
 443 |                 catch (Exception ex)
 444 |                 {
 445 |                     Trace.WriteLine($"[GameClient] Dispatch failed for {msg.GetType().Name}: {ex}");
 446 |                 }
 447 |             }
 448 |         }
 449 |         finally
 450 |         {
 451 |             // If the loop exits without Disconnected having been raised
 452 |             // (e.g. via cancellation that wasn't from DisconnectAsync —
 453 |             // an intentional cancel suppresses this via the
 454 |             // !ct.IsCancellationRequested check), raise it now so the
 455 |             // UI updates.
 456 |             if (!ct.IsCancellationRequested)
 457 |             {
 458 |                 RaiseEvent(Disconnected, new DisconnectedInfo("connection lost", _intentionalDisconnect));
 459 |             }
 460 |         }
 461 |     }
 462 | 
 463 |     private void DispatchIncoming(NetMessage msg)
 464 |     {
 465 |         switch (msg)
 466 |         {
 467 |             case WelcomeMsg w:        RaiseEvent(Welcomed, w); break;
 468 |             case MemberJoinedMsg m:   RaiseEvent(MemberJoined, m); break;
 469 |             case MemberLeftMsg m:     RaiseEvent(MemberLeft, m); break;
 470 |             case MemberReadyMsg m:    RaiseEvent(MemberReady, m); break;
 471 |             case StatusChangedMsg s:  RaiseEvent(StatusChanged, s); break;
 472 |             case ChatMsg c:           RaiseEvent(ChatReceived, c); break;
 473 |             case ActionQueuedMsg a:   RaiseEvent(ActionQueued, a); break;
 474 |             case ActionCancelledMsg a:RaiseEvent(ActionCancelled, a); break;
 475 |             case ActionResolvingMsg a:RaiseEvent(ActionResolving, a); break;
 476 |             case NarrativeDeltaMsg n: RaiseEvent(NarrativeDelta, n); break;
 477 |             case NarrativeFinalMsg n: RaiseEvent(NarrativeFinal, n); break;
 478 |             case StateUpdateMsg s:    RaiseEvent(StateUpdate, s); break;
 479 |             case TurnEndMsg t:        RaiseEvent(TurnEnd, t); break;
 480 |             case ErrorMsg e:          RaiseEvent(Error, e); break;
 481 |             case KickedMsg k:
 482 |                 RaiseEvent(Kicked, k);
 483 |                 // Mark the subsequent Disconnected (raised when the host
 484 |                 // closes the socket) as intentional — the user was
 485 |                 // kicked, not network-dropped. Suppresses the reconnect
 486 |                 // overlay.
 487 |                 _intentionalDisconnect = true;
 488 |                 break;
 489 |             case PingMsg ping:
 490 |                 // Reply with PongMsg echoing the Ts. Fire-and-forget —
 491 |                 // the read loop can't await without blocking subsequent
 492 |                 // messages. Best-effort: a failed pong just means the
 493 |                 // host's keepalive will time out later.
 494 |                 _ = Task.Run(async () =>
 495 |                 {
 496 |                     try { await SendAsync(new PongMsg { Ts = ping.Ts }, CancellationToken.None).ConfigureAwait(false); }
 497 |                     catch { /* ignore */ }
 498 |                 });
 499 |                 break;
 500 |             case PongMsg:
 501 |                 break;
 502 |             case LogSyncMsg log:
 503 |                 RaiseEvent(LogSynced, log);
 504 |                 break;
 505 |             case HelloMsg:
 506 |             case RejectMsg:
 507 |                 // Server-only messages — ignore.
 508 |                 break;
 509 |             default:
 510 |                 Trace.WriteLine($"[GameClient] Unhandled message kind: {msg.GetType().Name}");
 511 |                 break;
 512 |         }
 513 |     }
 514 | 
 515 |     // ─── Internal helpers ────────────────────────────────────────────
 516 | 
 517 |     private async Task SendAsyncInternalAsync(NetMessage msg, CancellationToken ct)
 518 |     {
 519 |         var ws = _ws;
 520 |         if (ws is null) return;
 521 | 
 522 |         var bytes = MultiplayerJson.SerializeToUtf8Bytes(msg);
 523 |         await _sendSemaphore.WaitAsync(ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 524 |         try
 525 |         {
 526 |             if (ws.State == WebSocketState.Open)
 527 |             {
 528 |                 await ws.SendAsync(new ArraySegment<byte>(bytes), WebSocketMessageType.Text, endOfMessage: true, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 529 |             }
 530 |         }
 531 |         finally
 532 |         {
 533 |             _sendSemaphore.Release();
 534 |         }
 535 |     }
 536 | 
 537 |     private static async Task CloseAsyncInternalAsync(WebSocket ws, WebSocketCloseStatus status, string description)
 538 |     {
 539 |         try
 540 |         {
 541 |             if (ws.State == WebSocketState.Open)
 542 |             {
 543 |                 await ws.CloseAsync(status, description, CancellationToken.None).ConfigureAwait(false);
 544 |             }
 545 |         }
 546 |         catch { /* ignore */ }
 547 |     }
 548 | 
 549 |     /// <summary>
 550 |     /// Receive one full WebSocket text message. Same accumulation logic
 551 |     /// as <c>HostServer.ReceiveMessageAsync</c> — duplicated here to keep
 552 |     /// the two classes independent (they don't share a base class).
 553 |     /// </summary>
 554 |     private static async Task<(string text, bool closed)> ReceiveMessageAsync(WebSocket ws, CancellationToken ct)
 555 |     {
 556 |         using var ms = new MemoryStream();
 557 |         var buffer = new byte[4096];
 558 |         while (true)
 559 |         {
 560 |             ct.ThrowIfCancellationRequested();
 561 |             var result = await ws.ReceiveAsync(new ArraySegment<byte>(buffer), ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 562 | 
 563 |             if (result.MessageType == WebSocketMessageType.Close)
 564 |             {
 565 |                 return (string.Empty, true);
 566 |             }
 567 | 
 568 |             if (result.Count > 0)
 569 |             {
 570 |                 ms.Write(buffer, 0, result.Count);
 571 |             }
 572 | 
 573 |             if (result.EndOfMessage) break;
 574 |         }
 575 | 
 576 |         var text = Encoding.UTF8.GetString(ms.GetBuffer(), 0, (int)ms.Length);
 577 |         return (text, false);
 578 |     }
 579 | 
 580 |     /// <summary>
 581 |     /// Raise an event with exception-safe dispatch. Each subscriber is
 582 |     /// wrapped in try/catch; one bad subscriber doesn't break the others.
 583 |     /// </summary>
 584 |     private static void RaiseEvent<T>(Action<T>? handler, T args)
 585 |     {
 586 |         if (handler is null) return;
 587 |         var delegates = handler.GetInvocationList();
 588 |         foreach (var d in delegates)
 589 |         {
 590 |             try { ((Action<T>)d)(args); }
 591 |             catch (Exception ex) { Trace.WriteLine($"[GameClient] event subscriber threw: {ex}"); }
 592 |         }
 593 |     }
 594 | }
 595 | 
 596 | /// <summary>
 597 | /// Payload for <see cref="GameClient.Disconnected"/>. Carries the
 598 | /// close reason (free-form string from the WebSocket close frame or
 599 | /// the read-loop's exception message) and an
 600 | /// <see cref="Intentional"/> flag distinguishing user/host-initiated
 601 | /// disconnects (Leave button, kick) from network drops.
 602 | ///
 603 | /// <para>
 604 | /// The UI uses <see cref="Intentional"/> to decide whether to show the
 605 | /// reconnect overlay: <c>false</c> → show overlay (network drop, offer
 606 | /// "Переподключиться" / "Выйти в меню"); <c>true</c> → suppress the
 607 | /// overlay (the Leave command already navigates to menu; a kick shows
 608 | /// its own inline error via <see cref="GameClient.Kicked"/>).
 609 | /// </para>
 610 | /// </summary>
 611 | public sealed record DisconnectedInfo(string Reason, bool Intentional);

--- src/MyGame.Core/Multiplayer/HostServer.cs (C#, 1094 lines, ~12,178 tokens) ---
   1 | using System.Collections.Concurrent;
   2 | using System.Diagnostics;
   3 | using System.Net;
   4 | using System.Net.WebSockets;
   5 | using System.Text;
   6 | using MyGame.Core.Multiplayer.Protocol;
   7 | using MyGame.Core.Profile;
   8 | 
   9 | namespace MyGame.Core.Multiplayer;
  10 | 
  11 | /// <summary>
  12 | /// In-process WebSocket host server. Replaces the TS source's
  13 | /// <c>mini-services/ws-server/index.ts</c> (socket.io + Next.js) with a
  14 | /// raw <see cref="HttpListener"/>-based WebSocket server running inside
  15 | /// the desktop app itself.
  16 | ///
  17 | /// <para>
  18 | /// When the user clicks "Host Game", the UI constructs a
  19 | /// <see cref="HostServer"/> with the local <c>Profile</c> and the
  20 | /// (already-loaded or freshly-built) <c>World</c>, calls
  21 | /// <see cref="StartAsync"/>, and the host is live on
  22 | /// <c>ws://&lt;lan-ip&gt;:&lt;Port&gt;/</c>. Other players (running their
  23 | /// own desktop app) connect via <see cref="GameClient"/>.
  24 | /// </para>
  25 | ///
  26 | /// <para><b>Architecture notes (deviations from the TS source):</b>
  27 | /// <list type="bullet">
  28 | ///   <item>NO socket.io. Raw <see cref="WebSocket"/> frames carrying
  29 | ///     JSON-serialised <see cref="NetMessage"/>s. The wire protocol is
  30 | ///     smaller and easier to debug.</item>
  31 | ///   <item>NO JWT / cookies / auth middleware. The client sends a
  32 | ///     <see cref="HelloMsg"/> with their local <c>Profile</c> identity;
  33 | ///     the host validates nickname uniqueness and assigns a connection
  34 | ///     id. That's it.</item>
  35 | ///   <item>NO separate "broadcast API server" on another port. The host
  36 | ///     process owns the <see cref="GameMaster"/> + <see cref="World"/> +
  37 | ///     <see cref="ActionQueue"/>, so broadcasts are direct method calls
  38 | ///     (<see cref="BroadcastAsync"/>), not localhost HTTP hops.</item>
  39 | ///   <item>The host is also a player. It's added as the first
  40 | ///     <see cref="MemberInfo"/> (with <see cref="MemberRole.Host"/>) at
  41 | ///     <see cref="StartAsync"/> time. The host's own actions go through
  42 | ///     the same <see cref="ActionQueue"/> as remote clients' actions.</item>
  43 | /// </list>
  44 | /// </para>
  45 | ///
  46 | /// <para><b>Port assignment:</b> the ctor takes a port (0 = OS-assigned).
  47 | /// Because <see cref="HttpListener"/> doesn't expose the bound port back
  48 | /// when port=0 (unlike <see cref="System.Net.Sockets.TcpListener"/>), we
  49 | /// pre-resolve a free port via a temporary <see cref="System.Net.Sockets.TcpListener"/>
  50 | /// before starting the <see cref="HttpListener"/>. There's a small
  51 | /// TOCTOU race (the port could be taken between the TcpListener.Close
  52 | /// and the HttpListener.Start), but for a desktop app it's acceptable
  53 | /// — if StartAsync fails, the caller can retry.</para>
  54 | ///
  55 | /// <para><b>Thread safety:</b> the connections dictionary is a
  56 | /// <see cref="ConcurrentDictionary{TKey,TValue}"/>. Each connection's
  57 | /// read loop runs on its own (ThreadPool) task; writes are guarded by
  58 | /// a per-connection <see cref="SemaphoreSlim"/> (because
  59 | /// <see cref="WebSocket.SendAsync"/> is NOT safe for concurrent callers
  60 | /// on the same instance). All event invocations are exception-safe
  61 | /// (try/catch around each subscriber).</para>
  62 | /// </summary>
  63 | public sealed class HostServer
  64 | {
  65 |     // ─── Config ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  66 | 
  67 |     private readonly Profile.Profile _hostProfile;
  68 |     private readonly int _requestedPort;
  69 |     private readonly string _bindHost;
  70 |     private readonly CancellationToken _shutdownToken;
  71 | 
  72 |     // ─── Runtime state ───────────────────────────────────────────────
  73 | 
  74 |     private HttpListener? _listener;
  75 |     private Task? _acceptLoopTask;
  76 |     private readonly ConcurrentDictionary<Guid, Connection> _connections = new();
  77 |     private readonly ConcurrentDictionary<string, Guid> _nicknamesByLower = new();
  78 | 
  79 |     private readonly object _statusLock = new();
  80 |     private PartyStatus _status = PartyStatus.Lobby;
  81 |     private string? _saveId;
  82 |     private int _turn;
  83 | 
  84 |     /// <summary>
  85 |     /// The host's own connection id (assigned at <see cref="StartAsync"/>
  86 |     /// time). Used as the <c>fromId</c> on host-originated chat / actions.
  87 |     /// </summary>
  88 |     public Guid HostConnectionId { get; private set; }
  89 | 
  90 |     /// <summary>
  91 |     /// The action queue. The HostSession drains this when processing a
  92 |     /// GM turn. Host actions and remote client actions both land here.
  93 |     /// </summary>
  94 |     public ActionQueue ActionQueue { get; } = new();
  95 | 
  96 |     /// <summary>
  97 |     /// Create a new host server. Does NOT start listening — call
  98 |     /// <see cref="StartAsync"/> to bind the socket.
  99 |     /// </summary>
 100 |     /// <param name="hostProfile">
 101 |     /// The local player's profile. The host is added to the roster as
 102 |     /// the first <see cref="MemberInfo"/> with role
 103 |     /// <see cref="MemberRole.Host"/> and nickname
 104 |     /// <see cref="Profile.Profile.Nickname"/>.
 105 |     /// </param>
 106 |     /// <param name="requestedPort">
 107 |     /// TCP port to listen on. 0 = OS-assigned (the actual port is read
 108 |     /// back from <see cref="Port"/> after <see cref="StartAsync"/>
 109 |     /// completes).
 110 |     /// </param>
 111 |     /// <param name="shutdownToken">
 112 |     /// Cancellation token that triggers graceful shutdown (the accept
 113 |     /// loop stops, all connections are closed, the listener is disposed).
 114 |     /// Typically the application shutdown token.
 115 |     /// </param>
 116 |     /// <param name="bindHost">
 117 |     /// Hostname to bind the <see cref="HttpListener"/> to. Default
 118 |     /// <c>"+"</c> (all interfaces — lets remote players on the LAN
 119 |     /// connect). Use <c>"localhost"</c> for single-machine testing.
 120 |     /// </param>
 121 |     public HostServer(
 122 |         Profile.Profile hostProfile,
 123 |         int requestedPort = 0,
 124 |         CancellationToken shutdownToken = default,
 125 |         string bindHost = "+")
 126 |     {
 127 |         _hostProfile = hostProfile ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(hostProfile));
 128 |         _requestedPort = requestedPort;
 129 |         _shutdownToken = shutdownToken;
 130 |         _bindHost = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(bindHost) ? "+" : bindHost;
 131 |     }
 132 | 
 133 |     // ─── Properties ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
 134 | 
 135 |     /// <summary>
 136 |     /// The actual port the server is listening on. Only valid after
 137 |     /// <see cref="StartAsync"/> completes. Returns 0 if not yet started.
 138 |     /// </summary>
 139 |     public int Port { get; private set; }
 140 | 
 141 |     /// <summary>
 142 |     /// Current party lifecycle status. Set via <see cref="SetStatusAsync"/>.
 143 |     /// </summary>
 144 |     public PartyStatus Status
 145 |     {
 146 |         get { lock (_statusLock) return _status; }
 147 |     }
 148 | 
 149 |     /// <summary>
 150 |     /// Save id the party is playing (or null if still in the lobby).
 151 |     /// Set via <see cref="SetStatusAsync"/> when transitioning to Playing.
 152 |     /// </summary>
 153 |     public string? SaveId
 154 |     {
 155 |         get { lock (_statusLock) return _saveId; }
 156 |     }
 157 | 
 158 |     /// <summary>
 159 |     /// Current turn number. Incremented by the HostSession after each GM
 160 |     /// turn; broadcast in <see cref="TurnEndMsg"/>.
 161 |     /// </summary>
 162 |     public int Turn
 163 |     {
 164 |         get { lock (_statusLock) return _turn; }
 165 |     }
 166 | 
 167 |     /// <summary>
 168 |     /// Current lobby roster (host + every connected client). Returns a
 169 |     /// snapshot — safe to enumerate outside the lock.
 170 |     /// </summary>
 171 |     public IReadOnlyList<MemberInfo> Members =>
 172 |         _connections.Values
 173 |             .Where(c => c.Member is not null)
 174 |             .Select(c => c.Member!)
 175 |             .OrderByDescending(m => m.Role == MemberRole.Host)
 176 |             .ThenBy(m => m.JoinedAt)
 177 |             .ToList();
 178 | 
 179 |     /// <summary>True if <see cref="StartAsync"/> has been called and the
 180 |     /// accept loop is running.</summary>
 181 |     public bool IsRunning => _listener is not null && _listener.IsListening;
 182 | 
 183 |     // ─── Events ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 184 | 
 185 |     /// <summary>Raised when a new client completes the handshake and
 186 |     /// joins the party. The host is NOT announced here (the host is
 187 |     /// added at StartAsync time before any client can connect).</summary>
 188 |     public event Action<MemberInfo>? MemberJoined;
 189 | 
 190 |     /// <summary>Raised when a client disconnects (graceful close or
 191 |     /// network drop). Passes the member that left (with status
 192 |     /// <see cref="MemberStatus.Disconnected"/>).</summary>
 193 |     public event Action<MemberInfo>? MemberLeft;
 194 | 
 195 |     /// <summary>Raised when a chat message arrives from a client. The
 196 |     /// host has already relayed it to all clients by the time this event
 197 |     /// fires — the HostSession can use it to display the chat locally
 198 |     /// (the host is also a player and sees the lobby chat in its UI).</summary>
 199 |     public event Action<ChatMsg>? ChatReceived;
 200 | 
 201 |     /// <summary>Raised when a player action is queued (either from a
 202 |     /// remote client or from the host player via
 203 |     /// <see cref="EnqueueActionAsync"/>). The action is already in
 204 |     /// <see cref="ActionQueue"/> and already broadcast to all clients.
 205 |     /// The HostSession can use this to wake up an idle GM turn loop.</summary>
 206 |     public event Action<PlayerAction>? ActionQueued;
 207 | 
 208 |     /// <summary>Raised when an action is cancelled. Passes the action id
 209 |     /// (or null if the id wasn't in the queue).</summary>
 210 |     public event Action<string?>? ActionCancelled;
 211 | 
 212 |     /// <summary>
 213 |     /// Raised when a client toggles their ready state in the lobby
 214 |     /// (issue #77). Carries the updated <see cref="MemberInfo"/> (with
 215 |     /// <see cref="MemberInfo.Status"/> set to Ready or Pending). The
 216 |     /// host UI uses this to refresh its lobby members list. Raised
 217 |     /// AFTER the new MemberInfo has been stored on the connection +
 218 |     /// AFTER the MemberReadyMsg has been broadcast to all clients — so
 219 |     /// by the time this event fires, the host UI can re-read
 220 |     /// <see cref="Members"/> and get the current snapshot.
 221 |     /// </summary>
 222 |     public event Action<MemberInfo>? MemberReady;
 223 | 
 224 |     /// <summary>
 225 |     /// Raised when the party lifecycle status changes (issue #77).
 226 |     /// Carries the new <see cref="PartyStatus"/>. The host UI subscribes
 227 |     /// to refresh its <c>IsLobby</c> flag (so the lobby layout
 228 |     /// disappears when the host starts the game via
 229 |     /// <see cref="HostSession.SetStatusAsync"/>). Raised AFTER the
 230 |     /// StatusChangedMsg has been broadcast to all clients.
 231 |     /// </summary>
 232 |     public event Action<PartyStatus>? StatusChanged;
 233 | 
 234 |     // ─── Lifecycle ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
 235 | 
 236 |     /// <summary>
 237 |     /// Bind the <see cref="HttpListener"/>, register the host as the
 238 |     /// first member, and start the accept loop. Returns the actual port
 239 |     /// the server is listening on (also available via <see cref="Port"/>).
 240 |     /// </summary>
 241 |     public Task<int> StartAsync()
 242 |     {
 243 |         if (_listener is not null)
 244 |             throw new InvalidOperationException("HostServer is already running.");
 245 | 
 246 |         // Pre-resolve a free port if the caller asked for OS-assigned.
 247 |         Port = _requestedPort == 0 ? GetFreePort() : _requestedPort;
 248 | 
 249 |         // The host's own connection id is a fresh Guid.
 250 |         HostConnectionId = Guid.NewGuid();
 251 | 
 252 |         _listener = new HttpListener();
 253 |         // HttpListener prefixes MUST end with '/'. The "+" binds to all
 254 |         // interfaces (use "localhost" for single-machine testing).
 255 |         _listener.Prefixes.Add($"http://{_bindHost}:{Port}/");
 256 | 
 257 |         try
 258 |         {
 259 |             _listener.Start();
 260 |         }
 261 |         catch (HttpListenerException ex)
 262 |         {
 263 |             _listener = null;
 264 |             throw new InvalidOperationException(
 265 |                 $"Failed to start HttpListener on {_bindHost}:{Port}. " +
 266 |                 $"On Windows, run `netsh http add urlacl url=http://{_bindHost}:{Port}/ user=Everyone` " +
 267 |                 $"or run as administrator. On Linux, ensure no other process is using the port. " +
 268 |                 $"Error: {ex.Message}", ex);
 269 |         }
 270 | 
 271 |         // Register the host as the first member. The host has no
 272 |         // WebSocket (it talks to itself directly via the HostSession),
 273 |         // so we mark its Connection with a null WebSocket.
 274 |         var hostMember = new MemberInfo
 275 |         {
 276 |             ConnectionId = HostConnectionId,
 277 |             ProfileId = _hostProfile.Id,
 278 |             Nickname = _hostProfile.Nickname,
 279 |             Role = MemberRole.Host,
 280 |             Status = MemberStatus.Ready,
 281 |             JoinedAt = DateTimeOffset.UtcNow,
 282 |         };
 283 |         var hostConn = new Connection(null!, hostMember);
 284 |         _connections[HostConnectionId] = hostConn;
 285 |         _nicknamesByLower[_hostProfile.Nickname.ToLowerInvariant()] = HostConnectionId;
 286 | 
 287 |         // Start the accept loop on the ThreadPool.
 288 |         _acceptLoopTask = Task.Run(AcceptLoopAsync);
 289 | 
 290 |         return Task.FromResult(Port);
 291 |     }
 292 | 
 293 |     /// <summary>
 294 |     /// Graceful shutdown: stop the accept loop, close all client
 295 |     /// connections with a "server shutting down" close code, dispose
 296 |     /// the listener. Safe to call multiple times.
 297 |     /// </summary>
 298 |     public async Task StopAsync()
 299 |     {
 300 |         var listener = Interlocked.Exchange(ref _listener, null);
 301 |         if (listener is null) return;
 302 | 
 303 |         // Stop accepting new connections first.
 304 |         try { listener.Stop(); } catch { /* ignore */ }
 305 | 
 306 |         // Close all client WebSockets. The host's own Connection has a
 307 |         // null WebSocket and is skipped.
 308 |         var closeTasks = new List<Task>();
 309 |         foreach (var kvp in _connections)
 310 |         {
 311 |             var ws = kvp.Value.WebSocket;
 312 |             if (ws is null) continue;
 313 |             try
 314 |             {
