 315 |                 if (ws.State == WebSocketState.Open)
 316 |                 {
 317 |                     closeTasks.Add(ws.CloseAsync(
 318 |                         WebSocketCloseStatus.EndpointUnavailable,
 319 |                         "server shutting down",
 320 |                         CancellationToken.None));
 321 |                 }
 322 |             }
 323 |             catch { /* ignore */ }
 324 |         }
 325 |         if (closeTasks.Count > 0)
 326 |         {
 327 |             try { await Task.WhenAll(closeTasks).ConfigureAwait(false); }
 328 |             catch { /* ignore */ }
 329 |         }
 330 | 
 331 |         // Wait for the accept loop to finish.
 332 |         if (_acceptLoopTask is not null)
 333 |         {
 334 |             try { await _acceptLoopTask.ConfigureAwait(false); }
 335 |             catch { /* ignore */ }
 336 |         }
 337 |         _acceptLoopTask = null;
 338 | 
 339 |         _connections.Clear();
 340 |         _nicknamesByLower.Clear();
 341 |     }
 342 | 
 343 |     // ─── Accept loop ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
 344 | 
 345 |     private async Task AcceptLoopAsync()
 346 |     {
 347 |         var listener = _listener;
 348 |         if (listener is null) return;
 349 | 
 350 |         while (!_shutdownToken.IsCancellationRequested && listener.IsListening)
 351 |         {
 352 |             HttpListenerContext ctx;
 353 |             try
 354 |             {
 355 |                 ctx = await listener.GetContextAsync().ConfigureAwait(false);
 356 |             }
 357 |             catch (HttpListenerException) when (_shutdownToken.IsCancellationRequested)
 358 |             {
 359 |                 break;
 360 |             }
 361 |             catch (ObjectDisposedException) when (_shutdownToken.IsCancellationRequested)
 362 |             {
 363 |                 break;
 364 |             }
 365 |             catch (Exception ex)
 366 |             {
 367 |                 Trace.WriteLine($"[HostServer] GetContextAsync threw: {ex}");
 368 |                 if (!listener.IsListening) break;
 369 |                 continue;
 370 |             }
 371 | 
 372 |             // Hand off to a background task so the accept loop keeps moving.
 373 |             _ = Task.Run(() => HandleConnectionAsync(ctx));
 374 |         }
 375 |     }
 376 | 
 377 |     private async Task HandleConnectionAsync(HttpListenerContext ctx)
 378 |     {
 379 |         // Reject non-WebSocket requests with a 400.
 380 |         if (!ctx.Request.IsWebSocketRequest)
 381 |         {
 382 |             try { ctx.Response.StatusCode = 400; ctx.Response.Close(); } catch { /* ignore */ }
 383 |             return;
 384 |         }
 385 | 
 386 |         HttpListenerWebSocketContext wsCtx;
 387 |         try
 388 |         {
 389 |             wsCtx = await ctx.AcceptWebSocketAsync(subProtocol: null).ConfigureAwait(false);
 390 |         }
 391 |         catch (Exception ex)
 392 |         {
 393 |             Trace.WriteLine($"[HostServer] AcceptWebSocketAsync threw: {ex}");
 394 |             try { ctx.Response.StatusCode = 500; ctx.Response.Close(); } catch { /* ignore */ }
 395 |             return;
 396 |         }
 397 | 
 398 |         var ws = wsCtx.WebSocket;
 399 | 
 400 |         // Pre-handshake state: the connection exists but no MemberInfo yet.
 401 |         // We use a temporary Guid so we can track the WebSocket before the
 402 |         // HelloMsg arrives. The ConnectionReadLoopAsync will swap this id
 403 |         // for a real one if/when the handshake completes.
 404 |         var pendingConnectionId = Guid.NewGuid();
 405 |         var conn = new Connection(ws, null);
 406 |         _connections[pendingConnectionId] = conn;
 407 | 
 408 |         // The read loop tracks the CURRENT connection id (which may change
 409 |         // after the handshake). We remove by that id in the finally block.
 410 |         var currentConnectionId = pendingConnectionId;
 411 |         try
 412 |         {
 413 |             currentConnectionId = await ConnectionReadLoopAsync(conn, pendingConnectionId).ConfigureAwait(false);
 414 |         }
 415 |         catch (Exception ex)
 416 |         {
 417 |             Trace.WriteLine($"[HostServer] Read loop threw: {ex}");
 418 |         }
 419 |         finally
 420 |         {
 421 |             // Remove by the current id. If the handshake completed, this
 422 |             // is the real connection id; if it didn't (or the loop threw
 423 |             // before the swap), it's the pending id. Both are no-ops if
 424 |             // the id is no longer in the dict.
 425 |             await RemoveConnectionAsync(currentConnectionId).ConfigureAwait(false);
 426 |         }
 427 |     }
 428 | 
 429 |     // ─── Per-connection read loop ────────────────────────────────────
 430 | 
 431 |     private async Task<Guid> ConnectionReadLoopAsync(Connection conn, Guid pendingConnectionId)
 432 |     {
 433 |         var ws = conn.WebSocket;
 434 |         if (ws is null) return pendingConnectionId;
 435 | 
 436 |         // The first message MUST be HelloMsg. Read it (with a timeout
 437 |         // so a dead connection doesn't leak).
 438 |         using var firstReadCts = new CancellationTokenSource(TimeSpan.FromSeconds(15));
 439 |         HelloMsg? hello;
 440 |         try
 441 |         {
 442 |             var (firstJson, firstClosed) = await ReceiveMessageAsync(ws, firstReadCts.Token).ConfigureAwait(false);
 443 |             if (firstClosed)
 444 |             {
 445 |                 // Client closed before sending Hello — drop silently.
 446 |                 return pendingConnectionId;
 447 |             }
 448 |             hello = MultiplayerJson.Deserialize(firstJson) as HelloMsg;
 449 |         }
 450 |         catch (OperationCanceledException)
 451 |         {
 452 |             await SendAndCloseAsync(ws, new RejectMsg { Reason = "Handshake timeout" }).ConfigureAwait(false);
 453 |             return pendingConnectionId;
 454 |         }
 455 | 
 456 |         if (hello is null)
 457 |         {
 458 |             await SendAndCloseAsync(ws, new RejectMsg { Reason = "Expected hello as first message" }).ConfigureAwait(false);
 459 |             return pendingConnectionId;
 460 |         }
 461 | 
 462 |         // Validate protocol version.
 463 |         if (!Common.Version.IsCompatible(hello.ProtocolVersion))
 464 |         {
 465 |             await SendAndCloseAsync(ws, new RejectMsg
 466 |             {
 467 |                 Reason = $"Protocol version mismatch: server={Common.Version.Current}, client={hello.ProtocolVersion}"
 468 |             }).ConfigureAwait(false);
 469 |             return pendingConnectionId;
 470 |         }
 471 | 
 472 |         // Validate nickname uniqueness (case-insensitive).
 473 |         if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(hello.Nickname))
 474 |         {
 475 |             await SendAndCloseAsync(ws, new RejectMsg { Reason = "Ник не может быть пустым" }).ConfigureAwait(false);
 476 |             return pendingConnectionId;
 477 |         }
 478 |         if (_nicknamesByLower.ContainsKey(hello.Nickname.ToLowerInvariant()))
 479 |         {
 480 |             await SendAndCloseAsync(ws, new RejectMsg { Reason = "Ник уже занят в этой партии" }).ConfigureAwait(false);
 481 |             return pendingConnectionId;
 482 |         }
 483 | 
 484 |         // Handshake succeeded — promote the connection to a full member.
 485 |         var connectionId = Guid.NewGuid();
 486 |         var member = new MemberInfo
 487 |         {
 488 |             ConnectionId = connectionId,
 489 |             ProfileId = hello.ProfileId,
 490 |             Nickname = hello.Nickname,
 491 |             Role = MemberRole.Player,
 492 |             Status = MemberStatus.Pending,
 493 |             JoinedAt = DateTimeOffset.UtcNow,
 494 |         };
 495 | 
 496 |         // Swap the pending connection id for the real one. The pending
 497 |         // entry has a null Member; the new entry has the full MemberInfo.
 498 |         _connections.TryRemove(pendingConnectionId, out _);
 499 |         _connections[connectionId] = conn;
 500 |         conn.Member = member;
 501 |         _nicknamesByLower[hello.Nickname.ToLowerInvariant()] = connectionId;
 502 | 
 503 |         // Send WelcomeMsg to the new client with the current party snapshot.
 504 |         var snapshot = BuildPartySnapshot();
 505 |         await SendAsync(conn, new WelcomeMsg
 506 |         {
 507 |             ConnectionId = connectionId,
 508 |             Party = snapshot,
 509 |             Role = MemberRole.Player,
 510 |         }, _shutdownToken).ConfigureAwait(false);
 511 | 
 512 |         // Broadcast MemberJoinedMsg to everyone (including the new client
 513 |         // — so they see themselves in the roster).
 514 |         await BroadcastAsync(new MemberJoinedMsg { Member = member }, _shutdownToken).ConfigureAwait(false);
 515 | 
 516 |         // Raise the event locally (for the HostSession).
 517 |         RaiseEvent(MemberJoined, member);
 518 | 
 519 |         // Main read loop.
 520 |         while (!_shutdownToken.IsCancellationRequested && ws.State == WebSocketState.Open)
 521 |         {
 522 |             string json;
 523 |             bool closed;
 524 |             try
 525 |             {
 526 |                 (json, closed) = await ReceiveMessageAsync(ws, _shutdownToken).ConfigureAwait(false);
 527 |             }
 528 |             catch (OperationCanceledException) when (_shutdownToken.IsCancellationRequested)
 529 |             {
 530 |                 break;
 531 |             }
 532 |             catch (WebSocketException ex)
 533 |             {
 534 |                 Trace.WriteLine($"[HostServer] WebSocket read failed for {member.Nickname}: {ex.Message}");
 535 |                 break;
 536 |             }
 537 | 
 538 |             if (closed) break;
 539 | 
 540 |             NetMessage? msg;
 541 |             try
 542 |             {
 543 |                 msg = MultiplayerJson.Deserialize(json);
 544 |             }
 545 |             catch (Exception ex)
 546 |             {
 547 |                 Trace.WriteLine($"[HostServer] Failed to parse message from {member.Nickname}: {ex.Message}");
 548 |                 await SendAsync(conn, new ErrorMsg { Message = "Invalid JSON" }, _shutdownToken).ConfigureAwait(false);
 549 |                 continue;
 550 |             }
 551 | 
 552 |             if (msg is null) continue;
 553 | 
 554 |             try
 555 |             {
 556 |                 await DispatchIncomingAsync(conn, member, msg).ConfigureAwait(false);
 557 |             }
 558 |             catch (Exception ex)
 559 |             {
 560 |                 Trace.WriteLine($"[HostServer] Dispatch failed for {msg.GetType().Name}: {ex}");
 561 |             }
 562 |         }
 563 | 
 564 |         // Return the real connection id so the caller (HandleConnectionAsync)
 565 |         // can remove it from _connections on cleanup. (If the handshake
 566 |         // never completed, we returned pendingConnectionId earlier.)
 567 |         return connectionId;
 568 |     }
 569 | 
 570 |     /// <summary>
 571 |     /// Dispatch one incoming client message. Handles relay-style messages
 572 |     /// (chat, actions) by re-broadcasting, and lifecycle messages (ready,
 573 |     /// cancel) by updating state + broadcasting.
 574 |     /// </summary>
 575 |     private async Task DispatchIncomingAsync(Connection conn, MemberInfo member, NetMessage msg)
 576 |     {
 577 |         switch (msg)
 578 |         {
 579 |             case ChatMsg chat:
 580 |                 {
 581 |                     // Rewrite fromId + fromNickname from the socket identity
 582 |                     // (clients cannot spoof another member's id).
 583 |                     var sanitized = SanitizeChatText(chat.Text);
 584 |                     if (sanitized is null) return; // empty after trim
 585 |                     var clean = chat with
 586 |                     {
 587 |                         FromId = member.ConnectionId,
 588 |                         FromNickname = member.Nickname,
 589 |                         Text = sanitized,
 590 |                         Ts = DateTimeOffset.UtcNow,
 591 |                     };
 592 |                     await BroadcastAsync(clean, _shutdownToken).ConfigureAwait(false);
 593 |                     RaiseEvent(ChatReceived, clean);
 594 |                     break;
 595 |                 }
 596 | 
 597 |             case ActionQueuedMsg action:
 598 |                 {
 599 |                     var sanitized = SanitizeActionText(action.Text);
 600 |                     if (sanitized is null) return;
 601 |                     var clean = action with
 602 |                     {
 603 |                         FromId = member.ConnectionId,
 604 |                         FromNickname = member.Nickname,
 605 |                         Text = sanitized,
 606 |                     };
 607 |                     var playerAction = new PlayerAction
 608 |                     {
 609 |                         Id = clean.ActionId,
 610 |                         PlayerId = member.ConnectionId,
 611 |                         PlayerNickname = member.Nickname,
 612 |                         Text = sanitized,
 613 |                         SubmittedAt = clean.Ts,
 614 |                     };
 615 |                     ActionQueue.Enqueue(playerAction);
 616 |                     await BroadcastAsync(clean, _shutdownToken).ConfigureAwait(false);
 617 |                     RaiseEvent(ActionQueued, playerAction);
 618 |                     break;
 619 |                 }
 620 | 
 621 |             case ActionCancelMsg cancel:
 622 |                 {
 623 |                     var ok = ActionQueue.Cancel(cancel.ActionId);
 624 |                     if (ok)
 625 |                     {
 626 |                         await BroadcastAsync(new ActionCancelledMsg { ActionId = cancel.ActionId }, _shutdownToken).ConfigureAwait(false);
 627 |                     }
 628 |                     RaiseEvent(ActionCancelled, ok ? cancel.ActionId : null);
 629 |                     break;
 630 |                 }
 631 | 
 632 |             case MemberReadyMsg ready:
 633 |                 {
 634 |                     // Update the member's status.
 635 |                     MemberInfo? updatedMember = null;
 636 |                     if (_connections.TryGetValue(member.ConnectionId, out var c) && c.Member is not null)
 637 |                     {
 638 |                         c.Member = c.Member with { Status = ready.Ready ? MemberStatus.Ready : MemberStatus.Pending };
 639 |                         updatedMember = c.Member;
 640 |                     }
 641 |                     var clean = ready with { ConnectionId = member.ConnectionId };
 642 |                     await BroadcastAsync(clean, _shutdownToken).ConfigureAwait(false);
 643 |                     // Raise the local event AFTER the broadcast so the host
 644 |                     // UI sees the post-update snapshot when it re-reads
 645 |                     // Members. Null when the member wasn't found (defensive
 646 |                     // — shouldn't happen because the handshake already
 647 |                     // registered them, but guards against a race with
 648 |                     // RemoveConnectionAsync).
 649 |                     if (updatedMember is not null)
 650 |                         RaiseEvent(MemberReady, updatedMember);
 651 |                     break;
 652 |                 }
 653 | 
 654 |             case StatusChangedMsg status:
 655 |                 {
 656 |                     // Only the host can change party status. Clients
 657 |                     // sending this are ignored (host drives transitions
 658 |                     // via SetStatusAsync).
 659 |                     if (member.Role != MemberRole.Host) return;
 660 |                     lock (_statusLock)
 661 |                     {
 662 |                         _status = status.Status;
 663 |                         if (status.SaveId is not null) _saveId = status.SaveId;
 664 |                         if (status.Turn > 0) _turn = status.Turn;
 665 |                     }
 666 |                     await BroadcastAsync(status, _shutdownToken).ConfigureAwait(false);
 667 |                     // Raise the local event AFTER the broadcast so the
 668 |                     // host UI can refresh its IsLobby flag.
 669 |                     RaiseEvent(StatusChanged, status.Status);
 670 |                     break;
 671 |                 }
 672 | 
 673 |             case PingMsg ping:
 674 |                 {
 675 |                     // Reply directly to the sender with a PongMsg echoing
 676 |                     // the ping's Ts.
 677 |                     await SendAsync(conn, new PongMsg { Ts = ping.Ts }, _shutdownToken).ConfigureAwait(false);
 678 |                     break;
 679 |                 }
 680 | 
 681 |             case HelloMsg:
 682 |             case WelcomeMsg:
 683 |             case RejectMsg:
 684 |             case MemberJoinedMsg:
 685 |             case MemberLeftMsg:
 686 |             case ActionCancelledMsg:
 687 |             case ActionResolvingMsg:
 688 |             case NarrativeDeltaMsg:
 689 |             case NarrativeFinalMsg:
 690 |             case StateUpdateMsg:
 691 |             case TurnEndMsg:
 692 |             case ErrorMsg:
 693 |             case KickedMsg:
 694 |             case PongMsg:
 695 |                 // Server-only messages — ignore if received from a client.
 696 |                 // (Future: log a warning.)
 697 |                 break;
 698 | 
 699 |             default:
 700 |                 Trace.WriteLine($"[HostServer] Unhandled message kind: {msg.GetType().Name}");
 701 |                 break;
 702 |         }
 703 |     }
 704 | 
 705 |     // ─── Connection removal ──────────────────────────────────────────
 706 | 
 707 |     private async Task RemoveConnectionAsync(Guid connectionId)
 708 |     {
 709 |         if (!_connections.TryRemove(connectionId, out var conn)) return;
 710 | 
 711 |         // Only announce MemberLeft for connections that completed the
 712 |         // handshake (i.e. have a MemberInfo). Pending connections (no
 713 |         // HelloMsg yet) are dropped silently.
 714 |         if (conn.Member is not null)
 715 |         {
 716 |             // Free the nickname.
 717 |             _nicknamesByLower.TryRemove(conn.Member.Nickname.ToLowerInvariant(), out _);
 718 | 
 719 |             // Broadcast MemberLeftMsg.
 720 |             try
 721 |             {
 722 |                 await BroadcastAsync(
 723 |                     new MemberLeftMsg { ConnectionId = connectionId, Reason = "disconnected" },
 724 |                     _shutdownToken).ConfigureAwait(false);
 725 |             }
 726 |             catch (Exception ex)
 727 |             {
 728 |                 Trace.WriteLine($"[HostServer] Failed to broadcast MemberLeft: {ex}");
 729 |             }
 730 | 
 731 |             // Raise the event locally.
 732 |             RaiseEvent(MemberLeft, conn.Member);
 733 |         }
 734 | 
 735 |         // Dispose the WebSocket.
 736 |         var ws = conn.WebSocket;
 737 |         if (ws is not null)
 738 |         {
 739 |             try { ws.Dispose(); } catch { /* ignore */ }
 740 |         }
 741 |     }
 742 | 
 743 |     // ─── Broadcast / send ────────────────────────────────────────────
 744 | 
 745 |     /// <summary>
 746 |     /// Send a message to ALL connected clients (host is skipped because
 747 |     /// it has no WebSocket — the HostSession raises the event locally).
 748 |     /// Each client send is best-effort: a single failed send doesn't
 749 |     /// stop the broadcast to other clients.
 750 |     /// </summary>
 751 |     public async Task BroadcastAsync(NetMessage msg, CancellationToken ct)
 752 |     {
 753 |         var bytes = MultiplayerJson.SerializeToUtf8Bytes(msg);
 754 |         var segment = new ArraySegment<byte>(bytes);
 755 | 
 756 |         // Snapshot the connections so we don't hold the dict during sends.
 757 |         var targets = _connections.Values
 758 |             .Where(c => c.WebSocket is not null && c.WebSocket.State == WebSocketState.Open)
 759 |             .ToList();
 760 | 
 761 |         foreach (var c in targets)
 762 |         {
 763 |             try
 764 |             {
 765 |                 await SendBytesAsync(c, segment, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 766 |             }
 767 |             catch (Exception ex)
 768 |             {
 769 |                 Trace.WriteLine($"[HostServer] Broadcast send failed: {ex.Message}");
 770 |             }
 771 |         }
 772 |     }
 773 | 
 774 |     /// <summary>
 775 |     /// Send a message to ONE client (identified by connection id). No-op
 776 |     /// if the connection doesn't exist or isn't open. The host's own
 777 |     /// connection id is a valid argument but is a no-op (no WebSocket).
 778 |     /// </summary>
 779 |     public async Task SendAsync(Guid connectionId, NetMessage msg, CancellationToken ct)
 780 |     {
 781 |         if (!_connections.TryGetValue(connectionId, out var conn)) return;
 782 |         var ws = conn.WebSocket;
 783 |         if (ws is null || ws.State != WebSocketState.Open) return;
 784 | 
 785 |         var bytes = MultiplayerJson.SerializeToUtf8Bytes(msg);
 786 |         try
 787 |         {
 788 |             await SendBytesAsync(conn, new ArraySegment<byte>(bytes), ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 789 |         }
 790 |         catch (Exception ex)
 791 |         {
 792 |             Trace.WriteLine($"[HostServer] Send to {connectionId} failed: {ex.Message}");
 793 |         }
 794 |     }
 795 | 
 796 |     /// <summary>
 797 |     /// Convenience: enqueue an action on behalf of the host player. The
 798 |     /// host has no WebSocket, so this method directly:
 799 |     /// 1. Constructs a PlayerAction + ActionQueuedMsg,
 800 |     /// 2. Enqueues into <see cref="ActionQueue"/>,
 801 |     /// 3. Broadcasts the ActionQueuedMsg to all remote clients,
 802 |     /// 4. Raises <see cref="ActionQueued"/> locally.
 803 |     /// </summary>
 804 |     public async Task EnqueueHostActionAsync(string actionId, string text, DateTimeOffset ts, CancellationToken ct)
 805 |     {
 806 |         var sanitized = SanitizeActionText(text);
 807 |         if (sanitized is null) return;
 808 | 
 809 |         var action = new PlayerAction
 810 |         {
 811 |             Id = actionId,
 812 |             PlayerId = HostConnectionId,
 813 |             PlayerNickname = _hostProfile.Nickname,
 814 |             Text = sanitized,
 815 |             SubmittedAt = ts,
 816 |         };
 817 |         ActionQueue.Enqueue(action);
 818 | 
 819 |         var msg = new ActionQueuedMsg
 820 |         {
 821 |             ActionId = actionId,
 822 |             FromId = HostConnectionId,
 823 |             FromNickname = _hostProfile.Nickname,
 824 |             Text = sanitized,
 825 |             Ts = ts,
 826 |         };
 827 |         await BroadcastAsync(msg, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 828 |         RaiseEvent(ActionQueued, action);
 829 |     }
 830 | 
 831 |     /// <summary>
 832 |     /// Cancel an action by id (host or any client). Cancels in the local
 833 |     /// <see cref="ActionQueue"/> and broadcasts
 834 |     /// <see cref="ActionCancelledMsg"/> to all clients.
 835 |     /// </summary>
 836 |     public async Task CancelActionAsync(string actionId, CancellationToken ct)
 837 |     {
 838 |         var ok = ActionQueue.Cancel(actionId);
 839 |         if (ok)
 840 |         {
 841 |             await BroadcastAsync(new ActionCancelledMsg { ActionId = actionId }, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 842 |         }
 843 |         RaiseEvent(ActionCancelled, ok ? actionId : null);
 844 |     }
 845 | 
 846 |     /// <summary>
 847 |     /// Update the party lifecycle status and broadcast
 848 |     /// <see cref="StatusChangedMsg"/> to all clients. Typically called by
 849 |     /// the HostSession when transitioning Lobby → Worldbuilding →
 850 |     /// CharacterCreation → Playing. Also raises the local
 851 |     /// <see cref="StatusChanged"/> event so the host UI can refresh
 852 |     /// (e.g. hide the lobby layout when transitioning to Playing —
 853 |     /// issue #77).
 854 |     /// </summary>
 855 |     public async Task SetStatusAsync(PartyStatus status, string? saveId = null, int turn = 0, CancellationToken ct = default)
 856 |     {
 857 |         lock (_statusLock)
 858 |         {
 859 |             _status = status;
 860 |             if (saveId is not null) _saveId = saveId;
 861 |             if (turn > 0) _turn = turn;
 862 |         }
 863 |         await BroadcastAsync(new StatusChangedMsg
 864 |         {
 865 |             Status = status,
 866 |             Turn = turn > 0 ? turn : _turn,
 867 |             SaveId = saveId ?? _saveId,
 868 |         }, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 869 |         // Raise the local event AFTER the broadcast so the host UI
 870 |         // sees the post-update Status when it re-reads. This is what
 871 |         // drives the IsLobby → in-game UI transition when the host
 872 |         // clicks «Начать игру» in the lobby (issue #77).
 873 |         RaiseEvent(StatusChanged, status);
 874 |     }
 875 | 
 876 |     /// <summary>
 877 |     /// Set the current turn number (called by HostSession after a GM turn
 878 |     /// completes). Does NOT broadcast — the HostSession is expected to
 879 |     /// send a <see cref="TurnEndMsg"/> via <see cref="BroadcastAsync"/>
 880 |     /// which carries the turn number.
 881 |     /// </summary>
 882 |     public void SetTurn(int turn)
 883 |     {
 884 |         lock (_statusLock) _turn = turn;
 885 |     }
 886 | 
 887 |     /// <summary>
 888 |     /// Kick a client. Sends <see cref="KickedMsg"/>, then closes the
 889 |     /// connection. No-op if the connection id is unknown (or belongs to
 890 |     /// the host, which cannot be kicked).
 891 |     /// </summary>
 892 |     public async Task KickAsync(Guid connectionId, string reason, CancellationToken ct)
 893 |     {
 894 |         if (connectionId == HostConnectionId) return;
 895 |         if (!_connections.TryGetValue(connectionId, out var conn)) return;
 896 |         var ws = conn.WebSocket;
 897 |         if (ws is null) return;
 898 | 
 899 |         try
 900 |         {
 901 |             await SendAsync(conn, new KickedMsg { Reason = reason }, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 902 |         }
 903 |         catch { /* ignore */ }
 904 | 
 905 |         try
 906 |         {
 907 |             if (ws.State == WebSocketState.Open)
 908 |             {
 909 |                 await ws.CloseAsync(WebSocketCloseStatus.NormalClosure, "kicked", ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 910 |             }
 911 |         }
 912 |         catch { /* ignore */ }
 913 |     }
 914 | 
 915 |     // ─── Internal helpers ────────────────────────────────────────────
 916 | 
 917 |     private PartySnapshot BuildPartySnapshot()
 918 |     {
 919 |         var members = Members;
 920 |         PartyStatus status; string? saveId; int turn;
 921 |         lock (_statusLock) { status = _status; saveId = _saveId; turn = _turn; }
 922 |         return new PartySnapshot
 923 |         {
 924 |             HostConnectionId = HostConnectionId,
 925 |             Members = members,
 926 |             Status = status,
 927 |             SaveId = saveId,
 928 |             Turn = turn,
 929 |         };
 930 |     }
 931 | 
 932 |     /// <summary>
 933 |     /// Send raw bytes to a connection's WebSocket, guarded by the
 934 |     /// per-connection <see cref="Connection.SendSemaphore"/>
 935 |     /// (<see cref="WebSocket.SendAsync"/> is NOT safe for concurrent
 936 |     /// callers on the same instance — multiple broadcasts in flight
 937 |     /// would corrupt the wire).
 938 |     /// </summary>
 939 |     private static async Task SendBytesAsync(Connection conn, ArraySegment<byte> bytes, CancellationToken ct)
 940 |     {
 941 |         var ws = conn.WebSocket;
 942 |         if (ws is null) return;
 943 |         await conn.SendSemaphore.WaitAsync(ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 944 |         try
 945 |         {
 946 |             await ws.SendAsync(bytes, WebSocketMessageType.Text, endOfMessage: true, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 947 |         }
 948 |         finally
 949 |         {
 950 |             conn.SendSemaphore.Release();
 951 |         }
 952 |     }
 953 | 
 954 |     /// <summary>
 955 |     /// Receive one full WebSocket text message. Returns the decoded
 956 |     /// UTF-8 string. If the client sends a Close frame, returns
 957 |     /// (empty, closed: true). Fragments are accumulated into a
 958 |     /// MemoryStream until <c>EndOfMessage</c>.
 959 |     /// </summary>
 960 |     private static async Task<(string text, bool closed)> ReceiveMessageAsync(WebSocket ws, CancellationToken ct)
 961 |     {
 962 |         using var ms = new MemoryStream();
 963 |         var buffer = new byte[4096];
 964 |         while (true)
 965 |         {
 966 |             ct.ThrowIfCancellationRequested();
 967 |             var result = await ws.ReceiveAsync(new ArraySegment<byte>(buffer), ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 968 | 
 969 |             if (result.MessageType == WebSocketMessageType.Close)
 970 |             {
 971 |                 return (string.Empty, true);
 972 |             }
 973 | 
 974 |             if (result.Count > 0)
 975 |             {
 976 |                 ms.Write(buffer, 0, result.Count);
 977 |             }
 978 | 
 979 |             if (result.EndOfMessage) break;
 980 |         }
 981 | 
 982 |         var text = Encoding.UTF8.GetString(ms.GetBuffer(), 0, (int)ms.Length);
 983 |         return (text, false);
 984 |     }
 985 | 
 986 |     private static async Task SendAndCloseAsync(WebSocket ws, NetMessage msg)
 987 |     {
 988 |         try
 989 |         {
 990 |             var bytes = MultiplayerJson.SerializeToUtf8Bytes(msg);
 991 |             await ws.SendAsync(new ArraySegment<byte>(bytes), WebSocketMessageType.Text, true, CancellationToken.None).ConfigureAwait(false);
 992 |         }
 993 |         catch { /* ignore */ }
 994 |         try
 995 |         {
 996 |             await ws.CloseAsync(WebSocketCloseStatus.NormalClosure, "rejected", CancellationToken.None).ConfigureAwait(false);
 997 |         }
 998 |         catch { /* ignore */ }
 999 |     }
1000 | 
1001 |     /// <summary>
1002 |     /// Send a message to a connection (used by the per-connection read
1003 |     /// loop for WelcomeMsg / PongMsg replies). Guarded by the
1004 |     /// per-connection semaphore so concurrent broadcasts can't corrupt
1005 |     /// the wire.
1006 |     /// </summary>
1007 |     private static async Task SendAsync(Connection conn, NetMessage msg, CancellationToken ct)
1008 |     {
1009 |         var ws = conn.WebSocket;
1010 |         if (ws is null || ws.State != WebSocketState.Open) return;
1011 |         var bytes = MultiplayerJson.SerializeToUtf8Bytes(msg);
1012 |         await SendBytesAsync(conn, new ArraySegment<byte>(bytes), ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
1013 |     }
1014 | 
1015 |     /// <summary>
1016 |     /// Sanitise chat text: trim, cap at 1000 chars. Returns null if the
1017 |     /// result is empty (the message is dropped).
1018 |     /// </summary>
1019 |     private static string? SanitizeChatText(string text)
1020 |     {
1021 |         if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(text)) return null;
1022 |         var trimmed = text.Trim();
1023 |         if (trimmed.Length == 0) return null;
1024 |         return trimmed.Length > 1000 ? trimmed[..1000] : trimmed;
1025 |     }
1026 | 
1027 |     /// <summary>
1028 |     /// Sanitise action text: trim, cap at 4000 chars. Returns null if
1029 |     /// the result is empty.
1030 |     /// </summary>
1031 |     private static string? SanitizeActionText(string text)
1032 |     {
1033 |         if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(text)) return null;
1034 |         var trimmed = text.Trim();
1035 |         if (trimmed.Length == 0) return null;
1036 |         return trimmed.Length > 4000 ? trimmed[..4000] : trimmed;
1037 |     }
1038 | 
1039 |     /// <summary>Find a free TCP port via a temporary TcpListener.</summary>
1040 |     private static int GetFreePort()
1041 |     {
1042 |         using var l = new System.Net.Sockets.TcpListener(IPAddress.Loopback, 0);
1043 |         l.Start();
1044 |         try
1045 |         {
1046 |             return ((IPEndPoint)l.LocalEndpoint).Port;
1047 |         }
1048 |         finally
1049 |         {
1050 |             l.Stop();
1051 |         }
1052 |     }
1053 | 
1054 |     /// <summary>
1055 |     /// Raise an event with exception-safe dispatch (each subscriber is
1056 |     /// wrapped in try/catch; one bad subscriber doesn't break the others).
1057 |     /// </summary>
1058 |     private static void RaiseEvent<T>(Action<T>? handler, T args)
1059 |     {
1060 |         if (handler is null) return;
1061 |         var delegates = handler.GetInvocationList();
1062 |         foreach (var d in delegates)
1063 |         {
1064 |             try { ((Action<T>)d)(args); }
1065 |             catch (Exception ex) { Trace.WriteLine($"[HostServer] event subscriber threw: {ex}"); }
1066 |         }
1067 |     }
1068 | 
1069 |     // ─── Per-connection state ────────────────────────────────────────
1070 | 
1071 |     /// <summary>
1072 |     /// One connection's state. The host's own Connection has a null
1073 |     /// <see cref="WebSocket"/> (it talks to itself directly).
1074 |     /// </summary>
1075 |     private sealed class Connection
1076 |     {
1077 |         public WebSocket? WebSocket { get; }
1078 |         public MemberInfo? Member { get; set; }
1079 |         public SemaphoreSlim SendSemaphore { get; } = new(1, 1);
1080 | 
1081 |         public Connection(WebSocket? ws, MemberInfo? member)
1082 |         {
1083 |             WebSocket = ws;
1084 |             Member = member;
1085 |         }
1086 | 
1087 |         // Note: we don't implement IDisposable here because the
1088 |         // HostServer manages disposal explicitly via RemoveConnectionAsync.
1089 |         // The SendSemaphore is disposed when the HostServer shuts down
1090 |         // (semaphore disposal is best-effort — abandoned waits just throw).
1091 |         public void DisposeSemaphore() => SendSemaphore.Dispose();
1092 |     }
1093 | }
