   1 | using System.Diagnostics;
   2 | using System.Text;
   3 | using MyGame.Core.AI.Agents;
   4 | using MyGame.Core.Multiplayer.Protocol;
   5 | using MyGame.Core.Profile;
   6 | using MyGame.Core.Saves;
   7 | using MyGame.Core.World;
   8 | using MyGame.Core.World.Content;
   9 | 
  10 | namespace MyGame.Core.Multiplayer;
  11 | 
  12 | // 'World' is both a namespace (MyGame.Core.World) and a type
  13 | // (MyGame.Core.World.World). Same alias trick as SaveManager.cs — alias
  14 | // to a different name to sidestep the namespace/type collision.
  15 | using GameWorld = MyGame.Core.World.World;
  16 | 
  17 | /// <summary>
  18 | /// High-level orchestrator that glues together the host's moving parts:
  19 | /// <see cref="HostServer"/> (transport) + <see cref="GameMaster"/> (AI) +
  20 | /// <see cref="SaveManager"/> (persistence) + <see cref="ActionQueue"/>
  21 | /// (player inputs).
  22 | ///
  23 | /// <para>
  24 | /// The UI layer talks to <see cref="HostSession"/> exclusively — it never
  25 | /// touches <see cref="HostServer"/> or <see cref="GameMaster"/> directly.
  26 | /// The session exposes a clean event-driven API:
  27 | /// </para>
  28 | /// <list type="bullet">
  29 | ///   <item><see cref="SubmitActionAsync"/> — local host player submits an action.</item>
  30 | ///   <item><see cref="CancelActionAsync"/> — cancel a pending action.</item>
  31 | ///   <item><see cref="ProcessNextTurnAsync"/> — drain the queue + run the GM +
  32 | ///     broadcast results + save state. Called by the UI's "Next Turn" button
  33 | ///     or by an idle-loop timer.</item>
  34 | ///   <item><see cref="SetStatusAsync"/> — transition the party lifecycle
  35 | ///     (Lobby → Worldbuilding → …).</item>
  36 | ///   <item>Events for member join/leave, chat, action queued, narrative
  37 | ///     delta/final, state update, turn end — all raised on the host's
  38 | ///     thread (UI subscribers must marshal to the UI thread).</item>
  39 | /// </list>
  40 | ///
  41 | /// <para><b>Single turn at a time:</b> the session uses a
  42 | /// <see cref="SemaphoreSlim"/> to ensure only one GM turn runs at a time.
  43 | /// Concurrent <see cref="ProcessNextTurnAsync"/> calls (e.g. UI button
  44 | /// spam) are serialised — the second call waits for the first to finish,
  45 | /// then drains whatever's in the queue at that point (which may be empty,
  46 | /// in which case it returns -1 immediately).</para>
  47 | /// </summary>
  48 | public sealed class HostSession
  49 | {
  50 |     // ─── Config ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  51 | 
  52 |     /// <summary>
  53 |     /// Default batching window in seconds (issue #12). When the first
  54 |     /// player action lands in the queue, the host waits up to this many
  55 |     /// seconds for other ready non-spectator members to submit their
  56 |     /// actions before draining the queue and running the GM. When all
  57 |     /// ready members have submitted (or there's only one ready member),
  58 |     /// the window fires immediately. Set to 0 to disable batching
  59 |     /// entirely (drain + run on every action).
  60 |     /// </summary>
  61 |     public const int DefaultBatchWindowSeconds = 5;
  62 | 
  63 |     private readonly Profile.Profile _hostProfile;
  64 |     private readonly GameWorld _world;
  65 |     private readonly GameMaster _gm;
  66 |     private readonly SaveManager? _saveManager;
  67 |     private readonly string? _saveId;
  68 |     private readonly int _requestedPort;
  69 |     private readonly string _bindHost;
  70 |     private readonly CancellationToken _shutdownToken;
  71 | 
  72 |     // ─── Runtime state ───────────────────────────────────────────────
  73 | 
  74 |     private readonly HostServer _server;
  75 |     private readonly SemaphoreSlim _turnLock = new(1, 1);
  76 | 
  77 |     /// <summary>
  78 |     /// In-memory log. Appended to on every action + narrative result.
  79 |     /// Persisted to disk via <see cref="SaveManager.SaveAll"/> after each
  80 |     /// turn (when <see cref="_saveManager"/> + <see cref="_saveId"/> are
  81 |     /// both set).
  82 |     /// </summary>
  83 |     private readonly List<LogEntry> _log = new();
  84 | 
  85 |     /// <summary>
  86 |     /// Current save meta. Loaded at startup if <see cref="_saveId"/> is
  87 |     /// set + the save exists; otherwise created lazily on the first
  88 |     /// <see cref="ProcessNextTurnAsync"/> call (so a brand-new game
  89 |     /// doesn't write to disk until something actually happens).
  90 |     /// </summary>
  91 |     private SaveMeta? _meta;
  92 | 
  93 |     /// <summary>
  94 |     /// Per-save cumulative prompt (input) tokens billed across all
  95 |     /// sessions on this save. Restored from <see cref="_meta"/> on
  96 |     /// <see cref="StartAsync"/>; accumulated after each GM turn; written
  97 |     /// back to <see cref="_meta"/> before each save. The host UI reads
  98 |     /// its display counter from the <see cref="NarrativeFinalMsg"/> event
  99 |     /// (which carries per-turn counts), so this field is purely for
 100 |     /// persistence.
 101 |     /// </summary>
 102 |     private int _sessionPromptTokens;
 103 | 
 104 |     /// <summary>
 105 |     /// Per-save cumulative completion (output) tokens. See
 106 |     /// <see cref="_sessionPromptTokens"/>.
 107 |     /// </summary>
 108 |     private int _sessionCompletionTokens;
 109 | 
 110 |     // ─── Batching window state (issue #12) ───────────────────────────
 111 |     //
 112 |     // When the first player action lands in the queue, the host starts a
 113 |     // batching window (DefaultBatchWindowSeconds, default 5). The window
 114 |     // lets other ready non-spectator members submit their actions before
 115 |     // the GM drains the queue. The window fires ProcessNextTurnAsync
 116 |     // immediately when all ready members have submitted, OR after the
 117 |     // timeout expires. Single-player-host (1 ready member) fires
 118 |     // immediately — no countdown.
 119 |     //
 120 |     // _batchLock guards _batchWindowCts / _batchSubmitted /
 121 |     // _batchReadyCount. The lock is held only briefly (no I/O, no event
 122 |     // raises) — the actual ProcessNextTurnAsync call happens outside the
 123 |     // lock to avoid re-entrancy with the turn lock.
 124 |     private readonly object _batchLock = new();
 125 |     private CancellationTokenSource? _batchWindowCts;
 126 |     private readonly HashSet<Guid> _batchSubmitted = new();
 127 |     private int _batchReadyCount;
 128 | 
 129 |     /// <summary>
 130 |     /// True after <see cref="StartAsync"/> has been called.
 131 |     /// </summary>
 132 |     public bool IsRunning { get; private set; }
 133 | 
 134 |     /// <summary>
 135 |     /// Create a host session. Does NOT start the server — call
 136 |     /// <see cref="StartAsync"/> to bind the WebSocket.
 137 |     /// </summary>
 138 |     /// <param name="hostProfile">The local host player's profile.</param>
 139 |     /// <param name="world">The world to play in. Mutated in-place by the
 140 |     ///     GameMaster's tool calls.</param>
 141 |     /// <param name="gm">The GameMaster bound to <paramref name="world"/>.
 142 |     ///     Must have been constructed with the same World instance.</param>
 143 |     /// <param name="saveManager">Optional save manager. When null,
 144 |     ///     turn-end state is broadcast to clients but NOT persisted to
 145 |     ///     disk (useful for ephemeral games / tests).</param>
 146 |     /// <param name="saveId">Optional save id. When null, a new save is
 147 |     ///     created lazily on the first turn (only if
 148 |     ///     <paramref name="saveManager"/> is provided).</param>
 149 |     /// <param name="requestedPort">TCP port for the WebSocket server.
 150 |     ///     0 = OS-assigned.</param>
 151 |     /// <param name="shutdownToken">Cancellation token that triggers
 152 |     ///     graceful shutdown of the server + any in-flight GM turn.</param>
 153 |     /// <param name="bindHost">HttpListener bind host. "+" = all
 154 |     ///     interfaces (LAN-playable); "localhost" = single-machine.</param>
 155 |     public HostSession(
 156 |         Profile.Profile hostProfile,
 157 |         GameWorld world,
 158 |         GameMaster gm,
 159 |         SaveManager? saveManager = null,
 160 |         string? saveId = null,
 161 |         int requestedPort = 0,
 162 |         CancellationToken shutdownToken = default,
 163 |         string bindHost = "+")
 164 |     {
 165 |         _hostProfile = hostProfile ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(hostProfile));
 166 |         _world = world ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(world));
 167 |         _gm = gm ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(gm));
 168 |         _saveManager = saveManager;
 169 |         _saveId = saveId;
 170 |         _requestedPort = requestedPort;
 171 |         _bindHost = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(bindHost) ? "+" : bindHost;
 172 |         _shutdownToken = shutdownToken;
 173 | 
 174 |         _server = new HostServer(hostProfile, requestedPort, shutdownToken, bindHost);
 175 | 
 176 |         // Bubble up HostServer events so the UI can subscribe to a
 177 |         // single object. Each subscriber is exception-safe (the
 178 |         // HostServer already wraps dispatch in try/catch, so this is
 179 |         // belt-and-suspenders).
 180 |         _server.MemberJoined += m => RaiseEvent(MemberJoined, m);
 181 |         // Issue #32: send last 20 log entries to the new joiner so they
 182 |         // have context. Fire-and-forget (best-effort — if the send fails,
 183 |         // the client just gets a shorter history).
 184 |         _server.MemberJoined += async m =>
 185 |         {
 186 |             try
 187 |             {
 188 |                 var tail = _log.Skip(Math.Max(0, _log.Count - 20))
 189 |                     .Select(e => e.Text)
 190 |                     .ToList();
 191 |                 if (tail.Count > 0)
 192 |                 {
 193 |                     await _server.SendAsync(m.ConnectionId, new LogSyncMsg { Entries = tail }, default);
 194 |                 }
 195 |             }
 196 |             catch { /* best-effort */ }
 197 |         };
 198 |         _server.MemberLeft += m => RaiseEvent(MemberLeft, m);
 199 |         _server.ChatReceived += c => RaiseEvent(ChatReceived, c);
 200 |         _server.ActionQueued += a => RaiseEvent(ActionQueued, a);
 201 |         _server.ActionCancelled += id => RaiseEventNullable(ActionCancelled, id);
 202 |         // Issue #77 — bubble up the lobby-ready + status-changed events
 203 |         // so the host UI can react to lobby state changes (members
 204 |         // toggling ready, host starting the game). Without these events,
 205 |         // the host UI would have to poll the Members list + Status
 206 |         // property — wasteful and prone to races.
 207 |         _server.MemberReady += m => RaiseEvent(MemberReady, m);
 208 |         _server.StatusChanged += s => RaiseEvent(StatusChanged, s);
 209 |         // Batching window (issue #12): every queued action starts (or
 210 |         // advances) a batching window. The window fires ProcessNextTurnAsync
 211 |         // after DefaultBatchWindowSeconds OR when all ready non-spectator
 212 |         // members have submitted. See OnActionQueuedBatching.
 213 |         _server.ActionQueued += OnActionQueuedBatching;
 214 |     }
 215 | 
 216 |     // ─── Properties ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
 217 | 
 218 |     /// <summary>The underlying host server. Exposed for advanced
 219 |     /// scenarios (e.g. direct send to a specific client). The UI layer
 220 |     /// should normally use the HostSession API instead.</summary>
 221 |     public HostServer Server => _server;
 222 | 
 223 |     /// <summary>The world being played. Mutated by the GameMaster's tool
 224 |     /// calls during <see cref="ProcessNextTurnAsync"/>.</summary>
 225 |     public GameWorld World => _world;
 226 | 
 227 |     /// <summary>The GameMaster. Exposed for advanced scenarios (e.g.
 228 |     /// direct history inspection).</summary>
 229 |     public GameMaster GameMaster => _gm;
 230 | 
 231 |     /// <summary>The action queue. Same as <see cref="HostServer.ActionQueue"/>.</summary>
 232 |     public ActionQueue ActionQueue => _server.ActionQueue;
 233 | 
 234 |     /// <summary>The TCP port the server is listening on. Valid only after
 235 |     /// <see cref="StartAsync"/> completes.</summary>
 236 |     public int Port => _server.Port;
 237 | 
 238 |     /// <summary>Current lobby roster.</summary>
 239 |     public IReadOnlyList<MemberInfo> Members => _server.Members;
 240 | 
 241 |     /// <summary>Current party status.</summary>
 242 |     public PartyStatus Status => _server.Status;
 243 | 
 244 |     /// <summary>Host's own connection id.</summary>
 245 |     public Guid HostConnectionId => _server.HostConnectionId;
 246 | 
 247 |     /// <summary>Current in-memory log (read-only snapshot).</summary>
 248 |     public IReadOnlyList<LogEntry> Log
 249 |     {
 250 |         get { lock (_log) return _log.ToArray(); }
 251 |     }
 252 | 
 253 |     /// <summary>
 254 |     /// Cumulative prompt (input) tokens billed across all sessions on
 255 |     /// this save. Restored from <see cref="SaveMeta.SessionPromptTokens"/>
 256 |     /// on <see cref="StartAsync"/>; accumulated after each GM turn; used
 257 |     /// by the host UI to seed its top-bar token counter so it survives
 258 |     /// reload. Persisted into meta on every save.
 259 |     /// </summary>
 260 |     public int SessionPromptTokens => _sessionPromptTokens;
 261 | 
 262 |     /// <summary>
 263 |     /// Cumulative completion (output) tokens. See
 264 |     /// <see cref="SessionPromptTokens"/>.
 265 |     /// </summary>
 266 |     public int SessionCompletionTokens => _sessionCompletionTokens;
 267 | 
 268 |     /// <summary>
 269 |     /// Convenience: sum of <see cref="SessionPromptTokens"/> +
 270 |     /// <see cref="SessionCompletionTokens"/>. Used by the host UI's
 271 |     /// "Сессия: Nk токенов" display.
 272 |     /// </summary>
 273 |     public int SessionTotalTokens => _sessionPromptTokens + _sessionCompletionTokens;
 274 | 
 275 |     // ─── Events ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 276 | 
 277 |     /// <summary>A new member joined the party.</summary>
 278 |     public event Action<MemberInfo>? MemberJoined;
 279 | 
 280 |     /// <summary>A member left the party.</summary>
 281 |     public event Action<MemberInfo>? MemberLeft;
 282 | 
 283 |     /// <summary>A chat message was received.</summary>
 284 |     public event Action<ChatMsg>? ChatReceived;
 285 | 
 286 |     /// <summary>An action was queued (by anyone — host or client).</summary>
 287 |     public event Action<PlayerAction>? ActionQueued;
 288 | 
 289 |     /// <summary>An action was cancelled. Passes the action id (or null
 290 |     /// if the id wasn't in the queue).</summary>
 291 |     public event Action<string?>? ActionCancelled;
 292 | 
 293 |     /// <summary>Raised at the start of a GM turn — the host is about to
 294 |     /// call the GM with the batched actions. Carries the action ids being
 295 |     /// resolved. UI can use this to disable the "Next Turn" button.</summary>
 296 |     public event Action<IReadOnlyList<string>>? TurnStarted;
 297 | 
 298 |     /// <summary>Raised when a narrative delta is broadcast to clients.
 299 |     /// The host UI can also append to its own narration buffer here
 300 |     /// (it doesn't get this via the WebSocket because it has no client).</summary>
 301 |     public event Action<string, int>? NarrativeDelta;
 302 | 
 303 |     /// <summary>Raised at the end of a GM turn with the full narration +
 304 |     /// tool events. Same payload as the <see cref="NarrativeFinalMsg"/>
 305 |     /// broadcast to clients.</summary>
 306 |     public event Action<NarrativeFinalMsg>? NarrativeFinal;
 307 | 
 308 |     /// <summary>Raised when a state update is broadcast to clients.
 309 |     /// Carries the world JSON + turn number.</summary>
 310 |     public event Action<StateUpdateMsg>? StateUpdate;
 311 | 
 312 |     /// <summary>Raised when a GM turn ends. Carries the turn number.</summary>
 313 |     public event Action<int>? TurnEnded;
 314 | 
 315 |     /// <summary>Raised if a GM turn fails (AI exception, etc.). Carries
 316 |     /// the error message. The turn is aborted; the action queue is left
 317 |     /// intact (the actions are NOT re-drained — caller can retry or
 318 |     /// cancel them).</summary>
 319 |     public event Action<string>? TurnFailed;
 320 | 
 321 |     /// <summary>
 322 |     /// Raised every second while the batching window (issue #12) is
 323 |     /// counting down. Carries the seconds remaining (5 → 4 → 3 → 2 → 1).
 324 |     /// A final 0 is raised when the window fires (either by timeout, by
 325 |     /// all-ready-submitted, or by shutdown). Not raised at all when the
 326 |     /// window fires immediately (single-player-host or
 327 |     /// <see cref="DefaultBatchWindowSeconds"/> = 0). UI subscribers should
 328 |     /// marshal to the UI thread — the event fires on a ThreadPool thread.
 329 |     /// </summary>
 330 |     public event Action<int>? BatchCountdownChanged;
 331 | 
 332 |     /// <summary>
 333 |     /// Raised when a client toggles their ready state in the lobby
 334 |     /// (issue #77). Carries the updated <see cref="MemberInfo"/>.
 335 |     /// Bubbled up from <see cref="HostServer.MemberReady"/>. The host
 336 |     /// UI subscribes to refresh its lobby members list.
 337 |     /// </summary>
 338 |     public event Action<MemberInfo>? MemberReady;
 339 | 
 340 |     /// <summary>
 341 |     /// Raised when the party lifecycle status changes (issue #77).
 342 |     /// Carries the new <see cref="PartyStatus"/>. Bubbled up from
 343 |     /// <see cref="HostServer.StatusChanged"/>. The host UI subscribes
 344 |     /// to refresh its <c>IsLobby</c> flag.
 345 |     /// </summary>
 346 |     public event Action<PartyStatus>? StatusChanged;
 347 | 
 348 |     // ─── Lifecycle ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
 349 | 
 350 |     /// <summary>
 351 |     /// Start the host server + load the save's existing log/meta (if
 352 |     /// applicable). Returns the actual port the server is listening on.
 353 |     /// </summary>
 354 |     public async Task<int> StartAsync()
 355 |     {
 356 |         if (IsRunning) throw new InvalidOperationException("HostSession is already running.");
 357 | 
 358 |         // If a save id was given, load the existing meta + log so the
 359 |         // session continues from where the save left off.
 360 |         if (_saveManager is not null && !string.IsNullOrEmpty(_saveId))
 361 |         {
 362 |             var loaded = _saveManager.LoadAll(_saveId);
 363 |             if (loaded is { } tuple)
 364 |             {
 365 |                 _meta = tuple.meta;
 366 |                 lock (_log) _log.AddRange(tuple.log);
 367 |                 // Restore session-tokens from the save so the counter
 368 |                 // survives reload.
 369 |                 _sessionPromptTokens = tuple.meta.SessionPromptTokens;
 370 |                 _sessionCompletionTokens = tuple.meta.SessionCompletionTokens;
 371 |             }
 372 |         }
 373 | 
 374 |         var port = await _server.StartAsync().ConfigureAwait(false);
 375 |         IsRunning = true;
 376 | 
 377 |         // Issue #29: attempt UPnP port forwarding (best-effort). If
 378 |         // successful, UpnpPublicAddress is set and the host UI can show
 379 |         // the public IP:port for internet play. If it fails (no router,
 380 |         // router doesn't support UPnP), UpnpPublicAddress stays null and
 381 |         // the host shows the local address (manual forwarding required).
 382 |         try
 383 |         {
 384 |             var upnp = await UpnpForwarder.TryForwardAsync(port, port, "Pathstone", default).ConfigureAwait(false);
 385 |             if (upnp is not null)
 386 |             {
 387 |                 UpnpPublicAddress = upnp.PublicAddress;
 388 |             }
 389 |         }
 390 |         catch { /* best-effort — silent */ }
 391 | 
 392 |         return port;
 393 |     }
 394 | 
 395 |     /// <summary>
 396 |     /// Public IP:port from UPnP forwarding (issue #29). Null if UPnP
 397 |     /// failed (host shows local address; user must forward manually).
 398 |     /// </summary>
 399 |     public string? UpnpPublicAddress { get; private set; }
 400 | 
 401 |     /// <summary>
 402 |     /// Graceful shutdown: stop the host server, cancel any in-flight GM
 403 |     /// turn, save state (if a save manager is configured). Safe to call
 404 |     /// multiple times.
 405 |     /// </summary>
 406 |     public async Task StopAsync()
 407 |     {
 408 |         if (!IsRunning) return;
 409 |         IsRunning = false;
 410 | 
 411 |         // Save the final state before shutting down (best-effort).
 412 |         if (_saveManager is not null && _saveId is not null && _meta is not null)
 413 |         {
 414 |             try
 415 |             {
 416 |                 LogEntry[] logSnapshot;
 417 |                 lock (_log) logSnapshot = _log.ToArray();
 418 |                 // Persist session-tokens into meta before the final save.
 419 |                 _meta = _meta with
 420 |                 {
 421 |                     SessionPromptTokens = _sessionPromptTokens,
 422 |                     SessionCompletionTokens = _sessionCompletionTokens,
 423 |                 };
 424 |                 _saveManager.SaveAll(_saveId, _world, _meta, logSnapshot);
 425 |             }
 426 |             catch (Exception ex)
 427 |             {
 428 |                 Trace.WriteLine($"[HostSession] Final save failed: {ex}");
 429 |             }
 430 |         }
 431 | 
 432 |         await _server.StopAsync().ConfigureAwait(false);
 433 |     }
 434 | 
 435 |     // ─── Player actions ──────────────────────────────────────────────
 436 | 
 437 |     /// <summary>
 438 |     /// Submit an action on behalf of the host player. Generates an
 439 |     /// action id, enqueues it in the action queue, broadcasts
 440 |     /// <see cref="ActionQueuedMsg"/> to all clients, and raises
 441 |     /// <see cref="ActionQueued"/> locally.
 442 |     /// </summary>
 443 |     public Task SubmitActionAsync(string text, CancellationToken ct = default)
 444 |     {
 445 |         if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(text))
 446 |             throw new ArgumentException("Action text cannot be empty.", nameof(text));
 447 | 
 448 |         var actionId = NewActionId();
 449 |         var ts = DateTimeOffset.UtcNow;
 450 |         return _server.EnqueueHostActionAsync(actionId, text, ts, ct);
 451 |     }
 452 | 
 453 |     /// <summary>
 454 |     /// Cancel a pending action by id. Returns true if the action was
 455 |     /// found and removed; false otherwise.
 456 |     /// </summary>
 457 |     public async Task<bool> CancelActionAsync(string actionId, CancellationToken ct = default)
 458 |     {
 459 |         // HostServer.CancelActionAsync doesn't return the bool — call the
 460 |         // queue directly to check, then broadcast.
 461 |         var ok = _server.ActionQueue.Cancel(actionId);
 462 |         if (ok)
 463 |         {
 464 |             await _server.BroadcastAsync(new ActionCancelledMsg { ActionId = actionId }, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 465 |         }
 466 |         RaiseEventNullable(ActionCancelled, ok ? actionId : null);
 467 |         return ok;
 468 |     }
 469 | 
 470 |     /// <summary>
 471 |     /// Send a lobby chat message on behalf of the host player.
 472 |     /// </summary>
 473 |     public async Task SendChatAsync(string text, CancellationToken ct = default)
 474 |     {
 475 |         if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(text)) return;
 476 |         var trimmed = text.Trim();
 477 |         if (trimmed.Length > 1000) trimmed = trimmed[..1000];
 478 | 
 479 |         var msg = new ChatMsg
 480 |         {
 481 |             FromId = _server.HostConnectionId,
 482 |             FromNickname = _hostProfile.Nickname,
 483 |             Text = trimmed,
 484 |             Ts = DateTimeOffset.UtcNow,
 485 |         };
 486 |         await _server.BroadcastAsync(msg, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 487 |         RaiseEvent(ChatReceived, msg);
 488 |     }
 489 | 
 490 |     /// <summary>
 491 |     /// Transition the party lifecycle status. Broadcasts
 492 |     /// <see cref="StatusChangedMsg"/> to all clients.
 493 |     /// </summary>
 494 |     public Task SetStatusAsync(PartyStatus status, CancellationToken ct = default) =>
 495 |         _server.SetStatusAsync(status, _saveId, _world.Turn, ct);
 496 | 
 497 |     /// <summary>
 498 |     /// Kick a member from the party (issue #30). Delegates to
 499 |     /// <see cref="HostServer.KickAsync"/>.
 500 |     /// </summary>
 501 |     public Task KickAsync(Guid connectionId, string reason, CancellationToken ct = default) =>
 502 |         _server.KickAsync(connectionId, reason, ct);
 503 | 
 504 |     // ─── GM turn ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 505 | 
 506 |     /// <summary>
 507 |     /// Drain the action queue + run one GM turn with the batched
 508 |     /// actions + broadcast results. Returns the new turn number, or -1
 509 |     /// if the queue was empty (no work to do).
 510 |     ///
 511 |     /// <para>Concurrent calls are serialised by an async semaphore —
 512 |     /// only one GM turn runs at a time. A second call waits for the
 513 |     /// first to finish, then drains whatever's in the queue at that
 514 |     /// point (which may be empty, returning -1).</para>
 515 |     /// </summary>
 516 |     public async Task<int> ProcessNextTurnAsync(CancellationToken ct = default)
 517 |     {
 518 |         await _turnLock.WaitAsync(ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 519 |         try
 520 |         {
 521 |             // Drain the queue INSIDE the lock so a racing SubmitAction
 522 |             // can't sneak into the queue after we've decided it's empty.
 523 |             var actions = _server.ActionQueue.DrainAll();
 524 |             if (actions.Count == 0) return -1;
 525 | 
 526 |             var actionIds = actions.Select(a => a.Id).ToArray();
 527 |             RaiseEvent(TurnStarted, actionIds);
 528 | 
 529 |             // Broadcast ActionResolvingMsg so clients grey out the
 530 |             // actions in their UIs.
 531 |             await _server.BroadcastAsync(
 532 |                 new ActionResolvingMsg { ActionIds = actionIds },
 533 |                 ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 534 | 
 535 |             // Extract just the action texts. The GM's
 536 |             // ProcessActionBatchAsync builds the combined user message
 537 |             // ("## Действия игроков в этом ходу:\n1. ...\n2. ...")
 538 |             // internally and resolves all queued actions in one GM turn
 539 |             // (issue #12). The per-action log entries above still record
 540 |             // who did what (with nicknames) for the save's log.json.
 541 |             var texts = actions.Select(a => a.Text).ToList();
 542 | 
 543 |             // Append an action log entry per queued action (so the save's
 544 |             // log.json records who did what).
 545 |             foreach (var a in actions)
 546 |             {
 547 |                 lock (_log)
 548 |                 {
 549 |                     _log.Add(LogEntry.Action(
 550 |                         $"{a.PlayerNickname}: {a.Text}",
 551 |                         authorId: a.PlayerId.ToString()));
 552 |                 }
 553 |             }
 554 | 
 555 |             // Run the GM. The GM's tool calls mutate the world in-place;
 556 |             // we then serialise the mutated world for StateUpdateMsg.
 557 |             NarrativeResult result;
 558 |             try
 559 |             {
 560 |                 result = await _gm.ProcessActionBatchAsync(texts, narrativeDelta: null, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 561 |             }
 562 |             catch (OperationCanceledException) when (ct.IsCancellationRequested)
 563 |             {
 564 |                 // Cancellation — re-queue the actions? For simplicity we
 565 |                 // don't (the user can resubmit). Just rethrow.
 566 |                 throw;
 567 |             }
 568 |             catch (Exception ex)
 569 |             {
 570 |                 Trace.WriteLine($"[HostSession] GM turn failed: {ex}");
 571 |                 RaiseEvent(TurnFailed, ex.Message);
 572 | 
 573 |                 // Broadcast an error to clients so their UI shows something.
 574 |                 await _server.BroadcastAsync(
 575 |                     new ErrorMsg { Message = $"GM turn failed: {ex.Message}" },
 576 |                     ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 577 | 
 578 |                 // Still increment the turn + end it so the UI unblocks.
 579 |                 _world.Turn++;
 580 |                 await _server.BroadcastAsync(
 581 |                     new TurnEndMsg { TurnNumber = _world.Turn },
 582 |                     ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 583 |                 RaiseEvent(TurnEnded, _world.Turn);
 584 |                 _server.SetTurn(_world.Turn);
 585 |                 return _world.Turn;
 586 |             }
 587 | 
 588 |             // Broadcast narrative: one delta with the full text (the GM
 589 |             // is non-streaming in this port), then a final. The wire
 590 |             // format supports streaming for a future streaming GM.
 591 |             if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(result.NarrativeText))
 592 |             {
 593 |                 var delta = new NarrativeDeltaMsg
 594 |                 {
 595 |                     TextDelta = result.NarrativeText,
 596 |                     Turn = _world.Turn + 1,
 597 |                 };
 598 |                 await _server.BroadcastAsync(delta, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 599 |                 RaiseEvent(NarrativeDelta, delta.TextDelta, delta.Turn);
 600 |             }
 601 | 
 602 |             // Accumulate token billing for this turn into the session
 603 |             // totals. The host UI reads per-turn counts from the
 604 |             // NarrativeFinalMsg event we raise just below; we persist
 605 |             // the cumulative totals into _meta on save (below).
 606 |             _sessionPromptTokens += result.PromptTokens;
 607 |             _sessionCompletionTokens += result.CompletionTokens;
 608 | 
 609 |             var toolEvents = result.ToolCalls
 610 |                 .Select(tc => new ToolEvent
 611 |                 {
 612 |                     Name = tc.Name,
 613 |                     ArgsJson = tc.ArgsJson,
 614 |                     Result = tc.Result,
 615 |                     IsError = tc.IsError,
 616 |                 })
 617 |                 .ToArray();
 618 | 
 619 |             var finalMsg = new NarrativeFinalMsg
 620 |             {
 621 |                 FullText = result.NarrativeText,
 622 |                 ToolEvents = toolEvents,
 623 |                 Turn = _world.Turn + 1,
 624 |                 PromptTokens = result.PromptTokens,
 625 |                 CompletionTokens = result.CompletionTokens,
 626 |                 TotalTokens = result.TotalTokens,
 627 |             };
 628 |             await _server.BroadcastAsync(finalMsg, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 629 |             RaiseEvent(NarrativeFinal, finalMsg);
 630 | 
 631 |             // Append a narrative log entry (and per-tool entries) so
 632 |             // save's log.json records the GM's output.
 633 |             lock (_log)
 634 |             {
 635 |                 _log.Add(LogEntry.Narrative(result.NarrativeText));
 636 |                 foreach (var tc in result.ToolCalls)
 637 |                 {
 638 |                     _log.Add(LogEntry.Tool(
 639 |                         $"{tc.Name}: {tc.Result}",
 640 |                         metadata: new Dictionary<string, object>
 641 |                         {
 642 |                             ["name"] = tc.Name,
 643 |                             ["args"] = tc.ArgsJson,
 644 |                             ["isError"] = tc.IsError,
 645 |                         }));
 646 |                 }
 647 |             }
 648 | 
 649 |             // Increment the world's turn counter (SaveManager.SaveAll
 650 |             // picks this up to refresh meta.Turn).
 651 |             _world.Turn++;
 652 | 
 653 |             // Save state (best-effort).
 654 |             if (_saveManager is not null)
 655 |             {
 656 |                 try
 657 |                 {
 658 |                     EnsureMeta();
 659 |                     LogEntry[] logSnapshot;
 660 |                     lock (_log) logSnapshot = _log.ToArray();
 661 |                     if (_meta is not null && _saveId is not null)
 662 |                     {
 663 |                         // Persist session-tokens into meta before saving
 664 |                         // so the counter survives reload.
 665 |                         _meta = _meta with
 666 |                         {
 667 |                             SessionPromptTokens = _sessionPromptTokens,
 668 |                             SessionCompletionTokens = _sessionCompletionTokens,
 669 |                         };
 670 |                         _saveManager.SaveAll(_saveId, _world, _meta, logSnapshot);
 671 |                     }
 672 |                 }
 673 |                 catch (Exception ex)
 674 |                 {
 675 |                     Trace.WriteLine($"[HostSession] Save failed: {ex}");
 676 |                 }
 677 |             }
 678 | 
 679 |             // Broadcast the new world state.
 680 |             var stateMsg = new StateUpdateMsg
 681 |             {
 682 |                 WorldJson = _world.ToJson(),
 683 |                 Turn = _world.Turn,
 684 |                 Full = true,
 685 |             };
 686 |             await _server.BroadcastAsync(stateMsg, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 687 |             RaiseEvent(StateUpdate, stateMsg);
 688 | 
 689 |             // Broadcast TurnEnd + raise the event locally.
 690 |             _server.SetTurn(_world.Turn);
 691 |             await _server.BroadcastAsync(
 692 |                 new TurnEndMsg { TurnNumber = _world.Turn },
 693 |                 ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 694 |             RaiseEvent(TurnEnded, _world.Turn);
 695 | 
 696 |             return _world.Turn;
 697 |         }
 698 |         finally
 699 |         {
 700 |             _turnLock.Release();
 701 |         }
 702 |     }
 703 | 
 704 |     // ─── Batching window (issue #12) ──────────────────────────────────
 705 | 
 706 |     /// <summary>
 707 |     /// Handler for <see cref="HostServer.ActionQueued"/>. Every queued
 708 |     /// action starts (or advances) a batching window. The window lets
 709 |     /// other ready non-spectator members submit their actions before the
 710 |     /// GM drains the queue, so multi-player turns resolve all queued
 711 |     /// actions in one GM call (see <see cref="GameMaster.ProcessActionBatchAsync"/>).
 712 |     ///
 713 |     /// <para>
 714 |     /// Window behavior:
 715 |     /// </para>
 716 |     /// <list type="bullet">
 717 |     ///   <item>If <see cref="DefaultBatchWindowSeconds"/> is 0, batching
 718 |     ///     is disabled — fire <see cref="ProcessNextTurnAsync"/> on every
 719 |     ///     action.</item>
 720 |     ///   <item>If a window is already active, record the submitter. If
 721 |     ///     all ready non-spectator members have now submitted, fire the
 722 |     ///     turn immediately (early fire).</item>
 723 |     ///   <item>If no window is active, start one. If only one ready
 724 |     ///     member exists (typical single-player-host), fire immediately
 725 |     ///     (no countdown). Otherwise, schedule a
 726 |     ///     <see cref="DefaultBatchWindowSeconds"/>-second timer that
 727 |     ///     fires the turn on expiry, and check for early fire after each
 728 |     ///     1-second tick.</item>
 729 |     /// </list>
 730 |     /// </summary>
 731 |     private void OnActionQueuedBatching(PlayerAction action)
 732 |     {
 733 |         // Batching disabled → fire immediately. (Read into a local so
 734 |         // the compiler doesn't const-fold the check away when
 735 |         // DefaultBatchWindowSeconds is a non-zero const — keeps the
 736 |         // "set to 0 to disable" path reachable for devs who change
 737 |         // the const.)
 738 |         var windowSeconds = DefaultBatchWindowSeconds;
 739 |         if (windowSeconds <= 0)
 740 |         {
 741 |             _ = ProcessNextTurnAsync(_shutdownToken);
 742 |             return;
 743 |         }
 744 |         StartOrAdvanceBatchWindow(action.PlayerId);
 745 |     }
 746 | 
 747 |     private void StartOrAdvanceBatchWindow(Guid submitterId)
 748 |     {
 749 |         bool fireNow = false;
 750 |         bool startTimer = false;
 751 |         CancellationTokenSource? localCts = null;
 752 | 
 753 |         lock (_batchLock)
 754 |         {
 755 |             if (_batchWindowCts is not null)
 756 |             {
 757 |                 // Window already active — record the submitter.
 758 |                 _batchSubmitted.Add(submitterId);
 759 |                 if (_batchReadyCount > 0 && _batchSubmitted.Count >= _batchReadyCount)
 760 |                 {
 761 |                     // All ready players have submitted — fire early.
 762 |                     localCts = _batchWindowCts;
 763 |                     _batchWindowCts = null;
 764 |                     _batchSubmitted.Clear();
 765 |                     fireNow = true;
 766 |                 }
 767 |             }
 768 |             else
 769 |             {
 770 |                 // No window active — start a new one.
 771 |                 _batchSubmitted.Clear();
 772 |                 _batchSubmitted.Add(submitterId);
 773 |                 _batchReadyCount = CountReadyPlayers();
 774 | 
 775 |                 if (_batchReadyCount <= 1)
 776 |                 {
 777 |                     // Single ready player (host only) — fire immediately,
 778 |                     // no countdown.
 779 |                     fireNow = true;
 780 |                 }
 781 |                 else
 782 |                 {
 783 |                     _batchWindowCts = CancellationTokenSource.CreateLinkedTokenSource(_shutdownToken);
 784 |                     localCts = _batchWindowCts;
 785 |                     startTimer = true;
 786 |                 }
 787 |             }
 788 |         }
 789 | 
 790 |         // Outside the lock: schedule the timer OR fire immediately.
 791 |         // (ProcessNextTurnAsync acquires _turnLock, which we don't want
 792 |         // to nest under _batchLock — re-entrancy hazard.)
 793 |         if (startTimer)
 794 |         {
 795 |             _ = RunBatchWindowAsync(localCts!);
 796 |             return;
 797 |         }
 798 | 
 799 |         if (fireNow)
 800 |         {
 801 |             if (localCts is not null)
 802 |             {
 803 |                 // Early-fire: cancel the running timer task. It will
 804 |                 // catch OperationCanceledException and exit cleanly.
 805 |                 try { localCts.Cancel(); localCts.Dispose(); } catch { /* ignore */ }
 806 |             }
 807 |             _ = ProcessNextTurnAsync(_shutdownToken);
 808 |         }
 809 |     }
 810 | 
 811 |     /// <summary>
 812 |     /// Run the batching window countdown. Raises
 813 |     /// <see cref="BatchCountdownChanged"/> every second (5 → 4 → 3 → 2 → 1),
 814 |     /// then fires <see cref="ProcessNextTurnAsync"/>. Exits early (and
 815 |     /// raises BatchCountdownChanged(0)) if:
 816 |     /// <list type="bullet">
 817 |     ///   <item>The CTS is cancelled (early fire from
 818 |     ///     <see cref="StartOrAdvanceBatchWindow"/> when all ready members
 819 |     ///     have submitted).</item>
 820 |     ///   <item>All ready members submit during a 1-second tick — early
 821 |     ///     fire from inside this loop.</item>
 822 |     ///   <item>The host shuts down (linked to
 823 |     ///     <see cref="_shutdownToken"/>).</item>
 824 |     /// </list>
 825 |     /// </summary>
 826 |     private async Task RunBatchWindowAsync(CancellationTokenSource cts)
 827 |     {
 828 |         try
 829 |         {
 830 |             for (int remaining = DefaultBatchWindowSeconds; remaining > 0; remaining--)
 831 |             {
 832 |                 RaiseEvent(BatchCountdownChanged, remaining);
 833 |                 try
 834 |                 {
 835 |                     await Task.Delay(TimeSpan.FromSeconds(1), cts.Token).ConfigureAwait(false);
 836 |                 }
 837 |                 catch (OperationCanceledException)
 838 |                 {
 839 |                     // Window was fired early (or shutdown). The early-fire
 840 |                     // path raises BatchCountdownChanged(0) — but in case
 841 |                     // this is the shutdown path, raise it here too.
 842 |                     RaiseEvent(BatchCountdownChanged, 0);
 843 |                     return;
 844 |                 }
 845 | 
 846 |                 // Check if all ready players have submitted during the
 847 |                 // 1-second wait. If so, fire early.
 848 |                 bool fireEarly = false;
 849 |                 lock (_batchLock)
 850 |                 {
 851 |                     if (ReferenceEquals(_batchWindowCts, cts) &&
 852 |                         _batchReadyCount > 0 &&
 853 |                         _batchSubmitted.Count >= _batchReadyCount)
 854 |                     {
 855 |                         _batchWindowCts = null;
 856 |                         _batchSubmitted.Clear();
 857 |                         fireEarly = true;
 858 |                     }
 859 |                 }
 860 |                 if (fireEarly)
 861 |                 {
 862 |                     try { cts.Cancel(); cts.Dispose(); } catch { /* ignore */ }
 863 |                     RaiseEvent(BatchCountdownChanged, 0);
 864 |                     _ = ProcessNextTurnAsync(_shutdownToken);
 865 |                     return;
 866 |                 }
 867 |             }
 868 | 
 869 |             // Timeout — fire.
 870 |             lock (_batchLock)
 871 |             {
 872 |                 if (ReferenceEquals(_batchWindowCts, cts))
 873 |                 {
 874 |                     _batchWindowCts = null;
 875 |                     _batchSubmitted.Clear();
 876 |                 }
 877 |             }
 878 |             try { cts.Cancel(); cts.Dispose(); } catch { /* ignore */ }
 879 |             RaiseEvent(BatchCountdownChanged, 0);
 880 |             _ = ProcessNextTurnAsync(_shutdownToken);
 881 |         }
 882 |         catch (Exception ex)
 883 |         {
 884 |             Trace.WriteLine($"[HostSession] Batch window failed: {ex}");
 885 |             try { cts.Cancel(); cts.Dispose(); } catch { /* ignore */ }
 886 |             RaiseEvent(BatchCountdownChanged, 0);
 887 |         }
 888 |     }
 889 | 
 890 |     /// <summary>
 891 |     /// Count the ready non-spectator members. "Ready" = MemberStatus.Ready
 892 |     /// or MemberStatus.Playing. The host is always Ready (set in
 893 |     /// HostServer.StartAsync). Spectators are excluded — they can't
 894 |     /// submit actions. Returns at least 1 (the host).
 895 |     /// </summary>
 896 |     private int CountReadyPlayers()
 897 |     {
 898 |         return _server.Members
 899 |             .Count(m => m.Role != MemberRole.Spectator &&
 900 |                         (m.Status == MemberStatus.Ready || m.Status == MemberStatus.Playing));
 901 |     }
 902 | 
 903 |     // ─── Helpers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 904 | 
 905 |     /// <summary>
 906 |     /// Ensure <see cref="_meta"/> is populated. If a save id was given
 907 |     /// but the save doesn't exist on disk yet, create it lazily. If no
 908 |     /// save id was given, mint a new save.
 909 |     /// </summary>
 910 |     private void EnsureMeta()
 911 |     {
 912 |         if (_meta is not null) return;
 913 |         if (_saveManager is null) return;
 914 | 
 915 |         var saveName = $"Multiplayer game {_hostProfile.Nickname}";
 916 |         // Use the world's first location name as the world title if available.
 917 |         var firstLoc = _world.Locations.FirstOrDefault();
 918 |         if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(firstLoc?.Name)) saveName = firstLoc.Name;
 919 | 
 920 |         _meta = _saveManager.CreateSave(saveName, _world, _hostProfile.Id);
 921 |     }
 922 | 
 923 |     /// <summary>Generate a 32-char lowercase hex action id (matches the
 924 |     /// client-side id format used by <see cref="ClientSession"/>).</summary>
 925 |     private static string NewActionId() => Guid.NewGuid().ToString("N");
 926 | 
 927 |     /// <summary>Exception-safe event raise (same pattern as HostServer).</summary>
 928 |     private static void RaiseEvent<T>(Action<T>? handler, T args)
 929 |     {
 930 |         if (handler is null) return;
 931 |         var delegates = handler.GetInvocationList();
 932 |         foreach (var d in delegates)
 933 |         {
 934 |             try { ((Action<T>)d)(args); }
 935 |             catch (Exception ex) { Trace.WriteLine($"[HostSession] event subscriber threw: {ex}"); }
 936 |         }
 937 |     }
 938 | 
 939 |     /// <summary>Overload for two-arg events (used by NarrativeDelta).</summary>
 940 |     private static void RaiseEvent<T1, T2>(Action<T1, T2>? handler, T1 arg1, T2 arg2)
 941 |     {
 942 |         if (handler is null) return;
 943 |         var delegates = handler.GetInvocationList();
 944 |         foreach (var d in delegates)
 945 |         {
 946 |             try { ((Action<T1, T2>)d)(arg1, arg2); }
 947 |             catch (Exception ex) { Trace.WriteLine($"[HostSession] event subscriber threw: {ex}"); }
 948 |         }
 949 |     }
 950 | 
 951 |     /// <summary>
 952 |     /// Overload for the <see cref="ActionCancelled"/> event, which
 953 |     /// passes a possibly-null action id (null when an unknown id was
 954 |     /// cancelled). The signature uses <c>string?</c> explicitly so the
 955 |     /// compiler doesn't pick the generic overload with T=string and
 956 |     /// complain about nullability mismatches.
 957 |     /// </summary>
 958 |     private static void RaiseEventNullable(Action<string?>? handler, string? args)
 959 |     {
 960 |         if (handler is null) return;
 961 |         var delegates = handler.GetInvocationList();
 962 |         foreach (var d in delegates)
 963 |         {
 964 |             try { ((Action<string?>)d)(args); }
 965 |             catch (Exception ex) { Trace.WriteLine($"[HostSession] event subscriber threw: {ex}"); }
 966 |         }
 967 |     }
 968 | }

   3 | // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   4 | //  Enums
   5 | // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   6 | 
   7 | /// <summary>
   8 | /// Role a member plays in a multiplayer party. The host owns the simulation
   9 | /// (GameMaster runs in their process); players submit actions and receive
  10 | /// narrative/state; spectators receive everything but cannot submit actions.
  11 | /// </summary>
  12 | public enum MemberRole
  13 | {
  14 |     /// <summary>
  15 |     /// The desktop app that started the in-process WebSocket server.
  16 |     /// Owns the GameMaster + World + SaveManager. There is exactly one
  17 |     /// Host per party.
  18 |     /// </summary>
  19 |     Host = 0,
  20 | 
  21 |     /// <summary>
  22 |     /// A regular participant — submits actions, receives narrative.
  23 |     /// </summary>
  24 |     Player = 1,
  25 | 
  26 |     /// <summary>
  27 |     /// Read-only participant — receives narrative + state updates but
  28 |     /// cannot enqueue actions. Useful for streaming / observing.
  29 |     /// </summary>
  30 |     Spectator = 2,
  31 | }
  32 | 
  33 | /// <summary>
  34 | /// Lifecycle status of a single member. Mirrors the lobby flow:
  35 | /// Pending → Ready → Playing, with Disconnected as a terminal state.
  36 | /// </summary>
  37 | public enum MemberStatus
  38 | {
  39 |     /// <summary>Just joined, hasn't signalled ready yet.</summary>
  40 |     Pending = 0,
  41 | 
  42 |     /// <summary>Ready in the lobby — waiting for the host to start the game.</summary>
  43 |     Ready = 1,
  44 | 
  45 |     /// <summary>Actively playing — game has started and the member has a character.</summary>
  46 |     Playing = 2,
  47 | 
  48 |     /// <summary>Connection lost or gracefully closed. Member is kept in the
  49 |     /// roster briefly so the UI can show "X disconnected" before removing.</summary>
  50 |     Disconnected = 3,
  51 | }
  52 | 
  53 | /// <summary>
  54 | /// Lifecycle status of the whole party. Drives which UI screen is shown.
  55 | /// Mirrors the TS app's party.status field.
  56 | /// </summary>
  57 | public enum PartyStatus
  58 | {
  59 |     /// <summary>In the lobby — waiting for players to join and ready up.</summary>
  60 |     Lobby = 0,
  61 | 
  62 |     /// <summary>Host is generating a new world via the WorldBuilder orchestrator.</summary>
  63 |     Worldbuilding = 1,
  64 | 
  65 |     /// <summary>Players are creating their characters.</summary>
  66 |     CharacterCreation = 2,
  67 | 
  68 |     /// <summary>Game is in progress — GM is processing turns.</summary>
  69 |     Playing = 3,
  70 | }
  71 | 
  72 | // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  73 | //  MemberInfo
  74 | // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  75 | 
  76 | /// <summary>
  77 | /// One member of a multiplayer party. Mirrors the TS app's
  78 | /// <c>party.members[]</c> shape, minus the auth/JWT fields (no auth in the
  79 | /// desktop rewrite — the local Profile is the identity).
  80 | ///
  81 | /// <para>
  82 | /// A <see cref="MemberInfo"/> is created by the HostServer when a client
  83 | /// completes the HelloMsg → WelcomeMsg handshake. The host assigns
  84 | /// <see cref="ConnectionId"/> (a fresh Guid per connection); the client
  85 | /// supplies <see cref="ProfileId"/> + <see cref="Nickname"/> via the
  86 | /// HelloMsg (copied from their local <c>Profile</c>). The host validates
  87 | /// nickname uniqueness before assigning the connection id.
  88 | /// </para>
  89 | ///
  90 | /// <para>
  91 | /// Record (immutable) — to update a member's status (e.g. Pending → Ready),
  92 | /// the HostServer constructs a new <see cref="MemberInfo"/> with the updated
  93 | /// field and replaces the entry in its members dictionary. The previous
  94 | /// instance is never mutated.
  95 | /// </para>
  96 | /// </summary>
  97 | public sealed record MemberInfo
  98 | {
  99 |     /// <summary>
 100 |     /// Server-assigned per-connection id. A new Guid every time a client
 101 |     /// connects (even if they reconnect with the same ProfileId). Used as
 102 |     /// the routing key for <see cref="HostServer.SendAsync"/> and as the
 103 |     /// <c>fromId</c> on chat / action messages.
 104 |     /// </summary>
 105 |     public required Guid ConnectionId { get; init; }
 106 | 
 107 |     /// <summary>
 108 |     /// Stable profile id from the local <c>Profile.Id</c>. Survives
 109 |     /// reconnects (the same player joining twice has the same ProfileId
 110 |     /// but different ConnectionIds).
 111 |     /// </summary>
 112 |     public required Guid ProfileId { get; init; }
 113 | 
 114 |     /// <summary>
 115 |     /// Display name from the local <c>Profile.Nickname</c>. The host
 116 |     /// validates uniqueness per party — duplicates are rejected in the
 117 |     /// handshake with a <see cref="Protocol.RejectMsg"/>.
 118 |     /// </summary>
 119 |     public required string Nickname { get; init; }
 120 | 
 121 |     /// <summary>Role in the party. The host is always <see cref="MemberRole.Host"/>.</summary>
 122 |     public MemberRole Role { get; init; } = MemberRole.Player;
 123 | 
 124 |     /// <summary>Current lifecycle status of this member.</summary>
 125 |     public MemberStatus Status { get; init; } = MemberStatus.Pending;
 126 | 
 127 |     /// <summary>UTC moment the member joined the party.</summary>
 128 |     public DateTimeOffset JoinedAt { get; init; } = DateTimeOffset.UtcNow;
 129 | }

--- src/MyGame.Core/Multiplayer/Protocol/Messages.cs (C#, 357 lines, ~3,898 tokens) ---
   1 | namespace MyGame.Core.Multiplayer.Protocol;
   2 | 
   3 | // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   4 | //  Handshake
   5 | // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   6 | 
   7 | /// <summary>
   8 | /// Client → Host. First message after the WebSocket upgrade. Carries the
   9 | /// client's protocol version + local profile identity. The host validates
  10 | /// the protocol version (must be compatible per
  11 | /// <see cref="MyGame.Core.Common.Version.IsCompatible"/>) and nickname
  12 | /// uniqueness, then either replies with <see cref="WelcomeMsg"/> (success)
  13 | /// or <see cref="RejectMsg"/> + closes the connection.
  14 | /// </summary>
  15 | public sealed record HelloMsg : NetMessage
  16 | {
  17 |     /// <summary>Protocol version the client speaks (compared to
  18 |     /// <see cref="MyGame.Core.Common.Version.Current"/>).</summary>
  19 |     public required string ProtocolVersion { get; init; }
  20 | 
  21 |     /// <summary>Display nickname from the local Profile.</summary>
  22 |     public required string Nickname { get; init; }
  23 | 
  24 |     /// <summary>Stable profile id from the local Profile.</summary>
  25 |     public required Guid ProfileId { get; init; }
  26 | }
  27 | 
  28 | /// <summary>
  29 | /// Host → Client. Successful handshake reply. Carries the server-assigned
  30 | /// connection id, the current party snapshot, and the role the client
  31 | /// has been assigned (Host is never assigned here — the host doesn't
  32 | /// send HelloMsg to itself; only Player/Spectator are sent).
  33 | /// </summary>
  34 | public sealed record WelcomeMsg : NetMessage
  35 | {
  36 |     /// <summary>Server-assigned connection id (use this for fromId in
  37 |     /// subsequent messages).</summary>
  38 |     public required Guid ConnectionId { get; init; }
  39 | 
  40 |     /// <summary>Full party state at the moment of join.</summary>
  41 |     public required PartySnapshot Party { get; init; }
  42 | 
  43 |     /// <summary>Role assigned to the joining client.</summary>
  44 |     public required MemberRole Role { get; init; }
  45 | }
  46 | 
  47 | /// <summary>
  48 | /// Host → Client. Handshake rejection. Sent when the protocol version is
  49 | /// incompatible, the nickname is invalid/taken, or the party is full.
  50 | /// The host closes the connection immediately after sending.
  51 | /// </summary>
  52 | public sealed record RejectMsg : NetMessage
  53 | {
  54 |     /// <summary>Human-readable rejection reason (RU, matches the
  55 |     /// Profile.ValidateNickname error strings).</summary>
  56 |     public required string Reason { get; init; }
  57 | }
  58 | 
  59 | // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  60 | //  Lobby
  61 | // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  62 | 
  63 | /// <summary>
  64 | /// Host → All. A new member has completed the handshake and joined the
  65 | /// party. The new member also receives this (after their WelcomeMsg) so
  66 | /// they see themselves in the roster.
  67 | /// </summary>
  68 | public sealed record MemberJoinedMsg : NetMessage
  69 | {
  70 |     /// <summary>The member that just joined.</summary>
  71 |     public required MemberInfo Member { get; init; }
  72 | }
  73 | 
  74 | /// <summary>
  75 | /// Host → All. A member has disconnected (graceful close or network
  76 | /// drop). The host keeps the member in the roster briefly with status
  77 | /// Disconnected before removing them; this message signals the
  78 | /// disconnection event.
  79 | /// </summary>
  80 | public sealed record MemberLeftMsg : NetMessage
  81 | {
  82 |     /// <summary>Connection id of the member that left.</summary>
  83 |     public required Guid ConnectionId { get; init; }
  84 | 
  85 |     /// <summary>Human-readable reason ("disconnected", "kicked", "timeout").</summary>
  86 |     public string? Reason { get; init; }
  87 | }
  88 | 
  89 | /// <summary>
  90 | /// Client → Host (then Host → All). A member toggles their ready state
  91 | /// in the lobby. The host validates the transition (Pending ⇄ Ready)
  92 | /// and re-broadcasts to all clients.
  93 | /// </summary>
  94 | public sealed record MemberReadyMsg : NetMessage
  95 | {
  96 |     /// <summary>Connection id of the member whose ready state changed.</summary>
  97 |     public required Guid ConnectionId { get; init; }
  98 | 
  99 |     /// <summary>New ready state.</summary>
 100 |     public required bool Ready { get; init; }
 101 | }

 103 | /// <summary>
 104 | /// Lobby chat. Client → Host → All (symmetric). One-to-many chat for
 105 | /// the lobby/character-creation phases. Host relays verbatim after
 106 | /// sanitising the text (trim + 1000-char cap, matching the TS source).
 107 | /// </summary>
 108 | public sealed record ChatMsg : NetMessage
 109 | {
 110 |     /// <summary>Connection id of the sender (host rewrites this from the
 111 |     /// socket identity — clients cannot spoof another member's id).</summary>
 112 |     public required Guid FromId { get; init; }
 113 | 
 114 |     /// <summary>Sender's nickname (denormalised for client convenience).</summary>
 115 |     public required string FromNickname { get; init; }
 116 | 
 117 |     /// <summary>Chat text (already trimmed + capped at 1000 chars by the host).</summary>
 118 |     public required string Text { get; init; }
 119 | 
 120 |     /// <summary>UTC moment the host received the message.</summary>
 121 |     public required DateTimeOffset Ts { get; init; }
 122 | }
 123 | 
 124 | /// <summary>
 125 | /// Host → All. Party lifecycle status changed (Lobby → Worldbuilding →
 126 | /// CharacterCreation → Playing). Clients use this to switch UI screens.
 127 | /// </summary>
 128 | public sealed record StatusChangedMsg : NetMessage
 129 | {
 130 |     /// <summary>New party status.</summary>
 131 |     public required PartyStatus Status { get; init; }
 132 | 
 133 |     /// <summary>Current turn (relevant when transitioning into Playing).</summary>
 134 |     public int Turn { get; init; }
 135 | 
 136 |     /// <summary>Save id (relevant when transitioning into Playing).</summary>
 137 |     public string? SaveId { get; init; }
 138 | }

 140 | // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 141 | //  Game — actions
 142 | // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 143 | 
 144 | /// <summary>
 145 | /// Player action queued for GM processing. Client → Host → All
 146 | /// (symmetric). The client generates the <see cref="ActionId"/> locally
 147 | /// (so they can track/cancel it before the host broadcasts it back); the
 148 | /// host validates, enqueues in the action queue, and re-broadcasts to
 149 | /// all clients so everyone's UI shows the new pending action.
 150 | /// </summary>
 151 | public sealed record ActionQueuedMsg : NetMessage
 152 | {
 153 |     /// <summary>Client-generated unique id for this action.</summary>
 154 |     public required string ActionId { get; init; }
 155 | 
 156 |     /// <summary>Connection id of the submitting player (host rewrites
 157 |     /// from socket identity — clients cannot spoof).</summary>
 158 |     public required Guid FromId { get; init; }
 159 | 
 160 |     /// <summary>Submitter's nickname (denormalised).</summary>
 161 |     public required string FromNickname { get; init; }
 162 | 
 163 |     /// <summary>Player's free-text action (trimmed, capped at 4000 chars).</summary>
 164 |     public required string Text { get; init; }
 165 | 
 166 |     /// <summary>UTC moment the action was submitted.</summary>
 167 |     public required DateTimeOffset Ts { get; init; }
 168 | }
 169 | 
 170 | /// <summary>
 171 | /// Client → Host. Cancel a previously-queued action. The host removes
 172 | /// it from the action queue (if it hasn't been picked up by the GM yet)
 173 | /// and re-broadcasts an <see cref="ActionCancelledMsg"/> to all clients.
 174 | /// </summary>
 175 | public sealed record ActionCancelMsg : NetMessage
 176 | {
 177 |     /// <summary>The action id to cancel.</summary>
 178 |     public required string ActionId { get; init; }
 179 | }
 180 | 
 181 | /// <summary>
 182 | /// Host → All. An action has been cancelled (either by the original
 183 | /// submitter or by the host). Clients remove it from their pending list.
 184 | /// </summary>
 185 | public sealed record ActionCancelledMsg : NetMessage
 186 | {
 187 |     /// <summary>The action id that was cancelled.</summary>
 188 |     public required string ActionId { get; init; }
 189 | }
 190 | 
 191 | /// <summary>
 192 | /// Host → All. The GM has picked up a batch of actions for resolution.
 193 | /// Clients mark these actions as "resolving" in the UI (greyed out,
 194 | /// non-cancellable). Sent before the first <see cref="NarrativeDeltaMsg"/>
 195 | /// for the turn.
 196 | /// </summary>
 197 | public sealed record ActionResolvingMsg : NetMessage
 198 | {
 199 |     /// <summary>Ids of the actions being resolved in this batch.</summary>
 200 |     public required IReadOnlyList<string> ActionIds { get; init; } = Array.Empty<string>();
 201 | }
 202 | 
 203 | // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 204 | //  Game — narrative + state
 205 | // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 206 | 
 207 | /// <summary>
 208 | /// Host → All. Streaming narrative text delta. Clients append to the
 209 | /// current narration buffer. Sent incrementally as the GM streams tokens
 210 | /// (in the current implementation the GM is non-streaming, so the
 211 | /// HostSession sends one large delta with the full narration followed by
 212 | /// a <see cref="NarrativeFinalMsg"/>; the streaming wire format is in
 213 | /// place so a future streaming GM can drop in without protocol changes).
 214 | /// </summary>
 215 | public sealed record NarrativeDeltaMsg : NetMessage
 216 | {
 217 |     /// <summary>Text chunk to append to the current narration.</summary>
 218 |     public required string TextDelta { get; init; }
 219 | 
 220 |     /// <summary>
 221 |     /// Turn number this narration belongs to. Lets clients correlate
 222 |     /// deltas with the eventual <see cref="TurnEndMsg"/>.
 223 |     /// </summary>
 224 |     public int Turn { get; init; }
 225 | }
 226 | 
 227 | /// <summary>
 228 | /// Host → All. Final narration for the turn — full text + the tool calls
 229 | /// the GM made. Clients replace their accumulated delta buffer with this
 230 | /// canonical full text (in case any deltas were lost or arrived out of
 231 | /// order) and render the tool-call feed.
 232 | /// </summary>
 233 | public sealed record NarrativeFinalMsg : NetMessage
 234 | {
 235 |     /// <summary>Full narration text for the turn.</summary>
 236 |     public required string FullText { get; init; }
 237 | 
 238 |     /// <summary>Tool calls the GM made this turn (may be empty).</summary>
 239 |     public IReadOnlyList<ToolEvent> ToolEvents { get; init; } = Array.Empty<ToolEvent>();
 240 | 
 241 |     /// <summary>Turn number this narration finalises.</summary>
 242 |     public int Turn { get; init; }
 243 | 
 244 |     /// <summary>
 245 |     /// Prompt (input) tokens billed for this turn across all GM
 246 |     /// iterations. 0 when the provider didn't return a usage block.
 247 |     /// Forwarded to the host UI so the token-billing widget can
 248 |     /// accumulate session totals. Clients ignore this (they don't run
 249 |     /// the GM).
 250 |     /// </summary>
 251 |     public int PromptTokens { get; init; }
 252 | 
 253 |     /// <summary>
 254 |     /// Completion (output) tokens billed for this turn. See
 255 |     /// <see cref="PromptTokens"/>.
 256 |     /// </summary>
 257 |     public int CompletionTokens { get; init; }
 258 | 
 259 |     /// <summary>
 260 |     /// Total tokens billed for this turn (prompt + completion).
 261 |     /// Convenience field so the UI doesn't need to add the two
 262 |     /// components itself.
 263 |     /// </summary>
 264 |     public int TotalTokens { get; init; }
 265 | }
 266 | 
 267 | /// <summary>
 268 | /// Host → All. World state update — either a full snapshot (the entire
 269 | /// World JSON) or, in a future revision, a diff. Clients replace their
 270 | /// local World cache with this. Sent after each GM turn so all clients
 271 | /// see the new entity state (HP, inventory, NPC positions, …).
 272 | /// </summary>
 273 | public sealed record StateUpdateMsg : NetMessage
 274 | {
 275 |     /// <summary>
 276 |     /// World state as a JSON string (World.ToJson()). Sent as a string
 277 |     /// (not a nested object) so the wire format stays simple and the
 278 |     /// client can either deserialize into a World or stash the raw JSON
 279 |     /// for later.
 280 |     /// </summary>
 281 |     public required string WorldJson { get; init; }
 282 | 
 283 |     /// <summary>Current turn number after the update.</summary>
 284 |     public int Turn { get; init; }
 285 | 
 286 |     /// <summary>
 287 |     /// True when <see cref="WorldJson"/> is a full World snapshot;
 288 |     /// false when it's a diff (future). Currently always true.
 289 |     /// </summary>
 290 |     public bool Full { get; init; } = true;
 291 | }
 292 | 
 293 | /// <summary>
 294 | /// Host → All. The current GM turn has ended. Clients stop appending to
 295 | /// the narration buffer and re-enable the action input.
 296 | /// </summary>
 297 | public sealed record TurnEndMsg : NetMessage
 298 | {
 299 |     /// <summary>The turn number that just ended.</summary>
 300 |     public required int TurnNumber { get; init; }
 301 | }
 302 | 
 303 | // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 304 | //  System
 305 | // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 306 | 
 307 | /// <summary>
 308 | /// Host → All (or Host → One). Generic error message — bad input, server
 309 | /// fault, etc. The host doesn't close the connection on Error; the client
 310 | /// can decide whether to recover or disconnect.
 311 | /// </summary>
 312 | public sealed record ErrorMsg : NetMessage
 313 | {
 314 |     /// <summary>Human-readable error message (RU).</summary>
 315 |     public required string Message { get; init; }
 316 | }
 317 | 
 318 | /// <summary>
 319 | /// Host → Client. The client has been kicked (e.g. host closed the
 320 | /// party, or the client was misbehaving). The host closes the connection
 321 | /// immediately after sending.
 322 | /// </summary>
 323 | public sealed record KickedMsg : NetMessage
 324 | {
 325 |     /// <summary>Human-readable kick reason.</summary>
 326 |     public required string Reason { get; init; }
 327 | }
 328 | 
 329 | /// <summary>
 330 | /// Bidirectional. Keepalive ping. Either side may send; the other side
 331 | /// replies with <see cref="PongMsg"/> with the same <see cref="Ts"/>.
 332 | /// </summary>
 333 | public sealed record PingMsg : NetMessage
 334 | {
 335 |     /// <summary>UTC moment the ping was sent.</summary>
 336 |     public required DateTimeOffset Ts { get; init; }
 337 | }
 338 | 
 339 | /// <summary>
 340 | /// Bidirectional. Keepalive pong — echoes the ping's <see cref="Ts"/>.
 341 | /// </summary>
 342 | public sealed record PongMsg : NetMessage
 343 | {
 344 |     /// <summary>UTC moment of the original ping.</summary>
 345 |     public required DateTimeOffset Ts { get; init; }
 346 | }
 347 | 
 348 | /// <summary>
 349 | /// Host → Client. Sent to a late joiner after <see cref="WelcomeMsg"/>.
 350 | /// Contains the last N log entries so the new client has context (issue #32).
 351 | /// </summary>
 352 | public sealed record LogSyncMsg : NetMessage
 353 | {
