--- src/MyGame.Core/Multiplayer/Protocol/MultiplayerJson.cs (C#, 86 lines, ~1,084 tokens) ---
   1 | using System.Text.Encodings.Web;
   2 | using System.Text.Json;
   3 | using System.Text.Json.Serialization;
   4 | 
   5 | namespace MyGame.Core.Multiplayer.Protocol;
   6 | 
   7 | /// <summary>
   8 | /// Shared <see cref="JsonSerializerOptions"/> for all Multiplayer-layer JSON.
   9 | ///
  10 | /// Convention (from the worklog): camelCase on the wire, raw UTF-8 (no
  11 | /// \uXXXX escapes for Cyrillic), null fields omitted. Matches the World /
  12 | /// Saves / Profile layers' JSON flavor so a single mental model applies
  13 | /// across the codebase.
  14 | ///
  15 | /// <para>
  16 | /// <b>Polymorphism:</b> the options pick up the <c>[JsonPolymorphic]</c>
  17 | /// and <c>[JsonDerivedType]</c> attributes on <see cref="NetMessage"/>
  18 | /// automatically — no converter registration needed. To serialise a
  19 | /// message polymorphically, use
  20 | /// <c>JsonSerializer.Serialize&lt;NetMessage&gt;(msg, MultiplayerJson.Options)</c>
  21 | /// (the explicit type parameter is required). To deserialise:
  22 | /// <c>JsonSerializer.Deserialize&lt;NetMessage&gt;(json, MultiplayerJson.Options)</c>.
  23 | /// </para>
  24 | /// </summary>
  25 | public static class MultiplayerJson
  26 | {
  27 |     /// <summary>
  28 |     /// Default options for the multiplayer wire format. Instance is
  29 |     /// immutable and shared across all callers (JsonSerializerOptions is
  30 |     /// thread-safe for reads after the first serialization).
  31 |     /// </summary>
  32 |     public static JsonSerializerOptions Options { get; } = BuildOptions();
  33 | 
  34 |     private static JsonSerializerOptions BuildOptions()
  35 |     {
  36 |         var opts = new JsonSerializerOptions
  37 |         {
  38 |             PropertyNamingPolicy = JsonNamingPolicy.CamelCase,
  39 |             PropertyNameCaseInsensitive = true,
  40 |             DefaultIgnoreCondition = JsonIgnoreCondition.WhenWritingNull,
  41 |             // Match WorldJson.Options: write Cyrillic (and all non-ASCII)
  42 |             // as raw UTF-8 so wire traffic is human-readable when sniffed.
  43 |             Encoder = JavaScriptEncoder.UnsafeRelaxedJsonEscaping,
  44 |             // Compact wire format — no pretty-printing (saves bytes per
  45 |             // message, which matters for streaming narrative deltas).
  46 |             WriteIndented = false,
  47 |             IncludeFields = false,
  48 |         };
  49 |         return opts;
  50 |     }
  51 | 
  52 |     /// <summary>
  53 |     /// Serialize a <see cref="NetMessage"/> polymorphically to UTF-8 bytes
  54 |     /// (the form <see cref="System.Net.WebSockets.WebSocket.SendAsync"/>
  55 |     /// wants). Use this in the host / client send loops.
  56 |     /// </summary>
  57 |     public static byte[] SerializeToUtf8Bytes(NetMessage msg) =>
  58 |         JsonSerializer.SerializeToUtf8Bytes<NetMessage>(msg, Options);
  59 | 
  60 |     /// <summary>
  61 |     /// Serialize a <see cref="NetMessage"/> polymorphically to a string.
  62 |     /// Mostly for tests / debugging — the wire path uses
  63 |     /// <see cref="SerializeToUtf8Bytes"/> directly to avoid an extra
  64 |     /// UTF-8 → string → UTF-8 round-trip.
  65 |     /// </summary>
  66 |     public static string Serialize(NetMessage msg) =>
  67 |         JsonSerializer.Serialize<NetMessage>(msg, Options);
  68 | 
  69 |     /// <summary>
  70 |     /// Deserialize a UTF-8 string into a <see cref="NetMessage"/> of the
  71 |     /// appropriate derived type. Returns null if the JSON is empty or
  72 |     /// the discriminator is unknown (the caller should treat null as a
  73 |     /// protocol error and close the connection).
  74 |     /// </summary>
  75 |     public static NetMessage? Deserialize(string json) =>
  76 |         string.IsNullOrEmpty(json) ? null : JsonSerializer.Deserialize<NetMessage>(json, Options);
  77 | 
  78 |     /// <summary>
  79 |     /// Deserialize from a UTF-8 byte buffer (the form
  80 |     /// <see cref="System.Net.WebSockets.WebSocket.ReceiveAsync"/> produces).
  81 |     /// Avoids an extra byte → string → byte round-trip on the receive path.
  82 |     /// </summary>
  83 |     public static NetMessage? Deserialize(ReadOnlySpan<byte> utf8Bytes) =>
  84 |         utf8Bytes.IsEmpty ? null : JsonSerializer.Deserialize<NetMessage>(utf8Bytes, Options);
  85 | }

--- src/MyGame.Core/Multiplayer/Protocol/NetMessage.cs (C#, 125 lines, ~1,577 tokens) ---
   1 | using System.Text.Json.Serialization;
   2 | 
   3 | namespace MyGame.Core.Multiplayer.Protocol;
   4 | 
   5 | // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   6 | //  NetMessage — polymorphic base
   7 | // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   8 | 
   9 | /// <summary>
  10 | /// Polymorphic base type for every message exchanged over the WebSocket
  11 | /// wire. Uses <c>System.Text.Json</c>'s .NET 7+ polymorphism support:
  12 | /// <c>[JsonPolymorphic(TypeDiscriminatorPropertyName = "kind")]</c> writes
  13 | /// a <c>"kind"</c> field on every message; <c>[JsonDerivedType]</c> maps
  14 | /// each derived record to a discriminator string.
  15 | ///
  16 | /// <para>
  17 | /// On the wire, a <see cref="HelloMsg"/> serialises as:
  18 | /// <code>
  19 | /// { "kind": "hello", "protocolVersion": "0.2.0", "nickname": "Бродяга", "profileId": "..." }
  20 | /// </code>
  21 | /// </para>
  22 | ///
  23 | /// <para>
  24 | /// Deserialize with <c>JsonSerializer.Deserialize&lt;NetMessage&gt;(json,
  25 | /// MultiplayerJson.Options)</c> — the returned reference points to the
  26 | /// actual derived type. Serialize with
  27 | /// <c>JsonSerializer.Serialize&lt;NetMessage&gt;(msg, MultiplayerJson.Options)</c>
  28 | /// (the explicit <c>&lt;NetMessage&gt;</c> type parameter is REQUIRED —
  29 | /// serialising as the derived type would skip polymorphism and not write
  30 | /// the discriminator).
  31 | /// </para>
  32 | ///
  33 | /// <para>
  34 | /// Wire convention (from the worklog): camelCase property names, raw
  35 | /// UTF-8 (no \uXXXX escapes for Cyrillic), null fields omitted. The
  36 | /// shared options live in <see cref="MultiplayerJson"/>.
  37 | /// </para>
  38 | ///
  39 | /// <para><b>Discriminator strings</b> use snake_case to match the TS
  40 | /// source's event names (<c>lobby:member_joined</c>, <c>game:action_queued</c>,
  41 | /// …) — the C# port drops the namespace prefix and keeps the snake_case
  42 | /// suffix. This makes the wire format recognisable to anyone familiar with
  43 | /// the TS protocol.</para>
  44 | /// </summary>
  45 | [JsonPolymorphic(TypeDiscriminatorPropertyName = "kind", IgnoreUnrecognizedTypeDiscriminators = true)]
  46 | [JsonDerivedType(typeof(HelloMsg),            "hello")]
  47 | [JsonDerivedType(typeof(WelcomeMsg),          "welcome")]
  48 | [JsonDerivedType(typeof(RejectMsg),           "reject")]
  49 | // Lobby
  50 | [JsonDerivedType(typeof(MemberJoinedMsg),     "member_joined")]
  51 | [JsonDerivedType(typeof(MemberLeftMsg),       "member_left")]
  52 | [JsonDerivedType(typeof(MemberReadyMsg),      "member_ready")]
  53 | [JsonDerivedType(typeof(ChatMsg),             "chat")]
  54 | [JsonDerivedType(typeof(StatusChangedMsg),    "status_changed")]
  55 | // Game
  56 | [JsonDerivedType(typeof(ActionQueuedMsg),     "action_queued")]
  57 | [JsonDerivedType(typeof(ActionCancelMsg),     "action_cancel")]
  58 | [JsonDerivedType(typeof(ActionCancelledMsg),  "action_cancelled")]
  59 | [JsonDerivedType(typeof(ActionResolvingMsg),  "action_resolving")]
  60 | [JsonDerivedType(typeof(NarrativeDeltaMsg),   "narrative_delta")]
  61 | [JsonDerivedType(typeof(NarrativeFinalMsg),   "narrative_final")]
  62 | [JsonDerivedType(typeof(StateUpdateMsg),      "state_update")]
  63 | [JsonDerivedType(typeof(TurnEndMsg),          "turn_end")]
  64 | // System
  65 | [JsonDerivedType(typeof(ErrorMsg),            "error")]
  66 | [JsonDerivedType(typeof(KickedMsg),           "kicked")]
  67 | [JsonDerivedType(typeof(PingMsg),             "ping")]
  68 | [JsonDerivedType(typeof(PongMsg),             "pong")]
  69 | [JsonDerivedType(typeof(LogSyncMsg),          "log_sync")]
  70 | public abstract record NetMessage;
  71 | 
  72 | // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  73 | //  Helper records embedded in messages
  74 | // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  75 | 
  76 | /// <summary>
  77 | /// Snapshot of the party state, sent to a client in the
  78 | /// <see cref="WelcomeMsg"/> right after the handshake completes. Carries
  79 | /// the host's connection id (so clients know who the host is), the
  80 | /// current roster, the party lifecycle status, the save id (if the
  81 | /// party is in/after worldbuilding), and the current turn number.
  82 | /// </summary>
  83 | public sealed record PartySnapshot
  84 | {
  85 |     /// <summary>Connection id of the host (the player who started the server).</summary>
  86 |     public required Guid HostConnectionId { get; init; }
  87 | 
  88 |     /// <summary>Current roster (host + every connected client).</summary>
  89 |     public IReadOnlyList<MemberInfo> Members { get; init; } = Array.Empty<MemberInfo>();
  90 | 
  91 |     /// <summary>Party lifecycle status.</summary>
  92 |     public PartyStatus Status { get; init; } = PartyStatus.Lobby;
  93 | 
  94 |     /// <summary>
  95 |     /// Save id the party is playing (or null if still in the lobby
  96 |     /// before worldbuilding). Sent so joining clients can request the
  97 |     /// save's world JSON via <see cref="StateUpdateMsg"/>.
  98 |     /// </summary>
  99 |     public string? SaveId { get; init; }
 100 | 
 101 |     /// <summary>Current turn number (0 before the game starts).</summary>
 102 |     public int Turn { get; init; }
 103 | }
 104 | 
 105 | /// <summary>
 106 | /// One tool call executed by the GameMaster during a turn. Sent in the
 107 | /// <see cref="NarrativeFinalMsg"/> so clients can render a "tool call
 108 | /// feed" alongside the narration. Mirrors the
 109 | /// <see cref="MyGame.Core.AI.Agents.AppliedToolCall"/> port-side record.
 110 | /// </summary>
 111 | public sealed record ToolEvent
 112 | {
 113 |     /// <summary>Tool name (matches the registered tool definition).</summary>
 114 |     public required string Name { get; init; }
 115 | 
 116 |     /// <summary>Raw JSON-string arguments the model produced.</summary>
 117 |     public required string ArgsJson { get; init; }
 118 | 
 119 |     /// <summary>Human-readable tool result text.</summary>
 120 |     public required string Result { get; init; }
 121 | 
 122 |     /// <summary>Whether the tool call errored.</summary>
 123 |     public bool IsError { get; init; }
 124 | }

--- src/MyGame.Core/Multiplayer/UpnpForwarder.cs (C#, 192 lines, ~2,230 tokens) ---
   1 | using System;
   2 | using System.Net;
   3 | using System.Net.Http;
   4 | using System.Net.NetworkInformation;
   5 | using System.Text;
   6 | using System.Text.RegularExpressions;
   7 | using System.Threading;
   8 | using System.Threading.Tasks;
   9 | using System.Xml.Linq;
  10 | 
  11 | namespace MyGame.Core.Multiplayer;
  12 | 
  13 | /// <summary>
  14 | /// Attempts UPnP/IGD port forwarding via SSDP discovery + SOAP requests.
  15 | /// Issue #29. Best-effort — silently falls back to manual forwarding if
  16 | /// UPnP is unavailable (no router, router doesn't support UPnP, etc.).
  17 | ///
  18 | /// <para>
  19 | /// No external NuGet packages — uses raw HTTP + SSDP multicast. This is
  20 | /// a minimal implementation that works with most consumer routers.
  21 | /// </para>
  22 | /// </summary>
  23 | public static class UpnpForwarder
  24 | {
  25 |     private const string SsdpMulticastAddress = "239.255.255.250";
  26 |     private const int SsdpPort = 1900;
  27 |     private static readonly TimeSpan DiscoverTimeout = TimeSpan.FromSeconds(3);
  28 | 
  29 |     /// <summary>
  30 |     /// Try to discover the IGD (Internet Gateway Device) on the local
  31 |     /// network via SSDP, then request a port mapping. Returns the public
  32 |     /// IP:port if successful, null if UPnP is unavailable.
  33 |     /// </summary>
  34 |     public static async Task<UpnpResult?> TryForwardAsync(
  35 |         int internalPort, int externalPort, string description, CancellationToken ct = default)
  36 |     {
  37 |         try
  38 |         {
  39 |             // 1. SSDP discover — send M-SEARCH multicast, wait for response.
  40 |             var controlUrl = await DiscoverIgdAsync(ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
  41 |             if (controlUrl is null) return null;
  42 | 
  43 |             // 2. Get external IP.
  44 |             var externalIp = await GetExternalIpAsync(controlUrl, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
  45 |             if (externalIp is null) return null;
  46 | 
  47 |             // 3. Add port mapping.
  48 |             var ok = await AddPortMappingAsync(controlUrl, internalPort, externalPort, description, ct)
  49 |                 .ConfigureAwait(false);
  50 |             if (!ok) return null;
  51 | 
  52 |             return new UpnpResult
  53 |             {
  54 |                 PublicIp = externalIp,
  55 |                 ExternalPort = externalPort,
  56 |                 ControlUrl = controlUrl,
  57 |             };
  58 |         }
  59 |         catch
  60 |         {
  61 |             return null; // best-effort
  62 |         }
  63 |     }
  64 | 
  65 |     private static async Task<string?> DiscoverIgdAsync(CancellationToken ct)
  66 |     {
  67 |         using var udp = new System.Net.Sockets.UdpClient();
  68 |         udp.Client.ReceiveTimeout = (int)DiscoverTimeout.TotalMilliseconds;
  69 | 
  70 |         var searchMessage = Encoding.ASCII.GetBytes(
  71 |             "M-SEARCH * HTTP/1.1\r\n" +
  72 |             $"HOST: {SsdpMulticastAddress}:{SsdpPort}\r\n" +
  73 |             "MAN: \"ssdp:discover\"\r\n" +
  74 |             "MX: 2\r\n" +
  75 |             "ST: urn:schemas-upnp-org:device:InternetGatewayDevice:1\r\n\r\n");
  76 | 
  77 |         var endpoint = new IPEndPoint(IPAddress.Parse(SsdpMulticastAddress), SsdpPort);
  78 |         await udp.SendAsync(searchMessage, searchMessage.Length, endpoint).ConfigureAwait(false);
  79 | 
  80 |         // Wait for a response (best-effort, short timeout).
  81 |         var receiveTask = udp.ReceiveAsync();
  82 |         var completed = await Task.WhenAny(receiveTask, Task.Delay(DiscoverTimeout, ct)).ConfigureAwait(false);
  83 |         if (completed != receiveTask) return null;
  84 | 
  85 |         var response = receiveTask.Result.Buffer;
  86 |         var responseStr = Encoding.ASCII.GetString(response);
  87 | 
  88 |         // Extract the LOCATION header.
  89 |         var match = Regex.Match(responseStr, @"LOCATION:\s*(\S+)", RegexOptions.IgnoreCase);
  90 |         if (!match.Success) return null;
  91 | 
  92 |         var deviceUrl = match.Groups[1].Value;
  93 | 
  94 |         // Fetch the device description XML to find the WANIPConnection control URL.
  95 |         using var http = new HttpClient { Timeout = DiscoverTimeout };
  96 |         var descXml = await http.GetStringAsync(deviceUrl, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
  97 |         var doc = XDocument.Parse(descXml);
  98 | 
  99 |         // Find the WANIPConnection service.
 100 |         var ns = XNamespace.Get("urn:schemas-upnp-org:device-1-0");
 101 |         var service = doc.Descendants(ns + "service")
 102 |             .FirstOrDefault(s => s.Element(ns + "serviceType")?.Value
 103 |                 ?.Contains("WANIPConnection") == true
 104 |                 || s.Element(ns + "serviceType")?.Value
 105 |                 ?.Contains("WANPPPConnection") == true);
 106 | 
 107 |         if (service is null) return null;
 108 | 
 109 |         var controlPath = service.Element(ns + "controlURL")?.Value;
 110 |         if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(controlPath)) return null;
 111 | 
 112 |         // Combine with the device URL base.
 113 |         var baseUri = new Uri(deviceUrl);
 114 |         var controlUri = new Uri(baseUri, controlPath);
 115 |         return controlUri.ToString();
 116 |     }
 117 | 
 118 |     private static async Task<string?> GetExternalIpAsync(string controlUrl, CancellationToken ct)
 119 |     {
 120 |         var soap = $@"<?xml version=""1.0""?>
 121 | <s:Envelope xmlns:s=""http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/"" s:encodingStyle=""http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/encoding/"">
 122 | <s:Body>
 123 | <u:GetExternalIPAddress xmlns:u=""urn:schemas-upnp-org:service:WANIPConnection:1""></u:GetExternalIPAddress>
 124 | </s:Body>
 125 | </s:Envelope>";
 126 | 
 127 |         using var http = new HttpClient { Timeout = DiscoverTimeout };
 128 |         var content = new StringContent(soap, Encoding.UTF8, "text/xml");
 129 |         content.Headers.Add("SOAPAction", "\"urn:schemas-upnp-org:service:WANIPConnection:1#GetExternalIPAddress\"");
 130 | 
 131 |         var response = await http.PostAsync(controlUrl, content, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 132 |         var body = await response.Content.ReadAsStringAsync(ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 133 | 
 134 |         var match = Regex.Match(body, @"<NewExternalIPAddress>([^<]+)</NewExternalIPAddress>");
 135 |         return match.Success ? match.Groups[1].Value.Trim() : null;
 136 |     }
 137 | 
 138 |     private static async Task<bool> AddPortMappingAsync(
 139 |         string controlUrl, int internalPort, int externalPort, string description, CancellationToken ct)
 140 |     {
 141 |         var localIp = GetLocalIp();
 142 |         var soap = $@"<?xml version=""1.0""?>
 143 | <s:Envelope xmlns:s=""http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/"" s:encodingStyle=""http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/encoding/"">
 144 | <s:Body>
 145 | <u:AddPortMapping xmlns:u=""urn:schemas-upnp-org:service:WANIPConnection:1"">
 146 | <NewRemoteHost></NewRemoteHost>
 147 | <NewExternalPort>{externalPort}</NewExternalPort>
 148 | <NewProtocol>TCP</NewProtocol>
 149 | <NewInternalPort>{internalPort}</NewInternalPort>
 150 | <NewInternalClient>{localIp}</NewInternalClient>
 151 | <NewEnabled>1</NewEnabled>
 152 | <NewPortMappingDescription>{description}</NewPortMappingDescription>
 153 | <NewLeaseDuration>86400</NewLeaseDuration>
 154 | </u:AddPortMapping>
 155 | </s:Body>
 156 | </s:Envelope>";
 157 | 
 158 |         using var http = new HttpClient { Timeout = DiscoverTimeout };
 159 |         var content = new StringContent(soap, Encoding.UTF8, "text/xml");
 160 |         content.Headers.Add("SOAPAction", "\"urn:schemas-upnp-org:service:WANIPConnection:1#AddPortMapping\"");
 161 | 
 162 |         var response = await http.PostAsync(controlUrl, content, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
 163 |         return response.IsSuccessStatusCode;
 164 |     }
 165 | 
 166 |     private static string GetLocalIp()
 167 |     {
 168 |         try
 169 |         {
 170 |             using var socket = new System.Net.Sockets.Socket(
 171 |                 System.Net.Sockets.AddressFamily.InterNetwork,
 172 |                 System.Net.Sockets.SocketType.Dgram,
 173 |                 System.Net.Sockets.ProtocolType.Udp);
 174 |             socket.Connect("8.8.8.8", 80);
 175 |             var endPoint = socket.LocalEndPoint as IPEndPoint;
 176 |             return endPoint?.Address.ToString() ?? "192.168.1.100";
 177 |         }
 178 |         catch { return "192.168.1.100"; }
 179 |     }
 180 | }
 181 | 
 182 | /// <summary>Result of a successful UPnP port forward.</summary>
 183 | public sealed record UpnpResult
 184 | {
 185 |     public required string PublicIp { get; init; }
 186 |     public required int ExternalPort { get; init; }
 187 |     public required string ControlUrl { get; init; }
 188 | 
 189 |     /// <summary>Public IP:port string for display.</summary>
 190 |     public string PublicAddress => $"{PublicIp}:{ExternalPort}";
 191 | }

--- src/MyGame.Core/Profile/Profile.cs (C#, 126 lines, ~1,346 tokens) ---
   1 | namespace MyGame.Core.Profile;
   2 | 
   3 | /// <summary>
   4 | /// The single local user profile. Replaces the TS <c>lib/auth.ts</c>
   5 | /// user/JWT/cookie system entirely — the desktop rewrite has NO auth, NO
   6 | /// tokens, NO cookies, NO multi-user. One profile per install, persisted
   7 | /// as <c>profile.json</c> in <see cref="ProfileStore.ProfileDirectory"/>.
   8 | ///
   9 | /// <para>
  10 | /// The profile carries only the identity the rest of the app needs: a
  11 | /// stable <see cref="Id"/> (so saves can record an <c>ownerId</c>), a
  12 | /// display <see cref="Nickname"/>, and the two timestamps for the
  13 | /// profile-management UI. There is no password, no email, no roles.
  14 | /// </para>
  15 | /// </summary>
  16 | public sealed record Profile
  17 | {
  18 |     /// <summary>
  19 |     /// Stable unique id for this profile. Generated once at first launch
  20 |     /// and never changed. Stored on every save as <c>ownerId</c> so a
  21 |     /// save always knows which profile created it (useful when the
  22 |     /// player exports a save to share with another install).
  23 |     /// </summary>
  24 |     public Guid Id { get; init; } = Guid.NewGuid();
  25 | 
  26 |     /// <summary>
  27 |     /// Display name shown in the UI and in multiplayer lobbies. Must
  28 |     /// pass <see cref="ValidateNickname"/> (2-20 chars; Latin or Cyrillic
  29 |     /// letters, digits, spaces, hyphens, underscores; must start/end with
  30 |     /// a letter or digit).
  31 |     /// </summary>
  32 |     public string Nickname { get; init; } = string.Empty;
  33 | 
  34 |     /// <summary>UTC timestamp when this profile was first created.</summary>
  35 |     public DateTimeOffset CreatedAt { get; init; } = DateTimeOffset.UtcNow;
  36 | 
  37 |     /// <summary>UTC timestamp of the last nickname change.</summary>
  38 |     public DateTimeOffset UpdatedAt { get; init; } = DateTimeOffset.UtcNow;
  39 | 
  40 |     // ─── Nickname validation ───────────────────────────────────────────
  41 | 
  42 |     /// <summary>
  43 |     /// Validate a candidate nickname. Port of <c>validateNickname</c>
  44 |     /// from <c>lib/auth.ts</c>. Rules:
  45 |     /// <list type="bullet">
  46 |     ///   <item>Length 2-20 chars (after trim).</item>
  47 |     ///   <item>First and last char must be a letter or digit
  48 |     ///         (Latin or Cyrillic).</item>
  49 |     ///   <item>Middle chars may be letter, digit, space, hyphen, or
  50 |     ///         underscore (Latin or Cyrillic letters).</item>
  51 |     /// </list>
  52 |     /// Returns true on success; on failure, <paramref name="error"/>
  53 |     /// carries a Russian-language message (kept verbatim from the TS
  54 |     /// source so existing UI strings keep matching).
  55 |     /// </summary>
  56 |     public static bool ValidateNickname(string nickname, out string error)
  57 |     {
  58 |         error = string.Empty;
  59 |         if (nickname is null)
  60 |         {
  61 |             error = "Ник не может быть пустым";
  62 |             return false;
  63 |         }
  64 | 
  65 |         var trimmed = nickname.Trim();
  66 |         if (trimmed.Length == 0)
  67 |         {
  68 |             error = "Ник не может быть пустым";
  69 |             return false;
  70 |         }
  71 |         if (trimmed.Length < 2)
  72 |         {
  73 |             error = "Минимум 2 символа";
  74 |             return false;
  75 |         }
  76 |         if (trimmed.Length > 20)
  77 |         {
  78 |             error = "Максимум 20 символов";
  79 |             return false;
  80 |         }
  81 |         if (!IsEdgeChar(trimmed[0]) || !IsEdgeChar(trimmed[^1]))
  82 |         {
  83 |             error = "Допустимы буквы, цифры, пробелы, дефисы и подчёркивания";
  84 |             return false;
  85 |         }
  86 |         for (int i = 1; i < trimmed.Length - 1; i++)
  87 |         {
  88 |             if (!IsMiddleChar(trimmed[i]))
  89 |             {
  90 |                 error = "Допустимы буквы, цифры, пробелы, дефисы и подчёркивания";
  91 |                 return false;
  92 |             }
  93 |         }
  94 |         return true;
  95 |     }
  96 | 
  97 |     /// <summary>
  98 |     /// Convenience wrapper that throws <see cref="ArgumentException"/> on
  99 |     /// invalid input. Use <see cref="ValidateNickname"/> for non-throwing
 100 |     /// checks (UI validation); use this when the caller already knows the
 101 |     /// input is valid.
 102 |     /// </summary>
 103 |     public static string NormalizeOrThrow(string nickname)
 104 |     {
 105 |         if (!ValidateNickname(nickname, out var error))
 106 |             throw new ArgumentException(error, nameof(nickname));
 107 |         return nickname.Trim();
 108 |     }
 109 | 
 110 |     private static bool IsEdgeChar(char c) =>
 111 |         IsLatinLetter(c) || IsDigit(c) || IsCyrillicLetter(c);
 112 | 
 113 |     private static bool IsMiddleChar(char c) =>
 114 |         IsEdgeChar(c) || c == ' ' || c == '_' || c == '-';
 115 | 
 116 |     private static bool IsLatinLetter(char c) =>
 117 |         (c >= 'a' && c <= 'z') || (c >= 'A' && c <= 'Z');
 118 | 
 119 |     private static bool IsDigit(char c) => c >= '0' && c <= '9';
 120 | 
 121 |     // Cyrillic block (U+0400..U+04FF) covers modern Russian + legacy
 122 |     // Cyrillic letters. Matches the TS regex range \u0400-\u04FF.
 123 |     private static bool IsCyrillicLetter(char c) =>
 124 |         c >= '\u0400' && c <= '\u04FF';
 125 | }

--- src/MyGame.Core/Profile/ProfileStore.cs (C#, 284 lines, ~2,980 tokens) ---
   1 | using System.IO;
   2 | using System.Text.Json;
   3 | using System.Text.Json.Serialization;
   4 | 
   5 | namespace MyGame.Core.Profile;
   6 | 
   7 | /// <summary>
   8 | /// Owns the single local <see cref="Profile"/>. Loads it from disk on
   9 | /// first access (or creates a new one with a random nickname and
  10 | /// persists it), caches it, and writes it back on rename.
  11 | ///
  12 | /// <para>
  13 | /// This class is the desktop replacement for the entire TS
  14 | /// <c>lib/auth.ts</c> subsystem. There is NO auth, NO JWT, NO cookies,
  15 | /// NO multi-user. One profile per install, end of story. The host of a
  16 | /// multiplayer session is identified by <see cref="Profile.Id"/>; clients
  17 | /// send their own profile id in the WebSocket handshake so the host can
  18 | /// attribute actions and show nicknames in the lobby.
  19 | /// </para>
  20 | /// </summary>
  21 | public sealed class ProfileStore
  22 | {
  23 |     // ─── On-disk layout ────────────────────────────────────────────────
  24 | 
  25 |     /// <summary>
  26 |     /// Default profile root directory:
  27 |     /// <c>%APPDATA%/MyGame</c> (Windows),
  28 |     /// <c>~/.config/MyGame</c> (Linux),
  29 |     /// <c>~/Library/Application Support/MyGame</c> (macOS).
  30 |     ///
  31 |     /// Falls back to <c>~/.mygame</c> when <c>SpecialFolder.ApplicationData</c>
  32 |     /// returns empty (no XDG env on a headless Linux box), and finally to
  33 |     /// a folder next to the executable when even the home dir is unknown.
  34 |     /// </summary>
  35 |     public static string DefaultProfileDirectory
  36 |     {
  37 |         get
  38 |         {
  39 |             var appData = Environment.GetFolderPath(Environment.SpecialFolder.ApplicationData);
  40 |             if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(appData))
  41 |                 return Path.Combine(appData, "MyGame");
  42 | 
  43 |             var home = Environment.GetFolderPath(Environment.SpecialFolder.UserProfile);
  44 |             if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(home))
  45 |                 return Path.Combine(home, ".mygame");
  46 | 
  47 |             return Path.Combine(AppContext.BaseDirectory, "MyGame");
  48 |         }
  49 |     }
  50 | 
  51 |     /// <summary>
  52 |     /// Profile root directory. Defaults to
  53 |     /// <see cref="DefaultProfileDirectory"/>; can be overridden in the
  54 |     /// ctor (for tests / portable mode).
  55 |     /// </summary>
  56 |     public string ProfileDirectory { get; }
  57 | 
  58 |     /// <summary>
  59 |     /// Path to the <c>profile.json</c> file — single source of truth for
  60 |     /// the local profile.
  61 |     /// </summary>
  62 |     public string ProfileFilePath => Path.Combine(ProfileDirectory, "profile.json");
  63 | 
  64 |     // ─── State ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  65 | 
  66 |     private Profile? _current;
  67 |     private readonly object _lock = new();
  68 | 
  69 |     /// <summary>
  70 |     /// Cached current profile. Null until <see cref="GetOrCreate"/> has
  71 |     /// been called at least once. Thread-safe (returns a snapshot; the
  72 |     /// caller can't mutate the cached instance).
  73 |     /// </summary>
  74 |     public Profile? Current
  75 |     {
  76 |         get
  77 |         {
  78 |             lock (_lock) return _current;
  79 |         }
  80 |     }
  81 | 
  82 |     // ─── Nickname generator ────────────────────────────────────────────
  83 | 
  84 |     /// <summary>
  85 |     /// Russian flavor prefixes for auto-generated nicknames. Ported
  86 |     /// verbatim from <c>lib/auth.ts</c>'s <c>NICKNAME_PREFIXES</c> array
  87 |     /// (14 entries). Picking uniformly at random gives each new install
  88 |     /// a friendly readable name without requiring the user to type one
  89 |     /// before they can play.
  90 |     /// </summary>
  91 |     private static readonly string[] NicknamePrefixes =
  92 |     {
  93 |         "Странник", "Бродяга", "Воин", "Маг", "Лучник", "Клерик", "Варвар",
  94 |         "Паладин", "Разбойник", "Друид", "Бард", "Монах", "Рейнджер", "Колдун",
  95 |     };
  96 | 
  97 |     /// <summary>
  98 |     /// Generate a fresh random nickname: <c>&lt;prefix&gt;-&lt;XXXX&gt;</c>
  99 |     /// where XXXX is a 4-char uppercase base36 suffix (matches the TS
 100 |     /// <c>generateRandomNickname</c> format byte-for-byte).
 101 |     /// </summary>
 102 |     public static string GenerateRandomNickname()
 103 |     {
 104 |         var prefix = NicknamePrefixes[Random.Shared.Next(NicknamePrefixes.Length)];
 105 |         var suffix = RandomBase36Upper(4);
 106 |         return $"{prefix}-{suffix}";
 107 |     }
 108 | 
 109 |     private const string Base36Alphabet = "0123456789ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ";
 110 | 
 111 |     private static string RandomBase36Upper(int length)
 112 |     {
 113 |         Span<char> buf = stackalloc char[length];
 114 |         for (int i = 0; i < length; i++)
 115 |             buf[i] = Base36Alphabet[Random.Shared.Next(36)];
 116 |         return new string(buf);
 117 |     }
 118 | 
 119 |     // ─── ctor ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 120 | 
 121 |     /// <param name="profileDirectory">
 122 |     /// Optional override for <see cref="ProfileDirectory"/>. Pass null
 123 |     /// (the default) to use <see cref="DefaultProfileDirectory"/>.
 124 |     /// </param>
 125 |     public ProfileStore(string? profileDirectory = null)
 126 |     {
 127 |         ProfileDirectory = profileDirectory ?? DefaultProfileDirectory;
 128 |     }
 129 | 
 130 |     // ─── Public API ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 131 | 
 132 |     /// <summary>
 133 |     /// Load the existing profile from <see cref="ProfileFilePath"/>, or
 134 |     /// create a new one with a random nickname and persist it. The
 135 |     /// result is cached and returned on subsequent calls. Thread-safe.
 136 |     /// </summary>
 137 |     /// <returns>
 138 |     /// The current profile. Never null (on a disk error falls back to
 139 |     /// an in-memory profile and logs to <c>Trace</c>).
 140 |     /// </returns>
 141 |     public Profile GetOrCreate()
 142 |     {
 143 |         lock (_lock)
 144 |         {
 145 |             if (_current is not null) return _current;
 146 | 
 147 |             var loaded = TryLoadFromDisk();
 148 |             if (loaded is not null)
 149 |             {
 150 |                 _current = loaded;
 151 |                 return loaded;
 152 |             }
 153 | 
 154 |             // First launch — mint a new profile with a random nickname.
 155 |             var created = new Profile
 156 |             {
 157 |                 Id = Guid.NewGuid(),
 158 |                 Nickname = GenerateRandomNickname(),
 159 |                 CreatedAt = DateTimeOffset.UtcNow,
 160 |                 UpdatedAt = DateTimeOffset.UtcNow,
 161 |             };
 162 |             TrySaveToDisk(created);
 163 |             _current = created;
 164 |             return created;
 165 |         }
 166 |     }
 167 | 
 168 |     /// <summary>
 169 |     /// Validate and apply a new nickname to the current profile. Persists
 170 |     /// the change immediately. Throws <see cref="InvalidOperationException"/>
 171 |     /// if <see cref="GetOrCreate"/> hasn't been called yet, and
 172 |     /// <see cref="ArgumentException"/> if the new nickname fails
 173 |     /// <see cref="Profile.ValidateNickname"/>.
 174 |     /// </summary>
 175 |     public Profile Rename(string newNickname)
 176 |     {
 177 |         if (!Profile.ValidateNickname(newNickname, out var error))
 178 |             throw new ArgumentException(error, nameof(newNickname));
 179 | 
 180 |         lock (_lock)
 181 |         {
 182 |             if (_current is null)
 183 |                 throw new InvalidOperationException(
 184 |                     "Profile not loaded. Call GetOrCreate() first.");
 185 | 
 186 |             var updated = _current with
 187 |             {
 188 |                 Nickname = newNickname.Trim(),
 189 |                 UpdatedAt = DateTimeOffset.UtcNow,
 190 |             };
 191 |             TrySaveToDisk(updated);
 192 |             _current = updated;
 193 |             return updated;
 194 |         }
 195 |     }
 196 | 
 197 |     /// <summary>
 198 |     /// Force a reload from disk on the next <see cref="GetOrCreate"/>
 199 |     /// call. Useful for tests; production code rarely needs this since
 200 |     /// the profile is single-writer.
 201 |     /// </summary>
 202 |     public void InvalidateCache()
 203 |     {
 204 |         lock (_lock) _current = null;
 205 |     }
 206 | 
 207 |     /// <summary>
 208 |     /// True when a <c>profile.json</c> file already exists on disk —
 209 |     /// i.e. this isn't the first launch. Used by the onboarding flow
 210 |     /// (issue #73) to decide whether to show the welcome wizard before
 211 |     /// the main menu. Safe to call before <see cref="GetOrCreate"/>
 212 |     /// (unlike <see cref="Current"/>, this method does NOT create the
 213 |     /// profile as a side effect).
 214 |     /// </summary>
 215 |     public bool ProfileExists() => File.Exists(ProfileFilePath);
 216 | 
 217 |     // ─── Disk I/O ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 218 | 
 219 |     private Profile? TryLoadFromDisk()
 220 |     {
 221 |         try
 222 |         {
 223 |             if (!File.Exists(ProfileFilePath)) return null;
 224 |             var json = File.ReadAllText(ProfileFilePath);
 225 |             return JsonSerializer.Deserialize<Profile>(json, JsonOptions);
 226 |         }
 227 |         catch (Exception ex)
 228 |         {
 229 |             System.Diagnostics.Trace.WriteLine(
 230 |                 $"[ProfileStore] Failed to load profile from {ProfileFilePath}: {ex.Message}");
 231 |             return null;
 232 |         }
 233 |     }
 234 | 
 235 |     private void TrySaveToDisk(Profile profile)
 236 |     {
 237 |         try
 238 |         {
 239 |             Directory.CreateDirectory(ProfileDirectory);
 240 |             AtomicWriteJson(ProfileFilePath, profile);
 241 |         }
 242 |         catch (Exception ex)
 243 |         {
 244 |             // Don't throw — the profile still exists in memory. The user
 245 |             // will see a "couldn't save profile" toast in the UI; the
 246 |             // next rename attempt will retry.
 247 |             System.Diagnostics.Trace.WriteLine(
 248 |                 $"[ProfileStore] Failed to save profile to {ProfileFilePath}: {ex.Message}");
 249 |         }
 250 |     }
 251 | 
 252 |     /// <summary>
 253 |     /// Write JSON atomically: serialize to <c>{path}.tmp</c>, then
 254 |     /// <see cref="File.Move(string, string, bool)"/> over the final path.
 255 |     /// A crash mid-write leaves the .tmp file (which is ignored on load)
 256 |     /// rather than a truncated final file.
 257 |     /// </summary>
 258 |     internal static void AtomicWriteJson(string path, object value)
 259 |     {
 260 |         var tmp = path + ".tmp";
 261 |         var json = JsonSerializer.Serialize(value, JsonOptions);
 262 |         File.WriteAllText(tmp, json);
 263 |         File.Move(tmp, path, overwrite: true);
 264 |     }
 265 | 
 266 |     /// <summary>
 267 |     /// Shared JSON options for the Profile layer: camelCase wire format
 268 |     /// (consistent with the World layer), indented for human-readability,
 269 |     /// case-insensitive deserialization, null fields omitted on write.
 270 |     /// Uses <see cref="System.Text.Encodings.Web.JavaScriptEncoder.
 271 |     /// UnsafeRelaxedJsonEscaping"/> so Cyrillic (and other non-ASCII)
 272 |     /// is written as raw UTF-8 rather than <c>\uXXXX</c> escapes —
 273 |     /// keeps profile.json / settings.json readable in any text editor.
 274 |     /// </summary>
 275 |     internal static JsonSerializerOptions JsonOptions { get; } = new()
 276 |     {
 277 |         PropertyNamingPolicy = JsonNamingPolicy.CamelCase,
 278 |         PropertyNameCaseInsensitive = true,
 279 |         WriteIndented = true,
 280 |         DefaultIgnoreCondition = JsonIgnoreCondition.WhenWritingNull,
 281 |         Encoder = System.Text.Encodings.Web.JavaScriptEncoder.UnsafeRelaxedJsonEscaping,
 282 |     };
 283 | }

   1 | using MyGame.Core.AI;
   2 | 
   3 | namespace MyGame.Core.Profile;
   4 | 
   5 | /// <summary>
   6 | /// User-configurable application settings, persisted as
   7 | /// <c>settings.json</c> next to <c>profile.json</c> in the profile
   8 | /// directory. Combines the AI connection settings (the only thing the TS
   9 | /// source's <c>lib/settings.ts</c> persisted) with the desktop-specific
  10 | /// tunables the rewrite needs (last server host/port, autosave cadence,
  11 | /// UI preferences, etc.).
  12 | /// </summary>
  13 | /// <remarks>
  14 | /// <b>Why a single record:</b> the TS app split this into several stores
  15 | /// (AI settings in <c>lib/settings.ts</c>, server config in
  16 | /// <c>lib/server-config.ts</c>, session in <c>lib/session-persistence.ts</c>)
  17 | /// because each had a different persistence mechanism (localStorage /
  18 | /// env / API call). The desktop rewrite has ONE persistence mechanism
  19 | /// (a JSON file in AppData), so collapsing them into a single record is
  20 | /// simpler and atomic to write.
  21 | /// </remarks>
  22 | public sealed record Settings
  23 | {
  24 |     // ─── AI connection ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
  25 | 
  26 |     /// <summary>
  27 |     /// OpenAI-compatible endpoint settings (base URL, API key, model,
  28 |     /// temperature, max tokens). Drives every
  29 |     /// <see cref="AiClient.ChatAsync"/> call.
  30 |     /// </summary>
  31 |     public AiSettings Ai { get; init; } = new();
  32 | 
  33 |     /// <summary>
  34 |     /// Hard cap on tool-call iterations per GM turn. Stops a misbehaving
  35 |     /// model from looping spawn-NPC forever. Default 8 (matches the TS
  36 |     /// <c>maxIterations</c> default).
  37 |     /// </summary>
  38 |     public int MaxToolIterations { get; init; } = 8;
  39 | 
  40 |     /// <summary>
  41 |     /// Estimated-token threshold above which the GameMaster triggers
  42 |     /// early summarization of the conversation history (issue #25). When
  43 |     /// the rough token count of (system prompt + summary + history + world
  44 |     /// state) exceeds 80% of this value, the oldest half of the history
  45 |     /// is sent to the summarizer and replaced with a short text recap.
  46 |     ///
  47 |     /// <para>
  48 |     /// Default 12000 — conservative for the typical 16k-context model
  49 |     /// class (gpt-4o-mini, deepseek-chat, llama3.1:8b). Larger-context
  50 |     /// models (32k, 128k) can safely set this higher; smaller-context
  51 |     /// models (8k) should set it lower. The 80% margin leaves room for
  52 |     /// the model's response + tool-call rounds before hitting the
  53 |     /// provider's hard limit.
  54 |     /// </para>
  55 |     /// </summary>
  56 |     public int MaxContextTokens { get; init; } = 12000;
  57 | 
  58 |     // ─── Multiplayer ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
  59 | 
  60 |     /// <summary>
  61 |     /// Last server host the user connected to (for the "Recent servers"
  62 |     /// dropdown in the multiplayer menu). Null if the user has never
  63 |     /// joined a remote host.
  64 |     /// </summary>
  65 |     public string? LastServerHost { get; init; }
  66 | 
  67 |     /// <summary>
  68 |     /// Last server port. Null if the user has never joined a remote host.
  69 |     /// When hosting locally, this is the port the in-process WebSocket
  70 |     /// listener bound to last time.
  71 |     /// </summary>
  72 |     public int? LastServerPort { get; init; }
  73 | 
  74 |     // ─── Gameplay / engine tunables ────────────────────────────────────
  75 | 
  76 |     /// <summary>
  77 |     /// Autosave cadence in seconds. 0 = disabled (user must save
  78 |     /// manually). Default 120 (every 2 minutes) — matches the TS app's
  79 |     /// autosave behavior.
  80 |     /// </summary>
  81 |     public int AutosaveIntervalSeconds { get; init; } = 120;
  82 | 
  83 |     /// <summary>
  84 |     /// Whether narrative events stream into the UI as they're generated
  85 |     /// (true) or only after the GM turn completes (false). Streaming
  86 |     /// gives better UX but uses slightly more CPU.
  87 |     /// </summary>
  88 |     public bool StreamNarrative { get; init; } = true;
  89 | 
  90 |     /// <summary>
  91 |     /// UI language code (<c>"ru"</c>, <c>"en"</c>, …). Defaults to
  92 |     /// <c>"ru"</c> — the TS source is Russian-first.
  93 |     /// </summary>
  94 |     public string Language { get; init; } = "ru";
  95 | 
  96 |     // ─── Audio / accessibility ─────────────────────────────────────────
  97 | 
  98 |     /// <summary>Master volume 0..100. 0 = muted.</summary>
  99 |     public int MasterVolume { get; init; } = 80;
 100 | 
 101 |     /// <summary>Whether sound effects are enabled at all.</summary>
 102 |     public bool SoundEnabled { get; init; } = true;
 103 | 
 104 |     /// <summary>
 105 |     /// Whether the UI animates transitions. Disable for users on slow
 106 |     /// machines or with motion sensitivity.
 107 |     /// </summary>
 108 |     public bool EnableAnimations { get; init; } = true;
 109 | 
 110 |     // ─── Appearance / theme (issue #47) ──────────────────────────────
 111 | 
 112 |     /// <summary>
 113 |     /// UI theme mode. One of <c>"Dark"</c> (default, matches the original
 114 |     /// Pathstone look), <c>"Light"</c>, or <c>"System"</c> (follows the OS
 115 |     /// preference at startup; we don't react to OS theme changes mid-session).
 116 |     /// Applied at app startup by <c>ThemeService.ApplyTheme</c> and on
 117 |     /// every SettingsStore.Changed event.
 118 |     /// </summary>
 119 |     public string ThemeMode { get; init; } = "Dark";
