 122 |     /// Accent color preset name. One of <c>"Indigo"</c> (default),
 123 |     /// <c>"Emerald"</c>, <c>"Amber"</c>, <c>"Rose"</c>, <c>"Cyan"</c>,
 124 |     /// <c>"Violet"</c>. ThemeService resolves the name to a hex color and
 125 |     /// writes it into the AppAccent / AppAccentFg application resources.
 126 |     /// </summary>
 127 |     public string AccentColor { get; init; } = "Indigo";
 128 | 
 129 |     // ─── Multiplayer (issue #78) ─────────────────────────────────────
 130 | 
 131 |     /// <summary>
 132 |     /// Whether the multiplayer client should auto-reconnect on an
 133 |     /// unexpected WebSocket drop (vs. surfacing the disconnect overlay
 134 |     /// and waiting for the user to click «Переподключиться»). Default
 135 |     /// false — explicit reconnect keeps the user in control.
 136 |     /// </summary>
 137 |     public bool AutoReconnect { get; init; } = false;
 138 | 
 139 |     // ─── Onboarding / tutorial (issue #73) ────────────────────────────
 140 | 
 141 |     /// <summary>
 142 |     /// Number of times the user has visited the main menu. Used to
 143 |     /// decide whether to show the first-time tooltip hints under the
 144 |     /// menu buttons (Новая игра / Создать мир / Хост игры). The hints
 145 |     /// are shown for the first three sessions (<c>SessionCount &lt; 3</c>)
 146 |     /// and hidden afterwards. Defaults to 0 — old settings.json files
 147 |     /// (written before this field existed) load with 0, which correctly
 148 |     /// triggers the hints for users who haven't seen them yet.
 149 |     /// </summary>
 150 |     /// <remarks>
 151 |     /// Incremented once per main-menu visit by
 152 |     /// <see cref="MyGame.Desktop.ViewModels.MainMenuViewModel"/> on
 153 |     /// construction. Never reset (a future "show hints again" affordance
 154 |     /// could reset it to 0).
 155 |     /// </remarks>
 156 |     public int SessionCount { get; init; } = 0;
 157 | 
 158 |     // ─── Saves (issue #80) ───────────────────────────────────────────
 159 | 
 160 |     /// <summary>
 161 |     /// Whether <see cref="MyGame.Core.Saves.SaveManager.SaveAll"/>
 162 |     /// gzip-compresses <c>world.json</c> / <c>log.json</c> /
 163 |     /// <c>state.json</c> on write (issue #80). <c>meta.json</c> stays
 164 |     /// uncompressed (it's small and ListSaves reads it on every
 165 |     /// save-list refresh). Default <c>true</c> — text-heavy world
 166 |     /// saves typically shrink 5-10× with gzip. Set to <c>false</c> for
 167 |     /// debugging (the save files become human-readable JSON).
 168 |     ///
 169 |     /// <para>
 170 |     /// <b>Backward compatibility:</b> reads always auto-detect
 171 |     /// compression (the SaveManager checks for <c>{file}.gz</c> first,
 172 |     /// then falls back to <c>{file}</c>), so a save written compressed
 173 |     /// loads fine when this is false, and vice versa. Toggling this
 174 |     /// between sessions is safe — the next <c>SaveAll</c> writes the
 175 |     /// new variant and deletes the stale one.
 176 |     /// </para>
 177 |     /// <para>
 178 |     /// The Desktop layer's <c>ServiceHost</c> reads this on startup and
 179 |     /// pushes it to the <c>SaveManager</c>'s
 180 |     /// <see cref="MyGame.Core.Saves.SaveManager.CompressSaves"/>
 181 |     /// property (which defaults to <c>false</c> so unit tests using a
 182 |     /// bare <c>SaveManager</c> keep writing plain JSON). A
 183 |     /// <c>SettingsStore.Changed</c> subscription keeps the property in
 184 |     /// sync if the user toggles the setting mid-session.
 185 |     /// </para>
 186 |     /// </summary>
 187 |     public bool CompressSaves { get; init; } = true;
 188 | }

--- src/MyGame.Core/Profile/SettingsStore.cs (C#, 141 lines, ~1,371 tokens) ---
   1 | using System.IO;
   2 | using System.Text.Json;
   3 | 
   4 | namespace MyGame.Core.Profile;
   5 | 
   6 | /// <summary>
   7 | /// Loads and saves <see cref="Settings"/> to
   8 | /// <c>{ProfileDirectory}/settings.json</c>. Notifies subscribers via
   9 | /// <see cref="Changed"/> when settings are written (so the UI / AI client
  10 | /// can re-read without polling).
  11 | /// </summary>
  12 | public sealed class SettingsStore
  13 | {
  14 |     private readonly string _settingsFilePath;
  15 |     private readonly object _lock = new();
  16 |     private Settings? _cached;
  17 | 
  18 |     /// <param name="profileDirectory">
  19 |     /// Directory containing <c>settings.json</c>. Defaults to
  20 |     /// <see cref="ProfileStore.DefaultProfileDirectory"/>.
  21 |     /// </param>
  22 |     public SettingsStore(string? profileDirectory = null)
  23 |     {
  24 |         var dir = profileDirectory ?? ProfileStore.DefaultProfileDirectory;
  25 |         _settingsFilePath = Path.Combine(dir, "settings.json");
  26 |     }
  27 | 
  28 |     /// <summary>Path to the settings file.</summary>
  29 |     public string SettingsFilePath => _settingsFilePath;
  30 | 
  31 |     /// <summary>
  32 |     /// Raised whenever <see cref="Save"/> writes a new settings object
  33 |     /// to disk. Subscribers receive the just-saved instance. Handlers
  34 |     /// are invoked on the calling thread; exceptions are caught and
  35 |     /// logged to <c>Trace</c> so a misbehaving handler can't break
  36 |     /// the save path.
  37 |     /// </summary>
  38 |     public event EventHandler<Settings>? Changed;
  39 | 
  40 |     /// <summary>
  41 |     /// Load settings from disk. Returns a <see cref="Settings"/> with
  42 |     /// defaults if the file doesn't exist or is corrupt (the corrupt
  43 |     /// file is left in place — the next <see cref="Save"/> will overwrite
  44 |     /// it with a known-good copy).
  45 |     /// </summary>
  46 |     public Settings Load()
  47 |     {
  48 |         lock (_lock)
  49 |         {
  50 |             if (_cached is not null) return _cached;
  51 | 
  52 |             try
  53 |             {
  54 |                 if (File.Exists(_settingsFilePath))
  55 |                 {
  56 |                     var json = File.ReadAllText(_settingsFilePath);
  57 |                     var loaded = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<Settings>(json, ProfileStore.JsonOptions);
  58 |                     if (loaded is not null)
  59 |                     {
  60 |                         _cached = loaded;
  61 |                         return loaded;
  62 |                     }
  63 |                 }
  64 |             }
  65 |             catch (Exception ex)
  66 |             {
  67 |                 System.Diagnostics.Trace.WriteLine(
  68 |                     $"[SettingsStore] Failed to load settings from {_settingsFilePath}: {ex.Message}");
  69 |             }
  70 | 
  71 |             // First launch OR corrupt file — fall back to defaults.
  72 |             _cached = new Settings();
  73 |             return _cached;
  74 |         }
  75 |     }
  76 | 
  77 |     /// <summary>
  78 |     /// Persist <paramref name="settings"/> to disk atomically (write to
  79 |     /// <c>{path}.tmp</c> then move), update the cached value, and raise
  80 |     /// <see cref="Changed"/>. Swallows disk errors (logged to
  81 |     /// <c>Trace</c>) — settings are also kept in-memory so the running
  82 |     /// session continues to work; the user sees a toast in the UI.
  83 |     /// </summary>
  84 |     public void Save(Settings settings)
  85 |     {
  86 |         if (settings is null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(settings));
  87 | 
  88 |         lock (_lock)
  89 |         {
  90 |             _cached = settings;
  91 |         }
  92 | 
  93 |         try
  94 |         {
  95 |             var dir = Path.GetDirectoryName(_settingsFilePath);
  96 |             if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(dir))
  97 |                 Directory.CreateDirectory(dir);
  98 |             ProfileStore.AtomicWriteJson(_settingsFilePath, settings);
  99 |         }
 100 |         catch (Exception ex)
 101 |         {
 102 |             System.Diagnostics.Trace.WriteLine(
 103 |                 $"[SettingsStore] Failed to save settings to {_settingsFilePath}: {ex.Message}");
 104 |         }
 105 | 
 106 |         // Raise outside the lock so handlers can call back into Load()
 107 |         // without deadlocking.
 108 |         RaiseChanged(settings);
 109 |     }
 110 | 
 111 |     /// <summary>
 112 |     /// Convenience: apply a patch to the current settings and save.
 113 |     /// Equivalent to <c>Save(Load() with { ...patch })</c> but reuses
 114 |     /// the cached instance for fewer allocations.
 115 |     /// </summary>
 116 |     public Settings Update(Func<Settings, Settings> patch)
 117 |     {
 118 |         if (patch is null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(patch));
 119 |         var current = Load();
 120 |         var next = patch(current);
 121 |         Save(next);
 122 |         return next;
 123 |     }
 124 | 
 125 |     private void RaiseChanged(Settings settings)
 126 |     {
 127 |         var handlers = Changed;
 128 |         if (handlers is null) return;
 129 |         foreach (EventHandler<Settings>? h in handlers.GetInvocationList())
 130 |         {
 131 |             if (h is null) continue;
 132 |             try { h(this, settings); }
 133 |             catch (Exception ex)
 134 |             {
 135 |                 System.Diagnostics.Trace.WriteLine(
 136 |                     $"[SettingsStore] Settings.Changed handler threw: {ex.Message}");
 137 |             }
 138 |         }
 139 |     }
 140 | }

--- src/MyGame.Core/Rules/Check.cs (C#, 93 lines, ~810 tokens) ---
   1 | using MyGame.Core.Common;
   2 | 
   3 | namespace MyGame.Core.Rules;
   4 | 
   5 | /// <summary>
   6 | /// Standard difficulty classes for a d20 check, modelled on D&amp;D 5e's
   7 | /// table. Use as the <c>dc</c> argument to <see cref="Check.RollCheck"/> via
   8 | /// the enum overload.
   9 | /// </summary>
  10 | public enum DifficultyClass
  11 | {
  12 |     /// <summary>Very easy task.</summary>
  13 |     Easy = 5,
  14 | 
  15 |     /// <summary>Moderate task.</summary>
  16 |     Medium = 10,
  17 | 
  18 |     /// <summary>Tough task.</summary>
  19 |     Hard = 15,
  20 | 
  21 |     /// <summary>Very tough task.</summary>
  22 |     VeryHard = 20,
  23 | 
  24 |     /// <summary>All but impossible.</summary>
  25 |     NearlyImpossible = 25,
  26 | }
  27 | 
  28 | /// <summary>
  29 | /// Outcome of a d20 check resolution.
  30 | ///
  31 | /// Port of the relevant slice of <c>engine/rules/check.ts</c>. The TS
  32 | /// <c>CheckResult</c> was a big multi-mechanic struct (d20, d100, 3d6, PbtA,
  33 | /// d6pool, ...). The C# port (per the task spec) keeps only the d20 mechanic
  34 | /// — the desktop rewrite is d20-only at this layer — but uses the same field
  35 | /// names where applicable so a future port of the other mechanics slots in
  36 | /// cleanly.
  37 | ///
  38 | /// Crit rules: a natural 20 is always a <see cref="CriticalSuccess"/> (and
  39 | /// counts as <see cref="Success"/>); a natural 1 is always a
  40 | /// <see cref="CriticalFailure"/> (and never <see cref="Success"/>); any other
  41 | /// roll is a success iff <see cref="Total"/> &gt;= DC.
  42 | /// </summary>
  43 | public readonly record struct CheckResult(
  44 |     int Roll,
  45 |     int Modifier,
  46 |     int Total,
  47 |     bool Success,
  48 |     bool CriticalSuccess,
  49 |     bool CriticalFailure);
  50 | 
  51 | /// <summary>
  52 | /// d20 skill/check resolver.
  53 | /// </summary>
  54 | public static class Check
  55 | {
  56 |     /// <summary>
  57 |     /// Resolve a d20 check against <paramref name="dc"/>.
  58 |     /// Roll = natural d20 result (1..20). Modifier is added to produce
  59 |     /// Total. Crit success on natural 20, crit failure on natural 1, else
  60 |     /// success iff Total &gt;= dc.
  61 |     /// </summary>
  62 |     public static CheckResult RollCheck(Rng rng, int modifier, int dc)
  63 |     {
  64 |         if (rng is null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(rng));
  65 | 
  66 |         int roll = D20.Roll(rng, 20);
  67 |         int total = roll + modifier;
  68 | 
  69 |         bool critSuccess = roll == 20;
  70 |         bool critFailure = roll == 1;
  71 | 
  72 |         bool success;
  73 |         if (critSuccess) success = true;
  74 |         else if (critFailure) success = false;
  75 |         else success = total >= dc;
  76 | 
  77 |         return new CheckResult(
  78 |             Roll: roll,
  79 |             Modifier: modifier,
  80 |             Total: total,
  81 |             Success: success,
  82 |             CriticalSuccess: critSuccess,
  83 |             CriticalFailure: critFailure);
  84 |     }
  85 | 
  86 |     /// <summary>
  87 |     /// Convenience overload taking a <see cref="DifficultyClass"/> enum
  88 |     /// instead of a raw int DC.
  89 |     /// </summary>
  90 |     public static CheckResult RollCheck(Rng rng, int modifier, DifficultyClass dc)
  91 |         => RollCheck(rng, modifier, (int)dc);
  92 | }

--- src/MyGame.Core/Rules/D20.cs (C#, 102 lines, ~1,008 tokens) ---
   1 | using MyGame.Core.Common;
   2 | 
   3 | namespace MyGame.Core.Rules;
   4 | 
   5 | /// <summary>
   6 | /// Dice primitives shared across ALL rulesets.
   7 | ///
   8 | /// Port of <c>engine/rules/d20.ts</c>. The TS module kept only the universal
   9 | /// pieces (flat dice rolling, advantage/disadvantage, stat roll); this C#
  10 | /// port preserves that scope and the exact roll semantics:
  11 | ///  - <see cref="Roll(Rng, int)"/> returns 1..<paramref name="sides"/>
  12 | ///    inclusive, matching <c>rng.die(sides)</c>.
  13 | ///  - <see cref="RollStat(Rng)"/> does 4d6-drop-lowest summing 3, matching
  14 | ///    the canonical D&amp;D ability-score generation.
  15 | ///  - <see cref="Advantage(Rng, int)"/> / <see cref="Disadvantage(Rng, int)"/>
  16 | ///    roll twice and take max / min respectively.
  17 | ///
  18 | /// All randomness goes through the injected <see cref="Rng"/> so rolls are
  19 | /// reproducible from a save's seed.
  20 | /// </summary>
  21 | public static class D20
  22 | {
  23 |     /// <summary>
  24 |     /// Roll a single die with <paramref name="sides"/> faces. Returns
  25 |     /// 1..<paramref name="sides"/> inclusive; returns 0 if
  26 |     /// <paramref name="sides"/> &lt; 1 (matches TS <c>rng.die</c>).
  27 |     /// </summary>
  28 |     public static int Roll(Rng rng, int sides = 20)
  29 |     {
  30 |         if (rng is null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(rng));
  31 |         if (sides < 1) return 0;
  32 |         // NextInt(min, max) is exclusive of max, so +1 to make it inclusive.
  33 |         return rng.NextInt(1, sides + 1);
  34 |     }
  35 | 
  36 |     /// <summary>
  37 |     /// Roll a single die and add <paramref name="modifier"/>.
  38 |     /// Equivalent to <c>Roll(rng, sides) + modifier</c>.
  39 |     /// </summary>
  40 |     public static int RollWithModifier(Rng rng, int sides, int modifier)
  41 |     {
  42 |         return Roll(rng, sides) + modifier;
  43 |     }
  44 | 
  45 |     /// <summary>
  46 |     /// Generate an ability score: roll 4d6, drop the lowest, sum the
  47 |     /// remaining 3. Result is in [3, 18].
  48 |     /// </summary>
  49 |     public static int RollStat(Rng rng)
  50 |     {
  51 |         if (rng is null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(rng));
  52 |         Span<int> rolls = stackalloc int[4];
  53 |         for (int i = 0; i < 4; i++)
  54 |             rolls[i] = rng.NextInt(1, 7); // 1..6 inclusive
  55 | 
  56 |         // Sort ascending, sum the top three (drop rolls[0]).
  57 |         SortAscending(rolls);
  58 |         return rolls[1] + rolls[2] + rolls[3];
  59 |     }
  60 | 
  61 |     /// <summary>
  62 |     /// Roll twice, take the higher. Matches D&amp;D 5e advantage.
  63 |     /// </summary>
  64 |     public static int Advantage(Rng rng, int sides = 20)
  65 |     {
  66 |         if (rng is null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(rng));
  67 |         if (sides < 1) return 0;
  68 |         int a = rng.NextInt(1, sides + 1);
  69 |         int b = rng.NextInt(1, sides + 1);
  70 |         return Math.Max(a, b);
  71 |     }
  72 | 
  73 |     /// <summary>
  74 |     /// Roll twice, take the lower. Matches D&amp;D 5e disadvantage.
  75 |     /// </summary>
  76 |     public static int Disadvantage(Rng rng, int sides = 20)
  77 |     {
  78 |         if (rng is null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(rng));
  79 |         if (sides < 1) return 0;
  80 |         int a = rng.NextInt(1, sides + 1);
  81 |         int b = rng.NextInt(1, sides + 1);
  82 |         return Math.Min(a, b);
  83 |     }
  84 | 
  85 |     // Tiny insertion sort for a length-4 span. Avoids allocating an array or
  86 |     // pulling in Array.Sort for a hot path.
  87 |     private static void SortAscending(Span<int> values)
  88 |     {
  89 |         for (int i = 1; i < values.Length; i++)
  90 |         {
  91 |             int v = values[i];
  92 |             int j = i - 1;
  93 |             while (j >= 0 && values[j] > v)
  94 |             {
  95 |                 values[j + 1] = values[j];
  96 |                 j--;
  97 |             }
  98 |             values[j + 1] = v;
  99 |         }
 100 |     }
 101 | }

--- src/MyGame.Core/Saves/CharacterSheet.cs (C#, 109 lines, ~1,330 tokens) ---
   1 | using MyGame.Core.Common;
   2 | 
   3 | namespace MyGame.Core.Saves;
   4 | 
   5 | /// <summary>
   6 | /// A standalone, exportable character sheet — the BG3-style "your
   7 | /// character travels with you between hosts" feature. NOT tied to a
   8 | /// World: it's a self-contained snapshot of one player character that
   9 | /// can be exported from a save, sent to another install, and imported
  10 | /// into a new game there.
  11 | /// </summary>
  12 | /// <remarks>
  13 | /// <b>Design choice — record vs. class:</b> a CharacterSheet is an
  14 | /// immutable snapshot (frozen at export time). It doesn't track live
  15 | /// HP changes — those happen on the World's Player entity, not on the
  16 | /// sheet. The sheet is just the "starting build" of the character:
  17 | /// race, class, attributes, level, inventory, equipment. When the sheet
  18 | /// is imported into a new world, the world's <c>SpawnPlayer</c> flow
  19 | /// materializes a live Player from it.
  20 | /// </remarks>
  21 | public sealed record CharacterSheet
  22 | {
  23 |     /// <summary>
  24 |     /// Stable character id (Guid, formatted as <c>char_{Guid:N}</c> on
  25 |     /// disk — see <see cref="CharacterSheetStore"/>). Generated at
  26 |     /// export time; reused if the same player is re-exported from the
  27 |     /// same source save (so a re-export overwrites rather than
  28 |     /// duplicating).
  29 |     /// </summary>
  30 |     public Guid Id { get; init; } = Guid.NewGuid();
  31 | 
  32 |     /// <summary>Display name (matches the source player's Name).</summary>
  33 |     public string Name { get; init; } = string.Empty;
  34 | 
  35 |     /// <summary>Optional flavor label (race / species / people).</summary>
  36 |     public string? Race { get; init; }
  37 | 
  38 |     /// <summary>Optional flavor label (class / archetype / role).</summary>
  39 |     public string? Class { get; init; }
  40 | 
  41 |     /// <summary>Player-authored background text.</summary>
  42 |     public string? Background { get; init; }
  43 | 
  44 |     /// <summary>
  45 |     /// Attribute values keyed by ruleset attribute key
  46 |     /// (<c>str</c>, <c>dex</c>, <c>con</c>, …). Frozen at export time —
  47 |     /// doesn't include status-effect modifiers.
  48 |     /// </summary>
  49 |     public Dictionary<string, int> Attributes { get; init; } = new();
  50 | 
  51 |     /// <summary>
  52 |     /// Resource pools (hp, mana, …) keyed by ruleset resource key. Frozen
  53 |     /// at the exported values — when imported into a new world, the
  54 |     /// engine recomputes maxima from the ruleset's formulas.
  55 |     /// </summary>
  56 |     public Dictionary<string, int> Resources { get; init; } = new();
  57 | 
  58 |     /// <summary>Character level (ruleset-driven progression).</summary>
  59 |     public int Level { get; init; } = 1;
  60 | 
  61 |     /// <summary>XP total (ruleset-driven progression).</summary>
  62 |     public int Xp { get; init; }
  63 | 
  64 |     /// <summary>
  65 |     /// Item template ids in the character's inventory at export time.
  66 |     /// Stored as template ids (not full item instances) so the import
  67 |     /// path can re-materialize fresh instances from the destination
  68 |     /// world's content registry.
  69 |     /// </summary>
  70 |     public IReadOnlyList<string> InventoryItemIds { get; init; } = Array.Empty<string>();
  71 | 
  72 |     /// <summary>
  73 |     /// Equipment slots → item template ids (e.g.
  74 |     /// <c>{ "weapon": "wpn_shortsword", "armor": "arm_leather" }</c>).
  75 |     /// </summary>
  76 |     public IReadOnlyDictionary<string, string> EquippedItemIds { get; init; }
  77 |         = new Dictionary<string, string>();
  78 | 
  79 |     /// <summary>Proficient skill labels (free-form).</summary>
  80 |     public IReadOnlyList<string> ProficientSkills { get; init; } = Array.Empty<string>();
  81 | 
  82 |     /// <summary>Movement speed (ruleset-defined; D&amp;D default is 30 ft).</summary>
  83 |     public int? Speed { get; init; }
  84 | 
  85 |     /// <summary>
  86 |     /// Id of the save this sheet was exported from. Null if the sheet
  87 |     /// was created from scratch (e.g. the import-side "new character"
  88 |     /// flow that starts from a sheet template). Useful for the UI to
  89 |     /// show "Exported from: &lt;save name&gt;" and for the import path
  90 |     /// to detect "are we re-importing into the same save?".
  91 |     /// </summary>
  92 |     public string? SourceSaveId { get; init; }
  93 | 
  94 |     /// <summary>
  95 |     /// EntityId (as string) of the player entity this sheet was
  96 |     /// exported from. Stored as a string rather than
  97 |     /// <see cref="EntityId"/> so the sheet JSON is portable across
  98 |     /// installs that might not have the EntityId converter loaded (e.g.
  99 |     /// a future web-based viewer).
 100 |     /// </summary>
 101 |     public string? SourcePlayerId { get; init; }
 102 | 
 103 |     /// <summary>UTC timestamp when this sheet was exported.</summary>
 104 |     public DateTimeOffset CreatedAt { get; init; } = DateTimeOffset.UtcNow;
 105 | 
 106 |     /// <summary>UTC timestamp of the last re-export (same player, same source).</summary>
 107 |     public DateTimeOffset UpdatedAt { get; init; } = DateTimeOffset.UtcNow;
 108 | }

--- src/MyGame.Core/Saves/CharacterSheetStore.cs (C#, 280 lines, ~3,004 tokens) ---
   1 | using System.IO;
   2 | using System.Text.Json;
   3 | using MyGame.Core.Common;
   4 | using MyGame.Core.Profile;
   5 | using MyGame.Core.World;
   6 | using MyGame.Core.World.Entities;
   7 | 
   8 | namespace MyGame.Core.Saves;
   9 | 
  10 | /// <summary>
  11 | /// Manage standalone <see cref="CharacterSheet"/> exports under
  12 | /// <c>{ProfileDirectory}/characters/</c>. Each sheet is a single JSON
  13 | /// file (<c>char_{Guid:N}.json</c>) — not a directory — because a
  14 | /// character sheet is much smaller than a full save and doesn't benefit
  15 | /// from the split-file layout.
  16 | /// </summary>
  17 | /// <remarks>
  18 | /// <b>Use case:</b> the BG3-style "your character travels with you
  19 | /// between hosts" feature. A player exports their character to a sheet,
  20 | /// sends the file to a friend, the friend imports it into their install
  21 | /// — the friend's world's <c>SpawnPlayer</c> flow materializes a live
  22 | /// Player from the sheet.
  23 | /// </remarks>
  24 | public sealed class CharacterSheetStore
  25 | {
  26 |     private readonly SaveManager _saveManager;
  27 |     private readonly string _charactersDirectory;
  28 | 
  29 |     /// <param name="saveManager">
  30 |     /// Used by <see cref="Export"/> to load the source save's World.
  31 |     /// </param>
  32 |     /// <param name="charactersDirectory">
  33 |     /// Optional override for the characters directory. Defaults to
  34 |     /// <c>{ProfileStore.DefaultProfileDirectory}/characters</c>.
  35 |     /// </param>
  36 |     public CharacterSheetStore(SaveManager saveManager, string? charactersDirectory = null)
  37 |     {
  38 |         _saveManager = saveManager ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(saveManager));
  39 |         _charactersDirectory = charactersDirectory
  40 |             ?? Path.Combine(ProfileStore.DefaultProfileDirectory, "characters");
  41 |     }
  42 | 
  43 |     /// <summary>Directory containing all character-sheet JSON files.</summary>
  44 |     public string CharactersDirectory => _charactersDirectory;
  45 | 
  46 |     /// <summary>
  47 |     /// Export a player from a save into a standalone
  48 |     /// <see cref="CharacterSheet"/>. Loads the save's World, finds the
  49 |     /// player by id, snapshots it, and writes the sheet to
  50 |     /// <c>{CharactersDirectory}/{charId}.json</c> atomically. Returns
  51 |     /// null if the save doesn't exist, the player doesn't exist in the
  52 |     /// save, or the write fails.
  53 |     /// </summary>
  54 |     /// <remarks>
  55 |     /// <b>Idempotency:</b> if a sheet for the same
  56 |     /// (<paramref name="saveId"/>, <paramref name="playerId"/>) pair
  57 |     /// already exists, this OVERWRITES it (refreshes the snapshot).
  58 |     /// We key sheets by <c>(sourceSaveId, sourcePlayerId)</c> rather
  59 |     /// than minting a fresh Guid each time, so a re-export doesn't
  60 |     /// duplicate. The first export mints the Guid; subsequent exports
  61 |     /// reuse it.
  62 |     /// </remarks>
  63 |     public CharacterSheet? Export(string saveId, EntityId playerId)
  64 |     {
  65 |         var world = _saveManager.LoadWorld(saveId);
  66 |         if (world is null) return null;
  67 |         var player = world.GetPlayer(playerId);
  68 |         if (player is null) return null;
  69 | 
  70 |         // Reuse the existing sheet id for this (saveId, playerId) pair
  71 |         // if one exists, so a re-export overwrites rather than
  72 |         // duplicating.
  73 |         var existing = FindBySource(saveId, playerId.Value);
  74 |         var id = existing?.Id ?? Guid.NewGuid();
  75 |         var now = DateTimeOffset.UtcNow;
  76 | 
  77 |         var sheet = new CharacterSheet
  78 |         {
  79 |             Id = id,
  80 |             Name = player.Name,
  81 |             Race = player.Race,
  82 |             Class = player.Class,
  83 |             Background = player.Background,
  84 |             Attributes = new Dictionary<string, int>(player.Attributes),
  85 |             Resources = new Dictionary<string, int>(player.Resources),
  86 |             Level = player.Level ?? 1,
  87 |             Xp = player.Experience ?? 0,
  88 |             InventoryItemIds = player.Inventory.Items
  89 |                 .Where(i => !string.IsNullOrEmpty(i.TemplateId))
  90 |                 .Select(i => i.TemplateId!)
  91 |                 .ToList(),
  92 |             EquippedItemIds = player.Equipped
  93 |                 .Where(kv => !string.IsNullOrEmpty(kv.Value.TemplateId))
  94 |                 .ToDictionary(kv => kv.Key, kv => kv.Value.TemplateId!),
  95 |             ProficientSkills = player.ProficientSkills ?? new List<string>(),
  96 |             Speed = player.Speed,
  97 |             SourceSaveId = saveId,
  98 |             SourcePlayerId = playerId.Value,
  99 |             CreatedAt = existing?.CreatedAt ?? now,
 100 |             UpdatedAt = now,
 101 |         };
 102 | 
 103 |         try
 104 |         {
 105 |             Directory.CreateDirectory(_charactersDirectory);
 106 |             var path = PathFor(sheet.Id);
 107 |             ProfileStore.AtomicWriteJson(path, sheet);
 108 |             return sheet;
 109 |         }
 110 |         catch (Exception ex)
 111 |         {
 112 |             System.Diagnostics.Trace.WriteLine(
 113 |                 $"[CharacterSheetStore] Failed to export character from {saveId}: {ex.Message}");
 114 |             return null;
 115 |         }
 116 |     }
 117 | 
 118 |     /// <summary>
 119 |     /// Load a sheet by id. Returns null if the file doesn't exist or
 120 |     /// fails to parse.
 121 |     /// </summary>
 122 |     public CharacterSheet? Load(string charId)
 123 |     {
 124 |         var id = ParseCharId(charId);
 125 |         if (id is null) return null;
 126 |         var path = PathFor(id.Value);
 127 |         if (!File.Exists(path)) return null;
 128 |         try
 129 |         {
 130 |             return JsonSerializer.Deserialize<CharacterSheet>(
 131 |                 File.ReadAllText(path), WorldJson.Options);
 132 |         }
 133 |         catch (Exception ex)
 134 |         {
 135 |             System.Diagnostics.Trace.WriteLine(
 136 |                 $"[CharacterSheetStore] Failed to load character {charId}: {ex.Message}");
 137 |             return null;
 138 |         }
 139 |     }
 140 | 
 141 |     /// <summary>
 142 |     /// Convenience overload: load by Guid id (no <c>char_</c> prefix
 143 |     /// needed).
 144 |     /// </summary>
 145 |     public CharacterSheet? Load(Guid charId) => Load(CharIdToString(charId));
 146 | 
 147 |     /// <summary>
 148 |     /// Load a sheet from an arbitrary file path — outside the
 149 |     /// <c>characters/</c> directory. Used by the import file-picker flow:
 150 |     /// the user selects a <c>.pathstone-char</c> file from anywhere on
 151 |     /// disk (USB stick, downloads folder, friend's email attachment…)
 152 |     /// and the importer loads it directly without copying. Returns null
 153 |     /// if the file doesn't exist or fails to parse.
 154 |     /// </summary>
 155 |     /// <remarks>
 156 |     /// Unlike <see cref="Load(string)"/>, this method does NOT validate
 157 |     /// the <c>char_{Guid:N}</c> filename format — the file can be named
 158 |     /// anything (the importer is filename-agnostic). The
 159 |     /// <see cref="CharacterSheet.Id"/> Guid inside the JSON is the
 160 |     /// source of truth.
 161 |     /// </remarks>
 162 |     public CharacterSheet? LoadByPath(string filePath)
 163 |     {
 164 |         if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(filePath)) return null;
 165 |         if (!File.Exists(filePath)) return null;
 166 |         try
 167 |         {
 168 |             return JsonSerializer.Deserialize<CharacterSheet>(
 169 |                 File.ReadAllText(filePath), WorldJson.Options);
 170 |         }
 171 |         catch (Exception ex)
 172 |         {
 173 |             System.Diagnostics.Trace.WriteLine(
 174 |                 $"[CharacterSheetStore] Failed to load character from {filePath}: {ex.Message}");
 175 |             return null;
 176 |         }
 177 |     }
 178 | 
 179 |     /// <summary>
 180 |     /// List all stored sheets. Returns an empty list if the directory
 181 |     /// doesn't exist. Corrupt files are silently skipped (logged to
 182 |     /// <c>Trace</c>). Sorted by <see cref="CharacterSheet.UpdatedAt"/>
 183 |     /// descending (most recently exported first).
 184 |     /// </summary>
 185 |     public IReadOnlyList<CharacterSheet> List()
 186 |     {
 187 |         var result = new List<CharacterSheet>();
 188 |         string[] files;
 189 |         try
 190 |         {
 191 |             if (!Directory.Exists(_charactersDirectory)) return result;
 192 |             files = Directory.GetFiles(_charactersDirectory, "char_*.json");
 193 |         }
 194 |         catch (Exception ex)
 195 |         {
 196 |             System.Diagnostics.Trace.WriteLine(
 197 |                 $"[CharacterSheetStore] Failed to list characters in {_charactersDirectory}: {ex.Message}");
 198 |             return result;
 199 |         }
 200 | 
 201 |         foreach (var file in files)
 202 |         {
 203 |             try
 204 |             {
 205 |                 var sheet = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<CharacterSheet>(
 206 |                     File.ReadAllText(file), WorldJson.Options);
 207 |                 if (sheet is not null) result.Add(sheet);
 208 |             }
 209 |             catch (Exception ex)
 210 |             {
 211 |                 System.Diagnostics.Trace.WriteLine(
 212 |                     $"[CharacterSheetStore] Skipping corrupt character at {file}: {ex.Message}");
 213 |             }
 214 |         }
 215 | 
 216 |         result.Sort((a, b) => b.UpdatedAt.CompareTo(a.UpdatedAt));
 217 |         return result;
 218 |     }
 219 | 
 220 |     /// <summary>
 221 |     /// Delete a sheet by id. No-op (returns false) if it doesn't exist.
 222 |     /// </summary>
 223 |     public bool Delete(string charId)
 224 |     {
 225 |         var id = ParseCharId(charId);
 226 |         if (id is null) return false;
 227 |         var path = PathFor(id.Value);
 228 |         if (!File.Exists(path)) return false;
 229 |         try
 230 |         {
 231 |             File.Delete(path);
 232 |             return true;
 233 |         }
 234 |         catch (Exception ex)
 235 |         {
 236 |             System.Diagnostics.Trace.WriteLine(
 237 |                 $"[CharacterSheetStore] Failed to delete character {charId}: {ex.Message}");
 238 |             return false;
 239 |         }
 240 |     }
 241 | 
 242 |     /// <summary>
 243 |     /// Convenience overload: delete by Guid id.
 244 |     /// </summary>
 245 |     public bool Delete(Guid charId) => Delete(CharIdToString(charId));
 246 | 
 247 |     // ─── Helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 248 | 
 249 |     private string PathFor(Guid id) => Path.Combine(_charactersDirectory, $"{CharIdToString(id)}.json");
 250 | 
 251 |     /// <summary>Format a Guid as the on-disk char id: <c>char_{Guid:N}</c>.</summary>
 252 |     public static string CharIdToString(Guid id) => $"char_{id:N}";
 253 | 
 254 |     /// <summary>
 255 |     /// Parse a <c>char_{Guid:N}</c> string back to a Guid. Returns null
 256 |     /// if the input doesn't match the expected format.
 257 |     /// </summary>
 258 |     public static Guid? ParseCharId(string charId)
 259 |     {
 260 |         if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(charId)) return null;
 261 |         if (!charId.StartsWith("char_", StringComparison.Ordinal)) return null;
 262 |         var hex = charId.Substring(5);
 263 |         return Guid.TryParseExact(hex, "N", out var g) ? g : null;
 264 |     }
 265 | 
 266 |     /// <summary>
 267 |     /// Find an existing sheet exported from the given (saveId, playerId)
 268 |     /// pair. Used by <see cref="Export"/> to make re-export idempotent.
 269 |     /// </summary>
 270 |     private CharacterSheet? FindBySource(string saveId, string playerId)
 271 |     {
 272 |         foreach (var sheet in List())
 273 |         {
 274 |             if (sheet.SourceSaveId == saveId && sheet.SourcePlayerId == playerId)
 275 |                 return sheet;
 276 |         }
 277 |         return null;
 278 |     }
 279 | }

--- src/MyGame.Core/Saves/LogEntry.cs (C#, 62 lines, ~778 tokens) ---
   1 | namespace MyGame.Core.Saves;
   2 | 
   3 | /// <summary>
   4 | /// One entry in a save's narrative/event log. The save's <c>log.json</c>
   5 | /// file is an array of these. Kinds mirror the TS app's log categories
   6 | /// (narrative / action / system / tool) so existing UI styling carries
   7 | /// over.
   8 | /// </summary>
   9 | /// <param name="Id">Unique per-entry id (so the UI can dedupe / key by id).</param>
  10 | /// <param name="Timestamp">UTC moment the entry was created.</param>
  11 | /// <param name="Kind">
  12 | /// One of <c>"narrative"</c>, <c>"action"</c>, <c>"system"</c>,
  13 | /// <c>"tool"</c>. Free-form string (kept as a string rather than an enum
  14 | /// so future kinds don't require a schema migration).
  15 | /// </param>
  16 | /// <param name="AuthorId">
  17 | /// Id of the player who triggered this entry. Null for system entries
  18 | /// (autosave, world-build stage, etc.).
  19 | /// </param>
  20 | /// <param name="Text">Human-readable entry text (may be markdown).</param>
  21 | /// <param name="Metadata">
  22 | /// Optional structured payload — e.g. for a tool entry, the tool name +
  23 | /// arguments + result JSON; for an action entry, the dice-check
  24 | /// breakdown. Values are <see cref="object"/> (System.Text.Json will
  25 | /// round-trip them as <see cref="System.Text.Json.JsonElement"/>).
  26 | /// </param>
  27 | public sealed record LogEntry(
  28 |     Guid Id,
  29 |     DateTimeOffset Timestamp,
  30 |     string Kind,
  31 |     string? AuthorId,
  32 |     string Text,
  33 |     IReadOnlyDictionary<string, object>? Metadata)
  34 | {
  35 |     /// <summary>
  36 |     /// Convenience factory for a fresh narrative entry (auto-generates
  37 |     /// Id + Timestamp). The most common entry kind.
  38 |     /// </summary>
  39 |     public static LogEntry Narrative(string text, string? authorId = null,
  40 |         IReadOnlyDictionary<string, object>? metadata = null) =>
  41 |         new(Guid.NewGuid(), DateTimeOffset.UtcNow, "narrative",
  42 |             authorId, text, metadata);
  43 | 
  44 |     /// <summary>Factory for a fresh action entry.</summary>
  45 |     public static LogEntry Action(string text, string? authorId = null,
  46 |         IReadOnlyDictionary<string, object>? metadata = null) =>
  47 |         new(Guid.NewGuid(), DateTimeOffset.UtcNow, "action",
  48 |             authorId, text, metadata);
  49 | 
  50 |     /// <summary>Factory for a fresh system entry (autosave, build stage, …).</summary>
  51 |     public static LogEntry System(string text,
  52 |         IReadOnlyDictionary<string, object>? metadata = null) =>
  53 |         new(Guid.NewGuid(), DateTimeOffset.UtcNow, "system",
  54 |             null, text, metadata);
  55 | 
  56 |     /// <summary>Factory for a fresh tool-call entry (GM tool invocation).</summary>
  57 |     public static LogEntry Tool(string text, string? authorId = null,
  58 |         IReadOnlyDictionary<string, object>? metadata = null) =>
  59 |         new(Guid.NewGuid(), DateTimeOffset.UtcNow, "tool",

--- src/MyGame.Core/Saves/SaveBackup.cs (C#, 36 lines, ~416 tokens) ---
   1 | namespace MyGame.Core.Saves;
   2 | 
   3 | /// <summary>
   4 | /// One entry in a save's backup history (issue #81 — auto-rotating
   5 | /// backups). Returned by <see cref="SaveManager.ListBackups"/>. The
   6 | /// <see cref="Timestamp"/> string is the on-disk backup directory name
   7 | /// (a sortable <c>yyyyMMddHHmmssfff</c> UTC timestamp, with an optional
   8 | /// <c>_N</c> suffix for same-millisecond collisions); pass it to
   9 | /// <see cref="SaveManager.RestoreBackup"/> to restore that snapshot.
  10 | /// </summary>
  11 | public readonly record struct SaveBackup
  12 | {
  13 |     /// <summary>
  14 |     /// On-disk backup directory name (e.g.
  15 |     /// <c>20240115143256000</c> or <c>20240115143256000_2</c>). Use
  16 |     /// this string verbatim as the <c>backupTimestamp</c> argument to
  17 |     /// <see cref="SaveManager.RestoreBackup"/>.
  18 |     /// </summary>
  19 |     public string Timestamp { get; init; }
  20 | 
  21 |     /// <summary>
  22 |     /// Parsed UTC timestamp of when the backup was created. Derived
  23 |     /// from <see cref="Timestamp"/> (the leading 17 chars before any
  24 |     /// <c>_N</c> suffix). Used by the UI to show a human-readable
  25 |     /// «2024-01-15 14:32» label and to sort backups newest-first.
  26 |     /// </summary>
  27 |     public DateTimeOffset CreatedAt { get; init; }
  28 | 
  29 |     /// <summary>
  30 |     /// Total byte size of all files in the backup directory (recursive).
  31 |     /// Lets the UI show «4.2 KB» next to each backup so the user can
  32 |     /// spot an abnormally large one.
  33 |     /// </summary>
  34 |     public long SizeBytes { get; init; }
  35 | }

--- src/MyGame.Core/Saves/SaveManager.cs (C#, 1221 lines, ~15,081 tokens) ---
   1 | using System.Globalization;
   2 | using System.IO;
   3 | using System.IO.Compression;
   4 | using System.Text;
   5 | using System.Text.Json;
   6 | using MyGame.Core.Common;
   7 | using MyGame.Core.Logging;
   8 | using MyGame.Core.Profile;
   9 | using MyGame.Core.World;
  10 | using MyGame.Core.World.Content;
  11 | 
  12 | // 'World' is both a namespace (MyGame.Core.World) and a type
  13 | // (MyGame.Core.World.World). Using-aliasing 'World' itself doesn't
  14 | // reliably win against the namespace import, so we alias to 'GameWorld'
  15 | // instead and use that name throughout this file.
  16 | using GameWorld = MyGame.Core.World.World;
  17 | 
  18 | namespace MyGame.Core.Saves;
  19 | 
  20 | /// <summary>
  21 | /// File-based save manager. Each save is a self-contained directory under
  22 | /// <see cref="SavesDirectory"/> holding four JSON files:
  23 | ///
  24 | /// <code>
  25 | /// {SavesDirectory}/{saveId}/
  26 | ///   meta.json         — SaveMeta (id, name, ownerId, partyId, characterName,
  27 | ///                       worldTitle, createdAt, updatedAt, buildStatus,
  28 | ///                       engineVersion, …) [always uncompressed]
  29 | ///   world.json[.gz]   — full World state (World.ToJson() round-trip)
  30 | ///   log.json[.gz]     — LogEntry[] (narrative / action / system / tool log)
  31 | ///   state.json[.gz]   — SaveState (turn, activePlayerId, playtimeMs, …)
  32 | ///   backups/{ts}/     — auto-rotating backups (issue #81)
  33 | ///   manifest.json     — present on imported saves (issue #33), ignored by
  34 | ///                       ListSaves / LoadAll
  35 | /// </code>
  36 | ///
  37 | /// <para>
  38 | /// Port of <c>engine/saves/saveManager.ts</c>. The TS source used a
  39 | /// 3-directory split (<c>worlds/</c> + <c>characters/</c> + <c>runs/</c>)
  40 | /// to enable sharing generated worlds and characters between runs — a
  41 | /// feature the desktop rewrite defers to a later task. This port
  42 | /// collapses everything into ONE directory per save (the spec's
  43 | /// <c>{ProfileDirectory}/saves/{saveId}/</c> layout), which keeps load/
  44 | /// save code simple and atomic-folder-rename-friendly. A future task can
  45 | /// add the world/character sharing layer on top without breaking the
  46 | /// save schema (the layout just gains two more sibling directories).
  47 | /// </para>
  48 | /// <para>
  49 | /// <b>Compression (issue #80):</b> when <see cref="CompressSaves"/> is
  50 | /// true, <c>world.json</c> / <c>log.json</c> / <c>state.json</c> are
  51 | /// gzip-compressed on write (<c>{name}.json.gz</c>). <c>meta.json</c>
  52 | /// stays uncompressed (small, fast to read for <see cref="ListSaves"/>).
  53 | /// Reads auto-detect compression by checking for the <c>.gz</c> file
  54 | /// first — old uncompressed saves (<c>{name}.json</c>) still load
  55 | /// (backward compatibility).
  56 | /// </para>
  57 | /// <para>
  58 | /// <b>Backups (issue #81):</b> on every <see cref="SaveAll"/> call, the
  59 | /// current save directory is copied to
  60 | /// <c>backups/{timestamp}/</c> BEFORE the new files are written. The
  61 | /// last 5 backups per save are kept; older ones are auto-deleted
  62 | /// (the 30-day retention rule is also applied). Use
  63 | /// <see cref="ListBackups"/> + <see cref="RestoreBackup"/> to browse
  64 | /// and roll back.
  65 | /// </para>
  66 | /// <para>
  67 | /// <b>Sharing (issue #33):</b> <see cref="ExportSave"/> zips the save
  68 | /// directory into a <c>.pathstone-world</c> archive with a
  69 | /// <see cref="SaveManifest"/> sidecar. <see cref="ImportSave"/> unzips
  70 | /// it into a new save directory with a fresh <c>saveId</c> so importing
  71 | /// the same archive twice doesn't collide.
  72 | /// </para>
  73 | /// <para>
  74 | /// <b>Concurrency:</b> all writes are atomic (write to <c>{file}.tmp</c>
  75 | /// then <see cref="File.Move(string, string, bool)"/>). Reads tolerate
  76 | /// partial writes (return null on parse failure rather than throwing).
  77 | /// The class is thread-safe for independent saveIds; concurrent writes
  78 | /// to the SAME saveId will race (last writer wins on each file).
  79 | /// </para>
  80 | /// </summary>
  81 | public sealed class SaveManager
  82 | {
  83 |     /// <summary>
  84 |     /// On-disk save layout version. Bumped when the file layout changes
  85 |     /// in a way that requires a migration on load. Mirrors
  86 |     /// <see cref="SaveMigrator.CurrentStorageVersion"/> — keep the two
  87 |     /// in sync (the SaveMigrator is the single source of truth for
  88 |     /// what migrations exist; this const is the SaveManager's snapshot
  89 |     /// of "what version do NEW saves get?").
  90 |     /// </summary>
  91 |     public const int CurrentStorageVersion = SaveMigrator.CurrentStorageVersion;
  92 | 
  93 |     /// <summary>
  94 |     /// Maximum number of backup snapshots kept per save (issue #81).
  95 |     /// Older backups are auto-deleted on every <see cref="SaveAll"/>.
  96 |     /// The 30-day age limit (<see cref="BackupMaxAgeDays"/>) is applied
  97 |     /// in parallel — whichever rule is more restrictive wins.
  98 |     /// </summary>
  99 |     public const int MaxBackupsPerSave = 5;
 100 | 
 101 |     /// <summary>
 102 |     /// Maximum age (in days) of a backup before it's auto-deleted
 103 |     /// (issue #81). Backups older than this are pruned regardless of
 104 |     /// the <see cref="MaxBackupsPerSave"/> count; backups beyond the
 105 |     /// count are pruned regardless of age. Whichever rule is more
 106 |     /// restrictive wins (i.e. a backup is kept only if it's BOTH within
 107 |     /// the 5 most recent AND less than 30 days old).
 108 |     /// </summary>
 109 |     public const int BackupMaxAgeDays = 30;
 110 | 
 111 |     private readonly string _savesDirectory;
 112 |     private ContentRegistry? _registries;
 113 |     private readonly object _lock = new();
 114 | 
 115 |     /// <param name="savesDirectory">
 116 |     /// Optional override for <see cref="SavesDirectory"/>. Defaults to
 117 |     /// <c>{ProfileStore.DefaultProfileDirectory}/saves</c>.
 118 |     /// </param>
 119 |     /// <param name="registries">
 120 |     /// Optional <see cref="ContentRegistry"/> used to rehydrate loaded
 121 |     /// worlds (World needs the live template registry because templates
 122 |     /// aren't persisted in saves). If null, loads
 123 |     /// <see cref="ContentRegistry.LoadDefault"/> on first use.
 124 |     /// </param>
 125 |     public SaveManager(string? savesDirectory = null, ContentRegistry? registries = null)
 126 |     {
 127 |         _savesDirectory = savesDirectory
 128 |             ?? Path.Combine(ProfileStore.DefaultProfileDirectory, "saves");
 129 |         _registries = registries;
 130 |     }
 131 | 
 132 |     /// <summary>
 133 |     /// Root directory holding all saves (one subdir per saveId).
 134 |     /// </summary>
 135 |     public string SavesDirectory => _savesDirectory;
 136 | 
 137 |     /// <summary>
 138 |     /// When true, <see cref="SaveAll"/> gzip-compresses
 139 |     /// <c>world.json</c> / <c>log.json</c> / <c>state.json</c> on write
 140 |     /// (issue #80). <c>meta.json</c> stays uncompressed (it's small and
 141 |     /// <see cref="ListSaves"/> reads it on every save-list refresh).
 142 |     /// Default is <c>false</c> so the unit tests (which construct a
 143 |     /// bare <see cref="SaveManager"/> without wiring Settings) keep
 144 |     /// writing plain JSON; the Desktop layer's ServiceHost reads
 145 |     /// <see cref="Profile.Settings.CompressSaves"/> (default <c>true</c>)
 146 |     /// on startup and sets this property accordingly. Reads always
 147 |     /// auto-detect compression, so toggling this between sessions is
 148 |     /// safe (a save written compressed loads fine when this is false,
 149 |     /// and vice versa).
 150 |     /// </summary>
 151 |     public bool CompressSaves { get; set; } = false;
 152 | 
 153 |     /// <summary>
 154 |     /// Lazily-loaded default content registry (used when no registry was
 155 |     /// injected in the ctor). Cached so repeated loads share one
 156 |     /// registry instance.
 157 |     /// </summary>
 158 |     private ContentRegistry Registries => _registries ??= ContentRegistry.LoadDefault();
 159 | 
 160 |     // ─── Path helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
 161 | 
 162 |     private string SaveDir(string saveId) => Path.Combine(_savesDirectory, saveId);
 163 |     private string MetaPath(string saveId) => Path.Combine(SaveDir(saveId), "meta.json");
 164 |     private string WorldPath(string saveId) => Path.Combine(SaveDir(saveId), "world.json");
 165 |     private string LogPath(string saveId) => Path.Combine(SaveDir(saveId), "log.json");
 166 |     private string StatePath(string saveId) => Path.Combine(SaveDir(saveId), "state.json");
 167 |     private string BackupsDir(string saveId) => Path.Combine(SaveDir(saveId), "backups");
 168 | 
 169 |     // ─── Create ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 170 | 
 171 |     /// <summary>
 172 |     /// Create a brand-new save slot. Generates a fresh <c>saveId</c>,
 173 |     /// writes all four files (meta / world / log / state), and returns
 174 |     /// the new <see cref="SaveMeta"/>. The log is initialized to an
 175 |     /// empty array (the caller can <see cref="SaveAll"/> later with a
 176 |     /// populated log).
 177 |     /// </summary>
 178 |     /// <exception cref="ArgumentException">
 179 |     /// <paramref name="name"/> is null/empty.
 180 |     /// </exception>
 181 |     public SaveMeta CreateSave(string name, GameWorld world, Guid? ownerId = null)
 182 |     {
 183 |         if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(name))
 184 |             throw new ArgumentException("Save name cannot be empty.", nameof(name));
 185 |         if (world is null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(world));
 186 | 
 187 |         var saveId = NewSaveId();
 188 |         try { GameLogger.Instance?.Info($"[SaveManager] CreateSave: {saveId} \"{name}\""); }
 189 |         catch { }
 190 |         var now = DateTimeOffset.UtcNow;
 191 | 
 192 |         var meta = new SaveMeta
 193 |         {
 194 |             Id = saveId,
 195 |             Name = name.Trim(),
 196 |             OwnerId = ownerId ?? Guid.Empty,
 197 |             CharacterName = world.Players.FirstOrDefault()?.Name,
 198 |             CharacterLevel = world.Players.FirstOrDefault()?.Level,
 199 |             WorldTitle = world.Locations.FirstOrDefault()?.Name,
 200 |             LocationName = world.GetLocation(world.Players.FirstOrDefault()?.LocationId ?? default)?.Name,
 201 |             CreatedAt = now,
 202 |             UpdatedAt = now,
 203 |             PlaytimeMs = world.PlaytimeMs,
 204 |             Turn = world.Turn,
 205 |             BuildStatus = BuildStatus.None,
 206 |             EngineVersion = Common.Version.Current,
 207 |             StorageVersion = CurrentStorageVersion,
 208 |         };
 209 | 
 210 |         SaveAll(saveId, world, meta, Array.Empty<LogEntry>());
 211 |         return meta;
 212 |     }
 213 | 
 214 |     // ─── Read ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 215 | 
 216 |     /// <summary>
 217 |     /// Read a save's meta. Returns null if the save directory or
 218 |     /// <c>meta.json</c> doesn't exist, or if the file is corrupt.
 219 |     /// </summary>
 220 |     public SaveMeta? LoadMeta(string saveId)
 221 |     {
 222 |         if (!TryIsValidSaveId(saveId)) return null;
 223 |         return TryReadJson<SaveMeta>(MetaPath(saveId));
 224 |     }
 225 | 
 226 |     /// <summary>
 227 |     /// Read a save's world. Returns null if the save directory or
 228 |     /// <c>world.json</c> doesn't exist, or if the file is corrupt. The
 229 |     /// loaded World is wired up with the content registry (default
 230 |     /// <see cref="ContentRegistry.LoadDefault"/> unless one was injected
 231 |     /// in the ctor) so template lookups work immediately.
 232 |     ///
 233 |     /// <para>
 234 |     /// <b>Compression:</b> transparently decompresses
 235 |     /// <c>world.json.gz</c> if present (issue #80). Falls back to
 236 |     /// <c>world.json</c> for old uncompressed saves.
 237 |     /// </para>
 238 |     ///
 239 |     /// <para>
 240 |     /// <b>Migration:</b> the save's <c>meta.json</c> is read in
 241 |     /// parallel to determine the save's
 242 |     /// <see cref="SaveMeta.StorageVersion"/>. If it predates
 243 |     /// <see cref="SaveMigrator.CurrentStorageVersion"/>,
 244 |     /// <see cref="SaveMigrator.MigrateWorld"/> is run on the
 245 |     /// deserialized world before returning it. The on-disk world.json
 246 |     /// is NOT re-written by this call (a future task can add
 247 |     /// lazy re-save so the next load skips the migrator).
 248 |     /// </para>
 249 |     /// </summary>
 250 |     public GameWorld? LoadWorld(string saveId)
 251 |     {
 252 |         if (!TryIsValidSaveId(saveId)) return null;
 253 |         var json = TryReadText(WorldPath(saveId));
 254 |         if (json is null) return null;
 255 |         try
 256 |         {
 257 |             var world = GameWorld.FromJson(json, Registries);
 258 | 
 259 |             // Run migration if the save predates the current schema.
 260 |             // The storage version lives in meta.json (not world.json),
 261 |             // so we read meta here just to get the version. If meta is
 262 |             // missing/corrupt, default to v1 (the migrator runs the
 263 |             // v1→v2 backfill defensively — it's idempotent).
 264 |             var meta = TryReadJson<SaveMeta>(MetaPath(saveId));
 265 |             var fromVersion = meta?.StorageVersion ?? 1;
 266 |             if (fromVersion < SaveMigrator.CurrentStorageVersion)
 267 |             {
 268 |                 SaveMigrator.MigrateWorld(world, fromVersion);
 269 |             }
 270 |             return world;
 271 |         }
 272 |         catch (Exception ex)
 273 |         {
 274 |             System.Diagnostics.Trace.WriteLine(
 275 |                 $"[SaveManager] Failed to deserialize world for {saveId}: {ex.Message}");
 276 |             return null;
 277 |         }
 278 |     }
 279 | 
 280 |     /// <summary>
 281 |     /// Read a save's log. Returns an empty array if the save directory
 282 |     /// or <c>log.json</c> doesn't exist (a save created by a partial
 283 |     /// write may legitimately have no log yet). Returns null only on
 284 |     /// hard parse failure (caller can decide whether to treat as empty
 285 |     /// or surface the error).
 286 |     /// </summary>
 287 |     public LogEntry[]? LoadLog(string saveId)
 288 |     {
 289 |         if (!TryIsValidSaveId(saveId)) return null;
 290 |         var log = TryReadJson<LogEntry[]>(LogPath(saveId));
 291 |         return log ?? Array.Empty<LogEntry>();
 292 |     }
 293 | 
 294 |     /// <summary>
 295 |     /// Full load: meta + world + log in one call. Returns null if any
 296 |     /// of the three is missing (a half-written save is treated as not
 297 |     /// loadable — the saves-list UI should hide such saves or mark them
 298 |     /// "corrupt").
 299 |     ///
 300 |     /// <para>
 301 |     /// <b>Migration:</b> if the meta's
 302 |     /// <see cref="SaveMeta.StorageVersion"/> predates
 303 |     /// <see cref="SaveMigrator.CurrentStorageVersion"/>, the world is
 304 |     /// migrated (via <see cref="LoadWorld"/>) and the meta is updated
 305 |     /// via <see cref="SaveMigrator.MigrateMeta"/>. The returned meta
 306 |     /// has the bumped version — callers that re-save will persist the
 307 |     /// new version so the next load skips the migrator.
 308 |     /// </para>
 309 |     /// </summary>
 310 |     public (GameWorld world, SaveMeta meta, LogEntry[] log)? LoadAll(string saveId)
 311 |     {
 312 |         var meta = LoadMeta(saveId);
 313 |         if (meta is null) return null;
 314 |         var world = LoadWorld(saveId);
 315 |         if (world is null) return null;
 316 |         var log = LoadLog(saveId) ?? Array.Empty<LogEntry>();
 317 | 
 318 |         // Bump the meta's StorageVersion + EngineVersion to current.
 319 |         // LoadWorld already ran the world migration (above); this just
 320 |         // refreshes the meta so a subsequent SaveAll persists the new
 321 |         // version. No-op when the meta was already at current.
 322 |         var fromVersion = meta.StorageVersion;
 323 |         if (fromVersion < SaveMigrator.CurrentStorageVersion
 324 |             || meta.EngineVersion != Common.Version.Current)
 325 |         {
 326 |             meta = SaveMigrator.MigrateMeta(meta, fromVersion);
 327 |         }
 328 |         return (world, meta, log);
 329 |     }
 330 | 
 331 |     /// <summary>
 332 |     /// Scan <see cref="SavesDirectory"/>, returning the meta of every
 333 |     /// loadable save, sorted by <see cref="SaveMeta.UpdatedAt"/>
 334 |     /// descending (most recently played first). Corrupt / partial saves
 335 |     /// are silently skipped (the saves-list UI shouldn't crash because
 336 |     /// one save's meta.json is half-written).
 337 |     /// </summary>
 338 |     public IReadOnlyList<SaveMeta> ListSaves()
 339 |     {
 340 |         var result = new List<SaveMeta>();
 341 | 
 342 |         string[] subdirs;
 343 |         try
 344 |         {
 345 |             if (!Directory.Exists(_savesDirectory)) return result;
 346 |             subdirs = Directory.GetDirectories(_savesDirectory);
 347 |         }
 348 |         catch (Exception ex)
 349 |         {
 350 |             System.Diagnostics.Trace.WriteLine(
 351 |                 $"[SaveManager] Failed to list saves in {_savesDirectory}: {ex.Message}");
 352 |             return result;
 353 |         }
 354 | 
 355 |         foreach (var dir in subdirs)
 356 |         {
 357 |             var metaPath = Path.Combine(dir, "meta.json");
 358 |             if (!File.Exists(metaPath)) continue;
 359 |             var dirName = Path.GetFileName(dir);
 360 |             try
 361 |             {
 362 |                 var meta = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<SaveMeta>(
 363 |                     File.ReadAllText(metaPath), WorldJson.Options);
 364 |                 if (meta is null) continue;
 365 |                 // If the meta's Id got out of sync with the directory
 366 |                 // name (manual copy / rename by the user), trust the
 367 |                 // directory name — it's the source of truth on disk.
 368 |                 if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(meta.Id) || meta.Id != dirName)
 369 |                 {
 370 |                     meta = meta with { Id = dirName };
 371 |                 }
 372 |                 result.Add(meta);
 373 |             }
 374 |             catch (Exception ex)
 375 |             {
 376 |                 System.Diagnostics.Trace.WriteLine(
 377 |                     $"[SaveManager] Skipping corrupt meta at {metaPath}: {ex.Message}");
 378 |             }
 379 |         }
 380 | 
 381 |         result.Sort((a, b) => b.UpdatedAt.CompareTo(a.UpdatedAt));
 382 |         return result;
 383 |     }
 384 | 
 385 |     // ─── Write ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 386 | 
 387 |     /// <summary>
 388 |     /// Persist the world + meta + log for an existing save. Overwrites
 389 |     /// all four files atomically (per-file atomic; the four-file set is
 390 |     /// not transactional, but the meta file is written LAST so a crash
 391 |     /// mid-<see cref="SaveAll"/> leaves a save whose meta points at the
 392 |     /// previous world state — loadable but slightly stale, never
 393 |     /// corrupt). Preserves <see cref="SaveMeta.CreatedAt"/> from the
 394 |     /// existing meta on disk (if present); refreshes
 395 |     /// <see cref="SaveMeta.UpdatedAt"/> to now.
 396 |     ///
 397 |     /// <para>
 398 |     /// <b>Backup (issue #81):</b> BEFORE writing the new files, the
 399 |     /// current save directory is copied to
 400 |     /// <c>backups/{timestamp}/</c> (if a previous save exists). The
 401 |     /// last <see cref="MaxBackupsPerSave"/> backups are kept; older
 402 |     /// ones (and any older than <see cref="BackupMaxAgeDays"/> days)
 403 |     /// are auto-deleted.
 404 |     /// </para>
 405 |     /// <para>
 406 |     /// <b>Compression (issue #80):</b> when <see cref="CompressSaves"/>
 407 |     /// is true, <c>world.json</c> / <c>log.json</c> /
 408 |     /// <c>state.json</c> are written as <c>{name}.json.gz</c>
 409 |     /// (gzip-compressed) and any stale plain-<c>.json</c> files are
 410 |     /// deleted. When false, the inverse happens (plain <c>.json</c>
 411 |     /// written, stale <c>.gz</c> deleted).
 412 |     /// </para>
 413 |     /// </summary>
 414 |     public void SaveAll(string saveId, GameWorld world, SaveMeta meta, LogEntry[] log)
 415 |     {
 416 |         if (!TryIsValidSaveId(saveId))
 417 |             throw new ArgumentException("Invalid save id.", nameof(saveId));
 418 |         if (world is null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(world));
 419 |         if (meta is null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(meta));
 420 |         if (log is null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(log));
 421 | 
 422 |         lock (_lock)
 423 |         {
 424 |             Directory.CreateDirectory(SaveDir(saveId));
 425 | 
 426 |             // Issue #81 — back up the current save BEFORE overwriting
 427 |             // it. The first SaveAll on a brand-new save has nothing to
 428 |             // back up (the directory was just created). Best-effort:
 429 |             // backup failures are logged but don't abort the save.
 430 |             try { BackupBeforeSave(saveId); }
 431 |             catch (Exception ex)
 432 |             {
 433 |                 System.Diagnostics.Trace.WriteLine(
 434 |                     $"[SaveManager] Backup-before-save failed for {saveId}: {ex.Message}");
 435 |             }
 436 | 
 437 |             // Preserve createdAt from existing meta (if any) — a
 438 |             // SaveAll call shouldn't reset the save's birthday.
 439 |             var existing = TryReadJson<SaveMeta>(MetaPath(saveId));
 440 |             var createdAt = existing?.CreatedAt ?? meta.CreatedAt;
 441 |             var now = DateTimeOffset.UtcNow;
 442 | 
 443 |             var updatedMeta = meta with
 444 |             {
 445 |                 Id = saveId,
 446 |                 CreatedAt = createdAt,
 447 |                 UpdatedAt = now,
 448 |                 PlaytimeMs = world.PlaytimeMs,
 449 |                 Turn = world.Turn,
 450 |                 EngineVersion = Common.Version.Current,
 451 |                 StorageVersion = CurrentStorageVersion,
 452 |                 // Refresh the denormalized character/location snapshots
 453 |                 // so the saves-list preview stays current.
 454 |                 CharacterName = world.Players.FirstOrDefault()?.Name ?? meta.CharacterName,
 455 |                 CharacterLevel = world.Players.FirstOrDefault()?.Level ?? meta.CharacterLevel,
 456 |             };
 457 |             var playerId = world.ActivePlayerId ?? world.Players.FirstOrDefault()?.Id;
 458 |             if (playerId is { } pid)
 459 |             {
 460 |                 var loc = world.GetLocation(world.GetPlayer(pid)?.LocationId ?? default);
 461 |                 if (loc is not null) updatedMeta = updatedMeta with { LocationName = loc.Name };
 462 |             }
 463 | 
 464 |             var state = new SaveState
 465 |             {
 466 |                 EngineVersion = Common.Version.Current,
 467 |                 ActivePlayerId = world.ActivePlayerId,
 468 |                 StartedAt = world.StartedAt,
 469 |                 PlaytimeMs = world.PlaytimeMs,
 470 |                 Turn = world.Turn,
 471 |             };
 472 | 
 473 |             // Write order: world, log, state, then meta LAST. A crash
 474 |             // before the meta write leaves the previous meta pointing at
 475 |             // the previous (still-loadable) world — never at a missing
 476 |             // or half-written world.
 477 |             //
 478 |             // meta.json is ALWAYS uncompressed (ListSaves reads it
 479 |             // hot-path on every save-list refresh; gzip-ing a ~1 KB
 480 |             // file would slow ListSaves with no size win). The other
 481 |             // three follow the CompressSaves flag.
 482 |             AtomicWriteJson(WorldPath(saveId), world.ToJson(), rawString: true, compress: CompressSaves);
 483 |             AtomicWriteJson(LogPath(saveId), log, compress: CompressSaves);
 484 |             AtomicWriteJson(StatePath(saveId), state, compress: CompressSaves);
 485 |             AtomicWriteJson(MetaPath(saveId), updatedMeta, compress: false);
 486 |         }
 487 |     }
 488 | 
 489 |     // ─── Delete ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 490 | 
 491 |     /// <summary>
 492 |     /// Delete a save directory entirely (including its
 493 |     /// <c>backups/</c> subdirectory). No-op if the save doesn't exist
 494 |     /// (returns false). Returns true on successful deletion. Failures
 495 |     /// (e.g. file in use) are logged to <c>Trace</c> and return false.
 496 |     /// </summary>
 497 |     public bool DeleteSave(string saveId)
 498 |     {
 499 |         if (!TryIsValidSaveId(saveId)) return false;
 500 |         var dir = SaveDir(saveId);
 501 |         if (!Directory.Exists(dir)) return false;
 502 |         try
 503 |         {
 504 |             Directory.Delete(dir, recursive: true);
 505 |             return true;
 506 |         }
 507 |         catch (Exception ex)
 508 |         {
 509 |             System.Diagnostics.Trace.WriteLine(
 510 |                 $"[SaveManager] Failed to delete save {saveId}: {ex.Message}");
 511 |             return false;
 512 |         }
 513 |     }
 514 | 
 515 |     // ─── Backups (issue #81) ───────────────────────────────────────────
 516 | 
 517 |     /// <summary>
 518 |     /// List all backups for a save, sorted newest-first. Returns an
 519 |     /// empty list if the save has no backups (or doesn't exist). Each
 520 |     /// entry's <see cref="SaveBackup.Timestamp"/> is the on-disk backup
 521 |     /// directory name — pass it to <see cref="RestoreBackup"/> to roll
 522 |     /// the save back to that snapshot.
 523 |     /// </summary>
 524 |     public IReadOnlyList<SaveBackup> ListBackups(string saveId)
 525 |     {
 526 |         var result = new List<SaveBackup>();
 527 |         if (!TryIsValidSaveId(saveId)) return result;
 528 |         var backupsRoot = BackupsDir(saveId);
 529 |         if (!Directory.Exists(backupsRoot)) return result;
 530 | 
 531 |         string[] dirs;
 532 |         try { dirs = Directory.GetDirectories(backupsRoot); }
 533 |         catch (Exception ex)
 534 |         {
 535 |             System.Diagnostics.Trace.WriteLine(
 536 |                 $"[SaveManager] Failed to list backups in {backupsRoot}: {ex.Message}");
 537 |             return result;
 538 |         }
 539 | 
 540 |         foreach (var dir in dirs)
 541 |         {
 542 |             var name = Path.GetFileName(dir);
 543 |             // The backup dir name is "yyyyMMddHHmmssfff" optionally
 544 |             // followed by "_N" (same-ms collision suffix). Parse the
 545 |             // leading 17 chars as the UTC timestamp.
 546 |             var tsStr = name.Contains('_')
 547 |                 ? name.Substring(0, name.IndexOf('_'))
 548 |                 : name;
 549 |             if (!DateTimeOffset.TryParseExact(
 550 |                 tsStr,
 551 |                 "yyyyMMddHHmmssfff",
 552 |                 CultureInfo.InvariantCulture,
 553 |                 DateTimeStyles.AssumeUniversal | DateTimeStyles.AdjustToUniversal,
 554 |                 out var ts))
 555 |             {
 556 |                 // Unrecognized backup dir name — skip rather than
 557 |                 // crashing the whole list (a user might have dropped a
 558 |                 // stray folder there).
 559 |                 continue;
 560 |             }
 561 | 
 562 |             long size = 0;
 563 |             try
 564 |             {
 565 |                 foreach (var f in Directory.EnumerateFiles(dir, "*", SearchOption.AllDirectories))
 566 |                 {
 567 |                     try { size += new FileInfo(f).Length; }
 568 |                     catch { /* skip locked / vanished file */ }
 569 |                 }
 570 |             }
 571 |             catch { /* size is best-effort */ }
 572 | 
 573 |             result.Add(new SaveBackup
 574 |             {
 575 |                 Timestamp = name,
 576 |                 CreatedAt = ts,
 577 |                 SizeBytes = size,
 578 |             });
 579 |         }
 580 | 
 581 |         result.Sort((a, b) => b.CreatedAt.CompareTo(a.CreatedAt));
 582 |         return result;
 583 |     }
 584 | 
 585 |     /// <summary>
 586 |     /// Restore a save from a previously-taken backup. The current save
 587 |     /// files (meta / world / log / state) are replaced with the backup's
 588 |     /// copies. The <c>backups/</c> subdirectory is preserved (so the
 589 |     /// user can roll back further if needed). Returns false if the save
 590 |     /// or the named backup doesn't exist; true on success.
 591 |     /// </summary>
 592 |     /// <param name="saveId">
 593 |     /// The save to restore into (must already exist on disk).
 594 |     /// </param>
 595 |     /// <param name="backupTimestamp">
 596 |     /// The <see cref="SaveBackup.Timestamp"/> of the backup to restore
 597 |     /// (returned by <see cref="ListBackups"/>).
 598 |     /// </param>
 599 |     /// <remarks>
 600 |     /// The restore is destructive — the current (pre-restore) save
 601 |     /// state is NOT auto-backed-up before being overwritten. If the
 602 |     /// user wants to undo a restore, they can pick an earlier backup
 603 |     /// (the restore itself doesn't create a new backup, so the
 604 |     /// pre-restore state isn't recoverable through the backup system).
 605 |     /// A future task could add a pre-restore snapshot if needed.
 606 |     /// </remarks>
 607 |     public bool RestoreBackup(string saveId, string backupTimestamp)
 608 |     {
 609 |         if (!TryIsValidSaveId(saveId)) return false;
 610 |         if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(backupTimestamp)) return false;
 611 |         // Reject path-traversal / weird names. The timestamp is a dir
 612 |         // name, but we still validate it to keep the restore path safe.
 613 |         if (backupTimestamp.Contains('/') || backupTimestamp.Contains('\\')
 614 |             || backupTimestamp.Contains("..", StringComparison.Ordinal))
 615 |             return false;
 616 | 
 617 |         var saveDir = SaveDir(saveId);
 618 |         if (!Directory.Exists(saveDir)) return false;
 619 | 
 620 |         var backupDir = Path.Combine(BackupsDir(saveId), backupTimestamp);
 621 |         if (!Directory.Exists(backupDir)) return false;
 622 | 
 623 |         lock (_lock)
 624 |         {
 625 |             // Delete the current top-level save files (meta / world /
 626 |             // log / state, both .json and .json.gz variants, plus any
 627 |             // stray .tmp files from a crashed previous write). The
 628 |             // backups/ subdir is preserved.
 629 |             foreach (var file in Directory.EnumerateFiles(saveDir, "*", SearchOption.TopDirectoryOnly))
 630 |             {
 631 |                 try { File.Delete(file); }
 632 |                 catch (Exception ex)
 633 |                 {
 634 |                     System.Diagnostics.Trace.WriteLine(
 635 |                         $"[SaveManager] Failed to delete {file} during restore: {ex.Message}");
 636 |                 }
 637 |             }
 638 | 
 639 |             // Copy the backup's files back into the save dir.
 640 |             foreach (var file in Directory.EnumerateFiles(backupDir, "*", SearchOption.TopDirectoryOnly))
 641 |             {
 642 |                 var name = Path.GetFileName(file);
 643 |                 try { File.Copy(file, Path.Combine(saveDir, name), overwrite: true); }
 644 |                 catch (Exception ex)
 645 |                 {
 646 |                     System.Diagnostics.Trace.WriteLine(
 647 |                         $"[SaveManager] Failed to restore {file}: {ex.Message}");
 648 |                 }
 649 |             }
 650 |         }
 651 |         return true;
 652 |     }
 653 | 
 654 |     // ─── Sharing: export / import (issue #33) ──────────────────────────
 655 | 
 656 |     /// <summary>
 657 |     /// Export a save as a <c>.pathstone-world</c> zip archive. The
 658 |     /// archive contains:
 659 |     /// <list type="bullet">
 660 |     ///   <item><c>manifest.json</c> — <see cref="SaveManifest"/> with
 661 |     ///     world title, engine version, owner profile id, export
 662 |     ///     timestamp.</item>
 663 |     ///   <item><c>meta.json</c> — copied verbatim from the save dir.</item>
 664 |     ///   <item><c>world.json</c> or <c>world.json.gz</c> — copied
 665 |     ///     verbatim (compression preserved).</item>
 666 |     ///   <item><c>log.json</c>[<c>.gz</c>] — copied verbatim.</item>
 667 |     ///   <item><c>state.json</c>[<c>.gz</c>] — copied verbatim.</item>
 668 |     /// </list>
 669 |     /// The output is written atomically (<c>{path}.tmp</c> → move) so
 670 |     /// a partial archive never appears at <paramref name="outputPath"/>.
 671 |     /// Returns false if the save doesn't exist; true on success.
 672 |     /// </summary>
 673 |     public bool ExportSave(string saveId, string outputPath)
 674 |     {
 675 |         if (!TryIsValidSaveId(saveId)) return false;
 676 |         if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(outputPath)) return false;
 677 |         var saveDir = SaveDir(saveId);
 678 |         if (!Directory.Exists(saveDir)) return false;
 679 | 
 680 |         var meta = TryReadJson<SaveMeta>(MetaPath(saveId));
 681 |         var manifest = new SaveManifest
 682 |         {
 683 |             WorldTitle = !string.IsNullOrEmpty(meta?.WorldTitle) ? meta!.WorldTitle : meta?.Name,
 684 |             SaveName = meta?.Name,
 685 |             EngineVersion = Common.Version.Current,
 686 |             StorageVersion = CurrentStorageVersion,
 687 |             OwnerProfileId = meta?.OwnerId ?? Guid.Empty,
 688 |             ExportTimestamp = DateTimeOffset.UtcNow,
 689 |         };
 690 | 
 691 |         // Write to a .tmp file first, then atomically move to the
 692 |         // destination. A crash mid-export leaves a .tmp file (which the
 693 |         // user can ignore / delete) rather than a truncated .pathstone-world.
 694 |         var tmpPath = outputPath + ".tmp";
 695 |         try
 696 |         {
 697 |             using (var fs = File.Create(tmpPath))
 698 |             using (var zip = new ZipArchive(fs, ZipArchiveMode.Create, leaveOpen: false))
 699 |             {
 700 |                 // manifest.json — written from the in-memory manifest
 701 |                 // (NOT copied from the save dir, because the save dir's
 702 |                 // manifest.json — if any — is the IMPORT manifest from
 703 |                 // when this save was imported; we want a fresh EXPORT
 704 |                 // manifest reflecting THIS export).
 705 |                 WriteZipEntryText(zip, "manifest.json",
 706 |                     JsonSerializer.Serialize(manifest, WorldJson.Options));
 707 | 
 708 |                 // Copy all top-level save files verbatim (preserves
 709 |                 // compression: world.json.gz stays world.json.gz).
 710 |                 // Skip .tmp files (crashed writes) and any pre-existing
 711 |                 // manifest.json (we just wrote a fresh one above).
 712 |                 foreach (var file in Directory.EnumerateFiles(saveDir, "*", SearchOption.TopDirectoryOnly))
 713 |                 {
 714 |                     var name = Path.GetFileName(file);
 715 |                     if (name.EndsWith(".tmp", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)) continue;
 716 |                     if (name.Equals("manifest.json", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)) continue;
 717 |                     WriteZipEntryFromStream(zip, name, file);
 718 |                 }
 719 |             }
 720 |             File.Move(tmpPath, outputPath, overwrite: true);
 721 |             return true;
 722 |         }
 723 |         catch (Exception ex)
 724 |         {
 725 |             System.Diagnostics.Trace.WriteLine(
 726 |                 $"[SaveManager] Failed to export save {saveId} to {outputPath}: {ex.Message}");
 727 |             try { if (File.Exists(tmpPath)) File.Delete(tmpPath); }
 728 |             catch { /* best-effort cleanup */ }
 729 |             return false;
 730 |         }
 731 |     }
 732 | 
 733 |     /// <summary>
 734 |     /// Import a <c>.pathstone-world</c> archive as a new save. The
 735 |     /// archive is unzipped into a fresh <c>{saveId}/</c> directory
 736 |     /// (a new saveId is generated so importing the same archive twice
 737 |     /// produces two separate saves instead of colliding). The archive's
 738 |     /// <c>manifest.json</c> (if present) is updated with the import
 739 |     /// timestamp and stored as a sidecar in the save dir (ignored by
 740 |     /// <see cref="ListSaves"/> / <see cref="LoadAll"/>). The meta's Id
 741 |     /// is rewritten to match the new saveId and UpdatedAt is refreshed.
 742 |     /// Returns the new saveId, or null on failure.
 743 |     /// </summary>
 744 |     /// <param name="inputPath">
 745 |     /// Path to the <c>.pathstone-world</c> zip archive.
 746 |     /// </param>
 747 |     /// <remarks>
 748 |     /// <b>Zip-slip defense:</b> entry names are sanitized to a flat
 749 |     /// single-level filename (any directory components or
 750 |     /// <c>..</c> traversal is rejected) so a malicious archive can't
 751 |     /// write outside the save dir.
 752 |     /// </remarks>
 753 |     public string? ImportSave(string inputPath)
 754 |     {
 755 |         if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(inputPath)) return null;
 756 |         if (!File.Exists(inputPath)) return null;
 757 | 
 758 |         var saveId = NewSaveId();
 759 |         var saveDir = SaveDir(saveId);
 760 | 
 761 |         try
 762 |         {
 763 |             Directory.CreateDirectory(saveDir);
 764 |             SaveManifest? manifest = null;
 765 | 
 766 |             using (var src = File.OpenRead(inputPath))
 767 |             using (var zip = new ZipArchive(src, ZipArchiveMode.Read, leaveOpen: false))
 768 |             {
 769 |                 foreach (var entry in zip.Entries)
 770 |                 {
 771 |                     // Skip directory entries (FullName ends with '/') —
 772 |                     // we create the save dir upfront, no nested dirs
 773 |                     // are needed (entries are flat-sanitized below).
 774 |                     if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(entry.Name)) continue;
 775 | 
 776 |                     var safeName = SanitizeZipEntryName(entry.Name);
