 777 |                     if (safeName is null) continue;
 778 | 
 779 |                     var destPath = Path.Combine(saveDir, safeName);
 780 | 
 781 |                     if (safeName.Equals("manifest.json", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
 782 |                     {
 783 |                         // Read the manifest, stamp the import timestamp,
 784 |                         // write the updated manifest as a sidecar.
 785 |                         using (var es = entry.Open())
 786 |                         using (var reader = new StreamReader(es, Encoding.UTF8))
 787 |                         {
 788 |                             var json = reader.ReadToEnd();
 789 |                             try { manifest = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<SaveManifest>(json, WorldJson.Options); }
 790 |                             catch (Exception ex)
 791 |                             {
 792 |                                 System.Diagnostics.Trace.WriteLine(
 793 |                                     $"[SaveManager] Failed to parse manifest.json in archive: {ex.Message}");
 794 |                             }
 795 |                         }
 796 |                         var updated = (manifest ?? new SaveManifest()) with
 797 |                         {
 798 |                             ImportTimestamp = DateTimeOffset.UtcNow,
 799 |                         };
 800 |                         File.WriteAllText(destPath,
 801 |                             JsonSerializer.Serialize(updated, WorldJson.Options));
 802 |                         continue;
 803 |                     }
 804 | 
 805 |                     // Copy the entry verbatim (preserves compression).
 806 |                     using (var es = entry.Open())
 807 |                     using (var dest = File.Create(destPath))
 808 |                     {
 809 |                         es.CopyTo(dest);
 810 |                     }
 811 |                 }
 812 |             }
 813 | 
 814 |             // Rewrite the meta's Id to match the new saveId (the
 815 |             // archive's meta.json has the ORIGINAL saveId, which won't
 816 |             // match the new save directory name — ListSaves would
 817 |             // re-stamp it on read, but rewriting here keeps the on-disk
 818 |             // meta consistent with the dir name and lets LoadAll find
 819 |             // it). Refresh UpdatedAt so the imported save sorts to the
 820 |             // top of the saves list.
 821 |             var meta = TryReadJson<SaveMeta>(MetaPath(saveId));
 822 |             if (meta is not null)
 823 |             {
 824 |                 var now = DateTimeOffset.UtcNow;
 825 |                 var updated = meta with
 826 |                 {
 827 |                     Id = saveId,
 828 |                     // Preserve CreatedAt (the original creation date —
 829 |                     // useful provenance) but refresh UpdatedAt so the
 830 |                     // save sorts to the top of the list after import.
 831 |                     UpdatedAt = now,
 832 |                 };
 833 |                 AtomicWriteJson(MetaPath(saveId), updated, compress: false);
 834 |             }
 835 | 
 836 |             // If the archive had no manifest.json, synthesize one so
 837 |             // the save dir always carries import provenance. (When the
 838 |             // archive DID have a manifest, it was already written above
 839 |             // with the import timestamp stamped on it.)
 840 |             var manifestPath = Path.Combine(saveDir, "manifest.json");
 841 |             if (!File.Exists(manifestPath))
 842 |             {
 843 |                 var synthesized = new SaveManifest
 844 |                 {
 845 |                     SaveName = meta?.Name,
 846 |                     WorldTitle = !string.IsNullOrEmpty(meta?.WorldTitle) ? meta!.WorldTitle : meta?.Name,
 847 |                     EngineVersion = meta?.EngineVersion ?? Common.Version.Current,
 848 |                     StorageVersion = meta?.StorageVersion ?? CurrentStorageVersion,
 849 |                     OwnerProfileId = meta?.OwnerId ?? Guid.Empty,
 850 |                     ImportTimestamp = DateTimeOffset.UtcNow,
 851 |                 };
 852 |                 File.WriteAllText(manifestPath,
 853 |                     JsonSerializer.Serialize(synthesized, WorldJson.Options));
 854 |             }
 855 | 
 856 |             return saveId;
 857 |         }
 858 |         catch (Exception ex)
 859 |         {
 860 |             System.Diagnostics.Trace.WriteLine(
 861 |                 $"[SaveManager] Failed to import save from {inputPath}: {ex.Message}");
 862 |             // Best-effort cleanup of the half-created save dir so the
 863 |             // user doesn't end up with a corrupt save slot.
 864 |             try { if (Directory.Exists(saveDir)) Directory.Delete(saveDir, recursive: true); }
 865 |             catch { /* best-effort */ }
 866 |             return null;
 867 |         }
 868 |     }
 869 | 
 870 |     // ─── Helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 871 | 
 872 |     /// <summary>
 873 |     /// Generate a new save id: <c>save_</c> + 32-char lowercase hex
 874 |     /// (Guid's "N" format). Mirrors the spec's <c>save_{Guid:N}</c>
 875 |     /// convention.
 876 |     /// </summary>
 877 |     public static string NewSaveId() => $"save_{Guid.NewGuid():N}";
 878 | 
 879 |     /// <summary>
 880 |     /// Reject obviously-bad save ids before they hit the filesystem
 881 |     /// (path traversal, empty, etc.). A valid save id is
 882 |     /// <c>save_</c> + 32 hex chars.
 883 |     /// </summary>
 884 |     private static bool TryIsValidSaveId(string saveId)
 885 |     {
 886 |         if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(saveId)) return false;
 887 |         if (saveId.Length != 37) return false;            // "save_" (5) + 32 hex
 888 |         if (!saveId.StartsWith("save_", StringComparison.Ordinal)) return false;
 889 |         for (int i = 5; i < saveId.Length; i++)
 890 |         {
 891 |             var c = saveId[i];
 892 |             if (!((c >= '0' && c <= '9') || (c >= 'a' && c <= 'f'))) return false;
 893 |         }
 894 |         return true;
 895 |     }
 896 | 
 897 |     /// <summary>
 898 |     /// Read a save file's text. Auto-detects gzip compression: if
 899 |     /// <c>{path}.gz</c> exists, decompress it; otherwise read
 900 |     /// <c>{path}</c> as plain UTF-8 text. Returns null if neither file
 901 |     /// exists or on read/parse failure (logged to <c>Trace</c>).
 902 |     ///
 903 |     /// <para>
 904 |     /// This is the heart of the compression feature's backward
 905 |     /// compatibility: a save written compressed (<c>.json.gz</c>)
 906 |     /// loads fine when <see cref="CompressSaves"/> is false, and a
 907 |     /// save written uncompressed (<c>.json</c>) loads fine when
 908 |     /// <see cref="CompressSaves"/> is true. The flag only affects
 909 |     /// writes — reads always probe both extensions.
 910 |     /// </para>
 911 |     /// </summary>
 912 |     private static string? TryReadText(string path)
 913 |     {
 914 |         var gzPath = path + ".gz";
 915 |         try
 916 |         {
 917 |             // Prefer the .gz variant if present (newer compressed
 918 |             // saves). Fall back to plain .json for old saves.
 919 |             if (File.Exists(gzPath))
 920 |             {
 921 |                 using var fs = File.OpenRead(gzPath);
 922 |                 using var gz = new GZipStream(fs, CompressionMode.Decompress);
 923 |                 using var reader = new StreamReader(gz, Encoding.UTF8);
 924 |                 return reader.ReadToEnd();
 925 |             }
 926 |             if (File.Exists(path))
 927 |             {
 928 |                 return File.ReadAllText(path);
 929 |             }
 930 |             return null;
 931 |         }
 932 |         catch (Exception ex)
 933 |         {
 934 |             System.Diagnostics.Trace.WriteLine(
 935 |                 $"[SaveManager] Failed to read {path}: {ex.Message}");
 936 |             return null;
 937 |         }
 938 |     }
 939 | 
 940 |     private static T? TryReadJson<T>(string path)
 941 |     {
 942 |         var raw = TryReadText(path);
 943 |         if (raw is null) return default;
 944 |         try
 945 |         {
 946 |             return JsonSerializer.Deserialize<T>(raw, WorldJson.Options);
 947 |         }
 948 |         catch (Exception ex)
 949 |         {
 950 |             System.Diagnostics.Trace.WriteLine(
 951 |                 $"[SaveManager] Failed to deserialize {path}: {ex.Message}");
 952 |             return default;
 953 |         }
 954 |     }
 955 | 
 956 |     /// <summary>
 957 |     /// Atomic write helper. Serializes <paramref name="value"/> to JSON,
 958 |     /// writes to <c>{path}.tmp</c> (or <c>{path}.gz.tmp</c> when
 959 |     /// compressing), then <see cref="File.Move"/>s over the final path.
 960 |     /// A crash mid-write leaves the <c>.tmp</c> file (ignored by
 961 |     /// readers) rather than a truncated final file.
 962 |     /// </summary>
 963 |     /// <param name="rawString">
 964 |     /// If true, <paramref name="value"/> is treated as a pre-serialized
 965 |     /// JSON string and written verbatim (used for <c>world.json</c> —
 966 |     /// the World has its own ToJson() that we don't want to
 967 |     /// double-serialize).
 968 |     /// </param>
 969 |     /// <param name="compress">
 970 |     /// If true, gzip-compress the bytes and write to
 971 |     /// <c>{path}.gz</c> (and delete any stale plain <c>{path}</c>).
 972 |     /// If false, write plain text to <c>{path}</c> (and delete any
 973 |     /// stale <c>{path}.gz</c>). The clean-up keeps the save dir from
 974 |     /// accumulating both compressed and uncompressed variants of the
 975 |     /// same file when the user toggles <see cref="CompressSaves"/>.
 976 |     /// </param>
 977 |     private static void AtomicWriteJson(string path, object value, bool rawString = false, bool compress = false)
 978 |     {
 979 |         var json = rawString ? (string)value : JsonSerializer.Serialize(value, WorldJson.Options);
 980 |         var bytes = Encoding.UTF8.GetBytes(json);
 981 | 
 982 |         var finalPath = compress ? path + ".gz" : path;
 983 |         var tmpPath = finalPath + ".tmp";
 984 | 
 985 |         using (var fs = File.Create(tmpPath))
 986 |         {
 987 |             if (compress)
 988 |             {
 989 |                 // Optimal compression — save size matters more than
 990 |                 // save speed (saves are written at most once per
 991 |                 // autosave interval, typically 2 min).
 992 |                 using var gz = new GZipStream(fs, CompressionLevel.Optimal, leaveOpen: false);
 993 |                 gz.Write(bytes, 0, bytes.Length);
 994 |             }
 995 |             else
 996 |             {
 997 |                 fs.Write(bytes, 0, bytes.Length);
 998 |             }
 999 |         }
1000 |         File.Move(tmpPath, finalPath, overwrite: true);
1001 | 
1002 |         // Clean up the OTHER variant so toggling CompressSaves doesn't
1003 |         // leave stale files that confuse readers (TryReadText prefers
1004 |         // .gz, so a stale .gz would shadow a freshly-written plain
1005 |         // .json — bad). Best-effort: a failed delete is logged but
1006 |         // doesn't abort the save.
1007 |         var otherPath = compress ? path : path + ".gz";
1008 |         if (File.Exists(otherPath))
1009 |         {
1010 |             try { File.Delete(otherPath); }
1011 |             catch (Exception ex)
1012 |             {
1013 |                 System.Diagnostics.Trace.WriteLine(
1014 |                     $"[SaveManager] Failed to delete stale {otherPath}: {ex.Message}");
1015 |             }
1016 |         }
1017 |     }
1018 | 
1019 |     // ─── Backup helpers (issue #81) ────────────────────────────────────
1020 | 
1021 |     /// <summary>
1022 |     /// Copy the current save directory's top-level files to
1023 |     /// <c>backups/{timestamp}/</c>. Skipped if the save directory
1024 |     /// doesn't exist or has no save files (the first SaveAll on a
1025 |     /// brand-new save has nothing to back up). After copying, prunes
1026 |     /// old backups per <see cref="MaxBackupsPerSave"/> +
1027 |     /// <see cref="BackupMaxAgeDays"/>.
1028 |     /// </summary>
1029 |     /// <remarks>
1030 |     /// Must be called BEFORE the new files are written — otherwise the
1031 |     /// backup would contain the NEW state instead of the previous one.
1032 |     /// </remarks>
1033 |     private void BackupBeforeSave(string saveId)
1034 |     {
1035 |         var saveDir = SaveDir(saveId);
1036 |         if (!Directory.Exists(saveDir)) return;
1037 | 
1038 |         // Only back up if there's at least one save file (meta.json is
1039 |         // the canonical marker — a save dir without it is half-created
1040 |         // or a stray folder). This also avoids backing up a dir that
1041 |         // only contains a backups/ subfolder.
1042 |         var hasMeta = File.Exists(MetaPath(saveId));
1043 |         if (!hasMeta) return;
1044 | 
1045 |         var backupsRoot = BackupsDir(saveId);
1046 |         Directory.CreateDirectory(backupsRoot);
1047 | 
1048 |         // Timestamp: yyyyMMddHHmmssfff (UTC, sortable, lexical =
1049 |         // chronological). Collisions within the same millisecond get
1050 |         // a _N suffix so each backup has a unique dir name.
1051 |         var tsStr = DateTimeOffset.UtcNow.ToString("yyyyMMddHHmmssfff", CultureInfo.InvariantCulture);
1052 |         var backupDir = Path.Combine(backupsRoot, tsStr);
1053 |         var suffix = 1;
1054 |         while (Directory.Exists(backupDir))
1055 |         {
1056 |             backupDir = Path.Combine(backupsRoot, $"{tsStr}_{suffix}");
1057 |             suffix++;
1058 |         }
1059 |         Directory.CreateDirectory(backupDir);
1060 | 
1061 |         // Copy the save files (NOT the backups/ subfolder — that would
1062 |         // recursively nest backups). TopDirectoryOnly keeps it flat.
1063 |         foreach (var file in Directory.EnumerateFiles(saveDir, "*", SearchOption.TopDirectoryOnly))
1064 |         {
1065 |             var name = Path.GetFileName(file);
1066 |             // Skip .tmp files (crashed writes) — they're not part of
1067 |             // the canonical save state.
1068 |             if (name.EndsWith(".tmp", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)) continue;
1069 |             File.Copy(file, Path.Combine(backupDir, name), overwrite: true);
1070 |         }
1071 | 
1072 |         PruneBackups(backupsRoot);
1073 |     }
1074 | 
1075 |     /// <summary>
1076 |     /// Delete backups that are older than <see cref="BackupMaxAgeDays"/>
1077 |     /// OR beyond the <see cref="MaxBackupsPerSave"/> most recent.
1078 |     /// Whichever rule is more restrictive wins (i.e. a backup is kept
1079 |     /// only if it's BOTH within the N most recent AND less than
1080 |     /// <see cref="BackupMaxAgeDays"/> days old). Best-effort: deletion
1081 |     /// failures are logged but don't abort the prune.
1082 |     /// </summary>
1083 |     private static void PruneBackups(string backupsRoot)
1084 |     {
1085 |         if (!Directory.Exists(backupsRoot)) return;
1086 | 
1087 |         string[] dirs;
1088 |         try { dirs = Directory.GetDirectories(backupsRoot); }
1089 |         catch (Exception ex)
1090 |         {
1091 |             System.Diagnostics.Trace.WriteLine(
1092 |                 $"[SaveManager] Failed to enumerate backups in {backupsRoot}: {ex.Message}");
1093 |             return;
1094 |         }
1095 | 
1096 |         // Parse each backup dir name into (timestamp, fullPath) so we
1097 |         // can apply both rules. Dirs with unparseable names are left
1098 |         // alone (defensive — a user might have dropped a stray folder).
1099 |         var parsed = new List<(DateTimeOffset Ts, string Dir)>();
1100 |         foreach (var dir in dirs)
1101 |         {
1102 |             var name = Path.GetFileName(dir);
1103 |             var tsStr = name.Contains('_')
1104 |                 ? name.Substring(0, name.IndexOf('_'))
1105 |                 : name;
1106 |             if (DateTimeOffset.TryParseExact(
1107 |                 tsStr,
1108 |                 "yyyyMMddHHmmssfff",
1109 |                 CultureInfo.InvariantCulture,
1110 |                 DateTimeStyles.AssumeUniversal | DateTimeStyles.AdjustToUniversal,
1111 |                 out var ts))
1112 |             {
1113 |                 parsed.Add((ts, dir));
1114 |             }
1115 |         }
1116 | 
1117 |         // Sort newest-first so we can identify the N most recent.
1118 |         parsed.Sort((a, b) => b.Ts.CompareTo(a.Ts));
1119 | 
1120 |         var cutoff = DateTimeOffset.UtcNow.AddDays(-BackupMaxAgeDays);
1121 |         var toDelete = new HashSet<string>(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
1122 | 
1123 |         // Rule 1: delete backups older than BackupMaxAgeDays.
1124 |         foreach (var (ts, dir) in parsed)
1125 |         {
1126 |             if (ts < cutoff) toDelete.Add(dir);
1127 |         }
1128 | 
1129 |         // Rule 2: delete backups beyond the N most recent (by index in
1130 |         // the sorted list). Whichever rule is more restrictive wins —
1131 |         // since both add to the same set, a backup is kept only if it's
1132 |         // BOTH within the N most recent AND less than BackupMaxAgeDays
1133 |         // old.
1134 |         foreach (var (_, dir) in parsed.Skip(MaxBackupsPerSave))
1135 |         {
1136 |             toDelete.Add(dir);
1137 |         }
1138 | 
1139 |         foreach (var dir in toDelete)
1140 |         {
1141 |             try { Directory.Delete(dir, recursive: true); }
1142 |             catch (Exception ex)
1143 |             {
1144 |                 System.Diagnostics.Trace.WriteLine(
1145 |                     $"[SaveManager] Failed to prune backup {dir}: {ex.Message}");
1146 |             }
1147 |         }
1148 |     }
1149 | 
1150 |     // ─── Zip helpers (issue #33) ───────────────────────────────────────
1151 | 
1152 |     /// <summary>
1153 |     /// Write a UTF-8 text entry to a zip archive. Used for
1154 |     /// <c>manifest.json</c> (the only entry we synthesize from memory;
1155 |     /// the save files are streamed from disk via
1156 |     /// <see cref="WriteZipEntryFromStream"/>).
1157 |     /// </summary>
1158 |     private static void WriteZipEntryText(ZipArchive zip, string entryName, string text)
1159 |     {
1160 |         var entry = zip.CreateEntry(entryName, CompressionLevel.Optimal);
1161 |         using var es = entry.Open();
1162 |         var bytes = Encoding.UTF8.GetBytes(text);
1163 |         es.Write(bytes, 0, bytes.Length);
1164 |     }
1165 | 
1166 |     /// <summary>
1167 |     /// Stream a disk file into a zip entry. The file is copied verbatim
1168 |     /// (no re-compression at the zip layer beyond the entry's own
1169 |     /// <see cref="CompressionLevel.Optimal"/> — for already-gzipped
1170 |     /// save files this is a near-no-op since gzip output is
1171 |     /// incompressible). Used for <c>meta.json</c> / <c>world.json(.gz)</c>
1172 |     /// / <c>log.json(.gz)</c> / <c>state.json(.gz)</c>.
1173 |     /// </summary>
1174 |     private static void WriteZipEntryFromStream(ZipArchive zip, string entryName, string sourcePath)
1175 |     {
1176 |         var entry = zip.CreateEntry(entryName, CompressionLevel.Optimal);
1177 |         using var es = entry.Open();
1178 |         using var src = File.OpenRead(sourcePath);
1179 |         src.CopyTo(es);
1180 |     }
1181 | 
1182 |     /// <summary>
1183 |     /// Sanitize a zip entry name to a flat, single-level filename.
1184 |     /// Rejects path-traversal (<c>..</c>) and rooted paths, and strips
1185 |     /// any directory components so a malicious archive can't write
1186 |     /// outside the save directory (the classic "zip-slip" attack).
1187 |     /// Returns null if the name is empty or wholly invalid after
1188 |     /// sanitizing.
1189 |     /// </summary>
1190 |     private static string? SanitizeZipEntryName(string name)
1191 |     {
1192 |         if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(name)) return null;
1193 |         // GetFileName strips directory components (and rejects trailing
1194 |         // slashes — but entry.Name already has no trailing slash for
1195 |         // file entries; we called this from a path where entry.Name is
1196 |         // just the file name). Belt-and-suspenders: also reject '..'.
1197 |         var safe = Path.GetFileName(name);
1198 |         if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(safe)) return null;
1199 |         if (safe.Contains("..", StringComparison.Ordinal)) return null;
1200 |         if (Path.IsPathRooted(name)) return null;
1201 |         return safe;
1202 |     }
1203 | 
1204 |     /// <summary>
1205 |     /// Runtime engine state stored in <c>state.json</c>. Mostly a
1206 |     /// denormalized subset of <see cref="World"/> (so the saves-list UI
1207 |     /// and a future "quick resume" path can read engine state without
1208 |     /// loading the full world) plus an <see cref="Extra"/> bag for
1209 |     /// future additions (token usage, build state, etc.).
1210 |     /// </summary>
1211 |     private sealed record SaveState
1212 |     {
1213 |         public string EngineVersion { get; init; } = Common.Version.Current;
1214 |         public EntityId? ActivePlayerId { get; init; }
1215 |         public long StartedAt { get; init; }
1216 |         public long PlaytimeMs { get; init; }
1217 |         public int Turn { get; init; }
1218 |         public Dictionary<string, object>? Extra { get; init; }
1219 |     }
1220 | }

--- src/MyGame.Core/Saves/SaveManifest.cs (C#, 87 lines, ~974 tokens) ---
   1 | using System.Text.Json.Serialization;
   2 | 
   3 | namespace MyGame.Core.Saves;
   4 | 
   5 | /// <summary>
   6 | /// Manifest embedded inside a <c>.pathstone-world</c> archive (issue #33 —
   7 | /// save sharing). The manifest is written as <c>manifest.json</c> at the
   8 | /// root of the zip alongside the four save files
   9 | /// (<c>meta.json</c>, <c>world.json</c>/<c>world.json.gz</c>,
  10 | /// <c>log.json</c>/<c>log.json.gz</c>,
  11 | /// <c>state.json</c>/<c>state.json.gz</c>).
  12 | ///
  13 | /// <para>
  14 | /// The manifest carries the export-time metadata that doesn't belong in
  15 | /// <see cref="SaveMeta"/> (which is the in-fiction save metadata):
  16 | /// who exported it, when, with which engine version, and (on import)
  17 | /// when it was imported. The receiving side can show this in the UI
  18 | /// («Импортирован: 2024-01-15 из мира Долина Туманов, экспортировал
  19 | /// Странник-AB12»).
  20 | /// </para>
  21 | ///
  22 | /// <para>
  23 | /// <b>Backward compatibility:</b> <c>manifest.json</c> is optional on
  24 | /// import — older archives (or hand-zipped saves) without a manifest
  25 | /// still import; the importer just won't have the export metadata.
  26 | /// </para>
  27 | /// </summary>
  28 | public sealed record SaveManifest
  29 | {
  30 |     /// <summary>
  31 |     /// Human-readable title of the exported world (copied from
  32 |     /// <see cref="SaveMeta.WorldTitle"/> at export time, falling back to
  33 |     /// <see cref="SaveMeta.Name"/> when the world title is empty).
  34 |     /// </summary>
  35 |     public string? WorldTitle { get; init; }
  36 | 
  37 |     /// <summary>
  38 |     /// Save slot name (copied from <see cref="SaveMeta.Name"/>). Lets the
  39 |     /// importer suggest a default name for the new save slot.
  40 |     /// </summary>
  41 |     public string? SaveName { get; init; }
  42 | 
  43 |     /// <summary>
  44 |     /// Engine version that wrote the save (e.g. «0.2.0»). Copied from
  45 |     /// <see cref="Common.Version.Current"/> at export time so the
  46 |     /// importer can warn if the archive was written by an incompatible
  47 |     /// engine version.
  48 |     /// </summary>
  49 |     public string? EngineVersion { get; init; }
  50 | 
  51 |     /// <summary>
  52 |     /// On-disk save layout version (mirrors
  53 |     /// <see cref="SaveMeta.StorageVersion"/>). The importer can use this
  54 |     /// to decide whether the save needs migration on load.
  55 |     /// </summary>
  56 |     public int StorageVersion { get; init; }
  57 | 
  58 |     /// <summary>
  59 |     /// Profile id of the user who exported the save. Lets the receiver
  60 |     /// attribute the save to its original creator (a future "shared by"
  61 |     /// UI could show this). <see cref="Guid.Empty"/> when the original
  62 |     /// save had no owner (hand-authored test saves).
  63 |     /// </summary>
  64 |     public Guid OwnerProfileId { get; init; }
  65 | 
  66 |     /// <summary>
  67 |     /// UTC timestamp when the archive was exported. The receiver can
  68 |     /// show this in the import UI («Экспортирован: 2024-01-15 14:32»).
  69 |     /// </summary>
  70 |     public DateTimeOffset ExportTimestamp { get; init; }
  71 | 
  72 |     /// <summary>
  73 |     /// UTC timestamp when the archive was imported into this profile.
  74 |     /// Null on a freshly-exported archive (the importer sets it on the
  75 |     /// way in). Persisted in the save directory as
  76 |     /// <c>manifest.json</c> so a future re-export carries the import
  77 |     /// history forward.
  78 |     /// </summary>
  79 |     public DateTimeOffset? ImportTimestamp { get; init; }
  80 | 
  81 |     /// <summary>
  82 |     /// Schema version of the manifest itself. Bumped if the manifest
  83 |     /// shape changes in a way that requires migration. Currently 1.
  84 |     /// </summary>
  85 |     public int ManifestVersion { get; init; } = 1;
  86 | }

--- src/MyGame.Core/Saves/SaveMeta.cs (C#, 186 lines, ~2,295 tokens) ---
   1 | using MyGame.Core.Common;
   2 | 
   3 | namespace MyGame.Core.Saves;
   4 | 
   5 | /// <summary>
   6 | /// Build status of a save's world — surfaces in the saves list so the UI
   7 | /// can show a "Resume build" button for interrupted builds and a "Play"
   8 | /// button for completed ones.
   9 | /// </summary>
  10 | public enum BuildStatus
  11 | {
  12 |     /// <summary>No world-builder has run yet (fresh save).</summary>
  13 |     None = 0,
  14 | 
  15 |     /// <summary>World-builder is mid-flight (some stages done, more pending).</summary>
  16 |     Building = 1,
  17 | 
  18 |     /// <summary>World-builder completed; the save is fully playable.</summary>
  19 |     Done = 2,
  20 | 
  21 |     /// <summary>World-builder failed mid-flight (API error, etc.).</summary>
  22 |     Error = 3,
  23 | }
  24 | 
  25 | /// <summary>
  26 | /// Metadata for a single save. Persisted as <c>meta.json</c> in the
  27 | /// save directory. The saves-list UI reads ONLY this file per slot (no
  28 | /// need to deserialize the full World), so keep it small and
  29 | /// self-contained.
  30 | /// </summary>
  31 | /// <remarks>
  32 | /// Port-side consolidation of the TS <c>SaveMeta</c> shape from
  33 | /// <c>engine/types/index.ts</c>. The TS source had ~15 fields; this port
  34 | /// keeps the spec-required subset (id, name, ownerId, partyId,
  35 | /// characterName, worldTitle, timestamps, buildStatus, engineVersion)
  36 | /// plus a few extras the UI needs (turn, playtimeMs, characterLevel,
  37 | /// locationName, storageVersion). Skipped: <c>worldId</c>/<c>characterId</c>
  38 | /// (the split-storage indirection isn't needed in the desktop port —
  39 | /// each save is one self-contained directory).
  40 | /// </remarks>
  41 | public sealed record SaveMeta
  42 | {
  43 |     /// <summary>
  44 |     /// Stable save id (e.g. <c>save_3f2a1b8c9d0e4f5a8b7c6d5e4f3a2b1c</c>).
  45 |     /// Used as the on-disk directory name and as the wire-protocol id
  46 |     /// when telling the host which save to load.
  47 |     /// </summary>
  48 |     public string Id { get; init; } = string.Empty;
  49 | 
  50 |     /// <summary>Display name (player-editable via the saves list).</summary>
  51 |     public string Name { get; init; } = string.Empty;
  52 | 
  53 |     /// <summary>
  54 |     /// Profile id of the save's owner. Lets a future "share save" feature
  55 |     /// attribute the save to its creator even after import.
  56 |     /// </summary>
  57 |     public Guid OwnerId { get; init; }
  58 | 
  59 |     /// <summary>
  60 |     /// Multiplayer party id this save is bound to, if any. Null for a
  61 |     /// single-player save. When non-null, only that party's host can
  62 |     /// load this save (prevents a second party from forking it).
  63 |     /// </summary>
  64 |     public Guid? PartyId { get; init; }
  65 | 
  66 |     /// <summary>Character name snapshot for the saves-list preview.</summary>
  67 |     public string? CharacterName { get; init; }
  68 | 
  69 |     /// <summary>Character level snapshot (saves-list preview).</summary>
  70 |     public int? CharacterLevel { get; init; }
  71 | 
  72 |     /// <summary>
  73 |     /// Current location name snapshot for the saves-list preview. Null
  74 |     /// if the player hasn't been spawned yet (mid world-build).
  75 |     /// </summary>
  76 |     public string? LocationName { get; init; }
  77 | 
  78 |     /// <summary>
  79 |     /// Title of the generated world (from the world-builder's plan).
  80 |     /// Distinct from <see cref="Name"/> (which is the save slot name the
  81 |     /// player chose); <see cref="WorldTitle"/> is the in-fiction world
  82 |     /// name (e.g. «Долина Туманов»).
  83 |     /// </summary>
  84 |     public string? WorldTitle { get; init; }
  85 | 
  86 |     /// <summary>UTC timestamp when the save was first created.</summary>
  87 |     public DateTimeOffset CreatedAt { get; init; } = DateTimeOffset.UtcNow;
  88 | 
  89 |     /// <summary>UTC timestamp of the last <c>SaveAll</c> write.</summary>
  90 |     public DateTimeOffset UpdatedAt { get; init; } = DateTimeOffset.UtcNow;
  91 | 
  92 |     /// <summary>Cumulative playtime in ms across all sessions on this save.</summary>
  93 |     public long PlaytimeMs { get; init; }
  94 | 
  95 |     /// <summary>Engine turn counter snapshot.</summary>
  96 |     public int Turn { get; init; }
  97 | 
  98 |     /// <summary>World-builder build status (drives the "Resume" vs "Play" button).</summary>
  99 |     public BuildStatus BuildStatus { get; init; } = BuildStatus.None;
 100 | 
 101 |     /// <summary>
 102 |     /// Engine version that wrote this save. Set to
 103 |     /// <see cref="Common.Version.Current"/> on every write. Used by the
 104 |     /// load path to decide whether the save is loadable by the current
 105 |     /// engine (see <see cref="Common.Version.IsCompatible"/>).
 106 |     /// </summary>
 107 |     public string EngineVersion { get; init; } = Common.Version.Current;
 108 | 
 109 |     /// <summary>
 110 |     /// Save-file layout version. Bumped when the on-disk file layout
 111 |     /// changes in a way that requires a migration on load. Currently 2
 112 |     /// (v2 = item-instance Weight field backfill — older saves had
 113 |     /// <c>Item.Weight = 0</c> default; the migrator backfills from the
 114 |     /// item's template on load). NOT compatible with the TS app's
 115 |     /// <c>SPLIT_STORAGE_VERSION = 2</c> saves, which use a 3-directory
 116 |     /// layout that this port deliberately collapses.
 117 |     ///
 118 |     /// <para>
 119 |     /// <b>Settable</b> (not init-only) so the
 120 |     /// <see cref="SaveMigrator"/> can bump the version on an in-memory
 121 |     /// meta after running migrations. (Init-only would force a
 122 |     /// <c>with</c>-expression clone, which works but is wasteful for a
 123 |     /// single-field update.)
 124 |     /// </para>
 125 |     /// </summary>
 126 |     public int StorageVersion { get; set; } = SaveMigrator.CurrentStorageVersion;
 127 | 
 128 |     /// <summary>
 129 |     /// Cumulative prompt (input) tokens billed across all sessions on
 130 |     /// this save. Persists across save/load so the token-billing widget
 131 |     /// in the top bar can show a meaningful "Сессия: Nk токенов" total
 132 |     /// that survives a reload. Defaults to 0 (older saves load with 0).
 133 |     /// </summary>
 134 |     public int SessionPromptTokens { get; init; }
 135 | 
 136 |     /// <summary>
 137 |     /// Cumulative completion (output) tokens billed across all sessions
 138 |     /// on this save. See <see cref="SessionPromptTokens"/>.
 139 |     /// </summary>
 140 |     public int SessionCompletionTokens { get; init; }
 141 | 
 142 |     /// <summary>
 143 |     /// Text summary of the older (already-summarised) portion of the
 144 |     /// GameMaster's conversation history (issue #25). Null when no
 145 |     /// summarization has happened yet on this save. Persisted across
 146 |     /// save/load so the GM doesn't lose context: on save, the host copies
 147 |     /// <c>GameMaster.HistorySummary</c> here; on load, the host restores
 148 |     /// it by assigning back.
 149 |     ///
 150 |     /// <para>
 151 |     /// The summary is a short (~300 words) Russian-language recap of the
 152 |     /// oldest half of the conversation, produced by the GM's
 153 |     /// summarization pass. It's prepended to the working message list as
 154 |     /// a user message (<c>## Предыдущие события (изложение)</c>) so the
 155 |     /// model sees the compressed history before the recent turns.
 156 |     /// </para>
 157 |     ///
 158 |     /// <para>
 159 |     /// <b>Backward compatibility:</b> old saves (written before issue #25)
 160 |     /// don't have this field — they load with <c>null</c>, which the GM
 161 |     /// treats as "no summary yet" (it'll start fresh summarization when
 162 |     /// the history grows). No migration needed.
 163 |     /// </para>
 164 |     /// </summary>
 165 |     public string? HistorySummary { get; init; }
 166 | 
 167 |     /// <summary>
 168 |     /// Original <see cref="WorldPlan"/> JSON produced by the planner
 169 |     /// during the world-build (issue #20 — chunked generation). When the
 170 |     /// build ran in <c>chunked</c> mode, only the start region's
 171 |     /// locations were committed; other regions are marked
 172 |     /// <c>GenStatus="cold"</c> in the plan and are generated on-demand
 173 |     /// when the player travels toward them. This field holds the full
 174 |     /// plan JSON so <see cref="WorldBuilderOrchestrator.GenerateRegionAsync"/>
 175 |     /// can reload it + ask the AI to fill in a specific region without
 176 |     /// re-running the planner from scratch.
 177 |     /// </summary>
 178 |     /// <remarks>
 179 |     /// <b>Backward compatibility:</b> old saves (written before issue #20)
 180 |     /// don't have this field — they load with <c>null</c>, which the
 181 |     /// travel handler treats as "no chunked regions to generate" (the
 182 |     /// world was built full in the first pass). No migration needed.
 183 |     /// </remarks>
 184 |     public string? OriginalPlanJson { get; init; }
 185 | }

--- src/MyGame.Core/Saves/SaveMigrator.cs (C#, 236 lines, ~2,870 tokens) ---
   1 | using MyGame.Core.Common;
   2 | using MyGame.Core.World;
   3 | using MyGame.Core.World.Entities;
   4 | 
   5 | // 'World' is both a namespace (MyGame.Core.World) and a type
   6 | // (MyGame.Core.World.World). Alias to GameWorld to disambiguate, matching
   7 | // the convention used by SaveManager.cs.
   8 | using GameWorld = MyGame.Core.World.World;
   9 | 
  10 | namespace MyGame.Core.Saves;
  11 | 
  12 | /// <summary>
  13 | /// Save-file migration pipeline. Runs on load to transform old saves to
  14 | /// the current schema. Each step (v1→v2, v2→v3, …) is a separate method;
  15 | /// <see cref="MigrateWorld"/> loops from the save's
  16 | /// <see cref="SaveMeta.StorageVersion"/> up to
  17 | /// <see cref="CurrentStorageVersion"/>, applying each step in order.
  18 | /// </summary>
  19 | ///
  20 | /// <remarks>
  21 | /// <b>Why a migrator?</b> The desktop port's save schema is intentionally
  22 | /// minimal (4 JSON files per save). When a field is added to an entity
  23 | /// (e.g. <see cref="Item.Weight"/>), old saves load with the default
  24 | /// value (0) — which is functionally wrong (the inventory panel shows
  25 | /// zero carried weight). Rather than silently leave the bad data in
  26 | /// place, the migrator backfills the missing field from the content
  27 | /// registry on load. The migrated world is returned to the caller; the
  28 | /// caller can optionally re-save it so the next load is fast.
  29 | ///
  30 | /// <para>
  31 | /// <b>Versioning:</b> the storage version lives in
  32 | /// <see cref="SaveMeta.StorageVersion"/>. Bump
  33 | /// <see cref="CurrentStorageVersion"/> whenever a migration step is
  34 | /// added. The migrator is forward-only: it only knows how to upgrade
  35 | /// old saves to the current schema, not downgrade. Old engines loading
  36 | /// new saves will see <c>StorageVersion &gt; expected</c> and should
  37 | /// refuse to load (the loader currently doesn't check this — a future
  38 | /// task).
  39 | /// </para>
  40 | ///
  41 | /// <para>
  42 | /// <b>Idempotence:</b> every migration step MUST be idempotent — running
  43 | /// it on an already-migrated save should be a no-op. This lets the
  44 | /// migrator run unconditionally on load (defensive: if the meta's
  45 | /// version is missing or wrong, the migrator still produces correct
  46 | /// data).
  47 | /// </para>
  48 | ///
  49 | /// <para>
  50 | /// <b>Backward compatibility:</b> the v1→v2 step (Item.Weight backfill)
  51 | /// is the only step currently defined. The Item.Weight field was added
  52 | /// after the initial release; old saves have <c>Weight = 0</c> on every
  53 | /// item instance. The migrator looks up the item's template via the
  54 | /// world's content registry and copies the template's Weight onto the
  55 | /// instance. Items without a template, or whose template doesn't exist
  56 | /// in the registry, are left at 0.
  57 | /// </para>
  58 | /// </remarks>
  59 | public static class SaveMigrator
  60 | {
  61 |     /// <summary>
  62 |     /// Current on-disk save layout version. Bump this when adding a new
  63 |     /// migration step (and add the corresponding
  64 |     /// <c>MigrateWorldV&lt;n-1&gt;ToV&lt;n&gt;</c> method +
  65 |     /// <c>switch</c> case below). Mirrored by
  66 |     /// <see cref="SaveManager.CurrentStorageVersion"/> (kept in sync).
  67 |     /// </summary>
  68 |     public const int CurrentStorageVersion = 2;
  69 | 
  70 |     // ─── World migration ────────────────────────────────────────────────
  71 | 
  72 |     /// <summary>
  73 |     /// Run every applicable world migration step from
  74 |     /// <paramref name="fromVersion"/> up to
  75 |     /// <see cref="CurrentStorageVersion"/>. The world is mutated
  76 |     /// in place AND returned (for fluent chaining). No-op if
  77 |     /// <paramref name="fromVersion"/> is already at or above the current
  78 |     /// version.
  79 |     /// </summary>
  80 |     /// <param name="world">
  81 |     /// The freshly-deserialized world. Its
  82 |     /// <see cref="World.Registries"/> must be wired up (the
  83 |     /// <see cref="SaveManager"/> does this before calling the migrator)
  84 |     /// — template lookups use it.
  85 |     /// </param>
  86 |     /// <param name="fromVersion">
  87 |     /// The save's <see cref="SaveMeta.StorageVersion"/> at load time.
  88 |     /// Clamped to &gt;= 1. If &gt;= <see cref="CurrentStorageVersion"/>,
  89 |     /// the method returns the world unchanged.
  90 |     /// </param>
  91 |     public static GameWorld MigrateWorld(GameWorld world, int fromVersion)
  92 |     {
  93 |         if (world is null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(world));
  94 |         if (fromVersion < 1) fromVersion = 1;
  95 |         if (fromVersion >= CurrentStorageVersion) return world;
  96 | 
  97 |         for (int v = fromVersion; v < CurrentStorageVersion; v++)
  98 |         {
  99 |             switch (v)
 100 |             {
 101 |                 case 1:
 102 |                     MigrateWorldV1ToV2(world);
 103 |                     break;
 104 |                 // Future migrations go here:
 105 |                 // case 2: MigrateWorldV2ToV3(world); break;
 106 |                 // case 3: MigrateWorldV3ToV4(world); break;
 107 |                 default:
 108 |                     // Unknown step — shouldn't happen (the loop bound is
 109 |                     // CurrentStorageVersion, and every version between 1
 110 |                     // and CurrentStorageVersion must have a case). Be
 111 |                     // defensive: stop migrating rather than throwing, so
 112 |                     // a missing step doesn't brick the save.
 113 |                     return world;
 114 |             }
 115 |         }
 116 |         return world;
 117 |     }
 118 | 
 119 |     /// <summary>
 120 |     /// v1 → v2: backfill <see cref="Item.Weight"/> from the item's
 121 |     /// template. Before v2, item instances didn't carry a Weight field;
 122 |     /// they loaded with <c>Weight = 0</c> (the default), which made the
 123 |     /// inventory panel's carried-weight display show 0 for every item.
 124 |     /// After v2, <see cref="EntityFactory.InstantiateItem"/> copies the
 125 |     /// template's Weight onto the instance at spawn time — but old
 126 |     /// saves need a one-time backfill.
 127 |     ///
 128 |     /// <para>
 129 |     /// Targets every item in the world: loose ground items, every
 130 |     /// character's carried inventory, and every character's equipped
 131 |     /// slots (players + NPCs). Items with Weight &gt; 0 are skipped
 132 |     /// (idempotence: already-migrated items don't get re-touched).
 133 |     /// Items without a TemplateId, or whose TemplateId isn't in the
 134 |     /// registry, are left at 0 (we can't backfill what we can't look
 135 |     /// up).
 136 |     /// </para>
 137 |     /// </summary>
 138 |     private static void MigrateWorldV1ToV2(GameWorld world)
 139 |     {
 140 |         // Loose ground items.
 141 |         BackfillItemWeights(world.Items, world);
 142 | 
 143 |         // Every character's carried inventory + equipped slots.
 144 |         foreach (var player in world.Players)
 145 |         {
 146 |             BackfillItemWeights(player.Inventory.Items, world);
 147 |             foreach (var kv in player.Equipped)
 148 |                 BackfillItemWeight(kv.Value, world);
 149 |         }
 150 |         foreach (var npc in world.Npcs)
 151 |         {
 152 |             BackfillItemWeights(npc.Inventory.Items, world);
 153 |             foreach (var kv in npc.Equipped)
 154 |                 BackfillItemWeight(kv.Value, world);
 155 |         }
 156 |     }
 157 | 
 158 |     /// <summary>
 159 |     /// Backfill Weight on a collection of items. Defensive against null
 160 |     /// collections (a malformed save could have null Inventory.Items).
 161 |     /// </summary>
 162 |     private static void BackfillItemWeights(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Item>? items, GameWorld world)
 163 |     {
 164 |         if (items is null) return;
 165 |         foreach (var item in items) BackfillItemWeight(item, world);
 166 |     }
 167 | 
 168 |     /// <summary>
 169 |     /// Backfill Weight on a single item. Skipped if:
 170 |     /// <list type="bullet">
 171 |     ///   <item>The item is null.</item>
 172 |     ///   <item>The item already has a non-zero Weight (idempotence).</item>
 173 |     ///   <item>The item has no TemplateId (hand-spawned, no template to
 174 |     ///     backfill from).</item>
 175 |     ///   <item>The TemplateId isn't in the registry (unknown template —
 176 |     ///     leave at 0).</item>
 177 |     /// </list>
 178 |     /// </summary>
 179 |     private static void BackfillItemWeight(Item? item, GameWorld world)
 180 |     {
 181 |         if (item is null) return;
 182 |         if (item.Weight != 0) return;
 183 |         if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(item.TemplateId)) return;
 184 |         var tpl = world.Registries.Items.Get(item.TemplateId);
 185 |         if (tpl is null) return;
 186 |         item.Weight = tpl.Weight;
 187 |     }
 188 | 
 189 |     // ─── Meta migration ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
 190 | 
 191 |     /// <summary>
 192 |     /// Update a <see cref="SaveMeta"/> to the current schema version.
 193 |     /// Sets <see cref="SaveMeta.StorageVersion"/> to
 194 |     /// <see cref="CurrentStorageVersion"/> and refreshes
 195 |     /// <see cref="SaveMeta.EngineVersion"/> to
 196 |     /// <see cref="Common.Version.Current"/>. The caller should use the
 197 |     /// returned meta on subsequent saves so the on-disk version is
 198 |     /// bumped (the next load is then a no-op).
 199 |     ///
 200 |     /// <para>
 201 |     /// Returns a new meta via <c>with</c> (the EngineVersion property
 202 |     /// is still init-only — only StorageVersion was changed to set for
 203 |     /// the migrator's in-place bump). The caller should use the
 204 |     /// returned reference, not the original.
 205 |     /// </para>
 206 |     /// </summary>
 207 |     /// <param name="meta">
 208 |     /// The meta to update. If <paramref name="fromVersion"/> is already
 209 |     /// at or above <see cref="CurrentStorageVersion"/> and the
 210 |     /// EngineVersion already matches, the meta is still returned
 211 |     /// unchanged (the version-bump is harmless and the caller can rely
 212 |     /// on the post-condition StorageVersion == CurrentStorageVersion).
 213 |     /// </param>
 214 |     /// <param name="fromVersion">
 215 |     /// The save's StorageVersion at load time. Clamped to &gt;= 1.
 216 |     /// Currently unused (the meta is always bumped to current
 217 |     /// regardless) — kept for future migrations that may need to
 218 |     /// inspect the source version.
 219 |     /// </param>
 220 |     public static SaveMeta MigrateMeta(SaveMeta meta, int fromVersion)
 221 |     {
 222 |         if (meta is null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(meta));
 223 |         if (fromVersion < 1) fromVersion = 1;
 224 | 
 225 |         // Always bump to current. (Idempotent: a meta already at v2
 226 |         // stays at v2; the EngineVersion refresh is also idempotent.)
 227 |         // Use `with` because EngineVersion is still init-only (only
 228 |         // StorageVersion was made settable for the migrator).
 229 |         return meta with
 230 |         {
 231 |             StorageVersion = CurrentStorageVersion,
 232 |             EngineVersion = Common.Version.Current,
 233 |         };
 234 |     }
 235 | }

--- src/MyGame.Core/Tooling/ModLoader.cs (C#, 60 lines, ~583 tokens) ---
   1 | using System;
   2 | using System.Collections.Generic;
   3 | using System.IO;
   4 | using System.IO.Compression;
   5 | using System.Linq;
   6 | using System.Text.Json;
   7 | using MyGame.Core.World.Content;
   8 | 
   9 | namespace MyGame.Core.Tooling;
  10 | 
  11 | /// <summary>
  12 | /// Loads mod packs (.pathstone-pack zip files) from a mods/ directory.
  13 | /// Issue #59. Each pack contains a content.json (same format as data.json)
  14 | /// with item/NPC/building/recipe templates. Packs are loaded at startup
  15 | /// and merged into the ContentRegistry (later packs override earlier by
  16 | /// template id — load order is alphabetical by filename).
  17 | /// </summary>
  18 | public static class ModLoader
  19 | {
  20 |     /// <summary>
  21 |     /// Scan the mods/ directory for .pathstone-pack files and load each
  22 |     /// into the registry. Returns a list of loaded mod names (filenames
  23 |     /// without extension). Silently skips invalid packs.
  24 |     /// </summary>
  25 |     public static List<string> LoadAll(string modsDir, ContentRegistry registry)
  26 |     {
  27 |         var loaded = new List<string>();
  28 |         if (!Directory.Exists(modsDir)) return loaded;
  29 | 
  30 |         var packFiles = Directory.GetFiles(modsDir, "*.pathstone-pack")
  31 |             .Concat(Directory.GetFiles(modsDir, "*.zip"))
  32 |             .OrderBy(f => f, StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
  33 |             .ToList();
  34 | 
  35 |         foreach (var file in packFiles)
  36 |         {
  37 |             try
  38 |             {
  39 |                 using var zip = ZipFile.OpenRead(file);
  40 |                 var contentEntry = zip.GetEntry("content.json")
  41 |                     ?? zip.Entries.FirstOrDefault(e => e.Name.EndsWith("content.json"));
  42 |                 if (contentEntry is null) continue;
  43 | 
  44 |                 using var stream = contentEntry.Open();
  45 |                 using var reader = new StreamReader(stream);
  46 |                 var json = reader.ReadToEnd();
  47 | 
  48 |                 registry.LoadFromJson(json);
  49 |                 loaded.Add(Path.GetFileNameWithoutExtension(file));
  50 |             }
  51 |             catch
  52 |             {
  53 |                 // Skip invalid packs — don't crash the app.
  54 |             }
  55 |         }
  56 | 
  57 |         return loaded;
  58 |     }
  59 | }

--- src/MyGame.Core/Tooling/ReplayRecorder.cs (C#, 63 lines, ~508 tokens) ---
   1 | using System;
   2 | using System.Collections.Generic;
   3 | using System.IO;
   4 | using System.Text.Json;
   5 | using MyGame.Core.Saves;
   6 | using MyGame.Core.World;
   7 | 
   8 | using GameWorld = MyGame.Core.World.World;
   9 | 
  10 | namespace MyGame.Core.Tooling;
  11 | 
  12 | /// <summary>
  13 | /// Records each GM turn to a replay file. Issue #85. Local-only.
  14 | /// </summary>
  15 | public sealed class ReplayRecorder
  16 | {
  17 |     private readonly string _replayDir;
  18 |     private readonly string _saveId;
  19 |     private readonly List<ReplayTurn> _turns = new();
  20 | 
  21 |     public ReplayRecorder(string replayDir, string saveId)
  22 |     {
  23 |         _replayDir = replayDir;
  24 |         _saveId = saveId;
  25 |         Directory.CreateDirectory(_replayDir);
  26 |     }
  27 | 
  28 |     public void RecordTurn(int turnNumber, string action, string narrative, GameWorld world)
  29 |     {
  30 |         _turns.Add(new ReplayTurn
  31 |         {
  32 |             TurnNumber = turnNumber,
  33 |             Timestamp = DateTimeOffset.UtcNow,
  34 |             Action = action,
  35 |             Narrative = narrative,
  36 |             WorldSnapshot = world.ToJson(),
  37 |         });
  38 |     }
  39 | 
  40 |     public void Save()
  41 |     {
  42 |         if (_turns.Count == 0) return;
  43 |         var path = Path.Combine(_replayDir, $"{_saveId}.pathstone-replay");
  44 |         var json = JsonSerializer.Serialize(_turns, new JsonSerializerOptions { WriteIndented = false });
  45 |         File.WriteAllText(path, json);
  46 |     }
  47 | 
  48 |     public static List<ReplayTurn>? Load(string filePath)
  49 |     {
  50 |         try { return JsonSerializer.Deserialize<List<ReplayTurn>>(File.ReadAllText(filePath)); }
  51 |         catch { return null; }
  52 |     }
  53 | }
  54 | 
  55 | public sealed class ReplayTurn
  56 | {
  57 |     public int TurnNumber { get; set; }
  58 |     public DateTimeOffset Timestamp { get; set; }
  59 |     public string Action { get; set; } = "";
  60 |     public string Narrative { get; set; } = "";
  61 |     public string WorldSnapshot { get; set; } = "";
  62 | }

--- src/MyGame.Core/Tooling/UpdateChecker.cs (C#, 47 lines, ~549 tokens) ---
   1 | using System;
   2 | using System.Net.Http;
   3 | using System.Text.Json;
   4 | using System.Threading;
   5 | using System.Threading.Tasks;
   6 | 
   7 | namespace MyGame.Core.Tooling;
   8 | 
   9 | /// <summary>
  10 | /// Checks GitHub releases for a newer version. Issue #54.
  11 | /// Notifies the user that a new version is available with a link.
  12 | /// No auto-download/swap — manual download (appropriate for free project).
  13 | /// </summary>
  14 | public static class UpdateChecker
  15 | {
  16 |     private const string ApiUrl = "https://api.github.com/repos/GardenXsa/Pathstone/releases/latest";
  17 | 
  18 |     public static async Task<UpdateInfo?> CheckAsync(Version currentVersion, CancellationToken ct = default)
  19 |     {
  20 |         try
  21 |         {
  22 |             using var http = new HttpClient();
  23 |             http.DefaultRequestHeaders.UserAgent.ParseAdd("Pathstone-UpdateChecker/1.0");
  24 |             http.Timeout = TimeSpan.FromSeconds(10);
  25 |             var json = await http.GetStringAsync(ApiUrl, ct).ConfigureAwait(false);
  26 |             using var doc = JsonDocument.Parse(json);
  27 |             var tagName = doc.RootElement.GetProperty("tag_name").GetString();
  28 |             if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(tagName)) return null;
  29 |             var versionStr = tagName.TrimStart('v', 'V');
  30 |             if (!Version.TryParse(versionStr, out var latest)) return null;
  31 |             if (latest <= currentVersion) return null;
  32 |             var htmlUrl = doc.RootElement.GetProperty("html_url").GetString() ?? "";
  33 |             var body = doc.RootElement.TryGetProperty("body", out var bodyEl) ? bodyEl.GetString() ?? "" : "";
  34 |             return new UpdateInfo { Version = latest, TagName = tagName, ReleaseUrl = htmlUrl, ReleaseNotes = body };
  35 |         }
  36 |         catch { return null; }
  37 |     }
  38 | }
  39 | 
  40 | public sealed record UpdateInfo
  41 | {
  42 |     public required Version Version { get; init; }
  43 |     public required string TagName { get; init; }
  44 |     public required string ReleaseUrl { get; init; }
  45 |     public string ReleaseNotes { get; init; } = "";
  46 | }

--- src/MyGame.Core/World/Calendar.cs (C#, 420 lines, ~4,553 tokens) ---
   1 | using System.Text.Json;
   2 | using System.Text.Json.Serialization;
   3 | 
   4 | namespace MyGame.Core.World;
   5 | 
   6 | /// <summary>
   7 | /// In-game clock and calendar machinery.
   8 | ///
   9 | /// Port of <c>engine/calendar.ts</c>. The TS module kept a single monotonic
  10 | /// <c>clockMinutes</c> counter and a separate <see cref="CalendarSpec"/>
  11 | /// describing how to render that counter as a human date. This C# port
  12 | /// preserves both pieces:
  13 | ///  - <see cref="GameTime"/>: a SIMPLIFIED immutable record holding
  14 | ///    (Day, Hour, Minute) — used by the desktop engine as the primary
  15 | ///    in-world clock surface. <see cref="GameTime.Advance"/> returns a new
  16 | ///    <see cref="GameTime"/>; <see cref="GameTime.Start"/> is day 1, 08:00.
  17 | ///  - <see cref="CalendarSpec"/> + <see cref="Calendar.BreakDownClock"/>: the
  18 | ///    full rich calendar (year/era/months/seasons/weekdays) ported from the
  19 | ///    TS, so a world-builder can install a custom calendar and have it
  20 | ///    round-trip through saves. <see cref="Calendar.FormatClock"/> /
  21 | ///    <see cref="Calendar.FormatClockLong"/> render the rich form.
  22 | ///
  23 | /// The desktop World holds a <see cref="GameTime"/> for the simple display
  24 | /// ("День 3, 14:30") the spec calls for; the richer CalendarSpec machinery
  25 | /// is available for the future world-builder subsystem.
  26 | /// </summary>
  27 | public static class Calendar
  28 | {
  29 |     /// <summary>
  30 |     /// Total days in one year of a calendar (sum of month lengths). Returns
  31 |     /// 0 for an empty calendar (defensive — should not happen for valid data).
  32 |     /// </summary>
  33 |     public static int DaysPerYear(CalendarSpec cal) =>
  34 |         cal.Months.Sum(m => m.Days);
  35 | 
  36 |     /// <summary>
  37 |     /// Convert elapsed-minutes-since-start into a full calendar date
  38 |     /// breakdown. Walks forward from
  39 |     /// (yearStart, startMonth, startDay, startHour:startMinute) by the given
  40 |     /// number of minutes, distributing across days/months/years. Handles
  41 |     /// variable-length months and year rollover correctly.
  42 |     ///
  43 |     /// Port of <c>breakDownClock</c> from <c>calendar.ts</c>.
  44 |     /// </summary>
  45 |     public static ClockBreakdown BreakDownClock(CalendarSpec cal, long clockMinutes)
  46 |     {
  47 |         // Absolute minutes from the start-of-day anchor (00:00 of startDay).
  48 |         int startDayMinutes = cal.StartHour * 60 + cal.StartMinute;
  49 |         long totalMinutes = startDayMinutes + Math.Max(0, clockMinutes);
  50 | 
  51 |         long remainingDays = totalMinutes / 1440;
  52 |         long dayMinute = totalMinutes % 1440;
  53 |         int hour = (int)(dayMinute / 60);
  54 |         int minute = (int)(dayMinute % 60);
  55 | 
  56 |         int year = cal.YearStart;
  57 |         int monthIdx = Math.Max(0, Math.Min(cal.Months.Count - 1, cal.StartMonth));
  58 |         int day = cal.StartDay;
  59 | 
  60 |         // Walk forward through months/years until remainingDays is absorbed.
  61 |         while (remainingDays > 0)
  62 |         {
  63 |             int monthLen = cal.Months[monthIdx].Days;
  64 |             int daysLeftInMonth = monthLen - day;
  65 |             if (remainingDays < daysLeftInMonth)
  66 |             {
  67 |                 day += (int)remainingDays;
  68 |                 remainingDays = 0;
  69 |             }
  70 |             else
  71 |             {
  72 |                 remainingDays -= daysLeftInMonth;
  73 |                 monthIdx += 1;
  74 |                 if (monthIdx >= cal.Months.Count)
  75 |                 {
  76 |                     monthIdx = 0;
  77 |                     year += 1;
  78 |                 }
  79 |                 day = 1;
  80 |                 // Consume full months/years as long as remainingDays >= monthLen.
  81 |                 while (remainingDays > 0)
  82 |                 {
  83 |                     int len = cal.Months[monthIdx].Days;
  84 |                     if (remainingDays >= len)
  85 |                     {
  86 |                         remainingDays -= len;
  87 |                         monthIdx += 1;
  88 |                         if (monthIdx >= cal.Months.Count)
  89 |                         {
  90 |                             monthIdx = 0;
  91 |                             year += 1;
  92 |                         }
  93 |                     }
  94 |                     else
  95 |                     {
  96 |                         day += (int)remainingDays;
  97 |                         remainingDays = 0;
  98 |                     }
  99 |                 }
 100 |             }
 101 |         }
 102 | 
 103 |         // Season lookup.
 104 |         string? seasonName = null;
 105 |         if (cal.Seasons is { } seasons)
 106 |         {
 107 |             foreach (var s in seasons)
 108 |             {
 109 |                 if (s.Months.Contains(monthIdx))
 110 |                 {
 111 |                     seasonName = s.Name;
 112 |                     break;
 113 |                 }
 114 |             }
 115 |         }
 116 | 
 117 |         // Weekday.
 118 |         long totalDaysElapsed = (startDayMinutes + Math.Max(0, clockMinutes)) / 1440;
 119 |         string? weekDayName = null;
 120 |         if (cal.WeekDays is { } weekDays && weekDays.Count > 0)
 121 |         {
 122 |             int anchor = cal.StartWeekDayIndex ?? 0;
 123 |             int idx = (int)(((anchor + totalDaysElapsed) % weekDays.Count + weekDays.Count) % weekDays.Count);
 124 |             weekDayName = weekDays[idx];
 125 |         }
 126 | 
 127 |         return new ClockBreakdown(
 128 |             Year: year,
 129 |             MonthIndex: monthIdx,
 130 |             MonthName: cal.Months[monthIdx]?.Name ?? $"Месяц {monthIdx + 1}",
 131 |             Day: day,
 132 |             Hour: hour,
 133 |             Minute: minute,
 134 |             SeasonName: seasonName,
 135 |             WeekDayName: weekDayName,
 136 |             Era: cal.Era,
 137 |             TotalDays: totalDaysElapsed);
 138 |     }
 139 | 
 140 |     /// <summary>
 141 |     /// Render a clock instant as a compact, locale-flavoured string:
 142 |     /// <c>"1 Месяц Снегов 600 г., 08:00"</c>. Falls back to
 143 |     /// <see cref="DefaultFantasyCalendar"/> when <paramref name="cal"/> is null.
 144 |     /// </summary>
 145 |     public static string FormatClock(CalendarSpec? cal, long clockMinutes)
 146 |     {
 147 |         var c = cal ?? DefaultFantasyCalendar;
 148 |         var b = BreakDownClock(c, clockMinutes);
 149 |         return $"{b.Day} {b.MonthName} {b.Year} г., {b.Hour:D2}:{b.Minute:D2}";
 150 |     }
 151 | 
 152 |     /// <summary>Long form with weekday + era + season — for prompts / tooltips.</summary>
 153 |     public static string FormatClockLong(CalendarSpec? cal, long clockMinutes)
 154 |     {
 155 |         var c = cal ?? DefaultFantasyCalendar;
 156 |         var b = BreakDownClock(c, clockMinutes);
 157 |         var parts = new List<string>();
 158 |         if (b.WeekDayName is { } wd) parts.Add(wd + ",");
 159 |         parts.Add($"{b.Day} {b.MonthName} {b.Year} г.");
 160 |         if (b.Era is { } era) parts.Add($"({era})");
 161 |         parts.Add($"{b.Hour:D2}:{b.Minute:D2}");
 162 |         if (b.SeasonName is { } sn) parts.Add($"· {sn}");
 163 |         return string.Join(' ', parts);
 164 |     }
 165 | 
 166 |     /// <summary>
 167 |     /// Format a time-advancement divider label:
 168 |     /// <c>"⏱ прошло 30 мин · 14:30, Месяц Туманов, 600 г."</c>.
 169 |     /// </summary>
 170 |     public static string FormatTimeLabel(CalendarSpec? cal, long deltaMinutes, long totalMinutes)
 171 |     {
 172 |         var c = cal ?? DefaultFantasyCalendar;
 173 |         var b = BreakDownClock(c, totalMinutes);
 174 |         string dur;
 175 |         if (deltaMinutes >= 60)
 176 |         {
 177 |             long h = deltaMinutes / 60;
 178 |             long m = deltaMinutes % 60;
 179 |             dur = m > 0 ? $"{h} ч {m} мин" : $"{h} ч";
 180 |         }
 181 |         else
 182 |         {
 183 |             dur = $"{deltaMinutes} мин";
 184 |         }
 185 |         return $"⏱ прошло {dur} · {b.Hour:D2}:{b.Minute:D2}, {b.Day} {b.MonthName} {b.Year} г.";
 186 |     }
 187 | 
 188 |     /// <summary>
 189 |     /// Build a Gregorian-flavoured calendar for worlds set in the real /
 190 |     /// near-future world (Stalker, modern, post-apoc). Year start and era
 191 |     /// are caller-supplied. Port of <c>makeRealWorldCalendar</c>.
 192 |     /// </summary>
 193 |     public static CalendarSpec MakeRealWorldCalendar(
 194 |         int yearStart,
 195 |         string? era = null,
 196 |         int startMonth = 0,
 197 |         int startDay = 1,
 198 |         int startHour = 8,
 199 |         int startMinute = 0)
 200 |     {
 201 |         return new CalendarSpec
 202 |         {
 203 |             YearStart = yearStart,
 204 |             Era = era,
 205 |             Months = new List<CalendarMonth>
 206 |             {
 207 |                 new("Январь", 31),

 209 |                 new("Март", 31),
 210 |                 new("Апрель", 30),
 211 |                 new("Май", 31),
 212 |                 new("Июнь", 30),
 213 |                 new("Июль", 31),
 214 |                 new("Август", 31),
 215 |                 new("Сентябрь", 30),
 216 |                 new("Октябрь", 31),
 217 |                 new("Ноябрь", 30),
 218 |                 new("Декабрь", 31),
 219 |             },
 220 |             Seasons = new List<CalendarSeason>
 221 |             {
 222 |                 new("Зима", new[] { 11, 0, 1 }),
 223 |                 new("Весна", new[] { 2, 3, 4 }),
 224 |                 new("Лето", new[] { 5, 6, 7 }),
 225 |                 new("Осень", new[] { 8, 9, 10 }),
 226 |             },
 227 |             WeekDays = new List<string>
 228 |             {
 229 |                 "Понедельник", "Вторник", "Среда", "Четверг",
 230 |                 "Пятница", "Суббота", "Воскресенье",
 231 |             },
 232 |             StartMonth = startMonth,
 233 |             StartDay = startDay,
 234 |             StartHour = startHour,
 235 |             StartMinute = startMinute,
 236 |             StartWeekDayIndex = 0,
 237 |         };
 238 |     }

 284 | /// <summary>One month of a calendar: a display name + how many days it has.</summary>
 285 | public sealed record CalendarMonth(string Name, int Days);
