 290 | /// <summary>
 291 | /// Full calendar definition. Lives on the World, persisted in state.json,
 292 | /// installed (or overwritten) by the world-builder via <c>set_calendar</c>.
 293 | /// </summary>
 294 | public sealed record CalendarSpec
 295 | {
 296 |     /// <summary>Year number at game start (e.g. 2014 for Stalker, 600 for fantasy).</summary>
 297 |     public int YearStart { get; init; }
 298 | 
 299 |     /// <summary>Era label, e.g. "от Катастрофы", "Третья эпоха". Optional flavor.</summary>
 300 |     public string? Era { get; init; }
 301 | 
 302 |     /// <summary>Months in calendar order, starting from the first month of yearStart.</summary>
 303 |     public required IReadOnlyList<CalendarMonth> Months { get; init; }
 304 | 
 305 |     /// <summary>Seasons (optional; if absent, no season display).</summary>
 306 |     public IReadOnlyList<CalendarSeason>? Seasons { get; init; }
 307 | 
 308 |     /// <summary>Names of weekdays (optional). If absent, weekday isn't shown.</summary>
 309 |     public IReadOnlyList<string>? WeekDays { get; init; }
 310 | 
 311 |     /// <summary>Start month (0-based).</summary>
 312 |     public int StartMonth { get; init; }
 313 | 
 314 |     /// <summary>Start day-of-month (1-based).</summary>
 315 |     public int StartDay { get; init; } = 1;
 316 | 
 317 |     /// <summary>Start hour (0-23).</summary>
 318 |     public int StartHour { get; init; } = 8;
 319 | 
 320 |     /// <summary>Start minute (0-59).</summary>
 321 |     public int StartMinute { get; init; }
 322 | 
 323 |     /// <summary>
 324 |     /// Anchor index (0-based into WeekDays) for the weekday of
 325 |     /// (yearStart, startMonth, startDay). Lets the builder pin "day 1 was a
 326 |     /// Monday". Only meaningful when WeekDays is set. Defaults to 0.
 327 |     /// </summary>
 328 |     public int? StartWeekDayIndex { get; init; }
 329 | }
 330 | 
 331 | /// <summary>Rich, calendar-aware breakdown of an in-world instant.</summary>
 332 | public sealed record ClockBreakdown(
 333 |     int Year,
 334 |     int MonthIndex,
 335 |     string MonthName,
 336 |     int Day,
 337 |     int Hour,
 338 |     int Minute,
 339 |     string? SeasonName,
 340 |     string? WeekDayName,
 341 |     string? Era,
 342 |     long TotalDays);
 343 | 
 344 | /// <summary>
 345 | /// SIMPLIFIED in-game clock — the desktop engine's primary time surface.
 346 | ///
 347 | /// Immutable: <see cref="Advance(int)"/> returns a new <see cref="GameTime"/>.
 348 | /// The world holds one of these and replaces it on time advances (so the
 349 | /// clock is purely functional from the consumer's POV).
 350 | ///
 351 | /// Display format: <c>"День N, HH:MM"</c> (e.g. <c>"День 3, 14:30"</c>).
 352 | /// </summary>
 353 | public readonly record struct GameTime(int Day, int Hour, int Minute)
 354 | {
 355 |     /// <summary>Minutes per day — used by <see cref="Advance"/>.</summary>
 356 |     public const int MinutesPerDay = 24 * 60;
 357 | 
 358 |     /// <summary>The game's canonical start: day 1, 08:00.</summary>
 359 |     public static readonly GameTime Start = new(1, 8, 0);
 360 | 
 361 |     /// <summary>
 362 |     /// Return a new <see cref="GameTime"/> advanced by <paramref name="minutes"/>.
 363 |     /// Negative or zero deltas are clamped to no-op (the clock never moves
 364 |     /// backward; the GM only ever advances time forward).
 365 |     /// </summary>
 366 |     public GameTime Advance(int minutes)
 367 |     {
 368 |         if (minutes <= 0) return this;
 369 |         // Day is 1-based; convert to 0-based internally for arithmetic.
 370 |         long total = ((long)(Day - 1)) * MinutesPerDay + Hour * 60 + Minute + minutes;
 371 |         long day = total / MinutesPerDay;
 372 |         long remainder = total % MinutesPerDay;
 373 |         return new GameTime((int)day + 1, (int)(remainder / 60), (int)(remainder % 60));
 374 |     }
 375 | 
 376 |     /// <summary>Localized display: <c>"День N, HH:MM"</c>.</summary>
 377 |     public override string ToString() => $"День {Day}, {Hour:D2}:{Minute:D2}";
 378 | 
 379 |     /// <summary>
 380 |     /// Time-of-day label for the given hour (issue #35). Returns one of
 381 |     /// <c>"рассвет"</c> (5–7), <c>"утро"</c> (8–11), <c>"день"</c>
 382 |     /// (12–16), <c>"вечер"</c> (17–19), <c>"ночь"</c> (20–4). Used by
 383 |     /// the GM context block to flag day/night mechanical effects
 384 |     /// (night = disadvantage Perception without darkvision, increased
 385 |     /// random-encounter chance) and by the desktop top-bar clock to
 386 |     /// show «День 3, 14:00 — день».
 387 |     /// </summary>
 388 |     public static string GetTimeOfDayLabel(int hour) => hour switch
 389 |     {
 390 |         >= 5 and < 8 => "рассвет",
 391 |         >= 8 and < 12 => "утро",
 392 |         >= 12 and < 17 => "день",
 393 |         >= 17 and < 20 => "вечер",
 394 |         >= 20 or < 5 => "ночь",
 395 |     };
 396 | 
 397 |     /// <summary>
 398 |     /// True when the given hour is night-time (issue #35): hour &gt;= 20
 399 |     /// or hour &lt; 5. At night, the GM context warns the model about
 400 |     /// Perception disadvantage and the random-encounter chance is
 401 |     /// increased (see <c>GameViewModel.MaybeRandomEncounter</c>).
 402 |     /// </summary>
 403 |     public static bool IsNight(int hour) => hour >= 20 || hour < 5;
 404 | 
 405 |     /// <summary>
 406 |     /// Time-of-day label for this clock instant. Convenience over
 407 |     /// <see cref="GetTimeOfDayLabel(int)"/> so callers don't have to
 408 |     /// pull <see cref="Hour"/> out themselves.
 409 |     /// </summary>
 410 |     public string TimeOfDayLabel() => GetTimeOfDayLabel(Hour);
 411 | 
 412 |     /// <summary>
 413 |     /// Display string with time-of-day label appended:
 414 |     /// <c>"День N, HH:MM — {label}"</c> (e.g. <c>"День 3, 14:00 — день"</c>).
 415 |     /// Used by the desktop top-bar clock so the player sees at a glance
 416 |     /// whether it's day or night.
 417 |     /// </summary>
 418 |     public string ToDisplayWithTimeOfDay() => $"{ToString()} — {TimeOfDayLabel()}";
 419 | }

--- src/MyGame.Core/World/CombatState.cs (C#, 80 lines, ~915 tokens) ---
   1 | using MyGame.Core.Common;
   2 | 
   3 | namespace MyGame.Core.World;
   4 | 
   5 | /// <summary>
   6 | /// Structured combat state — the round/turn/initiative tracker for an
   7 | /// in-progress encounter. Lives on <see cref="World.Combat"/> (nullable):
   8 | /// when null (or <see cref="Active"/> = false) the game is in freeform
   9 | /// mode and the GM is freeform-narrating; when active, the GM must
  10 | /// respect the turn order exposed in <see cref="TurnOrder"/>.
  11 | ///
  12 | /// <para>
  13 | /// The state machine is intentionally minimal: it tracks whose turn it
  14 | /// is, but the GM (driven via tools) is still responsible for the actual
  15 | /// attack / damage / status calls. The <see cref="GameMaster"/> surfaces
  16 | /// a "## БОЙ" block in the system-prompt world-state so the model knows
  17 | /// whose turn it is and doesn't act out of order.
  18 | /// </para>
  19 | ///
  20 | /// <para>
  21 | /// <b>Serialization:</b> plain POCO + System.Text.Json (via
  22 | /// <see cref="WorldJson.Options"/>). Round-trips cleanly through save/load
  23 | /// because <see cref="Combatant.EntityId"/> uses the
  24 | /// <see cref="EntityIdJsonConverter"/> (bare JSON string).
  25 | /// </para>
  26 | /// </summary>
  27 | public sealed class CombatState
  28 | {
  29 |     /// <summary>
  30 |     /// Whether combat is currently in progress. Theoretically redundant
  31 |     /// with <see cref="World.Combat"/> being non-null, but kept as an
  32 |     /// explicit flag so the GM context block can short-circuit on it
  33 |     /// without a null-check, and so a paused / ended state can be
  34 |     /// expressed without throwing away the turn order.
  35 |     /// </summary>
  36 |     public bool Active { get; set; }
  37 | 
  38 |     /// <summary>
  39 |     /// Current round number (1-based). Incremented when
  40 |     /// <see cref="CurrentActorIndex"/> wraps past the end of
  41 |     /// <see cref="TurnOrder"/>.
  42 |     /// </summary>
  43 |     public int Round { get; set; } = 1;
  44 | 
  45 |     /// <summary>
  46 |     /// Ordered list of combatants in initiative order (highest first).
  47 |     /// Dead combatants are removed from this list as they fall, so the
  48 |     /// index math stays simple.
  49 |     /// </summary>
  50 |     public List<Combatant> TurnOrder { get; set; } = new();
  51 | 
  52 |     /// <summary>
  53 |     /// Index into <see cref="TurnOrder"/> for the combatant whose turn it
  54 |     /// currently is. The <c>next_turn</c> tool advances this (wrapping
  55 |     /// around + incrementing <see cref="Round"/>).
  56 |     /// </summary>
  57 |     public int CurrentActorIndex { get; set; }
  58 | 
  59 |     /// <summary>
  60 |     /// World turn counter (<see cref="World.Turn"/>) snapshot at the
  61 |     /// moment combat started — informational, so the UI can show "бой
  62 |     /// длится N ходов" if desired.
  63 |     /// </summary>
  64 |     public long StartedAtTurn { get; set; }
  65 | }
  66 | 
  67 | /// <summary>
  68 | /// One entry in <see cref="CombatState.TurnOrder"/>. Records the entity
  69 | /// (player or NPC), their display name (snapshotted at combat start so
  70 | /// renames mid-combat don't desync the UI), their rolled initiative, and
  71 | /// whether they've already acted this round.
  72 | ///
  73 | /// <para>
  74 | /// A <c>record</c> (immutable snapshot) — the engine replaces entries
  75 | /// rather than mutating them, so the <c>HasActedThisRound</c> flag
  76 | /// updates as a new Combatant value with the same EntityId.
  77 | /// </para>
  78 | /// </summary>
  79 | public sealed record Combatant(EntityId EntityId, string Name, int Initiative, bool HasActedThisRound);

--- src/MyGame.Core/World/Content/ContentRegistry.cs (C#, 295 lines, ~3,100 tokens) ---
   1 | using System.Reflection;
   2 | using System.Text.Json;
   3 | using MyGame.Core.World.Entities;
   4 | 
   5 | namespace MyGame.Core.World.Content;
   6 | 
   7 | /// <summary>
   8 | /// NPC template — a static definition the world-builder / GM uses to spawn
   9 | /// NPCs with consistent attributes, equipment, and starting inventory.
  10 | /// Port of <c>NPCTemplate</c> from <c>engine/content/registry.ts</c>.
  11 | /// </summary>
  12 | public sealed record NpcTemplate
  13 | {
  14 |     /// <summary>Stable template id (e.g. <c>npc_tavern_keeper</c>).</summary>
  15 |     public required string Id { get; init; }
  16 | 
  17 |     /// <summary>Display name.</summary>
  18 |     public required string Name { get; init; }
  19 | 
  20 |     /// <summary>Optional flavor label (race / species).</summary>
  21 |     public string? Race { get; init; }
  22 | 
  23 |     /// <summary>Optional flavor label (class / archetype).</summary>
  24 |     public string? Class { get; init; }
  25 | 
  26 |     /// <summary>Level override (default = 1).</summary>
  27 |     public int? Level { get; init; }
  28 | 
  29 |     /// <summary>Attribute values keyed by ruleset attribute key.</summary>
  30 |     public required IReadOnlyDictionary<string, int> Attributes { get; init; }
  31 | 
  32 |     /// <summary>
  33 |     /// Optional explicit resource pools keyed by ruleset resource key. Any
  34 |     /// resource omitted here is derived from the ruleset's MaxFormula at
  35 |     /// spawn time.
  36 |     /// </summary>
  37 |     public IReadOnlyDictionary<string, int>? Resources { get; init; }
  38 | 
  39 |     /// <summary>Movement speed override.</summary>
  40 |     public int? Speed { get; init; }
  41 | 
  42 |     /// <summary>Default disposition toward the player.</summary>
  43 |     public string? Disposition { get; init; }
  44 | 
  45 |     /// <summary>AI GM behavior hint (<c>aggressive</c>, <c>merchant</c>, …).</summary>
  46 |     public required string Behavior { get; init; }
  47 | 
  48 |     /// <summary>Equipment template ids keyed by ruleset slot (weapon/armor/shield/…).</summary>
  49 |     public IReadOnlyDictionary<string, string>? Equipment { get; init; }
  50 | 
  51 |     /// <summary>Merchant inventory template ids (if merchant).</summary>
  52 |     public IReadOnlyList<string>? ShopInventory { get; init; }
  53 | 
  54 |     /// <summary>Default spawn inventory (template ids + quantities).</summary>
  55 |     public IReadOnlyList<TemplateStack>? StartingInventory { get; init; }
  56 | 
  57 |     /// <summary>Flavor description.</summary>
  58 |     public string? Description { get; init; }
  59 | 
  60 |     /// <summary>Aggro radius (ft) for hostile NPCs.</summary>
  61 |     public int? AggroRange { get; init; }
  62 | }
  63 | 
  64 | /// <summary>
  65 | /// A "stack" entry in an NPC template's starting inventory: a template id
  66 | /// and a quantity. Port of the inline shape from
  67 | /// <c>engine/content/registry.ts</c>.
  68 | /// </summary>
  69 | public sealed record TemplateStack(string TemplateId, int Quantity);
  70 | 
  71 | /// <summary>
  72 | /// Building template — a static definition used to spawn buildings with
  73 | /// consistent type/name/occupants. Port of <c>BuildingTemplate</c> from
  74 | /// <c>engine/content/registry.ts</c>.
  75 | /// </summary>
  76 | public sealed record BuildingTemplate
  77 | {
  78 |     /// <summary>Stable template id (e.g. <c>bld_tavern</c>).</summary>
  79 |     public required string Id { get; init; }
  80 | 
  81 |     /// <summary>Building type (ruleset-defined).</summary>
  82 |     public required string Type { get; init; }
  83 | 
  84 |     /// <summary>Display name.</summary>
  85 |     public required string Name { get; init; }
  86 | 
  87 |     /// <summary>Flavor text.</summary>
  88 |     public required string Description { get; init; }
  89 | 
  90 |     /// <summary>Whether the player can enter the building.</summary>
  91 |     public bool Enterable { get; init; }
  92 | 
  93 |     /// <summary>Whether the building starts locked.</summary>
  94 |     public bool? Locked { get; init; }
  95 | 
  96 |     /// <summary>NPC template ids that should occupy this building.</summary>
  97 |     public IReadOnlyList<string>? OccupantTemplateIds { get; init; }
  98 | 
  99 |     /// <summary>Shopkeeper NPC template id (if this building is a shop).</summary>
 100 |     public string? ShopkeeperTemplateId { get; init; }
 101 | }
 102 | 
 103 | /// <summary>
 104 | /// Aggregate content pack — the JSON shape loaded from the embedded
 105 | /// <c>data.json</c> resource. Port of <c>ContentPack</c> from
 106 | /// <c>engine/content/registry.ts</c>.
 107 | /// </summary>
 108 | public sealed class ContentPack
 109 | {
 110 |     /// <summary>Item templates.</summary>
 111 |     public List<ItemTemplate> Items { get; set; } = new();
 112 | 
 113 |     /// <summary>NPC templates.</summary>
 114 |     public List<NpcTemplate> Npcs { get; set; } = new();
 115 | 
 116 |     /// <summary>Building templates.</summary>
 117 |     public List<BuildingTemplate> Buildings { get; set; } = new();
 118 | 
 119 |     /// <summary>Crafting recipes (issue #65).</summary>
 120 |     public List<CraftingRecipe> Recipes { get; set; } = new();
 121 | }
 122 | 
 123 | // ─── Registries ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 124 | 
 125 | /// <summary>
 126 | /// Registry of <see cref="ItemTemplate"/>s, keyed by template id. Port of
 127 | /// <c>ItemRegistry</c> from <c>engine/content/registry.ts</c>.
 128 | /// </summary>
 129 | public sealed class ItemRegistry
 130 | {
 131 |     private readonly Dictionary<string, ItemTemplate> _items = new();
 132 | 
 133 |     /// <summary>Register a template (overwrites existing).</summary>
 134 |     public void Register(ItemTemplate template) => _items[template.Id] = template;
 135 | 
 136 |     /// <summary>Register many templates.</summary>
 137 |     public void RegisterAll(IEnumerable<ItemTemplate> templates)
 138 |     {
 139 |         foreach (var t in templates) Register(t);
 140 |     }
 141 | 
 142 |     /// <summary>Lookup by id. Null if not present.</summary>
 143 |     public ItemTemplate? Get(string id) => _items.TryGetValue(id, out var v) ? v : null;
 144 | 
 145 |     /// <summary>All registered templates.</summary>
 146 |     public IReadOnlyList<ItemTemplate> All() => _items.Values.ToList();
 147 | 
 148 |     /// <summary>All templates of a given category.</summary>
 149 |     public IReadOnlyList<ItemTemplate> ByCategory(string category) =>
 150 |         _items.Values.Where(t => t.Category == category).ToList();
 151 | }
 152 | 
 153 | /// <summary>
 154 | /// Registry of <see cref="NpcTemplate"/>s, keyed by template id.
 155 | /// </summary>
 156 | public sealed class NpcRegistry
 157 | {
 158 |     private readonly Dictionary<string, NpcTemplate> _templates = new();
 159 | 
 160 |     /// <summary>Register a template (overwrites existing).</summary>
 161 |     public void Register(NpcTemplate t) => _templates[t.Id] = t;
 162 | 
 163 |     /// <summary>Register many templates.</summary>
 164 |     public void RegisterAll(IEnumerable<NpcTemplate> list)
 165 |     {
 166 |         foreach (var t in list) Register(t);
 167 |     }
 168 | 
 169 |     /// <summary>Lookup by id. Null if not present.</summary>
 170 |     public NpcTemplate? Get(string id) => _templates.TryGetValue(id, out var v) ? v : null;
 171 | 
 172 |     /// <summary>All registered templates.</summary>
 173 |     public IReadOnlyList<NpcTemplate> All() => _templates.Values.ToList();
 174 | }
 175 | 
 176 | /// <summary>
 177 | /// Registry of <see cref="BuildingTemplate"/>s, keyed by template id.
 178 | /// </summary>
 179 | public sealed class BuildingRegistry
 180 | {
 181 |     private readonly Dictionary<string, BuildingTemplate> _templates = new();
 182 | 
 183 |     /// <summary>Register a template (overwrites existing).</summary>
 184 |     public void Register(BuildingTemplate t) => _templates[t.Id] = t;
 185 | 
 186 |     /// <summary>Register many templates.</summary>
 187 |     public void RegisterAll(IEnumerable<BuildingTemplate> list)
 188 |     {
 189 |         foreach (var t in list) Register(t);
 190 |     }
 191 | 
 192 |     /// <summary>Lookup by id. Null if not present.</summary>
 193 |     public BuildingTemplate? Get(string id) => _templates.TryGetValue(id, out var v) ? v : null;
 194 | 
 195 |     /// <summary>All registered templates.</summary>
 196 |     public IReadOnlyList<BuildingTemplate> All() => _templates.Values.ToList();
 197 | }
 198 | 
 199 | /// <summary>
 200 | /// The aggregate content registry — owns an <see cref="ItemRegistry"/>,
 201 | /// <see cref="NpcRegistry"/>, and <see cref="BuildingRegistry"/>. Loads
 202 | /// them from an embedded <c>Content/data.json</c> resource on first access
 203 | /// (or on explicit <see cref="Load"/>).
 204 | ///
 205 | /// Port of <c>createRegistries</c> / <c>Registries</c> from
 206 | /// <c>engine/content/registry.ts</c>.
 207 | /// </summary>
 208 | public sealed class ContentRegistry
 209 | {
 210 |     private static readonly Assembly Assembly = typeof(ContentRegistry).Assembly;
 211 |     private const string EmbeddedResourceName = "MyGame.Core.World.Content.data.json";
 212 | 
 213 |     /// <summary>Item templates.</summary>
 214 |     public ItemRegistry Items { get; } = new();
 215 | 
 216 |     /// <summary>NPC templates.</summary>
 217 |     public NpcRegistry Npcs { get; } = new();
 218 | 
 219 |     /// <summary>Building templates.</summary>
 220 |     public BuildingRegistry Buildings { get; } = new();
 221 | 
 222 |     /// <summary>Crafting recipes (issue #65).</summary>
 223 |     public RecipeRegistry Recipes { get; } = new();
 224 | 
 225 |     /// <summary>
 226 |     /// Load a <see cref="ContentPack"/> into this registry, replacing any
 227 |     /// prior content with the same template id.
 228 |     /// </summary>
 229 |     public void Load(ContentPack? pack)
 230 |     {
 231 |         if (pack is null) return;
 232 |         Items.RegisterAll(pack.Items);
 233 |         Npcs.RegisterAll(pack.Npcs);
 234 |         Buildings.RegisterAll(pack.Buildings);
 235 |         Recipes.RegisterAll(pack.Recipes);
 236 |     }
 237 | 
 238 |     /// <summary>
 239 |     /// Load content from a JSON string. The JSON shape must match
 240 |     /// <see cref="ContentPack"/>. Returns this registry for chaining.
 241 |     /// </summary>
 242 |     public ContentRegistry LoadFromJson(string json)
 243 |     {
 244 |         if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(json)) return this;
 245 |         var pack = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<ContentPack>(json, WorldJson.Options)
 246 |                    ?? new ContentPack();
 247 |         Load(pack);
 248 |         return this;
 249 |     }
 250 | 
 251 |     /// <summary>
 252 |     /// Load content from the embedded <c>Content/data.json</c> resource.
 253 |     /// Returns this registry for chaining. Throws if the resource is missing
 254 |     /// or malformed — that's a build-time error, not a runtime one.
 255 |     /// </summary>
 256 |     public ContentRegistry LoadEmbedded()
 257 |     {
 258 |         using var stream = Assembly.GetManifestResourceStream(EmbeddedResourceName)
 259 |             ?? throw new InvalidOperationException(
 260 |                 $"Embedded content resource not found: {EmbeddedResourceName}. " +
 261 |                 "Ensure Content/data.json is configured as an EmbeddedResource in the csproj.");
 262 |         using var reader = new StreamReader(stream);
 263 |         var json = reader.ReadToEnd();
 264 |         return LoadFromJson(json);
 265 |     }
 266 | 
 267 |     /// <summary>
 268 |     /// Convenience factory: build a fresh registry loaded with the embedded
 269 |     /// default content pack. Used by <c>DefaultWorldFactory</c>.
 270 |     /// </summary>
 271 |     public static ContentRegistry LoadDefault()
 272 |     {
 273 |         var reg = new ContentRegistry();
 274 |         reg.LoadEmbedded();
 275 |         return reg;
 276 |     }
 277 | }
 278 | 
 279 | /// <summary>
 280 | /// Registry of crafting recipes. Issue #65.
 281 | /// </summary>
 282 | public sealed class RecipeRegistry
 283 | {
 284 |     private readonly Dictionary<string, CraftingRecipe> _recipes = new(StringComparer.Ordinal);
 285 | 
 286 |     public void Register(CraftingRecipe recipe) => _recipes[recipe.Id] = recipe;
 287 |     public void RegisterAll(IEnumerable<CraftingRecipe> list)
 288 |     {
 289 |         if (list is null) return;
 290 |         foreach (var r in list) Register(r);
 291 |     }
 292 |     public CraftingRecipe? Get(string id) => _recipes.TryGetValue(id, out var v) ? v : null;
 293 |     public IReadOnlyList<CraftingRecipe> All() => _recipes.Values.ToList();
 294 | }

--- src/MyGame.Core/World/DefaultWorld.cs (C#, 411 lines, ~5,624 tokens) ---
   1 | using MyGame.Core.Common;
   2 | using MyGame.Core.World.Content;
   3 | using MyGame.Core.World.Entities;
   4 | 
   5 | namespace MyGame.Core.World;
   6 | 
   7 | /// <summary>
   8 | /// Factory that creates a fresh starting <see cref="World"/> for the "New
   9 | /// Game" flow.
  10 | ///
  11 | /// Port of <c>engine/content/defaultWorld.ts</c>. The TS file is 900+ lines
  12 | /// and builds «Эльдрион» — a continent-sized hand-authored world with 5
  13 | /// regions, hundreds of NPCs, and procedural-generation hooks. That's
  14 | /// tightly coupled to the AI planner (<c>WorldPlan</c> types) and the
  15 | /// procedural engine (<c>procedural.ts</c>) which don't exist in the C# port
  16 | /// yet. Per the task spec ("If a TS file is huge (&gt;500 lines) or has
  17 | /// features clearly tied to the Next.js app, SKIP those parts"), the
  18 | /// C# port instead builds a SMALL hand-authored starting scene — the
  19 | /// classic «Долина Туманов» village + a few surrounding locations,
  20 | /// populated from the content pack in <c>Content/data.json</c>.
  21 | ///
  22 | /// This is enough for the desktop app's MVP "New Game" experience: the
  23 | /// player wakes up in a tavern village, can explore the surrounding forest
  24 | /// and caves, talk to a few NPCs, and pick up a starting quest. The AI
  25 | /// world-builder subsystem (a later task) replaces this with bespoke worlds.
  26 | /// </summary>
  27 | public static class DefaultWorld
  28 | {
  29 |     /// <summary>
  30 |     /// Create a fresh starting World. The seed defaults to a fixed value
  31 |     /// (12345) so the same New Game always produces the same starting scene;
  32 |     /// callers can override it for "random New Game".
  33 |     /// </summary>
  34 |     public static World Create(long seed = 12345)
  35 |     {
  36 |         var registries = ContentRegistry.LoadDefault();
  37 |         var ruleset = Rulesets.DefaultDnd;
  38 |         var calendar = Calendar.DefaultFantasyCalendar;
  39 | 
  40 |         var world = new World
  41 |         {
  42 |             Seed = seed,
  43 |             Rng = new Rng(seed),
  44 |             Clock = GameTime.Start,
  45 |             CalendarSpec = calendar,
  46 |             Ruleset = ruleset,
  47 |             Turn = 0,
  48 |         };
  49 |         // Wire the content registry onto the world so SpawnNpcFromTemplate
  50 |         // can look up templates. (The World.Registries property defaults to
  51 |         // an empty registry when unset, so without this line the spawns
  52 |         // would silently no-op.)
  53 |         world.Registries = registries;
  54 | 
  55 |         // ─── Locations (small village + surrounding wilderness) ────────────
  56 |         //
  57 |         // The valley is laid out on a coarse integer grid with the village
  58 |         // at (0,0). The existing 6 locations form a small cross; the new
  59 |         // 8 add a southern road + cemetery, an eastern crossroads hub with
  60 |         // a farm, a western watchtower + forest lake + mountain pass, and
  61 |         // an eastern old mine off the cave. Every connection is cardinal
  62 |         // (север/юг/восток/запад/вглубь/наружу) so the Connect helper's
  63 |         // reverse-direction switch covers it.
  64 |         var village = NewLocation("Деревня Туманная",
  65 |             "Небольшая деревня у реки, окружённая туманными лесами. Дымок из труб, лай собак, скрип колодезного журавля.",
  66 |             "village", 0, 0, danger: 0);
  67 |         var forest = NewLocation("Северный Лес",
  68 |             "Густой хвойный лес. Туман здесь гуще, а под ногами хрустят то ветки, то кости.",
  69 |             "forest", 0, 1, danger: 2);
  70 |         var river = NewLocation("Брод через Туманную",
  71 |             "Мелководный брод через реку Туманную. Вода холодная, течение быстрое, на том берегу видны камни старого тракта.",
  72 |             "coast", 1, 0, danger: 1);
  73 |         var ruins = NewLocation("Древние руины",
  74 |             "Обломки каменной кладки, поросшие мхом. Под ними, по слухам, скрывается гробница первых жителей долины.",
  75 |             "ruin", 0, -1, danger: 4);
  76 |         var cave = NewLocation("Вход в пещеру",
  77 |             "Тёмный провал в скале, из которого тянет холодом и сыростью. На камнях у входа — старые кости и следы когтей.",
  78 |             "cave", 0, 2, danger: 5);
  79 |         var deepCave = NewLocation("Глубокая пещера",
  80 |             "Сырой извилистый туннель, уходящий в недра холма. Где-то в темноте журчит вода и слышно дыхание чего-то крупного.",
  81 |             "underground", 0, 3, danger: 7);
  82 | 
  83 |         // New: surrounding wilderness ring (8 locations, 14 total).
  84 |         var tower = NewLocation("Сторожевая башня",
  85 |             "Старая каменная башня на западном холме. Деревянный настил её верхней площадки ещё держит, и с него видно всю Долину Туманов — от перевала до пещер.",
  86 |             "landmark", -1, 0, danger: 0);
  87 |         var lake = NewLocation("Лесное озеро",
  88 |             "Тихий разлив ручья среди елей. Вода чёрная, гладкая, и иногда по ней бегут круги — не от ветра. На берегу — хижина отшельника.",
  89 |             "coast", -1, 1, danger: 1);
  90 |         var crossroads = NewLocation("Развилка",
  91 |             "Перекрёсток трёх дорог: на север — к деревне, на юг — к ферме, на запад — к руинам. Покосившийся дорожный столб, заросший мхом.",
  92 |             "road", 1, -1, danger: 0);
  93 |         var farm = NewLocation("Заброшенная ферма",
  94 |             "Дряхлая изба с провалившейся крышей и заросшее бурьяном поле. На заборе сохнет тряпка — то ли память о хозяине, то ли сигнал разбойникам.",
  95 |             "farmland", 1, -2, danger: 2);
  96 |         var southRoad = NewLocation("Тракт на юг",
  97 |             "Старая мощёная дорога, уходящая на юг мимо кладбища. Камни местами выворочены, но путь ещё различим.",
  98 |             "road", 0, -2, danger: 1);
  99 |         var cemetery = NewLocation("Старое кладбище",
 100 |             "Покосившиеся надгробия, склеп с ржавой решёткой, чья-то свежая яма. По ночам здесь, говорят, танцуют бледные огни.",
 101 |             "ruin", 0, -3, danger: 3);
 102 |         var pass = NewLocation("Перевал",
 103 |             "Каменистая тропа, вьющаяся между скал к западу от руин. Ветер здесь злой и холодный — он несёт запах снежных вершин.",
 104 |             "mountain", -1, -1, danger: 3);
 105 |         var oldMine = NewLocation("Старая шахта",
 106 |             "Брошенный наклонник с гнилыми крепями. В тьме поблёскивает слюда — и чьи-то глаза. Из глубины доносится стук кирки, хотя никого живого тут не осталось.",
 107 |             "underground", 1, 2, danger: 4);
 108 | 
 109 |         // Connect them (bidirectional).
 110 |         // Existing cross:
 111 |         Connect(village, forest, "север");
 112 |         Connect(village, river, "восток");
 113 |         Connect(village, ruins, "юг");
 114 |         Connect(forest, cave, "север");
 115 |         Connect(cave, deepCave, "вглубь");
 116 |         ruins.Exits.Add(new LocationExit { To = ruins.Id, Direction = "вниз (locked)", Locked = true });
 117 | 
 118 |         // New connections (cardinal — see Connect helper for reverse-dir
 119 |         // switch). All exits are unique per-location so the WorldPanel
 120 |         // can list them without ambiguity.
 121 |         Connect(village, tower, "запад");         // village ↔ tower
 122 |         Connect(forest, lake, "запад");           // forest  ↔ lake
 123 |         Connect(cave, oldMine, "восток");         // cave    ↔ oldMine
 124 |         Connect(ruins, crossroads, "восток");     // ruins   ↔ crossroads
 125 |         Connect(crossroads, farm, "юг");          // xroads  ↔ farm
 126 |         Connect(ruins, southRoad, "юг");          // ruins   ↔ southRoad
 127 |         Connect(southRoad, cemetery, "юг");       // sRoad   ↔ cemetery
 128 |         Connect(ruins, pass, "запад");            // ruins   ↔ pass
 129 | 
 130 |         world.AddLocation(village);
 131 |         world.AddLocation(forest);
 132 |         world.AddLocation(river);
 133 |         world.AddLocation(ruins);
 134 |         world.AddLocation(cave);
 135 |         world.AddLocation(deepCave);
 136 |         world.AddLocation(tower);
 137 |         world.AddLocation(lake);
 138 |         world.AddLocation(crossroads);
 139 |         world.AddLocation(farm);
 140 |         world.AddLocation(southRoad);
 141 |         world.AddLocation(cemetery);
 142 |         world.AddLocation(pass);
 143 |         world.AddLocation(oldMine);
 144 | 
 145 |         village.Visited = true;
 146 |         village.Discovered = true;
 147 |         forest.Discovered = true;   // visible on the map but not yet visited
 148 |         tower.Discovered = true;    // seen from the village square
 149 |         river.Discovered = true;    // the ford is visible from the village
 150 |         southRoad.Discovered = true;// the southern road is visible from the village
 151 | 
 152 |         // ─── Buildings in the village ──────────────────────────────────────
 153 |         SpawnBuildingFromTemplate(world, "bld_tavern", village.Id, registries);
 154 |         SpawnBuildingFromTemplate(world, "bld_smithy", village.Id, registries);
 155 |         SpawnBuildingFromTemplate(world, "bld_temple", village.Id, registries);
 156 |         SpawnBuildingFromTemplate(world, "bld_guard_tower", village.Id, registries);
 157 | 
 158 |         // Watchtower (a separate landmark location): a small fortress
 159 |         // building with a guard occupant. The guard gives the
 160 |         // "Зачистить шахту" quest below.
 161 |         SpawnBuildingFromTemplate(world, "bld_guard_tower", tower.Id, registries);
 162 | 
 163 |         // ─── NPCs at locations ─────────────────────────────────────────────
 164 |         // Village elder — quest giver.
 165 |         world.SpawnNpcFromTemplate("npc_village_elder", village.Id);
 166 |         // Tavern keeper, blacksmith, priest — spawned via building occupants.
 167 |         // (SpawnBuildingFromTemplate above already spawned them and wired them
 168 |         // to their buildings; the spawn helper also adds them to the village
 169 |         // location's NPC list.)
 170 | 
 171 |         // Wilderness: a wolf pack in the forest.
 172 |         world.SpawnNpcFromTemplate("npc_wolf", forest.Id);
 173 |         world.SpawnNpcFromTemplate("npc_wolf", forest.Id);
 174 | 
 175 |         // Cave: goblins.
 176 |         world.SpawnNpcFromTemplate("npc_goblin", cave.Id);
 177 |         world.SpawnNpcFromTemplate("npc_goblin", cave.Id);
 178 |         world.SpawnNpcFromTemplate("npc_goblin", deepCave.Id);
 179 | 
 180 |         // Ruins: a ghost (carrying the warden letter quest item).
 181 |         world.SpawnNpcFromTemplate("npc_ghost", ruins.Id);
 182 | 
 183 |         // Deep cave: an owlbear cub.
 184 |         world.SpawnNpcFromTemplate("npc_owl_bear_cub", deepCave.Id);
 185 | 
 186 |         // ─── New NPCs at the expanded locations ────────────────────────────
 187 |         //
 188 |         // Watchtower: a town guard (already spawned as the building's
 189 |         // occupant above — the guard tower template lists npc_town_guard
 190 |         // as an occupant). Find them and store the id for the quest giver
 191 |         // field below. (The guard is at the tower location, not the
 192 |         // village — the village's bld_guard_tower also spawned a guard,
 193 |         // but we want the tower one for this quest.)
 194 |         var towerGuard = world.Npcs.FirstOrDefault(n =>
 195 |             n.LocationId == tower.Id && n.TemplateId == "npc_town_guard");
 196 | 
 197 |         // Lake: a wandering merchant by the water (the closest thing to
 198 |         // a "hermit" the content pack offers — a lone traveler camped
 199 |         // out by the lake). Gives the "Найти пропавшего караванщика"
 200 |         // quest below.
 201 |         var lakeHermit = world.SpawnNpcFromTemplate("npc_merchant_traveler", lake.Id);
 202 | 
 203 |         // Crossroads: a second merchant (the merchant's colleague, asking
 204 |         // passers-by if they've seen the missing caravan).
 205 |         // — Actually to keep quest-giver discovery simple, the same lake
 206 |         // hermit gives the caravan quest. No NPC here; the crossroads is
 207 |         // a narrative anchor only.
 208 | 
 209 |         // Farm: a bandit ambush.
 210 |         world.SpawnNpcFromTemplate("npc_bandit", farm.Id);
 211 |         world.SpawnNpcFromTemplate("npc_bandit", farm.Id);
 212 | 
 213 |         // Cemetery: an undead (a ghost — same template, different
 214 |         // location, suggests "the dead walk here too").
 215 |         world.SpawnNpcFromTemplate("npc_ghost", cemetery.Id);
 216 | 
 217 |         // Old mine: goblins + an owlbear (a tougher monster for the
 218 |         // "Зачистить шахту" quest target).
 219 |         world.SpawnNpcFromTemplate("npc_goblin", oldMine.Id);
 220 |         world.SpawnNpcFromTemplate("npc_goblin", oldMine.Id);
 221 |         world.SpawnNpcFromTemplate("npc_owl_bear_cub", oldMine.Id);
 222 | 
 223 |         // Mountain pass: a wolf (mountain wolf).
 224 |         world.SpawnNpcFromTemplate("npc_wolf", pass.Id);
 225 | 
 226 |         // ─── Quests ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 227 |         //
 228 |         // Existing: "Потерянный амулет" — given by the village elder.
 229 |         // New: "Зачистить шахту" — given by the watchtower guard.
 230 |         // New: "Найти пропавшего караванщика" — given by the lake hermit.
 231 | 
 232 |         // Quest 1: Lost amulet (existing — keep).
 233 |         var amuletItem = registries.Items.Get("qst_lost_amulet");
 234 |         var quest = new Quest
 235 |         {
 236 |             Id = EntityId.NewId(),
 237 |             Name = "Потерянный амулет",
 238 |             Description = "Старейшина Олдрин просит найти фамильный амулет, украденный гоблинами из пещеры.",
 239 |             GiverNpcId = world.Npcs.First(n => n.TemplateId == "npc_village_elder").Id,
 240 |             Status = QuestStatus.Active,
 241 |             Objectives = new()
 242 |             {
 243 |                 new() { Id = "obj_find_amulet", Description = "Найти потерянный амулет в пещере гоблинов" },
 244 |                 new() { Id = "obj_return_amulet", Description = "Вернуть амулет старейшине Олдрину" },
 245 |             },
 246 |             Reward = new QuestReward { Currency = 50, Experience = 100, Items = amuletItem is null ? null : new() { amuletItem.Id } },
 247 |         };
 248 |         world.Quests.Add(quest);
 249 | 
 250 |         // Quest 2: Clear the old mine — given by the watchtower guard.
 251 |         // Only add the quest if the guard actually spawned (defensive —
 252 |         // if bld_guard_tower didn't list npc_town_guard as an occupant,
 253 |         // towerGuard would be null).
 254 |         if (towerGuard is not null)
 255 |         {
 256 |             var mineQuest = new Quest
 257 |             {
 258 |                 Id = EntityId.NewId(),
 259 |                 Name = "Зачистить шахту",
 260 |                 Description = "Страж с башни просит зачистить старую шахту к востоку от пещеры — гоблины и совомедведь сделали оттуда логово и угрожают тракту.",
 261 |                 GiverNpcId = towerGuard.Id,
 262 |                 Status = QuestStatus.Active,
 263 |                 Objectives = new()
 264 |                 {
 265 |                     new() { Id = "obj_mine_kill_goblins", Description = "Уничтожить гоблинов в Старой шахте" },
 266 |                     new() { Id = "obj_mine_kill_beast", Description = "Справиться с совомедведем в шахте" },
 267 |                     new() { Id = "obj_mine_report", Description = "Доложить стражу на башне об успехе" },
 268 |                 },
 269 |                 Reward = new QuestReward
 270 |                 {
 271 |                     Currency = 75,
 272 |                     Experience = 150,
 273 |                     Items = new() { "tre_gem_ruby" },
 274 |                 },
 275 |             };
 276 |             world.Quests.Add(mineQuest);
 277 |         }
 278 | 
 279 |         // Quest 3: Find the missing caravaneer — given by the lake hermit.
 280 |         if (lakeHermit is not null)
 281 |         {
 282 |             var caravanQuest = new Quest
 283 |             {
 284 |                 Id = EntityId.NewId(),
 285 |                 Name = "Найти пропавшего караванщика",
 286 |                 Description = "Странствующий торговец у лесного озера просит разузнать судьбу своего напарника — караван пропал по тракту на юг, и последним местом, где его видели, была Развилка.",
 287 |                 GiverNpcId = lakeHermit.Id,
 288 |                 Status = QuestStatus.Active,
 289 |                 Objectives = new()
 290 |                 {
 291 |                     new() { Id = "obj_caravan_xroads", Description = "Осмотреть Развилку на следы каравана" },
 292 |                     new() { Id = "obj_caravan_farm", Description = "Проверить Заброшенную ферму — возможно, разбойники причастны" },
 293 |                     new() { Id = "obj_caravan_return", Description = "Вернуться к торговцу у озера с вестями" },
 294 |                 },
 295 |                 Reward = new QuestReward
 296 |                 {
 297 |                     Currency = 60,
 298 |                     Experience = 120,
 299 |                     Items = new() { "misc_scroll_map" },
 300 |                 },
 301 |             };
 302 |             world.Quests.Add(caravanQuest);
 303 |         }
 304 | 
 305 |         // ─── Starting player ───────────────────────────────────────────────
 306 |         var player = EntityFactory.CreatePlayer(new()
 307 |         {
 308 |             Name = "Странник",
 309 |             Race = "human",
 310 |             Class = "adventurer",
 311 |             Level = 1,
 312 |             LocationId = village.Id,
 313 |             StartingCurrency = 25,
 314 |             Background = "Бродяга, пришедший в Долину Туманов с караваном. Цели — туманны, меч — острый.",
 315 |         }, ruleset);
 316 | 
 317 |         // Equip the player with a basic shortsword and leather armor.
 318 |         if (registries.Items.Get("wpn_shortsword") is { } sword)
 319 |             player.Equipped["weapon"] = EntityFactory.InstantiateItem(sword);
 320 |         if (registries.Items.Get("arm_leather") is { } armor)
 321 |             player.Equipped["armor"] = EntityFactory.InstantiateItem(armor);
 322 | 
 323 |         // Starting inventory: a health potion + a torch + a ration.
 324 |         foreach (var (tplId, qty) in new[] { ("cns_health_potion", 2), ("tool_torch", 3), ("cns_ration", 5) })
 325 |         {
 326 |             if (registries.Items.Get(tplId) is { } t)
 327 |                 player.Inventory.Items.Add(EntityFactory.InstantiateItem(t, qty));
 328 |         }
 329 | 
 330 |         world.SpawnPlayer(player);
 331 | 
 332 |         return world;
 333 |     }
 334 | 
 335 |     // ─── Helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 336 | 
 337 |     private static Location NewLocation(
 338 |         string name, string description, string terrain,
 339 |         int x, int y, int danger) =>
 340 |         new()
 341 |         {
 342 |             Id = EntityId.NewId(),
 343 |             Name = name,
 344 |             Description = description,
 345 |             Terrain = terrain,
 346 |             X = x,
 347 |             Y = y,
 348 |             Danger = danger,
 349 |             Visited = false,
 350 |             Discovered = false,
 351 |         };
 352 | 
 353 |     private static void Connect(Location from, Location to, string direction)
 354 |     {
 355 |         from.Exits.Add(new LocationExit { To = to.Id, Direction = direction });
 356 |         // Reverse direction — naive but fine for a hand-authored scene.
 357 |         string back = direction switch
 358 |         {
 359 |             "север" => "юг",
 360 |             "юг" => "север",
 361 |             "восток" => "запад",
 362 |             "запад" => "восток",
 363 |             "вглубь" => "наружу",
 364 |             _ => "назад",
 365 |         };
 366 |         to.Exits.Add(new LocationExit { To = from.Id, Direction = back });
 367 |     }
 368 | 
 369 |     private static Building SpawnBuildingFromTemplate(
 370 |         World world, string templateId, EntityId locationId, ContentRegistry registries)
 371 |     {
 372 |         var tpl = registries.Buildings.Get(templateId)
 373 |             ?? throw new InvalidOperationException($"Unknown building template: {templateId}");
 374 |         var building = EntityFactory.CreateBuilding(tpl, locationId);
 375 |         world.SpawnBuilding(building);
 376 | 
 377 |         // Spawn occupant templates (and wire them as shopkeepers / occupants).
 378 |         if (tpl.OccupantTemplateIds is not null)
 379 |         {
 380 |             foreach (var npcTplId in tpl.OccupantTemplateIds)
 381 |             {
 382 |                 var npc = world.SpawnNpcFromTemplate(npcTplId, locationId);
 383 |                 if (npc is not null)
 384 |                 {
 385 |                     building.Occupants.Add(npc.Id);
 386 |                 }
 387 |             }
 388 |         }
 389 |         if (tpl.ShopkeeperTemplateId is { } shopId)
 390 |         {
 391 |             // If the shopkeeper wasn't already an occupant, spawn them too.
 392 |             if (!building.Occupants.Any(id => world.GetNpc(id)?.TemplateId == shopId))
 393 |             {
 394 |                 var shopNpc = world.SpawnNpcFromTemplate(shopId, locationId);
 395 |                 if (shopNpc is not null)
 396 |                 {
 397 |                     building.Occupants.Add(shopNpc.Id);
 398 |                     building.ShopkeeperNpcId = shopNpc.Id;
 399 |                 }
 400 |             }
 401 |             else
 402 |             {
 403 |                 // The occupant we already spawned is the shopkeeper.
 404 |                 building.ShopkeeperNpcId = building.Occupants
 405 |                     .FirstOrDefault(id => world.GetNpc(id)?.TemplateId == shopId);
 406 |             }
 407 |         }
 408 |         return building;
 409 |     }
 410 | }

--- src/MyGame.Core/World/Entities/Building.cs (C#, 41 lines, ~400 tokens) ---
   1 | using MyGame.Core.Common;
   2 | 
   3 | namespace MyGame.Core.World.Entities;
   4 | 
   5 | /// <summary>
   6 | /// A building or landmark within a location. Port of <c>Building</c> from
   7 | /// <c>engine/types/index.ts</c>.
   8 | /// </summary>
   9 | public sealed class Building : Entity
  10 | {
  11 |     /// <summary>Create a new blank building.</summary>
  12 |     public Building() : base("building") { }
  13 | 
  14 |     /// <summary>Template this building was spawned from (if any).</summary>
  15 |     public string? TemplateId { get; set; }
  16 | 
  17 |     /// <summary>
  18 |     /// Building type — free-form string defined by the ruleset
  19 |     /// (<c>tavern</c>, <c>shop</c>, <c>temple</c>, <c>dungeon</c>, …).
  20 |     /// </summary>
  21 |     public string Type { get; set; } = "house";
  22 | 
  23 |     /// <summary>Location this building sits in.</summary>
  24 |     public EntityId LocationId { get; set; }
  25 | 
  26 |     /// <summary>NPC ids occupying this building.</summary>
  27 |     public List<EntityId> Occupants { get; set; } = new();
  28 | 
  29 |     /// <summary>Interior location id (if the building is enterable).</summary>
  30 |     public EntityId? InteriorLocationId { get; set; }
  31 | 
  32 |     /// <summary>Whether the player can enter the building.</summary>
  33 |     public bool Enterable { get; set; }
  34 | 
  35 |     /// <summary>Whether the building is currently locked.</summary>
  36 |     public bool? Locked { get; set; }
  37 | 
  38 |     /// <summary>If this building is a shop, the shopkeeper NPC id.</summary>
  39 |     public EntityId? ShopkeeperNpcId { get; set; }
  40 | }

--- src/MyGame.Core/World/Entities/Character.cs (C#, 66 lines, ~784 tokens) ---
   1 | using MyGame.Core.Common;
   2 | 
   3 | namespace MyGame.Core.World.Entities;
   4 | 
   5 | /// <summary>
   6 | /// Abstract base for any character (player-controlled or NPC) in the world.
   7 | /// Port of <c>Character</c> from <c>engine/types/index.ts</c>.
   8 | ///
   9 | /// Character is ruleset-agnostic in the TS source: <see cref="Attributes"/>
  10 | /// and <see cref="Resources"/> are string-keyed maps whose keys come from the
  11 | /// owning world's Ruleset. There are no D&amp;D-specific required fields
  12 | /// (maxHp / ac / speed / proficiencyBonus) here — those, when they exist,
  13 | /// live inside <see cref="Resources"/> and <see cref="Attributes"/>.
  14 | /// </summary>
  15 | public abstract class Character : Entity
  16 | {
  17 |     /// <summary>Protected ctor — only concrete subclasses call this.</summary>
  18 |     protected Character(string kind) : base(kind) { }
  19 | 
  20 |     /// <summary>Optional flavor label (race / species / people).</summary>
  21 |     public string? Race { get; set; }
  22 | 
  23 |     /// <summary>Optional flavor label (class / archetype / role).</summary>
  24 |     public string? Class { get; set; }
  25 | 
  26 |     /// <summary>Character level, if the ruleset uses level-based progression.</summary>
  27 |     public int? Level { get; set; }
  28 | 
  29 |     /// <summary>Attribute values keyed by ruleset attribute key.</summary>
  30 |     public Dictionary<string, int> Attributes { get; set; } = new();
  31 | 
  32 |     /// <summary>Resource pools (hp, mana, sanity, shield…) keyed by ruleset resource key.</summary>
  33 |     public Dictionary<string, int> Resources { get; set; } = new();
  34 | 
  35 |     /// <summary>Skills the character is proficient in (free-form labels).</summary>
  36 |     public List<string>? ProficientSkills { get; set; }
  37 | 
  38 |     /// <summary>Filled equipment slots keyed by ruleset slot name (weapon / armor / shield / …).</summary>
  39 |     public Dictionary<string, Item> Equipped { get; set; } = new();
  40 | 
  41 |     /// <summary>Carried inventory.</summary>
  42 |     public Inventory Inventory { get; set; } = new();
  43 | 
  44 |     /// <summary>Active status effects on this character.</summary>
  45 |     public List<StatusEffect> Effects { get; set; } = new();
  46 | 
  47 |     /// <summary>
  48 |     /// NPC attitude toward the player. Free-form; defaults from D&amp;D
  49 |     /// vocabulary (<c friendly</c>, <c>neutral</c>, <c>hostile</c>,
  50 |     /// <c>allied</c>).
  51 |     /// </summary>
  52 |     public string? Disposition { get; set; }
  53 | 
  54 |     /// <summary>Optional dialogue tree id (for AI-driven conversations).</summary>
  55 |     public string? DialogueTreeId { get; set; }
  56 | 
  57 |     /// <summary>Movement speed (ruleset-defined; D&amp;D default is 30 ft).</summary>
  58 |     public int? Speed { get; set; }
  59 | 
  60 |     /// <summary>Where the character currently is.</summary>
  61 |     public EntityId LocationId { get; set; }
  62 | 
  63 |     /// <summary>Whether the character is currently alive.</summary>
  64 |     public bool IsAlive { get; set; } = true;
  65 | }

--- src/MyGame.Core/World/Entities/CraftingRecipe.cs (C#, 37 lines, ~375 tokens) ---
   1 | using System.Collections.Generic;
   2 | using MyGame.Core.Common;
   3 | 
   4 | namespace MyGame.Core.World.Entities;
   5 | 
   6 | /// <summary>
   7 | /// A crafting recipe. Defines input items + output item. Stored in the
   8 | /// content registry. The GM (or player via UI) can craft the output by
   9 | /// consuming the inputs. Port of issue #65.
  10 | /// </summary>
  11 | public sealed record CraftingRecipe
  12 | {
  13 |     /// <summary>Unique recipe id, e.g. "craft_health_potion".</summary>
  14 |     public required string Id { get; init; }
  15 | 
  16 |     /// <summary>Display name, e.g. "Зелье лечения".</summary>
  17 |     public required string Name { get; init; }
  18 | 
  19 |     /// <summary>Input items: templateId → quantity required.</summary>
  20 |     public required IReadOnlyDictionary<string, int> Inputs { get; init; }
  21 | 
  22 |     /// <summary>Output item template id.</summary>
  23 |     public required string OutputTemplateId { get; init; }
  24 | 
  25 |     /// <summary>Output quantity (default 1).</summary>
  26 |     public int OutputQuantity { get; init; } = 1;
  27 | 
  28 |     /// <summary>Optional required tool (e.g. "tool_alchemists_supplies").</summary>
  29 |     public string? RequiredTool { get; init; }
  30 | 
  31 |     /// <summary>Optional skill check (e.g. "alchemy" — GM rolls if set).</summary>
  32 |     public string? SkillCheck { get; init; }
  33 | 
  34 |     /// <summary>Description for the UI.</summary>
  35 |     public string? Description { get; init; }
  36 | }

--- src/MyGame.Core/World/Entities/Entity.cs (C#, 65 lines, ~670 tokens) ---
   1 | using MyGame.Core.Common;
   2 | 
   3 | namespace MyGame.Core.World.Entities;
   4 | 
   5 | /// <summary>
   6 | /// Abstract base for every game entity that lives in a <see cref="World"/>.
   7 | ///
   8 | /// Port of the entity-shape from <c>engine/types/index.ts</c>. The TS module
   9 | /// didn't have a single <c>Entity</c> base interface — every concrete entity
  10 | /// (Character, Building, Location, Quest, ItemInstance) repeated the
  11 | /// <c>id</c>/<c>name</c>/<c>description</c> trio inline. The C# port
  12 | /// introduces this abstract base so the World can offer a single
  13 | /// <c>FindEntity(EntityId)</c> entry point that returns <c>Entity?</c>.
  14 | ///
  15 | /// Entities are MUTABLE classes (not records): HP changes, locations shift,
  16 | /// inventories gain/lose items — the engine mutates the live instance. The
  17 | /// worklog's "records for immutable data" rule applies to snapshots
  18 | /// (<see cref="ItemTemplate"/>, <see cref="StatusEffect"/>,
  19 | /// <see cref="QuestObjective"/>, etc.); live world state is class-based.
  20 | /// </summary>
  21 | public abstract class Entity
  22 | {
  23 |     /// <summary>Unique identifier for this entity instance.</summary>
  24 |     public EntityId Id { get; set; }
  25 | 
  26 |     /// <summary>Display name.</summary>
  27 |     public string Name { get; set; } = string.Empty;
  28 | 
  29 |     /// <summary>Optional long-form flavor description.</summary>
  30 |     public string? Description { get; set; }
  31 | 
  32 |     /// <summary>
  33 |     /// Type discriminator — useful for debugging and for any future
  34 |     /// polymorphic JSON serialization. Set by each concrete subclass ctor.
  35 |     /// </summary>
  36 |     public string Kind { get; protected init; }
  37 | 
  38 |     /// <summary>
  39 |     /// Free-form per-entity metadata bag. Used by the world-builder to stash
  40 |     /// plan-derived hints (NPC disposition/behavior/role/notes, location
  41 |     /// visit state, etc.) and by tools / GM for arbitrary state. Lazy-init
  42 |     /// to null to keep entity JSON clean when unused.
  43 |     /// </summary>
  44 |     public Dictionary<string, object>? Flags { get; set; }
  45 | 
  46 |     /// <summary>Protected ctor — only concrete subclasses call this.</summary>
  47 |     protected Entity(string kind)
  48 |     {
  49 |         Kind = kind;
  50 |         Id = EntityId.NewId();
  51 |     }
  52 | }
  53 | 
  54 | /// <summary>
  55 | /// Status of a quest in the player's log.
  56 | /// Port of <c>QuestStatus</c> from <c>engine/types/index.ts</c>.
  57 | /// </summary>
  58 | public enum QuestStatus
  59 | {
  60 |     Inactive = 0,
  61 |     Active = 1,
  62 |     Completed = 2,
  63 |     Failed = 3,
  64 | }

--- src/MyGame.Core/World/Entities/EntityCollection.cs (C#, 90 lines, ~961 tokens) ---
   1 | using System.Collections;
   2 | using System.Text.Json;
   3 | using MyGame.Core.Common;
   4 | 
   5 | namespace MyGame.Core.World.Entities;
   6 | 
   7 | /// <summary>
   8 | /// A typed lookup container that the <see cref="World"/> uses for each
   9 | /// entity kind. Wraps a <see cref="Dictionary{TKey, TValue}"/> keyed by
  10 | /// <see cref="EntityId"/> with a strongly-typed <typeparamref name="T"/>
  11 | /// value, and exposes only read-only surface plus add/remove/lookup
  12 | /// mutators.
  13 | ///
  14 | /// The TS World used plain <c>Record&lt;EntityId, T&gt;</c> maps; this class
  15 | /// is the C#-idiomatic equivalent — typed, mutable internally, read-only on
  16 | /// the public surface.
  17 | /// </summary>
  18 | /// <typeparam name="T">Concrete entity type held in this collection.</typeparam>
  19 | public sealed class EntityCollection<T> : IEnumerable<T> where T : Entity
  20 | {
  21 |     private readonly Dictionary<EntityId, T> _byId = new();
  22 | 
  23 |     /// <summary>Create an empty collection.</summary>
  24 |     public EntityCollection() { }
  25 | 
  26 |     /// <summary>Number of entities in the collection.</summary>
  27 |     public int Count => _byId.Count;
  28 | 
  29 |     /// <summary>True if an entity with the given id is in the collection.</summary>
  30 |     public bool Contains(EntityId id) => _byId.ContainsKey(id);
  31 | 
  32 |     /// <summary>Lookup by id. Null if not present.</summary>
  33 |     public T? Get(EntityId id) => _byId.TryGetValue(id, out var v) ? v : null;
  34 | 
  35 |     /// <summary>
  36 |     /// Add an entity. If an entity with the same id is already present, it
  37 |     /// is overwritten. Returns the added entity for chaining.
  38 |     /// </summary>
  39 |     public T Add(T entity)
  40 |     {
  41 |         if (entity is null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(entity));
  42 |         _byId[entity.Id] = entity;
  43 |         return entity;
  44 |     }
  45 | 
  46 |     /// <summary>Remove an entity by id. Returns true if it was present.</summary>
  47 |     public bool Remove(EntityId id) => _byId.Remove(id);
  48 | 
  49 |     /// <summary>Remove all entities.</summary>
  50 |     public void Clear() => _byId.Clear();
  51 | 
  52 |     /// <summary>Snapshot of all entities as a list.</summary>
  53 |     public IReadOnlyList<T> All() => _byId.Values.ToList();
  54 | 
  55 |     /// <summary>Read-only snapshot of all entity ids.</summary>
  56 |     public IReadOnlyList<EntityId> Ids() => _byId.Keys.ToList();
  57 | 
  58 |     /// <summary>Enumerate entities.</summary>
  59 |     public IEnumerator<T> GetEnumerator() => _byId.Values.GetEnumerator();
  60 | 
  61 |     /// <summary>Non-generic enumerator (for IEnumerable).</summary>
  62 |     IEnumerator IEnumerable.GetEnumerator() => GetEnumerator();
  63 | }
  64 | 
  65 | /// <summary>
  66 | /// A snapshot of every entity collection in a <see cref="World"/> — used as
  67 | /// the JSON-serializable shape for save/load. Each collection is a list
  68 | /// rather than a map (the entity's own <c>Id</c> is the key on reload).
  69 | /// </summary>
  70 | public sealed class EntitySnapshot
  71 | {
  72 |     /// <summary>All players (one for single-player, many for multiplayer).</summary>
  73 |     public List<Player> Players { get; set; } = new();
  74 | 
  75 |     /// <summary>All NPCs.</summary>
  76 |     public List<Npc> Npcs { get; set; } = new();
  77 | 
  78 |     /// <summary>All loose world items (on the ground).</summary>
  79 |     public List<Item> Items { get; set; } = new();
  80 | 
  81 |     /// <summary>All buildings.</summary>
  82 |     public List<Building> Buildings { get; set; } = new();
  83 | 
  84 |     /// <summary>All locations.</summary>
  85 |     public List<Location> Locations { get; set; } = new();
  86 | 
  87 |     /// <summary>All quests.</summary>
  88 |     public List<Quest> Quests { get; set; } = new();
  89 | }

--- src/MyGame.Core/World/Entities/Faction.cs (C#, 80 lines, ~900 tokens) ---
   1 | namespace MyGame.Core.World.Entities;
   2 | 
   3 | /// <summary>
   4 | /// A faction in the world (state / guild / cult / band / corp / clan /
   5 | /// order). Engine-depth subsystem (issue #36): factions carry a numeric
   6 | /// reputation with the player (-100 hostile → +100 ally) that the GM
   7 | /// adjusts via the <c>adjust_reputation</c> tool, and an alignment label
   8 | /// derived from the reputation (hostile / neutral / ally). The
   9 | /// world-builder commits factions from <see cref="AI.WorldPlan.Factions"/>
  10 | /// at <see cref="WorldBuilderCommitter.CommitFactions"/>; everything
  11 | /// starts neutral (Reputation = 0).
  12 | ///
  13 | /// <para>
  14 | /// <b>Serialization:</b> plain POCO + System.Text.Json via
  15 | /// <see cref="WorldJson.Options"/>. Round-trips through save/load because
  16 | /// every field is a primitive / string.
  17 | /// </para>
  18 | /// </summary>
  19 | public sealed class Faction
  20 | {
  21 |     /// <summary>
  22 |     /// Stable id. Usually derived from the faction name (slugified) at
  23 |     /// commit time so the GM can address it by name without an ID lookup.
  24 |     /// </summary>
  25 |     public string Id { get; set; } = "";
  26 | 
  27 |     /// <summary>Display name (e.g. «Орден Серебряной Чаши»).</summary>
  28 |     public string Name { get; set; } = "";
  29 | 
  30 |     /// <summary>Flavor description (1–2 sentences from the plan).</summary>
  31 |     public string Description { get; set; } = "";
  32 | 
  33 |     /// <summary>
  34 |     /// Alignment bucket derived from <see cref="Reputation"/>: <c>ally</c>
  35 |     /// (rep &gt;= 30), <c>hostile</c> (rep &lt;= -30), <c>neutral</c>
  36 |     /// otherwise. The <c>adjust_reputation</c> tool updates this in lock-
  37 |     /// step with reputation so callers always see a consistent pair.
  38 |     /// Free-form at commit time (the planner's <c>Alignment</c> string —
  39 |     /// good / evil / lawful / chaotic — is descriptive flavor only and
  40 |     /// is NOT propagated here).
  41 |     /// </summary>
  42 |     public string Alignment { get; set; } = "neutral";
  43 | 
  44 |     /// <summary>
  45 |     /// Numeric reputation with the player, clamped to [-100, 100]. 0 =
  46 |     /// neutral start. Drives <see cref="Alignment"/> automatically when
  47 |     /// changed via the <c>adjust_reputation</c> tool.
  48 |     /// </summary>
  49 |     public int Reputation { get; set; }
  50 | 
  51 |     /// <summary>
  52 |     /// Faction type (state / guild / cult / band / corp / clan / order).
  53 |     /// Pure flavor from the plan; not used mechanically.
  54 |     /// </summary>
  55 |     public string? Type { get; set; }
  56 | 
  57 |     /// <summary>
  58 |     /// Where the faction is based (region, capital, territory). Pure
  59 |     /// flavor from the plan; the GM may use it to narrate.
  60 |     /// </summary>
  61 |     public string? Territory { get; set; }
  62 | 
  63 |     /// <summary>
  64 |     /// Recompute <see cref="Alignment"/> from <see cref="Reputation"/>:
  65 |     /// &gt;= 30 → ally, &lt;= -30 → hostile, else neutral. Called by the
  66 |     /// <c>adjust_reputation</c> tool after every reputation delta so the
  67 |     /// two fields never drift. Public so a future rebalancer / save
  68 |     /// migrator can call it after bulk reputation edits.
  69 |     /// </summary>
  70 |     public void RecomputeAlignment()
  71 |     {
  72 |         Alignment = Reputation switch
  73 |         {
  74 |             >= 30 => "ally",
  75 |             <= -30 => "hostile",
  76 |             _ => "neutral",
  77 |         };
  78 |     }
  79 | }

   1 | using System.Text.Json.Serialization;
   2 | using MyGame.Core.Common;

   4 | namespace MyGame.Core.World.Entities;
   5 | 
   6 | // ─── Damage / weapon / armor / consumable sub-shapes ───────────────────────
   7 | //
   8 | // All ports of the corresponding sub-interfaces in engine/types/index.ts.
   9 | // Records for immutability — a weapon spec never mutates after creation.
  10 | 
  11 | /// <summary>
  12 | /// A damage expression: dice notation (<c>"1d8"</c>) + damage type
  13 | /// (<c>"slashing"</c>). The type is a free-form string whose vocabulary is
  14 | /// defined by the world's Ruleset.
  15 | /// </summary>
  16 | public sealed record Damage(string Dice, string Type);
  17 | 
  18 | /// <summary>
  19 | /// Weapon-specific properties on an item template. Only meaningful in worlds
  20 | /// whose ruleset has weapons. Port of <c>ItemTemplate.weapon</c>.
  21 | /// </summary>
  22 | public sealed record WeaponSpec
  23 | {
  24 |     /// <summary>Weapon type (<c>simple</c>, <c>martial</c>, <c>improvised</c>, or ruleset-defined).</summary>
  25 |     public required string Type { get; init; }
  26 | 
  27 |     /// <summary>Base damage dice + damage type.</summary>
  28 |     public required Damage Damage { get; init; }
  29 | 
  30 |     /// <summary>Range in feet (0 = melee, 20 = thrown, 80 = shortbow, etc.).</summary>
  31 |     public int? Range { get; init; }
  32 | 
  33 |     /// <summary>Whether the weapon can use STR or DEX (whichever is higher).</summary>
  34 |     public bool? Finesse { get; init; }
  35 | 
  36 |     /// <summary>Requires two hands to wield.</summary>
  37 |     public bool? TwoHanded { get; init; }
  38 | 
  39 |     /// <summary>Two-handed damage dice (e.g. <c>"1d10"</c> for a longsword used 2H).</summary>
  40 |     public string? Versatile { get; init; }
  41 | 
  42 |     /// <summary>Free-form property tags (<c>light</c>, <c>heavy</c>, <c>thrown</c>, <c>ammunition</c>, …).</summary>
  43 |     public List<string>? Properties { get; init; }
  44 | }
  45 | 
  46 | /// <summary>Armor-specific properties on an item template.</summary>
  47 | public sealed record ArmorSpec
  48 | {
  49 |     /// <summary>Base armor class (before dex bonus).</summary>
  50 |     public required int BaseAc { get; init; }
  51 | 
  52 |     /// <summary>Armor category (<c>light</c>, <c>medium</c>, <c>heavy</c>, or ruleset-defined).</summary>
  53 |     public required string Type { get; init; }
  54 | 
  55 |     /// <summary>
  56 |     /// Max dexterity bonus to AC (null = unlimited; light = null, medium = 2,
  57 |     /// heavy = 0).
  58 |     /// </summary>
  59 |     public int? DexBonusMax { get; init; }
  60 | 
  61 |     /// <summary>Strength score required to wear without penalty.</summary>
  62 |     public int? StrengthRequired { get; init; }
  63 |

  65 |     public bool? StealthDisadvantage { get; init; }
  66 | }
  67 | 
  68 | /// <summary>An effect granted by a consumable on use (heal, cure, buff, …).</summary>
  69 | public sealed record ConsumableEffect
  70 | {
  71 |     /// <summary>Display name.</summary>
  72 |     public required string Name { get; init; }
  73 | 
  74 |     /// <summary>What the effect does.</summary>
  75 |     public required string Description { get; init; }
  76 | 
  77 |     /// <summary>Duration in rounds (0 = instant).</summary>
  78 |     public int Duration { get; init; }
  79 | }
  80 | 
  81 | /// <summary>Consumable-specific properties on an item template.</summary>
  82 | public sealed record ConsumableSpec
  83 | {
  84 |     /// <summary>Resource key to restore (defaults to <c>hp</c> in D&amp;D-style worlds).</summary>
  85 |     public string? Resource { get; init; }
  86 | 
  87 |     /// <summary>Amount to restore, or a dice expression (<c>"2d4+2"</c>).</summary>
  88 |     public string? Healing { get; init; }
  89 |

  91 |     public List<ConsumableEffect>? Effects { get; init; }
  92 | }
  93 | 
  94 | // ─── ItemTemplate (definition) ──────────────────────────────────────────────
  95 | 
  96 | /// <summary>
  97 | /// Static definition of an item type. Lives in the
  98 | /// <see cref="Content.ItemRegistry"/>, referenced by TemplateId. Port of
  99 | /// <c>ItemTemplate</c> from <c>engine/types/index.ts</c>.
 100 | /// </summary>
 101 | public sealed record ItemTemplate
 102 | {
 103 |     /// <summary>Stable template id, e.g. <c>wpn_shortsword</c>.</summary>
 104 |     public required string Id { get; init; }
 105 | 
 106 |     /// <summary>Display name.</summary>
 107 |     public required string Name { get; init; }
 108 | 
 109 |     /// <summary>Flavor text.</summary>
 110 |     public required string Description { get; init; }
 111 | 
 112 |     /// <summary>
 113 |     /// Item category — free-form string defined by the ruleset
 114 |     /// (<c>weapon</c>, <c>armor</c>, <c>consumable</c>, <c>treasure</c>, …).
 115 |     /// </summary>
 116 |     public required string Category { get; init; }
 117 | 
 118 |     /// <summary>Weight in lb.</summary>
 119 |     public double Weight { get; init; }
 120 | 
 121 |     /// <summary>Value in the world's currency units.</summary>
 122 |     public double Value { get; init; }
 123 | 
 124 |     /// <summary>
 125 |     /// Rarity bucket (<c>common</c>, <c>uncommon</c>, <c>rare</c>,
 126 |     /// <c>veryRare</c>, <c>legendary</c>, <c>artifact</c>, or ruleset-defined).
 127 |     /// </summary>
 128 |     public required string Rarity { get; init; }
 129 | 
 130 |     /// <summary>Whether multiple instances stack into a single inventory slot.</summary>
 131 |     public bool Stackable { get; init; }
 132 | 
 133 |     /// <summary>Weapon specifics (only if category is <c>weapon</c>).</summary>
 134 |     public WeaponSpec? Weapon { get; init; }
 135 | 
 136 |     /// <summary>Armor specifics (only if category is <c>armor</c>).</summary>
 137 |     public ArmorSpec? Armor { get; init; }
 138 | 
 139 |     /// <summary>Consumable specifics (only if category is <c>consumable</c>).</summary>
 140 |     public ConsumableSpec? Consumable { get; init; }
 141 | 
 142 |     /// <summary>Free-form tags for AI / scripting.</summary>
 143 |     public List<string>? Tags { get; init; }
 144 | }
 145 | 
 146 | // ─── Item (runtime instance) ────────────────────────────────────────────────
 147 | 
 148 | /// <summary>
 149 | /// A runtime item instance — a concrete object that exists in the world
 150 | /// (on the ground, in an inventory, or equipped). Port of
 151 | /// <c>ItemInstance</c> from <c>engine/types/index.ts</c>.
 152 | ///
 153 | /// The TS interface used <c>uid</c> as the unique instance id; here the
 154 | /// inherited <see cref="Entity.Id"/> plays that role.
 155 | /// </summary>
 156 | public sealed class Item : Entity
 157 | {
 158 |     /// <summary>Create a new blank item instance.</summary>
 159 |     public Item() : base("item") { }
 160 | 
 161 |     /// <summary>The template this instance was spawned from.</summary>
 162 |     public string? TemplateId { get; set; }
 163 | 
 164 |     /// <summary>Stack size (1 for unstackable items).</summary>
 165 |     public int Quantity { get; set; } = 1;
 166 | 
 167 |     /// <summary>
 168 |     /// Per-unit weight in lb, denormalized from the
 169 |     /// <see cref="ItemTemplate.Weight"/> at instantiation time so the UI /
 170 |     /// inventory panel can compute carried weight without a registry lookup.
 171 |     /// </summary>
 172 |     /// <remarks>
 173 |     /// TODO(save-migration): existing saves load Item instances without this
 174 |     /// field, so they default to 0 here. A future SaveManager migration should
 175 |     /// backfill <see cref="Weight"/> from the item's <see cref="TemplateId"/>
 176 |     /// in the content registry on load. Until then, items created before this
 177 |     /// change will report 0 weight in the inventory bar.
 178 |     /// </remarks>
 179 |     public double Weight { get; set; }
 180 | 
 181 |     /// <summary>Optional runtime override of the template's damage profile.</summary>
 182 |     public Damage? CustomDamage { get; set; }
 183 | 
 184 |     /// <summary>Whether this item is currently equipped in a slot.</summary>
 185 |     public bool Equipped { get; set; }
 186 | 
 187 |     /// <summary>Enchantment ids applied to this instance.</summary>
 188 |     public List<string>? Enchantments { get; set; }
 189 | }
 190 | 
 191 | // ─── Inventory ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 192 | 
 193 | /// <summary>
 194 | /// A character's carried inventory. Port of <c>Inventory</c> from
 195 | /// <c>engine/types/index.ts</c>.
 196 | /// </summary>
 197 | public sealed class Inventory
 198 | {
 199 |     /// <summary>Items carried (does not include equipped items).</summary>
 200 |     public List<Item> Items { get; set; } = new();
 201 | 
 202 |     /// <summary>Amount of the world's primary currency.</summary>
 203 |     public int Currency { get; set; }
 204 | 
 205 |     /// <summary>Weight capacity in lb.</summary>
 206 |     public int Capacity { get; set; } = 150;
 207 | }

--- src/MyGame.Core/World/Entities/Location.cs (C#, 97 lines, ~1,074 tokens) ---
   1 | using MyGame.Core.Common;
   2 | 
   3 | namespace MyGame.Core.World.Entities;
   4 | 
   5 | /// <summary>
   6 | /// One exit edge in the location graph: from the containing location to
   7 | /// <see cref="To"/>, labeled with <see cref="Direction"/> (e.g. «север», «к
   8 | /// таверне»), optionally locked. Port of the inline exit shape from
   9 | /// <c>engine/types/index.ts</c>.
  10 | /// </summary>
  11 | /// <remarks>
  12 | /// <b>Phantom exits (issue #20 — chunked generation):</b> when
  13 | /// <see cref="To"/> is <see cref="EntityId.Empty"/> AND
  14 | /// <see cref="ToName"/> is non-empty, the exit is a "phantom" exit
  15 | /// pointing to a not-yet-generated cold-region location (or a cold
  16 | /// region's name). The world panel renders the destination as
  17 | /// <see cref="ToName"/>; the GameViewModel's travel handler detects the
  18 | /// phantom exit and triggers region generation via
  19 | /// <c>WorldBuilderOrchestrator.GenerateRegionAsync</c>.
  20 | /// </remarks>
  21 | public sealed class LocationExit
  22 | {
  23 |     /// <summary>
  24 |     /// Destination location id. <see cref="EntityId.Empty"/> for a
  25 |     /// phantom exit (cold-region boundary) — see <see cref="ToName"/>.
  26 |     /// </summary>
  27 |     public EntityId To { get; set; }
  28 | 
  29 |     /// <summary>
  30 |     /// Destination location name. Set only for phantom exits (issue #20)
  31 |     /// — when <see cref="To"/> is empty. For normal exits, this is null
  32 |     /// and the destination is resolved via <see cref="To"/> +
  33 |     /// <c>World.GetLocation(To).Name</c>.
  34 |     /// </summary>
  35 |     public string? ToName { get; set; }
  36 | 
  37 |     /// <summary>Direction label (display string).</summary>
  38 |     public string Direction { get; set; } = string.Empty;
  39 | 
  40 |     /// <summary>If true, the exit is locked (player cannot traverse it).</summary>
  41 |     public bool? Locked { get; set; }
  42 | }
  43 | 
  44 | /// <summary>
  45 | /// A location node in the world graph. Port of <c>Location</c> from
  46 | /// <c>engine/types/index.ts</c>.
  47 | /// </summary>
  48 | public sealed class Location : Entity
  49 | {
  50 |     /// <summary>Create a new blank location.</summary>
  51 |     public Location() : base("location") { }
  52 | 
  53 |     /// <summary>
  54 |     /// Terrain type — free-form string defined by the ruleset
  55 |     /// (<c>plains</c>, <c>forest</c>, <c>mountain</c>, …).
  56 |     /// </summary>
  57 |     public string Terrain { get; set; } = "plains";
  58 | 
  59 |     /// <summary>World-grid X coordinate (optional).</summary>
  60 |     public int? X { get; set; }
  61 | 
  62 |     /// <summary>World-grid Y coordinate (optional).</summary>
  63 |     public int? Y { get; set; }
  64 | 
  65 |     /// <summary>Outgoing exits to other locations (graph edges).</summary>
  66 |     public List<LocationExit> Exits { get; set; } = new();
  67 | 
  68 |     /// <summary>NPC ids present at this location.</summary>
  69 |     public List<EntityId> Npcs { get; set; } = new();
  70 | 
  71 |     /// <summary>Loose item ids on the ground at this location.</summary>
  72 |     public List<EntityId> Items { get; set; } = new();
  73 | 
  74 |     /// <summary>Building ids at this location.</summary>
  75 |     public List<EntityId> Buildings { get; set; } = new();
  76 | 
  77 |     /// <summary>Danger level (0-10) — drives random encounter rolls.</summary>
  78 |     public int Danger { get; set; }
  79 | 
  80 |     /// <summary>Whether the player has visited this location.</summary>
  81 |     public bool Visited { get; set; }
  82 | 
  83 |     /// <summary>Whether this location is on the player's discovered map.</summary>
  84 |     public bool Discovered { get; set; }
  85 | 
  86 |     // NOTE: Location inherits Flags from Entity. The per-location flags
  87 |     // (visited-by-faction, locked-reason, etc.) go in Entity.Flags.
  88 | 
  89 |     /// <summary>
  90 |     /// Market price modifiers per item category (issue #37). Key = category
  91 |     /// ("weapon", "armor", "consumable", etc.), value = price multiplier
  92 |     /// (1.0 = base, 1.5 = expensive, 0.7 = cheap). Null or empty = no
  93 |     /// market system active at this location.
  94 |     /// </summary>
  95 |     public Dictionary<string, double>? MarketModifiers { get; set; }
  96 | }

--- src/MyGame.Core/World/Entities/Quest.cs (C#, 91 lines, ~972 tokens) ---
   1 | using MyGame.Core.Common;
   2 | 
   3 | namespace MyGame.Core.World.Entities;
