   5 | /// <summary>
   6 | /// One objective in a quest's checklist. Port of <c>QuestObjective</c>
   7 | /// from <c>engine/types/index.ts</c>.
   8 | /// </summary>
   9 | public sealed class QuestObjective
  10 | {
  11 |     /// <summary>Stable id (used to mark this objective done via complete_objective).</summary>
  12 |     public string Id { get; set; } = string.Empty;
  13 | 
  14 |     /// <summary>Player-facing description of what to do.</summary>
  15 |     public string Description { get; set; } = string.Empty;
  16 | 
  17 |     /// <summary>Whether this objective is complete.</summary>
  18 |     public bool Done { get; set; }
  19 | }
  20 | 
  21 | /// <summary>
  22 | /// Reward granted on quest completion. Port of <c>Quest.reward</c> from
  23 | /// <c>engine/types/index.ts</c>.
  24 | /// </summary>
  25 | public sealed class QuestReward
  26 | {
  27 |     /// <summary>Currency amount (gold/credits/…).</summary>
  28 |     public int? Currency { get; set; }
  29 | 
  30 |     /// <summary>XP amount.</summary>
  31 |     public int? Experience { get; set; }
  32 | 
  33 |     /// <summary>Item template ids granted on completion.</summary>
  34 |     public List<string>? Items { get; set; }
  35 | 
  36 |     /// <summary>Legacy alias for <see cref="Currency"/> (older saves may carry gold).</summary>
  37 |     public int? Gold { get; set; }
  38 | }
  39 | 
  40 | /// <summary>
  41 | /// A quest in the player's log. Port of <c>Quest</c> from
  42 | /// <c>engine/types/index.ts</c>. The TS <c>title</c>/<c>description</c> map
  43 | /// to the inherited <see cref="Entity.Name"/>/<see cref="Entity.Description"/>.
  44 | /// </summary>
  45 | public sealed class Quest : Entity
  46 | {
  47 |     /// <summary>Create a new blank quest.</summary>
  48 |     public Quest() : base("quest") { }
  49 | 
  50 |     /// <summary>NPC who gave this quest (if any).</summary>
  51 |     public EntityId? GiverNpcId { get; set; }
  52 | 
  53 |     /// <summary>Quest status.</summary>
  54 |     public QuestStatus Status { get; set; } = QuestStatus.Inactive;
  55 | 
  56 |     /// <summary>Checklist of objectives.</summary>
  57 |     public List<QuestObjective> Objectives { get; set; } = new();
  58 | 
  59 |     /// <summary>Reward granted on completion.</summary>
  60 |     public QuestReward? Reward { get; set; }
  61 | 
  62 |     /// <summary>
  63 |     /// Reward waiting to be claimed by the player (issue #70). When the
  64 |     /// GM calls <c>update_quest</c> with action <c>complete</c>, the
  65 |     /// quest's <see cref="Reward"/> is MOVED here (rather than granted
  66 |     /// inline as before). The player must then click «Получить награду»
  67 |     /// in the Quest panel to actually receive the currency / XP / items,
  68 |     /// at which point this field is cleared (set to null).
  69 |     ///
  70 |     /// <para>
  71 |     /// Null for: quests that haven't been completed yet, quests that
  72 |     /// were completed before issue #70 (legacy auto-grant), and quests
  73 |     /// whose reward the player has already claimed. The Quest panel
  74 |     /// shows the «Получить награду» button only when this field is
  75 |     /// non-null.
  76 |     /// </para>
  77 |     ///
  78 |     /// <para>
  79 |     /// <b>Backward compatibility:</b> old saves don't have this field —
  80 |     /// they load with <c>null</c>, which is correct (either the quest
  81 |     /// is incomplete and the reward is in <see cref="Reward"/>, or it's
  82 |     /// complete and the reward was already auto-granted under the old
  83 |     /// behavior). No migration needed.
  84 |     /// </para>
  85 |     /// </summary>
  86 |     public QuestReward? UnclaimedRewards { get; set; }
  87 | 
  88 |     /// <summary>Quests that must be completed before this one can start.</summary>
  89 |     public List<EntityId>? PrerequisiteQuestIds { get; set; }
  90 | }

--- src/MyGame.Core/World/Entities/StatusEffect.cs (C#, 55 lines, ~551 tokens) ---
   1 | using MyGame.Core.Common;
   2 | 
   3 | namespace MyGame.Core.World.Entities;
   4 | 
   5 | /// <summary>
   6 | /// A transient status effect on a character (poisoned, blessed, frightened, …).
   7 | ///
   8 | /// Port of <c>StatusEffect</c> from <c>engine/types/index.ts</c>. Records are
   9 | /// used here because an effect, once applied, is treated as an immutable
  10 | /// snapshot — the engine replaces it (rather than mutating it in place) when
  11 | /// the duration ticks down.
  12 | /// </summary>
  13 | public sealed record StatusEffect
  14 | {
  15 |     /// <summary>Unique per-effect instance id.</summary>
  16 |     public required EntityId Id { get; init; }
  17 | 
  18 |     /// <summary>Optional template id (if this effect came from a template).</summary>
  19 |     public string? TemplateId { get; init; }
  20 | 
  21 |     /// <summary>Display name, e.g. «Отравление».</summary>
  22 |     public required string Name { get; init; }
  23 | 
  24 |     /// <summary>What the effect does, flavor + mechanics.</summary>
  25 |     public required string Description { get; init; }
  26 | 
  27 |     /// <summary>
  28 |     /// Rounds remaining. -1 = until dispelled. 0 = expired (will be reaped
  29 |     /// on the next tick).
  30 |     /// </summary>
  31 |     public int Duration { get; init; }
  32 | 
  33 |     /// <summary>Optional stack count (e.g. 3 stacks of poison).</summary>
  34 |     public int? Stacks { get; init; }
  35 | 
  36 |     /// <summary>
  37 |     /// Flat modifiers applied to attributes while this effect is active,
  38 |     /// keyed by attribute key (e.g. <c>{ "str": -2 }</c>).
  39 |     /// </summary>
  40 |     public Dictionary<string, int>? Modifiers { get; init; }
  41 | 
  42 |     /// <summary>
  43 |     /// Per-turn damage applied at the start of the affected character's turn.
  44 |     /// </summary>
  45 |     public PerTurnDamage? DamagePerTurn { get; init; }
  46 | 
  47 |     /// <summary>Optional UI icon hint (sprite name / emoji / etc.).</summary>
  48 |     public string? Icon { get; init; }
  49 | }
  50 | 
  51 | /// <summary>
  52 | /// Per-turn damage dealt by a status effect (e.g. <c>1d6</c> poison).
  53 | /// </summary>
  54 | public sealed record PerTurnDamage(string Dice, string Type);

--- src/MyGame.Core/World/EntityFactory.cs (C#, 370 lines, ~4,048 tokens) ---
   1 | using MyGame.Core.Common;
   2 | using MyGame.Core.Rules;
   3 | using MyGame.Core.World.Content;
   4 | using MyGame.Core.World.Entities;
   5 | 
   6 | namespace MyGame.Core.World;
   7 | 
   8 | /// <summary>
   9 | /// Entity factories (ruleset-driven).
  10 | ///
  11 | /// Port of <c>engine/entities/factory.ts</c>. Pure functions that construct
  12 | /// runtime entities (<see cref="Player"/>, <see cref="Npc"/>,
  13 | /// <see cref="Item"/>, <see cref="Building"/>) from either explicit args or
  14 | /// content templates. Resource pools and attribute defaults are derived from
  15 | /// the world's <see cref="Ruleset"/> — no D&amp;D-specific derivation lives
  16 | /// here. <c>world.Ruleset</c> is the single source of truth for "what a
  17 | /// fresh character looks like".
  18 | /// </summary>
  19 | public static class EntityFactory
  20 | {
  21 |     // ─── Attributes / resources ────────────────────────────────────────────
  22 | 
  23 |     /// <summary>
  24 |     /// Build an attribute map seeded from the ruleset's per-attribute
  25 |     /// <see cref="AttributeDef.Default"/> values, overridden by any explicit
  26 |     /// values in <paramref name="partial"/>. Missing keys fall back to the
  27 |     /// ruleset default. Extra keys (not in the ruleset) are passed through
  28 |     /// unchanged so world-defined-on-the-fly attributes stay alive.
  29 |     /// </summary>
  30 |     public static Dictionary<string, int> MakeAttributes(
  31 |         Ruleset ruleset,
  32 |         IReadOnlyDictionary<string, int>? partial = null)
  33 |     {
  34 |         if (ruleset is null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(ruleset));
  35 |         var output = new Dictionary<string, int>();
  36 |         foreach (var def in ruleset.Attributes)
  37 |         {
  38 |             output[def.Key] = (partial is not null && partial.TryGetValue(def.Key, out var v))
  39 |                 ? v
  40 |                 : def.Default;
  41 |         }
  42 |         // Pass through any extra keys the caller passed that the ruleset
  43 |         // doesn't know about.
  44 |         if (partial is not null)
  45 |         {
  46 |             foreach (var (k, v) in partial)
  47 |             {
  48 |                 if (!output.ContainsKey(k)) output[k] = v;
  49 |             }
  50 |         }
  51 |         return output;
  52 |     }
  53 | 
  54 |     /// <summary>
  55 |     /// Build the full resource pool map for a character: every resource in
  56 |     /// the ruleset starts at its derived maximum (or <see cref="ResourceDef.Default"/>
  57 |     /// if no MaxFormula). Explicit overrides in <paramref name="partial"/>
  58 |     /// win, e.g. a wounded NPC spawning at half hp.
  59 |     /// </summary>
  60 |     public static Dictionary<string, int> MakeResources(
  61 |         Ruleset ruleset,
  62 |         IReadOnlyDictionary<string, int> attributes,
  63 |         int level = 1,
  64 |         IReadOnlyDictionary<string, int>? partial = null)
  65 |     {
  66 |         var derived = Rulesets.DeriveResources(ruleset, attributes, level);
  67 |         if (partial is not null)
  68 |         {
  69 |             foreach (var (k, v) in partial) derived[k] = v;
  70 |         }
  71 |         return derived;
  72 |     }
  73 | 
  74 |     // ─── Player ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  75 | 
  76 |     /// <summary>Arguments for <see cref="CreatePlayer"/>.</summary>
  77 |     public sealed record CreatePlayerArgs
  78 |     {
  79 |         public required string Name { get; init; }
  80 |         public string? Race { get; init; }
  81 |         public string? Class { get; init; }
  82 |         public int? Level { get; init; }
  83 |         public IReadOnlyDictionary<string, int>? Attributes { get; init; }
  84 |         public IReadOnlyDictionary<string, int>? Resources { get; init; }
  85 |         public required EntityId LocationId { get; init; }
  86 |         public IReadOnlyList<string>? ProficientSkills { get; init; }
  87 |         public int? StartingCurrency { get; init; }
  88 |         public string? Background { get; init; }
  89 |         public int? Speed { get; init; }
  90 |     }
  91 | 
  92 |     /// <summary>
  93 |     /// Construct a fresh player character. Resource pools and attribute
  94 |     /// defaults derive from <paramref name="ruleset"/> unless overridden.
  95 |     /// </summary>
  96 |     public static Player CreatePlayer(CreatePlayerArgs args, Ruleset? ruleset = null)
  97 |     {
  98 |         if (args is null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(args));
  99 |         var rs = ruleset ?? Rulesets.DefaultDnd;
 100 | 
 101 |         int level = args.Level ?? 1;
 102 |         var attributes = MakeAttributes(rs, args.Attributes);
 103 |         var resources = MakeResources(rs, attributes, level, args.Resources);
 104 |         int currency = args.StartingCurrency ?? rs.Currency.Default;
 105 | 
 106 |         return new Player
 107 |         {
 108 |             Id = EntityId.NewId(),
 109 |             Name = args.Name,
 110 |             Race = args.Race ?? "Human",
 111 |             Class = args.Class ?? "Adventurer",
 112 |             Level = level,
 113 |             Attributes = attributes,
 114 |             Resources = resources,
 115 |             ProficientSkills = args.ProficientSkills?.ToList() ?? new List<string>(),
 116 |             Equipped = new(),
 117 |             Inventory = new()
 118 |             {
 119 |                 Items = new(),
 120 |                 Currency = currency,
 121 |                 Capacity = 150,
 122 |             },
 123 |             Effects = new(),
 124 |             Speed = args.Speed ?? 30,
 125 |             LocationId = args.LocationId,
 126 |             IsAlive = true,
 127 |             Experience = 0,
 128 |             Background = args.Background,
 129 |         };
 130 |     }
 131 | 
 132 |     // ─── NPC ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 133 | 
 134 |     /// <summary>
 135 |     /// Construct an NPC from a content template. Resource pools are derived
 136 |     /// from the ruleset formulas (with template overrides winning); equipment
 137 |     /// and starting inventory are instantiated from the item registry.
 138 |     /// </summary>
 139 |     public static Npc CreateNpcFromTemplate(
 140 |         NpcTemplate template,
 141 |         EntityId locationId,
 142 |         ContentRegistry? registries = null,
 143 |         Ruleset? ruleset = null)
 144 |     {
 145 |         if (template is null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(template));
 146 |         var rs = ruleset ?? Rulesets.DefaultDnd;
 147 | 
 148 |         var attributes = MakeAttributes(rs, template.Attributes);
 149 |         int level = template.Level ?? 1;
 150 |         var resources = MakeResources(rs, attributes, level, template.Resources);
 151 | 
 152 |         var npc = new Npc
 153 |         {
 154 |             Id = EntityId.NewId(),
 155 |             Name = template.Name,
 156 |             TemplateId = template.Id,
 157 |             Race = template.Race,
 158 |             Class = template.Class,
 159 |             Level = level,
 160 |             Attributes = attributes,
 161 |             Resources = resources,
 162 |             ProficientSkills = new(),
 163 |             Equipped = new(),
 164 |             Inventory = new()
 165 |             {
 166 |                 Items = new(),
 167 |                 Currency = 0,
 168 |                 Capacity = 150,
 169 |             },
 170 |             Effects = new(),
 171 |             Disposition = template.Disposition ?? "neutral",
 172 |             Behavior = template.Behavior,
 173 |             AggroRange = template.AggroRange,
 174 |             ShopInventory = template.ShopInventory?.ToList(),
 175 |             Speed = template.Speed ?? 30,
 176 |             LocationId = locationId,
 177 |             IsAlive = true,
 178 |         };
 179 | 
 180 |         // Equip from templates — slot keys come from the template's equipment map.
 181 |         if (template.Equipment is not null && registries is not null)
 182 |         {
 183 |             foreach (var (slot, tplId) in template.Equipment)
 184 |             {
 185 |                 if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(tplId)) continue;
 186 |                 var t = registries.Items.Get(tplId);
 187 |                 if (t is null) continue;
 188 |                 var inst = InstantiateItem(t);
 189 |                 npc.Equipped[slot] = inst;
 190 |             }
 191 |             RecomputeAcResource(npc, rs);
 192 |         }
 193 | 
 194 |         // Starting inventory.
 195 |         if (template.StartingInventory is not null && registries is not null)
 196 |         {
 197 |             foreach (var entry in template.StartingInventory)
 198 |             {
 199 |                 var t = registries.Items.Get(entry.TemplateId);
 200 |                 if (t is null) continue;
 201 |                 npc.Inventory.Items.Add(InstantiateItem(t, entry.Quantity));
 202 |             }
 203 |         }
 204 | 
 205 |         return npc;
 206 |     }
 207 | 
 208 |     // ─── Items ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 209 | 
 210 |     /// <summary>
 211 |     /// Instantiate a runtime <see cref="Item"/> from an
 212 |     /// <see cref="ItemTemplate"/>. Quantity is clamped to &gt;= 1.
 213 |     /// </summary>
 214 |     public static Item InstantiateItem(ItemTemplate template, int quantity = 1)
 215 |     {
 216 |         if (template is null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(template));
 217 |         return new Item
 218 |         {
 219 |             Id = EntityId.NewId(),
 220 |             TemplateId = template.Id,
 221 |             Name = template.Name,
 222 |             Quantity = Math.Max(1, quantity),
 223 |             Equipped = false,
 224 |             // Denormalize the template's per-unit weight onto the instance so
 225 |             // the inventory panel can compute carried weight without a
 226 |             // registry lookup (older saves had Item without this field — they
 227 |             // default to 0 until re-instantiated; see Item.Weight TODO).
 228 |             Weight = template.Weight,
 229 |         };
 230 |     }
 231 | 
 232 |     // ─── Buildings ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 233 | 
 234 |     /// <summary>
 235 |     /// Construct a runtime <see cref="Building"/> from a content template.
 236 |     /// </summary>
 237 |     public static Building CreateBuilding(BuildingTemplate template, EntityId locationId)
 238 |     {
 239 |         if (template is null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(template));
 240 |         return new Building
 241 |         {
 242 |             Id = EntityId.NewId(),
 243 |             TemplateId = template.Id,
 244 |             Type = template.Type,
 245 |             Name = template.Name,
 246 |             Description = template.Description,
 247 |             LocationId = locationId,
 248 |             Occupants = new(),
 249 |             Enterable = template.Enterable,
 250 |             Locked = template.Locked,
 251 |         };
 252 |     }
 253 | 
 254 |     // ─── Status effects ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 255 | 
 256 |     /// <summary>Create a fresh status effect with a new id.</summary>
 257 |     public static StatusEffect CreateEffect(string name, string description, int duration = 3) =>
 258 |         new()
 259 |         {
 260 |             Id = EntityId.NewId(),
 261 |             Name = name,
 262 |             Description = description,
 263 |             Duration = duration,
 264 |         };
 265 | 
 266 |     // ─── Combat hints (legacy D&D armor/shield model) ──────────────────────
 267 |     //
 268 |     // The combat/inventory systems historically read private hint fields
 269 |     // attached to equipped Items at equip time, avoiding a registry lookup
 270 |     // per attack. The C# port keeps this contract for D&D-style worlds; for
 271 |     // worlds without armor/weapon blocks the hints simply aren't attached
 272 |     // and combat falls back to flat rolls.
 273 | 
 274 |     /// <summary>
 275 |     /// Recompute the <c>ac</c> resource (if present in the ruleset) from
 276 |     /// equipped armor/shield using the legacy D&amp;D model. Worlds without
 277 |     /// an <c>ac</c> resource or without armor items are unaffected.
 278 |     /// </summary>
 279 |     public static void RecomputeAcResource(Character c, Ruleset ruleset)
 280 |     {
 281 |         if (c is null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(c));
 282 |         if (ruleset is null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(ruleset));
 283 |         bool hasAc = ruleset.Resources.Any(r => r.Key == "ac");
 284 |         if (!hasAc) return;
 285 | 
 286 |         int dexVal = c.Attributes.TryGetValue("dex", out var dv) ? dv : 10;
 287 |         int dexMod = (int)Rulesets.AttributeModifier(ruleset, "dex", dexVal);
 288 | 
 289 |         int ac;
 290 |         if (c.Equipped.TryGetValue("armor", out var armor) && armor.TemplateId is not null)
 291 |         {
 292 |             // We don't have the template here without a registry hop; the
 293 |             // legacy code cached baseAc/dexBonusMax on the instance. For the
 294 |             // C# port, we instead fall back to 10 + dexMod when the armor
 295 |             // template can't be looked up. A future combat layer can fetch
 296 |             // the template via the content registry and recompute precisely.
 297 |             ac = 10 + dexMod;
 298 |         }
 299 |         else
 300 |         {
 301 |             ac = 10 + dexMod;
 302 |         }
 303 |         if (c.Equipped.ContainsKey("shield")) ac += 2;
 304 |         c.Resources["ac"] = ac;
 305 |     }
 306 | 
 307 |     /// <summary>
 308 |     /// Get the effective attribute values including effect modifiers.
 309 |     /// Defensive: ensures all ruleset attributes are present (fills in
 310 |     /// defaults for missing keys).
 311 |     /// </summary>
 312 |     public static Dictionary<string, int> EffectiveAttributes(
 313 |         Character c, Ruleset ruleset)
 314 |     {
 315 |         if (c is null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(c));
 316 |         if (ruleset is null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(ruleset));
 317 |         var output = new Dictionary<string, int>(c.Attributes);
 318 |         foreach (var eff in c.Effects)
 319 |         {
 320 |             if (eff.Modifiers is null) continue;
 321 |             foreach (var (k, v) in eff.Modifiers)
 322 |             {
 323 |                 output.TryGetValue(k, out var cur);
 324 |                 output[k] = cur + v;
 325 |             }
 326 |         }
 327 |         // Ensure all ruleset attributes are present (defensive).
 328 |         foreach (var def in ruleset.Attributes)
 329 |         {
 330 |             if (!output.ContainsKey(def.Key)) output[def.Key] = def.Default;
 331 |         }
 332 |         return output;
 333 |     }
 334 | 
 335 |     /// <summary>
 336 |     /// 4d6-drop-lowest for each of the ruleset's attributes. Useful for
 337 |     /// quick randomized character generation in D&amp;D-style worlds; in
 338 |     /// other worlds it's still mathematically fine but flavour-inappropriate.
 339 |     /// </summary>
 340 |     public static Dictionary<string, int> RollStartingAttributes(Rng rng, Ruleset? ruleset = null)
 341 |     {
 342 |         if (rng is null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(rng));
 343 |         var rs = ruleset ?? Rulesets.DefaultDnd;
 344 |         var output = new Dictionary<string, int>();
 345 |         foreach (var def in rs.Attributes)
 346 |         {
 347 |             int score = Roll4d6DropLowest(rng);
 348 |             // Clamp to the ruleset range.
 349 |             int clamped = Math.Max(def.Range.Min, Math.Min(def.Range.Max, score));
 350 |             output[def.Key] = clamped;
 351 |         }
 352 |         return output;
 353 |     }
 354 | 
 355 |     private static int Roll4d6DropLowest(Rng rng)
 356 |     {
 357 |         Span<int> rolls = stackalloc int[4];
 358 |         for (int i = 0; i < 4; i++) rolls[i] = rng.NextInt(1, 7);
 359 |         // Sort ascending, sum top 3.
 360 |         for (int i = 1; i < 4; i++)
 361 |         {
 362 |             int v = rolls[i];
 363 |             int j = i - 1;
 364 |             while (j >= 0 && rolls[j] > v) { rolls[j + 1] = rolls[j]; j--; }
 365 |             rolls[j + 1] = v;
 366 |         }
 367 |         return rolls[1] + rolls[2] + rolls[3];
 368 |     }
 369 | }

--- src/MyGame.Core/World/LoreDatabase.cs (C#, 62 lines, ~690 tokens) ---
   1 | namespace MyGame.Core.World;
   2 | 
   3 | /// <summary>
   4 | /// One entry in the world's lore database (issue #43): a topic (e.g.
   5 | /// <c>"history"</c>, <c>"magic"</c>) + its canonical content. The GM
   6 | /// queries entries via the <c>get_lore</c> tool to avoid hallucinating
   7 | /// world facts.
   8 | /// </summary>
   9 | public sealed class LoreEntry
  10 | {
  11 |     /// <summary>
  12 |     /// Topic key. Canonical values populated by the world-builder:
  13 |     /// <c>"deities"</c> (cosmology), <c>"history"</c>,
  14 |     /// <c>"magic"</c> (magic system), <c>"cultures"</c>,
  15 |     /// <c>"economy"</c>, <c>"current events"</c>. Free-form beyond
  16 |     /// that — the planner can introduce custom topics.
  17 |     /// </summary>
  18 |     public string Topic { get; set; } = "";
  19 | 
  20 |     /// <summary>
  21 |     /// Canonical content for this topic. Multi-line plain text (RU).
  22 |     /// The world-builder compiles this from the plan's structured lore
  23 |     /// fields (cosmology / history / magicSystem / cultures / economy /
  24 |     /// currentEvents).
  25 |     /// </summary>
  26 |     public string Content { get; set; } = "";
  27 | }
  28 | 
  29 | /// <summary>
  30 | /// Lore database for a world (issue #43): a list of <see cref="LoreEntry"/>
  31 | /// the GM can query via the <c>get_lore</c> tool. Null on
  32 | /// <see cref="World.Lore"/> means the world doesn't have a lore DB
  33 | /// (legacy saves / worlds built before this task); an empty database
  34 | /// means the builder ran but found no lore fields in the plan.
  35 | ///
  36 | /// <para>
  37 | /// <b>Serialization:</b> plain POCO + System.Text.Json via
  38 | /// <see cref="WorldJson.Options"/>. Round-trips through save/load.
  39 | /// </para>
  40 | /// </summary>
  41 | public sealed class LoreDatabase
  42 | {
  43 |     /// <summary>All lore entries, in planner order.</summary>
  44 |     public List<LoreEntry> Entries { get; set; } = new();
  45 | 
  46 |     /// <summary>
  47 |     /// Case-insensitive topic lookup. Returns null when the topic isn't
  48 |     /// in the database (the GM tool surfaces this as an error to the
  49 |     /// model so it can re-prompt with the available topics).
  50 |     /// </summary>
  51 |     public LoreEntry? Get(string topic) =>
  52 |         Entries.FirstOrDefault(e =>
  53 |             e.Topic.Equals(topic, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase));
  54 | 
  55 |     /// <summary>
  56 |     /// All available topic keys, in planner order. Used by the
  57 |     /// <c>get_lore</c> tool when no topic is supplied (lists what's
  58 |     /// queryable) and by the GM context block as a hint.
  59 |     /// </summary>
  60 |     public IReadOnlyList<string> Topics => Entries.Select(e => e.Topic).ToList();
  61 | }

--- src/MyGame.Core/World/Ruleset.cs (C#, 911 lines, ~10,657 tokens) ---
   1 | using System.Globalization;
   2 | using System.Text;
   3 | using System.Text.Json;
   4 | using System.Text.Json.Serialization;
   5 | using MyGame.Core.Common;
   6 | 
   7 | namespace MyGame.Core.World;
   8 | 
   9 | /// <summary>
  10 | /// How turns are scheduled in a multiplayer session. Port-side addition
  11 | /// (not in TS <c>ruleset.ts</c>) — the desktop rewrite's host server uses
  12 | /// this to decide when players may submit actions.
  13 | /// </summary>
  14 | public enum TurnModel
  15 | {
  16 |     /// <summary>
  17 |     /// Players enqueue actions as they arrive; the GM resolves them in order
  18 |     /// (one at a time, FIFO). Default for single-player and small groups.
  19 |     /// </summary>
  20 |     ActionQueue = 0,
  21 | 
  22 |     /// <summary>Strict turn-taking: only the active player may act.</summary>
  23 |     TurnBased = 1,
  24 | 
  25 |     /// <summary>
  26 |     /// Free-form actions outside combat, switch to TurnBased inside combat.
  27 |     /// </summary>
  28 |     Hybrid = 2,
  29 | }
  30 | 
  31 | /// <summary>
  32 | /// How LLM token cost is divided across players in a multiplayer session.
  33 | /// Port-side addition; the host computes each player's share using this.
  34 | /// </summary>
  35 | public enum TokenBillMode
  36 | {
  37 |     /// <summary>Host pays for everything (single-player mode default).</summary>
  38 |     Host = 0,
  39 | 
  40 |     /// <summary>Each player pays for their own turns' tokens.</summary>
  41 |     Random = 1,
  42 | 
  43 |     /// <summary>Tokens are pooled and split evenly across active players.</summary>
  44 |     Balanced = 2,
  45 | }
  46 | 
  47 | /// <summary>
  48 | /// What entities a player can see on the map. Drives the World's
  49 | /// visibility filter for the desktop UI.
  50 | /// </summary>
  51 | public enum Visibility
  52 | {
  53 |     /// <summary>Everything (debug / GM view).</summary>
  54 |     All = 0,
  55 | 
  56 |     /// <summary>Only the player's current location + discovered neighbors.</summary>
  57 |     Location = 1,
  58 | 
  59 |     /// <summary>
  60 |     /// Location + anything within a perception-radius (uses player's PER
  61 |     /// attribute to expand the visible set).
  62 |     /// </summary>
  63 |     Perception = 2,
  64 | }
  65 | 
  66 | /// <summary>
  67 | /// The dice/check mechanic the engine uses to resolve actions. Port of
  68 | /// <c>DiceMechanic</c> from <c>ruleset.ts</c>.
  69 | /// </summary>
  70 | public enum DiceMechanic
  71 | {
  72 |     D20 = 0,
  73 |     D100 = 1,
  74 |     D3d6 = 2,
  75 |     D6Pool = 3,
  76 |     TwoD6Pbta = 4,
  77 |     Coin = 5,
  78 |     D10Pool = 6,
  79 |     D6 = 7,
  80 | }
  81 | 
  82 | /// <summary>How a check result is interpreted against the difficulty.</summary>
  83 | public enum DifficultyModel
  84 | {
  85 |     Dc = 0,
  86 |     Tn = 1,
  87 |     SuccessCount = 2,
  88 |     Mixed = 3,
  89 | }
  90 | 
  91 | /// <summary>Visual category the UI uses to pick colors/icons for a resource.</summary>
  92 | public enum ResourceUI
  93 | {
  94 |     Health = 0,
  95 |     Mental = 1,
  96 |     Energy = 2,
  97 |     Armor = 3,
  98 |     Currency = 4,
  99 | }
 100 | 
 101 | /// <summary>What happens when a resource hits 0.</summary>
 102 | public enum OnZeroBehaviour
 103 | {
 104 |     Death = 0,
 105 |     Unconscious = 1,
 106 |     Madness = 2,
 107 |     Destruction = 3,
 108 |     Nothing = 4,
 109 | }
 110 | 
 111 | /// <summary>Progression model.</summary>
 112 | public enum ProgressionType
 113 | {
 114 |     LevelXp = 0,
 115 |     Milestone = 1,
 116 |     SkillBased = 2,
 117 |     None = 3,
 118 | }
 119 | 
 120 | /// <summary>A measurable character attribute (formerly the 6 D&D ability scores).</summary>
 121 | public sealed record AttributeDef
 122 | {
 123 |     /// <summary>Stable key referenced in formulas and character attributes, e.g. <c>str</c>.</summary>
 124 |     public required string Key { get; init; }
 125 | 
 126 |     /// <summary>Display name, e.g. «Сила».</summary>
 127 |     public required string Name { get; init; }
 128 | 
 129 |     /// <summary>Short UI abbreviation, e.g. «СИЛ».</summary>
 130 |     public string? Abbr { get; init; }
 131 | 
 132 |     /// <summary>Inclusive [min, max] range for the value.</summary>
 133 |     public required (int Min, int Max) Range { get; init; }
 134 | 
 135 |     /// <summary>Value used when a character has no explicit value set.</summary>
 136 |     public required int Default { get; init; }
 137 | 
 138 |     /// <summary>
 139 |     /// Flat modifier formula applied on checks, e.g. <c>floor((v-10)/2)</c>.
 140 |     /// <c>v</c> is the attribute value. Omit = no modifier.
 141 |     /// </summary>
 142 |     public string? ModifierFormula { get; init; }
 143 | }
 144 | 
 145 | /// <summary>A pool that depletes and refills (hp, mana, sanity, shield, stamina…).</summary>
 146 | public sealed record ResourceDef
 147 | {
 148 |     /// <summary>Stable key, e.g. <c>hp</c>, <c>mana</c>, <c>sanity</c>.</summary>
 149 |     public required string Key { get; init; }
 150 | 
 151 |     /// <summary>Display name, e.g. «Здоровье».</summary>
 152 |     public required string Name { get; init; }
 153 | 
 154 |     /// <summary>Short abbreviation, e.g. «HP».</summary>
 155 |     public string? Abbr { get; init; }
 156 | 
 157 |     /// <summary>
 158 |     /// Formula for the maximum value, evaluated against
 159 |     /// <c>{ level, &lt;attrKeys&gt;, &lt;attrKeys&gt;Mod }</c>. Omit if the
 160 |     /// resource has a fixed default instead.
 161 |     /// </summary>
 162 |     public string? MaxFormula { get; init; }
 163 | 
 164 |     /// <summary>Fixed starting/maximum value when no MaxFormula is provided.</summary>
 165 |     public int? Default { get; init; }
 166 | 
 167 |     /// <summary>Amount regenerated at the end of every combat turn.</summary>
 168 |     public int? RegenPerTurn { get; init; }
 169 | 
 170 |     /// <summary>Amount regenerated when the player rests.</summary>
 171 |     public int? RegenOnRest { get; init; }
 172 | 
 173 |     /// <summary>What happens when the resource hits 0.</summary>
 174 |     public required OnZeroBehaviour OnZero { get; init; }
 175 | 
 176 |     /// <summary>UI styling hint.</summary>
 177 |     public required ResourceUI UI { get; init; }
 178 | }
 179 | 
 180 | /// <summary>Currency definition (one per world).</summary>
 181 | public sealed record CurrencyDef
 182 | {
 183 |     /// <summary>Stable key, e.g. <c>gold</c>, <c>credits</c>, <c>caps</c>.</summary>
 184 |     public required string Key { get; init; }
 185 | 
 186 |     /// <summary>Display name, e.g. «Золото».</summary>
 187 |     public required string Name { get; init; }
 188 | 
 189 |     /// <summary>Symbol/prefix for the UI, e.g. <c>ζ</c>, <c>€$</c>.</summary>
 190 |     public string? Symbol { get; init; }
 191 | 
 192 |     /// <summary>Starting amount for a new player.</summary>
 193 |     public int Default { get; init; }
 194 | }
 195 | 
 196 | /// <summary>Progression model descriptor.</summary>
 197 | public sealed record ProgressionDef
 198 | {
 199 |     /// <summary>How characters grow.</summary>
 200 |     public required ProgressionType Type { get; init; }
 201 | 
 202 |     /// <summary>XP threshold formula for the next level, e.g. <c>level*300</c>.</summary>
 203 |     public string? XpFormula { get; init; }
 204 | 
 205 |     /// <summary>Maximum level cap. Null = uncapped.</summary>
 206 |     public int? MaxLevel { get; init; }
 207 | }
 208 | 
 209 | /// <summary>
 210 | /// The full rulebook for one world. Plain data — JSON-serializable.
 211 | ///
 212 | /// Port of <c>Ruleset</c> from <c>ruleset.ts</c>. The engine INTERPRETS the
 213 | /// ruleset — it never hardcodes D&amp;D. The default world ships
 214 | /// <see cref="Rulesets.DefaultDnd"/>; a world built from a cyberpunk/horror/
 215 | /// sci-fi brief gets a bespoke ruleset authored by the AI planner.
 216 | ///
 217 | /// Three port-side fields (<see cref="TurnModel"/>, <see cref="TokenBill"/>,
 218 | /// <see cref="Visibility"/>) are added for the multiplayer/desktop layer —
 219 | /// they configure host behavior and the UI's visibility filter. The TS
 220 | /// original lived entirely in the AI/engine layer and didn't need them.
 221 | /// </summary>
 222 | public sealed record Ruleset
 223 | {
 224 |     /// <summary>Stable identifier (e.g. <c>dnd5e-default</c>).</summary>
 225 |     public required string Id { get; init; }
 226 | 
 227 |     /// <summary>Human label, e.g. «D&D 5e (Долина Туманов)».</summary>
 228 |     public required string Name { get; init; }
 229 | 
 230 |     /// <summary>Genre/setting hint for prompts and UI, e.g. <c>dark-fantasy</c>.</summary>
 231 |     public required string Genre { get; init; }
 232 | 
 233 |     /// <summary>Multiplayer turn model. Port-side addition.</summary>
 234 |     public TurnModel TurnModel { get; init; } = TurnModel.ActionQueue;
 235 | 
 236 |     /// <summary>Token billing model. Port-side addition.</summary>
 237 |     public TokenBillMode TokenBill { get; init; } = TokenBillMode.Host;
 238 | 
 239 |     /// <summary>Player visibility mode. Port-side addition.</summary>
 240 |     public Visibility Visibility { get; init; } = Visibility.Location;
 241 | 
 242 |     /// <summary>Attribute definitions (size &gt;= 1 for a valid ruleset).</summary>
 243 |     public required IReadOnlyList<AttributeDef> Attributes { get; init; }
 244 | 
 245 |     /// <summary>Resource definitions (hp, mana, sanity…).</summary>
 246 |     public required IReadOnlyList<ResourceDef> Resources { get; init; }
 247 | 
 248 |     /// <summary>Primary dice/check mechanic.</summary>
 249 |     public DiceMechanic Dice { get; init; } = DiceMechanic.D20;
 250 | 
 251 |     /// <summary>How a check result is interpreted against the difficulty.</summary>
 252 |     public DifficultyModel Difficulty { get; init; } = DifficultyModel.Dc;
 253 | 
 254 |     /// <summary>
 255 |     /// Natural roll values that count as auto-crit (success) and auto-fumble
 256 |     /// (failure) on the primary die. For d20 defaults to {20, 1}. Null =
 257 |     /// crit-less system.
 258 |     /// </summary>
 259 |     public (int Success, int Failure)? CriticalRange { get; init; }
 260 | 
 261 |     /// <summary>Currency definition.</summary>
 262 |     public required CurrencyDef Currency { get; init; }
 263 | 
 264 |     /// <summary>Progression model.</summary>
 265 |     public required ProgressionDef Progression { get; init; }
 266 | 
 267 |     /// <summary>Damage type vocabulary for this world.</summary>
 268 |     public required IReadOnlyList<string> DamageTypes { get; init; }
 269 | 
 270 |     /// <summary>Interaction keywords the damage resolver understands.</summary>
 271 |     public IReadOnlyList<string>? DamageInteractions { get; init; }
 272 | 
 273 |     /// <summary>Item categories this world uses.</summary>
 274 |     public required IReadOnlyList<string> ItemCategories { get; init; }
 275 | 
 276 |     /// <summary>Building types this world uses.</summary>
 277 |     public required IReadOnlyList<string> BuildingTypes { get; init; }
 278 | 
 279 |     /// <summary>Terrain types this world uses.</summary>
 280 |     public required IReadOnlyList<string> TerrainTypes { get; init; }
 281 | 
 282 |     /// <summary>Equipment slot keys characters can fill.</summary>
 283 |     public required IReadOnlyList<string> EquipmentSlots { get; init; }
 284 | 
 285 |     /// <summary>Optional genre-specific subsystems to enable.</summary>
 286 |     public IReadOnlyList<string>? Modules { get; init; }
 287 | 
 288 |     // ─── Custom-ruleset overlays (issue #21) ─────────────────────────────
 289 |     //
 290 |     // The desktop MVP's default ruleset is <see cref="Rulesets.DefaultDnd"/>
 291 |     // (D&D 5e-flavoured). When the world-builder orchestrator's ruleset
 292 |     // stage runs for a non-fantasy theme (cyberpunk / sci-fi / steampunk /
 293 |     // postapoc / horror / modern), it asks the AI to design a lightweight
 294 |     // overlay on top of the default — custom display names for the six
 295 |     // standard attribute keys + custom resource-pool names + a custom skill
 296 |     // list — without forcing the AI to design a full AttributeDef /
 297 |     // ResourceDef schema. This keeps the ruleset design call short (cheap
 298 |     // and reliable) while still letting a cyberpunk world call its
 299 |     // attributes "Cool / Edge / Meat / Tech / Luck / Will" and its hp pool
 300 |     // "Stress".
 301 |     //
 302 |     // All three fields are nullable — null means "use the defaults from
 303 |     // <see cref="Attributes"/> / <see cref="Resources"/> / the standard
 304 |     // D&D skill list". The orchestrator sets them after a successful
 305 |     // ruleset-design AI call; the GM context block reads them so the GM
 306 |     // knows what to call the attributes; the EntityFactory uses them when
 307 |     // materialising fresh characters so a new player gets a "Cool" entry
 308 |     // instead of "СИЛ".
 309 | 
 310 |     /// <summary>
 311 |     /// Optional map from standard attribute keys (<c>str</c>, <c>dex</c>,
 312 |     /// <c>con</c>, <c>int</c>, <c>wis</c>, <c>cha</c>) to custom display
 313 |     /// names for non-fantasy worlds. Set by the world-builder's ruleset
 314 |     /// stage when the AI designs a custom overlay (issue #21). Null means
 315 |     /// use the default attribute names from <see cref="Attributes"/>.
 316 |     /// </summary>
 317 |     public Dictionary<string, string>? AttributeNames { get; init; }
 318 | 
 319 |     /// <summary>
 320 |     /// Optional map from standard resource keys (<c>hp</c>, <c>ac</c>) to
 321 |     /// custom display names. Set by the world-builder's ruleset stage
 322 |     /// (issue #21). Null means use the default resource names from
 323 |     /// <see cref="Resources"/>.
 324 |     /// </summary>
 325 |     public Dictionary<string, string>? ResourcePools { get; init; }
 326 | 
 327 |     /// <summary>
 328 |     /// Optional custom skill list for the world (e.g. <c>hacking</c>,
 329 |     /// <c>gunnery</c>, <c>streetwise</c>). Set by the world-builder's
 330 |     /// ruleset stage (issue #21). Null means use the default D&D skill
 331 |     /// list. Players' <c>ProficientSkills</c> are matched by string, so a
 332 |     /// custom list flows naturally into character creation.
 333 |     /// </summary>
 334 |     public IReadOnlyList<string>? Skills { get; init; }
 335 | }
 336 | 
 337 | /// <summary>
 338 | /// Default ruleset instances and static helpers.
 339 | /// </summary>
 340 | public static class Rulesets
 341 | {
 342 |     /// <summary>
 343 |     /// The D&amp;D-5e-flavoured ruleset used by the default world and as a
 344 |     /// fallback when a custom build fails to commit a valid ruleset. Encoded
 345 |     /// purely as data — no behaviour beyond what the generic engine implements.
 346 |     ///
 347 |     /// HP formula: at L1 the fighter-style hit die (8) + CON mod; each
 348 |     /// additional level adds 5 + CON mod (average of a d8).
 349 |     /// </summary>
 350 |     public static Ruleset DefaultDnd { get; } = new Ruleset
 351 |     {
 352 |         Id = "dnd5e-default",
 353 |         Name = "D&D 5e (Долина Туманов)",
 354 |         Genre = "dark-fantasy",
 355 |         TurnModel = TurnModel.ActionQueue,
 356 |         TokenBill = TokenBillMode.Host,
 357 |         Visibility = Visibility.Location,
 358 |         Attributes = new List<AttributeDef>
 359 |         {
 360 |             new() { Key = "str", Name = "Сила", Abbr = "СИЛ", Range = (3, 20), Default = 10, ModifierFormula = "floor((v-10)/2)" },
 361 |             new() { Key = "dex", Name = "Ловкость", Abbr = "ЛОВ", Range = (3, 20), Default = 10, ModifierFormula = "floor((v-10)/2)" },
 362 |             new() { Key = "con", Name = "Телосложение", Abbr = "ТЕЛ", Range = (3, 20), Default = 10, ModifierFormula = "floor((v-10)/2)" },
 363 |             new() { Key = "int", Name = "Интеллект", Abbr = "ИНТ", Range = (3, 20), Default = 10, ModifierFormula = "floor((v-10)/2)" },
 364 |             new() { Key = "per", Name = "Восприятие", Abbr = "ВСП", Range = (3, 20), Default = 10, ModifierFormula = "floor((v-10)/2)" },
 365 |             new() { Key = "cha", Name = "Харизма", Abbr = "ХАР", Range = (3, 20), Default = 10, ModifierFormula = "floor((v-10)/2)" },
 366 |         },
 367 |         Resources = new List<ResourceDef>
 368 |         {
 369 |             new()
 370 |             {
 371 |                 Key = "hp",
 372 |                 Name = "Здоровье",
 373 |                 Abbr = "HP",
 374 |                 MaxFormula = "8 + conMod + (level-1) * (5 + conMod)",
 375 |                 RegenOnRest = 0,
 376 |                 OnZero = OnZeroBehaviour.Death,
 377 |                 UI = ResourceUI.Health,
 378 |             },
 379 |         },
 380 |         Dice = DiceMechanic.D20,
 381 |         Difficulty = DifficultyModel.Dc,
 382 |         CriticalRange = (20, 1),
 383 |         Currency = new CurrencyDef { Key = "gold", Name = "Золото", Default = 10 },
 384 |         Progression = new ProgressionDef { Type = ProgressionType.LevelXp, XpFormula = "level*300", MaxLevel = 20 },
 385 |         DamageTypes = new List<string>
 386 |         {
 387 |             "slashing", "piercing", "bludgeoning",
 388 |             "fire", "cold", "lightning", "thunder", "acid", "poison",
 389 |             "psychic", "necrotic", "radiant", "force",
 390 |         },
 391 |         DamageInteractions = new List<string> { "resistant", "immune", "vulnerable" },
 392 |         ItemCategories = new List<string>
 393 |         {
 394 |             "weapon", "armor", "shield", "consumable",
 395 |             "tool", "treasure", "key", "misc", "quest",
 396 |         },
 397 |         BuildingTypes = new List<string>
 398 |         {
 399 |             "tavern", "shop", "temple", "house", "tower",
 400 |             "dungeon", "cave", "ruin", "fortress", "landmark", "custom",
 401 |         },
 402 |         TerrainTypes = new List<string>
 403 |         {
 404 |             "plains", "forest", "mountain", "desert", "swamp",
 405 |             "water", "urban", "underground", "snow", "coast", "void",
 406 |         },
 407 |         EquipmentSlots = new List<string> { "weapon", "armor", "shield" },
 408 |     };
 409 | 
 410 |     /// <summary>
 411 |     /// Minimal starter ruleset handed to a fresh custom world BEFORE the
 412 |     /// planner commits its bespoke ruleset. The planner's commit_ruleset
 413 |     /// call overwrites <c>world.Ruleset</c> entirely.
 414 |     /// </summary>
 415 |     public static Ruleset Starter { get; } = DefaultDnd with
 416 |     {
 417 |         Id = "starter-pending",
 418 |         Name = "Система мира (в разработке)",
 419 |     };
 420 | 
 421 |     // ─── Lookup helpers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 422 | 
 423 |     /// <summary>Find an attribute def by key. Null if not present.</summary>
 424 |     public static AttributeDef? GetAttribute(Ruleset rs, string key) =>
 425 |         rs.Attributes.FirstOrDefault(a => a.Key == key);
 426 | 
 427 |     /// <summary>Find a resource def by key. Null if not present.</summary>
 428 |     public static ResourceDef? GetResource(Ruleset rs, string key) =>
 429 |         rs.Resources.FirstOrDefault(r => r.Key == key);
 430 | 
 431 |     // ─── Custom-ruleset overlay helpers (issue #21) ──────────────────────
 432 | 
 433 |     /// <summary>
 434 |     /// Resolve the display name for an attribute key. When
 435 |     /// <see cref="Ruleset.AttributeNames"/> is set and contains the key,
 436 |     /// returns the custom name; otherwise falls back to the matching
 437 |     /// <see cref="AttributeDef.Name"/> from the ruleset; otherwise
 438 |     /// returns the key itself. Used by the GM context block (so the GM
 439 |     /// knows what to call the attributes) and by the character panel UI.
 440 |     /// </summary>
 441 |     public static string GetAttributeName(Ruleset rs, string key)
 442 |     {
 443 |         if (rs is null) return key;
 444 |         if (rs.AttributeNames is not null && rs.AttributeNames.TryGetValue(key, out var custom) && !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(custom))
 445 |             return custom;
 446 |         var def = GetAttribute(rs, key);
 447 |         return def?.Name ?? key;
 448 |     }
 449 | 
 450 |     /// <summary>
 451 |     /// Resolve the display name for a resource key (e.g. <c>hp</c> →
 452 |     /// «Здоровье» or «Стресс» for a cyberpunk world). Falls back to the
 453 |     /// <see cref="ResourceDef.Name"/>; finally to the key itself.
 454 |     /// </summary>
 455 |     public static string GetResourceName(Ruleset rs, string key)
 456 |     {
 457 |         if (rs is null) return key;
 458 |         if (rs.ResourcePools is not null && rs.ResourcePools.TryGetValue(key, out var custom) && !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(custom))
 459 |             return custom;
 460 |         var def = GetResource(rs, key);
 461 |         return def?.Name ?? key;
 462 |     }
 463 | 
 464 |     // ─── Formula evaluation ────────────────────────────────────────────────
 465 | 
 466 |     /// <summary>
 467 |     /// Apply an attribute's <see cref="AttributeDef.ModifierFormula"/> to a
 468 |     /// raw value. If the attribute has no formula (or isn't found), returns 0.
 469 |     /// </summary>
 470 |     public static double AttributeModifier(Ruleset rs, string key, int value)
 471 |     {
 472 |         var def = GetAttribute(rs, key);
 473 |         if (def?.ModifierFormula is null) return 0;
 474 |         return FormulaEval.Eval(def.ModifierFormula, new Dictionary<string, double> { ["v"] = value });
 475 |     }
 476 | 
 477 |     /// <summary>
 478 |     /// Build the full variable scope used by MaxFormula/XpFormula:
 479 |     /// <c>{ level, &lt;attrKey&gt;, &lt;attrKey&gt;Mod }</c> for every attribute
 480 |     /// in the ruleset.
 481 |     /// </summary>
 482 |     public static Dictionary<string, double> BuildFormulaScope(
 483 |         Ruleset rs,
 484 |         IReadOnlyDictionary<string, int> attributes,
 485 |         int level = 1)
 486 |     {
 487 |         var scope = new Dictionary<string, double> { ["level"] = level };
 488 |         foreach (var def in rs.Attributes)
 489 |         {
 490 |             int v = attributes.TryGetValue(def.Key, out var av) ? av : def.Default;
 491 |             scope[def.Key] = v;
 492 |             scope[def.Key + "Mod"] = AttributeModifier(rs, def.Key, v);
 493 |         }
 494 |         return scope;
 495 |     }
 496 | 
 497 |     /// <summary>
 498 |     /// Derive every resource maximum from the ruleset given a character's
 499 |     /// attributes and level. Resources with no MaxFormula use their
 500 |     /// <see cref="ResourceDef.Default"/> (or 0). Returns a map keyed by
 501 |     /// resource key.
 502 |     /// </summary>
 503 |     public static Dictionary<string, int> DeriveResources(
 504 |         Ruleset rs,
 505 |         IReadOnlyDictionary<string, int> attributes,
 506 |         int level = 1)
 507 |     {
 508 |         var scope = BuildFormulaScope(rs, attributes, level);
 509 |         var output = new Dictionary<string, int>();
 510 |         foreach (var def in rs.Resources)
 511 |         {
 512 |             if (def.MaxFormula is { } formula)
 513 |             {
 514 |                 output[def.Key] = Math.Max(0, (int)Math.Floor(FormulaEval.Eval(formula, scope)));
 515 |             }
 516 |             else
 517 |             {
 518 |                 output[def.Key] = def.Default ?? 0;
 519 |             }
 520 |         }
 521 |         return output;
 522 |     }
 523 | 
 524 |     /// <summary>
 525 |     /// Find the world's "vital" resource — the one whose OnZero is Death or
 526 |     /// Destruction (hp in D&amp;D, hull in a starship, structure for a mech).
 527 |     /// Null if the world has none (pure social / puzzle world).
 528 |     /// </summary>
 529 |     public static string? VitalResourceKey(Ruleset rs) =>
 530 |         rs.Resources.FirstOrDefault(r => r.OnZero is OnZeroBehaviour.Death or OnZeroBehaviour.Destruction)?.Key;
 531 | 
 532 |     /// <summary>
 533 |     /// Temporary-HP resource key (a resource named <c>temphp</c> or
 534 |     /// <c>tempHp</c>), if any.
 535 |     /// </summary>
 536 |     public static string? TempResourceKey(Ruleset rs) =>
 537 |         rs.Resources.FirstOrDefault(r => r.Key is "temphp" or "tempHp")?.Key;
 538 | 
 539 |     /// <summary>
 540 |     /// Compute a resource's current max for a given attribute map and level —
 541 |     /// either from its MaxFormula (evaluated against the scope) or its flat
 542 |     /// <see cref="ResourceDef.Default"/>. Used by damage/heal clamps and the UI.
 543 |     /// Returns <see cref="double.PositiveInfinity"/> if the resource isn't
 544 |     /// found OR has neither a formula nor a default (treated as unbounded).
 545 |     /// </summary>
 546 |     public static double DerivedResourceMax(
 547 |         Ruleset rs,
 548 |         IReadOnlyDictionary<string, int> attributes,
 549 |         int level,
 550 |         string resourceKey)
 551 |     {
 552 |         var def = GetResource(rs, resourceKey);
 553 |         if (def is null) return double.PositiveInfinity;
 554 |         if (def.MaxFormula is null) return def.Default ?? double.PositiveInfinity;
 555 |         var scope = BuildFormulaScope(rs, attributes, level);
 556 |         return Math.Max(0, Math.Floor(FormulaEval.Eval(def.MaxFormula, scope)));
 557 |     }
 558 | 
 559 |     /// <summary>True if this ruleset uses level/xp or milestone progression.</summary>
 560 |     public static bool HasLevels(Ruleset rs) =>
 561 |         rs.Progression.Type is ProgressionType.LevelXp or ProgressionType.Milestone;
 562 | 
 563 |     /// <summary>XP needed to advance from the given level to the next.</summary>
 564 |     public static int XpForLevel(Ruleset rs, int level)
 565 |     {
 566 |         if (rs.Progression.XpFormula is null) return int.MaxValue;
 567 |         return Math.Max(1, (int)Math.Floor(
 568 |             FormulaEval.Eval(rs.Progression.XpFormula, new Dictionary<string, double> { ["level"] = level })));
 569 |     }
 570 | 
 571 |     /// <summary>
 572 |     /// Validate a candidate ruleset. Returns a list of human-readable
 573 |     /// problems; empty list = valid. Used by the commit_ruleset tool to
 574 |     /// reject malformed AI-authored schemas before they touch world state.
 575 |     /// </summary>
 576 |     public static List<string> Validate(Ruleset? rs)
 577 |     {
 578 |         var problems = new List<string>();
 579 |         if (rs is null) { problems.Add("ruleset is null"); return problems; }
 580 |         if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(rs.Id)) problems.Add("id must be a non-empty string");
 581 |         if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(rs.Name)) problems.Add("name must be a non-empty string");
 582 |         if (rs.Attributes is null || rs.Attributes.Count == 0)
 583 |         {
 584 |             problems.Add("attributes must be a non-empty array");
 585 |         }
 586 |         else
 587 |         {
 588 |             var seen = new HashSet<string>();
 589 |             foreach (var a in rs.Attributes)
 590 |             {
 591 |                 if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(a.Key)) problems.Add($"attribute missing key");
 592 |                 else if (!seen.Add(a.Key)) problems.Add($"duplicate attribute key: {a.Key}");
 593 |                 if (a.Range.Min > a.Range.Max) problems.Add($"attribute {a.Key}: range.Min > range.Max");
 594 |                 if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(a.ModifierFormula))
 595 |                 {
 596 |                     if (!FormulaEval.TryCompile(a.ModifierFormula, out var ferr))
 597 |                         problems.Add($"attribute {a.Key}: invalid modifierFormula \"{a.ModifierFormula}\" ({ferr})");
 598 |                 }
 599 |             }
 600 |         }
 601 |         if (rs.Resources is null) problems.Add("resources must be an array");
 602 |         else
 603 |         {
 604 |             var seen = new HashSet<string>();
 605 |             foreach (var r in rs.Resources)
 606 |             {
 607 |                 if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(r.Key)) problems.Add("resource missing key");
 608 |                 else if (!seen.Add(r.Key)) problems.Add($"duplicate resource key: {r.Key}");
 609 |                 if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(r.MaxFormula))
 610 |                 {
 611 |                     if (!FormulaEval.TryCompile(r.MaxFormula, out var ferr))
 612 |                         problems.Add($"resource {r.Key}: invalid maxFormula \"{r.MaxFormula}\" ({ferr})");
 613 |                 }
 614 |             }
 615 |         }
 616 |         if (!Enum.IsDefined(rs.Dice)) problems.Add($"dice invalid: {rs.Dice}");
 617 |         if (!Enum.IsDefined(rs.Difficulty)) problems.Add($"difficulty invalid: {rs.Difficulty}");
 618 |         if (rs.Currency is null || string.IsNullOrEmpty(rs.Currency.Key))
 619 |             problems.Add("currency.key must be a non-empty string");
 620 |         if (rs.Progression is null || !Enum.IsDefined(rs.Progression.Type))
 621 |             problems.Add("progression.type invalid");
 622 |         if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(rs.Progression?.XpFormula))
 623 |         {
 624 |             if (!FormulaEval.TryCompile(rs.Progression.XpFormula, out var ferr))
 625 |                 problems.Add($"invalid progression.xpFormula \"{rs.Progression.XpFormula}\" ({ferr})");
 626 |         }
 627 |         if (rs.DamageTypes is null) problems.Add("damageTypes must be an array");
 628 |         if (rs.ItemCategories is null) problems.Add("itemCategories must be an array");
 629 |         if (rs.BuildingTypes is null) problems.Add("buildingTypes must be an array");
 630 |         if (rs.TerrainTypes is null) problems.Add("terrainTypes must be an array");
 631 |         if (rs.EquipmentSlots is null) problems.Add("equipmentSlots must be an array");
 632 |         return problems;
 633 |     }
 634 | }
 635 | 
 636 | /// <summary>
 637 | /// Tiny sandboxed arithmetic expression evaluator used by
 638 | /// <see cref="Rulesets"/> to evaluate attribute modifier formulas, resource
 639 | /// max formulas, and XP formulas.
 640 | ///
 641 | /// Replaces the TS dependency on the <c>expr-eval</c> npm package. Supports:
 642 | ///  - integer and decimal literals (e.g. <c>8</c>, <c>0.5</c>),
 643 | ///  - variable references (e.g. <c>v</c>, <c>level</c>, <c>conMod</c>),
 644 | ///  - binary <c>+ - * /</c>,
 645 | ///  - unary <c>- +</c>,
 646 | ///  - parentheses,
 647 | ///  - function calls: <c>floor</c>, <c>ceil</c>, <c>round</c>, <c>abs</c>,
 648 | ///    <c>min</c>, <c>max</c>, <c>sqrt</c>, <c>pow</c>.
 649 | ///
 650 | /// No member access, no assignments, no string ops — strictly numeric. A
 651 | /// malformed expression throws <see cref="FormatException"/>; callers should
 652 | /// use <see cref="Eval"/> which never throws (returns 0 on failure, matching
 653 | /// the TS <c>evalFormula</c> semantics).
 654 | /// </summary>
 655 | public static class FormulaEval
 656 | {
 657 |     /// <summary>
 658 |     /// Evaluate an expression against the given variable scope. Never throws:
 659 |     /// returns <paramref name="fallback"/> (default 0) on parse/eval failure
 660 |     /// so a malformed AI-authored formula can never crash the engine.
 661 |     /// </summary>
 662 |     public static double Eval(string expr, IReadOnlyDictionary<string, double> vars, double fallback = 0)
 663 |     {
 664 |         if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(expr)) return fallback;
 665 |         try
 666 |         {
 667 |             var tokens = Tokenize(expr);
 668 |             var parser = new Parser(tokens, vars);
 669 |             double result = parser.ParseExpression();
 670 |             parser.ExpectEnd();
 671 |             return double.IsFinite(result) ? result : fallback;
 672 |         }
 673 |         catch
 674 |         {
 675 |             return fallback;
 676 |         }
 677 |     }
 678 | 
 679 |     /// <summary>
 680 |     /// Check whether an expression parses cleanly. Does NOT evaluate it (so
 681 |     /// missing variable bindings don't fail — formulas reference runtime
 682 |     /// variables like <c>v</c>, <c>level</c>, <c>conMod</c> that aren't bound
 683 |     /// until a character is in scope). Mirrors the TS <c>parser.parse()</c>
 684 |     /// syntax-only check. Returns the parse error message via
 685 |     /// <paramref name="error"/> when it fails.
 686 |     /// </summary>
 687 |     public static bool TryCompile(string expr, out string? error)
 688 |     {
 689 |         if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(expr)) { error = "empty expression"; return false; }
 690 |         try
 691 |         {
 692 |             var tokens = Tokenize(expr);
 693 |             // Use a permissive variable scope that returns 0 for any key, so
 694 |             // the parse-and-evaluate walk exercises the full grammar without
 695 |             // tripping on unbound runtime variables.
 696 |             var parser = new Parser(tokens, PermissiveVars.Instance);
 697 |             parser.ParseExpression();
 698 |             parser.ExpectEnd();
 699 |             error = null;
 700 |             return true;
 701 |         }
 702 |         catch (Exception ex)
 703 |         {
 704 |             error = ex.Message;
 705 |             return false;
 706 |         }
 707 |     }
 708 | 
 709 |     /// <summary>
 710 |     /// Variable scope that returns 0 for any key — used by
 711 |     /// <see cref="TryCompile"/> so the syntax check doesn't reject formulas
 712 |     /// that reference runtime variables (like <c>v</c> or <c>level</c>).
 713 |     /// </summary>
 714 |     private sealed class PermissiveVars : IReadOnlyDictionary<string, double>
 715 |     {
 716 |         public static readonly PermissiveVars Instance = new();
 717 |         public double this[string key] => 0;
 718 |         public IEnumerable<string> Keys => Array.Empty<string>();
 719 |         public IEnumerable<double> Values => Array.Empty<double>();
 720 |         public int Count => 0;
 721 |         public bool ContainsKey(string key) => true;
 722 |         public IEnumerator<KeyValuePair<string, double>> GetEnumerator() => Enumerable.Empty<KeyValuePair<string, double>>().GetEnumerator();
 723 |         System.Collections.IEnumerator System.Collections.IEnumerable.GetEnumerator() => GetEnumerator();
 724 |         public bool TryGetValue(string key, out double value) { value = 0; return true; }
 725 |     }
 726 | 
 727 |     // ── Tokenizer ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 728 |     private enum TokenType { Number, Ident, Plus, Minus, Star, Slash, Comma, LParen, RParen, End }
 729 | 
 730 |     private readonly struct Token(TokenType type, string text, double value)
 731 |     {
 732 |         public readonly TokenType Type = type;
 733 |         public readonly string Text = text;
 734 |         public readonly double Value = value;
 735 |     }
 736 | 
 737 |     private static List<Token> Tokenize(string expr)
 738 |     {
 739 |         var tokens = new List<Token>();
 740 |         int i = 0;
 741 |         while (i < expr.Length)
 742 |         {
 743 |             char c = expr[i];
 744 |             if (char.IsWhiteSpace(c)) { i++; continue; }
 745 |             switch (c)
 746 |             {
 747 |                 case '+': tokens.Add(new Token(TokenType.Plus, "+", 0)); i++; break;
 748 |                 case '-': tokens.Add(new Token(TokenType.Minus, "-", 0)); i++; break;
 749 |                 case '*': tokens.Add(new Token(TokenType.Star, "*", 0)); i++; break;
 750 |                 case '/': tokens.Add(new Token(TokenType.Slash, "/", 0)); i++; break;
 751 |                 case ',': tokens.Add(new Token(TokenType.Comma, ",", 0)); i++; break;
 752 |                 case '(': tokens.Add(new Token(TokenType.LParen, "(", 0)); i++; break;
 753 |                 case ')': tokens.Add(new Token(TokenType.RParen, ")", 0)); i++; break;
 754 |                 default:
 755 |                     if (char.IsDigit(c) || c == '.')
 756 |                     {
 757 |                         int start = i;
 758 |                         while (i < expr.Length && (char.IsDigit(expr[i]) || expr[i] == '.')) i++;
 759 |                         if (!double.TryParse(expr.AsSpan(start, i - start), NumberStyles.Float, CultureInfo.InvariantCulture, out var d))
 760 |                             throw new FormatException($"invalid number at offset {start}");
 761 |                         tokens.Add(new Token(TokenType.Number, expr.Substring(start, i - start), d));
 762 |                     }
 763 |                     else if (char.IsLetter(c) || c == '_')
 764 |                     {
 765 |                         int start = i;
 766 |                         while (i < expr.Length && (char.IsLetterOrDigit(expr[i]) || expr[i] == '_')) i++;
 767 |                         tokens.Add(new Token(TokenType.Ident, expr.Substring(start, i - start), 0));
 768 |                     }
 769 |                     else
 770 |                     {
 771 |                         throw new FormatException($"unexpected char '{c}' at offset {i}");
 772 |                     }
 773 |                     break;
 774 |             }
 775 |         }
 776 |         tokens.Add(new Token(TokenType.End, "", 0));
 777 |         return tokens;
 778 |     }
 779 | 
 780 |     // ── Recursive-descent parser ───────────────────────────────────────────
 781 |     //
 782 |     // Grammar:
 783 |     //   expression := term (('+' | '-') term)*
 784 |     //   term       := factor (('*' | '/') factor)*
 785 |     //   factor     := number | ident | func-call | '(' expression ')' | ('-' | '+') factor
 786 |     //   func-call  := ident '(' expression (',' expression)* ')'
 787 |     private sealed class Parser
 788 |     {
 789 |         private readonly List<Token> _tokens;
 790 |         private int _pos;
 791 |         private readonly IReadOnlyDictionary<string, double> _vars;
 792 | 
 793 |         public Parser(List<Token> tokens, IReadOnlyDictionary<string, double> vars)
 794 |         {
 795 |             _tokens = tokens;
 796 |             _vars = vars;
 797 |         }
 798 | 
 799 |         public double ParseExpression()
 800 |         {
 801 |             double v = ParseTerm();
 802 |             while (Peek().Type is TokenType.Plus or TokenType.Minus)
 803 |             {
 804 |                 var t = Next();
 805 |                 double rhs = ParseTerm();
 806 |                 v = t.Type == TokenType.Plus ? v + rhs : v - rhs;
 807 |             }
 808 |             return v;
 809 |         }
 810 | 
 811 |         private double ParseTerm()
 812 |         {
 813 |             double v = ParseFactor();
 814 |             while (Peek().Type is TokenType.Star or TokenType.Slash)
 815 |             {
 816 |                 var t = Next();
 817 |                 double rhs = ParseFactor();
 818 |                 if (t.Type == TokenType.Star) v *= rhs;
 819 |                 else
 820 |                 {
 821 |                     if (rhs == 0) throw new DivideByZeroException("division by zero in formula");
 822 |                     v /= rhs;
 823 |                 }
 824 |             }
 825 |             return v;
 826 |         }
 827 | 
 828 |         private double ParseFactor()
 829 |         {
 830 |             var t = Peek();
 831 |             // Unary minus / plus.
 832 |             if (t.Type == TokenType.Minus) { Next(); return -ParseFactor(); }
 833 |             if (t.Type == TokenType.Plus) { Next(); return ParseFactor(); }
 834 | 
 835 |             if (t.Type == TokenType.Number)
 836 |             {
 837 |                 Next();
 838 |                 return t.Value;
 839 |             }
 840 |             if (t.Type == TokenType.Ident)
 841 |             {
 842 |                 Next();
 843 |                 // Function call?
 844 |                 if (Peek().Type == TokenType.LParen)
 845 |                 {
 846 |                     Next(); // consume (
 847 |                     var args = new List<double>();
 848 |                     if (Peek().Type != TokenType.RParen)
 849 |                     {
 850 |                         args.Add(ParseExpression());
 851 |                         while (Peek().Type == TokenType.Comma)
 852 |                         {
 853 |                             Next();
 854 |                             args.Add(ParseExpression());
 855 |                         }
 856 |                     }
 857 |                     Expect(TokenType.RParen);
 858 |                     return ApplyFunction(t.Text, args);
 859 |                 }
 860 |                 // Variable reference.
 861 |                 if (_vars.TryGetValue(t.Text, out var v)) return v;
 862 |                 throw new FormatException($"unknown variable '{t.Text}'");
 863 |             }
 864 |             if (t.Type == TokenType.LParen)
 865 |             {
 866 |                 Next();
 867 |                 double v = ParseExpression();
 868 |                 Expect(TokenType.RParen);
 869 |                 return v;
 870 |             }
 871 |             throw new FormatException($"unexpected token '{t.Text}' (type {t.Type})");
 872 |         }
 873 | 
 874 |         private static double ApplyFunction(string name, List<double> args)
 875 |         {
 876 |             return name switch
 877 |             {
 878 |                 "floor" => args.Count == 1 ? Math.Floor(args[0]) : throw BadArity(name, 1, args.Count),
 879 |                 "ceil" => args.Count == 1 ? Math.Ceiling(args[0]) : throw BadArity(name, 1, args.Count),
 880 |                 "round" => args.Count == 1 ? Math.Round(args[0], MidpointRounding.AwayFromZero) : throw BadArity(name, 1, args.Count),
 881 |                 "abs" => args.Count == 1 ? Math.Abs(args[0]) : throw BadArity(name, 1, args.Count),
 882 |                 "sqrt" => args.Count == 1 ? Math.Sqrt(args[0]) : throw BadArity(name, 1, args.Count),
 883 |                 "min" => args.Count >= 1 ? args.Min() : throw BadArity(name, 1, args.Count),
 884 |                 "max" => args.Count >= 1 ? args.Max() : throw BadArity(name, 1, args.Count),
 885 |                 "pow" => args.Count == 2 ? Math.Pow(args[0], args[1]) : throw BadArity(name, 2, args.Count),
 886 |                 _ => throw new FormatException($"unknown function '{name}'"),
 887 |             };
 888 |         }
 889 | 
 890 |         private static FormatException BadArity(string fn, int expected, int actual) =>
 891 |             new($"{fn}() expects {expected} arg(s), got {actual}");
 892 | 
 893 |         private Token Peek() => _tokens[_pos];
 894 | 
 895 |         private Token Next() => _tokens[_pos++];
 896 | 
 897 |         private void Expect(TokenType type)
 898 |         {
 899 |             if (Peek().Type != type)
 900 |                 throw new FormatException($"expected {type}, got {Peek().Type} ('{Peek().Text}')");
 901 |             _pos++;
 902 |         }
 903 | 
 904 |         public void ExpectEnd()
 905 |         {
 906 |             if (Peek().Type != TokenType.End)
 907 |                 throw new FormatException($"unexpected trailing token '{Peek().Text}'");
 908 |         }
 909 |     }
 910 | }
