   1 | using System.Text.Json;
   2 | using System.Text.Json.Serialization;
   3 | using MyGame.Core.Common;
   4 | using MyGame.Core.World.Content;
   5 | using MyGame.Core.World.Entities;
   6 | 
   7 | namespace MyGame.Core.World;
   8 | 
   9 | /// <summary>
  10 | /// The aggregate root: the authoritative simulation container holding every
  11 | /// entity collection, the in-world clock, the ruleset, and the deterministic
  12 | /// RNG. Port of <c>engine/world/world.ts</c>.
  13 | ///
  14 | /// Scope of this port: the spec asks for the aggregate root with
  15 | /// <see cref="SpawnPlayer"/>, <see cref="SpawnNpc"/>, <see cref="AddLocation"/>,
  16 | /// <see cref="FindEntity"/>, and <see cref="ToJson"/>/<see cref="FromJson"/>.
  17 | /// The TS World has many more methods (movePlayer, damage, heal, giveItem,
  18 | /// logEntry, advanceTime, tick, conversation history, buildState, procedural
  19 | /// gen, token usage). Those are SKIPPED here — they belong to subsystems
  20 | /// (combat, narrative, AI) that don't exist in the C# port yet. They can be
  21 | /// layered onto <see cref="World"/> in later tasks without changing the
  22 | /// save schema. Skips are documented in the worklog.
  23 | ///
  24 | /// Serialization: <see cref="World"/> is plain POCO + System.Text.Json.
  25 | /// Round-trip guarantees:
  26 | ///  - All entity collections (players / npcs / items / buildings / locations /
  27 | ///    quests) round-trip as JSON arrays.
  28 | ///  - <see cref="RngState"/> (the PCG32 64-bit state) round-trips as a long;
  29 | ///    the live <see cref="Rng"/> is reconstructed lazily from it on first
  30 | ///    access after deserialize, so a reloaded save resumes deterministically
  31 | ///    from the exact stream position the save was taken at.
  32 | ///  - <see cref="Clock"/> (the simple <see cref="GameTime"/>) round-trips as
  33 | ///    a struct.
  34 | ///  - <see cref="CalendarSpec"/> (the rich calendar) round-trips as a record,
  35 | ///    if the world-builder has installed one.
  36 | ///  - <see cref="Ruleset"/> round-trips as a record (all enums + nested defs).
  37 | /// </summary>
  38 | public sealed class World
  39 | {
  40 |     // ─── Non-serialized runtime services ───────────────────────────────────
  41 | 
  42 |     /// <summary>
  43 |     /// In-process event bus. Subscribers wire up via
  44 |     /// <see cref="EventBus.Subscribe{T}"/>. Marked <c>[JsonIgnore]</c> — the
  45 |     /// bus is a runtime concern, not part of save state.
  46 |     /// </summary>
  47 |     [JsonIgnore]
  48 |     public EventBus Events { get; } = new();
  49 | 
  50 |     private ContentRegistry? _registries;
  51 | 
  52 |     /// <summary>
  53 |     /// Content registry (item / NPC / building templates). Injected by the
  54 |     /// caller — not serialized (templates live in the embedded
  55 |     /// <c>Content/data.json</c>, not in save files).
  56 |     /// </summary>
  57 |     [JsonIgnore]
  58 |     public ContentRegistry Registries
  59 |     {
  60 |         get => _registries ??= new ContentRegistry();
  61 |         set => _registries = value;
  62 |     }
  63 | 
  64 |     // ─── RNG (state round-trips; Rng object reconstructed lazily) ───────────
  65 | 
  66 |     /// <summary>
  67 |     /// The PCG32 internal state, snapshotted for save/load. The live
  68 |     /// <see cref="Rng"/> object is reconstructed from this on first access
  69 |     /// after deserialize. Mutating this property invalidates the cached
  70 |     /// <see cref="Rng"/> (the next getter call rebuilds it).
  71 |     /// </summary>
  72 |     public long RngState { get; set; }
  73 | 
  74 |     private Rng? _rng;
  75 | 
  76 |     /// <summary>
  77 |     /// The deterministic RNG. Lazily built from <see cref="RngState"/> when
  78 |     /// first accessed. Setting this property updates <see cref="RngState"/>
  79 |     /// to match (so a fresh New Game save immediately captures the seed).
  80 |     /// </summary>
  81 |     [JsonIgnore]
  82 |     public Rng Rng
  83 |     {
  84 |         get => _rng ??= Rng.FromState(RngState);
  85 |         set
  86 |         {
  87 |             _rng = value;
  88 |             RngState = value.State;
  89 |         }
  90 |     }
  91 | 
  92 |     /// <summary>
  93 |     /// Original seed used to start this save. Informational only (the engine
  94 |     /// resumes from <see cref="RngState"/>, not from this). Persisted so the
  95 |     /// save UI can show «Seed: 12345».
  96 |     /// </summary>
  97 |     public long Seed { get; set; }
  98 | 
  99 |     // ─── Clock + ruleset ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
 100 | 
 101 |     /// <summary>
 102 |     /// Simple in-world clock — primary time surface for the desktop UI.
 103 |     /// Defaults to <see cref="GameTime.Start"/> (day 1, 08:00).
 104 |     /// </summary>
 105 |     public GameTime Clock { get; set; } = GameTime.Start;
 106 | 
 107 |     /// <summary>
 108 |     /// Optional rich calendar (year/era/months/seasons/weekdays). When null,
 109 |     /// the World uses <see cref="Calendar.DefaultFantasyCalendar"/> for any
 110 |     /// rich formatting. Persisted so a world-builder-installed calendar
 111 |     /// survives save/reload.
 112 |     /// </summary>
 113 |     public CalendarSpec? CalendarSpec { get; set; }
 114 | 
 115 |     /// <summary>
 116 |     /// The ruleset that defines how this world plays. Every World has one —
 117 |     /// set at construction and mutated only by commit_ruleset /
 118 |     /// set_attribute / set_resource tools. Defaults to
 119 |     /// <see cref="Rulesets.DefaultDnd"/>.
 120 |     /// </summary>
 121 |     public Ruleset Ruleset { get; set; } = Rulesets.DefaultDnd;
 122 | 
 123 |     /// <summary>Turn counter — incremented by the simulation tick (TBD).</summary>
 124 |     public int Turn { get; set; }
 125 | 
 126 |     /// <summary>
 127 |     /// Structured combat state. Null when no combat is in progress
 128 |     /// (freeform mode — the default). When non-null and
 129 |     /// <see cref="CombatState.Active"/> = true, the GM must respect the
 130 |     /// turn order in <see cref="CombatState.TurnOrder"/> (the system-prompt
 131 |     /// world-state surfaces a "## БОЙ" block to enforce this). Set by the
 132 |     /// <c>start_combat</c> tool, cleared by <c>end_combat</c> (and
 133 |     /// auto-cleared when only the player remains in the turn order).
 134 |     /// </summary>
 135 |     public CombatState? Combat { get; set; }
 136 | 
 137 |     /// <summary>
 138 |     /// Active player id. In single-player, always the only player. In multi,
 139 |     /// rotates as the action queue resolves. Null until a player is spawned.
 140 |     /// </summary>
 141 |     public EntityId? ActivePlayerId { get; set; }
 142 | 
 143 |     /// <summary>Unix-ms timestamp when this World was first created.</summary>
 144 |     public long StartedAt { get; set; } = DateTimeOffset.UtcNow.ToUnixTimeMilliseconds();
 145 | 
 146 |     /// <summary>Cumulative playtime in ms across all sessions.</summary>
 147 |     public long PlaytimeMs { get; set; }
 148 | 
 149 |     // ─── Entity collections ────────────────────────────────────────────────
 150 |     //
 151 |     // Serialized as JSON arrays (not maps) — the entity's own Id is the key
 152 |     // on reload. This keeps the JSON compact and avoids the EntityId-as-key
 153 |     // serialization quirk (EntityId serializes as a string, which would make
 154 |     // Dictionary<EntityId, T> serialize with string keys — works but ugly).
 155 | 
 156 |     /// <summary>All players (one for single-player; many for multiplayer).</summary>
 157 |     public List<Player> Players { get; set; } = new();
 158 | 
 159 |     /// <summary>All NPCs.</summary>
 160 |     public List<Npc> Npcs { get; set; } = new();
 161 | 
 162 |     /// <summary>All loose world items (on the ground).</summary>
 163 |     public List<Item> Items { get; set; } = new();
 164 | 
 165 |     /// <summary>All buildings.</summary>
 166 |     public List<Building> Buildings { get; set; } = new();
 167 | 
 168 |     /// <summary>All locations (the world graph).</summary>
 169 |     public List<Location> Locations { get; set; } = new();
 170 | 
 171 |     /// <summary>All quests.</summary>
 172 |     public List<Quest> Quests { get; set; } = new();
 173 | 
 174 |     /// <summary>
 175 |     /// Free-form world-level metadata bag. Used by the world-builder to
 176 |     /// stash plan-derived atmosphere/title/setting/hook fields, and by the
 177 |     /// GM for arbitrary cross-entity state. Lazy-init to null to keep
 178 |     /// World JSON clean when unused.
 179 |     /// </summary>
 180 |     public Dictionary<string, object>? Flags { get; set; }
 181 | 
 182 |     // ─── Optional engine-depth subsystems (issue #34/#35/#36/#43) ─────────
 183 |     //
 184 |     // Weather, day/night, factions, and lore are all OPTIONAL — null / empty
 185 |     // means the feature isn't active for that world (backward-compatible with
 186 |     // pre-existing saves: the JSON deserializer just leaves these at their
 187 |     // defaults when the field is absent).
 188 | 
 189 |     /// <summary>
 190 |     /// Current weather state (issue #34): one of <c>"clear"</c>,
 191 |     /// <c>"rain"</c>, <c>"storm"</c>, <c>"fog"</c>, <c>"snow"</c>,
 192 |     /// <c>"overcast"</c>. Null when the weather subsystem isn't active
 193 |     /// for this world (legacy saves / worlds without a set_weather call).
 194 |     /// When set, the GM is told about it in <see cref="AI.Agents.GameMaster"/>
 195 |     /// context and travel/encounter/perception mechanics are adjusted.
 196 |     /// </summary>
 197 |     public string? CurrentWeather { get; set; }
 198 | 
 199 |     /// <summary>
 200 |     /// Human-readable weather forecast (issue #34): a short RU string the
 201 |     /// GM uses to narrate upcoming conditions (<c>"К вечеру ожидается гроза"</c>,
 202 |     /// <c>"Туман рассеется к полудню"</c>). Null until <c>set_weather</c>
 203 |     /// populates it. Optional companion to <see cref="CurrentWeather"/>.
 204 |     /// </summary>
 205 |     public string? WeatherForecast { get; set; }
 206 | 
 207 |     /// <summary>
 208 |     /// Factions active in this world (issue #36). Empty by default — the
 209 |     /// world-builder populates it from <see cref="AI.WorldPlan.Factions"/>
 210 |     /// during commit. The GM context surfaces reputation + alignment so
 211 |     /// the model knows which factions are friendly/hostile toward the
 212 |     /// player; the <c>adjust_reputation</c> tool mutates entries here.
 213 |     /// </summary>
 214 |     public List<Faction> Factions { get; set; } = new();
 215 | 
 216 |     /// <summary>
 217 |     /// Lore database (issue #43): canonical world lore entries the GM
 218 |     /// can query via the <c>get_lore</c> tool instead of hallucinating.
 219 |     /// Null when the world-builder didn't populate it (e.g. legacy saves
 220 |     /// or worlds built before this task). When non-null, the GM context
 221 |     /// lists the available topics as a hint.
 222 |     /// </summary>
 223 |     public LoreDatabase? Lore { get; set; }
 224 | 
 225 |     // ─── Lookup index (rebuilt on demand, never serialized) ────────────────
 226 | 
 227 |     private Dictionary<EntityId, Entity>? _index;
 228 | 
 229 |     /// <summary>
 230 |     /// Find an entity by id across ALL collections (players, npcs, items,
 231 |     /// buildings, locations, quests). Returns null if not found.
 232 |     ///
 233 |     /// The index is rebuilt lazily and invalidated whenever a spawn / remove
 234 |     /// mutates a collection.
 235 |     /// </summary>
 236 |     public Entity? FindEntity(EntityId id)
 237 |     {
 238 |         _index ??= BuildIndex();
 239 |         return _index.TryGetValue(id, out var e) ? e : null;
 240 |     }
 241 | 
 242 |     /// <summary>Find a player by id. Null if not present.</summary>
 243 |     public Player? GetPlayer(EntityId id) => Players.FirstOrDefault(p => p.Id == id);
 244 | 
 245 |     /// <summary>Find an NPC by id. Null if not present.</summary>
 246 |     public Npc? GetNpc(EntityId id) => Npcs.FirstOrDefault(n => n.Id == id);
 247 | 
 248 |     /// <summary>Find a location by id. Null if not present.</summary>
 249 |     public Location? GetLocation(EntityId id) => Locations.FirstOrDefault(l => l.Id == id);
 250 | 
 251 |     /// <summary>Find a building by id. Null if not present.</summary>
 252 |     public Building? GetBuilding(EntityId id) => Buildings.FirstOrDefault(b => b.Id == id);
 253 | 
 254 |     /// <summary>Find an item (loose world item) by id. Null if not present.</summary>
 255 |     public Item? GetItem(EntityId id) => Items.FirstOrDefault(i => i.Id == id);
 256 | 
 257 |     /// <summary>Find a quest by id. Null if not present.</summary>
 258 |     public Quest? GetQuest(EntityId id) => Quests.FirstOrDefault(q => q.Id == id);
 259 | 
 260 |     /// <summary>
 261 |     /// Convenience accessor for the primary (first) player. Throws if the
 262 |     /// world has no players yet (single-player bootstrap phase).
 263 |     /// </summary>
 264 |     [JsonIgnore]
 265 |     public Player Player =>
 266 |         Players.Count > 0
 267 |             ? Players[0]
 268 |             : throw new InvalidOperationException("World has no players.");
 269 | 
 270 |     /// <summary>
 271 |     /// The active player (whose turn / action is being processed). Null
 272 |     /// before any player is spawned or if <see cref="ActivePlayerId"/> isn't
 273 |     /// set.
 274 |     /// </summary>
 275 |     [JsonIgnore]
 276 |     public Player? ActivePlayer
 277 |     {
 278 |         get
 279 |         {
 280 |             if (ActivePlayerId is null) return null;
 281 |             return Players.FirstOrDefault(p => p.Id == ActivePlayerId);
 282 |         }
 283 |     }
 284 | 
 285 |     // ─── Spawn / add methods ───────────────────────────────────────────────
 286 | 
 287 |     /// <summary>
 288 |     /// Add a player to the world. The first player added becomes the active
 289 |     /// player. Returns the added player (same reference) for chaining.
 290 |     /// </summary>
 291 |     public Player SpawnPlayer(Player player)
 292 |     {
 293 |         if (player is null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(player));
 294 |         Players.Add(player);
 295 |         if (ActivePlayerId is null) ActivePlayerId = player.Id;
 296 |         InvalidateIndex();
 297 |         Events.Publish(new EntitySpawned(player.Id, "player", player.Name));
 298 |         return player;
 299 |     }
 300 | 
 301 |     /// <summary>
 302 |     /// Spawn an NPC in the world. The NPC is added to the global NPC list AND
 303 |     /// registered in its <see cref="Character.LocationId"/>'s
 304 |     /// <see cref="Location.Npcs"/> list (so location-scoped lookups find it).
 305 |     /// Returns the spawned NPC for chaining.
 306 |     /// </summary>
 307 |     public Npc SpawnNpc(Npc npc)
 308 |     {
 309 |         if (npc is null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(npc));
 310 |         Npcs.Add(npc);
 311 |         var loc = GetLocation(npc.LocationId);
 312 |         if (loc is not null && !loc.Npcs.Contains(npc.Id))
 313 |         {
 314 |             loc.Npcs.Add(npc.Id);
 315 |         }
 316 |         InvalidateIndex();
 317 |         Events.Publish(new EntitySpawned(npc.Id, "npc", npc.Name));
 318 |         return npc;
 319 |     }
 320 | 
 321 |     /// <summary>
 322 |     /// Spawn an NPC by looking up the template id in the content registry
 323 |     /// and instantiating it at the given location. Returns null if the
 324 |     /// template isn't registered.
 325 |     ///
 326 |     /// Port of <c>world.spawnNpcFromTemplate</c>. The actual template-to-NPC
 327 |     /// materialization (attribute defaults, equipment, starting inventory)
 328 |     /// is delegated to <see cref="EntityFactory.CreateNpcFromTemplate"/>.
 329 |     /// </summary>
 330 |     public Npc? SpawnNpcFromTemplate(string templateId, EntityId locationId)
 331 |     {
 332 |         var tpl = Registries.Npcs.Get(templateId);
 333 |         if (tpl is null) return null;
 334 |         var npc = EntityFactory.CreateNpcFromTemplate(tpl, locationId, Registries, Ruleset);
 335 |         return SpawnNpc(npc);
 336 |     }
 337 | 
 338 |     /// <summary>
 339 |     /// Add a location to the world graph. Returns the added location for
 340 |     /// chaining. Does NOT connect it to anything — use
 341 |     /// <c>world.Locations[i].Exits.Add(...)</c> or a future Connect() helper.
 342 |     /// </summary>
 343 |     public Location AddLocation(Location location)
 344 |     {
 345 |         if (location is null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(location));
 346 |         Locations.Add(location);
 347 |         InvalidateIndex();
 348 |         Events.Publish(new EntitySpawned(location.Id, "location", location.Name));
 349 |         return location;
 350 |     }
 351 | 
 352 |     /// <summary>
 353 |     /// Spawn a building at a location. Adds the building to the world AND
 354 |     /// registers it in the location's <see cref="Location.Buildings"/> list.
 355 |     /// </summary>
 356 |     public Building SpawnBuilding(Building building)
 357 |     {
 358 |         if (building is null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(building));
 359 |         Buildings.Add(building);
 360 |         var loc = GetLocation(building.LocationId);
 361 |         if (loc is not null && !loc.Buildings.Contains(building.Id))
 362 |         {
 363 |             loc.Buildings.Add(building.Id);
 364 |         }
 365 |         InvalidateIndex();
 366 |         Events.Publish(new EntitySpawned(building.Id, "building", building.Name));
 367 |         return building;
 368 |     }
 369 | 
 370 |     /// <summary>
 371 |     /// Spawn a loose world item (on the ground) at a location. Adds the item
 372 |     /// to the world AND registers it in the location's
 373 |     /// <see cref="Location.Items"/> list.
 374 |     /// </summary>
 375 |     public Item SpawnItemOnGround(Item item, EntityId locationId)
 376 |     {
 377 |         if (item is null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(item));
 378 |         Items.Add(item);
 379 |         var loc = GetLocation(locationId);
 380 |         if (loc is not null && !loc.Items.Contains(item.Id))
 381 |         {
 382 |             loc.Items.Add(item.Id);
 383 |         }
 384 |         InvalidateIndex();
 385 |         Events.Publish(new EntitySpawned(item.Id, "item", item.Name));
 386 |         return item;
 387 |     }
 388 | 
 389 |     // ─── Serialization ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 390 | 
 391 |     /// <summary>
 392 |     /// Snapshot this World to a JSON string. Captures every entity collection,
 393 |     /// the clock, the calendar, the ruleset, and the RNG state — enough for a
 394 |     /// save/load round-trip that resumes the simulation deterministically.
 395 |     /// </summary>
 396 |     public string ToJson() =>
 397 |         JsonSerializer.Serialize(this, WorldJson.Options);
 398 | 
 399 |     /// <summary>
 400 |     /// Rehydrate a World from a JSON string produced by <see cref="ToJson"/>.
 401 |     /// The caller must supply the live <see cref="ContentRegistry"/>
 402 |     /// (templates aren't persisted in saves).
 403 |     /// </summary>
 404 |     public static World FromJson(string json, ContentRegistry registries)
 405 |     {
 406 |         if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(json))
 407 |             throw new ArgumentException("JSON string is empty.", nameof(json));
 408 |         if (registries is null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(registries));
 409 | 
 410 |         var world = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<World>(json, WorldJson.Options)
 411 |             ?? throw new InvalidOperationException("Failed to deserialize World from JSON.");
 412 |         world.Registries = registries;
 413 |         // Force the lazy Rng to be built from the deserialized RngState (so
 414 |         // the next caller who touches Rng gets a fresh stream continuing
 415 |         // exactly where the save was taken).
 416 |         _ = world.Rng;
 417 |         return world;
 418 |     }
 419 | 
 420 |     // ─── Index management ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
 421 | 
 422 |     private void InvalidateIndex() => _index = null;
 423 | 
 424 |     private Dictionary<EntityId, Entity> BuildIndex()
 425 |     {
 426 |         var idx = new Dictionary<EntityId, Entity>();
 427 |         foreach (var p in Players) idx[p.Id] = p;
 428 |         foreach (var n in Npcs) idx[n.Id] = n;
 429 |         foreach (var i in Items) idx[i.Id] = i;
 430 |         foreach (var b in Buildings) idx[b.Id] = b;
 431 |         foreach (var l in Locations) idx[l.Id] = l;
 432 |         foreach (var q in Quests) idx[q.Id] = q;
 433 |         return idx;
 434 |     }
 435 | }
 436 | 
 437 | /// <summary>
 438 | /// Event published by <see cref="World.SpawnPlayer"/> /
 439 | /// <see cref="World.SpawnNpc"/> / <see cref="World.AddLocation"/> (and the
 440 | /// other spawn helpers) whenever a new entity enters the world. Subscribers
 441 | /// wire up via <c>world.Events.Subscribe&lt;EntitySpawned&gt;(...)</c>.
 442 | /// </summary>
 443 | public sealed record EntitySpawned(EntityId Id, string Kind, string Name);

--- src/MyGame.Core/World/WorldBuilderCommitter.cs (C#, 780 lines, ~9,102 tokens) ---
   1 | using System.Text;
   2 | using MyGame.Core.AI;
   3 | using MyGame.Core.Common;
   4 | using MyGame.Core.World.Content;
   5 | using MyGame.Core.World.Entities;
   6 | 
   7 | namespace MyGame.Core.World;
   8 | 
   9 | /// <summary>
  10 | /// Deterministic plan→world committer. Takes a <see cref="WorldPlan"/>
  11 | /// (produced by the world-planner AI) and mutates a <see cref="World"/> to
  12 | /// match it: registers custom templates, creates locations and connects
  13 | /// them into a graph, spawns NPCs / buildings, grants the player starter
  14 | /// gear, sets the world title.
  15 | ///
  16 | /// <para>
  17 | /// No AI calls here — this stage is pure data transformation. The planner
  18 | /// already made the creative decisions; the committer just materialises
  19 | /// them. This is intentionally simpler than the TS source's TODO-driven
  20 | /// single-agent loop (which needed <c>add_todo</c> / <c>mark_todo_done</c>
  21 | /// / <c>end_worldbuilding</c> tools and ~200 iterations). The desktop MVP
  22 | /// trades flexibility for reliability: one planner call + one deterministic
  23 | /// commit + one narrator call.
  24 | /// </para>
  25 | ///
  26 | /// <para>
  27 | /// The committer is IDEMPOTENT in the sense that re-running it on a world
  28 | /// that already has the plan's locations will not duplicate them — it
  29 | /// matches by location name. This lets the orchestrator safely re-enter
  30 | /// the commit stage after a crash without creating a parallel world.
  31 | /// </para>
  32 | /// </summary>
  33 | public sealed class WorldBuilderCommitter
  34 | {
  35 |     private readonly World _world;
  36 |     private readonly ContentRegistry _registries;
  37 | 
  38 |     /// <summary>
  39 |     /// Map from plan location name → world Location, built during
  40 |     /// <see cref="CommitLocations"/>. Used by every later stage to resolve
  41 |     /// <c>PlanNpc.Location</c> / <c>PlanBuilding.Location</c> name
  42 |     /// references to real <see cref="EntityId"/>s.
  43 |     /// </summary>
  44 |     private readonly Dictionary<string, Location> _locationByName =
  45 |         new(StringComparer.Ordinal);
  46 | 
  47 |     public WorldBuilderCommitter(World world)
  48 |     {
  49 |         _world = world ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(world));
  50 |         _registries = world.Registries ?? throw new ArgumentException(
  51 |             "World.Registries must be wired before committing a plan.", nameof(world));
  52 |     }
  53 | 
  54 |     /// <summary>
  55 |     /// Commit the full plan to the world. Stages run in order:
  56 |     /// custom templates → locations → population → buildings → content →
  57 |     /// starter player → factions → lore → title. Each stage is fault-
  58 |     /// tolerant: a single broken entry is skipped (and counted in the
  59 |     /// result), the rest proceed.
  60 |     /// </summary>
  61 |     public CommitStats Commit(WorldPlan plan)
  62 |     {
  63 |         if (plan is null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(plan));
  64 | 
  65 |         var stats = new CommitStats();
  66 | 
  67 |         // Stage A: register custom templates so later stages can reference
  68 |         // them by id (the planner may invent non-fantasy items / NPCs /
  69 |         // buildings for cyberpunk / sci-fi / custom settings).
  70 |         CommitCustomTemplates(plan, stats);
  71 | 
  72 |         // Stage B: create locations + connect exits. Must run before any
  73 |         // entity spawn — NPCs / buildings need a valid LocationId.
  74 |         CommitLocations(plan, stats);
  75 | 
  76 |         // Stage C: spawn planned NPCs at their locations.
  77 |         CommitPopulation(plan, stats);
  78 | 
  79 |         // Stage D: place planned buildings at their locations.
  80 |         CommitBuildings(plan, stats);
  81 | 
  82 |         // Stage E: grant starter gear + currency to the player. Also
  83 |         // creates the starter player if the world has none (e.g. fresh
  84 |         // build vs. host re-build).
  85 |         CommitContent(plan, stats);
  86 | 
  87 |         // Stage F: register factions from the plan (issue #36). Empty for
  88 |         // plans without a Factions list — the world runs fine without
  89 |         // factions (Factions stays an empty list).
  90 |         CommitFactions(plan, stats);
  91 | 
  92 |         // Stage G: populate the lore database from the plan's structured
  93 |         // lore fields (issue #43). Empty for plans without lore — World.Lore
  94 |         // stays null in that case (the get_lore tool surfaces an error).
  95 |         CommitLore(plan, stats);
  96 | 
  97 |         // Stage H: set the world title + atmosphere note. The title is the
  98 |         // primary display name in the UI ("Мир: «Тёмные Шпили Велариса»").
  99 |         CommitTitle(plan);
 100 | 
 101 |         return stats;
 102 |     }
 103 | 
 104 |     // ─── Stage A: custom templates ───────────────────────────────────────
 105 | 
 106 |     internal void CommitCustomTemplates(WorldPlan plan, CommitStats stats)
 107 |     {
 108 |         foreach (var tpl in plan.CustomItemTemplates ?? new())
 109 |         {
 110 |             try
 111 |             {
 112 |                 _registries.Items.Register(ToItemTemplate(tpl));
 113 |                 stats.CustomItems++;
 114 |             }
 115 |             catch (Exception ex)
 116 |             {
 117 |                 stats.Errors.Add($"item template «{tpl.Id}»: {ex.Message}");
 118 |             }
 119 |         }
 120 | 
 121 |         foreach (var tpl in plan.CustomNpcTemplates ?? new())
 122 |         {
 123 |             try
 124 |             {
 125 |                 _registries.Npcs.Register(ToNpcTemplate(tpl));
 126 |                 stats.CustomNpcs++;
 127 |             }
 128 |             catch (Exception ex)
 129 |             {
 130 |                 stats.Errors.Add($"npc template «{tpl.Id}»: {ex.Message}");
 131 |             }
 132 |         }
 133 | 
 134 |         foreach (var tpl in plan.CustomBuildingTemplates ?? new())
 135 |         {
 136 |             try
 137 |             {
 138 |                 _registries.Buildings.Register(ToBuildingTemplate(tpl));
 139 |                 stats.CustomBuildings++;
 140 |             }
 141 |             catch (Exception ex)
 142 |             {
 143 |                 stats.Errors.Add($"building template «{tpl.Id}»: {ex.Message}");
 144 |             }
 145 |         }
 146 |     }
 147 | 
 148 |     private static ItemTemplate ToItemTemplate(PlanCustomItemTemplate t) => new()
 149 |     {
 150 |         Id = t.Id,
 151 |         Name = t.Name,
 152 |         Description = t.Description,
 153 |         Category = t.Category,
 154 |         Weight = t.Weight ?? 0.5,
 155 |         Value = t.Value ?? 0,
 156 |         Rarity = t.Rarity ?? "common",
 157 |         Stackable = false,
 158 |         Weapon = t.Weapon is null ? null : new WeaponSpec
 159 |         {
 160 |             Type = t.Weapon.Type,
 161 |             Damage = new Damage(t.Weapon.Damage.Dice, t.Weapon.Damage.Type),
 162 |             Finesse = t.Weapon.Finesse,
 163 |             TwoHanded = t.Weapon.TwoHanded,
 164 |             Range = t.Weapon.Range,
 165 |         },
 166 |         Armor = t.Armor is null ? null : new ArmorSpec
 167 |         {
 168 |             BaseAc = t.Armor.BaseAc,
 169 |             Type = t.Armor.Type,
 170 |             DexBonusMax = t.Armor.DexBonusMax,
 171 |             StealthDisadvantage = t.Armor.StealthDisadvantage,
 172 |         },
 173 |         Consumable = t.Consumable is null ? null : new ConsumableSpec
 174 |         {
 175 |             Healing = t.Consumable.Healing,
 176 |             Effects = t.Consumable.Effect is null ? null : new()
 177 |             {
 178 |                 new() { Name = t.Consumable.Effect, Description = t.Consumable.Effect, Duration = 0 },
 179 |             },
 180 |         },
 181 |     };
 182 | 
 183 |     private static NpcTemplate ToNpcTemplate(PlanCustomNpcTemplate t) => new()
 184 |     {
 185 |         Id = t.Id,
 186 |         Name = t.Name,
 187 |         Race = t.Race,
 188 |         Class = t.Class,
 189 |         Level = t.Level,
 190 |         Attributes = t.Attributes ?? new(),
 191 |         Resources = t.Resources,
 192 |         Disposition = t.Disposition,
 193 |         Behavior = t.Behavior,
 194 |         Equipment = t.Equipment,
 195 |         Description = t.Description,
 196 |     };
 197 | 
 198 |     private static BuildingTemplate ToBuildingTemplate(PlanCustomBuildingTemplate t) => new()
 199 |     {
 200 |         Id = t.Id,
 201 |         Name = t.Name,
 202 |         Type = t.Type,
 203 |         Description = t.Description,
 204 |         Enterable = t.Enterable,
 205 |     };
 206 | 
 207 |     // ─── Stage B: locations ──────────────────────────────────────────────
 208 | 
 209 |     internal void CommitLocations(WorldPlan plan, CommitStats stats)
 210 |     {
 211 |         // Issue #20 (chunked generation): when the plan's GenerationMode
 212 |         // is "chunked", only the start region's locations are detailed in
 213 |         // the plan; other regions are marked GenStatus="cold" and have no
 214 |         // locations. We must NOT commit a cold-region location even if
 215 |         // the planner accidentally listed one — those will be generated
 216 |         // on-demand when the player travels toward them (see
 217 |         // WorldBuilderOrchestrator.GenerateRegionAsync). The start region
 218 |         // is the one with GenStatus="ready" (or ContainsStart=true); if
 219 |         // neither marker is present, we treat all locations as committable
 220 |         // (backward-compat with non-chunked plans).
 221 |         var isChunked = string.Equals(plan.GenerationMode, "chunked", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
 222 |         var readyRegionNames = (plan.Regions ?? new())
 223 |             .Where(r => string.Equals(r.GenStatus, "ready", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
 224 |                      || (r.ContainsStart ?? false))
 225 |             .Select(r => r.Name)
 226 |             .Where(n => !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(n))
 227 |             .ToHashSet(StringComparer.Ordinal);
 228 |         var coldRegionNames = (plan.Regions ?? new())
 229 |             .Where(r => string.Equals(r.GenStatus, "cold", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
 230 |             .Select(r => r.Name)
 231 |             .Where(n => !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(n))
 232 |             .ToHashSet(StringComparer.Ordinal);
 233 | 
 234 |         // Filter the plan's locations: in chunked mode, skip any that
 235 |         // belong to a cold region. Locations with no Region field (or a
 236 |         // region not in either set) are always committed (backward compat).
 237 |         var committableLocations = (plan.Locations ?? new())
 238 |             .Where(pl => !isChunked
 239 |                 || string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(pl.Region)
 240 |                 || !coldRegionNames.Contains(pl.Region))
 241 |             .ToList();
 242 | 
 243 |         // First pass: create all locations (no exits yet — they reference
 244 |         // siblings that may not exist on a forward pass).
 245 |         foreach (var pl in committableLocations)
 246 |         {
 247 |             if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(pl.Name))
 248 |             {
 249 |                 stats.Errors.Add("location with empty name — skipped");
 250 |                 continue;
 251 |             }
 252 | 
 253 |             // Idempotency: if a location with this name already exists
 254 |             // (re-commit / hybrid with DefaultWorld), reuse it.
 255 |             var existing = _world.Locations.FirstOrDefault(l =>
 256 |                 string.Equals(l.Name, pl.Name, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase));
 257 |             if (existing is not null)
 258 |             {
 259 |                 _locationByName[pl.Name] = existing;
 260 |                 continue;
 261 |             }
 262 | 
 263 |             var loc = new Location
 264 |             {
 265 |                 Id = EntityId.NewId(),
 266 |                 Name = pl.Name,
 267 |                 Description = pl.Description ?? string.Empty,
 268 |                 Terrain = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(pl.Terrain) ? "plains" : pl.Terrain,
 269 |                 Danger = pl.Danger,
 270 |                 // Mark the start location as visited/discovered so the
 271 |                 // player UI shows it immediately.
 272 |                 Visited = pl.Role == LocationRole.Start,
 273 |                 Discovered = pl.Role == LocationRole.Start || pl.Role == LocationRole.Hub,
 274 |             };
 275 |             _world.AddLocation(loc);
 276 |             _locationByName[pl.Name] = loc;
 277 |             stats.Locations++;
 278 |         }
 279 | 
 280 |         // Second pass: wire up exits. PlanLocation.Connections is a list
 281 |         // of target location names; we make the connection bidirectional
 282 |         // unless the source already has an exit to the target.
 283 |         // Issue #20 (chunked generation): exits pointing to cold-region
 284 |         // locations (not yet created) become PHANTOM exits — the exit's
 285 |         // To is EntityId.Empty and ToName is the target name. The travel
 286 |         // handler detects phantom exits and triggers region generation
 287 |         // when the player clicks them.
 288 |         foreach (var pl in committableLocations)
 289 |         {
 290 |             if (!_locationByName.TryGetValue(pl.Name, out var from)) continue;
 291 | 
 292 |             foreach (var targetName in pl.Connections ?? new())
 293 |             {
 294 |                 if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(targetName)) continue;
 295 |                 if (_locationByName.TryGetValue(targetName, out var to))
 296 |                 {
 297 |                     // Target exists — wire a normal bidirectional exit.
 298 |                     if (from.Exits.Any(e => e.To == to.Id)) continue; // already wired
 299 | 
 300 |                     var direction = ResolveDirection(pl.Name, targetName, pl.DirectionFromHub);
 301 |                     from.Exits.Add(new LocationExit
 302 |                     {
 303 |                         To = to.Id,
 304 |                         Direction = direction,
 305 |                         Locked = false,
 306 |                     });
 307 | 
 308 |                     // Bidirectional: if the target doesn't yet point back,
 309 |                     // add a reverse exit with the opposite direction.
 310 |                     if (!to.Exits.Any(e => e.To == from.Id))
 311 |                     {
 312 |                         to.Exits.Add(new LocationExit
 313 |                         {
 314 |                             To = from.Id,
 315 |                             Direction = OppositeDirection(direction),
 316 |                             Locked = false,
 317 |                         });
 318 |                     }
 319 |                 }
 320 |                 else if (isChunked)
 321 |                 {
 322 |                     // Phantom exit — target doesn't exist (cold region).
 323 |                     // Skip if a phantom exit with the same target name is
 324 |                     // already on the source (idempotent re-commit).
 325 |                     if (from.Exits.Any(e => e.To == EntityId.Empty
 326 |                         && string.Equals(e.ToName, targetName, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)))
 327 |                         continue;
 328 | 
 329 |                     var direction = ResolveDirection(pl.Name, targetName, pl.DirectionFromHub);
 330 |                     from.Exits.Add(new LocationExit
 331 |                     {
 332 |                         To = EntityId.Empty,
 333 |                         ToName = targetName,
 334 |                         Direction = direction,
 335 |                         Locked = false,
 336 |                     });
 337 |                 }
 338 |                 // else: non-chunked plan, target missing — silently skip
 339 |                 // (the existing behavior; preserves back-compat).
 340 |             }
 341 |         }
 342 | 
 343 |         // Stash the cold-region set on the world's Flags so the travel
 344 |         // handler can detect "this exit leads to a not-yet-generated
 345 |         // region" without re-parsing the plan. We also stash the start
 346 |         // region name so GenerateRegionAsync knows which region is the
 347 |         // "boundary" the player is currently in.
 348 |         if (isChunked)
 349 |         {
 350 |             if (_world.Flags is null) _world.Flags = new();
 351 |             _world.Flags["coldRegions"] = coldRegionNames.ToList();
 352 |             _world.Flags["readyRegions"] = readyRegionNames.ToList();
 353 |             _world.Flags["generationMode"] = "chunked";
 354 |         }
 355 |     }
 356 | 
 357 |     /// <summary>
 358 |     /// Resolve a human-readable direction label for an exit. If the plan
 359 |     /// gave a <c>DirectionFromHub</c> for the source location, use that;
 360 |     /// otherwise derive from the cardinal relationship between names (we
 361 |     /// don't have real coordinates — names are the only signal). Falls
 362 |     /// back to the target name itself as the label.
 363 |     /// </summary>
 364 |     private static string ResolveDirection(string fromName, string toName, string? directionFromHub)
 365 |     {
 366 |         if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(directionFromHub))
 367 |             return directionFromHub;
 368 |         // Heuristic: if the target name contains a direction word, use it.
 369 |         var lower = toName.ToLowerInvariant();
 370 |         if (lower.Contains("север")) return "север";
 371 |         if (lower.Contains("юг")) return "юг";
 372 |         if (lower.Contains("восток")) return "восток";
 373 |         if (lower.Contains("запад")) return "запад";
 374 |         if (lower.Contains("лес")) return "к лесу";
 375 |         if (lower.Contains("пещер")) return "к пещере";
 376 |         if (lower.Contains("руин")) return "к руинам";
 377 |         if (lower.Contains("деревн") || lower.Contains("город")) return "к поселению";
 378 |         return $"к «{toName}»";
 379 |     }
 380 | 
 381 |     private static string OppositeDirection(string direction)
 382 |     {
 383 |         var d = direction.ToLowerInvariant();
 384 |         if (d.Contains("север")) return "юг";
 385 |         if (d.Contains("юг")) return "север";
 386 |         if (d.Contains("восток")) return "запад";
 387 |         if (d.Contains("запад")) return "восток";
 388 |         if (d.Contains("вглубь")) return "наружу";
 389 |         if (d.Contains("наружу") || d.Contains("наверх")) return "внутрь";
 390 |         return $"к началу";
 391 |     }
 392 | 
 393 |     // ─── Stage C: population ─────────────────────────────────────────────
 394 | 
 395 |     internal void CommitPopulation(WorldPlan plan, CommitStats stats)
 396 |     {
 397 |         foreach (var pn in plan.Npcs ?? new())
 398 |         {
 399 |             if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(pn.Name) || string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(pn.Template))
 400 |             {
 401 |                 stats.Errors.Add("npc with empty name/template — skipped");
 402 |                 continue;
 403 |             }
 404 |             if (!_locationByName.TryGetValue(pn.Location, out var loc))
 405 |             {
 406 |                 stats.Errors.Add($"npc «{pn.Name}»: location «{pn.Location}» not found — skipped");
 407 |                 continue;
 408 |             }
 409 | 
 410 |             var npc = _world.SpawnNpcFromTemplate(pn.Template, loc.Id);
 411 |             if (npc is null)
 412 |             {
 413 |                 stats.Errors.Add($"npc «{pn.Name}»: template «{pn.Template}» not found — skipped");
 414 |                 continue;
 415 |             }
 416 | 
 417 |             // Apply plan overrides.
 418 |             npc.Name = pn.Name;
 419 |             if (pn.Level > 0) npc.Level = pn.Level;
 420 |             if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(pn.Disposition))
 421 |                 SetFlag(npc, "disposition", pn.Disposition);
 422 |             if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(pn.Behavior))
 423 |                 SetFlag(npc, "behavior", pn.Behavior);
 424 |             if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(pn.Role))
 425 |                 SetFlag(npc, "role", pn.Role);
 426 |             if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(pn.Notes))
 427 |                 SetFlag(npc, "notes", pn.Notes);
 428 | 
 429 |             stats.Npcs++;
 430 |         }
 431 |     }
 432 | 
 433 |     // ─── Stage D: buildings ──────────────────────────────────────────────
 434 | 
 435 |     internal void CommitBuildings(WorldPlan plan, CommitStats stats)
 436 |     {
 437 |         foreach (var pb in plan.Buildings ?? new())
 438 |         {
 439 |             if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(pb.Template))
 440 |             {
 441 |                 stats.Errors.Add("building with empty template — skipped");
 442 |                 continue;
 443 |             }
 444 |             if (!_locationByName.TryGetValue(pb.Location, out var loc))
 445 |             {
 446 |                 stats.Errors.Add($"building «{pb.Template}»: location «{pb.Location}» not found — skipped");
 447 |                 continue;
 448 |             }
 449 | 
 450 |             var tpl = _registries.Buildings.Get(pb.Template);
 451 |             if (tpl is null)
 452 |             {
 453 |                 stats.Errors.Add($"building template «{pb.Template}» not found — skipped");
 454 |                 continue;
 455 |             }
 456 | 
 457 |             var building = EntityFactory.CreateBuilding(tpl, loc.Id);
 458 |             if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(pb.NameOverride))
 459 |                 building.Name = pb.NameOverride;
 460 |             _world.SpawnBuilding(building);
 461 |             stats.Buildings++;
 462 |         }
 463 |     }
 464 | 
 465 |     // ─── Stage E: content + starter player ───────────────────────────────
 466 | 
 467 |     internal void CommitContent(WorldPlan plan, CommitStats stats)
 468 |     {
 469 |         // Ensure a starter player exists. The plan doesn't carry a player
 470 |         // object (the planner designs the world; the player is added by
 471 |         // the host flow). If the world has no player yet, create a
 472 |         // generic adventurer at the Start location (or the first location).
 473 |         var player = _world.ActivePlayer ?? _world.Players.FirstOrDefault();
 474 |         if (player is null)
 475 |         {
 476 |             var startLoc = (_locationByName.Values.FirstOrDefault(l =>
 477 |                 l.Name.Equals("start", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
 478 |                 ?? _locationByName.Values.FirstOrDefault())!;
 479 | 
 480 |             if (startLoc is not null)
 481 |             {
 482 |                 player = EntityFactory.CreatePlayer(new()
 483 |                 {
 484 |                     Name = "Странник",
 485 |                     Race = "human",
 486 |                     Class = "adventurer",
 487 |                     Level = 1,
 488 |                     LocationId = startLoc.Id,
 489 |                     StartingCurrency = plan.StarterCurrency > 0 ? plan.StarterCurrency : 25,
 490 |                     Background = plan.Setting,
 491 |                 }, _world.Ruleset);
 492 |                 _world.SpawnPlayer(player);
 493 |                 stats.StarterPlayerCreated = true;
 494 |             }
 495 |         }
 496 |         else if (plan.StarterCurrency > 0)
 497 |         {
 498 |             player.Inventory.Currency += plan.StarterCurrency;
 499 |         }
 500 | 
 501 |         if (player is null) return;
 502 | 
 503 |         // Grant starter gear.
 504 |         foreach (var tplId in plan.StarterGear ?? new())
 505 |         {
 506 |             var tpl = _registries.Items.Get(tplId);
 507 |             if (tpl is null)
 508 |             {
 509 |                 stats.Errors.Add($"starter gear «{tplId}»: template not found — skipped");
 510 |                 continue;
 511 |             }
 512 |             var item = EntityFactory.InstantiateItem(tpl, 1);
 513 |             player.Inventory.Items.Add(item);
 514 |             stats.StarterItems++;
 515 |         }
 516 | 
 517 |         // If the plan gave starter currency and the player already existed,
 518 |         // we already added it above. For a fresh player, CreatePlayer used
 519 |         // it as the starting currency — don't double-count.
 520 |     }
 521 | 
 522 |     // ─── Stage F: title ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
 523 | 
 524 |     internal void CommitTitle(WorldPlan plan)
 525 |     {
 526 |         // World doesn't have a dedicated Title field in the current model;
 527 |         // we stash it in Flags so the UI can read it without changing the
 528 |         // schema. A future schema revision should promote this to a real
 529 |         // field.
 530 |         if (_world.Flags is null) _world.Flags = new();
 531 |         _world.Flags["worldTitle"] = plan.Title;
 532 |         _world.Flags["worldTheme"] = plan.Theme;
 533 |         _world.Flags["worldAtmosphere"] = plan.Atmosphere;
 534 |         _world.Flags["worldSetting"] = plan.Setting;
 535 |         _world.Flags["startingHook"] = plan.StartingHook;
 536 |     }
 537 | 
 538 |     // ─── Stage G: factions (issue #36) ──────────────────────────────────
 539 | 
 540 |     /// <summary>
 541 |     /// Register factions from <see cref="WorldPlan.Factions"/> into
 542 |     /// <see cref="World.Factions"/>. Each <see cref="PlanFaction"/> becomes
 543 |     /// a <see cref="Faction"/> with Reputation = 0 (neutral start) and
 544 |     /// alignment = "neutral" (the planner's free-form Alignment string is
 545 |     /// descriptive flavor only — good/evil/lawful/chaotic — and is NOT
 546 |     /// propagated to the runtime alignment bucket, which is derived from
 547 |     /// reputation). Idempotent: re-running on a world that already has a
 548 |     /// faction with the same name skips it (no duplicate registration).
 549 |     /// </summary>
 550 |     internal void CommitFactions(WorldPlan plan, CommitStats stats)
 551 |     {
 552 |         foreach (var pf in plan.Factions ?? new())
 553 |         {
 554 |             if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(pf.Name))
 555 |             {
 556 |                 stats.Errors.Add("faction with empty name — skipped");
 557 |                 continue;
 558 |             }
 559 | 
 560 |             // Idempotency: skip if a faction with this name is already
 561 |             // registered (re-commit / hybrid build).
 562 |             var existing = _world.Factions.FirstOrDefault(f =>
 563 |                 string.Equals(f.Name, pf.Name, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase));
 564 |             if (existing is not null) continue;
 565 | 
 566 |             var faction = new Faction
 567 |             {
 568 |                 Id = Slugify(pf.Name),
 569 |                 Name = pf.Name,
 570 |                 Description = pf.Description ?? string.Empty,
 571 |                 Alignment = "neutral", // reputation-derived; starts neutral
 572 |                 Reputation = 0,
 573 |                 Type = pf.Type,
 574 |                 Territory = pf.Territory,
 575 |             };
 576 |             _world.Factions.Add(faction);
 577 |             stats.Factions++;
 578 |         }
 579 |     }
 580 | 
 581 |     /// <summary>
 582 |     /// Slugify a faction name into a stable id: lowercase, ASCII alnum +
 583 |     /// underscores, everything else collapsed to a single underscore.
 584 |     /// <c>"Орден Серебряной Чаши"</c> → <c>"_"</c> (Cyrillic strips to
 585 |     /// nothing) — so for non-ASCII names we fall back to a Guid suffix
 586 |     /// to keep ids unique. Pure-ASCII names slugify cleanly:
 587 |     /// <c>"Silver Chalice Order"</c> → <c>"silver_chalice_order"</c>.
 588 |     /// </summary>
 589 |     private static string Slugify(string name)
 590 |     {
 591 |         if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(name)) return EntityId.NewId().ToString();
 592 | 
 593 |         var sb = new StringBuilder();
 594 |         bool lastWasUnderscore = false;
 595 |         foreach (var ch in name.Trim())
 596 |         {
 597 |             if (char.IsAsciiLetterOrDigit(ch))
 598 |             {
 599 |                 sb.Append(char.ToLowerInvariant(ch));
 600 |                 lastWasUnderscore = false;
 601 |             }
 602 |             else if (!lastWasUnderscore)
 603 |             {
 604 |                 sb.Append('_');
 605 |                 lastWasUnderscore = true;
 606 |             }
 607 |         }
 608 |         var slug = sb.ToString().Trim('_');
 609 |         if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(slug))
 610 |             return "faction_" + EntityId.NewId().ToString();
 611 |         return slug;
 612 |     }
 613 | 
 614 |     // ─── Stage H: lore (issue #43) ──────────────────────────────────────
 615 | 
 616 |     /// <summary>
 617 |     /// Populate <see cref="World.Lore"/> from the plan's structured lore
 618 |     /// fields (cosmology / history / magic system / cultures / economy /
 619 |     /// current events). Each becomes a <see cref="LoreEntry"/> with a
 620 |     /// canonical topic key. Idempotent: a fresh <see cref="LoreDatabase"/>
 621 |     /// is created only when at least one lore field is present in the plan;
 622 |     /// otherwise World.Lore stays null (the get_lore tool surfaces an error
 623 |     /// in that case).
 624 |     /// </summary>
 625 |     internal void CommitLore(WorldPlan plan, CommitStats stats)
 626 |     {
 627 |         var db = new LoreDatabase();
 628 | 
 629 |         // Cosmology → "deities" topic. The plan's cosmology object covers
 630 |         // origin / nature / higher powers / cosmic threats — we collapse
 631 |         // it into a single multi-line entry so the GM can query it.
 632 |         if (plan.Cosmology is { } cos)
 633 |         {
 634 |             var sb = new StringBuilder();
 635 |             sb.AppendLine($"Происхождение мира: {cos.Origin}");
 636 |             sb.AppendLine($"Природа мира: {cos.Nature}");
 637 |             if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(cos.HigherPowers))
 638 |                 sb.AppendLine($"Высшие силы: {cos.HigherPowers}");
 639 |             if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(cos.CosmicThreats))
 640 |                 sb.AppendLine($"Космические угрозы: {cos.CosmicThreats}");
 641 |             db.Entries.Add(new LoreEntry { Topic = "deities", Content = sb.ToString().TrimEnd() });
 642 |         }
 643 | 
 644 |         // History → "history" topic. One entry per era, concatenated.
 645 |         if (plan.History is { Count: > 0 } history)
 646 |         {
 647 |             var sb = new StringBuilder();
 648 |             foreach (var era in history)
 649 |             {
 650 |                 var years = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(era.YearsAgo) ? "" : $" ({era.YearsAgo})";
 651 |                 sb.AppendLine($"## {era.Name}{years}");
 652 |                 if (era.Events is { Count: > 0 } events)
 653 |                 {
 654 |                     foreach (var ev in events)
 655 |                         sb.AppendLine($"- {ev}");
 656 |                 }
 657 |                 sb.AppendLine($"Наследие: {era.Legacy}");
 658 |                 sb.AppendLine();
 659 |             }
 660 |             db.Entries.Add(new LoreEntry { Topic = "history", Content = sb.ToString().TrimEnd() });
 661 |         }
 662 | 
 663 |         // Magic system → "magic" topic.
 664 |         if (plan.MagicSystem is { } magic)
 665 |         {
 666 |             var sb = new StringBuilder();
 667 |             sb.AppendLine($"Тип: {magic.Type}");
 668 |             sb.AppendLine($"Источник: {magic.Source}");
 669 |             sb.AppendLine($"Владельцы: {magic.Wielders}");
 670 |             sb.AppendLine($"Ограничения: {magic.Limits}");
 671 |             sb.AppendLine($"Отношение публики: {magic.PublicAttitude}");
 672 |             db.Entries.Add(new LoreEntry { Topic = "magic", Content = sb.ToString().TrimEnd() });
 673 |         }
 674 | 
 675 |         // Cultures → "cultures" topic. One block per culture.
 676 |         if (plan.Cultures is { Count: > 0 } cultures)
 677 |         {
 678 |             var sb = new StringBuilder();
 679 |             foreach (var c in cultures)
 680 |             {
 681 |                 sb.AppendLine($"## {c.Name} ({c.Race})");
 682 |                 if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(c.Religion))
 683 |                     sb.AppendLine($"Религия: {c.Religion}");
 684 |                 if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(c.Language))
 685 |                     sb.AppendLine($"Язык: {c.Language}");
 686 |                 sb.AppendLine($"Обычаи: {c.Customs}");
 687 |                 sb.AppendLine($"Отношение к чужакам: {c.AttitudeToOutsiders}");
 688 |                 if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(c.Appearance))
 689 |                     sb.AppendLine($"Внешность: {c.Appearance}");
 690 |                 sb.AppendLine();
 691 |             }
 692 |             db.Entries.Add(new LoreEntry { Topic = "cultures", Content = sb.ToString().TrimEnd() });
 693 |         }
 694 | 
 695 |         // Economy → "economy" topic.
 696 |         if (plan.Economy is { } econ)
 697 |         {
 698 |             var sb = new StringBuilder();
 699 |             sb.AppendLine($"Валюты: {econ.Currencies}");
 700 |             sb.AppendLine($"Торговые пути: {econ.TradeRoutes}");
 701 |             sb.AppendLine($"Ключевые товары: {econ.KeyGoods}");
 702 |             if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(econ.MajorGuilds))
 703 |                 sb.AppendLine($"Крупные гильдии: {econ.MajorGuilds}");
 704 |             sb.AppendLine($"Процветание: {econ.Prosperity}");
 705 |             db.Entries.Add(new LoreEntry { Topic = "economy", Content = sb.ToString().TrimEnd() });
 706 |         }
 707 | 
 708 |         // Current events → "current events" topic.
 709 |         if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(plan.CurrentEvents))
 710 |         {
 711 |             db.Entries.Add(new LoreEntry { Topic = "current events", Content = plan.CurrentEvents });
 712 |         }
 713 | 
 714 |         if (db.Entries.Count > 0)
 715 |         {
 716 |             _world.Lore = db;
 717 |             stats.LoreEntries = db.Entries.Count;
 718 |         }
 719 |         // else: leave World.Lore null — the get_lore tool surfaces an error.
 720 |     }
 721 | 
 722 |     // ─── Helpers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 723 | 
 724 |     private static void SetFlag(Entity e, string key, string value)
 725 |     {
 726 |         e.Flags ??= new();
 727 |         e.Flags[key] = value;
 728 |     }
 729 | }
 730 | 
 731 | /// <summary>
 732 | /// Statistics from <see cref="WorldBuilderCommitter.Commit"/>. Returned to
 733 | /// the orchestrator so it can report "Created N locations, M NPCs, K
 734 | /// buildings" to the progress UI.
 735 | /// </summary>
 736 | public sealed class CommitStats
 737 | {
 738 |     public int CustomItems { get; set; }
 739 |     public int CustomNpcs { get; set; }
 740 |     public int CustomBuildings { get; set; }
 741 |     public int Locations { get; set; }
 742 |     public int Npcs { get; set; }
 743 |     public int Buildings { get; set; }
 744 |     public int StarterItems { get; set; }
 745 |     public bool StarterPlayerCreated { get; set; }
 746 | 
 747 |     /// <summary>
 748 |     /// Number of factions registered from the plan (issue #36). Zero when
 749 |     /// the plan had no Factions list — the world runs fine without factions.
 750 |     /// </summary>
 751 |     public int Factions { get; set; }
 752 | 
 753 |     /// <summary>
 754 |     /// Number of lore entries written to World.Lore (issue #43). Zero when
 755 |     /// the plan had no lore fields — World.Lore stays null in that case.
 756 |     /// </summary>
 757 |     public int LoreEntries { get; set; }
 758 | 
 759 |     public List<string> Errors { get; } = new();
 760 | 
 761 |     public string Summary()
 762 |     {
 763 |         var sb = new StringBuilder();
 764 |         sb.Append($"Локаций: {Locations}, NPC: {Npcs}, Зданий: {Buildings}");
 765 |         if (CustomItems > 0 || CustomNpcs > 0 || CustomBuildings > 0)
 766 |             sb.Append($" | Кастомных шаблонов: {CustomItems}/{CustomNpcs}/{CustomBuildings}");
 767 |         if (StarterItems > 0)
 768 |             sb.Append($" | Стартовых предметов: {StarterItems}");
 769 |         if (StarterPlayerCreated)
 770 |             sb.Append(" | Создан стартовый игрок");
 771 |         if (Factions > 0)
 772 |             sb.Append($" | Фракций: {Factions}");
 773 |         if (LoreEntries > 0)
 774 |             sb.Append($" | Лор: {LoreEntries} тем");
 775 |         if (Errors.Count > 0)
 776 |             sb.Append($" | Ошибок: {Errors.Count}");
 777 |         return sb.ToString();
 778 |     }
 779 | }

--- src/MyGame.Core/World/WorldJson.cs (C#, 65 lines, ~809 tokens) ---
   1 | using System.Text.Json;
   2 | using System.Text.Json.Serialization;
   3 | using MyGame.Core.Common;
   4 | 
   5 | namespace MyGame.Core.World;
   6 | 
   7 | /// <summary>
   8 | /// Shared <see cref="JsonSerializerOptions"/> for all World-layer JSON.
   9 | ///
  10 | /// Convention (from the worklog): camelCase on the wire. The TS source uses
  11 | /// camelCase everywhere, and the default content pack (<c>data.json</c>) is
  12 | /// ported verbatim from <c>data.ts</c>, so camelCase keeps the C# port
  13 | /// JSON-compatible with the existing TS-format data.
  14 | ///
  15 | /// Save files (state.json) also use camelCase — that's a deviation from the
  16 | /// worklog's "PascalCase for save files" rule, but the task spec for this
  17 | /// stage explicitly says "BE CONSISTENT across all your files", so we
  18 | /// standardize on camelCase here. A future SaveManager can swap options if
  19 | /// it needs to write PascalCase saves.
  20 | /// </summary>
  21 | public static class WorldJson
  22 | {
  23 |     /// <summary>
  24 |     /// Default options: case-insensitive deserialization, camelCase
  25 |     /// serialization, indented writes for human readability, full
  26 |     /// <see cref="EntityId"/> support (already handled by the
  27 |     /// <see cref="EntityIdJsonConverter"/> attached via <c>[JsonConverter]</c>
  28 |     /// on <see cref="EntityId"/>).
  29 |     /// </summary>
  30 |     public static JsonSerializerOptions Options { get; } = BuildOptions();
  31 | 
  32 |     private static JsonSerializerOptions BuildOptions()
  33 |     {
  34 |         var opts = new JsonSerializerOptions
  35 |         {
  36 |             PropertyNamingPolicy = JsonNamingPolicy.CamelCase,
  37 |             PropertyNameCaseInsensitive = true,
  38 |             WriteIndented = true,
  39 |             DefaultIgnoreCondition = JsonIgnoreCondition.WhenWritingNull,
  40 |             IncludeFields = false,
  41 |             // Write Cyrillic (and all other non-ASCII) as raw UTF-8
  42 |             // rather than \uXXXX escapes — keeps save files and the
  43 |             // embedded data.json human-readable in any text editor.
  44 |             // Backward-compatible: JSON with raw UTF-8 parses identically
  45 |             // to JSON with \uXXXX escapes. Added in task 3-d (Saves) so
  46 |             // save meta / log files are readable alongside the Profile
  47 |             // layer's files (which use the same encoder).
  48 |             Encoder = System.Text.Encodings.Web.JavaScriptEncoder.UnsafeRelaxedJsonEscaping,
  49 |         };
  50 |         return opts;
  51 |     }
  52 | 
  53 |     /// <summary>Serialize using <see cref="Options"/>.</summary>
  54 |     public static string Serialize<T>(T value) =>
  55 |         JsonSerializer.Serialize(value, Options);
  56 | 
  57 |     /// <summary>Deserialize using <see cref="Options"/>.</summary>
  58 |     public static T? Deserialize<T>(string json) =>
  59 |         JsonSerializer.Deserialize<T>(json, Options);
  60 | 
  61 |     /// <summary>Deserialize using <see cref="Options"/> from a stream.</summary>
  62 |     public static T? Deserialize<T>(Stream stream) =>
  63 |         JsonSerializer.Deserialize<T>(stream, Options);
  64 | }

  16 | {
  17 |     public override void Initialize()
  18 |     {
  19 |         AvaloniaXamlLoader.Load(this);
  20 |     }
  21 | 
  22 |     public override void OnFrameworkInitializationCompleted()
  23 |     {
  24 |         ServiceHost.Initialize();
  25 | 
  26 |         if (ApplicationLifetime is IClassicDesktopStyleApplicationLifetime desktop)
  27 |         {
  28 |             var settingsStore = ServiceHost.Resolve<SettingsStore>();
  29 |             try
  30 |             {
  31 |                 ThemeService.ApplyFromSettings(settingsStore.Load());
  32 |                 settingsStore.Changed += (_, settings) => ThemeService.ApplyFromSettings(settings);
  33 |             }
  34 |             catch
  35 |             {
  36 |                 ThemeService.ApplyTheme("Dark", "Indigo", enableAnimations: true);
  37 |             }
  38 | 
  39 |             var shell = new MainViewModel(
  40 |                 ServiceHost.Resolve<ProfileStore>(),
  41 |                 settingsStore,
  42 |                 ServiceHost.Resolve<SaveManager>());
  43 | 
  44 |             var window = new MainWindow();
  45 |             window.SetViewModel(shell);
  46 |             desktop.MainWindow = window;
  47 | 
  48 |             try { ThemeService.ApplyFromSettings(settingsStore.Load()); }
  49 |             catch { }
  50 | 
  51 |             // Issue #54: check for updates in the background. Never blocks
  52 |             // startup — runs fire-and-forget. If a newer version is found,
  53 |             // a notification is shown on the main menu (via UpdateAvailable
  54 |             // property on MainViewModel).
  55 |             _ = CheckForUpdatesAsync(shell);
  56 |         }
  57 | 
  58 |         base.OnFrameworkInitializationCompleted();
  59 |     }
  60 | 
  61 |     /// <summary>
  62 |     /// Best-effort update check (issue #54). Fetches the latest GitHub
  63 |     /// release version and, if newer than the current version, sets the
  64 |     /// shell's UpdateAvailable property so the main menu shows a
  65 |     /// notification with a link to the release page.
  66 |     /// </summary>
  67 |     private static async Task CheckForUpdatesAsync(MainViewModel shell)
  68 |     {
  69 |         try
  70 |         {
  71 |             var current = Version.Parse(MyGame.Core.Common.Version.Current);
  72 |             var update = await UpdateChecker.CheckAsync(current);
  73 |             if (update is not null)
  74 |             {
  75 |                 shell.SetUpdateAvailableOnMenu(update);
  76 |             }
  77 |         }
  78 |         catch { /* best-effort — network errors are silent */ }
  79 |     }
  80 | }

--- src/MyGame.Desktop/Converters/MemberStatusToReadyVisibilityConverter.cs (C#, 50 lines, ~620 tokens) ---
   1 | using System;
   2 | using System.Globalization;
   3 | using Avalonia.Data.Converters;
   4 | using MyGame.Core.Multiplayer;
   5 | 
   6 | namespace MyGame.Desktop.Converters;
   7 | 
   8 | /// <summary>
   9 | /// Issue #77 — visibility converter for the lobby's per-member
  10 | /// ready-status indicators. Bound to a <see cref="MemberStatus"/> enum
  11 | /// value; the <c>ConverterParameter</c> is a string naming the status
  12 | /// to match ("Ready" / "Pending" / "Playing" / "Disconnected"). Returns
  13 | /// true when the bound status matches the parameter, false otherwise.
  14 | ///
  15 | /// <para>
  16 | /// Typical XAML usage (two TextBlocks side-by-side, one shown when the
  17 | /// member is Ready, the other when Pending):
  18 | /// <code>
  19 | /// &lt;TextBlock Text="✓"
  20 | ///            IsVisible="{Binding Status,
  21 | ///                Converter={x:Static conv:MemberStatusToReadyVisibilityConverter.Instance},
  22 | ///                ConverterParameter=Ready}"/&gt;
  23 | /// &lt;TextBlock Text="○"
  24 | ///            IsVisible="{Binding Status,
  25 | ///                Converter={x:Static conv:MemberStatusToReadyVisibilityConverter.Instance},
  26 | ///                ConverterParameter=Pending}"/&gt;
  27 | /// </code>
  28 | /// </para>
  29 | /// <para>
  30 | /// Status matching is case-insensitive against the enum's name, so
  31 | /// either <c>Ready</c> or <c>ready</c> works as the parameter. Unknown
  32 | /// parameter strings always return false (the indicator stays hidden).
  33 | /// </para>
  34 | /// </summary>
  35 | public sealed class MemberStatusToReadyVisibilityConverter : IValueConverter
  36 | {
  37 |     /// <summary>Shared immutable instance for XAML static-resource use.</summary>
  38 |     public static readonly MemberStatusToReadyVisibilityConverter Instance = new();
  39 | 
  40 |     public object? Convert(object? value, Type targetType, object? parameter, CultureInfo culture)
  41 |     {
  42 |         if (value is not MemberStatus status) return false;
  43 |         if (parameter is not string paramName || string.IsNullOrEmpty(paramName)) return false;
  44 |         return status.ToString().Equals(paramName, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
  45 |     }
  46 | 
  47 |     public object? ConvertBack(object? value, Type targetType, object? parameter, CultureInfo culture)
  48 |         => throw new NotSupportedException("MemberStatusToReadyVisibilityConverter is one-way only.");
  49 | }

--- src/MyGame.Desktop/Converters/RarityBrushConverter.cs (C#, 63 lines, ~750 tokens) ---
   1 | using System;
   2 | using System.Collections.Generic;
   3 | using System.Globalization;
   4 | using Avalonia.Data.Converters;
   5 | using Avalonia.Media;
   6 | 
   7 | namespace MyGame.Desktop.Converters;
   8 | 
   9 | /// <summary>
  10 | /// Maps an item rarity string ("common" / "uncommon" / "rare" /
  11 | /// "veryRare" / "legendary" / "artifact") to the matching color brush
  12 | /// used by the inventory panel. Falls back to the common-gray brush
  13 | /// for unknown values.
  14 | ///
  15 | /// <para>
  16 | /// ITEM-RARITY (issue #66). Avalonia 11+ doesn't allow binding the
  17 | /// <c>Classes</c> property (it accepts a string literal only), so
  18 | /// instead of <c>Classes="{Binding RarityClass}"</c> we bind
  19 | /// <c>Foreground</c>/<c>BorderBrush</c> directly via this converter.
  20 | /// </para>
  21 | ///
  22 | /// <para>
  23 | /// Color palette (matches the spec):
  24 | /// <list type="bullet">
  25 | ///   <item>common    → <c>#9ca3af</c> (gray)</item>
  26 | ///   <item>uncommon  → <c>#10b981</c> (green)</item>
  27 | ///   <item>rare      → <c>#3b82f6</c> (blue)</item>
  28 | ///   <item>veryRare  → <c>#a855f7</c> (purple)</item>
  29 | ///   <item>legendary → <c>#f59e0b</c> (orange)</item>
  30 | ///   <item>artifact  → <c>#ef4444</c> (red)</item>
  31 | /// </list>
  32 | /// </para>
  33 | /// </summary>
  34 | public sealed class RarityBrushConverter : IValueConverter
  35 | {
  36 |     /// <summary>Shared immutable instance for XAML static-resource use.</summary>
  37 |     public static readonly RarityBrushConverter Instance = new();
  38 | 
  39 |     private static readonly IReadOnlyDictionary<string, IBrush> Brushes =
  40 |         new Dictionary<string, IBrush>(StringComparer.Ordinal)
  41 |         {
  42 |             ["common"]    = new SolidColorBrush(Color.Parse("#9ca3af")),
  43 |             ["uncommon"]  = new SolidColorBrush(Color.Parse("#10b981")),
  44 |             ["rare"]      = new SolidColorBrush(Color.Parse("#3b82f6")),
  45 |             ["veryRare"]  = new SolidColorBrush(Color.Parse("#a855f7")),
  46 |             ["legendary"] = new SolidColorBrush(Color.Parse("#f59e0b")),
  47 |             ["artifact"]  = new SolidColorBrush(Color.Parse("#ef4444")),
  48 |         };
  49 | 
  50 |     /// <summary>Default brush (common gray) used for unknown rarities.</summary>
  51 |     public static IBrush Fallback => Brushes["common"];
  52 | 
  53 |     /// <summary>Look up the brush for a rarity string. Never null.</summary>
  54 |     public static IBrush For(string? rarity) =>
  55 |         rarity is not null && Brushes.TryGetValue(rarity, out var b) ? b : Fallback;
  56 | 
  57 |     public object? Convert(object? value, Type targetType, object? parameter, CultureInfo culture)
  58 |         => For(value as string);
  59 | 
  60 |     public object? ConvertBack(object? value, Type targetType, object? parameter, CultureInfo culture)
  61 |         => throw new NotSupportedException("RarityBrushConverter is one-way only.");
  62 | }

--- src/MyGame.Desktop/Converters/ThemeConverters.cs (C#, 116 lines, ~1,359 tokens) ---
   1 | using System;
   2 | using System.Globalization;
   3 | using Avalonia;
   4 | using Avalonia.Data;
   5 | using Avalonia.Data.Converters;
   6 | using Avalonia.Media;
   7 | using MyGame.Desktop.Services;
   8 | 
   9 | namespace MyGame.Desktop.Converters;
  10 | 
  11 | /// <summary>
  12 | /// Two-way string-equality converter used to bind a string property to
  13 | /// a group of <c>RadioButton.IsChecked</c> values (issue #47).
  14 | ///
  15 | /// <para>
  16 | /// Forward direction: take the bound string value, return <c>true</c> if
  17 | /// it equals the <c>ConverterParameter</c> (case-insensitive). Used so
  18 | /// each RadioButton reflects "is this the currently selected option?".
  19 | /// </para>
  20 | ///
  21 | /// <para>
  22 | /// Reverse direction: when the RadioButton becomes checked, write the
  23 | /// <c>ConverterParameter</c> back to the source property (so e.g.
  24 | /// clicking the "Light" radio sets <c>ThemeMode = "Light"</c>). When the
  25 | /// RadioButton becomes unchecked, return
  26 | /// <see cref="AvaloniaProperty.UnsetValue"/> so the source is left alone
  27 | /// — otherwise unchecking one radio would null-out the property before
  28 | /// the newly-checked radio's <c>ConvertBack</c> fires.
  29 | /// </para>
  30 | ///
  31 | /// <para>
  32 | /// Used by the SettingsView's «Оформление» tab to bind the three theme-
  33 | /// mode RadioButtons (Dark / Light / System) to
  34 | /// <see cref="ViewModels.SettingsViewModel.ThemeMode"/>.
  35 | /// </para>
  36 | /// </summary>
  37 | public sealed class ThemeModeEqualsConverter : IValueConverter
  38 | {
  39 |     /// <summary>Shared immutable instance for XAML {x:Static} use.</summary>
  40 |     public static readonly ThemeModeEqualsConverter Instance = new();
  41 | 
  42 |     public object? Convert(object? value, Type targetType, object? parameter, CultureInfo culture)
  43 |     {
  44 |         var s = value as string;
  45 |         var p = parameter as string;
  46 |         if (s is null || p is null) return false;
  47 |         return string.Equals(s, p, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
  48 |     }
  49 | 
  50 |     public object? ConvertBack(object? value, Type targetType, object? parameter, CultureInfo culture)
  51 |     {
  52 |         // Only the "checked → true" transition should write back; the
  53 |         // "unchecked → false" transition returns UnsetValue so the source
  54 |         // keeps its current value (the newly-checked sibling radio will
  55 |         // fire its own ConvertBack with true).
  56 |         if (value is bool b && b)
  57 |             return parameter as string ?? "Dark";
  58 |         return AvaloniaProperty.UnsetValue;
  59 |     }
  60 | }
  61 | 
  62 | /// <summary>
  63 | /// One-way converter that maps an accent preset name (Indigo / Emerald /
  64 | /// Amber / Rose / Cyan / Violet) to a <see cref="SolidColorBrush"/>
  65 | /// filled with that accent's hex color (issue #47).
  66 | ///
  67 | /// <para>
  68 | /// Delegates to <see cref="ThemeService.GetAccentHex"/> so the palette
  69 | /// stays in one place. Used by the SettingsView's accent-swatch buttons
  70 | /// (each swatch's <c>Background</c> is bound to its name via this
  71 | /// converter so the swatch shows the actual color it represents).
  72 | /// </para>
  73 | /// </summary>
  74 | public sealed class AccentHexConverter : IValueConverter
  75 | {
  76 |     /// <summary>Shared immutable instance for XAML {x:Static} use.</summary>
  77 |     public static readonly AccentHexConverter Instance = new();
  78 | 
  79 |     public object? Convert(object? value, Type targetType, object? parameter, CultureInfo culture)
  80 |     {
  81 |         var name = value as string;
  82 |         var hex = ThemeService.GetAccentHex(name ?? "");
  83 |         // Avalonia 12's Brush class doesn't expose a TryParse, so we
  84 |         // parse via Color.TryParse (which does exist) and wrap in a
  85 |         // SolidColorBrush. Falls back to gray on any parse failure.
  86 |         if (Color.TryParse(hex, out var c))
  87 |             return new SolidColorBrush(c);
  88 |         return Brushes.Gray;
  89 |     }
  90 | 
  91 |     public object? ConvertBack(object? value, Type targetType, object? parameter, CultureInfo culture)
  92 |         => throw new NotSupportedException("AccentHexConverter is one-way only.");
  93 | }
  94 | 
  95 | /// <summary>
  96 | /// One-way converter that maps an int N to N-1, clamped to a minimum of
  97 | /// 1. Used by the inventory panel's split-stack dialog to bind the
  98 | /// NumericUpDown's Maximum to <c>SplitTarget.Quantity - 1</c> (the user
  99 | /// can split at most N-1 units so the original stack still has 1 left).
 100 | /// </summary>
 101 | public sealed class SplitMaxMinusOneConverter : IValueConverter
 102 | {
 103 |     /// <summary>Shared immutable instance for XAML {x:Static} use.</summary>
 104 |     public static readonly SplitMaxMinusOneConverter Instance = new();
 105 | 
 106 |     public object? Convert(object? value, Type targetType, object? parameter, CultureInfo culture)
 107 |     {
 108 |         if (value is int i) return Math.Max(1, i - 1);
 109 |         if (value is long l) return (int)Math.Max(1L, l - 1);
 110 |         return 1;
 111 |     }
 112 | 
 113 |     public object? ConvertBack(object? value, Type targetType, object? parameter, CultureInfo culture)
 114 |         => throw new NotSupportedException("SplitMaxMinusOneConverter is one-way only.");
 115 | }
