--- src/MyGame.Desktop/Program.cs (C#, 272 lines, ~2,922 tokens) ---
   1 | ﻿using System;
   2 | using System.Diagnostics;
   3 | using System.IO;
   4 | using System.Runtime.CompilerServices;
   5 | using System.Text;
   6 | using System.Threading.Tasks;
   7 | using Avalonia;
   8 | using Avalonia.Controls;
   9 | using Avalonia.Controls.ApplicationLifetimes;
  10 | using Avalonia.Input.Platform;
  11 | using Avalonia.Layout;
  12 | using Avalonia.Threading;
  13 | using MyGame.Core.Logging;
  14 | using MyGame.Core.Profile;
  15 | using MyGame.Desktop.Services;
  16 | 
  17 | namespace MyGame.Desktop;
  18 | 
  19 | class Program
  20 | {
  21 |     // Initialization code. Don't use any Avalonia, third-party APIs or any
  22 |     // SynchronizationContext-reliant code before AppMain is called: things aren't initialized
  23 |     // yet and stuff might break.
  24 |     [STAThread]
  25 |     public static void Main(string[] args)
  26 |     {
  27 |         // Initialize the structured logger FIRST, before anything else.
  28 |         // Issue #71: writes to %APPDATA%/MyGame/logs/game-{date}.log.
  29 |         var logDir = Path.Combine(ProfileStore.DefaultProfileDirectory, "logs");
  30 |         GameLogger.Initialize(logDir, GameLogger.Level.Info);
  31 |         GameLogger.Instance.Info("=== Pathstone starting ===");
  32 | 
  33 |         // Register global exception handlers.
  34 |         RegisterGlobalExceptionHandlers();
  35 | 
  36 |         try
  37 |         {
  38 |             BuildAvaloniaApp().StartWithClassicDesktopLifetime(args);
  39 |         }
  40 |         catch (Exception ex)
  41 |         {
  42 |             // Top-level catch — the global handlers don't always fire
  43 |             // for exceptions on the main thread during startup. This
  44 |             // ensures we always write a dump + try to show a dialog
  45 |             // before the process dies.
  46 |             HandleFatalException(ex, context: "Program.Main top-level catch");
  47 |         }
  48 |     }
  49 | 
  50 |     // Avalonia configuration, don't remove; also used by visual designer.
  51 |     public static AppBuilder BuildAvaloniaApp()
  52 |         => AppBuilder.Configure<App>()
  53 |             .UsePlatformDetect()
  54 | #if DEBUG
  55 |             .WithDeveloperTools()
  56 | #endif
  57 |             .WithInterFont()
  58 |             .LogToTrace();
  59 | 
  60 |     // ─── Global exception handlers ────────────────────────────────────
  61 | 
  62 |     /// <summary>
  63 |     /// Wire <see cref="AppDomain.UnhandledException"/> +
  64 |     /// <see cref="TaskScheduler.UnobservedTaskException"/> so even
  65 |     /// background-thread faults get a crash dump + (best-effort) dialog.
  66 |     /// </summary>
  67 |     private static void RegisterGlobalExceptionHandlers()
  68 |     {
  69 |         // AppDomain.UnhandledException fires for any uncaught exception
  70 |         // in any thread (managed). The CLR may terminate the process
  71 |         // after the handler returns (depending on
  72 |         // UnhandledExceptionEventArgs.IsTerminating), so we MUST write
  73 |         // the dump synchronously here.
  74 |         AppDomain.CurrentDomain.UnhandledException += (sender, e) =>
  75 |         {
  76 |             var ex = e.ExceptionObject as Exception;
  77 |             var terminating = e.IsTerminating;
  78 |             var context = terminating
  79 |                 ? "AppDomain.UnhandledException (terminating)"
  80 |                 : "AppDomain.UnhandledException (non-terminating)";
  81 |             HandleFatalException(ex, context: context);
  82 |         };
  83 | 
  84 |         // TaskScheduler.UnobservedTaskException fires when a Task's
  85 |         // exception is never observed (no await, no .Wait, no
  86 |         // .Exception access) and the Task is GC'd. By default this
  87 |         // would tear down the process in .NET 4.5+; we mark it observed
  88 |         // (so it doesn't escalate) and log a crash dump.
  89 |         TaskScheduler.UnobservedTaskException += (sender, e) =>
  90 |         {
  91 |             HandleFatalException(e.Exception, context: "TaskScheduler.UnobservedTaskException");
  92 |             e.SetObserved(); // prevent process termination
  93 |         };
  94 |     }
  95 | 
  96 |     /// <summary>
  97 |     /// Central fatal-exception handler. Writes a crash dump via
  98 |     /// <see cref="CrashLogger"/> and tries to show a user-visible dialog
  99 |     /// (best-effort — if Avalonia isn't initialized yet, or the dialog
 100 |     /// itself throws, we just leave the dump on disk). Never rethrows.
 101 |     /// </summary>
 102 |     /// <param name="exception">The exception (may be null when the
 103 |     /// AppDomain handler couldn't surface one).</param>
 104 |     /// <param name="context">Short string identifying which handler
 105 |     /// caught this — included as a header in the dump.</param>
 106 |     private static void HandleFatalException(Exception? exception, string context)
 107 |     {
 108 |         // 1) Always write the crash dump first. CrashLogger is robust
 109 |         //    (wraps its own I/O in try/catch) so this won't throw.
 110 |         string? dumpPath = null;
 111 |         try
 112 |         {
 113 |             dumpPath = CrashLogger.Log(exception, context);
 114 |         }
 115 |         catch
 116 |         {
 117 |             // CrashLogger's own try/catch should have caught everything,
 118 |             // but if even that fails, we have nothing more we can do.
 119 |         }
 120 | 
 121 |         // 2) Try to show a dialog. Best-effort — if Avalonia isn't
 122 |         //    initialized yet (crash during startup), or if we're on a
 123 |         //    non-UI thread we can't marshal to, or the dialog itself
 124 |         //    throws, we just skip the dialog and leave the dump on disk.
 125 |         try
 126 |         {
 127 |             TryShowCrashDialog(exception, dumpPath);
 128 |         }
 129 |         catch
 130 |         {
 131 |             // Swallow — the dump is already on disk.
 132 |         }
 133 |     }
 134 | 
 135 |     /// <summary>
 136 |     /// Best-effort crash dialog. Marshals to the Avalonia UI thread (if
 137 |     /// one exists) and shows a small modal Window with the error message
 138 |     /// + dump file path + a "Copy to clipboard" button. No-op if no
 139 |     /// Avalonia application is running yet (crash during startup before
 140 |     /// the UI thread exists).
 141 |     /// </summary>
 142 |     private static void TryShowCrashDialog(Exception? exception, string? dumpPath)
 143 |     {
 144 |         // If the Avalonia application isn't running, we can't show a
 145 |         // dialog. Application.Current is null until
 146 |         // OnFrameworkInitializationCompleted runs.
 147 |         if (Application.Current is null) return;
 148 | 
 149 |         // The IClassicDesktopStyleApplicationLifetime exposes MainWindow;
 150 |         // if it's null, no main window has been created yet.
 151 |         if (Application.Current.ApplicationLifetime is not IClassicDesktopStyleApplicationLifetime desktop)
 152 |             return;
 153 | 
 154 |         var message = exception?.Message ?? "(no exception object available)";
 155 |         var dumpText = dumpPath is not null && File.Exists(dumpPath)
 156 |             ? File.ReadAllText(dumpPath)
 157 |             : "(no dump file available)";
 158 | 
 159 |         // Marshal to the UI thread — the global handlers may fire on
 160 |         // background threads.
 161 |         Dispatcher.UIThread.Post(() =>
 162 |         {
 163 |             try
 164 |             {
 165 |                 var dialog = BuildCrashDialog(message, dumpPath ?? "(no dump file)", dumpText);
 166 |                 dialog.ShowDialog(desktop.MainWindow ?? new Window());
 167 |             }
 168 |             catch
 169 |             {
 170 |                 // If building/showing the dialog fails, just give up —
 171 |                 // the dump file is already on disk and the user can
 172 |                 // find it there.
 173 |             }
 174 |         });
 175 |     }
 176 | 
 177 |     /// <summary>
 178 |     /// Build the crash dialog window programmatically (no XAML file).
 179 |     /// Two-column layout: an error message on top, a ScrollViewer with
 180 |     /// the full dump text below, and a "Copy to clipboard" + "Close"
 181 |     /// button row at the bottom.
 182 |     /// </summary>
 183 |     private static Window BuildCrashDialog(string message, string dumpPath, string dumpText)
 184 |     {
 185 |         var titleText = new TextBlock
 186 |         {
 187 |             Text = "Pathstone crashed",
 188 |             FontSize = 18,
 189 |             FontWeight = Avalonia.Media.FontWeight.Bold,
 190 |             Margin = new Thickness(0, 0, 0, 8),
 191 |         };
 192 | 
 193 |         var introText = new TextBlock
 194 |         {
 195 |             Text = $"A crash dump was written to:\n{dumpPath}\n\nPlease report this on GitHub.\n\nError:\n{message}",
 196 |             TextWrapping = Avalonia.Media.TextWrapping.Wrap,
 197 |             Margin = new Thickness(0, 0, 0, 12),
 198 |         };
 199 | 
 200 |         var dumpBox = new TextBox
 201 |         {
 202 |             Text = dumpText,
 203 |             IsReadOnly = true,
 204 |             AcceptsReturn = true,
 205 |             TextWrapping = Avalonia.Media.TextWrapping.Wrap,
 206 |             FontFamily = Avalonia.Media.FontFamily.Parse("Cascadia Mono,Consolas,Menlo,monospace"),
 207 |             MinHeight = 200,
 208 |             MaxHeight = 400,
 209 |         };
 210 | 
 211 |         var copyButton = new Button
 212 |         {
 213 |             Content = "Copy to clipboard",
 214 |             Padding = new Thickness(12, 4),
 215 |         };
 216 |         copyButton.Click += async (s, e) =>
 217 |         {
 218 |             try
 219 |             {
 220 |                 var clipboard = TopLevel.GetTopLevel(dumpBox)?.Clipboard;
 221 |                 if (clipboard is not null)
 222 |                     await clipboard.SetTextAsync(dumpText);
 223 |             }
 224 |             catch { /* best-effort */ }
 225 |         };
 226 | 
 227 |         var closeButton = new Button
 228 |         {
 229 |             Content = "Close",
 230 |             Padding = new Thickness(12, 4),
 231 |         };
 232 | 
 233 |         var window = new Window
 234 |         {
 235 |             Title = "Pathstone — Crash",
 236 |             Width = 600,
 237 |             Height = 480,
 238 |             CanResize = true,
 239 |             WindowStartupLocation = WindowStartupLocation.CenterScreen,
 240 |             Content = new DockPanel
 241 |             {
 242 |                 LastChildFill = true,
 243 |                 Margin = new Thickness(16),
 244 |                 Children =
 245 |                 {
 246 |                     // Button row docked at the bottom
 247 |                     new StackPanel
 248 |                     {
 249 |                         Orientation = Orientation.Horizontal,
 250 |                         HorizontalAlignment = HorizontalAlignment.Right,
 251 |                         Spacing = 8,
 252 |                         Margin = new Thickness(0, 12, 0, 0),
 253 |                         Children = { copyButton, closeButton },
 254 |                         [DockPanel.DockProperty] = Dock.Bottom,
 255 |                     },
 256 |                     // Header (title + intro) docked at the top
 257 |                     new StackPanel
 258 |                     {
 259 |                         [DockPanel.DockProperty] = Dock.Top,
 260 |                         Children = { titleText, introText },
 261 |                     },
 262 |                     // Dump text fills the rest
 263 |                     dumpBox,
 264 |                 },
 265 |             },
 266 |         };
 267 | 
 268 |         closeButton.Click += (s, e) => window.Close();
 269 |         return window;

--- src/MyGame.Desktop/Services/CrashLogger.cs (C#, 209 lines, ~2,394 tokens) ---
   1 | using System;
   2 | using System.IO;
   3 | using System.Runtime.InteropServices;
   4 | using System.Text;
   5 | 
   6 | namespace MyGame.Desktop.Services;
   7 | 
   8 | /// <summary>
   9 | /// Crash dump writer for the Pathstone desktop app. Writes a timestamped
  10 | /// text dump of an uncaught exception to
  11 | /// <c>%APPDATA%/MyGame/logs/crash-{yyyyMMdd-HHmmss}.txt</c> (alongside
  12 | /// the saves/profile so all per-user state is in one place). The dump
  13 | /// contains:
  14 | /// <list type="bullet">
  15 | ///   <item>Timestamp (UTC + local).</item>
  16 | ///   <item>.NET runtime version + OS description (RuntimeInformation).</item>
  17 | ///   <item>Exception type + message + full stack trace, walking inner
  18 | ///     exceptions recursively.</item>
  19 | ///   <item>A short footer pointing the user at the logs directory.</item>
  20 | /// </list>
  21 | ///
  22 | /// <para>
  23 | /// <b>Robustness</b>: every step is wrapped in try/catch — the crash
  24 | /// logger MUST NOT throw while crashing. If even the file write fails
  25 | /// (disk full, permissions, etc.), the dump is written to
  26 | /// <see cref="Console.Error"/> as a last resort.
  27 | /// </para>
  28 | ///
  29 | /// <para>
  30 | /// <b>Thread-safety</b>: <see cref="Log"/> is safe to call from any
  31 | /// thread (it takes a process-wide lock so two concurrent crashes don't
  32 | /// interleave their dumps).
  33 | /// </para>
  34 | /// </summary>
  35 | public static class CrashLogger
  36 | {
  37 |     private static readonly object s_lock = new();
  38 | 
  39 |     /// <summary>
  40 |     /// Root directory for crash dumps:
  41 |     /// <c>%APPDATA%/MyGame/logs</c> on Windows,
  42 |     /// <c>~/.config/MyGame/logs</c> on Linux,
  43 |     /// <c>~/Library/Application Support/MyGame/logs</c> on macOS.
  44 |     /// Falls back to <c>{AppContext.BaseDirectory}/logs</c> if APPDATA
  45 |     /// isn't available.
  46 |     /// </summary>
  47 |     public static string LogsDirectory
  48 |     {
  49 |         get
  50 |         {
  51 |             try
  52 |             {
  53 |                 var appData = Environment.GetFolderPath(Environment.SpecialFolder.ApplicationData);
  54 |                 if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(appData))
  55 |                     return Path.Combine(appData, "MyGame", "logs");
  56 |                 var home = Environment.GetFolderPath(Environment.SpecialFolder.UserProfile);
  57 |                 if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(home))
  58 |                     return Path.Combine(home, ".mygame", "logs");
  59 |                 return Path.Combine(AppContext.BaseDirectory, "logs");
  60 |             }
  61 |             catch
  62 |             {
  63 |                 // Last-resort: folder next to the executable.
  64 |                 return Path.Combine(AppContext.BaseDirectory, "logs");
  65 |             }
  66 |         }
  67 |     }
  68 | 
  69 |     /// <summary>
  70 |     /// Write a crash dump for <paramref name="exception"/> and return the
  71 |     /// absolute path to the dump file. Returns null if even the file
  72 |     /// write fails (the dump is also written to
  73 |     /// <see cref="Console.Error"/> in that case so it isn't lost).
  74 |     /// </summary>
  75 |     /// <param name="exception">The exception to dump. Null is treated as
  76 |     /// an "unknown crash" sentinel.</param>
  77 |     /// <param name="context">Optional extra context — e.g. which handler
  78 |     /// caught the exception (<c>"AppDomain.UnhandledException"</c>,
  79 |     /// <c>"TaskScheduler.UnobservedTaskException"</c>, etc.). Included
  80 |     /// as a header line in the dump.</param>
  81 |     public static string? Log(Exception? exception, string? context = null)
  82 |     {
  83 |         lock (s_lock)
  84 |         {
  85 |             string? filePath = null;
  86 |             try
  87 |             {
  88 |                 var dir = LogsDirectory;
  89 |                 Directory.CreateDirectory(dir);
  90 |                 var stamp = DateTime.UtcNow.ToString("yyyyMMdd-HHmmss");
  91 |                 filePath = Path.Combine(dir, $"crash-{stamp}.txt");
  92 |                 var dump = BuildDump(exception, context, filePath);
  93 |                 File.WriteAllText(filePath, dump, Encoding.UTF8);
  94 |             }
  95 |             catch (Exception writeEx)
  96 |             {
  97 |                 // The crash logger must NEVER throw. Fall back to
  98 |                 // Console.Error so the dump isn't lost entirely.
  99 |                 try
 100 |                 {
 101 |                     Console.Error.WriteLine("=== CRASH LOGGER FAILED TO WRITE FILE ===");
 102 |                     Console.Error.WriteLine($"File path attempted: {filePath}");
 103 |                     Console.Error.WriteLine($"Write error: {writeEx}");
 104 |                     Console.Error.WriteLine("=== ORIGINAL EXCEPTION ===");
 105 |                     Console.Error.WriteLine(BuildDump(exception, context, filePath));
 106 |                 }
 107 |                 catch { /* nothing else we can do */ }
 108 |             }
 109 |             return filePath;
 110 |         }
 111 |     }
 112 | 
 113 |     /// <summary>
 114 |     /// Build the human-readable crash dump string. Walks inner exceptions
 115 |     /// recursively so a deeply-nested AggregateException or
 116 |     /// TargetInvocationException is fully visible.
 117 |     /// </summary>
 118 |     private static string BuildDump(Exception? exception, string? context, string? filePath)
 119 |     {
 120 |         var sb = new StringBuilder();
 121 |         sb.AppendLine("=== Pathstone Crash Dump ===");
 122 |         sb.AppendLine();
 123 |         sb.AppendLine($"Timestamp (UTC): {DateTimeOffset.UtcNow:O}");
 124 |         sb.AppendLine($"Timestamp (local): {DateTimeOffset.Now:O}");
 125 |         sb.AppendLine();
 126 |         sb.AppendLine("--- Runtime ---");
 127 |         try
 128 |         {
 129 |             sb.AppendLine($".NET version: {RuntimeInformation.FrameworkDescription}");
 130 |             sb.AppendLine($"OS: {RuntimeInformation.OSDescription}");
 131 |             sb.AppendLine($"OS architecture: {RuntimeInformation.OSArchitecture}");
 132 |             sb.AppendLine($"Process architecture: {RuntimeInformation.ProcessArchitecture}");
 133 |         }
 134 |         catch (Exception ex)
 135 |         {
 136 |             sb.AppendLine($"(failed to read RuntimeInformation: {ex.Message})");
 137 |         }
 138 |         sb.AppendLine();
 139 |         sb.AppendLine("--- Context ---");
 140 |         sb.AppendLine(string.IsNullOrEmpty(context) ? "(no context provided)" : context);
 141 |         sb.AppendLine();
 142 |         sb.AppendLine("--- Exception ---");
 143 |         AppendException(sb, exception, depth: 0);
 144 |         sb.AppendLine();
 145 |         sb.AppendLine("--- End of dump ---");
 146 |         if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(filePath))
 147 |             sb.AppendLine($"Dump file: {filePath}");
 148 |         sb.AppendLine("Please report this on GitHub with the dump file attached.");
 149 |         return sb.ToString();
 150 |     }
 151 | 
 152 |     /// <summary>
 153 |     /// Append one exception + walk inner exceptions recursively. Depth
 154 |     /// is capped at 10 to prevent infinite loops on pathological
 155 |     /// cyclic exception chains (which shouldn't happen, but defense in
 156 |     /// depth).
 157 |     /// </summary>
 158 |     private static void AppendException(StringBuilder sb, Exception? ex, int depth)
 159 |     {
 160 |         if (ex is null) return;
 161 |         if (depth > 10)
 162 |         {
 163 |             sb.AppendLine("  [max inner-exception depth reached]");
 164 |             return;
 165 |         }
 166 |         var indent = depth > 0 ? new string(' ', depth * 2) : "";
 167 |         if (depth > 0)
 168 |             sb.AppendLine($"{indent}--- Inner exception (depth {depth}) ---");
 169 |         sb.AppendLine($"{indent}Type: {ex.GetType().FullName}");
 170 |         sb.AppendLine($"{indent}Message: {ex.Message}");
 171 |         if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(ex.Source))
 172 |             sb.AppendLine($"{indent}Source: {ex.Source}");
 173 |         if (ex.TargetSite is not null)
 174 |             sb.AppendLine($"{indent}TargetSite: {ex.TargetSite}");
 175 |         sb.AppendLine($"{indent}StackTrace:");
 176 |         var stack = ex.StackTrace;
 177 |         if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(stack))
 178 |         {
 179 |             sb.AppendLine($"{indent}  (no stack trace)");
 180 |         }
 181 |         else
 182 |         {
 183 |             // Indent each stack line so it lines up with the header.
 184 |             foreach (var line in stack.Split('\n'))
 185 |                 sb.AppendLine($"{indent}  {line.TrimEnd('\r')}");
 186 |         }
 187 |         // Recurse into inner exception(s).
 188 |         if (ex is AggregateException agg)
 189 |         {
 190 |             // AggregateException flattens inner exceptions — walk each.
 191 |             var inners = agg.InnerExceptions;
 192 |             if (inners is { Count: > 0 })
 193 |             {
 194 |                 sb.AppendLine($"{indent}--- Aggregate inner exceptions ({inners.Count}) ---");
 195 |                 for (int i = 0; i < inners.Count; i++)
 196 |                 {
 197 |                     sb.AppendLine($"{indent}[{i}]");
 198 |                     AppendException(sb, inners[i], depth + 1);
 199 |                 }
 200 |                 return;
 201 |             }
 202 |         }
 203 |         if (ex.InnerException is not null)
 204 |         {
 205 |             AppendException(sb, ex.InnerException, depth + 1);
 206 |         }
 207 |     }
 208 | }

--- src/MyGame.Desktop/Services/ServiceHost.cs (C#, 114 lines, ~1,269 tokens) ---
   1 | using System;
   2 | using Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection;
   3 | using MyGame.Core.AI.Prompts;
   4 | using MyGame.Core.Common;
   5 | using MyGame.Core.Profile;
   6 | using MyGame.Core.Saves;
   7 | 
   8 | namespace MyGame.Desktop.Services;
   9 | 
  10 | /// <summary>
  11 | /// Static facade over the Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection
  12 | /// container. Built once on app startup in
  13 | /// <see cref="App.OnFrameworkInitializationCompleted"/>; the rest of
  14 | /// the app resolves Core-layer services via <see cref="Resolve{T}"/>.
  15 | ///
  16 | /// <para>
  17 | /// All Core services are registered as singletons — they're either
  18 | /// stateless (PromptLoader), wrap a single on-disk file
  19 | /// (ProfileStore, SettingsStore), or own a process-lifetime directory
  20 | /// (SaveManager, CharacterSheetStore). The shared
  21 | /// <see cref="EventBus"/> is also a singleton so any code path can
  22 | /// subscribe to engine events.
  23 | /// </para>
  24 | ///
  25 | /// <para>
  26 | /// ViewModels are NOT registered here — they're constructed on demand
  27 | /// by <see cref="ViewModels.MainViewModel"/> with the services they
  28 | /// need. This keeps the ViewModel tree lightweight and lets the
  29 | /// navigation root decide when to spawn each screen.
  30 | /// </para>
  31 | /// </summary>
  32 | public static class ServiceHost
  33 | {
  34 |     private static ServiceProvider? _provider;
  35 |     private static readonly object _buildLock = new();
  36 | 
  37 |     /// <summary>
  38 |     /// Build the DI container if not already built. Idempotent —
  39 |     /// subsequent calls are no-ops. Thread-safe.
  40 |     /// </summary>
  41 |     public static void Initialize()
  42 |     {
  43 |         if (_provider is not null) return;
  44 |         lock (_buildLock)
  45 |         {
  46 |             if (_provider is not null) return;
  47 | 
  48 |             var services = new ServiceCollection();
  49 | 
  50 |             // ─── Core.Common ─────────────────────────────────────────────
  51 |             // One shared event bus for the whole app. Any code path can
  52 |             // publish/subscribe (engine events, MP events, etc.).
  53 |             services.AddSingleton<EventBus>();
  54 | 
  55 |             // ─── Core.Profile ────────────────────────────────────────────
  56 |             services.AddSingleton<ProfileStore>();
  57 |             services.AddSingleton<SettingsStore>();
  58 | 
  59 |             // ─── Core.Saves ──────────────────────────────────────────────
  60 |             services.AddSingleton<SaveManager>();
  61 |             services.AddSingleton<CharacterSheetStore>();
  62 | 
  63 |             // ─── Core.AI.Prompts ─────────────────────────────────────────
  64 |             // The prompt loader reads embedded .md resources — a single
  65 |             // instance caches them all.
  66 |             services.AddSingleton<PromptLoader>();
  67 | 
  68 |             // ─── Issue #59: Mod loading ──────────────────────────────────
  69 |             // Load .pathstone-pack files from {profileDir}/mods/ into a
  70 |             // shared ContentRegistry. The registry is used by DefaultWorld
  71 |             // + WorldBuilder + all GM tools.
  72 |             var profileDir = MyGame.Core.Profile.ProfileStore.DefaultProfileDirectory;
  73 |             var modsDir = System.IO.Path.Combine(profileDir, "mods");
  74 |             var modRegistry = MyGame.Core.World.Content.ContentRegistry.LoadDefault();
  75 |             var loadedMods = MyGame.Core.Tooling.ModLoader.LoadAll(modsDir, modRegistry);
  76 |             if (loadedMods.Count > 0)
  77 |             {
  78 |                 System.Diagnostics.Trace.WriteLine($"[ServiceHost] Loaded {loadedMods.Count} mod(s): {string.Join(", ", loadedMods)}");
  79 |             }
  80 |             services.AddSingleton(modRegistry);
  81 | 
  82 |             _provider = services.BuildServiceProvider();
  83 |         }
  84 |     }
  85 | 
  86 |     /// <summary>
  87 |     /// Resolve a registered service. Throws if
  88 |     /// <see cref="Initialize"/> hasn't been called yet, or if the
  89 |     /// service isn't registered.
  90 |     /// </summary>
  91 |     public static T Resolve<T>() where T : notnull
  92 |     {
  93 |         if (_provider is null)
  94 |             throw new InvalidOperationException(
  95 |                 "ServiceHost has not been initialized. Call ServiceHost.Initialize() at app startup.");
  96 |         return _provider.GetRequiredService<T>();
  97 |     }
  98 | 
  99 |     /// <summary>
 100 |     /// Get the underlying provider (advanced scenarios — e.g. spawning
 101 |     /// scopes). Most callers should use <see cref="Resolve{T}"/>.
 102 |     /// </summary>
 103 |     public static IServiceProvider Provider
 104 |     {
 105 |         get
 106 |         {
 107 |             if (_provider is null)
 108 |                 throw new InvalidOperationException(
 109 |                     "ServiceHost has not been initialized. Call ServiceHost.Initialize() at app startup.");
 110 |             return _provider;
 111 |         }
 112 |     }
 113 | }

--- src/MyGame.Desktop/Services/ThemeService.cs (C#, 208 lines, ~2,179 tokens) ---
   1 | using System;
   2 | using System.Collections.Generic;
   3 | using System.Diagnostics;
   4 | using Avalonia;
   5 | using Avalonia.Controls;
   6 | using Avalonia.Controls.ApplicationLifetimes;
   7 | using Avalonia.Media;
   8 | using Avalonia.Styling;
   9 | using MyGame.Core.Profile;
  10 | 
  11 | namespace MyGame.Desktop.Services;
  12 | 
  13 | /// <summary>
  14 | /// Runtime theme + accent + animation switching (issue #47 + #46).
  15 | /// </summary>
  16 | public static class ThemeService
  17 | {
  18 |     private static readonly Dictionary<string, string> DarkPalette = new()
  19 |     {
  20 |         ["AppBackground"] = "#0F1115",
  21 |         ["AppSurface"]    = "#171A21",
  22 |         ["AppSurfaceAlt"] = "#1F2330",
  23 |         ["AppBorder"]     = "#2C3142",
  24 |         ["AppForeground"] = "#E6E8EC",
  25 |         ["AppMuted"]      = "#8A93A6",
  26 |         ["AppDanger"]     = "#E5484D",
  27 |         ["AppSuccess"]    = "#3DD68C",
  28 |     };
  29 | 
  30 |     private static readonly Dictionary<string, string> LightPalette = new()
  31 |     {
  32 |         ["AppBackground"] = "#FAFAFA",
  33 |         ["AppSurface"]    = "#FFFFFF",
  34 |         ["AppSurfaceAlt"] = "#F3F4F6",
  35 |         ["AppBorder"]     = "#D1D5DB",
  36 |         ["AppForeground"] = "#1F2937",
  37 |         ["AppMuted"]      = "#6B7280",
  38 |         ["AppDanger"]     = "#DC2626",
  39 |         ["AppSuccess"]    = "#059669",
  40 |     };
  41 | 
  42 |     private static readonly Dictionary<string, (string Accent, string AccentFg)> Accents = new(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
  43 |     {
  44 |         ["Indigo"]  = ("#7C5CFF", "#FFFFFF"),
  45 |         ["Emerald"] = ("#10B981", "#FFFFFF"),
  46 |         ["Amber"]   = ("#F59E0B", "#1F2937"),
  47 |         ["Rose"]    = ("#F43F5E", "#FFFFFF"),
  48 |         ["Cyan"]    = ("#06B6D4", "#FFFFFF"),
  49 |         ["Violet"]  = ("#8B5CF6", "#FFFFFF"),
  50 |     };
  51 | 
  52 |     public static IReadOnlyList<string> AccentPresetNames { get; } =
  53 |         new[] { "Indigo", "Emerald", "Amber", "Rose", "Cyan", "Violet" };
  54 | 
  55 |     public static string GetAccentHex(string name) =>
  56 |         Accents.TryGetValue(name ?? "", out var v) ? v.Accent : Accents["Indigo"].Accent;
  57 | 
  58 |     public static string GetAccentFgHex(string name) =>
  59 |         Accents.TryGetValue(name ?? "", out var v) ? v.AccentFg : Accents["Indigo"].AccentFg;
  60 | 
  61 |     /// <summary>
  62 |     /// Apply theme + accent + animation flag. Never throws — all errors
  63 |     /// are caught and logged via Trace.
  64 |     /// </summary>
  65 |     public static void ApplyTheme(string themeMode, string accentName, bool enableAnimations)
  66 |     {
  67 |         try
  68 |         {
  69 |             ApplyThemeInternal(themeMode, accentName, enableAnimations);
  70 |         }
  71 |         catch (Exception ex)
  72 |         {
  73 |             Trace.WriteLine($"[ThemeService] ApplyTheme FAILED: {ex}");
  74 |         }
  75 |     }
  76 | 
  77 |     private static void ApplyThemeInternal(string themeMode, string accentName, bool enableAnimations)
  78 |     {
  79 |         var app = Application.Current;
  80 |         if (app is null) return;
  81 | 
  82 |         // 1) FluentTheme variant.
  83 |         var mode = NormalizeMode(themeMode);
  84 |         app.RequestedThemeVariant = mode switch
  85 |         {
  86 |             "Light"  => ThemeVariant.Light,
  87 |             "System" => ThemeVariant.Default,
  88 |             _        => ThemeVariant.Dark,
  89 |         };
  90 | 
  91 |         // 2) Custom palette.
  92 |         var useDark = ResolveIsDark(mode);
  93 |         var palette = useDark ? DarkPalette : LightPalette;
  94 | 
  95 |         // Parse + set Color resources.
  96 |         foreach (var (key, hex) in palette)
  97 |         {
  98 |             if (Color.TryParse(hex, out var color))
  99 |             {
 100 |                 app.Resources[key] = color;
 101 |             }
 102 |         }
 103 | 
 104 |         // 3) Accent colors.
 105 |         var accentHex = GetAccentHex(accentName);
 106 |         var accentFgHex = GetAccentFgHex(accentName);
 107 |         if (Color.TryParse(accentHex, out var accentColor))
 108 |             app.Resources["AppAccent"] = accentColor;
 109 |         if (Color.TryParse(accentFgHex, out var accentFgColor))
 110 |             app.Resources["AppAccentFg"] = accentFgColor;
 111 | 
 112 |         // 4) Update SolidColorBrush resources. Use TryGetResource to
 113 |         //    safely read the existing brush (IResourceDictionary does NOT
 114 |         //    support indexer reading — only writing). If the brush exists
 115 |         //    and is a SolidColorBrush, mutate its Color in place (safe —
 116 |         //    Color is observable). Otherwise create a new brush.
 117 |         UpdateBrush(app, "AppBackgroundBrush",  useDark ? "#0F1115" : "#FAFAFA");
 118 |         UpdateBrush(app, "AppSurfaceBrush",     useDark ? "#171A21" : "#FFFFFF");
 119 |         UpdateBrush(app, "AppSurfaceAltBrush",  useDark ? "#1F2330" : "#F3F4F6");
 120 |         UpdateBrush(app, "AppBorderBrush",      useDark ? "#2C3142" : "#D1D5DB");
 121 |         UpdateBrush(app, "AppForegroundBrush",  useDark ? "#E6E8EC" : "#1F2937");
 122 |         UpdateBrush(app, "AppMutedBrush",       useDark ? "#8A93A6" : "#6B7280");
 123 |         UpdateBrush(app, "AppAccentBrush",      accentHex);
 124 |         UpdateBrush(app, "AppAccentFgBrush",    accentFgHex);
 125 |         UpdateBrush(app, "AppDangerBrush",      useDark ? "#E5484D" : "#DC2626");
 126 |         UpdateBrush(app, "AppSuccessBrush",     useDark ? "#3DD68C" : "#059669");
 127 | 
 128 |         // 5) Animations gate.
 129 |         if (app.ApplicationLifetime is IClassicDesktopStyleApplicationLifetime desktop
 130 |             && desktop.MainWindow is Window w)
 131 |         {
 132 |             if (enableAnimations)
 133 |             {
 134 |                 if (!w.Classes.Contains("Anim"))
 135 |                 {
 136 |                     w.Classes.Add("Anim");
 137 |                     Trace.WriteLine("[ThemeService] Added 'Anim' class to MainWindow");
 138 |                 }
 139 |             }
 140 |             else
 141 |             {
 142 |                 if (w.Classes.Contains("Anim"))
 143 |                 {
 144 |                     w.Classes.Remove("Anim");
 145 |                     Trace.WriteLine("[ThemeService] Removed 'Anim' class from MainWindow");
 146 |                 }
 147 |             }
 148 |         }
 149 |         else
 150 |         {
 151 |             Trace.WriteLine("[ThemeService] WARNING: MainWindow not found — Anim class not toggled");
 152 |         }
 153 |     }
 154 | 
 155 |     /// <summary>
 156 |     /// Safely update a brush resource. Uses TryGetResource (the correct
 157 |     /// Avalonia API for reading from IResourceDictionary — indexer reading
 158 |     /// is NOT supported and throws).
 159 |     /// </summary>
 160 |     private static void UpdateBrush(Application app, string brushKey, string hex)
 161 |     {
 162 |         if (!Color.TryParse(hex, out var color)) return;
 163 | 
 164 |         // TryGetResource is the correct way to read from IResourceDictionary.
 165 |         // Avalonia 12 signature: TryGetResource(object key, ThemeVariant? theme, out object? value)
 166 |         if (app.Resources.TryGetResource(brushKey, null, out var existing) && existing is SolidColorBrush brush)
 167 |         {
 168 |             // Mutate in place — Color is observable, all bindings update.
 169 |             brush.Color = color;
 170 |         }
 171 |         else
 172 |         {
 173 |             // First time — create new brush.
 174 |             app.Resources[brushKey] = new SolidColorBrush(color);
 175 |         }
 176 |     }
 177 | 
 178 |     public static void ApplyFromSettings(Settings settings)
 179 |     {
 180 |         if (settings is null) return;
 181 |         ApplyTheme(
 182 |             settings.ThemeMode ?? "Dark",
 183 |             settings.AccentColor ?? "Indigo",
 184 |             settings.EnableAnimations);
 185 |     }
 186 | 
 187 |     private static string NormalizeMode(string? mode) =>
 188 |         string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(mode) ? "Dark" :
 189 |         string.Equals(mode, "Light", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) ? "Light" :
 190 |         string.Equals(mode, "System", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) ? "System" :
 191 |         "Dark";
 192 | 
 193 |     private static bool ResolveIsDark(string mode)
 194 |     {
 195 |         if (mode == "System")
 196 |         {
 197 |             try
 198 |             {
 199 |                 var actual = Application.Current?.ActualThemeVariant;
 200 |                 if (actual == ThemeVariant.Light) return false;
 201 |             }
 202 |             catch { }
 203 |             return true;
 204 |         }
 205 |         return mode == "Dark";
 206 |     }
 207 | }

--- src/MyGame.Desktop/Services/WorldBuilderStateStore.cs (C#, 138 lines, ~1,570 tokens) ---
   1 | using System;
   2 | using System.IO;
   3 | using System.Text.Json;
   4 | using System.Text.Json.Serialization;
   5 | using MyGame.Core.AI.Agents;
   6 | using MyGame.Core.Profile;
   7 | 
   8 | namespace MyGame.Desktop.Services;
   9 | 
  10 | /// <summary>
  11 | /// On-disk persistence for the <see cref="WorldBuilderState"/> snapshot
  12 | /// (issue #19). When the user cancels a world-build mid-flight, the
  13 | /// <see cref="ViewModels.WorldBuildViewModel"/> writes the orchestrator's
  14 | /// current state to <c>%APPDATA%/MyGame/worldbuilder-state.json</c> via
  15 | /// <see cref="Save"/>. On next app launch, the main menu reads it via
  16 | /// <see cref="Load"/> and offers to resume the build.
  17 | /// </summary>
  18 | public static class WorldBuilderStateStore
  19 | {
  20 |     /// <summary>
  21 |     /// Path to the on-disk state file. Lives in the profile directory
  22 |     /// alongside <c>profile.json</c> + <c>settings.json</c> so it
  23 |     /// follows the same per-user / per-machine conventions.
  24 |     /// </summary>
  25 |     public static string StateFilePath =>
  26 |         Path.Combine(ProfileStore.DefaultProfileDirectory, "worldbuilder-state.json");
  27 | 
  28 |     // Reuse a single JsonSerializerOptions instance across all
  29 |     // serializations. The default System.Text.Json policy round-trips
  30 |     // the WorldBuilderState record + the WorldPlan record (which uses
  31 |     // init-only properties + JsonPropertyName attributes) cleanly.
  32 |     // PropertyNameCaseInsensitive lets us read files written by older
  33 |     // versions that might have used a different casing policy.
  34 |     private static readonly JsonSerializerOptions s_options = new()
  35 |     {
  36 |         PropertyNamingPolicy = JsonNamingPolicy.CamelCase,
  37 |         PropertyNameCaseInsensitive = true,
  38 |         DefaultIgnoreCondition = JsonIgnoreCondition.WhenWritingNull,
  39 |         WriteIndented = true,
  40 |     };
  41 | 
  42 |     /// <summary>
  43 |     /// Persist the given <see cref="WorldBuilderState"/> + brief to disk
  44 |     /// atomically (write to <c>.tmp</c> then rename). Safe to call from
  45 |     /// any thread. Non-fatal on any I/O error — the caller catches and
  46 |     /// logs, since a failed state-save shouldn't break the cancel flow.
  47 |     /// </summary>
  48 |     /// <param name="state">The orchestrator's saved state.</param>
  49 |     /// <param name="brief">The original world brief (so a resumed run
  50 |     /// uses the same brief as the original). Stored alongside the state
  51 |     /// in a wrapper record.</param>
  52 |     public static void Save(WorldBuilderState state, string? brief)
  53 |     {
  54 |         if (state is null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(state));
  55 |         var wrapper = new WorldBuilderStateFile
  56 |         {
  57 |             State = state,
  58 |             Brief = brief ?? string.Empty,
  59 |             SavedAt = DateTimeOffset.UtcNow,
  60 |         };
  61 |         var dir = Path.GetDirectoryName(StateFilePath);
  62 |         if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(dir) && !Directory.Exists(dir))
  63 |             Directory.CreateDirectory(dir);
  64 | 
  65 |         var json = JsonSerializer.Serialize(wrapper, s_options);
  66 |         var tmp = StateFilePath + ".tmp";
  67 |         File.WriteAllText(tmp, json);
  68 |         // File.Move with overwrite=true is atomic on the same volume
  69 |         // (NTFS / ext4). This matches the SaveManager's atomic-write
  70 |         // pattern so a crash mid-write leaves either the old file or
  71 |         // the new file — never a half-written one.
  72 |         File.Move(tmp, StateFilePath, overwrite: true);
  73 |     }
  74 | 
  75 |     /// <summary>
  76 |     /// Load the saved state from disk. Returns null if the file doesn't
  77 |     /// exist or is unparseable (defensive — a corrupt state file
  78 |     /// shouldn't crash the main menu).
  79 |     /// </summary>
  80 |     public static WorldBuilderStateFile? Load()
  81 |     {
  82 |         if (!File.Exists(StateFilePath)) return null;
  83 |         try
  84 |         {
  85 |             var json = File.ReadAllText(StateFilePath);
  86 |             return JsonSerializer.Deserialize<WorldBuilderStateFile>(json, s_options);
  87 |         }
  88 |         catch (Exception ex)
  89 |         {
  90 |             System.Diagnostics.Trace.WriteLine(
  91 |                 $"[WorldBuilderStateStore] failed to load state file: {ex.Message}");
  92 |             return null;
  93 |         }
  94 |     }
  95 | 
  96 |     /// <summary>
  97 |     /// Delete the saved state file (called after a successful build so
  98 |     /// the next launch doesn't prompt to resume a finished build). Safe
  99 |     /// to call when the file doesn't exist.
 100 |     /// </summary>
 101 |     public static void Delete()
 102 |     {
 103 |         try { if (File.Exists(StateFilePath)) File.Delete(StateFilePath); }
 104 |         catch (Exception ex)
 105 |         {
 106 |             System.Diagnostics.Trace.WriteLine(
 107 |                 $"[WorldBuilderStateStore] failed to delete state file: {ex.Message}");
 108 |         }
 109 |     }
 110 | 
 111 |     /// <summary>
 112 |     /// True when a saved state file exists on disk. Used by the main
 113 |     /// menu to decide whether to show the «Незавершённая генерация мира.
 114 |     /// Продолжить?» prompt.
 115 |     /// </summary>
 116 |     public static bool Exists() => File.Exists(StateFilePath);
 117 | }
 118 | 
 119 | /// <summary>
 120 | /// On-disk wrapper for <see cref="WorldBuilderState"/>: carries the
 121 | /// state itself + the original brief + a timestamp so the resume UI can
 122 | /// show "saved N minutes ago".
 123 | /// </summary>
 124 | public sealed class WorldBuilderStateFile
 125 | {
 126 |     /// <summary>The orchestrator's saved progress.</summary>
 127 |     public WorldBuilderState? State { get; set; }
 128 | 
 129 |     /// <summary>
 130 |     /// The original world brief (so a resumed run uses the same brief
 131 |     /// as the original — the planner needs it).
 132 |     /// </summary>
 133 |     public string Brief { get; set; } = string.Empty;
 134 | 
 135 |     /// <summary>UTC timestamp when the state was saved.</summary>
 136 |     public DateTimeOffset SavedAt { get; set; }
 137 | }

--- src/MyGame.Desktop/ViewLocator.cs (C#, 101 lines, ~1,040 tokens) ---
   1 | using System;
   2 | using Avalonia.Controls;
   3 | using Avalonia.Controls.Templates;
   4 | using MyGame.Desktop.ViewModels;
   5 | 
   6 | namespace MyGame.Desktop;
   7 | 
   8 | /// <summary>
   9 | /// Standard Avalonia view locator: maps
  10 | /// <c>MyGame.Desktop.ViewModels.XxxViewModel</c> →
  11 | /// <c>MyGame.Desktop.Views.XxxView</c> by name. Registered as a
  12 | /// <c>DataTemplate</c> in <c>App.axaml</c> so any control bound to a
  13 | /// ViewModel gets its matching view rendered automatically.
  14 | ///
  15 | /// <para>
  16 | /// The locator uses a naming convention: a ViewModel named
  17 | /// <c>FooBarViewModel</c> resolves to <c>FooBarView</c>. If the view
  18 | /// type can't be found (e.g. the ViewModel is a built-in type or the
  19 | /// view hasn't been authored yet), a placeholder <c>TextBlock</c>
  20 | /// showing the ViewModel's type name is returned — keeps the UI from
  21 | /// crashing on missing views during development.
  22 | /// </para>
  23 | /// </summary>
  24 | public class ViewLocator : IDataTemplate
  25 | {
  26 |     private const string ViewModelSuffix = "ViewModel";
  27 |     private const string ViewSuffix = "View";
  28 |     private const string ViewModelNamespace = "MyGame.Desktop.ViewModels";
  29 |     private const string ViewNamespace = "MyGame.Desktop.Views";
  30 | 
  31 |     /// <summary>
  32 |     /// Supports any object — the data template is registered
  33 |     /// application-wide and Avalonia asks each DataContext whether the
  34 |     /// locator supports it. We answer true for anything in the
  35 |     /// ViewModels namespace.
  36 |     /// </summary>
  37 |     public bool SupportsRecycling => false;
  38 | 
  39 |     public bool Match(object? data)
  40 |     {
  41 |         return data is ViewModelBase
  42 |             || (data is not null
  43 |                 && data.GetType().Namespace?.StartsWith(ViewModelNamespace, StringComparison.Ordinal) == true);
  44 |     }
  45 | 
  46 |     /// <summary>
  47 |     /// Build the view for the given ViewModel. Uses
  48 |     /// <see cref="Activator.CreateInstance(Type)"/> so views don't need
  49 |     /// a parameterless constructor registered anywhere — the type just
  50 |     /// has to exist.
  51 |     /// </summary>
  52 |     public Control? Build(object? data)
  53 |     {
  54 |         if (data is null) return null;
  55 | 
  56 |         var name = data.GetType().FullName;
  57 |         if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(name)) return Fallback(data);
  58 | 
  59 |         // Replace the namespace and the suffix.
  60 |         if (!name.EndsWith(ViewModelSuffix, StringComparison.Ordinal)) return Fallback(data);
  61 |         var viewName = name.Substring(0, name.Length - ViewModelSuffix.Length) + ViewSuffix;
  62 |         viewName = viewName.Replace(ViewModelNamespace, ViewNamespace, StringComparison.Ordinal);
  63 | 
  64 |         var type = Type.GetType(viewName);
  65 |         if (type is null)
  66 |         {
  67 |             // Try loading via the same assembly (the FullName above is
  68 |             // already assembly-qualified-less, so the runtime won't
  69 |             // search other assemblies). Walk the loaded assemblies in
  70 |             // this AppDomain as a fallback.
  71 |             foreach (var asm in AppDomain.CurrentDomain.GetAssemblies())
  72 |             {
  73 |                 var t = asm.GetType(viewName);
  74 |                 if (t is not null) { type = t; break; }
  75 |             }
  76 |         }
  77 | 
  78 |         if (type is null) return Fallback(data);
  79 | 
  80 |         try
  81 |         {
  82 |             return (Control?)Activator.CreateInstance(type);
  83 |         }
  84 |         catch
  85 |         {
  86 |             return Fallback(data);
  87 |         }
  88 |     }
  89 | 
  90 |     /// <summary>
  91 |     /// Build a placeholder TextBlock so the UI doesn't crash on a
  92 |     /// missing/broken view. Useful during early development when not
  93 |     /// every screen has its XAML yet.
  94 |     /// </summary>
  95 |     private static Control Fallback(object data) => new TextBlock
  96 |     {
  97 |         Text = $"(no view for {data.GetType().Name})",
  98 |         Margin = new Avalonia.Thickness(8),
  99 |     };
 100 | }

--- src/MyGame.Desktop/ViewModels/CharacterCreationViewModel.cs (C#, 260 lines, ~3,018 tokens) ---
   1 | using System;
   2 | using System.Collections.Generic;
   3 | using System.Linq;
   4 | using System.Threading.Tasks;
   5 | using CommunityToolkit.Mvvm.ComponentModel;
   6 | using CommunityToolkit.Mvvm.Input;
   7 | using MyGame.Core.Common;
   8 | using MyGame.Core.Saves;
   9 | using MyGame.Core.World;
  10 | using MyGame.Core.World.Entities;
  11 | 
  12 | namespace MyGame.Desktop.ViewModels;
  13 | 
  14 | /// <summary>
  15 | /// Character-creation screen. Runs between world-build and game entry.
  16 | /// Lets the player pick a name / race / class / background, then
  17 | /// materializes a fresh <see cref="Player"/> (replacing the auto-created
  18 | /// «Странник» that the world-builder seeded), grants class-appropriate
  19 | /// starter gear, persists, and navigates into the game in single-player
  20 | /// mode.
  21 | /// </summary>
  22 | /// <remarks>
  23 | /// Flow: <c>WorldBuildViewModel</c> → on success creates a save →
  24 | /// <c>MainViewModel.NavigateToCharacterCreation(saveId)</c> → this VM
  25 | /// → on <c>Create</c> loads the save, swaps the player, saves again,
  26 | /// <c>_shell.NavigateToGame(saveId)</c>.
  27 | /// </remarks>
  28 | public partial class CharacterCreationViewModel : ViewModelBase
  29 | {
  30 |     private readonly SaveManager _saveManager;
  31 |     private readonly MainViewModel _shell;
  32 |     private readonly string _saveId;
  33 | 
  34 |     public CharacterCreationViewModel(
  35 |         SaveManager saveManager,
  36 |         MainViewModel shell,
  37 |         string saveId)
  38 |     {
  39 |         _saveManager = saveManager ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(saveManager));
  40 |         _shell = shell ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(shell));
  41 |         _saveId = saveId ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(saveId));
  42 |         Title = "Создание персонажа";
  43 |     }
  44 | 
  45 |     // ─── Observable properties ───────────────────────────────────────
  46 | 
  47 |     /// <summary>Character name. Defaults to «Странник» (matches the
  48 |     /// auto-created player the world-builder seeds).</summary>
  49 |     [ObservableProperty] private string _name = "Странник";
  50 | 
  51 |     /// <summary>Selected race (key into <see cref="RaceOptions"/>).</summary>
  52 |     [ObservableProperty] private string _race = "human";
  53 | 
  54 |     /// <summary>Selected class (key into <see cref="ClassOptions"/>).</summary>
  55 |     [ObservableProperty] private string _class = "fighter";
  56 | 
  57 |     /// <summary>Selected background (key into <see cref="BackgroundOptions"/>).</summary>
  58 |     [ObservableProperty] private string _background = "soldier";
  59 | 
  60 |     // ─── Dropdown options ────────────────────────────────────────────
  61 | 
  62 |     public string[] RaceOptions { get; } =
  63 |         { "human", "elf", "dwarf", "halfling", "orc", "tiefling" };
  64 | 
  65 |     public string[] ClassOptions { get; } =
  66 |         { "fighter", "wizard", "rogue", "cleric", "ranger", "barbarian" };
  67 | 
  68 |     public string[] BackgroundOptions { get; } =
  69 |         { "soldier", "sage", "criminal", "acolyte", "folk-hero", "urchin" };
  70 | 
  71 |     // ─── Class preview (live, derived from selected class) ───────────
  72 | 
  73 |     /// <summary>
  74 |     /// Human-readable summary of the selected class's starting attributes
  75 |     /// + starter gear + proficient skills. Updates whenever the user
  76 |     /// changes the class dropdown (CommunityToolkit source-generator
  77 |     /// wires the property-changed notification via OnClassChanged partial).
  78 |     /// </summary>
  79 |     public string PreviewText => BuildPreviewText(Class);
  80 | 
  81 |     partial void OnClassChanged(string value)
  82 |     {
  83 |         OnPropertyChanged(nameof(PreviewText));
  84 |     }
  85 | 
  86 |     private static string BuildPreviewText(string cls)
  87 |     {
  88 |         var (attrs, gear, skills) = GetClassProfile(cls);
  89 |         var attrLine = string.Join(", ",
  90 |             attrs.OrderBy(kv => kv.Key).Select(kv => $"{kv.Key.ToUpperInvariant()}={kv.Value}"));
  91 |         var gearLine = gear.Length > 0 ? string.Join(", ", gear) : "—";
  92 |         var skillsLine = skills.Length > 0 ? string.Join(", ", skills) : "—";
  93 |         return $"Характеристики: {attrLine}\nСнаряжение: {gearLine}\nВладения: {skillsLine}";
  94 |     }
  95 | 
  96 |     // ─── Commands ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  97 | 
  98 |     /// <summary>
  99 |     /// Validate the name, load the save, swap the auto-player for the
 100 |     /// chosen one (attributes + gear + skills based on class), save, and
 101 |     /// navigate into the game in single-player mode.
 102 |     /// </summary>
 103 |     [RelayCommand(CanExecute = nameof(CanCreate))]
 104 |     private async Task CreateAsync()
 105 |     {
 106 |         if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(Name))
 107 |         {
 108 |             ErrorMessage = "Введите имя персонажа.";
 109 |             return;
 110 |         }
 111 | 
 112 |         IsBusy = true;
 113 |         ErrorMessage = null;
 114 |         try
 115 |         {
 116 |             var loaded = _saveManager.LoadAll(_saveId);
 117 |             if (loaded is null)
 118 |             {
 119 |                 ErrorMessage = $"Сохранение {_saveId} не найдено.";
 120 |                 return;
 121 |             }
 122 |             var (world, meta, log) = loaded.Value;
 123 | 
 124 |             // Remember where the auto-player was standing so the new
 125 |             // character starts at the same spot.
 126 |             var oldPlayer = world.ActivePlayer ?? world.Players.FirstOrDefault();
 127 |             var startLocationId = oldPlayer?.LocationId
 128 |                 ?? world.Locations.FirstOrDefault()?.Id
 129 |                 ?? EntityId.Empty;
 130 | 
 131 |             // Remove the auto-player. SpawnPlayer set ActivePlayerId on
 132 |             // the first spawn, so we clear both the Players list and
 133 |             // the active id before re-spawning.
 134 |             world.Players.Clear();
 135 |             world.ActivePlayerId = null;
 136 | 
 137 |             // Build the chosen class profile (attributes + starter gear
 138 |             // template ids + proficient skills).
 139 |             var (attrs, gearTplIds, skills) = GetClassProfile(Class);
 140 | 
 141 |             var player = EntityFactory.CreatePlayer(new()
 142 |             {
 143 |                 Name = Name.Trim(),
 144 |                 Race = Race,
 145 |                 Class = Class,
 146 |                 Level = 1,
 147 |                 LocationId = startLocationId,
 148 |                 Background = Background,
 149 |                 StartingCurrency = 25,
 150 |                 Attributes = attrs,
 151 |                 ProficientSkills = skills,
 152 |             }, world.Ruleset);
 153 | 
 154 |             // Equip starter weapon/armor from the world's content
 155 |             // registry. Each class gets a different loadout; armor is
 156 |             // only granted to melee classes (fighter/rogue/cleric/
 157 |             // barbarian).
 158 |             foreach (var tplId in gearTplIds)
 159 |             {
 160 |                 var tpl = world.Registries.Items.Get(tplId);
 161 |                 if (tpl is null) continue;
 162 |                 var inst = EntityFactory.InstantiateItem(tpl);
 163 |                 // Slot from category: weapon → "weapon", armor → "armor".
 164 |                 string slot = tpl.Weapon is not null ? "weapon"
 165 |                     : tpl.Armor is not null ? "armor"
 166 |                     : "misc";
 167 |                 player.Equipped[slot] = inst;
 168 |             }
 169 | 
 170 |             // Everyone gets a basic survival kit: 2 health potions, a
 171 |             // torch, and 3 rations. Matches the auto-player defaults so
 172 |             // the player isn't worse off than «Странник» was.
 173 |             foreach (var (tplId, qty) in new[]
 174 |                 { ("cns_health_potion", 2), ("tool_torch", 1), ("cns_ration", 3) })
 175 |             {
 176 |                 var t = world.Registries.Items.Get(tplId);
 177 |                 if (t is not null)
 178 |                     player.Inventory.Items.Add(EntityFactory.InstantiateItem(t, qty));
 179 |             }
 180 | 
 181 |             // Recompute AC after equipping (D&D-style worlds).
 182 |             EntityFactory.RecomputeAcResource(player, world.Ruleset);
 183 | 
 184 |             world.SpawnPlayer(player);
 185 | 
 186 |             // Persist the swapped world so the game screen loads the
 187 |             // chosen character (not «Странник»).
 188 |             _saveManager.SaveAll(_saveId, world, meta, log);
 189 | 
 190 |             // Hand off to the game screen.
 191 |             await _shell.NavigateToGame(_saveId);
 192 |         }
 193 |         catch (Exception ex)
 194 |         {
 195 |             ErrorMessage = $"Не удалось создать персонажа: {ex.Message}";
 196 |         }
 197 |         finally
 198 |         {
 199 |             IsBusy = false;
 200 |         }
 201 |     }
 202 | 
 203 |     private bool CanCreate() => !IsBusy && !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(Name);
 204 | 
 205 |     /// <summary>
 206 |     /// Discard the save (the auto-player «Странник» remains in place;
 207 |     /// the user can still load it later from the main menu) and go back
 208 |     /// to the main menu.
 209 |     /// </summary>
 210 |     [RelayCommand]
 211 |     private void Back() => _shell.NavigateToMenu();
 212 | 
 213 |     // ─── Class profile lookup ────────────────────────────────────────
 214 | 
 215 |     /// <summary>
 216 |     /// Hardcoded class → (attributes, starter-gear-template-ids, skills)
 217 |     /// lookup. Attributes use the DefaultDnd ruleset keys (str/dex/con/
 218 |     /// int/per/cha). Gear uses standard template ids from data.json
 219 |     /// (wpn_shortsword, arm_leather, wpn_dagger, wpn_staff, wpn_club,
 220 |     /// wpn_shortbow, wpn_greataxe).
 221 |     /// </summary>
 222 |     private static (
 223 |         Dictionary<string, int> attrs,
 224 |         string[] gear,
 225 |         string[] skills) GetClassProfile(string cls) => cls switch
 226 |     {
 227 |         "fighter" => (
 228 |             new() { ["str"] = 16, ["dex"] = 12, ["con"] = 14, ["int"] = 10, ["per"] = 10, ["cha"] = 10 },
 229 |             new[] { "wpn_shortsword", "arm_leather" },
 230 |             new[] { "Athletics", "Intimidation" }),
 231 |         "wizard" => (
 232 |             new() { ["str"] = 8, ["dex"] = 12, ["con"] = 12, ["int"] = 16, ["per"] = 12, ["cha"] = 10 },
 233 |             new[] { "wpn_staff" },
 234 |             new[] { "Arcana", "Investigation" }),
 235 |         "rogue" => (
 236 |             new() { ["str"] = 10, ["dex"] = 16, ["con"] = 12, ["int"] = 12, ["per"] = 14, ["cha"] = 10 },
 237 |             new[] { "wpn_dagger", "arm_leather" },
 238 |             new[] { "Stealth", "Sleight of Hand" }),
 239 |         "cleric" => (
 240 |             new() { ["str"] = 12, ["dex"] = 10, ["con"] = 14, ["int"] = 10, ["per"] = 15, ["cha"] = 12 },
 241 |             new[] { "wpn_club", "arm_leather" },
 242 |             new[] { "Religion", "Medicine" }),
 243 |         "ranger" => (
 244 |             new() { ["str"] = 12, ["dex"] = 15, ["con"] = 12, ["int"] = 10, ["per"] = 14, ["cha"] = 10 },
 245 |             new[] { "wpn_shortbow" },
 246 |             new[] { "Survival", "Animal Handling" }),
 247 |         "barbarian" => (
 248 |             new() { ["str"] = 16, ["dex"] = 12, ["con"] = 16, ["int"] = 8, ["per"] = 10, ["cha"] = 10 },
 249 |             new[] { "wpn_greataxe", "arm_leather" },
 250 |             new[] { "Athletics", "Survival" }),
 251 |         _ => (
 252 |             new() { ["str"] = 12, ["dex"] = 12, ["con"] = 12, ["int"] = 12, ["per"] = 12, ["cha"] = 12 },
 253 |             Array.Empty<string>(),
 254 |             Array.Empty<string>()),
 255 |     };
 256 | 
 257 |     // Keep the create-command enabled state in sync as Name/IsBusy change.
 258 |     partial void OnNameChanged(string value) => CreateCommand.NotifyCanExecuteChanged();
 259 | }

--- src/MyGame.Desktop/ViewModels/GameViewModel.cs (C#, 2658 lines, ~31,505 tokens) ---
   1 | using System;
   2 | using System.Collections.Generic;
   3 | using System.Collections.ObjectModel;
   4 | using System.Linq;
   5 | using System.Threading;
   6 | using System.Threading.Tasks;
   7 | using Avalonia.Threading;
   8 | using CommunityToolkit.Mvvm.ComponentModel;
   9 | using CommunityToolkit.Mvvm.Input;
  10 | using MyGame.Core.AI;
  11 | using MyGame.Core.AI.Agents;
  12 | using MyGame.Core.AI.Prompts;
  13 | using MyGame.Core.AI.Tools;
  14 | using MyGame.Core.Common;
  15 | using MyGame.Core.Engine;
  16 | using MyGame.Core.Multiplayer;
  17 | using MyGame.Core.Multiplayer.Protocol;
  18 | using MyGame.Core.Profile;
  19 | using MyGame.Core.Saves;
  20 | using MyGame.Core.World;
  21 | using MyGame.Core.World.Content;
  22 | using MyGame.Core.World.Entities;
  23 | using MyGame.Desktop.Services;
  24 | using MyGame.Desktop.ViewModels.Panels;
  25 | 
  26 | namespace MyGame.Desktop.ViewModels;
  27 | 
  28 | /// <summary>
  29 | /// The main play screen. Three modes (mutually exclusive):
  30 | ///
  31 | /// <list type="bullet">
  32 | ///   <item><b>Single-player</b> (<see cref="StandaloneSinglePlayer"/>=true):
  33 | ///     a save was loaded; the GM runs locally; no HostSession/ClientSession.
  34 | ///     SubmitAction runs the GM directly and saves state.</item>
  35 | ///   <item><b>Host</b> (<see cref="IsHost"/>=true): a HostSession was
  36 | ///     started by the HostGame screen. SubmitAction enqueues into the
  37 | ///     session's ActionQueue and runs ProcessNextTurnAsync. State saves
  38 | ///     via the session's SaveManager.</item>
  39 | ///   <item><b>Client</b> (<see cref="IsHost"/>=false AND not standalone):
  40 | ///     a ClientSession was connected by the JoinGame screen.
  41 | ///     SubmitAction sends ActionQueuedMsg to the host. State updates +
  42 | ///     narrative arrive as events.</item>
  43 | /// </list>
  44 | ///
  45 | /// <para>
  46 | /// All Core event handlers marshal to the UI thread via
  47 | /// <see cref="Dispatcher.UIThread.Post"/> — the HostSession/ClientSession
  48 | /// raise events on ThreadPool threads (network read loops, GM task
  49 | /// continuation), but UI bindings only refresh safely on the UI thread.
  50 | /// </para>
  51 | /// </summary>
  52 | public partial class GameViewModel : ViewModelBase
  53 | {
  54 |     private readonly ProfileStore _profileStore;
  55 |     private readonly SettingsStore _settingsStore;
  56 |     private readonly SaveManager _saveManager;
  57 |     private readonly MainViewModel _shell;
  58 | 
  59 |     // ─── Mode flags ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
  60 |     public bool IsHost { get; }
  61 |     public bool StandaloneSinglePlayer { get; }
  62 |     public bool IsMultiplayer => !StandaloneSinglePlayer;
  63 |     public bool IsClient => !StandaloneSinglePlayer && !IsHost;
  64 | 
  65 |     /// <summary>
  66 |     /// True when this screen is in the multiplayer lobby phase (issue #77):
  67 |     /// the host has started a session but hasn't transitioned to Playing
  68 |     /// yet, OR the client has connected but the host hasn't started the
  69 |     /// game yet. Drives the lobby layout in the center column (lobby
  70 |     /// panel with members list + ready toggle + start-game button) vs
  71 |     /// the normal game layout (narrative log + action input).
  72 |     /// </summary>
  73 |     /// <remarks>
  74 |     /// Refreshed on every relevant lifecycle event:
  75 |     /// <list type="bullet">
  76 |     ///   <item>Host: <see cref="InitHost"/> (initial status from
  77 |     ///     HostSession.Status) + HostSession.StatusChanged (host
  78 |     ///     transitioned Lobby → Playing via StartGameCommand).</item>
  79 |     ///   <item>Client: <see cref="InitClientAsync"/> (initial status
  80 |     ///     from ClientSession.Status) + ClientSession.StatusChanged
  81 |     ///     (host transitioned).</item>
  82 |     /// </list>
  83 |     /// Always false in single-player mode (no lobby phase).
  84 |     /// </remarks>
  85 |     [ObservableProperty] private bool _isLobby;
  86 | 
  87 |     // ─── Sessions (one of these is non-null depending on mode) ───────
  88 |     public HostSession? HostSession { get; private set; }
  89 |     public ClientSession? ClientSession { get; private set; }
  90 |     private CancellationTokenSource? _hostShutdownCts;
  91 | 
  92 |     // ─── Single-player runtime state ─────────────────────────────────
  93 |     private GameMaster? _gm;
  94 |     private MyGame.Core.Tooling.ReplayRecorder? _replayRecorder;
  95 |     private World? _world;
  96 |     private SaveMeta? _meta;
  97 |     private string? _saveId;
  98 |     private readonly List<LogEntry> _log = new();
  99 |     private readonly object _logLock = new();
 100 | 
 101 |     /// <summary>
 102 |     /// Player level as of the previous turn. Used to detect level-ups after
 103 |     /// a GM turn resolves (single-player + host modes). Null until the first
 104 |     /// refresh, at which point it's seeded with the current player level
 105 |     /// (so the first turn never reports a false level-up).
 106 |     /// </summary>
 107 |     private int? _previousLevel;
 108 | 
 109 |     // ─── Chat panel state ────────────────────────────────────────────
 110 |     private readonly ObservableCollection<ChatLine> _chat = new();
 111 | 
 112 |     public GameViewModel(
 113 |         ProfileStore profileStore,
 114 |         SettingsStore settingsStore,
 115 |         SaveManager saveManager,
 116 |         MainViewModel shell,
 117 |         bool isHost,
 118 |         bool standaloneSinglePlayer,
 119 |         string? saveId,
 120 |         string? host,
 121 |         int port)
 122 |     {
 123 |         _profileStore = profileStore ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(profileStore));
 124 |         _settingsStore = settingsStore ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(settingsStore));
 125 |         _saveManager = saveManager ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(saveManager));
 126 |         _shell = shell ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(shell));
 127 | 
 128 |         IsHost = isHost;
 129 |         StandaloneSinglePlayer = standaloneSinglePlayer;
 130 |         _saveId = saveId;
 131 | 
 132 |         // Title shows the mode so the user knows what they're playing.
 133 |         Title = standaloneSinglePlayer
 134 |             ? "Одиночная игра"
 135 |             : (isHost ? "Игра (хост)" : "Игра (клиент)");
 136 | 
 137 |         // Wire inventory panel actions → world mutations. The panel raises
 138 |         // an event; this VM decides how to apply it (single-player mutates
 139 |         // the world directly; multiplayer routes through the GM tool flow).
 140 |         InventoryPanel.ItemActionRequested += OnInventoryAction;
 141 | 
 142 |         // Wire world-panel travel → world mutations. Each exit row is a
 143 |         // Button bound to WorldPanel.TravelCommand; the panel forwards the
 144 |         // clicked ExitRow here, and we move the player, mark the
 145 |         // destination visited/discovered, log the trip, persist, and
 146 |         // (if the destination is dangerous) roll a random encounter.
 147 |         WorldPanel.TravelRequested += OnTravel;
 148 | 
 149 |         // Wire character-panel export → CharacterSheetStore.Export (issue
 150 |         // #62). The panel raises ExportRequested (no payload — the panel
 151 |         // doesn't know the save id); this VM resolves the save id + active
 152 |         // player id, calls Export, and writes the result path / error back
 153 |         // to the panel's ExportStatus property so the user sees inline
 154 |         // feedback under the «Экспорт» button.
 155 |         CharacterPanel.ExportRequested += OnCharacterExportRequested;
 156 | 
 157 |         // Wire quest-panel reward claiming → world mutations (issue #70).
 158 |         // The QuestPanel raises ClaimRewardsRequested(questId) when the
 159 |         // user clicks «Получить награду» on a completed quest whose
 160 |         // rewards are staged in Quest.UnclaimedRewards. We grant the
 161 |         // currency / XP / items, clear the field, log a summary entry,
 162 |         // refresh the panels, and persist.
 163 |         QuestPanel.ClaimRewardsRequested += OnQuestClaimRewards;
 164 |     }
 165 | 
 166 |     // ─── Observable props ────────────────────────────────────────────
 167 | 
 168 |     [ObservableProperty] private string _currentAction = string.Empty;
 169 |     [ObservableProperty] private string? _chatInput;
 170 |     [ObservableProperty] private bool _isWaiting;
 171 |     [ObservableProperty] private string? _statusText;
 172 |     [ObservableProperty] private string _clockDisplay = "День 1, 08:00";
 173 |     [ObservableProperty] private string _characterSummary = string.Empty;
 174 |     [ObservableProperty] private string _worldInfo = string.Empty;
 175 |     [ObservableProperty] private string _gameName = "Игра";
 176 |     [ObservableProperty] private string _shareAddress = string.Empty;
 177 |     [ObservableProperty] private bool _canSave;
 178 |     [ObservableProperty] private bool _canSubmit = true;
 179 | 
 180 |     // ─── Combat + death UI state (issues #88, #63) ──────────────────────
 181 |     //
 182 |     // CombatDisplay: a one-line combat indicator for the top bar — null
 183 |     // when no combat is active. Refreshed in RefreshFromWorld.
 184 |     // IsPlayerDead: drives the death overlay. True when the active
 185 |     // player's IsAlive flag is false; set in RefreshFromWorld. The
 186 |     // overlay's "reload last save" and "leave to menu" buttons are
 187 |     // bound to the dedicated relay commands below.
 188 |     [ObservableProperty] private string? _combatDisplay;
 189 |     [ObservableProperty] private bool _isPlayerDead;
 190 | 
 191 |     // ─── Reconnect overlay state (issue #7) ────────────────────────────
 192 |     //
 193 |     // IsDisconnected: drives the reconnect overlay. True when the
 194 |     // ClientSession's Disconnected event fires with Intentional=false
 195 |     // (network drop). The overlay offers "Переподключиться" (calls
 196 |     // ReconnectCommand, retries 3x with 2s backoff) and "Выйти в меню"
 197 |     // (calls LeaveToMenuCommand). On reconnect success the Welcomed
 198 |     // event fires and refreshes the members list + party status.
 199 |     //
 200 |     // ReconnectFailed: true after 3 reconnect attempts have all failed.
 201 |     // Switches the overlay to a simpler "Не удалось переподключиться"
 202 |     // message with only the "Выйти в меню" button. Reset to false on a
 203 |     // successful reconnect (so a subsequent drop starts fresh).
 204 |     //
 205 |     // DisconnectReason: the close reason from the Disconnected event,
 206 |     // shown in the overlay body. Empty string when no disconnect has
 207 |     // occurred.
 208 |     [ObservableProperty] private bool _isDisconnected;
 209 |     [ObservableProperty] private bool _reconnectFailed;
 210 |     [ObservableProperty] private string _disconnectReason = string.Empty;
 211 |     [ObservableProperty] private bool _hasSavedWorldLocally;
 212 | 
 213 |     // ─── Token billing (issue #6) ─────────────────────────────────────
 214 |     //
 215 |     // Per-turn + per-session token counters. Accumulate after each GM
 216 |     // turn (single-player + host). Persist on SaveMeta so the session
 217 |     // total survives reload. The "last turn" counter resets each turn;
 218 |     // the "session" counter accumulates until the save is reset.
 219 |     [ObservableProperty] private int _sessionPromptTokens;
 220 |     [ObservableProperty] private int _sessionCompletionTokens;
 221 |     [ObservableProperty] private int _sessionTotalTokens;
 222 |     [ObservableProperty] private int _lastTurnTokens;
 223 | 
 224 |     // ─── Action-queue batching (issue #12) ──────────────────────────
 225 |     //
 226 |     // BatchCountdown: seconds remaining in the host's batching window
 227 |     // (5 → 4 → 3 → 2 → 1 → 0). 0 means no batching in progress. The
 228 |     // host sets this via the HostSession.BatchCountdownChanged event
 229 |     // when the batching window starts ticking. The pending-actions
 230 |     // list (right column) already shows queued actions; this countdown
 231 |     // gives the user a sense of when the next turn will fire.
 232 |     //
 233 |     // For single-player-host (1 ready member), the batching window
 234 |     // fires immediately — no countdown is shown.
 235 |     [ObservableProperty] private int _batchCountdown;
 236 | 
 237 |     /// <summary>
 238 |     /// Live-streaming narrative buffer. Bound to a TextBlock at the end
 239 |     /// of the log so the user sees the GM's narration appear word-by-word
 240 |     /// as the SSE stream delivers content deltas. Cleared on turn start
 241 |     /// and after the final NarrativeResult is appended to the Log.
 242 |     /// </summary>
 243 |     [ObservableProperty] private string _streamingNarrativeText = string.Empty;
 244 | 
 245 |     /// <summary>
 246 |     /// Throttled streaming-narrative buffer. Deltas are appended here as
 247 |     /// they arrive; the property <see cref="StreamingNarrativeText"/> is
 248 |     /// only refreshed when at least 50ms have passed since the last
 249 |     /// flush, to avoid flooding the UI with PropertyChanged events.
 250 |     /// </summary>
 251 |     private readonly System.Text.StringBuilder _streamBuffer = new();
 252 |     private long _lastStreamFlushTicks;
 253 | 
 254 |     /// <summary>
 255 |     /// Session-tokens display string for the top-bar widget. Format:
 256 |     /// "Сессия: 12.3k токенов" (k-suffix for ≥1000). Empty when 0.
 257 |     /// </summary>
 258 |     public string SessionTokensDisplay => FormatTokenCount("Сессия", SessionTotalTokens);
 259 | 
 260 |     /// <summary>
 261 |     /// Last-turn tokens display string for the top-bar widget. Format:
 262 |     /// "Ход: 456" (plain int, no k-suffix — single turns are small).
 263 |     /// </summary>
 264 |     public string LastTurnTokensDisplay => LastTurnTokens > 0
 265 |         ? $"Ход: {LastTurnTokens}"
 266 |         : string.Empty;
 267 | 
 268 |     /// <summary>
 269 |     /// Format a token count as "Label: N токенов" (or "Label: N.Nk токенов"
 270 |     /// for ≥1000). Returns empty string when count is 0 (so the TextBlock
 271 |     /// can collapse cleanly when no tokens have been billed yet).
 272 |     /// </summary>
 273 |     private static string FormatTokenCount(string label, int count)
 274 |     {
 275 |         if (count <= 0) return string.Empty;
 276 |         if (count < 1000) return $"{label}: {count} токенов";
 277 |         // 1234 → "1.2k", 12345 → "12.3k", 123456 → "123k"
 278 |         double k = count / 1000.0;
 279 |         return $"{label}: {k:F1}k токенов";
 280 |     }
 281 | 
 282 |     /// <summary>
 283 |     /// Refresh the token-display properties after a turn. Called on the
 284 |     /// UI thread (the OnPropertyChanged notifications fire here).
 285 |     /// </summary>
 286 |     private void RefreshTokenDisplays()
 287 |     {
 288 |         // Touch each property to fire PropertyChanged so the bound
 289 |         // TextBlocks re-read the computed display strings.
 290 |         OnPropertyChanged(nameof(SessionTokensDisplay));
 291 |         OnPropertyChanged(nameof(LastTurnTokensDisplay));
 292 |     }
 293 | 
 294 |     /// <summary>
 295 |     /// Accumulate token usage from one GM turn into the session totals
 296 |     /// and the last-turn counter. Also refreshes the display properties.
 297 |     /// Safe to call from any thread — but PropertyChanged fires here, so
 298 |     /// callers should be on the UI thread (or marshal).
 299 |     /// </summary>
 300 |     private void AccumulateTokens(int promptTokens, int completionTokens, int totalTokens)
 301 |     {
 302 |         SessionPromptTokens += promptTokens;
 303 |         SessionCompletionTokens += completionTokens;
 304 |         SessionTotalTokens += totalTokens;
 305 |         LastTurnTokens = totalTokens;
 306 |         RefreshTokenDisplays();
 307 |     }
 308 | 
 309 |     /// <summary>
 310 |     /// Write the current session-tokens into <see cref="_meta"/>. Called
 311 |     /// before every save so the totals persist across reloads. Also copies
 312 |     /// the GM's conversation-history summary (issue #25) so the GM doesn't
 313 |     /// lose context across save/load. No-op when <see cref="_meta"/> is null.
 314 |     /// </summary>
 315 |     private void PersistTokensToMeta()
 316 |     {
 317 |         if (_meta is null) return;
 318 |         _meta = _meta with
 319 |         {
 320 |             SessionPromptTokens = SessionPromptTokens,
 321 |             SessionCompletionTokens = SessionCompletionTokens,
 322 |             // Persist the conversation-history summary (issue #25). Null
 323 |             // when no summarization has happened yet — saves load with
 324 |             // null and the GM will start fresh summarization when needed.
 325 |             HistorySummary = _gm?.HistorySummary ?? _meta.HistorySummary,
 326 |         };
 327 |     }
 328 | 
 329 |     /// <summary>
 330 |     /// Restore session-tokens from <see cref="_meta"/> into the VM's
 331 |     /// observable properties. Called after a save load so the counter
 332 |     /// survives reload. No-op when <see cref="_meta"/> is null.
 333 |     /// </summary>
 334 |     private void RestoreTokensFromMeta()
 335 |     {
 336 |         if (_meta is null) return;
 337 |         SessionPromptTokens = _meta.SessionPromptTokens;
 338 |         SessionCompletionTokens = _meta.SessionCompletionTokens;
 339 |         SessionTotalTokens = _meta.SessionPromptTokens + _meta.SessionCompletionTokens;
 340 |         LastTurnTokens = 0; // last-turn counter resets on reload
 341 |         RefreshTokenDisplays();
 342 |     }
 343 | 
 344 |     /// <summary>Observable narrative + action + tool log.</summary>
 345 |     public ObservableCollection<LogEntry> Log { get; } = new();
 346 | 
 347 |     /// <summary>Observable lobby/chat roster (multiplayer).</summary>
 348 |     public ObservableCollection<MemberInfo> Members { get; } = new();
 349 | 
 350 |     /// <summary>Observable chat panel (lobby + in-game chat).</summary>
 351 |     public ObservableCollection<ChatLine> Chat => _chat;
 352 | 
 353 |     /// <summary>Observable pending-action queue (multiplayer only).</summary>
 354 |     public ObservableCollection<PlayerAction> PendingActions { get; } = new();
 355 | 
 356 |     // ─── Side-panel VMs (character / inventory / quests / world) ─────
 357 |     // Each panel VM is owned by the GameViewModel. RefreshFromWorld()
 358 |     // pushes the live World into all four so they stay in sync after
 359 |     // every GM turn / state update. The View binds a TabControl to these.
 360 |     public CharacterPanelViewModel CharacterPanel { get; } = new();
 361 |     public InventoryPanelViewModel InventoryPanel { get; } = new();
 362 |     public QuestPanelViewModel QuestPanel { get; } = new();
 363 |     public WorldPanelViewModel WorldPanel { get; } = new();
 364 | 
 365 |     // ─── Initialization ──────────────────────────────────────────────
 366 | 
 367 |     /// <summary>
 368 |     /// Async init: pulls the started session from the transfer channel
 369 |     /// (multiplayer modes), wires event subscriptions, and loads the
 370 |     /// save's world (single-player/host modes).
 371 |     /// </summary>
 372 |     public async Task InitializeAsync()
 373 |     {
 374 |         if (StandaloneSinglePlayer)
 375 |         {
 376 |             await InitSinglePlayerAsync();
 377 |         }
