2055 |                     AppendLog(LogEntry.System($"Регион «{regionName}» уже был готов — обновляем выходы."));
2056 |                 }
2057 |                 else
2058 |                 {
2059 |                     AppendLog(LogEntry.System(
2060 |                         $"Регион «{regionName}» сгенерирован: +{result.LocationsAdded} лок., " +
2061 |                         $"+{result.NpcsAdded} NPC, +{result.BuildingsAdded} зд."));
2062 |                     foreach (var err in result.Errors)
2063 |                         AppendLog(LogEntry.System($"  ⚠ {err}"));
2064 |                 }
2065 | 
2066 |                 // Remove the phantom exit that triggered the generation
2067 |                 // (if any) — the new real exits are now in place from
2068 |                 // the committer's CommitLocations pass.
2069 |                 var player = _world.ActivePlayer ?? _world.Players.FirstOrDefault();
2070 |                 if (player is not null)
2071 |                 {
2072 |                     var loc = _world.GetLocation(player.LocationId);
2073 |                     if (loc is not null)
2074 |                     {
2075 |                         var phantoms = loc.Exits
2076 |                             .Where(e => e.To == EntityId.Empty
2077 |                                 && string.Equals(e.ToName, regionName, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
2078 |                             .ToList();
2079 |                         foreach (var p in phantoms) loc.Exits.Remove(p);
2080 |                     }
2081 |                 }
2082 | 
2083 |                 RefreshFromWorld();
2084 | 
2085 |                 // Persist the updated world (with the new locations +
2086 |                 // readyRegions flag).
2087 |                 _meta = freshMeta; // keep the OriginalPlanJson
2088 |                 PersistTokensToMeta();
2089 |                 try { _saveManager.SaveAll(_saveId, _world, _meta, Log.ToArray()); }
2090 |                 catch (Exception ex) { System.Diagnostics.Trace.WriteLine($"[save] {ex.Message}"); }
2091 |             }
2092 |             else
2093 |             {
2094 |                 AppendLog(LogEntry.System(
2095 |                     $"⚠ Не удалось сгенерировать регион «{regionName}»: {result.Error}"));
2096 |             }
2097 |         }
2098 |         catch (Exception ex)
2099 |         {
2100 |             AppendLog(LogEntry.System($"⚠ Ошибка генерации региона: {ex.Message}"));
2101 |         }
2102 |         finally
2103 |         {
2104 |             IsRegionGenerating = false;
2105 |             RegionGenerationStatus = null;
2106 |         }
2107 |     }
2108 | 
2109 |     /// <summary>
2110 |     /// True while a cold-region generation is in flight (issue #20).
2111 |     /// Drives a "Генерация региона…" overlay in the game view. Set by
2112 |     /// <see cref="TriggerColdRegionGenerationAsync"/>; cleared on
2113 |     /// completion / failure.
2114 |     /// </summary>
2115 |     [ObservableProperty] private bool _isRegionGenerating;
2116 | 
2117 |     /// <summary>
2118 |     /// Live status text for the region-generation overlay (issue #20).
2119 |     /// Mirrors the orchestrator's progress callback label.
2120 |     /// </summary>
2121 |     [ObservableProperty] private string? _regionGenerationStatus;
2122 | 
2123 | 
2124 |     /// <summary>
2125 |     /// Handle the character panel's ExportRequested event (issue #62).
2126 |     /// Resolves the active save id + player id from this VM's runtime
2127 |     /// state, calls <see cref="CharacterSheetStore.Export"/> (which writes
2128 |     /// a portable .json sheet under <c>{ProfileDirectory}/characters/</c>),
2129 |     /// and surfaces the result path / error back to the panel via
2130 |     /// <see cref="CharacterPanelViewModel.ExportStatus"/>.
2131 |     /// </summary>
2132 |     /// <remarks>
2133 |     /// Single-player + host modes only — clients have no save authority,
2134 |     /// so the export would fail anyway. The button is still visible to
2135 |     /// clients (so they see the affordance) but a click yields a friendly
2136 |     /// «no save» error instead of a silent no-op.
2137 |     /// </remarks>
2138 |     private void OnCharacterExportRequested()
2139 |     {
2140 |         if (_world is null)
2141 |         {
2142 |             CharacterPanel.ExportStatus = "Мир не загружен — экспорт невозможен.";
2143 |             return;
2144 |         }
2145 |         if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(_saveId))
2146 |         {
2147 |             CharacterPanel.ExportStatus = "Нет активного сохранения — экспорт невозможен.";
2148 |             return;
2149 |         }
2150 |         var player = _world.ActivePlayer ?? _world.Players.FirstOrDefault();
2151 |         if (player is null)
2152 |         {
2153 |             CharacterPanel.ExportStatus = "Нет активного персонажа для экспорта.";
2154 |             return;
2155 |         }
2156 | 
2157 |         try
2158 |         {
2159 |             var store = ServiceHost.Resolve<CharacterSheetStore>();
2160 |             var sheet = store.Export(_saveId, player.Id);
2161 |             if (sheet is null)
2162 |             {
2163 |                 CharacterPanel.ExportStatus = "Не удалось экспортировать персонажа (см. лог).";
2164 |                 return;
2165 |             }
2166 |             // Compose the on-disk path the same way PathFor does, so the
2167 |             // user gets a copyable path they can find in their file
2168 |             // manager. We don't expose PathFor publicly (it's an internal
2169 |             // helper); reconstructing it here keeps the store API tight.
2170 |             var path = System.IO.Path.Combine(
2171 |                 store.CharactersDirectory,
2172 |                 $"{CharacterSheetStore.CharIdToString(sheet.Id)}.json");
2173 |             CharacterPanel.ExportStatus = $"Персонаж экспортирован в:\n{path}";
2174 |         }
2175 |         catch (Exception ex)
2176 |         {
2177 |             CharacterPanel.ExportStatus = $"Ошибка экспорта: {ex.Message}";
2178 |         }
2179 |     }
2180 | 
2181 |     /// <summary>
2182 |     /// Simple inline random-encounter roll. If the destination danger > 0
2183 |     /// and a d100 roll lands below <paramref name="destLoc"/>.Danger * 10,
2184 |     /// spawn 1–2 hostile NPCs at the destination. Terrain picks the
2185 |     /// creature template (wolves in forests, goblins in caves/underground,
2186 |     /// goblins elsewhere as a default).
2187 |     ///
2188 |     /// <para>
2189 |     /// This is the minimal hook that makes travel immediately dangerous
2190 |     /// and gives the GM something to work with. A future random-encounters
2191 |     /// task can expand this into a proper subsystem (party size scaling,
2192 |     /// ambush detection, terrain-aware creature tables, faction-based
2193 |     /// hostility).
2194 |     /// </para>
2195 |     ///
2196 |     /// <para>
2197 |     /// ENGINE-DEPTH (issue #35): at night (hour &gt;= 20 or &lt; 5) the
2198 |     /// encounter chance is bumped from Danger*10% to Danger*15% — the
2199 |     /// wild is more dangerous after dark. ENGINE-DEPTH (issue #34): rain
2200 |     /// or fog also bumps the chance to Danger*15% (low visibility makes
2201 |     /// ambushes easier).
2202 |     /// </para>
2203 |     /// </summary>
2204 |     private void MaybeRandomEncounter(Location destLoc)
2205 |     {
2206 |         if (_world is null) return;
2207 |         if (destLoc.Danger <= 0) return;
2208 | 
2209 |         // Danger is on a 0-10 scale; *10 gives a 0-100 percent chance.
2210 |         int chance = destLoc.Danger * 10;
2211 | 
2212 |         // Issue #35: night bumps the chance to Danger*15%. Issue #34:
2213 |         // rain/fog weather also bumps to Danger*15% (low visibility).
2214 |         bool isNight = GameTime.IsNight(_world.Clock.Hour);
2215 |         bool badWeather = string.Equals(_world.CurrentWeather, "rain", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
2216 |                        || string.Equals(_world.CurrentWeather, "fog", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
2217 |         if (isNight || badWeather)
2218 |             chance = destLoc.Danger * 15;
2219 | 
2220 |         if (_world.Rng.NextInt(100) >= chance) return;
2221 | 
2222 |         // Pick the creature template based on terrain.
2223 |         string creatureTplId = destLoc.Terrain switch
2224 |         {
2225 |             "forest" => "npc_wolf",
2226 |             "swamp" => "npc_wolf",
2227 |             "cave" => "npc_goblin",
2228 |             "underground" => "npc_goblin",
2229 |             "ruin" => "npc_ghost",
2230 |             _ => "npc_goblin",
2231 |         };
2232 | 
2233 |         // Spawn 1-2 of them.
2234 |         int count = _world.Rng.NextInt(1, 3); // 1 or 2
2235 |         int spawned = 0;
2236 |         for (int i = 0; i < count; i++)
2237 |         {
2238 |             var npc = _world.SpawnNpcFromTemplate(creatureTplId, destLoc.Id);
2239 |             if (npc is not null)
2240 |             {
2241 |                 // Force hostile so the GM picks up combat cues.
2242 |                 npc.Disposition = "hostile";
2243 |                 spawned++;
2244 |             }
2245 |         }
2246 | 
2247 |         if (spawned > 0)
2248 |         {
2249 |             var label = creatureTplId == "npc_wolf" ? "волки"
2250 |                 : creatureTplId == "npc_ghost" ? "призраки"
2251 |                 : "гоблины";
2252 |             AppendLog(LogEntry.System(
2253 |                 $"⚠ Из тени выходит угроза: {spawned} × {label}!"));
2254 |         }
2255 |     }
2256 | 
2257 |     // ─── HostSession event handlers (marshal to UI thread) ───────────

2259 |     private void OnMemberJoined(MemberInfo m) =>
2260 |         Dispatcher.UIThread.Post(() =>
2261 |         {
2262 |             // Replace if present, else add.
2263 |             for (int i = 0; i < Members.Count; i++)
2264 |                 if (Members[i].ConnectionId == m.ConnectionId) { Members[i] = m; return; }
2265 |             Members.Add(m);
2266 |             AppendLog(LogEntry.System($"{m.Nickname} присоединился."));
2267 |             // Issue #77 — refresh the lobby StartGame CanExecute (a
2268 |             // newly-joined Pending member disables the button until
2269 |             // they toggle ready).

2271 |             OnPropertyChanged(nameof(LocalMemberInfo));
2272 |             OnPropertyChanged(nameof(IsLocalReady));

2280 |                 if (Members[i].ConnectionId == m.ConnectionId) { Members.RemoveAt(i); break; }
2281 |             AppendLog(LogEntry.System($"{m.Nickname} отключился."));

2283 |             // departing Pending member may have been blocking start).

2285 |             OnPropertyChanged(nameof(LocalMemberInfo));
2286 |             OnPropertyChanged(nameof(IsLocalReady));

2327 |         Dispatcher.UIThread.Post(() =>
2328 |         {
2329 |             if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(msg.FullText))
2330 |                 AppendLog(LogEntry.Narrative(msg.FullText));
2331 |             foreach (var tc in msg.ToolEvents)
2332 |             {
2333 |                 AppendLog(LogEntry.Tool($"{tc.Name}: {tc.Result}",
2334 |                     metadata: new System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary<string, object>
2335 |                     {
2336 |                         ["name"] = tc.Name,
2337 |                         ["args"] = tc.ArgsJson,
2338 |                         ["isError"] = tc.IsError,
2339 |                     }));
2340 | 
2341 |                 // Issue #70 — same quest-completion notification as in
2342 |                 // the single-player turn flow. See the corresponding
2343 |                 // block in RunSinglePlayerTurnAsync for details.
2344 |                 if (tc.Name == "update_quest" && !tc.IsError)
2345 |                 {
2346 |                     var questName = TryExtractQuestCompletedName(tc.Result);
2347 |                     if (questName is not null)
2348 |                     {
2349 |                         AppendLog(LogEntry.System(
2350 |                             $"Квест «{questName}» завершён! Награда доступна во вкладке Квесты."));
2351 |                     }
2352 |                 }
2353 |             }
2354 |             // Clear the pending-actions panel (the turn is done).
2355 |             PendingActions.Clear();
2356 |             IsWaiting = false;
2357 |             StatusText = null;
2358 |             // Accumulate token billing for this turn into the VM's
2359 |             // session totals + last-turn counter. (Persistence is handled
2360 |             // by HostSession itself, which writes the cumulative totals
2361 |             // into _meta on its own save path.)
2362 |             AccumulateTokens(msg.PromptTokens, msg.CompletionTokens, msg.TotalTokens);
2363 |             RefreshFromHostWorld();
2364 |         });

2442 |                     };

2632 |     private void OnClientDisconnected(DisconnectedInfo info) =>
2633 |         Dispatcher.UIThread.Post(() =>
2634 |         {
2635 |             // Intentional disconnect (user clicked Leave, or the host
2636 |             // kicked us): don't show the reconnect overlay. The Leave
2637 |             // command already navigates to menu; the Kicked event
2638 |             // already surfaced an inline error via OnClientKicked.
2639 |             if (info.Intentional)
2640 |             {
2641 |                 AppendLog(LogEntry.System($"Соединение разорвано: {info.Reason}"));
2642 |                 return;
2643 |             }
2644 | 
2645 |             // Unexpected network drop: show the reconnect overlay.
2646 |             IsDisconnected = true;
2647 |             ReconnectFailed = false;
2648 |             DisconnectReason = info.Reason ?? string.Empty;
2649 |             ErrorMessage = $"Соединение потеряно: {info.Reason}";
2650 |             AppendLog(LogEntry.System($"Разрыв соединения: {info.Reason}"));
2651 |         });

--- src/MyGame.Desktop/ViewModels/HostGameViewModel.cs (C#, 258 lines, ~2,747 tokens) ---
   1 | using System;
   2 | using System.Threading;
   3 | using System.Threading.Tasks;
   4 | using CommunityToolkit.Mvvm.ComponentModel;
   5 | using CommunityToolkit.Mvvm.Input;
   6 | using MyGame.Core.AI;
   7 | using MyGame.Core.AI.Agents;
   8 | using MyGame.Core.AI.Prompts;
   9 | using MyGame.Core.AI.Tools;
  10 | using MyGame.Core.Multiplayer;
  11 | using MyGame.Core.Profile;
  12 | using MyGame.Core.Saves;
  13 | using MyGame.Core.World;
  14 | using MyGame.Core.World.Content;
  15 | using MyGame.Desktop.Services;
  16 | 
  17 | namespace MyGame.Desktop.ViewModels;
  18 | 
  19 | /// <summary>
  20 | /// Host setup screen. Lets the host pick a game name and the max-players
  21 | /// cap, then creates a HostSession bound to a fresh DefaultWorld save
  22 | /// and starts the in-process WebSocket server.
  23 | ///
  24 | /// <para>
  25 | /// On success, the screen navigates to the game view as host. The
  26 | /// HostSession is stashed on the GameViewModel — the HostGame screen
  27 | /// itself doesn't keep a reference after navigation.
  28 | /// </para>
  29 | /// </summary>
  30 | public partial class HostGameViewModel : ViewModelBase
  31 | {
  32 |     private readonly ProfileStore _profileStore;
  33 |     private readonly SettingsStore _settingsStore;
  34 |     private readonly SaveManager _saveManager;
  35 |     private readonly MainViewModel _shell;
  36 | 
  37 |     private string _gameName = "Новая партия";
  38 |     private int _maxPlayers = 4;
  39 |     private string? _shareAddress;
  40 |     private bool _useAiWorldBuild;
  41 |     private string _worldBrief = string.Empty;
  42 | 
  43 |     // Holds the started session between the StartCommand's completion
  44 |     // and the shell's navigation. Read by the GameViewModel ctor via
  45 |     // the <see cref="StartedSession"/> property.
  46 |     internal HostSession? StartedSession { get; private set; }
  47 | 
  48 |     public HostGameViewModel(
  49 |         ProfileStore profileStore,
  50 |         SettingsStore settingsStore,
  51 |         SaveManager saveManager,
  52 |         MainViewModel shell)
  53 |     {
  54 |         _profileStore = profileStore ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(profileStore));
  55 |         _settingsStore = settingsStore ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(settingsStore));
  56 |         _saveManager = saveManager ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(saveManager));
  57 |         _shell = shell ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(shell));
  58 |     }
  59 | 
  60 |     // ─── Form fields ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
  61 | 
  62 |     public string GameName
  63 |     {
  64 |         get => _gameName;
  65 |         set => SetProperty(ref _gameName, value);
  66 |     }
  67 | 
  68 |     public int MaxPlayers
  69 |     {
  70 |         get => _maxPlayers;
  71 |         set => SetProperty(ref _maxPlayers, Math.Max(1, Math.Min(8, value)));
  72 |     }
  73 | 
  74 |     /// <summary>
  75 |     /// After the host starts, shows the IP:port string the host can
  76 |     /// share with friends. Null before StartAsync.
  77 |     /// </summary>
  78 |     public string? ShareAddress
  79 |     {
  80 |         get => _shareAddress;
  81 |         private set => SetProperty(ref _shareAddress, value);
  82 |     }
  83 | 
  84 |     /// <summary>
  85 |     /// If true, the host wants the world generated via AI (world-builder
  86 |     /// orchestrator) instead of the stock DefaultWorld. Drives the
  87 |     /// visibility of the brief TextBox and routes the start flow to the
  88 |     /// WorldBrief screen.
  89 |     /// </summary>
  90 |     public bool UseAiWorldBuild
  91 |     {
  92 |         get => _useAiWorldBuild;
  93 |         set => SetProperty(ref _useAiWorldBuild, value);
  94 |     }
  95 | 
  96 |     /// <summary>
  97 |     /// Free-form world brief shown only when <see cref="UseAiWorldBuild"/>
  98 |     /// is on. If left empty, the planner creates a default dark-fantasy
  99 |     /// world.
 100 |     /// </summary>
 101 |     public string WorldBrief
 102 |     {
 103 |         get => _worldBrief;
 104 |         set => SetProperty(ref _worldBrief, value);
 105 |     }
 106 | 
 107 |     // ─── Commands ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 108 | 
 109 |     /// <summary>
 110 |     /// Start hosting: build a fresh world, save it, create a GameMaster
 111 |     /// bound to that world, start a HostSession on an OS-assigned port,
 112 |     /// then navigate to the game view as host.
 113 |     /// </summary>
 114 |     [RelayCommand(CanExecute = nameof(CanStart))]
 115 |     private async Task StartAsync()
 116 |     {
 117 |         IsBusy = true;
 118 |         ErrorMessage = null;
 119 |         try
 120 |         {
 121 |             // If the host opted into AI world-build, route to the world-build
 122 |             // screen with the current brief (or the brief entry screen if the
 123 |             // brief is empty). The world-build screen will create the save
 124 |             // itself; the host flow here is abandoned in favour of the
 125 |             // single-player-style "create world then play" path. The host can
 126 |             // start the actual server after the build completes via the game
 127 |             // screen's host controls (TBD). For now, this keeps the flow
 128 |             // simple: build world → play single-player → optionally host.
 129 |             if (UseAiWorldBuild)
 130 |             {
 131 |                 if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(WorldBrief))
 132 |                 {
 133 |                     _shell.NavigateToWorldBrief();
 134 |                 }
 135 |                 else
 136 |                 {
 137 |                     _shell.NavigateToWorldBuild(WorldBrief);
 138 |                 }
 139 |                 return;
 140 |             }
 141 | 
 142 |             var profile = _profileStore.GetOrCreate();
 143 |             var settings = _settingsStore.Load();
 144 | 
 145 |             // 1) Create the starting world + persist it as a save so
 146 |             //    the HostSession has something to mutate + save.
 147 |             var world = DefaultWorld.Create();
 148 |             var meta = _saveManager.CreateSave(
 149 |                 string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(_gameName) ? "Мультиплеер" : _gameName.Trim(),
 150 |                 world, profile.Id);
 151 | 
 152 |             // 2) Wire the AI client + GM + tool registry onto the
 153 |             //    world. The GM mutates the world in-place via tool
 154 |             //    calls during ProcessNextTurnAsync.
 155 |             var ai = new AiClient(settings.Ai);
 156 |             var prompts = ServiceHost.Resolve<PromptLoader>();
 157 |             var tools = new ToolRegistry(world);
 158 |             var gm = new GameMaster(
 159 |                 ai, world, prompts, tools, settings.MaxToolIterations,
 160 |                 aiSettings: settings.Ai,
 161 |                 maxContextTokens: settings.MaxContextTokens);
 162 | 
 163 |             // 3) Build the session + start the WebSocket server.
 164 |             var cts = new CancellationTokenSource();
 165 |             var session = new HostSession(
 166 |                 profile, world, gm,
 167 |                 saveManager: _saveManager,
 168 |                 saveId: meta.Id,
 169 |                 requestedPort: 0,
 170 |                 shutdownToken: cts.Token,
 171 |                 bindHost: "+");
 172 |             var port = await session.StartAsync();
 173 |             StartedSession = session;
 174 | 
 175 |             // 4) Issue #77 — leave the party in Lobby status. The host
 176 |             //    clicks «Начать игру» in the lobby UI (StartGameCommand
 177 |             //    on the GameViewModel) to transition to Playing once all
 178 |             //    non-spectator members are Ready. Previously this
 179 |             //    auto-transitioned to Playing immediately, skipping the
 180 |             //    lobby entirely — but with the new lobby UI the host
 181 |             //    can see who's joined, share the address, and start the
 182 |             //    game on demand. The HostServer's default status is
 183 |             //    Lobby, so we don't need to set it explicitly here.
 184 |             ShareAddress = session.UpnpPublicAddress is { } publicAddr
 185 |                 ? $"Публичный адрес: {publicAddr}  (локальный: localhost:{port})"
 186 |                 : $"localhost:{port}  (порт {port})";
 187 | 
 188 |             // 5) Hand off to the GameViewModel. The shell constructs
 189 |             //    the VM with the save id; the VM pulls the session via
 190 |             //    a transient channel — we use a static slot here for
 191 |             //    simplicity (only one host session can exist per
 192 |             //    process at a time).
 193 |             PendingHostSessionTransfer.Set(session, cts);
 194 |             _shell.NavigateToGameAsHost(meta.Id).FireAndForget();
 195 |         }
 196 |         catch (Exception ex)
 197 |         {
 198 |             ErrorMessage = $"Не удалось запустить хост: {ex.Message}";
 199 |         }
 200 |         finally
 201 |         {
 202 |             IsBusy = false;
 203 |         }
 204 |     }
 205 | 
 206 |     /// <summary>Back to the main menu.</summary>
 207 |     [RelayCommand]
 208 |     private void Back() => _shell.NavigateToMenu();
 209 | 
 210 |     private bool CanStart() => !IsBusy && !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(_gameName);
 211 | }
 212 | 
 213 | /// <summary>
 214 | /// Tiny channel for transferring a freshly-started HostSession from
 215 | /// the HostGame screen to the Game screen without exposing it via the
 216 | /// shell. There's only ever one host session per process at a time —
 217 | /// the Game screen pulls it from here on construction and clears the
 218 | /// slot. (A cleaner DI scope would also work; this keeps the shell's
 219 | /// API surface lean.)
 220 | /// </summary>
 221 | internal static class PendingHostSessionTransfer
 222 | {
 223 |     private static HostSession? _session;
 224 |     private static CancellationTokenSource? _cts;
 225 |     private static readonly object _lock = new();
 226 | 
 227 |     public static void Set(HostSession session, CancellationTokenSource cts)
 228 |     {
 229 |         lock (_lock) { _session = session; _cts = cts; }
 230 |     }
 231 | 
 232 |     public static (HostSession? session, CancellationTokenSource? cts) Take()
 233 |     {
 234 |         lock (_lock)
 235 |         {
 236 |             var s = _session; var c = _cts;
 237 |             _session = null; _cts = null;
 238 |             return (s, c);
 239 |         }
 240 |     }
 241 | }
 242 | 
 243 | /// <summary>
 244 | /// Tiny helper extension — fire-and-forget an async task while
 245 | /// surfacing any exception via Trace (avoids unobserved-task crashes).
 246 | /// </summary>
 247 | internal static class TaskExtensions
 248 | {
 249 |     public static void FireAndForget(this Task task)
 250 |     {
 251 |         _ = task.ContinueWith(t =>
 252 |         {
 253 |             if (t.IsFaulted && t.Exception is not null)
 254 |                 System.Diagnostics.Trace.WriteLine($"[FireAndForget] {t.Exception}");
 255 |         }, TaskScheduler.Default);
 256 |     }
 257 | }

--- src/MyGame.Desktop/ViewModels/JoinGameViewModel.cs (C#, 135 lines, ~1,171 tokens) ---
   1 | using System;
   2 | using System.Threading;
   3 | using System.Threading.Tasks;
   4 | using CommunityToolkit.Mvvm.ComponentModel;
   5 | using CommunityToolkit.Mvvm.Input;
   6 | using MyGame.Core.Multiplayer;
   7 | using MyGame.Core.Profile;
   8 | 
   9 | namespace MyGame.Desktop.ViewModels;
  10 | 
  11 | /// <summary>
  12 | /// Join screen. Lets the player enter a host IP/hostname + port, then
  13 | /// opens a GameClient, performs the HelloMsg→WelcomeMsg handshake,
  14 | /// and on success navigates to the game view as a client.
  15 | /// </summary>
  16 | public partial class JoinGameViewModel : ViewModelBase
  17 | {
  18 |     private readonly ProfileStore _profileStore;
  19 |     private readonly SettingsStore _settingsStore;
  20 |     private readonly MainViewModel _shell;
  21 | 
  22 |     private string _host = "localhost";
  23 |     private int _port = 51920;
  24 | 
  25 |     public JoinGameViewModel(
  26 |         ProfileStore profileStore,
  27 |         SettingsStore settingsStore,
  28 |         MainViewModel shell)
  29 |     {
  30 |         _profileStore = profileStore ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(profileStore));
  31 |         _settingsStore = settingsStore ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(settingsStore));
  32 |         _shell = shell ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(shell));
  33 | 
  34 |         // Pre-fill from the last-used server (if any).
  35 |         try
  36 |         {
  37 |             var s = _settingsStore.Load();
  38 |             if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(s.LastServerHost))
  39 |                 _host = s.LastServerHost;
  40 |             if (s.LastServerPort is int p && p > 0)
  41 |                 _port = p;
  42 |         }
  43 |         catch { /* fall back to defaults */ }
  44 |     }
  45 | 
  46 |     // ─── Form fields ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
  47 | 
  48 |     public string Host
  49 |     {
  50 |         get => _host;
  51 |         set => SetProperty(ref _host, value);
  52 |     }
  53 | 
  54 |     public int Port
  55 |     {
  56 |         get => _port;
  57 |         set => SetProperty(ref _port, Math.Max(1, Math.Min(65535, value)));
  58 |     }
  59 | 
  60 |     // ─── Commands ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  61 | 
  62 |     /// <summary>
  63 |     /// Connect to the host. Builds a ClientSession, runs the handshake,
  64 |     /// remembers the host/port in settings for next time, and on
  65 |     /// success hands the session off to the GameViewModel.
  66 |     /// </summary>
  67 |     [RelayCommand(CanExecute = nameof(CanConnect))]
  68 |     private async Task ConnectAsync()
  69 |     {
  70 |         IsBusy = true;
  71 |         ErrorMessage = null;
  72 |         try
  73 |         {
  74 |             var profile = _profileStore.GetOrCreate();
  75 |             var session = new ClientSession(profile);
  76 |             await session.ConnectAsync(_host.Trim(), _port, CancellationToken.None);
  77 | 
  78 |             // Remember for next time.
  79 |             try
  80 |             {
  81 |                 _settingsStore.Update(s => s with
  82 |                 {
  83 |                     LastServerHost = _host.Trim(),
  84 |                     LastServerPort = _port,
  85 |                 });
  86 |             }
  87 |             catch { /* non-fatal */ }
  88 | 
  89 |             // Hand off to the GameViewModel.
  90 |             PendingClientSessionTransfer.Set(session);
  91 |             _shell.NavigateToGameClient(_host.Trim(), _port).FireAndForget();
  92 |         }
  93 |         catch (Exception ex)
  94 |         {
  95 |             ErrorMessage = $"Не удалось подключиться: {ex.Message}";
  96 |         }
  97 |         finally
  98 |         {
  99 |             IsBusy = false;
 100 |         }
 101 |     }
 102 | 
 103 |     /// <summary>Back to the main menu.</summary>
 104 |     [RelayCommand]
 105 |     private void Back() => _shell.NavigateToMenu();
 106 | 
 107 |     private bool CanConnect() => !IsBusy && !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(_host) && _port > 0;
 108 | }
 109 | 
 110 | /// <summary>
 111 | /// Tiny channel for transferring a freshly-connected ClientSession from
 112 | /// the JoinGame screen to the Game screen (mirrors the host-side
 113 | /// PendingHostSessionTransfer).
 114 | /// </summary>
 115 | internal static class PendingClientSessionTransfer
 116 | {
 117 |     private static MyGame.Core.Multiplayer.ClientSession? _session;
 118 |     private static readonly object _lock = new();
 119 | 
 120 |     public static void Set(MyGame.Core.Multiplayer.ClientSession session)
 121 |     {
 122 |         lock (_lock) _session = session;
 123 |     }
 124 | 
 125 |     public static MyGame.Core.Multiplayer.ClientSession? Take()
 126 |     {
 127 |         lock (_lock)
 128 |         {
 129 |             var s = _session;
 130 |             _session = null;
 131 |             return s;
 132 |         }
 133 |     }
 134 | }

  18 | /// <summary>

  44 | /// <summary>
  45 | /// Landing screen after app startup. Shows an inline nickname editor in
  46 | /// the top-right corner (no separate profile screen — nickname is the
  47 | /// only profile field, no auth, no settings mixed in) and the primary
  48 | /// buttons:
  49 | /// <list type="bullet">
  50 | ///   <item>«Новая игра» — single-player, creates a save with DefaultWorld.</item>
  51 | ///   <item>«Создать мир (AI)» — open the AI world-build flow.</item>
  52 | ///   <item>«Хост игры» — start a multiplayer host.</item>
  53 | ///   <item>«Подключиться» — join a multiplayer host.</item>
  54 | ///   <item>«Загрузить» — pick from existing saves.</item>
  55 | ///   <item>«Настройки» — edit AI settings only.</item>
  56 | /// </list>
  57 | ///
  58 | /// <para>
  59 | /// <b>Resume prompt (issue #19):</b> on construction the menu checks
  60 | /// for a leftover <c>worldbuilder-state.json</c> (written when the user
  61 | /// cancelled a previous build). If present, <see cref="HasPendingResumeBuild"/>
  62 | /// is true and the UI shows a «Незавершённая генерация мира. Продолжить?»
  63 | /// prompt with «Продолжить» (<see cref="ResumeBuildCommand"/>) and
  64 | /// «Отменить» (<see cref="DiscardPendingResumeCommand"/>) buttons.
  65 | /// </para>
  66 | ///
  67 | /// <para>
  68 | /// <b>First-time hints (issue #73):</b> <see cref="ShowHints"/> is true
  69 | /// for the first three sessions (tracked via
  70 | /// <see cref="Settings.SessionCount"/>). When true, the menu shows small
  71 | /// tooltip hints under the primary buttons. After the third session the
  72 | /// hints disappear.
  73 | /// </para>
  74 | ///
  75 | /// <para>
  76 | /// <b>Save browser (issue #74):</b> the saves list now supports:
  77 | /// <list type="bullet">
  78 | ///   <item>Search box — filters by Name / WorldTitle / CharacterName
  79 | ///     (case-insensitive). Re-filters on every keystroke.</item>
  80 | ///   <item>Sort dropdown — ByDate (default) / ByName / ByCreatedDate.</item>
  81 | ///   <item>Multi-select delete — each row has a checkbox; the
  82 | ///     «Удалить выбранные» button (with a confirm overlay) deletes all
  83 | ///     checked saves in one go.</item>
  84 | ///   <item>Richer row metadata — WorldTitle, LocationName, Playtime
  85 | ///     (formatted «Xч Ym»), SaveSize («X KB» / «X MB»), EngineVersion.</item>
  86 | /// </list>
  87 | /// </para>
  88 | /// </summary>
  89 | public partial class MainMenuViewModel : ViewModelBase
  90 | {

  92 |     private readonly SaveManager _saveManager;
  93 |     private readonly ProfileStore _profileStore;
  94 |     private readonly SettingsStore _settingsStore;
  95 | 
  96 |     // The nickname TextBox binds two-way to this. Editing + Enter (or
  97 |     // focus loss) calls SaveNicknameCommand, which validates + persists
  98 |     // via ProfileStore. No separate profile screen — the nickname is the
  99 |     // only profile field, and there's no auth to wrap it in.
 100 |     private string _nickname = string.Empty;
 101 |     private string? _nicknameError;
 102 | 
 103 |     // Cached snapshot of the saved world-builder state (issue #19).
 104 |     // Populated in the ctor when WorldBuilderStateStore detects a
 105 |     // leftover state file. Cleared by DiscardPendingResume (user clicks
 106 |     // «Отменить» on the resume prompt) or by ResumeBuild (user clicks
 107 |     // «Продолжить» — the state file is consumed by the resumed build).
 108 |     private WorldBuilderStateFile? _pendingResume;
 109 | 
 110 |     // Source-of-truth list of loaded saves (before search/sort filters
 111 |     // are applied). The bound <see cref="Saves"/> collection is the
 112 |     // filtered + sorted view of this list. Kept in sync by
 113 |     // <see cref="RefreshSaves"/> + <see cref="ApplyFilterAndSort"/>.
 114 |     private readonly List<SaveSlotViewModel> _allSaves = new();
 115 | 
 116 |     public MainMenuViewModel(
 117 |         MainViewModel shell,
 118 |         SaveManager saveManager,
 119 |         ProfileStore profileStore,
 120 |         SettingsStore settingsStore)
 121 |     {
 122 |         _shell = shell ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(shell));
 123 |         _saveManager = saveManager ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(saveManager));
 124 |         _profileStore = profileStore ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(profileStore));
 125 |         _settingsStore = settingsStore ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(settingsStore));
 126 | 
 127 |         try { _nickname = _profileStore.GetOrCreate().Nickname; }
 128 |         catch { _nickname = "Игрок"; }
 129 | 
 130 |         // Issue #19 — detect a leftover world-builder state file (the
 131 |         // user cancelled a previous build). If found, surface a resume
 132 |         // prompt in the UI. We snapshot the file into _pendingResume;
 133 |         // the user's choice (resume / discard) consumes it.
 134 |         try
 135 |         {
 136 |             if (WorldBuilderStateStore.Exists())
 137 |             {
 138 |                 _pendingResume = WorldBuilderStateStore.Load();
 139 |                 HasPendingResumeBuild = _pendingResume?.State is not null;
 140 |             }
 141 |         }
 142 |         catch (Exception ex)
 143 |         {
 144 |             System.Diagnostics.Trace.WriteLine(
 145 |                 $"[MainMenuViewModel] failed to check worldbuilder-state.json: {ex.Message}");
 146 |         }
 147 | 
 148 |         // Issue #73 — show first-time tooltip hints for the first three
 149 |         // sessions. Increment the session counter on each visit so the
 150 |         // hints disappear after the third session. Reads + writes via
 151 |         // SettingsStore.Update (atomic patch + persist).
 152 |         try
 153 |         {
 154 |             var settings = _settingsStore.Load();
 155 |             ShowHints = settings.SessionCount < 3;
 156 |             _settingsStore.Update(s => s with { SessionCount = s.SessionCount + 1 });
 157 |         }
 158 |         catch (Exception ex)
 159 |         {
 160 |             System.Diagnostics.Trace.WriteLine(
 161 |                 $"[MainMenuViewModel] failed to update session count: {ex.Message}");
 162 |             // Default to showing hints when the settings read fails —
 163 |             // it's better to over-show than to silently hide them on a
 164 |             // fresh install where the user hasn't seen them yet.
 165 |             ShowHints = true;
 166 |         }
 167 |     }

 344 |     /// <summary>

 369 | 
 370 |     /// <summary>
 371 |     /// «Создать мир» — open the AI world-build flow. The user types a
 372 |     /// brief, then the orchestrator plans + commits + narrates a custom
 373 |     /// world. Requires an AI API key (set in Настройки → AI settings).
 374 |     /// </summary>
 375 |     [RelayCommand(CanExecute = nameof(CanNavigate))]
 376 |     private void CreateWorld() => _shell.NavigateToWorldBrief();
 377 | 
 378 |     /// <summary>«Хост игры» — open the multiplayer host setup screen.</summary>
 379 |     [RelayCommand(CanExecute = nameof(CanNavigate))]
 380 |     private void HostGame() => _shell.NavigateToHost();
 381 | 
 382 |     /// <summary>«Подключиться» — open the multiplayer join screen.</summary>
 383 |     [RelayCommand(CanExecute = nameof(CanNavigate))]
 384 |     private void JoinGame() => _shell.NavigateToJoin();
 385 | 
 386 |     /// <summary>
 387 |     /// «Настройки» — open the settings screen (AI settings only; the
 388 |     /// nickname is edited inline on the main menu).
 389 |     /// </summary>
 390 |     [RelayCommand(CanExecute = nameof(CanNavigate))]
 391 |     private void OpenSettings() => _shell.NavigateToSettings();
 392 | 
 393 |     // ─── Resume-pending-build commands (issue #19) ──────────────────
 394 | 
 395 |     /// <summary>
 396 |     /// Resume the pending world-build. Loads the saved state + brief
 397 |     /// from the on-disk <c>worldbuilder-state.json</c> and navigates to
 398 |     /// the world-build screen with the state staged for
 399 |     /// <see cref="WorldBuilderOrchestrator.LoadState"/>. The resumed
 400 |     /// build auto-starts and skips already-completed stages.
 401 |     /// </summary>
 402 |     [RelayCommand(CanExecute = nameof(CanNavigate))]
 403 |     private void ResumeBuild()
 404 |     {
 405 |         if (_pendingResume?.State is null) return;
 406 |         var state = _pendingResume.State;
 407 |         var brief = _pendingResume.Brief ?? string.Empty;
 408 |         // Clear the in-memory snapshot + the HasPendingResumeBuild flag
 409 |         // BEFORE navigating (so re-entering the menu doesn't re-prompt).
 410 |         // The on-disk state file is left in place — the resumed build
 411 |         // will delete it on completion (or rewrite it on another cancel).
 412 |         _pendingResume = null;
 413 |         HasPendingResumeBuild = false;
 414 |         _shell.NavigateToWorldBuildForResume(state, brief);
 415 |     }
 416 | 
 417 |     /// <summary>
 418 |     /// Discard the pending world-build state. Deletes the on-disk file
 419 |     /// and clears the resume-prompt UI. Used when the user clicks
 420 |     /// «Отменить» on the resume prompt — they don't want to resume, so
 421 |     /// the state file is removed (a fresh build will be required next
 422 |     /// time).
 423 |     /// </summary>
 424 |     [RelayCommand]
 425 |     private void DiscardPendingResume()
 426 |     {
 427 |         try { WorldBuilderStateStore.Delete(); }
 428 |         catch (Exception ex)
 429 |         {
 430 |             System.Diagnostics.Trace.WriteLine(
 431 |                 $"[MainMenuViewModel] failed to delete worldbuilder-state.json: {ex.Message}");
 432 |         }
 433 |         _pendingResume = null;
 434 |         HasPendingResumeBuild = false;
 435 |         OnPropertyChanged(nameof(PendingResumeSummary));
 436 |     }
 437 | 
 438 |     /// <summary>
 439 |     /// Validate + persist the current nickname. Called from the TextBox's
 440 |     /// KeyDown (Enter) or LostFocus. On validation failure, sets
 441 |     /// <see cref="NicknameError"/> and leaves the TextBox content intact
 442 |     /// so the user can fix it. On success, clears the error.
 443 |     /// </summary>
 444 |     [RelayCommand]
 445 |     private void SaveNickname()
 446 |     {
 447 |         var trimmed = (_nickname ?? string.Empty).Trim();
 448 |         if (!MyGame.Core.Profile.Profile.ValidateNickname(trimmed, out var error))
 449 |         {
 450 |             NicknameError = error;
 451 |             return;
 452 |         }
 453 |         try
 454 |         {
 455 |             _profileStore.Rename(trimmed);
 456 |             Nickname = trimmed; // normalize (trim)
 457 |             NicknameError = null;
 458 |         }
 459 |         catch (Exception ex)
 460 |         {
 461 |             NicknameError = ex.Message;
 462 |         }
 463 |     }
 464 | 
 465 |     /// <summary>«Загрузить» — toggle the saves-list panel. The list is refreshed
 466 |     /// every time the panel is opened so newly-created saves appear.
 467 |     /// </summary>
 468 |     [RelayCommand(CanExecute = nameof(CanNavigate))]
 469 |     private void ToggleLoad()
 470 |     {
 471 |         if (SavesLoaded)
 472 |         {
 473 |             SavesLoaded = false;
 474 |             return;
 475 |         }
 476 |         RefreshSaves();
 477 |     }
 478 | 
 479 |     /// <summary>Reload the saves list from disk.</summary>
 480 |     [RelayCommand]
 481 |     private void RefreshSaves()
 482 |     {
 483 |         // Unsubscribe from the previous batch of slots so we don't
 484 |         // leak event handlers when the list is reloaded. (Each slot
 485 |         // holds a strong reference to the parent VM via the
 486 |         // SelectionChanged event; if we didn't unsubscribe, the slots
 487 |         // would stay alive until the next RefreshSaves.)
 488 |         foreach (var s in _allSaves) s.SelectionChanged -= OnSlotSelectionChangedInternal;
 489 |         _allSaves.Clear();
 490 |         try
 491 |         {
 492 |             var savesRoot = _saveManager.SavesDirectory;
 493 |             foreach (var meta in _saveManager.ListSaves())
 494 |             {
 495 |                 var saveDir = Path.Combine(savesRoot, meta.Id);
 496 |                 var slot = new SaveSlotViewModel(
 497 |                     meta, saveDir,
 498 |                     id => _shell.NavigateToGame(id),
 499 |                     id => _shell.NavigateToRebuild(id));
 500 |                 slot.SelectionChanged += OnSlotSelectionChangedInternal;
 501 |                 _allSaves.Add(slot);
 502 |             }
 503 |         }
 504 |         catch (Exception ex)
 505 |         {
 506 |             ErrorMessage = $"Не удалось прочитать список сохранений: {ex.Message}";
 507 |         }
 508 |         SavesLoaded = true;
 509 |         ApplyFilterAndSort();
 510 |     }
 511 | 
 512 |     /// <summary>
 513 |     /// Internal handler for <see cref="SaveSlotViewModel.SelectionChanged"/>
 514 |     /// events from individual slots. Forwards to the public
 515 |     /// <see cref="OnSlotSelectionChanged"/> method (which fires
 516 |     /// PropertyChanged on <see cref="HasSelectedSaves"/>).
 517 |     /// </summary>
 518 |     private void OnSlotSelectionChangedInternal(SaveSlotViewModel slot) => OnSlotSelectionChanged();
 519 | 
 520 |     /// <summary>
 521 |     /// Apply the current <see cref="SearchText"/> filter + <see cref="SortMode"/>
 522 |     /// to <see cref="_allSaves"/> and replace <see cref="Saves"/> with the
 523 |     /// result. Called whenever the search text or sort mode changes, and
 524 |     /// after <see cref="RefreshSaves"/>. Selection state (IsSelected) is
 525 |     /// preserved across re-sorts because we mutate the same VM instances
 526 |     /// (we just reorder / filter the bound collection, we don't recreate
 527 |     /// the row VMs).
 528 |     /// </summary>
 529 |     private void ApplyFilterAndSort()
 530 |     {
 531 |         var query = (_searchText ?? string.Empty).Trim();
 532 |         IEnumerable<SaveSlotViewModel> filtered = _allSaves;
 533 |         if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(query))
 534 |         {
 535 |             filtered = _allSaves.Where(s =>
 536 |                 s.Name.Contains(query, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
 537 |                 || s.WorldTitle.Contains(query, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
 538 |                 || s.Character.Contains(query, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase));
 539 |         }
 540 | 
 541 |         filtered = _sortMode switch
 542 |         {
 543 |             SaveSortMode.ByName => filtered.OrderBy(s => s.Name, StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase),
 544 |             SaveSortMode.ByCreatedDate => filtered.OrderByDescending(s => s.CreatedAtRaw),
 545 |             _ => filtered.OrderByDescending(s => s.UpdatedAtRaw),
 546 |         };
 547 | 
 548 |         Saves.Clear();
 549 |         foreach (var s in filtered) Saves.Add(s);
 550 |         OnPropertyChanged(nameof(HasSelectedSaves));
 551 |     }
 552 | 
 553 |     /// <summary>
 554 |     /// Issue #74 — multi-select delete trigger. Shows a confirmation
 555 |     /// overlay (bound to <see cref="IsDeleteConfirmVisible"/>). The
 556 |     /// overlay's «Да» button calls <see cref="ConfirmDeleteSelectedCommand"/>,
 557 |     /// «Отмена» calls <see cref="CancelDeleteSelectedCommand"/>.
 558 |     /// </summary>
 559 |     [RelayCommand]
 560 |     private void RequestDeleteSelected()
 561 |     {
 562 |         if (!HasSelectedSaves) return;
 563 |         IsDeleteConfirmVisible = true;
 564 |     }
 565 | 
 566 |     /// <summary>
 567 |     /// Confirmation overlay — «Отмена»: hide the overlay without
 568 |     /// deleting anything. Selections are preserved (the user can change
 569 |     /// their mind and click «Удалить выбранные» again).
 570 |     /// </summary>
 571 |     [RelayCommand]
 572 |     private void CancelDeleteSelected()
 573 |     {
 574 |         IsDeleteConfirmVisible = false;
 575 |     }
 576 | 
 577 |     /// <summary>
 578 |     /// Confirmation overlay — «Да»: delete every checked save, then
 579 |     /// hide the overlay + refresh the list. Best-effort: individual
 580 |     /// delete failures are logged to Trace and don't abort the batch
 581 |     /// (so one locked file doesn't block the rest of the deletes).
 582 |     /// </summary>
 583 |     [RelayCommand]
 584 |     private void ConfirmDeleteSelected()
 585 |     {
 586 |         IsDeleteConfirmVisible = false;
 587 |         var selected = _allSaves.Where(s => s.IsSelected).ToList();
 588 |         if (selected.Count == 0) return;
 589 |         foreach (var s in selected)
 590 |         {
 591 |             try
 592 |             {
 593 |                 _saveManager.DeleteSave(s.Id);
 594 |             }
 595 |             catch (Exception ex)
 596 |             {
 597 |                 System.Diagnostics.Trace.WriteLine(
 598 |                     $"[MainMenuViewModel] failed to delete save {s.Id}: {ex.Message}");
 599 |             }
 600 |         }
 601 |         RefreshSaves();
 602 |     }
 603 | 
 604 |     /// <summary>
 605 |     /// Issue #74 — clear all row checkboxes. Useful when the user wants
 606 |     /// to reset the multi-select state without deleting anything.
 607 |     /// </summary>
 608 |     [RelayCommand]
 609 |     private void ClearSelection()
 610 |     {
 611 |         foreach (var s in _allSaves) s.IsSelected = false;
 612 |         OnPropertyChanged(nameof(HasSelectedSaves));
 613 |     }
 614 | 
 615 |     /// <summary>
 616 |     /// Called by <see cref="SaveSlotViewModel"/> whenever its
 617 |     /// <see cref="SaveSlotViewModel.IsSelected"/> flag changes — so the
 618 |     /// «Удалить выбранные» button's enabled state refreshes in real time.
 619 |     /// </summary>
 620 |     internal void OnSlotSelectionChanged() => OnPropertyChanged(nameof(HasSelectedSaves));
 621 | 
 622 |     /// <summary>
 623 |     /// Import a portable character sheet (.pathstone-char / .json) from
 624 |     /// an arbitrary path (issue #62). The file picker itself is owned by
 625 |     /// the View (code-behind — Avalonia's StorageProvider needs the
 626 |     /// TopLevel); the View calls this method with the chosen path.
 627 |     ///
 628 |     /// <para>
 629 |     /// Flow: load the sheet via <see cref="CharacterSheetStore.LoadByPath"/>,
 630 |     /// create a fresh <see cref="DefaultWorld"/>, replace the auto-spawned
 631 |     /// «Странник» with a player built from the sheet (name, race, class,
 632 |     /// background, attributes, level, xp, inventory + equipment
 633 |     /// materialized from the sheet's item template ids via the new
 634 |     /// world's content registry), persist as a new save, and navigate
 635 |     /// into the game.
 636 |     /// </para>
 637 |     /// </summary>
 638 |     /// <param name="filePath">
 639 |     /// Absolute path to the .pathstone-char / .json sheet file picked by
 640 |     /// the user. Null / empty (e.g. user cancelled the picker) is a
 641 |     /// silent no-op.
 642 |     /// </param>
 643 |     public async Task ImportCharacterFromFileAsync(string? filePath)
 644 |     {
 645 |         if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(filePath)) return; // picker cancelled
 646 |         IsBusy = true;
 647 |         ErrorMessage = null;
 648 |         try
 649 |         {
 650 |             var store = ServiceHost.Resolve<CharacterSheetStore>();
 651 |             var sheet = store.LoadByPath(filePath);
 652 |             if (sheet is null)
 653 |             {
 654 |                 ErrorMessage = "Не удалось прочитать файл персонажа. Проверьте, что это корректный экспорт Pathstone (.json).";
 655 |                 return;
 656 |             }
 657 |             if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(sheet.Name))
 658 |             {
 659 |                 ErrorMessage = "Файл персонажа не содержит имени — импорт невозможен.";
 660 |                 return;
 661 |             }
 662 | 
 663 |             var world = DefaultWorld.Create();
 664 |             // Capture the auto-spawned player's location so the imported
 665 |             // player starts at the same spot (the starting village).
 666 |             var defaultPlayer = world.Players.FirstOrDefault();
 667 |             var startLocId = defaultPlayer?.LocationId
 668 |                 ?? (world.Locations.FirstOrDefault()?.Id ?? default);
 669 |             world.Players.Clear();
 670 |             world.ActivePlayerId = null;
 671 | 
 672 |             var player = EntityFactory.CreatePlayer(new()
 673 |             {
 674 |                 Name = sheet.Name,
 675 |                 Race = sheet.Race,
 676 |                 Class = sheet.Class,
 677 |                 Level = sheet.Level,
 678 |                 Attributes = sheet.Attributes,
 679 |                 Resources = sheet.Resources,
 680 |                 LocationId = startLocId,
 681 |                 ProficientSkills = sheet.ProficientSkills,
 682 |                 Background = sheet.Background,
 683 |                 Speed = sheet.Speed,
 684 |                 StartingCurrency = 25, // default starting gold
 685 |             }, world.Ruleset);
 686 |             player.Experience = sheet.Xp;
 687 | 
 688 |             // Materialize inventory items from template ids. Templates
 689 |             // missing in the new world's content registry are silently
 690 |             // skipped (the player just doesn't get that item — better
 691 |             // than failing the whole import).
 692 |             foreach (var tplId in sheet.InventoryItemIds)
 693 |             {
 694 |                 if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(tplId)) continue;
 695 |                 var tpl = world.Registries.Items.Get(tplId);
 696 |                 if (tpl is null) continue;
 697 |                 player.Inventory.Items.Add(EntityFactory.InstantiateItem(tpl));
 698 |             }
 699 |             // Materialize equipped items (slot → template id).
 700 |             foreach (var kv in sheet.EquippedItemIds)
 701 |             {
 702 |                 if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(kv.Value)) continue;
 703 |                 var tpl = world.Registries.Items.Get(kv.Value);
 704 |                 if (tpl is null) continue;
 705 |                 player.Equipped[kv.Key] = EntityFactory.InstantiateItem(tpl);
 706 |             }
 707 | 
 708 |             world.SpawnPlayer(player);
 709 | 
 710 |             var profile = _profileStore.GetOrCreate();
 711 |             var title = $"Импорт: {sheet.Name}";
 712 |             var meta = _saveManager.CreateSave(title, world, profile.Id);
 713 |             await _shell.NavigateToGame(meta.Id);
 714 |         }
 715 |         catch (Exception ex)
 716 |         {
 717 |             ErrorMessage = $"Не удалось импортировать персонажа: {ex.Message}";
 718 |         }
 719 |         finally
 720 |         {
 721 |             IsBusy = false;
 722 |         }
 723 |     }
 724 | 
 725 |     /// <summary>
 726 |     /// Import a .pathstone-world file into a new save (issue #33).
 727 |     /// </summary>
 728 |     public async Task ImportWorldFromFileAsync(string? filePath)
 729 |     {
 730 |         if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(filePath)) return;
 731 |         try
 732 |         {
 733 |             var newSaveId = _saveManager.ImportSave(filePath);
 734 |             if (newSaveId is null)
 735 |             {
 736 |                 ErrorMessage = "Не удалось импортировать мир.";
 737 |                 return;
 738 |             }
 739 |             RefreshSaves();
 740 |         }
 741 |         catch (Exception ex)
 742 |         {
 743 |             ErrorMessage = $"Импорт не удался: {ex.Message}";
 744 |         }
 745 |         await Task.CompletedTask;
 746 |     }
 747 | 
 748 |     /// <summary>
 749 |     /// Export a save to a file path (issue #33). The file path comes from
 750 |     /// the code-behind StorageProvider save picker.
 751 |     /// </summary>
 752 |     public async Task ExportSaveToPathAsync(string saveId, string outputPath)
 753 |     {
 754 |         try
 755 |         {
 756 |             _saveManager.ExportSave(saveId, outputPath);
 757 |         }
 758 |         catch (Exception ex)
 759 |         {
 760 |             ErrorMessage = $"Экспорт не удался: {ex.Message}";
 761 |         }
 762 |         await Task.CompletedTask;
 763 |     }
 764 | 
 765 |     private bool CanNavigate() => !IsBusy;
 766 | }
 767 | 
 768 | /// <summary>
 769 | /// One row in the «Загрузить» list. Wraps a <see cref="SaveMeta"/> with
 770 | /// display fields (name, character, world, location, turn, playtime,
 771 | /// size, engine version, timestamps) and a Load command.
 772 | /// </summary>
 773 | /// <remarks>
 774 | /// Issue #74 expanded the row's display fields beyond the original
 775 | /// Name / Character / World / Turn / UpdatedAt. The new fields:
 776 | /// <list type="bullet">
 777 | ///   <item><see cref="WorldTitle"/> — from <see cref="SaveMeta.WorldTitle"/>
 778 | ///     (the in-fiction world name).</item>
 779 | ///   <item><see cref="LocationName"/> — from <see cref="SaveMeta.LocationName"/>
 780 | ///     (current player location).</item>
 781 | ///   <item><see cref="Playtime"/> — formatted «Xч Ym» from
 782 | ///     <see cref="SaveMeta.PlaytimeMs"/>.</item>
 783 | ///   <item><see cref="SaveSize"/> — total size of the save directory's
 784 | ///     files, formatted «X KB» / «X MB».</item>
 785 | ///   <item><see cref="EngineVersion"/> — small/muted, from
 786 | ///     <see cref="SaveMeta.EngineVersion"/>.</item>
 787 | /// </list>
 788 | /// </remarks>
 789 | public sealed class SaveSlotViewModel : ObservableObject
 790 | {
 791 |     private readonly SaveMeta _meta;
 792 |     private readonly Action<string> _load;
 793 |     private readonly Action<string>? _rebuild;
 794 |     private bool _isSelected;
 795 | 
 796 |     public SaveSlotViewModel(SaveMeta meta, string saveDirectoryPath, Action<string> load, Action<string>? rebuild = null, Action<string>? exportSave = null)
 797 |     {
 798 |         _meta = meta ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(meta));
 799 |         _load = load ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(load));
 800 |         _rebuild = rebuild;
 801 |         SaveDirectoryPath = saveDirectoryPath ?? string.Empty;
 802 |         LoadCommand = new RelayCommand(() => _load(_meta.Id));
 803 |         RebuildCommand = rebuild is null ? null : new RelayCommand(() => rebuild(_meta.Id));
 804 |         ExportCommand = exportSave is null ? null : new RelayCommand(() => exportSave(_meta.Id));
 805 |         _cachedSizeBytes = ComputeSaveSize();
 806 |     }
 807 | 
 808 |     /// <summary>
 809 |     /// Absolute path to the save directory on disk. Used to compute
 810 |     /// <see cref="SaveSize"/>. Empty string when not provided (defensive
 811 |     /// — the save browser always passes the real path).
 812 |     /// </summary>
 813 |     public string SaveDirectoryPath { get; }
 814 | 
 815 |     /// <summary>The save's stable id (e.g. <c>save_…</c>). Used for
 816 |     /// multi-select delete + the Load command.</summary>
 817 |     public string Id => _meta.Id;
 818 | 
 819 |     public string Name => string.IsNullOrEmpty(_meta.Name) ? "(без названия)" : _meta.Name;
 820 | 
 821 |     public string Character => string.IsNullOrEmpty(_meta.CharacterName)
 822 |         ? "—"
 823 |         : $"{_meta.CharacterName} (ур. {_meta.CharacterLevel?.ToString() ?? "?"})";
 824 | 
 825 |     /// <summary>In-fiction world title (e.g. «Долина Туманов»). Falls
 826 |     /// back to location name when the world title is empty (covers
 827 |     /// older saves that didn't track WorldTitle separately).</summary>
 828 |     public string World => _meta.WorldTitle ?? _meta.LocationName ?? "—";
 829 | 
 830 |     /// <summary>World title from <see cref="SaveMeta.WorldTitle"/>
 831 |     /// (issue #74). «—» when empty.</summary>
 832 |     public string WorldTitle => string.IsNullOrEmpty(_meta.WorldTitle) ? "—" : _meta.WorldTitle!;
 833 | 
 834 |     /// <summary>Current location name from <see cref="SaveMeta.LocationName"/>
 835 |     /// (issue #74). «—» when empty.</summary>
 836 |     public string LocationName => string.IsNullOrEmpty(_meta.LocationName) ? "—" : _meta.LocationName!;
 837 | 
 838 |     /// <summary>Formatted updated-at timestamp («yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm», local time).</summary>
 839 |     public string UpdatedAt => _meta.UpdatedAt.ToLocalTime().ToString("yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm");
 840 | 
 841 |     /// <summary>Formatted created-at timestamp («yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm», local time).</summary>
 842 |     public string CreatedAt => _meta.CreatedAt.ToLocalTime().ToString("yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm");
 843 | 
 844 |     /// <summary>Raw updated-at timestamp (UTC) — used for sorting
 845 |     /// without re-parsing the formatted string.</summary>
 846 |     public DateTimeOffset UpdatedAtRaw => _meta.UpdatedAt;
 847 | 
 848 |     /// <summary>Raw created-at timestamp (UTC) — used for sorting.</summary>
 849 |     public DateTimeOffset CreatedAtRaw => _meta.CreatedAt;
 850 | 
 851 |     public string TurnInfo => $"Ход {_meta.Turn}";
 852 | 
 853 |     /// <summary>
 854 |     /// Cumulative playtime formatted as «Xч Ym» (or «Ym» / «Xч» when
 855 |     /// one component is zero). Issue #74.
 856 |     /// </summary>
 857 |     public string Playtime => FormatPlaytime(_meta.PlaytimeMs);
 858 | 
 859 |     /// <summary>
 860 |     /// Total size of the save directory's files, formatted as
 861 |     /// «X KB» / «X MB». Issue #74. Computed once on construction (see
 862 |     /// <see cref="ComputeSaveSize"/>) and cached — save files don't
 863 |     /// change size while the menu is open.
 864 |     /// </summary>
 865 |     public string SaveSize => FormatSize(_cachedSizeBytes);
 866 | 
 867 |     private readonly long _cachedSizeBytes;
 868 | 
 869 |     /// <summary>
 870 |     /// Engine version that wrote this save (e.g. «0.2.0»). Shown small
 871 |     /// and muted so the user can identify which saves were written by
 872 |     /// older/newer engines.
 873 |     /// </summary>
 874 |     public string EngineVersion => string.IsNullOrEmpty(_meta.EngineVersion) ? "—" : _meta.EngineVersion;
 875 | 
 876 |     /// <summary>
 877 |     /// Checkbox state for multi-select delete (issue #74). Two-way
 878 |     /// bound to a CheckBox in the save row template. Notifies the
 879 |     /// parent MainMenuViewModel via the <see cref="SelectionChanged"/>
 880 |     /// event so the «Удалить выбранные» button's CanExecute can refresh.
 881 |     /// </summary>
 882 |     public bool IsSelected
 883 |     {
 884 |         get => _isSelected;
 885 |         set
 886 |         {
 887 |             if (SetProperty(ref _isSelected, value))
 888 |                 SelectionChanged?.Invoke(this);
 889 |         }
 890 |     }
 891 | 
 892 |     /// <summary>
 893 |     /// Raised when <see cref="IsSelected"/> changes. The parent
 894 |     /// <see cref="MainMenuViewModel"/> subscribes to refresh its
 895 |     /// <c>HasSelectedSaves</c> computed property.
 896 |     /// </summary>
 897 |     public event Action<SaveSlotViewModel>? SelectionChanged;
 898 | 
 899 |     public ICommand LoadCommand { get; }
 900 | 
 901 |     /// <summary>
 902 |     /// Rebuild command (issue #23). Opens the rebuild dialog for this
 903 |     /// save. Null when the parent menu didn't wire a rebuild action
 904 |     /// (the button is hidden in that case via IsVisible binding).
 905 |     /// </summary>
 906 |     public ICommand? RebuildCommand { get; }
 907 |     public ICommand? ExportCommand { get; }
 908 | 
 909 |     /// <summary>
 910 |     /// True when the rebuild button should be visible (the parent menu
 911 |     /// wired a rebuild action). Bound to the rebuild button's IsVisible.
 912 |     /// </summary>
 913 |     public bool CanRebuild => RebuildCommand is not null;
 914 | 
 915 |     // ─── Formatting helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────
 916 | 
 917 |     /// <summary>
 918 |     /// Format playtime as «Xч Ym» (or «Ym» / «Xч» when one component is
 919 |     /// zero). Returns «0м» for a fresh save (no playtime yet).
 920 |     /// </summary>
 921 |     private static string FormatPlaytime(long ms)
 922 |     {
 923 |         if (ms <= 0) return "0м";
 924 |         var ts = TimeSpan.FromMilliseconds(ms);
 925 |         int hours = (int)ts.TotalHours;
 926 |         int mins = ts.Minutes;
 927 |         if (hours > 0 && mins > 0) return $"{hours}ч {mins}м";
 928 |         if (hours > 0) return $"{hours}ч";
 929 |         return $"{mins}м";
 930 |     }
 931 | 
 932 |     /// <summary>
 933 |     /// Format a byte count as «X B» / «X KB» / «X MB». Returns «—» for
 934 |     /// zero / negative (covers the case where the save directory can't
 935 |     /// be read — the user sees a placeholder instead of «0 KB»).
 936 |     /// </summary>
 937 |     private static string FormatSize(long bytes)
 938 |     {
 939 |         if (bytes <= 0) return "—";
 940 |         if (bytes < 1024) return $"{bytes} B";
 941 |         if (bytes < 1024L * 1024) return $"{bytes / 1024.0:F1} KB";
 942 |         return $"{bytes / (1024.0 * 1024):F1} MB";
 943 |     }
 944 | 
 945 |     /// <summary>
 946 |     /// Compute the total size of the save directory's files
 947 |     /// (recursive). Uses <see cref="Directory.GetFiles(string, string, EnumerationOptions)"/>
 948 |     /// with the AllDirectories option so nested files (none currently,
 949 |     /// but defensive for future layout changes) are counted. Swallows
 950 |     /// IO errors (locked files, permission denied, …) — returns 0 on
 951 |     /// any failure so the row shows «—» instead of crashing the menu.
 952 |     /// </summary>
 953 |     private long ComputeSaveSize()
 954 |     {
 955 |         try
 956 |         {
 957 |             if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(SaveDirectoryPath)) return 0;
 958 |             if (!Directory.Exists(SaveDirectoryPath)) return 0;
 959 |             // Spec: use Directory.GetFiles(saveDir).Sum(f => new FileInfo(f).Length).
 960 |             // We extend to recursive enumeration so a future layout
 961 |             // with subdirectories (e.g. assets/, snapshots/) is also
 962 |             // counted. The non-recursive case is a subset.
 963 |             var files = Directory.GetFiles(SaveDirectoryPath, "*", new EnumerationOptions
 964 |             {
 965 |                 RecurseSubdirectories = true,
 966 |                 IgnoreInaccessible = true,
 967 |             });
 968 |             long total = 0;
 969 |             foreach (var f in files)
 970 |             {
 971 |                 try { total += new FileInfo(f).Length; }
 972 |                 catch { /* skip locked / vanished file */ }
 973 |             }
 974 |             return total;
 975 |         }
 976 |         catch
 977 |         {
 978 |             return 0;
 979 |         }
 980 |     }
 981 | }

  39 |         // The nickname is shown at the top of every screen — keep a

  42 |         // First-run note (issue #73): if no profile.json exists yet, we
  43 |         // DON'T call GetOrCreate here — that would create the profile as

  56 |                 _currentNickname = p.Nickname;
  57 |             }

  61 |             }

 241 |     {

 245 |         vm.LoadStateForResume(state, brief ?? string.Empty);
 246 |         CurrentView = vm;
 247 |         // Auto-start the build on navigation. StartAsync will see the
 248 |         // staged state and call orchestrator.LoadState() before RunAsync.
 249 |         vm.StartCommand.Execute(null);
 250 |     }

 322 |         string? saveId,
 323 |         bool isHost,
 324 |         bool standaloneSinglePlayer,

 326 |         int port)

 330 |             _settingsStore,
 331 |             _saveManager,
 332 |             this,

 337 |             port: port);

--- src/MyGame.Desktop/ViewModels/OnboardingViewModel.cs (C#, 238 lines, ~2,404 tokens) ---
   1 | using System;
   2 | using System.Threading.Tasks;
   3 | using CommunityToolkit.Mvvm.ComponentModel;
   4 | using CommunityToolkit.Mvvm.Input;
   5 | using MyGame.Core.Profile;
   6 | 
   7 | namespace MyGame.Desktop.ViewModels;
   8 | 
   9 | /// <summary>
  10 | /// First-run welcome wizard (issue #73). Three steps:
  11 | /// <list type="number">
  12 | ///   <item><b>Nickname</b> — validate + persist via
  13 | ///     <see cref="ProfileStore.Rename"/>. The user can't skip this step
  14 | ///     (a valid nickname is required to play).</item>
  15 | ///   <item><b>AI setup</b> — optional API key entry for the OpenAI-
  16 | ///     compatible provider. «Пропустить» lets the user defer to the
  17 | ///     Settings screen; «Далее» saves the key to
  18 | ///     <see cref="Settings.Ai.ApiKey"/> via <see cref="SettingsStore.Update"/>.
  19 | ///     A «Расширенные настройки» link opens the Settings screen for
  20 | ///     BaseUrl / model / per-role overrides.</item>
  21 | ///   <item><b>Ready</b> — short summary of what the user can do next
  22 | ///     (build a world via AI, start a standard game, join a friend).
  23 | ///     «Начать» navigates to the main menu.</item>
  24 | /// </list>
  25 | ///
  26 | /// <para>
  27 | /// The wizard is shown by <see cref="MainViewModel.NavigateToMenu"/> when
  28 | /// <see cref="ProfileStore.ProfileExists"/> returns false (no
  29 | /// <c>profile.json</c> yet). On finishing step 1 the profile is created
  30 | /// (Rename calls GetOrCreate internally), so the next launch goes
  31 | /// straight to the menu.
  32 | /// </para>
  33 | /// <para>
  34 | /// Step transitions fire <see cref="OnStepChanged"/> so the view can
  35 | /// refresh its <c>IsStep1/2/3</c> visibility bindings.
  36 | /// </para>
  37 | /// </summary>
  38 | public partial class OnboardingViewModel : ViewModelBase
  39 | {
  40 |     private readonly MainViewModel _shell;
  41 |     private readonly ProfileStore _profileStore;
  42 |     private readonly SettingsStore _settingsStore;
  43 | 
  44 |     private int _step = 1;
  45 |     private string _nickname = string.Empty;
  46 |     private string? _nicknameError;
  47 |     private string? _apiKey;
  48 |     private string? _apiKeyError;
  49 | 
  50 |     public OnboardingViewModel(
  51 |         MainViewModel shell,
  52 |         ProfileStore profileStore,
  53 |         SettingsStore settingsStore)
  54 |     {
  55 |         _shell = shell ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(shell));
  56 |         _profileStore = profileStore ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(profileStore));
  57 |         _settingsStore = settingsStore ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(settingsStore));
  58 | 
  59 |         // Pre-fill the nickname with a random suggestion (NOT loaded
  60 |         // from a profile — we intentionally DON'T call GetOrCreate here
  61 |         // because that would create profile.json as a side effect, and
  62 |         // we want the first-run check (ProfileStore.ProfileExists) to
  63 |         // remain false until the user actually confirms their nickname
  64 |         // on step 1). The user can keep the suggestion or overwrite it.
  65 |         _nickname = ProfileStore.GenerateRandomNickname();
  66 | 
  67 |         Title = "Добро пожаловать";
  68 |     }
  69 | 
  70 |     // ─── Step state ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
  71 | 
  72 |     /// <summary>
  73 |     /// Current step (1, 2, or 3). Bound to the view's step visibility
  74 |     /// via <see cref="IsStep1"/> / <see cref="IsStep2"/> / <see cref="IsStep3"/>.
  75 |     /// </summary>
  76 |     public int Step
  77 |     {
  78 |         get => _step;
  79 |         private set
  80 |         {
  81 |             if (SetProperty(ref _step, value))
  82 |             {
  83 |                 OnPropertyChanged(nameof(IsStep1));
  84 |                 OnPropertyChanged(nameof(IsStep2));
  85 |                 OnPropertyChanged(nameof(IsStep3));
  86 |             }
  87 |         }
  88 |     }
  89 | 
  90 |     public bool IsStep1 => Step == 1;
  91 |     public bool IsStep2 => Step == 2;
  92 |     public bool IsStep3 => Step == 3;
  93 | 
  94 |     // ─── Step 1: nickname ─────────────────────────────────────────────
  95 | 
  96 |     /// <summary>
  97 |     /// Editable nickname. Pre-filled with the auto-generated profile
  98 |     /// nickname. Validated (2-20 chars, Latin/Cyrillic letters, digits,
  99 |     /// spaces, hyphens, underscores — see <see cref="Profile.ValidateNickname"/>)
 100 |     /// on «Далее».
 101 |     /// </summary>
 102 |     public string Nickname
 103 |     {
 104 |         get => _nickname;
 105 |         set => SetProperty(ref _nickname, value);
 106 |     }
 107 | 
 108 |     /// <summary>
 109 |     /// Inline validation error for the nickname. Null when the current
 110 |     /// value is valid or unchanged.
 111 |     /// </summary>
 112 |     public string? NicknameError
 113 |     {
 114 |         get => _nicknameError;
 115 |         private set => SetProperty(ref _nicknameError, value);
 116 |     }
 117 | 
 118 |     // ─── Step 2: AI key ───────────────────────────────────────────────
 119 | 
 120 |     /// <summary>
 121 |     /// Optional API key. Saved to <see cref="Settings.Ai.ApiKey"/> on
 122 |     /// «Далее». The user can skip this step (key can be added later in
 123 |     /// Settings).
 124 |     /// </summary>
 125 |     public string? ApiKey
 126 |     {
 127 |         get => _apiKey;
 128 |         set => SetProperty(ref _apiKey, value);
 129 |     }
 130 | 
 131 |     /// <summary>Inline error for the API key (currently unused — kept
 132 |     /// for future validation). Null when no error.</summary>
 133 |     public string? ApiKeyError
 134 |     {
 135 |         get => _apiKeyError;
 136 |         private set => SetProperty(ref _apiKeyError, value);
 137 |     }
 138 | 
 139 |     // ─── Commands ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 140 | 
 141 |     /// <summary>
 142 |     /// Step 1 «Далее»: validate + persist the nickname, then advance to
 143 |     /// step 2. On validation failure, sets <see cref="NicknameError"/>
 144 |     /// and stays on step 1.
 145 |     /// </summary>
 146 |     [RelayCommand]
 147 |     private void NextFromStep1()
 148 |     {
 149 |         var trimmed = (_nickname ?? string.Empty).Trim();
 150 |         if (!Profile.ValidateNickname(trimmed, out var error))
 151 |         {
 152 |             NicknameError = error;
 153 |             return;
 154 |         }
 155 |         try
 156 |         {
 157 |             // GetOrCreate creates profile.json on first call (the file
 158 |             // didn't exist before this — that's the whole point of the
 159 |             // first-run wizard). Rename then validates + applies the
 160 |             // user's chosen nickname. After this call, profile.json
 161 |             // exists on disk, so the next NavigateToMenu call will
 162 |             // skip the wizard.
 163 |             _profileStore.GetOrCreate();
 164 |             _profileStore.Rename(trimmed);
 165 |             Nickname = trimmed; // normalize (trim)
 166 |             NicknameError = null;
 167 |             Step = 2;
 168 |         }
 169 |         catch (Exception ex)
 170 |         {
 171 |             NicknameError = ex.Message;
 172 |         }
 173 |     }
 174 | 
 175 |     /// <summary>
 176 |     /// Step 2 «Пропустить»: skip API key entry (the user can add it
 177 |     /// later in Settings). Advance to step 3 without persisting.
 178 |     /// </summary>
 179 |     [RelayCommand]
 180 |     private void SkipStep2()
 181 |     {
 182 |         ApiKeyError = null;
 183 |         Step = 3;
 184 |     }
 185 | 
 186 |     /// <summary>
 187 |     /// Step 2 «Далее»: persist the API key (when non-empty) to
 188 |     /// <see cref="Settings.Ai.ApiKey"/> via <see cref="SettingsStore.Update"/>,
 189 |     /// then advance to step 3. An empty key is treated the same as
 190 |     /// «Пропустить» — the user can add it later.
 191 |     /// </summary>
 192 |     [RelayCommand]
 193 |     private void NextFromStep2()
 194 |     {
 195 |         try
 196 |         {
 197 |             var key = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(_apiKey) ? null : _apiKey.Trim();
 198 |             _settingsStore.Update(s => s with
 199 |             {
 200 |                 Ai = s.Ai with { ApiKey = key },
 201 |             });
 202 |             ApiKey = key;
 203 |             ApiKeyError = null;
 204 |             Step = 3;
 205 |         }
 206 |         catch (Exception ex)
 207 |         {
 208 |             ApiKeyError = ex.Message;
 209 |         }
 210 |     }
 211 | 
 212 |     /// <summary>
 213 |     /// Step 2 «Расширенные настройки»: open the Settings screen so the
 214 |     /// user can configure BaseUrl, model, per-role overrides, etc. The
 215 |     /// wizard's state is preserved — when the user navigates back to the
 216 |     /// menu (via Settings → Сохранить or Отмена), NavigateToMenu sees
 217 |     /// the profile already exists and shows the menu (skipping the
 218 |     /// wizard). The user can re-enter the wizard only by deleting
 219 |     /// profile.json manually.
 220 |     /// </summary>
 221 |     /// <remarks>
 222 |     /// Practically, clicking this link abandons the wizard — the user
 223 |     /// ends up on the Settings screen, then on the menu. The wizard's
 224 |     /// step 3 (Ready) is a one-tap gateway from there. Acceptable UX
 225 |     /// for a first-run flow.
 226 |     /// </remarks>
 227 |     [RelayCommand]
 228 |     private void OpenSettings() => _shell.NavigateToSettings();
 229 | 
 230 |     /// <summary>
 231 |     /// Step 3 «Начать»: navigate to the main menu. By this point the
 232 |     /// profile has been created (step 1) and the API key optionally
 233 |     /// persisted (step 2).
 234 |     /// </summary>
 235 |     [RelayCommand]
 236 |     private void Finish() => _shell.NavigateToMenu();
 237 | }

--- src/MyGame.Desktop/ViewModels/Panels/CharacterPanelViewModel.cs (C#, 205 lines, ~2,316 tokens) ---
   1 | using System;
   2 | using System.Collections.Generic;
   3 | using System.Collections.ObjectModel;
   4 | using System.Linq;
   5 | using CommunityToolkit.Mvvm.ComponentModel;
   6 | using CommunityToolkit.Mvvm.Input;
