   8 | using MyGame.Core.World;
   9 | using MyGame.Core.World.Entities;
  10 | 
  11 | namespace MyGame.Desktop.ViewModels.Panels;
  12 | 
  13 | /// <summary>
  14 | /// View model for the character sheet panel. Shows the active player's
  15 | /// identity, attributes, resources (HP/MP/etc.), equipped gear, skills,
  16 | /// background. Read-only — no commands (character editing happens via the
  17 | /// GM tool flow, not direct UI manipulation) — EXCEPT for the
  18 | /// <see cref="ExportCommand"/> / <see cref="ExportRequested"/> event,
  19 | /// which lets the user export this character as a portable
  20 | /// <c>CharacterSheet</c> (issue #62). The actual export call (which needs
  21 | /// the active save id + player id) is handled by the
  22 | /// <see cref="GameViewModel"/>, which subscribes to
  23 | /// <see cref="ExportRequested"/> and resolves the
  24 | /// <c>CharacterSheetStore</c> from DI.
  25 | /// </summary>
  26 | public partial class CharacterPanelViewModel : ObservableObject
  27 | {
  28 |     private Player? _player;
  29 | 
  30 |     /// <summary>
  31 |     /// Raised when the user clicks the «Экспорт» button. The
  32 |     /// <see cref="GameViewModel"/> subscribes and handles the actual
  33 |     /// <c>CharacterSheetStore.Export</c> call (it has the save id + active
  34 |     /// player). The handler should set <see cref="ExportStatus"/> to a
  35 |     /// success/error message so the panel shows the result inline.
  36 |     /// </summary>
  37 |     public event Action? ExportRequested;
  38 | 
  39 |     /// <summary>
  40 |     /// Fire <see cref="ExportRequested"/>. Bound to the «Экспорт» button
  41 |     /// in the identity section. No-op if no handler is attached (the
  42 |     /// panel might be shown outside a game session, e.g. in a future
  43 |     /// read-only character viewer).
  44 |     /// </summary>
  45 |     [RelayCommand]
  46 |     private void Export()
  47 |     {
  48 |         if (_player is null)
  49 |         {
  50 |             ExportStatus = "Нет активного персонажа для экспорта.";
  51 |             return;
  52 |         }
  53 |         ExportRequested?.Invoke();
  54 |     }
  55 | 
  56 |     /// <summary>
  57 |     /// Last export result message (success path summary or error). Bound
  58 |     /// to a small status TextBlock under the «Экспорт» button. Cleared
  59 |     /// on the next <see cref="RefreshFromWorld"/> call (so stale messages
  60 |     /// don't linger after a turn). Null hides the block.
  61 |     /// </summary>
  62 |     [ObservableProperty] private string? _exportStatus;
  63 | 
  64 |     /// <summary>
  65 |     /// Refresh the panel from the given world. Reads the active player
  66 |     /// (or the first player if no active is set) and rebuilds all
  67 |     /// observable collections.
  68 |     /// </summary>
  69 |     public void RefreshFromWorld(World world)
  70 |     {
  71 |         if (world is null) { Clear(); return; }
  72 |         _player = world.ActivePlayer ?? world.Players.FirstOrDefault();
  73 |         if (_player is null) { Clear(); return; }
  74 | 
  75 |         var p = _player;
  76 |         Name = p.Name;
  77 |         Race = p.Race ?? "—";
  78 |         Class = p.Class ?? "—";
  79 |         var lvl = p.Level ?? 1;
  80 |         Level = lvl.ToString();
  81 |         // XP / leveling. NextLevelXp uses the simple "level * 100" curve.
  82 |         // The award_xp tool handles the actual XP grant + level-up math;
  83 |         // the panel just reflects the current totals.
  84 |         Experience = p.Experience ?? 0;
  85 |         NextLevelXp = lvl * 100;
  86 |         Background = p.Background ?? "—";
  87 |         Speed = p.Speed?.ToString() ?? "30";
  88 | 
  89 |         Attributes.Clear();
  90 |         foreach (var kv in p.Attributes.OrderBy(kv => kv.Key))
  91 |             Attributes.Add(new AttributeRow(kv.Key, kv.Value));
  92 | 
  93 |         Resources.Clear();
  94 |         foreach (var kv in p.Resources.OrderBy(kv => kv.Key))
  95 |             Resources.Add(new ResourceRow(kv.Key, kv.Value));
  96 | 
  97 |         Equipped.Clear();
  98 |         foreach (var kv in p.Equipped)
  99 |             Equipped.Add(new EquippedRow(kv.Key, kv.Value.Name, kv.Value.Description ?? ""));
 100 | 
 101 |         // Active status effects. Populated from the character's Effects list.
 102 |         // Each effect is rendered as a row in the «Эффекты» section (below
 103 |         // Equipped, above Skills). Hidden entirely when no effects are active.
 104 |         Effects.Clear();
 105 |         if (p.Effects is not null)
 106 |         {
 107 |             foreach (var eff in p.Effects)
 108 |             {
 109 |                 Effects.Add(new StatusEffectRow(
 110 |                     eff.Name,
 111 |                     eff.Description ?? "",
 112 |                     eff.Duration));
 113 |             }
 114 |         }
 115 | 
 116 |         ProficientSkills.Clear();
 117 |         if (p.ProficientSkills is not null)
 118 |             foreach (var s in p.ProficientSkills)
 119 |                 ProficientSkills.Add(s);
 120 | 
 121 |         Currency = p.Inventory.Currency;
 122 |         InventoryCount = p.Inventory.Items.Count;
 123 |         OnPropertyChanged(nameof(HasAttributes));
 124 |         OnPropertyChanged(nameof(HasResources));
 125 |         OnPropertyChanged(nameof(HasEquipped));
 126 |         OnPropertyChanged(nameof(HasEffects));
 127 |         OnPropertyChanged(nameof(HasSkills));
 128 |         OnPropertyChanged(nameof(XpProgress));
 129 | 
 130 |         // Clear the export-status toast on every refresh — the next
 131 |         // export will set a fresh message.
 132 |         ExportStatus = null;
 133 |     }
 134 | 
 135 |     private void Clear()
 136 |     {
 137 |         Name = Race = Class = Level = Background = Speed = "—";
 138 |         Experience = 0;
 139 |         NextLevelXp = 100;
 140 |         Attributes.Clear();
 141 |         Resources.Clear();
 142 |         Equipped.Clear();
 143 |         Effects.Clear();
 144 |         ProficientSkills.Clear();
 145 |         Currency = 0;
 146 |         InventoryCount = 0;
 147 |         OnPropertyChanged(nameof(HasAttributes));
 148 |         OnPropertyChanged(nameof(HasResources));
 149 |         OnPropertyChanged(nameof(HasEquipped));
 150 |         OnPropertyChanged(nameof(HasEffects));
 151 |         OnPropertyChanged(nameof(HasSkills));
 152 |         OnPropertyChanged(nameof(XpProgress));
 153 |     }
 154 | 
 155 |     // ─── Observable properties ───────────────────────────────────────
 156 | 
 157 |     [ObservableProperty] private string _name = "—";
 158 |     [ObservableProperty] private string _race = "—";
 159 |     [ObservableProperty] private string _class = "—";
 160 |     [ObservableProperty] private string _level = "1";
 161 |     [ObservableProperty] private string _background = "—";
 162 |     [ObservableProperty] private string _speed = "30";
 163 |     [ObservableProperty] private int _currency;
 164 |     [ObservableProperty] private int _inventoryCount;
 165 | 
 166 |     /// <summary>Total XP the player has accumulated so far.</summary>
 167 |     [ObservableProperty] private int _experience;
 168 | 
 169 |     /// <summary>XP threshold to reach the next level (level * 100).</summary>
 170 |     [ObservableProperty] private int _nextLevelXp = 100;
 171 | 
 172 |     /// <summary>Progress 0-100 toward the next level, for the XP bar.</summary>
 173 |     public double XpProgress => NextLevelXp > 0
 174 |         ? System.Math.Min(100.0, (Experience / (double)NextLevelXp) * 100.0)
 175 |         : 0;
 176 | 
 177 |     public ObservableCollection<AttributeRow> Attributes { get; } = new();
 178 |     public ObservableCollection<ResourceRow> Resources { get; } = new();
 179 |     public ObservableCollection<EquippedRow> Equipped { get; } = new();
 180 |     public ObservableCollection<StatusEffectRow> Effects { get; } = new();
 181 |     public ObservableCollection<string> ProficientSkills { get; } = new();
 182 | 
 183 |     public bool HasAttributes => Attributes.Count > 0;
 184 |     public bool HasResources => Resources.Count > 0;
 185 |     public bool HasEquipped => Equipped.Count > 0;
 186 |     public bool HasEffects => Effects.Count > 0;
 187 |     public bool HasSkills => ProficientSkills.Count > 0;
 188 | }
 189 | 
 190 | /// <summary>One row in the attributes grid (e.g. STR=16).</summary>
 191 | public sealed record AttributeRow(string Name, int Value);
 192 | 
 193 | /// <summary>One row in the resources list (e.g. HP=12/12, MP=5/5).</summary>
 194 | public sealed record ResourceRow(string Name, int Value);
 195 | 
 196 | /// <summary>One row in the equipped-gear list (slot → item name).</summary>
 197 | public sealed record EquippedRow(string Slot, string ItemName, string Description);
 198 | 
 199 | /// <summary>
 200 | /// One active status effect on the character. Duration &lt; 0 means
 201 | /// «until dispelled»; 0 means «expired» (will be reaped on the next tick);
 202 | /// positive values are rounds remaining.
 203 | /// </summary>
 204 | public sealed record StatusEffectRow(string Name, string Description, int Duration);

--- src/MyGame.Desktop/ViewModels/Panels/InventoryPanelViewModel.cs (C#, 320 lines, ~3,648 tokens) ---
   1 | using System;
   2 | using System.Collections.ObjectModel;
   3 | using System.Linq;
   4 | using CommunityToolkit.Mvvm.ComponentModel;
   5 | using CommunityToolkit.Mvvm.Input;
   6 | using MyGame.Core.Common;
   7 | using MyGame.Core.World;
   8 | using MyGame.Core.World.Entities;
   9 | 
  10 | namespace MyGame.Desktop.ViewModels.Panels;
  11 | 
  12 | /// <summary>
  13 | /// View model for the inventory panel. Lists the active player's carried
  14 | /// items (with quantity, weight, value, rarity), the equipped gear, and
  15 | /// the carrying-capacity bar. Item actions (use/equip/drop) are surfaced
  16 | /// as commands that emit <see cref="ItemActionRequested"/> events the
  17 | /// GameViewModel can wire to the GM or to direct world mutation.
  18 | ///
  19 | /// <para>
  20 | /// STACK-SPLIT (issue #64): a separate SplitCommand opens a small dialog
  21 | /// overlay (managed by the view's code-behind). The VM exposes
  22 | /// <see cref="SplitTarget"/> + <see cref="SplitQuantity"/> +
  23 | /// <see cref="IsSplitDialogOpen"/> so the view can bind the dialog's
  24 | /// fields two-way. <see cref="ConfirmSplitCommand"/> raises an
  25 | /// <see cref="ItemAction"/> with the new <see cref="ItemActionKind.Split"/>
  26 | /// kind + the split quantity in <see cref="ItemAction"/>'s Quantity
  27 | /// field; the GameViewModel handles the actual world mutation (creating a
  28 | /// new Item, decrementing the original, refreshing the panel).
  29 | /// </para>
  30 | /// </summary>
  31 | public partial class InventoryPanelViewModel : ObservableObject
  32 | {
  33 |     private Player? _player;
  34 |     private World? _world;
  35 | 
  36 |     /// <summary>
  37 |     /// Raised when the user clicks use/equip/drop on an item. The
  38 |     /// GameViewModel subscribes and either mutates the world directly
  39 |     /// (single-player) or sends a tool-call request to the host
  40 |     /// (multiplayer). The <see cref="ItemAction"/> record carries the
  41 |     /// item id + action kind (+ quantity for Split).
  42 |     /// </summary>
  43 |     public event Action<ItemAction>? ItemActionRequested;
  44 | 
  45 |     /// <summary>Refresh from the given world.</summary>
  46 |     public void RefreshFromWorld(World world)
  47 |     {
  48 |         _world = world;
  49 |         _player = world?.ActivePlayer ?? world?.Players.FirstOrDefault();
  50 |         if (_player is null) { Clear(); return; }
  51 | 
  52 |         var p = _player;
  53 |         Currency = p.Inventory.Currency;
  54 |         Capacity = p.Inventory.Capacity;
  55 | 
  56 |         // ITEM-RARITY (issue #66): look up the template for each item so
  57 |         // the row can carry the template's rarity (color-coding in the
  58 |         // view). Items whose template can't be found (e.g. loaded from a
  59 |         // pre-migration save) fall back to "common".
  60 |         var items = world?.Registries?.Items;
  61 |         Items.Clear();
  62 |         foreach (var it in p.Inventory.Items)
  63 |         {
  64 |             var tmpl = it.TemplateId is null ? null : items?.Get(it.TemplateId);
  65 |             Items.Add(new InventoryItemRow(it, rarity: tmpl?.Rarity, stackable: tmpl?.Stackable ?? false));
  66 |         }
  67 | 
  68 |         Equipped.Clear();
  69 |         foreach (var kv in p.Equipped)
  70 |         {
  71 |             var tmpl = kv.Value.TemplateId is null ? null : items?.Get(kv.Value.TemplateId);
  72 |             Equipped.Add(new InventoryItemRow(kv.Value, slot: kv.Key, rarity: tmpl?.Rarity, stackable: tmpl?.Stackable ?? false));
  73 |         }
  74 | 
  75 |         // Compute total carried weight. Stackable items contribute
  76 |         // weight * quantity; non-stackables (quantity==1) just contribute
  77 |         // their weight. Math.Max(1, qty) guards against malformed saves
  78 |         // where quantity slipped to 0 or negative.
  79 |         var total = p.Inventory.Items.Sum(i => ItemWeight(i) * Math.Max(1, i.Quantity));
  80 |         CarriedWeight = Math.Round(total, 1);
  81 |         OnPropertyChanged(nameof(WeightPercent));
  82 |         OnPropertyChanged(nameof(IsOverweight));
  83 |         OnPropertyChanged(nameof(HasItems));
  84 |         OnPropertyChanged(nameof(HasEquipped));
  85 |     }
  86 | 
  87 |     private void Clear()
  88 |     {
  89 |         Currency = 0;
  90 |         Capacity = 150;
  91 |         CarriedWeight = 0;
  92 |         Items.Clear();
  93 |         Equipped.Clear();
  94 |         OnPropertyChanged(nameof(WeightPercent));
  95 |         OnPropertyChanged(nameof(IsOverweight));
  96 |         OnPropertyChanged(nameof(HasItems));
  97 |         OnPropertyChanged(nameof(HasEquipped));
  98 |     }
  99 | 
 100 |     private static double ItemWeight(Item it)
 101 |     {
 102 |         // The Item entity carries its per-unit weight (denormalized from
 103 |         // ItemTemplate.Weight at instantiation time by EntityFactory). Items
 104 |         // loaded from pre-migration saves default to 0 here — they'll report
 105 |         // no weight until re-instantiated (see Item.Weight TODO).
 106 |         return it.Weight;
 107 |     }
 108 | 
 109 |     // ─── Observable properties ───────────────────────────────────────
 110 | 
 111 |     [ObservableProperty] private int _currency;
 112 |     [ObservableProperty] private int _capacity = 150;
 113 |     [ObservableProperty] private double _carriedWeight;
 114 | 
 115 |     public ObservableCollection<InventoryItemRow> Items { get; } = new();
 116 |     public ObservableCollection<InventoryItemRow> Equipped { get; } = new();
 117 | 
 118 |     public double WeightPercent => Capacity > 0 ? Math.Min(100.0, (CarriedWeight / Capacity) * 100.0) : 0;
 119 |     public bool IsOverweight => CarriedWeight >= Capacity;
 120 |     public bool HasItems => Items.Count > 0;
 121 |     public bool HasEquipped => Equipped.Count > 0;
 122 | 
 123 |     // ─── Stack-split dialog state (issue #64) ──────────────────────────
 124 |     //
 125 |     // The split dialog is a Border overlay in InventoryPanelView (like
 126 |     // the death / reconnect overlays in GameView). When the user clicks
 127 |     // «Разделить» on a stackable row, OpenSplit sets SplitTarget + a
 128 |     // sensible default SplitQuantity (half the stack, clamped to 1..qty-1)
 129 |     // and flips IsSplitDialogOpen=true. The view's overlay binds its
 130 |     // NumericUpDown to SplitQuantity and its title to SplitTarget.Name.
 131 |     // ConfirmSplit raises the ItemAction event with the Split kind; the
 132 |     // GameViewModel mutates the world. CancelSplit just closes the dialog.
 133 | 
 134 |     /// <summary>
 135 |     /// True when the split-stack dialog overlay is visible. Bound to the
 136 |     /// overlay Border's IsVisible so the view doesn't need code-behind
 137 |     /// for show/hide.
 138 |     /// </summary>
 139 |     [ObservableProperty] private bool _isSplitDialogOpen;
 140 | 
 141 |     /// <summary>
 142 |     /// The row the user is splitting. Null when the dialog is closed.
 143 |     /// The view binds the dialog's title to <c>SplitTarget.Name</c> +
 144 |     /// <c>SplitTarget.Quantity</c>.
 145 |     /// </summary>
 146 |     [ObservableProperty] private InventoryItemRow? _splitTarget;
 147 | 
 148 |     /// <summary>
 149 |     /// The quantity the user wants to split off into a new stack. Bound
 150 |     /// two-way to the dialog's NumericUpDown (1..SplitTarget.Quantity-1).
 151 |     /// </summary>
 152 |     [ObservableProperty] private int _splitQuantity = 1;
 153 | 
 154 |     // ─── Commands ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 155 | 
 156 |     [RelayCommand]
 157 |     private void Use(InventoryItemRow row) =>
 158 |         ItemActionRequested?.Invoke(new ItemAction(row.Id, ItemActionKind.Use));
 159 | 
 160 |     [RelayCommand]
 161 |     private void Equip(InventoryItemRow row) =>
 162 |         ItemActionRequested?.Invoke(new ItemAction(row.Id, ItemActionKind.Equip));
 163 | 
 164 |     [RelayCommand]
 165 |     private void Unequip(InventoryItemRow row) =>
 166 |         ItemActionRequested?.Invoke(new ItemAction(row.Id, ItemActionKind.Unequip));
 167 | 
 168 |     [RelayCommand]
 169 |     private void Drop(InventoryItemRow row) =>
 170 |         ItemActionRequested?.Invoke(new ItemAction(row.Id, ItemActionKind.Drop));
 171 | 
 172 |     /// <summary>
 173 |     /// Open the split-stack dialog for the given row. Defaults the split
 174 |     /// quantity to half the stack (clamped to 1..qty-1). No-op when the
 175 |     /// row isn't stackable or has fewer than 2 items.
 176 |     /// </summary>
 177 |     [RelayCommand]
 178 |     private void OpenSplit(InventoryItemRow row)
 179 |     {
 180 |         if (row is null || !row.Stackable || row.Quantity < 2) return;
 181 |         SplitTarget = row;
 182 |         // Default to half the stack, rounded down, clamped to 1..qty-1.
 183 |         var half = row.Quantity / 2;
 184 |         if (half < 1) half = 1;
 185 |         if (half > row.Quantity - 1) half = row.Quantity - 1;
 186 |         SplitQuantity = half;
 187 |         IsSplitDialogOpen = true;
 188 |     }
 189 | 
 190 |     /// <summary>
 191 |     /// Confirm the split: raise an ItemAction with the Split kind +
 192 |     /// the chosen quantity. The GameViewModel does the actual world
 193 |     /// mutation. Closes the dialog afterwards.
 194 |     /// </summary>
 195 |     [RelayCommand]
 196 |     private void ConfirmSplit()
 197 |     {
 198 |         if (SplitTarget is null) return;
 199 |         var qty = SplitQuantity;
 200 |         var max = SplitTarget.Quantity - 1;
 201 |         if (qty < 1) qty = 1;
 202 |         if (qty > max) qty = max;
 203 |         ItemActionRequested?.Invoke(new ItemAction(
 204 |             SplitTarget.Id,
 205 |             ItemActionKind.Split,
 206 |             Quantity: qty));
 207 |         IsSplitDialogOpen = false;
 208 |         SplitTarget = null;
 209 |     }
 210 | 
 211 |     /// <summary>Close the split dialog without applying.</summary>
 212 |     [RelayCommand]
 213 |     private void CancelSplit()
 214 |     {
 215 |         IsSplitDialogOpen = false;
 216 |         SplitTarget = null;
 217 |     }
 218 | }
 219 | 
 220 | /// <summary>One row in the inventory list.</summary>
 221 | public sealed class InventoryItemRow
 222 | {
 223 |     public InventoryItemRow(Item item, string? slot = null, string? rarity = null, bool stackable = false)
 224 |     {
 225 |         Id = item.Id;
 226 |         Name = item.Name;
 227 |         Quantity = item.Quantity;
 228 |         TemplateId = item.TemplateId ?? "";
 229 |         Slot = slot;
 230 |         Description = item.Description ?? "";
 231 |         Equipped = item.Equipped || slot is not null;
 232 |         // Category / rarity / weapon / armor / consumable flags would
 233 |         // come from the template registry; the Item entity itself
 234 |         // doesn't carry them. We expose them as empty/default so the
 235 |         // view can bind without nulls; a later task that denormalizes
 236 |         // template info onto Item instances fills them in.
 237 |         Category = "";
 238 |         // ITEM-RARITY (issue #66): caller passes the template's rarity
 239 |         // (looked up via World.Registries.Items). Defaults to "common"
 240 |         // when the template wasn't found or the rarity is empty.
 241 |         Rarity = NormalizeRarity(rarity);
 242 |         IsWeapon = false;
 243 |         IsArmor = false;
 244 |         IsConsumable = false;
 245 |         // STACK-SPLIT (issue #64): true when the source template is
 246 |         // stackable (consumables, ammunition, currency). The split
 247 |         // button is shown only for Stackable rows with Quantity > 1.
 248 |         Stackable = stackable;
 249 |     }
 250 | 
 251 |     public EntityId Id { get; }
 252 |     public string Name { get; }
 253 |     public int Quantity { get; }
 254 |     public string TemplateId { get; }
 255 |     public string? Slot { get; }
 256 |     public string Description { get; }
 257 |     public bool Equipped { get; }
 258 |     public string Category { get; }
 259 |     public string Rarity { get; }
 260 |     public bool IsWeapon { get; }
 261 |     public bool IsArmor { get; }
 262 |     public bool IsConsumable { get; }
 263 |     /// <summary>
 264 |     /// True when the source template is stackable (consumables,
 265 |     /// ammunition, currency). Drives the Split button's visibility.
 266 |     /// </summary>
 267 |     public bool Stackable { get; }
 268 |     public bool CanUse => IsConsumable;
 269 |     public bool CanEquip => IsWeapon || IsArmor;
 270 |     /// <summary>
 271 |     /// True when the Split button should be shown: stackable item with
 272 |     /// more than one unit in the stack.
 273 |     /// </summary>
 274 |     public bool CanSplit => Stackable && Quantity > 1;
 275 | 
 276 |     // ─── Rarity-driven UI helpers (issue #66) ──────────────────────────
 277 |     //
 278 |     // RarityClass maps the rarity string to the CSS-like style class
 279 |     // applied to the row's name TextBlock + Border (App.axaml defines
 280 |     // matching TextBlock.Rarity* / Border.ItemRow.Rarity* selectors that
 281 |     // set the foreground / left-border color).
 282 |     //
 283 |     // RarityLabel is the localized (Russian) adjective shown in the
 284 |     // expanded item detail under the name.
 285 |     public string RarityClass => Rarity switch
 286 |     {
 287 |         "common"    => "RarityCommon",
 288 |         "uncommon"  => "RarityUncommon",
 289 |         "rare"      => "RarityRare",
 290 |         "veryRare"  => "RarityVeryRare",
 291 |         "legendary" => "RarityLegendary",
 292 |         "artifact"  => "RarityArtifact",
 293 |         _           => "RarityCommon",
 294 |     };
 295 | 
 296 |     public string RarityLabel => Rarity switch
 297 |     {
 298 |         "common"    => "Обычный",
 299 |         "uncommon"  => "Необычная",
 300 |         "rare"      => "Редкая",
 301 |         "veryRare"  => "Очень редкая",
 302 |         "legendary" => "Легендарная",
 303 |         "artifact"  => "Артефакт",
 304 |         _           => "Обычная",
 305 |     };
 306 | 
 307 |     private static string NormalizeRarity(string? r) =>
 308 |         string.IsNullOrEmpty(r) ? "common" : r;
 309 | }
 310 | 
 311 | /// <summary>Kind of item action the user requested.</summary>
 312 | public enum ItemActionKind { Use, Equip, Unequip, Drop, Split }
 313 | 
 314 | /// <summary>
 315 | /// An item action raised by the inventory panel. For
 316 | /// <see cref="ItemActionKind.Split"/>, <see cref="Quantity"/> carries the
 317 | /// number of units to split off into a new stack.
 318 | /// </summary>
 319 | public sealed record ItemAction(EntityId ItemId, ItemActionKind Kind, int Quantity = 0);

--- src/MyGame.Desktop/ViewModels/Panels/QuestPanelViewModel.cs (C#, 391 lines, ~4,524 tokens) ---
   1 | using System;
   2 | using System.Collections.Generic;
   3 | using System.Collections.ObjectModel;
   4 | using System.Linq;
   5 | using CommunityToolkit.Mvvm.ComponentModel;
   6 | using CommunityToolkit.Mvvm.Input;
   7 | using MyGame.Core.Common;
   8 | using MyGame.Core.World;
   9 | using MyGame.Core.World.Entities;
  10 | 
  11 | namespace MyGame.Desktop.ViewModels.Panels;
  12 | 
  13 | /// <summary>
  14 | /// View model for the quest panel. Lists active, completed, and failed
  15 | /// quests with their objectives (with done/pending markers) and rewards.
  16 | /// Read-only — quest state changes happen via the GM tool flow (the GM
  17 | /// marks objectives done via <c>update_quest</c>).
  18 | ///
  19 | /// <para>
  20 | /// <b>Reward claiming (issue #70):</b> completed quests with unclaimed
  21 | /// rewards (<see cref="Quest.UnclaimedRewards"/> != null) show a
  22 | /// «Получить награду» button. Clicking it raises
  23 | /// <see cref="ClaimRewardsRequested"/>, which GameViewModel subscribes
  24 | /// to. The host grants the currency / XP / items, clears
  25 | /// <see cref="Quest.UnclaimedRewards"/>, appends a log entry, refreshes
  26 | /// the panels, and persists.
  27 | /// </para>
  28 | ///
  29 | /// <para>
  30 | /// <b>Polish (issue #69):</b> the panel supports:
  31 | /// <list type="bullet">
  32 | ///   <item>Search box — filters all three sections by name/description
  33 | ///     substring (case-insensitive). Bound to <see cref="SearchText"/>;
  34 | ///     re-filtering happens automatically on change.</item>
  35 | ///   <item>Sort dropdown — orders the active-quests section by creation
  36 | ///     order (<see cref="QuestSortMode.Newness"/>), name
  37 | ///     (<see cref="QuestSortMode.Alphabetical"/>), or giver's location
  38 | ///     name (<see cref="QuestSortMode.Location"/>). Completed/Failed
  39 | ///     sections stay in creation order (they're already collapsed
  40 | ///     summaries; sorting adds no value there).</item>
  41 | ///   <item>Collapse/expand all — two small buttons that fold/unfold every
  42 | ///     active quest row. Each row has its own <see cref="QuestRow.IsExpanded"/>
  43 | ///     flag so the user can also toggle individually by clicking the row
  44 | ///     header.</item>
  45 | /// </list>
  46 | /// </para>
  47 | /// </summary>
  48 | public partial class QuestPanelViewModel : ObservableObject
  49 | {
  50 |     // Source-of-truth quest rows in creation order. ApplyFilter copies
  51 |     // (with optional search filter + sort) into the observable collections
  52 |     // the View binds to. We keep the source separate so re-filtering on
  53 |     // SearchText / SortMode change doesn't require a full RefreshFromWorld
  54 |     // (which would blow away IsExpanded state on every keystroke).
  55 |     private readonly List<QuestRow> _allActive = new();
  56 |     private readonly List<QuestRow> _allCompleted = new();
  57 |     private readonly List<QuestRow> _allFailed = new();
  58 | 
  59 |     /// <summary>
  60 |     /// Raised when the user clicks «Получить награду» on a completed
  61 |     /// quest with unclaimed rewards (issue #70). The GameViewModel
  62 |     /// subscribes and grants the rewards (currency, XP, items), clears
  63 |     /// <see cref="Quest.UnclaimedRewards"/>, appends a log entry,
  64 |     /// refreshes the panels, and persists.
  65 |     /// </summary>
  66 |     public event Action<EntityId>? ClaimRewardsRequested;
  67 | 
  68 |     /// <summary>Refresh from the given world.</summary>
  69 |     public void RefreshFromWorld(World world)
  70 |     {
  71 |         _allActive.Clear();
  72 |         _allCompleted.Clear();
  73 |         _allFailed.Clear();
  74 | 
  75 |         if (world is not null)
  76 |         {
  77 |             foreach (var q in world.Quests)
  78 |             {
  79 |                 var locationName = ResolveGiverLocationName(world, q);
  80 |                 // Pass a callback the row invokes when its «Получить
  81 |                 // награду» button is clicked. The callback re-raises
  82 |                 // ClaimRewardsRequested on this panel; GameViewModel
  83 |                 // subscribes and grants the rewards. This keeps the row
  84 |                 // decoupled from the host (the row doesn't need a
  85 |                 // reference to the panel or the GameViewModel).
  86 |                 var row = new QuestRow(q, locationName,
  87 |                     claimCallback: id => ClaimRewardsRequested?.Invoke(id));
  88 |                 switch (q.Status)
  89 |                 {
  90 |                     case QuestStatus.Active:
  91 |                         _allActive.Add(row);
  92 |                         break;
  93 |                     case QuestStatus.Completed:
  94 |                         _allCompleted.Add(row);
  95 |                         break;
  96 |                     case QuestStatus.Failed:
  97 |                         _allFailed.Add(row);
  98 |                         break;
  99 |                 }
 100 |             }
 101 |         }
 102 | 
 103 |         ApplyFilter();
 104 |     }
 105 | 
 106 |     /// <summary>
 107 |     /// Rebuild the observable collections from the source lists using the
 108 |     /// current <see cref="SearchText"/> + <see cref="SortMode"/>. Called
 109 |     /// on every SearchText / SortMode change (via the partial OnChanged
 110 |     /// hooks) and at the end of <see cref="RefreshFromWorld"/>.
 111 |     /// </summary>
 112 |     private void ApplyFilter()
 113 |     {
 114 |         ApplyFilter(_allActive, ActiveQuests, sort: true);
 115 |         ApplyFilter(_allCompleted, CompletedQuests, sort: false);
 116 |         ApplyFilter(_allFailed, FailedQuests, sort: false);
 117 |         UpdateHasFlags();
 118 |     }
 119 | 
 120 |     private void ApplyFilter(List<QuestRow> source, ObservableCollection<QuestRow> target, bool sort)
 121 |     {
 122 |         IEnumerable<QuestRow> rows = source;
 123 | 
 124 |         // Search filter — case-insensitive substring on name + description.
 125 |         if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(SearchText))
 126 |         {
 127 |             var needle = SearchText.Trim();
 128 |             rows = rows.Where(r =>
 129 |                 (r.Name?.Contains(needle, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) ?? false)
 130 |                 || (r.Description?.Contains(needle, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) ?? false));
 131 |         }
 132 | 
 133 |         // Sort — only applied to the active section (completed/failed are
 134 |         // short summary rows in creation order; sorting adds noise).
 135 |         if (sort)
 136 |         {
 137 |             rows = SortMode switch
 138 |             {
 139 |                 QuestSortMode.Alphabetical =>
 140 |                     rows.OrderBy(r => r.Name ?? "", StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase),
 141 |                 QuestSortMode.Location =>
 142 |                     // Quests without a known giver location sort LAST (the
 143 |                     // "~~" sentinel sorts after any letter). Stable within
 144 |                     // the same location by name as a tiebreaker.
 145 |                     rows.OrderBy(r => string.IsNullOrEmpty(r.LocationName) ? "~~" : r.LocationName,
 146 |                                  StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
 147 |                         .ThenBy(r => r.Name ?? "", StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase),
 148 |                 _ => rows, // Newness = preserve creation order
 149 |             };
 150 |         }
 151 | 
 152 |         target.Clear();
 153 |         foreach (var r in rows) target.Add(r);
 154 |     }
 155 | 
 156 |     /// <summary>
 157 |     /// Resolve the location name of the NPC that gave this quest. Used by
 158 |     /// the «По локации» sort mode. Returns null for quests with no giver
 159 |     /// or whose giver can't be located in the world (shouldn't happen in
 160 |     /// normal play, but defensive — saves can drift).
 161 |     /// </summary>
 162 |     private static string? ResolveGiverLocationName(World world, Quest q)
 163 |     {
 164 |         if (q.GiverNpcId is null) return null;
 165 |         var npc = world.GetNpc(q.GiverNpcId.Value);
 166 |         if (npc is null) return null;
 167 |         var loc = world.GetLocation(npc.LocationId);
 168 |         return loc?.Name;
 169 |     }
 170 | 
 171 |     private void UpdateHasFlags()
 172 |     {
 173 |         OnPropertyChanged(nameof(HasActive));
 174 |         OnPropertyChanged(nameof(HasCompleted));
 175 |         OnPropertyChanged(nameof(HasFailed));
 176 |         OnPropertyChanged(nameof(HasAny));
 177 |     }
 178 | 
 179 |     // ─── Search + sort state ─────────────────────────────────────────
 180 | 
 181 |     /// <summary>
 182 |     /// Search text — filters all three sections by name/description
 183 |     /// substring (case-insensitive). Empty string shows all quests.
 184 |     /// Re-filtering is automatic via <see cref="OnSearchTextChanged"/>.
 185 |     /// </summary>
 186 |     [ObservableProperty] private string _searchText = string.Empty;
 187 | 
 188 |     /// <summary>
 189 |     /// Sort mode for the active-quests section. Bound to the ComboBox
 190 |     /// via <see cref="SelectedSortOption"/>.
 191 |     /// </summary>
 192 |     [ObservableProperty] private QuestSortMode _sortMode = QuestSortMode.Newness;
 193 | 
 194 |     /// <summary>
 195 |     /// Re-filter on search text change. Fires whenever the user types in
 196 |     /// the search box (the binding is two-way, so this runs on every
 197 |     /// keystroke after a small delay baked into the TextBox binding).
 198 |     /// </summary>
 199 |     partial void OnSearchTextChanged(string value) => ApplyFilter();
 200 | 
 201 |     /// <summary>Re-sort when the dropdown selection changes.</summary>
 202 |     partial void OnSortModeChanged(QuestSortMode value) => ApplyFilter();
 203 | 
 204 |     /// <summary>
 205 |     /// Sort options shown in the dropdown. Each entry pairs a Russian
 206 |     /// label with a <see cref="QuestSortMode"/>. The ComboBox binds
 207 |     /// SelectedItem to <see cref="SelectedSortOption"/>.
 208 |     /// </summary>
 209 |     public IReadOnlyList<QuestSortOption> SortOptions { get; } = new[]
 210 |     {
 211 |         new QuestSortOption("По новизне", QuestSortMode.Newness),
 212 |         new QuestSortOption("По алфавиту", QuestSortMode.Alphabetical),
 213 |         new QuestSortOption("По локации", QuestSortMode.Location),
 214 |     };
 215 | 
 216 |     /// <summary>
 217 |     /// Currently-selected sort option. Getter finds the option matching
 218 |     /// <see cref="SortMode"/>; setter updates <see cref="SortMode"/> (which
 219 |     /// fires <see cref="OnSortModeChanged"/> and triggers re-sort).
 220 |     /// </summary>
 221 |     public QuestSortOption? SelectedSortOption
 222 |     {
 223 |         get => SortOptions.FirstOrDefault(o => o.Mode == SortMode);
 224 |         set
 225 |         {
 226 |             if (value is not null && value.Mode != SortMode)
 227 |             {
 228 |                 SortMode = value.Mode;
 229 |                 OnPropertyChanged(nameof(SelectedSortOption));
 230 |             }
 231 |         }
 232 |     }
 233 | 
 234 |     // ─── Collapse/expand all ─────────────────────────────────────────
 235 | 
 236 |     /// <summary>
 237 |     /// Collapse every active quest row (sets <see cref="QuestRow.IsExpanded"/>
 238 |     /// = false). No-op on the completed/failed sections — they're already
 239 |     /// single-line summaries without an expand state.
 240 |     /// </summary>
 241 |     [RelayCommand]
 242 |     private void CollapseAll()
 243 |     {
 244 |         foreach (var r in _allActive) r.IsExpanded = false;
 245 |     }
 246 | 
 247 |     /// <summary>Expand every active quest row.</summary>
 248 |     [RelayCommand]
 249 |     private void ExpandAll()
 250 |     {
 251 |         foreach (var r in _allActive) r.IsExpanded = true;
 252 |     }
 253 | 
 254 |     // ─── Observable collections ──────────────────────────────────────
 255 | 
 256 |     public ObservableCollection<QuestRow> ActiveQuests { get; } = new();
 257 |     public ObservableCollection<QuestRow> CompletedQuests { get; } = new();
 258 |     public ObservableCollection<QuestRow> FailedQuests { get; } = new();
 259 | 
 260 |     public bool HasActive => ActiveQuests.Count > 0;
 261 |     public bool HasCompleted => CompletedQuests.Count > 0;
 262 |     public bool HasFailed => FailedQuests.Count > 0;
 263 |     public bool HasAny => HasActive || HasCompleted || HasFailed;
 264 | }
 265 | 
 266 | /// <summary>
 267 | /// Sort mode for the active-quests section of <see cref="QuestPanelViewModel"/>.
 268 | /// </summary>
 269 | public enum QuestSortMode
 270 | {
 271 |     /// <summary>Creation order (the order quests appear in <c>world.Quests</c>).</summary>
 272 |     Newness,
 273 |     /// <summary>Alphabetical by quest name (case-insensitive).</summary>
 274 |     Alphabetical,
 275 |     /// <summary>By the giver NPC's location name (case-insensitive).</summary>
 276 |     Location,
 277 | }
 278 | 
 279 | /// <summary>
 280 | /// One entry in the sort dropdown. Pairs a Russian display label with a
 281 | /// <see cref="QuestSortMode"/> value.
 282 | /// </summary>
 283 | public sealed record QuestSortOption(string Label, QuestSortMode Mode);
 284 | 
 285 | /// <summary>
 286 | /// One row in the quest list. Wraps a <see cref="Quest"/>. Observable so
 287 | /// <see cref="IsExpanded"/> can be toggled by the row header click and
 288 | /// the collapse/expand-all commands.
 289 | /// </summary>
 290 | public sealed partial class QuestRow : ObservableObject
 291 | {
 292 |     private readonly Action<EntityId>? _claimRewardsCallback;
 293 | 
 294 |     public QuestRow(Quest q, string? locationName = null, Action<EntityId>? claimCallback = null)
 295 |     {
 296 |         Id = q.Id;
 297 |         Name = q.Name;
 298 |         Description = q.Description ?? "";
 299 |         Status = q.Status.ToString();
 300 |         Objectives = (q.Objectives ?? new()).Select(o => new QuestObjectiveRow(o)).ToList();
 301 |         RewardCurrency = q.Reward?.Currency ?? q.Reward?.Gold ?? 0;
 302 |         RewardExperience = q.Reward?.Experience ?? 0;
 303 |         RewardItemIds = q.Reward?.Items ?? new();
 304 |         CompletedObjectives = Objectives.Count(o => o.Done);
 305 |         TotalObjectives = Objectives.Count;
 306 |         LocationName = locationName;
 307 |         // Issue #70: surface whether the quest has unclaimed rewards so
 308 |         // the View can show a «Получить награду» button. The button is
 309 |         // only shown for completed quests (the QuestPanelView template
 310 |         // checks both HasUnclaimedRewards AND Status=="Completed" before
 311 |         // rendering the button — defensive, since old saves may have
 312 |         // non-completed quests with a stale UnclaimedRewards field from
 313 |         // a prior implementation).
 314 |         HasUnclaimedRewards = q.UnclaimedRewards is not null;
 315 |         _claimRewardsCallback = claimCallback;
 316 |     }
 317 | 
 318 |     /// <summary>
 319 |     /// The quest's <see cref="Entity.Id"/> — passed back to the
 320 |     /// GameViewModel via <see cref="ClaimRewardsCommand"/> so the host
 321 |     /// can locate the quest in the live <see cref="World"/> and grant
 322 |     /// its rewards.
 323 |     /// </summary>
 324 |     public EntityId Id { get; }
 325 | 
 326 |     public string Name { get; }
 327 |     public string Description { get; }
 328 |     public string Status { get; }
 329 |     public IReadOnlyList<QuestObjectiveRow> Objectives { get; }
 330 |     public int RewardCurrency { get; }
 331 |     public int RewardExperience { get; }
 332 |     public IReadOnlyList<string> RewardItemIds { get; }
 333 |     public int CompletedObjectives { get; }
 334 |     public int TotalObjectives { get; }
 335 |     public string? LocationName { get; }
 336 |     public bool HasRewards => RewardCurrency > 0 || RewardExperience > 0 || RewardItemIds.Count > 0;
 337 |     public bool HasLocation => !string.IsNullOrEmpty(LocationName);
 338 | 
 339 |     /// <summary>
 340 |     /// True when the quest has rewards waiting to be claimed (issue #70).
 341 |     /// Drives the «Получить награду» button visibility in the View.
 342 |     /// </summary>
 343 |     public bool HasUnclaimedRewards { get; }
 344 | 
 345 |     private bool _isExpanded = true;
 346 |     /// <summary>
 347 |     /// Whether this row's full description + objectives are visible. True
 348 |     /// by default (so a fresh refresh shows everything); toggled by
 349 |     /// clicking the row header chevron or by the collapse/expand-all
 350 |     /// commands on <see cref="QuestPanelViewModel"/>.
 351 |     /// </summary>
 352 |     public bool IsExpanded
 353 |     {
 354 |         get => _isExpanded;
 355 |         set => SetProperty(ref _isExpanded, value);
 356 |     }
 357 | 
 358 |     /// <summary>
 359 |     /// Toggle <see cref="IsExpanded"/>. Bound to the row header Button's
 360 |     /// Command (generated as <c>ToggleExpandedCommand</c>) so a click
 361 |     /// anywhere on the header folds/unfolds the row.
 362 |     /// </summary>
 363 |     [RelayCommand]
 364 |     public void ToggleExpanded() => IsExpanded = !IsExpanded;
 365 | 
 366 |     /// <summary>
 367 |     /// Claim this quest's unclaimed rewards (issue #70). Invokes the
 368 |     /// callback the panel passed in at construction, which re-raises
 369 |     /// <see cref="QuestPanelViewModel.ClaimRewardsRequested"/>. The
 370 |     /// GameViewModel handles the actual grant (currency, XP, items) +
 371 |     /// clears <see cref="Quest.UnclaimedRewards"/> + appends a log entry
 372 |     /// + refreshes the panels + persists.
 373 |     /// </summary>
 374 |     [RelayCommand]
 375 |     private void ClaimRewards() => _claimRewardsCallback?.Invoke(Id);
 376 | }
 377 | 
 378 | /// <summary>One objective in a quest.</summary>
 379 | public sealed class QuestObjectiveRow
 380 | {
 381 |     public QuestObjectiveRow(QuestObjective o)
 382 |     {
 383 |         Id = o.Id;
 384 |         Description = o.Description;
 385 |         Done = o.Done;
 386 |     }
 387 |     public string Id { get; }
 388 |     public string Description { get; }
 389 |     public bool Done { get; }
 390 | }

--- src/MyGame.Desktop/ViewModels/Panels/WorldPanelViewModel.cs (C#, 255 lines, ~3,089 tokens) ---
   1 | using System;
   2 | using System.Collections.ObjectModel;
   3 | using System.Linq;
   4 | using CommunityToolkit.Mvvm.ComponentModel;
   5 | using CommunityToolkit.Mvvm.Input;
   6 | using MyGame.Core.Common;
   7 | using MyGame.Core.World;
   8 | using MyGame.Core.World.Entities;
   9 | 
  10 | namespace MyGame.Desktop.ViewModels.Panels;
  11 | 
  12 | /// <summary>
  13 | /// View model for the world panel. Shows the current location (with its
  14 | /// description, exits, inhabitants, buildings, ground items), the
  15 | /// discovered-locations map (visited + discovered flags), and the world
  16 | /// clock. Exits are clickable: <see cref="TravelRequested"/> fires when
  17 | /// the user clicks one, and the owning GameViewModel performs the actual
  18 | /// world mutation (set LocationId, mark Visited/Discovered, log, save,
  19 | /// and roll a random encounter if the destination is dangerous).
  20 | /// </summary>
  21 | public partial class WorldPanelViewModel : ObservableObject
  22 | {
  23 |     /// <summary>
  24 |     /// Raised when the user clicks an exit row. The owning GameViewModel
  25 |     /// subscribes and mutates the world directly (single-player) — moving
  26 |     /// the active player, marking the destination visited, and rolling
  27 |     /// a random encounter if the destination danger > 0.
  28 |     /// </summary>
  29 |     public event Action<ExitRow>? TravelRequested;
  30 |     /// <summary>Refresh from the given world.</summary>
  31 |     public void RefreshFromWorld(World world)
  32 |     {
  33 |         if (world is null) { Clear(); return; }
  34 | 
  35 |         ClockDisplay = world.Clock.ToString();
  36 |         Turn = world.Turn;
  37 |         WorldTitle = world.Flags?.TryGetValue("worldTitle", out var t) == true
  38 |             ? t.ToString() ?? "Мир"
  39 |             : "Мир";
  40 | 
  41 |         // ENGINE-DEPTH (issue #34): weather row. Null when the subsystem
  42 |         // isn't active (no set_weather call yet) — the UI section collapses.
  43 |         WeatherDisplay = FormatWeatherDisplay(world.CurrentWeather, world.WeatherForecast);
  44 |         HasWeather = !string.IsNullOrEmpty(WeatherDisplay);
  45 | 
  46 |         var player = world.ActivePlayer ?? world.Players.FirstOrDefault();
  47 |         var loc = player is null ? null : world.GetLocation(player.LocationId);
  48 | 
  49 |         CurrentLocationName = loc?.Name ?? "—";
  50 |         CurrentLocationDescription = loc?.Description ?? "";
  51 |         CurrentLocationTerrain = loc?.Terrain ?? "—";
  52 |         CurrentLocationDanger = loc?.Danger ?? 0;
  53 | 
  54 |         Exits.Clear();
  55 |         Inhabitants.Clear();
  56 |         BuildingsHere.Clear();
  57 |         GroundItems.Clear();
  58 | 
  59 |         if (loc is not null)
  60 |         {
  61 |             foreach (var exit in loc.Exits)
  62 |             {
  63 |                 // Issue #20 (chunked generation): phantom exits (cold-region
  64 |                 // boundaries) have To=EntityId.Empty and ToName set. Render
  65 |                 // them with the ToName + a "⚠" marker so the user knows
  66 |                 // clicking it triggers region generation.
  67 |                 string toName;
  68 |                 if (exit.To == EntityId.Empty && !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(exit.ToName))
  69 |                 {
  70 |                     toName = exit.ToName + " ⚠";
  71 |                 }
  72 |                 else
  73 |                 {
  74 |                     toName = world.GetLocation(exit.To)?.Name ?? exit.To.ToString();
  75 |                 }
  76 |                 Exits.Add(new ExitRow(exit.Direction, toName, exit.Locked == true));
  77 |             }
  78 | 
  79 |             foreach (var npcId in loc.Npcs)
  80 |             {
  81 |                 var n = world.GetNpc(npcId);
  82 |                 if (n is not null)
  83 |                     Inhabitants.Add(new NpcRow(n.Name, n.Race ?? "—", n.Class ?? "—",
  84 |                         n.Level?.ToString() ?? "?"));
  85 |             }
  86 | 
  87 |             foreach (var bId in loc.Buildings)
  88 |             {
  89 |                 var b = world.GetBuilding(bId);
  90 |                 if (b is not null)
  91 |                     BuildingsHere.Add(new BuildingRow(b.Name, b.Description ?? ""));
  92 |             }
  93 | 
  94 |             foreach (var iId in loc.Items)
  95 |             {
  96 |                 var it = world.GetItem(iId);
  97 |                 if (it is not null)
  98 |                     GroundItems.Add(new GroundItemRow(it.Name, it.Quantity));
  99 |             }
 100 |         }
 101 | 
 102 |         AllLocations.Clear();
 103 |         foreach (var l in world.Locations)
 104 |             AllLocations.Add(new LocationRow(l.Name, l.Terrain, l.Danger, l.Visited, l.Discovered, l.X, l.Y));
 105 | 
 106 |         // ENGINE-DEPTH (issue #36): factions list. Empty when the world
 107 |         // has no factions — the UI section collapses via HasFactions.
 108 |         Factions.Clear();
 109 |         foreach (var f in world.Factions)
 110 |             Factions.Add(new FactionRow(f.Name, f.Alignment, f.Reputation));
 111 | 
 112 |         OnPropertyChanged(nameof(HasExits));
 113 |         OnPropertyChanged(nameof(HasInhabitants));
 114 |         OnPropertyChanged(nameof(HasBuildings));
 115 |         OnPropertyChanged(nameof(HasGroundItems));
 116 |         OnPropertyChanged(nameof(HasLocations));
 117 |         OnPropertyChanged(nameof(HasFactions));
 118 |     }
 119 | 
 120 |     /// <summary>
 121 |     /// Format the weather row for the WorldPanel (issue #34). Returns
 122 |     /// an empty string when the weather subsystem isn't active (no
 123 |     /// CurrentWeather set). Otherwise "🌤 Ясно" (+ optional forecast in
 124 |     /// parentheses). Mirrors the labels used by the GM context block in
 125 |     /// GameMaster.BuildWorldStateBlock so the player and the model see
 126 |     /// the same string.
 127 |     /// </summary>
 128 |     private static string FormatWeatherDisplay(string? weather, string? forecast)
 129 |     {
 130 |         if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(weather)) return string.Empty;
 131 |         var label = weather switch
 132 |         {
 133 |             "clear" => "🌤 Ясно",
 134 |             "rain" => "🌧 Дождь",
 135 |             "storm" => "⛈ Гроза",
 136 |             "fog" => "🌫 Туман",
 137 |             "snow" => "❄ Снег",
 138 |             "overcast" => "☁ Облачно",
 139 |             _ => weather,
 140 |         };
 141 |         return string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(forecast) ? label : $"{label} — {forecast}";
 142 |     }
 143 | 
 144 |     private void Clear()
 145 |     {
 146 |         ClockDisplay = "—";
 147 |         Turn = 0;
 148 |         WorldTitle = "Мир";
 149 |         WeatherDisplay = string.Empty;
 150 |         HasWeather = false;
 151 |         CurrentLocationName = "—";
 152 |         CurrentLocationDescription = "";
 153 |         CurrentLocationTerrain = "—";
 154 |         CurrentLocationDanger = 0;
 155 |         Exits.Clear();
 156 |         Inhabitants.Clear();
 157 |         BuildingsHere.Clear();
 158 |         GroundItems.Clear();
 159 |         AllLocations.Clear();
 160 |         Factions.Clear();
 161 |         OnPropertyChanged(nameof(HasExits));
 162 |         OnPropertyChanged(nameof(HasInhabitants));
 163 |         OnPropertyChanged(nameof(HasBuildings));
 164 |         OnPropertyChanged(nameof(HasGroundItems));
 165 |         OnPropertyChanged(nameof(HasLocations));
 166 |         OnPropertyChanged(nameof(HasFactions));
 167 |     }
 168 | 
 169 |     // ─── Observable properties ───────────────────────────────────────
 170 | 
 171 |     [ObservableProperty] private string _clockDisplay = "—";
 172 |     [ObservableProperty] private int _turn;
 173 |     [ObservableProperty] private string _worldTitle = "Мир";
 174 |     [ObservableProperty] private string _currentLocationName = "—";
 175 |     [ObservableProperty] private string _currentLocationDescription = "";
 176 |     [ObservableProperty] private string _currentLocationTerrain = "—";
 177 |     [ObservableProperty] private int _currentLocationDanger;
 178 | 
 179 |     /// <summary>
 180 |     /// Weather row text (issue #34): "🌤 Ясно" / "🌧 Дождь — к вечеру гроза"
 181 |     /// etc. Empty when the weather subsystem isn't active (collapses the
 182 |     /// UI section via HasWeather).
 183 |     /// </summary>
 184 |     [ObservableProperty] private string _weatherDisplay = string.Empty;
 185 | 
 186 |     /// <summary>True when the weather subsystem is active for this world.</summary>
 187 |     public bool HasWeather { get; private set; }
 188 | 
 189 |     public ObservableCollection<ExitRow> Exits { get; } = new();
 190 |     public ObservableCollection<NpcRow> Inhabitants { get; } = new();
 191 |     public ObservableCollection<BuildingRow> BuildingsHere { get; } = new();
 192 |     public ObservableCollection<GroundItemRow> GroundItems { get; } = new();
 193 |     public ObservableCollection<LocationRow> AllLocations { get; } = new();
 194 | 
 195 |     /// <summary>
 196 |     /// Factions active in this world (issue #36). Empty when the world has
 197 |     /// no factions — the UI section collapses via <see cref="HasFactions"/>.
 198 |     /// </summary>
 199 |     public ObservableCollection<FactionRow> Factions { get; } = new();
 200 | 
 201 |     public bool HasExits => Exits.Count > 0;
 202 |     public bool HasInhabitants => Inhabitants.Count > 0;
 203 |     public bool HasBuildings => BuildingsHere.Count > 0;
 204 |     public bool HasGroundItems => GroundItems.Count > 0;
 205 |     public bool HasLocations => AllLocations.Count > 0;
 206 |     public bool HasFactions => Factions.Count > 0;
 207 | 
 208 |     // ─── Commands ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 209 | 
 210 |     /// <summary>
 211 |     /// Raised by the exit-row Button in the view. Forwards the chosen
 212 |     /// exit row to <see cref="TravelRequested"/> subscribers (the
 213 |     /// GameViewModel owns the actual world mutation).
 214 |     /// </summary>
 215 |     [RelayCommand]
 216 |     private void Travel(ExitRow exit) => TravelRequested?.Invoke(exit);
 217 | }
 218 | 
 219 | /// <summary>One exit from the current location.</summary>
 220 | public sealed record ExitRow(string Direction, string ToName, bool Locked);
 221 | 
 222 | /// <summary>One NPC at the current location.</summary>
 223 | public sealed record NpcRow(string Name, string Race, string Class, string Level);
 224 | 
 225 | /// <summary>One building at the current location.</summary>
 226 | public sealed record BuildingRow(string Name, string Description);
 227 | 
 228 | /// <summary>One loose item on the ground at the current location.</summary>
 229 | public sealed record GroundItemRow(string Name, int Quantity);
 230 | 
 231 | /// <summary>One location in the world map list.</summary>
 232 | public sealed record LocationRow(string Name, string Terrain, int Danger, bool Visited, bool Discovered, int? X = null, int? Y = null);
 233 | 
 234 | /// <summary>
 235 | /// One faction row in the world panel (issue #36). Reputation is on the
 236 | /// -100..100 scale; alignment is "ally" / "neutral" / "hostile". The view
 237 | /// color-codes by alignment (green / gray / red) and renders the
 238 | /// reputation as a bar centered at 0. <see cref="IsAlly"/> /
 239 | /// <see cref="IsNeutral"/> / <see cref="IsHostile"/> are computed
 240 | /// booleans the XAML binds <c>IsVisible</c> to (no custom converter
 241 | /// needed — Avalonia's binding system handles bool→Visibility via
 242 | /// <c>BooleanToVisibilityConverter</c> implicitly on <c>IsVisible</c>).
 243 | /// </summary>
 244 | public sealed record FactionRow(string Name, string Alignment, int Reputation)
 245 | {
 246 |     /// <summary>True when alignment is "ally" — drives the green badge's IsVisible.</summary>
 247 |     public bool IsAlly => string.Equals(Alignment, "ally", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
 248 | 
 249 |     /// <summary>True when alignment is "neutral" — drives the gray badge's IsVisible.</summary>
 250 |     public bool IsNeutral => string.Equals(Alignment, "neutral", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
 251 | 
 252 |     /// <summary>True when alignment is "hostile" — drives the red badge's IsVisible.</summary>
 253 |     public bool IsHostile => string.Equals(Alignment, "hostile", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
 254 | }

--- src/MyGame.Desktop/ViewModels/PetDelegationProgress.cs (C#, 102 lines, ~1,102 tokens) ---
   1 | using System.ComponentModel;
   2 | using System.Runtime.CompilerServices;
   3 | using CommunityToolkit.Mvvm.ComponentModel;
   4 | 
   5 | namespace MyGame.Desktop.ViewModels;
   6 | 
   7 | /// <summary>
   8 | /// One row in the world-build progress dialog's «Pet-агенты» section
   9 | /// (issue #76). Tracks the live status of a single
  10 | /// <c>PetDelegation</c> the orchestrator runs after the deterministic
  11 | /// committer stage. Bound to the WorldBuildView's ItemsControl; each row
  12 | /// shows the delegation label, a status glyph (▶ running / ✓ done / ✗
  13 | /// error), and either a summary (when done) or an error message (when
  14 | /// failed).
  15 | /// </summary>
  16 | /// <remarks>
  17 | /// The orchestrator reports progress via
  18 | /// <c>IProgress&lt;WorldBuildProgress&gt;</c>; the
  19 | /// <see cref="WorldBuildViewModel"/> translates each
  20 | /// <c>Stage == "pet"</c> event into an update on the matching
  21 | /// <see cref="PetDelegationProgress"/> entry (matched by the delegation
  22 | /// label embedded in the progress event's Label field).
  23 | /// </remarks>
  24 | public sealed class PetDelegationProgress : ObservableObject
  25 | {
  26 |     /// <summary>
  27 |     /// Display label of the delegation (matches
  28 |     /// <c>PetDelegation.Label</c>). Set at construction and never
  29 |     /// changes; the orchestrator includes it in every pet-stage progress
  30 |     /// event so the VM can route updates to the right row.
  31 |     /// </summary>
  32 |     public string Label { get; set; } = "";
  33 | 
  34 |     private string _status = "pending"; // pending | running | done | error
  35 |     /// <summary>
  36 |     /// Current status of this delegation. One of:
  37 |     /// <list type="bullet">
  38 |     ///   <item><c>pending</c> — pre-populated; the orchestrator hasn't
  39 |     ///     started this delegation yet.</item>
  40 |     ///   <item><c>running</c> — the orchestrator reported Active for this
  41 |     ///     delegation.</item>
  42 |     ///   <item><c>done</c> — the orchestrator reported Done; see
  43 |     ///     <see cref="Summary"/> for what it accomplished.</item>
  44 |     ///   <item><c>error</c> — the orchestrator reported Error; see
  45 |     ///     <see cref="Error"/> for the failure message.</item>
  46 |     /// </list>
  47 |     /// </summary>
  48 |     public string Status
  49 |     {
  50 |         get => _status;
  51 |         set => SetProperty(ref _status, value);
  52 |     }
  53 | 
  54 |     private string? _summary;
  55 |     /// <summary>
  56 |     /// Short summary of what the delegation accomplished (set when
  57 |     /// <see cref="Status"/> becomes <c>done</c>). Bound to a muted
  58 |     /// TextBlock under the row's status glyph.
  59 |     /// </summary>
  60 |     public string? Summary
  61 |     {
  62 |         get => _summary;
  63 |         set => SetProperty(ref _summary, value);
  64 |     }
  65 | 
  66 |     private string? _error;
  67 |     /// <summary>
  68 |     /// Error message (set when <see cref="Status"/> becomes
  69 |     /// <c>error</c>). Bound to a danger-colored TextBlock under the row's
  70 |     /// status glyph.
  71 |     /// </summary>
  72 |     public string? Error
  73 |     {
  74 |         get => _error;
  75 |         set => SetProperty(ref _error, value);
  76 |     }
  77 | 
  78 |     // ─── Convenience flags for XAML bindings ────────────────────────
  79 | 
  80 |     /// <summary>True when <see cref="Status"/> == "running".</summary>
  81 |     public bool IsRunning => Status == "running";
  82 |     /// <summary>True when <see cref="Status"/> == "done".</summary>
  83 |     public bool IsDone => Status == "done";
  84 |     /// <summary>True when <see cref="Status"/> == "error".</summary>
  85 |     public bool IsError => Status == "error";
  86 |     /// <summary>True when <see cref="Status"/> == "pending".</summary>
  87 |     public bool IsPending => Status == "pending";
  88 | 
  89 |     /// <summary>
  90 |     /// Update <see cref="Status"/> and refresh the derived Is* flags so
  91 |     /// the XAML bindings re-evaluate the status glyph + color.
  92 |     /// </summary>
  93 |     public void SetStatus(string newStatus)
  94 |     {
  95 |         Status = newStatus;
  96 |         OnPropertyChanged(nameof(IsRunning));
  97 |         OnPropertyChanged(nameof(IsDone));
  98 |         OnPropertyChanged(nameof(IsError));
  99 |         OnPropertyChanged(nameof(IsPending));
 100 |     }
 101 | }

--- src/MyGame.Desktop/ViewModels/PetDelegationViewModel.cs (C#, 86 lines, ~888 tokens) ---
   1 | using System;
   2 | using CommunityToolkit.Mvvm.ComponentModel;
   3 | using MyGame.Core.AI.Agents;
   4 | 
   5 | namespace MyGame.Desktop.ViewModels;
   6 | 
   7 | /// <summary>
   8 | /// One editable row in the WorldBrief screen's pet-delegation list
   9 | /// (issue #22). Wraps a <see cref="PetDelegation"/> with two-way
  10 | /// bindable properties (Label, Task, MaxIterations) so the user can
  11 | /// edit the delegation inline. The WorldBriefViewModel.Build command
  12 | /// reads the final values via <see cref="ToDelegation"/> when
  13 | /// assembling the orchestrator's delegation list.
  14 | /// </summary>
  15 | /// <remarks>
  16 | /// Pre-populated at construction with the matching
  17 | /// <see cref="PetDelegation"/> field values; changes propagate
  18 | /// immediately to the bound UI controls (TextBox / NumericUpDown).
  19 | /// </remarks>
  20 | public sealed class PetDelegationViewModel : ObservableObject
  21 | {
  22 |     private string _label = string.Empty;
  23 |     private string _task = string.Empty;
  24 |     private int _maxIterations = 6;
  25 | 
  26 |     /// <summary>
  27 |     /// Create a row view-model from an existing
  28 |     /// <see cref="PetDelegation"/>. The row copies the delegation's
  29 |     /// fields; subsequent edits to the row don't mutate the original
  30 |     /// delegation (so the user can cancel their edits by closing the
  31 |     /// screen without building).
  32 |     /// </summary>
  33 |     public PetDelegationViewModel(PetDelegation delegation)
  34 |     {
  35 |         _label = delegation?.Label ?? string.Empty;
  36 |         _task = delegation?.Task ?? string.Empty;
  37 |         _maxIterations = delegation?.MaxIterations ?? 6;
  38 |     }
  39 | 
  40 |     /// <summary>
  41 |     /// Display name shown in the orchestrator's progress UI
  42 |     /// ("Pet: {Label}"). Bound to a TextBox in the editor row.
  43 |     /// </summary>
  44 |     public string Label
  45 |     {
  46 |         get => _label;
  47 |         set => SetProperty(ref _label, value);
  48 |     }
  49 | 
  50 |     /// <summary>
  51 |     /// The task description handed to the pet agent. Should be concrete
  52 |     /// and self-contained — the pet agent doesn't see the orchestrator's
  53 |     /// other context. Bound to a multi-line TextBox in the editor row.
  54 |     /// </summary>
  55 |     public string Task
  56 |     {
  57 |         get => _task;
  58 |         set => SetProperty(ref _task, value);
  59 |     }
  60 | 
  61 |     /// <summary>
  62 |     /// Iteration cap for this delegation (1..20). Bound to a
  63 |     /// NumericUpDown in the editor row. Defaults to 6 (matches
  64 |     /// <see cref="PetAgent.DefaultMaxIterations"/>).
  65 |     /// </summary>
  66 |     public int MaxIterations
  67 |     {
  68 |         get => _maxIterations;
  69 |         set => SetProperty(ref _maxIterations, value);
  70 |     }
  71 | 
  72 |     /// <summary>
  73 |     /// Build a fresh <see cref="PetDelegation"/> from the row's current
  74 |     /// values. Called by the WorldBriefViewModel.Build command when
  75 |     /// assembling the orchestrator's delegation list. The Settings field
  76 |     /// is left null (per-pet AI overrides aren't exposed in the brief UI
  77 |     /// — they're an advanced feature handled elsewhere).
  78 |     /// </summary>
  79 |     public PetDelegation ToDelegation() => new()
  80 |     {
  81 |         Label = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(Label) ? "(без названия)" : Label.Trim(),
  82 |         Task = Task ?? string.Empty,
  83 |         MaxIterations = Math.Max(1, Math.Min(20, MaxIterations)),
  84 |     };
  85 | }

--- src/MyGame.Desktop/ViewModels/RebuildViewModel.cs (C#, 273 lines, ~2,786 tokens) ---
   1 | using System;
   2 | using System.Collections.Generic;
   3 | using System.Threading;
   4 | using System.Threading.Tasks;
   5 | using CommunityToolkit.Mvvm.Input;
   6 | using MyGame.Core.AI;
   7 | using MyGame.Core.AI.Agents;
   8 | using MyGame.Core.AI.Prompts;
   9 | using MyGame.Core.AI.Tools;
  10 | using MyGame.Core.Profile;
  11 | using MyGame.Core.Saves;
  12 | using MyGame.Desktop.Services;
  13 | 
  14 | namespace MyGame.Desktop.ViewModels;
  15 | 
  16 | /// <summary>
  17 | /// Rebuild dialog (issue #23). Lets the user regenerate parts of an
  18 | /// existing world without starting over. Surfaces checkboxes for the
  19 | /// rebuild categories (locations / NPC / items / narration / full
  20 | /// rebuild), an optional rebuild brief, and a Run button that calls
  21 | /// <see cref="WorldBuilderOrchestrator.RebuildAsync"/> on a background
  22 | /// task. On success, navigates into the game with the rebuilt save.
  23 | /// </summary>
  24 | /// <remarks>
  25 | /// <b>Partial vs full rebuild (issue #23 conventions):</b>
  26 | /// <list type="bullet">
  27 | ///   <item>Partial flags (Locations / Npcs / Items / Narration) are
  28 | ///     ADDITIVE — the existing world is preserved, the AI generates
  29 | ///     additional content in the selected categories, and the
  30 | ///     committer (idempotent) commits only the new entries. The
  31 | ///     player's character + progress survive.</item>
  32 | ///   <item><see cref="FullRebuild"/> is the only destructive option —
  33 | ///     it discards all entities and regenerates from scratch. The
  34 | ///     dialog shows a prominent warning when this is checked.</item>
  35 | /// </list>
  36 | /// </remarks>
  37 | public partial class RebuildViewModel : ViewModelBase
  38 | {
  39 |     private readonly ProfileStore _profileStore;
  40 |     private readonly SettingsStore _settingsStore;
  41 |     private readonly SaveManager _saveManager;
  42 |     private readonly MainViewModel _shell;
  43 |     private readonly string _saveId;
  44 | 
  45 |     private bool _rebuildLocations;
  46 |     private bool _rebuildNpcs;
  47 |     private bool _rebuildItems;
  48 |     private bool _rebuildNarration = true; // narration-only is a cheap, common case
  49 |     private bool _fullRebuild;
  50 |     private string _brief = string.Empty;
  51 |     private string? _progressLabel;
  52 |     private int _progressPercent;
  53 |     private bool _isRunning;
  54 |     private string? _resultSummary;
  55 | 
  56 |     public RebuildViewModel(
  57 |         ProfileStore profileStore,
  58 |         SettingsStore settingsStore,
  59 |         SaveManager saveManager,
  60 |         MainViewModel shell,
  61 |         string saveId)
  62 |     {
  63 |         _profileStore = profileStore ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(profileStore));
  64 |         _settingsStore = settingsStore ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(settingsStore));
  65 |         _saveManager = saveManager ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(saveManager));
  66 |         _shell = shell ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(shell));
  67 |         _saveId = saveId ?? string.Empty;
  68 |         Title = "Перестроить мир";
  69 | 
  70 |         // Surface the save's name in the title bar so the user knows
  71 |         // which world they're rebuilding.
  72 |         try
  73 |         {
  74 |             var meta = _saveManager.LoadMeta(_saveId);
  75 |             if (meta is not null && !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(meta.Name))
  76 |                 Title = $"Перестроить: {meta.Name}";
  77 |         }
  78 |         catch { /* fall back to generic title */ }
  79 |     }
  80 | 
  81 |     /// <summary>Regenerate locations (additive — keeps existing).</summary>
  82 |     public bool RebuildLocations
  83 |     {
  84 |         get => _rebuildLocations;
  85 |         set => SetProperty(ref _rebuildLocations, value);
  86 |     }
  87 | 
  88 |     /// <summary>Regenerate population (additive — keeps existing NPCs).</summary>
  89 |     public bool RebuildNpcs
  90 |     {
  91 |         get => _rebuildNpcs;
  92 |         set => SetProperty(ref _rebuildNpcs, value);
  93 |     }
  94 | 
  95 |     /// <summary>Regenerate loot/items (additive — keeps existing).</summary>
  96 |     public bool RebuildItems
  97 |     {
  98 |         get => _rebuildItems;
  99 |         set => SetProperty(ref _rebuildItems, value);
 100 |     }
 101 | 
 102 |     /// <summary>Re-write the opening narration from the current state.</summary>
 103 |     public bool RebuildNarration
 104 |     {
 105 |         get => _rebuildNarration;
 106 |         set => SetProperty(ref _rebuildNarration, value);
 107 |     }
 108 | 
 109 |     /// <summary>
 110 |     /// Full rebuild — discard all entities and regenerate from scratch.
 111 |     /// The only destructive option. When checked, the partial flags are
 112 |     /// ignored (the orchestrator runs the full pipeline regardless).
 113 |     /// </summary>
 114 |     public bool FullRebuild
 115 |     {
 116 |         get => _fullRebuild;
 117 |         set
 118 |         {
 119 |             if (SetProperty(ref _fullRebuild, value))
 120 |             {
 121 |                 // Reflect the new state in derived flags.
 122 |                 OnPropertyChanged(nameof(IsPartialDisabled));
 123 |                 OnPropertyChanged(nameof(IsFullRebuildWarningVisible));
 124 |                 RebuildCommand.NotifyCanExecuteChanged();
 125 |             }
 126 |         }
 127 |     }
 128 | 
 129 |     /// <summary>
 130 |     /// Optional rebuild brief. When empty, the orchestrator falls back
 131 |     /// to the original world's theme/setting (stashed on World.Flags).
 132 |     /// </summary>
 133 |     public string Brief
 134 |     {
 135 |         get => _brief;
 136 |         set => SetProperty(ref _brief, value);
 137 |     }
 138 | 
 139 |     /// <summary>Live progress label (bound to a TextBlock under the bar).</summary>
 140 |     public string? ProgressLabel
 141 |     {
 142 |         get => _progressLabel;
 143 |         private set => SetProperty(ref _progressLabel, value);
 144 |     }
 145 | 
 146 |     /// <summary>0–100 percent complete for the current rebuild.</summary>
 147 |     public int ProgressPercent
 148 |     {
 149 |         get => _progressPercent;
 150 |         private set => SetProperty(ref _progressPercent, value);
 151 |     }
 152 | 
 153 |     /// <summary>True while the rebuild task is in flight.</summary>
 154 |     public bool IsRunning
 155 |     {
 156 |         get => _isRunning;
 157 |         private set
 158 |         {
 159 |             if (SetProperty(ref _isRunning, value))
 160 |                 RebuildCommand.NotifyCanExecuteChanged();
 161 |         }
 162 |     }
 163 | 
 164 |     /// <summary>Final summary (success message or error).</summary>
 165 |     public string? ResultSummary
 166 |     {
 167 |         get => _resultSummary;
 168 |         private set => SetProperty(ref _resultSummary, value);
 169 |     }
 170 | 
 171 |     /// <summary>
 172 |     /// True when the partial checkboxes should be disabled (because
 173 |     /// <see cref="FullRebuild"/> is checked — the orchestrator ignores
 174 |     /// them in that case anyway, so we grey them out for clarity).
 175 |     /// </summary>
 176 |     public bool IsPartialDisabled => FullRebuild || IsRunning;
 177 | 
 178 |     /// <summary>True when the destructive full-rebuild warning should show.</summary>
 179 |     public bool IsFullRebuildWarningVisible => FullRebuild;
 180 | 
 181 |     /// <summary>
 182 |     /// Run the rebuild. Validates that at least one category is selected
 183 |     /// (or full rebuild), constructs the orchestrator bound to the
 184 |     /// loaded world, calls <see cref="WorldBuilderOrchestrator.RebuildAsync"/>,
 185 |     /// and on success navigates into the game with the rebuilt save.
 186 |     /// </summary>
 187 |     [RelayCommand(CanExecute = nameof(CanRebuild))]
 188 |     private async Task RebuildAsync()
 189 |     {
 190 |         if (!FullRebuild && !RebuildLocations && !RebuildNpcs && !RebuildItems && !RebuildNarration)
 191 |         {
 192 |             ResultSummary = "Выберите хотя бы одну категорию для перестройки.";
 193 |             return;
 194 |         }
 195 | 
 196 |         IsRunning = true;
 197 |         ResultSummary = null;
 198 |         ProgressLabel = "Подготовка…";
 199 |         ProgressPercent = 0;
 200 | 
 201 |         try
 202 |         {
 203 |             var settings = _settingsStore.Load();
 204 |             if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(settings.Ai.ApiKey))
 205 |             {
 206 |                 ResultSummary = "Не задан API-ключ. Откройте Настройки AI и введите ключ.";
 207 |                 return;
 208 |             }
 209 | 
 210 |             // The orchestrator loads the world itself via the SaveManager
 211 |             // (we pass the SaveManager into RebuildAsync). We need a
 212 |             // throwaway World for the orchestrator's ctor — the actual
 213 |             // world is loaded inside RebuildAsync.
 214 |             var placeholderWorld = new MyGame.Core.World.World();
 215 |             var ai = new AiClient(settings.Ai);
 216 |             var prompts = ServiceHost.Resolve<PromptLoader>();
 217 |             var tools = new ToolRegistry(placeholderWorld);
 218 | 
 219 |             var orchestrator = new WorldBuilderOrchestrator(
 220 |                 ai, placeholderWorld, prompts, tools, aiSettings: settings.Ai);
 221 | 
 222 |             var options = new WorldBuilderOrchestrator.RebuildOptions
 223 |             {
 224 |                 Locations = RebuildLocations,
 225 |                 Npcs = RebuildNpcs,
 226 |                 Items = RebuildItems,
 227 |                 Narration = RebuildNarration,
 228 |                 FullRebuild = FullRebuild,
 229 |             };
 230 | 
 231 |             var progress = new Progress<WorldBuildProgress>(p =>
 232 |                 Avalonia.Threading.Dispatcher.UIThread.Post(() =>
 233 |                 {
 234 |                     ProgressLabel = p.Label;
 235 |                     ProgressPercent = p.Percent;
 236 |                 }));
 237 | 
 238 |             var result = await Task.Run(() => orchestrator.RebuildAsync(
 239 |                 _saveManager, _saveId, options,
 240 |                 brief: string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(Brief) ? null : Brief,
 241 |                 log: null,
 242 |                 progress: progress,
 243 |                 ct: default));
 244 | 
 245 |             if (result.Success)
 246 |             {
 247 |                 ResultSummary = result.Summary ?? "Перестройка завершена.";
 248 |                 ProgressPercent = 100;
 249 |                 // Navigate into the game with the rebuilt save.
 250 |                 await _shell.NavigateToGame(_saveId);
 251 |             }
 252 |             else
 253 |             {
 254 |                 ResultSummary = "Ошибка: " + (result.Error ?? "неизвестная ошибка");
 255 |             }
 256 |         }
 257 |         catch (System.Exception ex)
 258 |         {
 259 |             ResultSummary = "Ошибка: " + ex.Message;
 260 |         }
 261 |         finally
 262 |         {
 263 |             IsRunning = false;
 264 |         }
 265 |     }
 266 | 
 267 |     private bool CanRebuild() => !IsRunning;
 268 | 
 269 |     /// <summary>Cancel — back to the main menu without rebuilding.</summary>
 270 |     [RelayCommand]
 271 |     private void Back() => _shell.NavigateToMenu();
 272 | }

--- src/MyGame.Desktop/ViewModels/SettingsViewModel.cs (C#, 534 lines, ~5,659 tokens) ---
   1 | using System;
   2 | using System.Collections.Generic;
   3 | using System.Threading.Tasks;
   4 | using CommunityToolkit.Mvvm.ComponentModel;
   5 | using CommunityToolkit.Mvvm.Input;
   6 | using MyGame.Core.AI;
   7 | using MyGame.Core.Profile;
   8 | using MyGame.Desktop.Services;
   9 | 
  10 | namespace MyGame.Desktop.ViewModels;
  11 | 
  12 | /// <summary>
  13 | /// Settings screen. Edits the OpenAI-compatible AI connection (BaseUrl,
  14 | /// ApiKey, Model, Temperature, MaxTokens) plus per-role model overrides
  15 | /// and the context-window threshold (issue #25 + #26). The nickname is
  16 | /// NOT edited here — it lives inline on the main menu (no separate
  17 | /// profile screen, since the nickname is the only profile field and
  18 | /// there's no auth).
  19 | ///
  20 | /// <para>
  21 | /// <b>Tabbed dialog (issue #78):</b> the screen exposes four groups of
  22 | /// settings — AI (existing), Оформление (theme + accent + animations,
  23 | /// issue #47), Мультиплеер (server host/port + auto-reconnect), and
  24 | /// Продвинутое (autosave, max tool iterations, stream-narrative). All
  25 | /// four persist on <see cref="SaveCommand"/>; the Cancel command
  26 | /// discards every tab's edits.
  27 | /// </para>
  28 | ///
  29 | /// <para>
  30 | /// <b>Live theme preview (issue #47):</b> changing <see cref="ThemeMode"/>,
  31 | /// <see cref="AccentColor"/>, or <see cref="EnableAnimations"/> calls
  32 | /// <see cref="ThemeService.ApplyTheme"/> immediately so the user sees the
  33 | /// effect without saving. If they hit Cancel, the previous settings are
  34 | /// re-applied (because Cancel doesn't persist, but the live preview has
  35 | /// already mutated the running app — we re-apply from the on-disk
  36 | /// settings to revert).
  37 | /// </para>
  38 | /// </summary>
  39 | public partial class SettingsViewModel : ViewModelBase
  40 | {
  41 |     private readonly SettingsStore _settingsStore;
  42 |     private readonly MainViewModel _shell;
  43 |
