  78 |     public SettingsViewModel(
  79 |         SettingsStore settingsStore,
  80 |         MainViewModel shell)
  81 |     {
  82 |         _settingsStore = settingsStore ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(settingsStore));
  83 |         _shell = shell ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(shell));
  84 | 
  85 |         // Pre-fill the form from the on-disk state.
  86 |         try
  87 |         {
  88 |             var settings = _settingsStore.Load();
  89 |             _baseUrl = settings.Ai.BaseUrl;
  90 |             _apiKey = settings.Ai.ApiKey;
  91 |             _model = settings.Ai.Model;
  92 |             _temperature = settings.Ai.Temperature;
  93 |             _maxTokens = settings.Ai.MaxTokens;
  94 |             _plannerModel = settings.Ai.PlannerModel;
  95 |             _gmModel = settings.Ai.GMModel;
  96 |             _narratorModel = settings.Ai.NarratorModel;
  97 |             _petModel = settings.Ai.PetModel;
  98 |             _maxContextTokens = settings.MaxContextTokens;
  99 | 
 100 |             // Appearance
 101 |             _themeMode = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(settings.ThemeMode) ? "Dark" : settings.ThemeMode;
 102 |             _accentColor = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(settings.AccentColor) ? "Indigo" : settings.AccentColor;
 103 |             _enableAnimations = settings.EnableAnimations;
 104 | 
 105 |             // Multiplayer
 106 |             _lastServerHost = settings.LastServerHost;
 107 |             _lastServerPort = settings.LastServerPort;
 108 |             _autoReconnect = settings.AutoReconnect;
 109 | 
 110 |             // Advanced
 111 |             _autosaveIntervalSeconds = settings.AutosaveIntervalSeconds;
 112 |             _maxToolIterations = settings.MaxToolIterations;
 113 |             _streamNarrative = settings.StreamNarrative;
 114 |         }
 115 |         catch (Exception ex)
 116 |         {
 117 |             // Defaults would still load if SettingsStore.Load failed,
 118 |             // but be defensive anyway.
 119 |             var defaults = new AiSettings();
 120 |             _baseUrl = defaults.BaseUrl;
 121 |             _model = defaults.Model;
 122 |             _temperature = defaults.Temperature;
 123 |             _maxTokens = defaults.MaxTokens;
 124 |             _apiKey = null;
 125 |             _plannerModel = null;
 126 |             _gmModel = null;
 127 |             _narratorModel = null;
 128 |             _petModel = null;
 129 |             _maxContextTokens = new Settings().MaxContextTokens;
 130 | 
 131 |             var s = new Settings();
 132 |             _themeMode = s.ThemeMode;
 133 |             _accentColor = s.AccentColor;
 134 |             _enableAnimations = s.EnableAnimations;
 135 |             _autoReconnect = s.AutoReconnect;
 136 |             _autosaveIntervalSeconds = s.AutosaveIntervalSeconds;
 137 |             _maxToolIterations = s.MaxToolIterations;
 138 |             _streamNarrative = s.StreamNarrative;
 139 |             ErrorMessage = $"Не удалось загрузить настройки: {ex.Message}";
 140 |         }
 141 |     }

 269 |                 ApplyLiveTheme();

 273 |     /// <summary>

 287 |     /// <summary>
 288 |     /// Apply the live (in-memory) theme values to the running app. Used
 289 |     /// by the property setters above for live preview. ThemeService is
 290 |     /// idempotent so calling it on every keystroke is fine.
 291 |     /// </summary>
 292 |     private void ApplyLiveTheme()
 293 |     {
 294 |         try
 295 |         {
 296 |             ThemeService.ApplyTheme(_themeMode ?? "Dark", _accentColor ?? "Indigo", _enableAnimations);
 297 |         }
 298 |         catch (Exception ex)
 299 |         {
 300 |             System.Diagnostics.Trace.WriteLine($"[SettingsViewModel] ApplyLiveTheme failed: {ex}");
 301 |         }

 356 |         set => SetProperty(ref _autosaveIntervalSeconds, value);
 357 |     }

 359 |     /// <summary>
 360 |     /// Hard cap on tool-call iterations per GM turn. Stops a misbehaving
 361 |     /// model from looping spawn-NPC forever. Default 8.
 362 |     /// </summary>
 363 |     public int MaxToolIterations
 364 |     {
 365 |         get => _maxToolIterations;
 366 |         set => SetProperty(ref _maxToolIterations, value);
 367 |     }

 372 |     /// </summary>

 376 |         set => SetProperty(ref _streamNarrative, value);

 379 |     // ─── Provider presets (issue #27) ───────────────────────────────
 380 |     //
 381 |     // The presets list is bound to a row of buttons in the settings
 382 |     // view. Clicking a preset fills in BaseUrl + Model (+ ApiKey for
 383 |     // cloud providers) so the user doesn't have to look up the endpoint
 384 |     // URLs for OpenAI / DeepSeek / Ollama / llama.cpp. The presets are
 385 |     // defined statically here (no settings file round-trip) — they
 386 |     // represent known provider configurations.
 387 | 
 388 |     /// <summary>
 389 |     /// Read-only list of provider presets shown in the settings UI
 390 |     /// (issue #27). Each preset is a <see cref="PresetCommand"/> relay
 391 |     /// that fills in BaseUrl + Model (+ ApiKey when
 392 |     /// <see cref="AiPreset.ClearsApiKey"/> is true).
 393 |     /// </summary>
 394 |     public IReadOnlyList<AiPreset> Presets { get; } = new[]
 395 |     {
 396 |         new AiPreset("OpenAI", "https://api.openai.com/v1", "gpt-4o-mini",
 397 |             ClearsApiKey: false,
 398 |             Tooltip: "OpenAI — облачный API, требуется ключ."),
 399 |         new AiPreset("DeepSeek", "https://api.deepseek.com/v1", "deepseek-chat",
 400 |             ClearsApiKey: false,
 401 |             Tooltip: "DeepSeek — облачный API, требуется ключ."),
 402 |         new AiPreset("Ollama (локально)", "http://localhost:11434/v1", "llama3.1:8b",
 403 |             ClearsApiKey: true,
 404 |             Tooltip: "Ollama — локальный сервер, ключ не нужен. Сначала установите Ollama и выполните `ollama pull llama3.1:8b`."),
 405 |         new AiPreset("llama.cpp", "http://localhost:8080/v1", "local-model",
 406 |             ClearsApiKey: true,
 407 |             Tooltip: "llama.cpp server — локальный сервер, ключ не нужен. Запустите сервер с флагом --host 0.0.0.0."),
 408 |     };
 409 | 
 410 |     /// <summary>
 411 |     /// Apply a preset: set BaseUrl + Model from the preset, and clear
 412 |     /// ApiKey when the preset is for a local provider (Ollama /
 413 |     /// llama.cpp). Cloud-provider presets (OpenAI, DeepSeek) leave the
 414 |     /// existing ApiKey intact so the user doesn't have to re-enter it
 415 |     /// when switching between cloud providers.
 416 |     /// </summary>
 417 |     /// <param name="preset">The preset to apply. Must not be null.</param>
 418 |     [RelayCommand]
 419 |     private void ApplyPreset(AiPreset? preset)
 420 |     {
 421 |         if (preset is null) return;
 422 |         BaseUrl = preset.BaseUrl;
 423 |         Model = preset.Model;
 424 |         if (preset.ClearsApiKey)
 425 |             ApiKey = null;
 426 |     }
 427 | 
 428 |     // ─── Commands ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 429 | 
 430 |     /// <summary>
 431 |     /// Persist every tab's settings (AI + appearance + multiplayer +
 432 |     /// advanced), then return to the main menu. Catches disk errors and
 433 |     /// surfaces them inline rather than crashing.
 434 |     /// </summary>
 435 |     [RelayCommand]
 436 |     private async Task SaveAsync()
 437 |     {
 438 |         IsBusy = true;
 439 |         ErrorMessage = null;
 440 |         try
 441 |         {
 442 |             // Patch AI settings via the Update helper (preserves other
 443 |             // settings like MaxToolIterations, AutosaveInterval, etc.).
 444 |             // Per-role model overrides are persisted as null when blank
 445 |             // so GetModelForRole falls back to Model cleanly.
 446 |             var patchedAi = new AiSettings
 447 |             {
 448 |                 BaseUrl = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(_baseUrl) ? new AiSettings().BaseUrl : _baseUrl.Trim(),
 449 |                 ApiKey = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(_apiKey) ? null : _apiKey,
 450 |                 Model = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(_model) ? new AiSettings().Model : _model.Trim(),
 451 |                 Temperature = _temperature,
 452 |                 MaxTokens = Math.Max(64, _maxTokens),
 453 |                 PlannerModel = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(_plannerModel) ? null : _plannerModel.Trim(),
 454 |                 GMModel = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(_gmModel) ? null : _gmModel.Trim(),
 455 |                 NarratorModel = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(_narratorModel) ? null : _narratorModel.Trim(),
 456 |                 PetModel = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(_petModel) ? null : _petModel.Trim(),
 457 |             };
 458 |             // Clamp MaxContextTokens to a sane range — too small and
 459 |             // summarization fires on every turn; too large and the model
 460 |             // runs out of context. 1024 is the floor (just enough for the
 461 |             // system prompt + a couple turns); 200000 is the ceiling
 462 |             // (covers even 200k-context models like gpt-4-turbo-long).
 463 |             var clampedContext = Math.Max(1024, Math.Min(200000, _maxContextTokens));
 464 | 
 465 |             // Clamp autosave interval to a sane range — 0 (disabled) to
 466 |             // 3600 (hour). Negative values are coerced to 0.
 467 |             var clampedAutosave = Math.Max(0, Math.Min(3600, _autosaveIntervalSeconds));
 468 |             // Clamp MaxToolIterations to 1..50 — too small and the GM
 469 |             // can't finish a turn; too large and a misbehaving model
 470 |             // could burn the whole request budget.
 471 |             var clampedIterations = Math.Max(1, Math.Min(50, _maxToolIterations));
 472 | 
 473 |             // Server port range — 1..65535. Null is preserved (no last
 474 |             // server). 0 is treated as null.
 475 |             int? clampedPort = _lastServerPort is int p && p > 0 && p <= 65535 ? p : null;
 476 | 
 477 |             _settingsStore.Update(s => s with
 478 |             {
 479 |                 Ai = patchedAi,
 480 |                 MaxContextTokens = clampedContext,
 481 |                 ThemeMode = _themeMode,
 482 |                 AccentColor = _accentColor,
 483 |                 EnableAnimations = _enableAnimations,
 484 |                 LastServerHost = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(_lastServerHost) ? null : _lastServerHost.Trim(),
 485 |                 LastServerPort = clampedPort,
 486 |                 AutoReconnect = _autoReconnect,
 487 |                 AutosaveIntervalSeconds = clampedAutosave,
 488 |                 MaxToolIterations = clampedIterations,
 489 |                 StreamNarrative = _streamNarrative,
 490 |             });
 491 | 
 492 |             await Task.CompletedTask;
 493 |             _shell.NavigateToMenu();
 494 |         }
 495 |         catch (Exception ex)
 496 |         {
 497 |             ErrorMessage = $"Не удалось сохранить: {ex.Message}";
 498 |         }
 499 |         finally
 500 |         {
 501 |             IsBusy = false;
 502 |         }
 503 |     }
 504 | 
 505 |     /// <summary>
 506 |     /// Discard changes and return to the menu. Live-preview theme
 507 |     /// changes are reverted by re-applying the on-disk settings (the
 508 |     /// user's last saved state).
 509 |     /// </summary>
 510 |     [RelayCommand]
 511 |     private void Cancel()
 512 |     {
 513 |         // Revert live-preview theme changes by re-applying the on-disk
 514 |         // settings. If the load fails, fall back to defaults so the UI
 515 |         // is at least in a known state.
 516 |         try { ThemeService.ApplyFromSettings(_settingsStore.Load()); }
 517 |         catch { ThemeService.ApplyTheme("Dark", "Indigo", enableAnimations: true); }
 518 |         _shell.NavigateToMenu();
 519 |     }
 520 | }
 521 | 
 522 | /// <summary>
 523 | /// One AI-provider preset for the settings UI (issue #27). Pairs a
 524 | /// human-readable label (shown on the preset button) with a BaseUrl +
 525 | /// Model + a flag for whether the preset should clear the existing
 526 | /// ApiKey (local providers don't use one).
 527 | /// </summary>
 528 | public sealed record AiPreset(
 529 |     string Label,
 530 |     string BaseUrl,
 531 |     string Model,
 532 |     bool ClearsApiKey = false,
 533 |     string? Tooltip = null);

--- src/MyGame.Desktop/ViewModels/ViewModelBase.cs (C#, 69 lines, ~664 tokens) ---
   1 | using CommunityToolkit.Mvvm.ComponentModel;
   2 | 
   3 | namespace MyGame.Desktop.ViewModels;
   4 | 
   5 | /// <summary>
   6 | /// Base class for every screen-level view model. Provides the common
   7 | /// <see cref="Title"/> (bound to the window title or a header) and
   8 | /// <see cref="IsBusy"/> (bound to a busy indicator overlay) properties,
   9 | /// plus an optional <see cref="ErrorMessage"/> channel for screen-local
  10 | /// errors that should be surfaced inline rather than in a dialog.
  11 | /// </summary>
  12 | public abstract class ViewModelBase : ObservableObject
  13 | {
  14 |     private string _title = string.Empty;
  15 |     private bool _isBusy;
  16 |     private string? _errorMessage;
  17 | 
  18 |     /// <summary>
  19 |     /// Screen title — bound to a header label and (for the root shell)
  20 |     /// to the window title.
  21 |     /// </summary>
  22 |     public string Title
  23 |     {
  24 |         get => _title;
  25 |         set => SetProperty(ref _title, value);
  26 |     }
  27 | 
  28 |     /// <summary>
  29 |     /// True while a long-running async operation is in flight. Bound to
  30 |     /// a busy overlay or a spinner; commands can disable themselves
  31 |     /// when this is true (see the per-VM CanExecute pattern).
  32 |     /// </summary>
  33 |     public bool IsBusy
  34 |     {
  35 |         get => _isBusy;
  36 |         protected set => SetProperty(ref _isBusy, value);
  37 |     }
  38 | 
  39 |     /// <summary>
  40 |     /// Inline error message. Null = no error. ViewModels should clear
  41 |     /// this on successful operations and set it on caught exceptions
  42 |     /// the user should see inline (network errors, save failures).
  43 |     /// </summary>
  44 |     public string? ErrorMessage
  45 |     {
  46 |         get => _errorMessage;
  47 |         protected set => SetProperty(ref _errorMessage, value);
  48 |     }
  49 | 
  50 |     /// <summary>Convenience: set the error and return false (so a
  51 |     /// command can <c>return Error(...);</c> in a one-liner).</summary>
  52 |     protected bool Error(string message)
  53 |     {
  54 |         ErrorMessage = message;
  55 |         return false;
  56 |     }
  57 | 
  58 |     /// <summary>Clear any previously-set inline error.</summary>
  59 |     protected void ClearError() => ErrorMessage = null;
  60 | 
  61 |     /// <summary>
  62 |     /// Public error-setter for callers that aren't subclasses (e.g. the
  63 |     /// shell view model wrapping initialization in a try/catch). The
  64 |     /// setter is otherwise protected so subclasses fully own their
  65 |     /// error channel.
  66 |     /// </summary>
  67 |     public void SetError(string message) => ErrorMessage = message;
  68 | }

--- src/MyGame.Desktop/ViewModels/WorldBriefViewModel.cs (C#, 242 lines, ~2,740 tokens) ---
   1 | using System.Collections.Generic;
   2 | using System.Collections.ObjectModel;
   3 | using System.Threading.Tasks;
   4 | using CommunityToolkit.Mvvm.Input;
   5 | using MyGame.Core.AI.Agents;
   6 | using MyGame.Core.Profile;
   7 | using MyGame.Core.Saves;
   8 | 
   9 | namespace MyGame.Desktop.ViewModels;
  10 | 
  11 | /// <summary>
  12 | /// World-brief entry screen. Lets the user type a free-form description of
  13 | /// the world they want (genre, tone, key locations, starting situation),
  14 | /// then navigates to the WorldBuildViewModel which runs the AI build.
  15 | ///
  16 | /// <para>
  17 | /// Offers a few preset briefs (dark fantasy village / cyberpunk district /
  18 | /// post-apoc bunker) for users who don't want to write from scratch.
  19 | /// </para>
  20 | ///
  21 | /// <para>
  22 | /// An "advanced" toggle enables optional pet-agent delegations — extra AI
  23 | /// sub-tasks that enrich the world after the deterministic committer
  24 | /// stage (mass NPC spawning, batch item creation, lore markers). Off by
  25 | /// default to keep the simple 3-stage pipeline as the default. When
  26 | /// enabled, an editable list of delegations is shown (issue #22 — the
  27 | /// user can add/remove/edit rows; the defaults are pre-populated).
  28 | /// </para>
  29 | ///
  30 | /// <para>
  31 | /// <b>Generation mode (issue #20):</b> a radio-button selector picks
  32 | /// between «Полный» (full — planner designs the whole world) and
  33 | /// «По регионам» (chunked — planner designs only the start region,
  34 | /// others are generated on-demand as the player travels toward them).
  35 | /// Default is «Полный».
  36 | /// </para>
  37 | /// </summary>
  38 | public partial class WorldBriefViewModel : ViewModelBase
  39 | {
  40 |     private readonly ProfileStore _profileStore;
  41 |     private readonly SettingsStore _settingsStore;
  42 |     private readonly SaveManager _saveManager;
  43 |     private readonly MainViewModel _shell;
  44 | 
  45 |     private string _brief = string.Empty;
  46 |     private bool _usePetDelegations;
  47 |     private string _generationMode = "full"; // "full" | "chunked"
  48 | 
  49 |     public WorldBriefViewModel(
  50 |         ProfileStore profileStore,
  51 |         SettingsStore settingsStore,
  52 |         SaveManager saveManager,
  53 |         MainViewModel shell)
  54 |     {
  55 |         _profileStore = profileStore;
  56 |         _settingsStore = settingsStore;
  57 |         _saveManager = saveManager;
  58 |         _shell = shell;
  59 |         Title = "Создать мир";
  60 | 
  61 |         // Issue #22 — pre-populate the default delegations so the user
  62 |         // sees the affordance and can edit them rather than starting
  63 |         // from a blank list.
  64 |         foreach (var del in BuildDefaultDelegations())
  65 |             Delegations.Add(new PetDelegationViewModel(del));
  66 |     }
  67 | 
  68 |     /// <summary>
  69 |     /// Free-form world brief. Bound to a multi-line TextBox. Empty brief is
  70 |     /// allowed — the planner will make up its own world (defaulting to dark
  71 |     /// fantasy).
  72 |     /// </summary>
  73 |     public string Brief
  74 |     {
  75 |         get => _brief;
  76 |         set => SetProperty(ref _brief, value);
  77 |     }
  78 | 
  79 |     /// <summary>
  80 |     /// If true, the build flow adds optional pet-agent delegations after
  81 |     /// the deterministic committer stage. Each delegation is a separate
  82 |     /// AI sub-task (mass NPC spawn, batch item creation, lore markers)
  83 |     /// that enriches the world. Off by default — adds ~30-60s + extra
  84 |     /// token cost. When checked, an editable list of delegations is
  85 |     /// shown (issue #22).
  86 |     /// </summary>
  87 |     public bool UsePetDelegations
  88 |     {
  89 |         get => _usePetDelegations;
  90 |         set => SetProperty(ref _usePetDelegations, value);
  91 |     }
  92 | 
  93 |     /// <summary>
  94 |     /// Generation mode (issue #20): "full" (planner designs the whole
  95 |     /// world) or "chunked" (planner designs only the start region; others
  96 |     /// are generated on-demand as the player travels toward them).
  97 |     /// Default "full". Two-way bound to a radio-button selector in the
  98 |     /// view; the Build command passes it through to the
  99 |     /// <see cref="WorldPlanRequest"/>.
 100 |     /// </summary>
 101 |     public string GenerationMode
 102 |     {
 103 |         get => _generationMode;
 104 |         set => SetProperty(ref _generationMode, value);
 105 |     }
 106 | 
 107 |     /// <summary>
 108 |     /// True when <see cref="GenerationMode"/> is "full" — bound to the
 109 |     /// «Полный» radio button's IsChecked.
 110 |     /// </summary>
 111 |     public bool IsGenerationModeFull
 112 |     {
 113 |         get => _generationMode == "full";
 114 |         set { if (value) GenerationMode = "full"; }
 115 |     }
 116 | 
 117 |     /// <summary>
 118 |     /// True when <see cref="GenerationMode"/> is "chunked" — bound to the
 119 |     /// «По регионам» radio button's IsChecked.
 120 |     /// </summary>
 121 |     public bool IsGenerationModeChunked
 122 |     {
 123 |         get => _generationMode == "chunked";
 124 |         set { if (value) GenerationMode = "chunked"; }
 125 |     }

 128 |     /// Editable list of pet delegations (issue #22). Pre-populated with
 129 |     /// the two defaults (background population + loot/scatter); the user
 130 |     /// can add / remove / edit rows. The Build command filters out rows
 131 |     /// with an empty Task before passing them to the orchestrator.
 132 |     /// </summary>
 133 |     public ObservableCollection<PetDelegationViewModel> Delegations { get; } = new();

 135 |     [RelayCommand]
 136 |     private void UsePresetDarkFantasy() =>
 137 |         Brief = "Тёмное фэнтези. Изолированная долина, отрезанная от большого мира войной и зимой. " +
 138 |                 "Деревня у тракта, окружённая лесом, в котором завелось что-то голодное. " +
 139 |                 "Старый культ пытается возродить погребённого бога. " +
 140 |                 "Магия редкая и пугает; сталь надёжнее. Тон — мрачный, паранойя, мало надежды.";
 141 |

 143 |     private void UsePresetCyberpunk() =>
 144 |         Brief = "Киберпанк. Неоновый район мегаполиса, зажатый между корп-башнями и трущобами. " +
 145 |                 "Дождь, рекламные голограммы, чёрный рынок имплантов. " +
 146 |                 "Фиксер собирает команду для ограбления конвоя. " +
 147 |                 "Тон — паранойя, неон, влажный асфальт, короткие рубленые фразы.";

 150 |     private void UsePresetPostapoc() =>
 151 |         Brief = "Постапокалипсис. Бункер под руинами старого города, 80 лет после ядерной войны. " +
 152 |                 "Жители никогда не выходили наружу. Запасы еды кончаются. " +
 153 |                 "Разведчики пропадают. Тон — пыль, ржавчина, тишина, одиночество, страх перед外面的世界.";
 154 | 
 155 |     /// <summary>
 156 |     /// Add a blank delegation row to the editable list (issue #22).
 157 |     /// The user fills in the Label + Task + MaxIterations inline.
 158 |     /// </summary>
 159 |     [RelayCommand]
 160 |     private void AddDelegation()
 161 |     {
 162 |         Delegations.Add(new PetDelegationViewModel(new PetDelegation
 163 |         {
 164 |             Label = $"Делегация {Delegations.Count + 1}",
 165 |             Task = string.Empty,
 166 |             MaxIterations = 6,
 167 |         }));
 168 |     }
 169 | 
 170 |     /// <summary>
 171 |     /// Remove the given delegation row from the editable list. Bound to
 172 |     /// the per-row «Удалить» button (CommandParameter = the row VM).
 173 |     /// </summary>
 174 |     [RelayCommand]
 175 |     private void RemoveDelegation(PetDelegationViewModel? row)
 176 |     {
 177 |         if (row is null) return;
 178 |         Delegations.Remove(row);
 179 |     }
 180 | 
 181 |     /// <summary>
 182 |     /// Continue to the world-build progress screen with the current brief.
 183 |     /// </summary>
 184 |     [RelayCommand]
 185 |     private void Build()
 186 |     {
 187 |         // Empty brief is fine — the planner prompt has a default branch.
 188 |         // Filter the editable delegations to non-empty tasks; pass null
 189 |         // when the user hasn't opted in (so the orchestrator skips the
 190 |         // pet stage entirely).
 191 |         IReadOnlyCollection<PetDelegation>? delegations = null;
 192 |         if (UsePetDelegations)
 193 |         {
 194 |             var filtered = new List<PetDelegation>();
 195 |             foreach (var vm in Delegations)
 196 |             {
 197 |                 var del = vm.ToDelegation();
 198 |                 if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(del.Task))
 199 |                     filtered.Add(del);
 200 |             }
 201 |             delegations = filtered.Count > 0 ? filtered : null;
 202 |         }
 203 |         _shell.NavigateToWorldBuild(Brief ?? string.Empty, delegations, GenerationMode);
 204 |     }
 205 | 
 206 |     /// <summary>
 207 |     /// Build the default set of pet-agent delegations. These run after the
 208 |     /// deterministic committer stage and enrich the world with extra
 209 |     /// detail. Each delegation is a focused AI sub-task with its own
 210 |     /// tool-call loop. Used to pre-populate the editable list (issue #22)
 211 |     /// — the user can edit/delete/add to taste.
 212 |     /// </summary>
 213 |     private static IReadOnlyCollection<PetDelegation> BuildDefaultDelegations()
 214 |     {
 215 |         return new List<PetDelegation>
 216 |         {
 217 |             new()
 218 |             {
 219 |                 Label = "Фоновое население",
 220 |                 Task = "Добавь 5-8 фоновых NPC в уже созданные локации. Используй spawn_npc с подходящими шаблонами " +
 221 |                        "(npc_villager, npc_merchant, npc_guard если есть, иначе любые подходящие). " +
 222 |                        "Дай каждому имя через nameOverride и короткое описание поведения через флаги. " +
 223 |                        "Не трогай уже существующих NPC. Заверши вызовом pet_done с кратким summary.",
 224 |                 MaxIterations = 6,
 225 |             },
 226 |             new()
 227 |             {
 228 |                 Label = "Лут и сокровища",
 229 |                 Task = "Размести 4-6 предметов на земле в разных локациях (используй spawn_item_on_ground если доступен, " +
 230 |                        "иначе give_item существующим NPC как инвентарь). Подбирай предметы по теме локации: " +
 231 |                        "в руинах — старые артефакты, в лесу — травы, в пещерах — ценности. " +
 232 |                        "Заверши вызовом pet_done с кратким summary.",
 233 |                 MaxIterations = 5,
 234 |             },
 235 |         };
 236 |     }
 237 | 
 238 |     /// <summary>Back to the main menu.</summary>
 239 |     [RelayCommand]
 240 |     private void Back() => _shell.NavigateToMenu();
 241 | }

  20 | namespace MyGame.Desktop.ViewModels;
  21 | 
  22 | /// <summary>
  23 | /// World-build screen. Runs the WorldBuilderOrchestrator in the
  24 | /// background and shows live progress (stage / label / percent / detail)
  25 | /// to the user. Supports cancellation. On success, creates a save with the
  26 | /// opening narration as the first log entry and navigates to the game
  27 | /// screen in single-player mode.
  28 | ///
  29 | /// <para>
  30 | /// <b>Pause / resume (issue #19):</b> the user can pause the build
  31 | /// mid-flight via <see cref="PauseCommand"/>; the orchestrator blocks at
  32 | /// the next checkpoint and the UI shows «Приостановлено: {stage}».
  33 | /// <see cref="ResumeCommand"/> continues from where it stopped. On
  34 | /// <see cref="CancelCommand"/> (cancel — distinct from pause), the
  35 | /// orchestrator's saved state is written to
  36 | /// <c>%APPDATA%/MyGame/worldbuilder-state.json</c> so a future session
  37 | /// can offer to resume.
  38 | /// </para>
  39 | /// </summary>
  40 | public partial class WorldBuildViewModel : ViewModelBase
  41 | {
  42 |     private readonly ProfileStore _profileStore;
  43 |     private readonly SettingsStore _settingsStore;
  44 |     private readonly SaveManager _saveManager;
  45 |     private readonly MainViewModel _shell;
  46 | 
  47 |     private readonly string _brief;
  48 |     private readonly IReadOnlyCollection<MyGame.Core.AI.Agents.PetDelegation>? _petDelegations;
  49 |     private readonly string? _generationMode;
  50 |     private CancellationTokenSource? _cts;
  51 |     private WorldBuilderResult? _result;
  52 | 
  53 |     // The orchestrator is created inside StartAsync but Pause/Resume need
  54 |     // to reach it from the UI thread → keep a field reference. Cleared in
  55 |     // the finally block of StartAsync so a stale orchestrator can't be
  56 |     // paused/resumed after the build has finished.
  57 |     private WorldBuilderOrchestrator? _orchestrator;
  58 | 
  59 |     public WorldBuildViewModel(
  60 |         ProfileStore profileStore,
  61 |         SettingsStore settingsStore,
  62 |         SaveManager saveManager,
  63 |         MainViewModel shell,
  64 |         string brief,
  65 |         IReadOnlyCollection<MyGame.Core.AI.Agents.PetDelegation>? petDelegations = null,
  66 |         string? generationMode = null)
  67 |     {
  68 |         _profileStore = profileStore ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(profileStore));
  69 |         _settingsStore = settingsStore ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(settingsStore));
  70 |         _saveManager = saveManager ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(saveManager));
  71 |         _shell = shell ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(shell));
  72 |         _brief = brief ?? string.Empty;
  73 |         _petDelegations = petDelegations;
  74 |         _generationMode = generationMode;
  75 | 
  76 |         Title = "Создание мира";
  77 | 
  78 |         // Pre-populate the pet-progress rows so the user sees the
  79 |         // upcoming delegations immediately (issue #76). Each starts in
  80 |         // "pending" status; the orchestrator's pet-stage progress events
  81 |         // flip them to running/done/error as the build progresses.
  82 |         if (_petDelegations is not null)
  83 |         {
  84 |             foreach (var del in _petDelegations)
  85 |             {
  86 |                 PetProgress.Add(new PetDelegationProgress
  87 |                 {
  88 |                     Label = del.Label,
  89 |                     Status = "pending",
  90 |                 });
  91 |             }
  92 |         }
  93 |     }
  94 | 
  95 |     // ─── Live progress state ─────────────────────────────────────────
  96 | 
  97 |     private int _percent;
  98 |     private string _stageLabel = "Подготовка…";
  99 |     private string? _stageDetail;
 100 |     private string? _finalSummary;
 101 |     private bool _completed;
 102 |     private bool _failed;
 103 |     private bool _isPaused;
 104 | 
 105 |     /// <summary>0–100 percent complete for the whole build.</summary>
 106 |     public int Percent
 107 |     {
 108 |         get => _percent;
 109 |         private set => SetProperty(ref _percent, value);
 110 |     }
 111 | 
 112 |     /// <summary>Human-readable current stage label (RU).</summary>
 113 |     public string StageLabel
 114 |     {
 115 |         get => _stageLabel;
 116 |         private set => SetProperty(ref _stageLabel, value);
 117 |     }
 118 | 
 119 |     /// <summary>Optional sub-step detail text.</summary>
 120 |     public string? StageDetail
 121 |     {
 122 |         get => _stageDetail;
 123 |         private set => SetProperty(ref _stageDetail, value);
 124 |     }
 125 | 
 126 |     /// <summary>
 127 |     /// Set when the build completes successfully. Drives the
 128 |     /// «Продолжить» button visibility.
 129 |     /// </summary>
 130 |     public bool Completed
 131 |     {
 132 |         get => _completed;
 133 |         private set
 134 |         {
 135 |             if (SetProperty(ref _completed, value))
 136 |                 EnterGameCommand.NotifyCanExecuteChanged();
 137 |         }
 138 |     }
 139 | 
 140 |     /// <summary>
 141 |     /// Set when the build fails or is cancelled. Drives the
 142 |     /// «Назад» button prominence.
 143 |     /// </summary>
 144 |     public bool Failed
 145 |     {
 146 |         get => _failed;
 147 |         private set => SetProperty(ref _failed, value);
 148 |     }
 149 | 
 150 |     /// <summary>
 151 |     /// True while the user has paused the build (issue #19). Drives the
 152 |     /// «Пауза» / «Продолжить» button toggle in the View. Set by
 153 |     /// <see cref="Pause"/> / <see cref="Resume"/>; cleared on
 154 |     /// completion / failure / cancel. When true, <see cref="StageLabel"/>
 155 |     /// is prefixed with «Приостановлено: » so the user sees what stage
 156 |     /// the build is waiting on.
 157 |     /// </summary>
 158 |     public bool IsPaused
 159 |     {
 160 |         get => _isPaused;
 161 |         private set
 162 |         {
 163 |             if (SetProperty(ref _isPaused, value))
 164 |             {
 165 |                 PauseCommand.NotifyCanExecuteChanged();
 166 |                 ResumeCommand.NotifyCanExecuteChanged();
 167 |                 CancelCommand.NotifyCanExecuteChanged();
 168 |                 OnPropertyChanged(nameof(IsRunning));
 169 |             }
 170 |         }
 171 |     }
 172 | 
 173 |     /// <summary>
 174 |     /// Hide the inherited <see cref="ViewModelBase.IsBusy"/> so we can
 175 |     /// also notify <see cref="IsRunning"/> (which depends on IsBusy)
 176 |     /// when the build starts / finishes. The backing field + base
 177 |     /// setter still do the heavy lifting; we just add the extra
 178 |     /// PropertyChanged notification.
 179 |     /// </summary>
 180 |     public new bool IsBusy
 181 |     {
 182 |         get => base.IsBusy;
 183 |         private set
 184 |         {
 185 |             if (base.IsBusy != value)
 186 |             {
 187 |                 base.IsBusy = value;
 188 |                 OnPropertyChanged(nameof(IsBusy));
 189 |                 OnPropertyChanged(nameof(IsRunning));
 190 |                 PauseCommand.NotifyCanExecuteChanged();
 191 |                 ResumeCommand.NotifyCanExecuteChanged();
 192 |                 CancelCommand.NotifyCanExecuteChanged();
 193 |                 StartCommand.NotifyCanExecuteChanged();
 194 |             }
 195 |         }
 196 |     }
 197 | 
 198 |     /// <summary>
 199 |     /// True while the build is in flight and not paused (i.e. the
 200 |     /// orchestrator is actively making progress). Used by the View to
 201 |     /// decide whether to show the «Пауза» button.
 202 |     /// </summary>
 203 |     public bool IsRunning => IsBusy && !IsPaused && !Completed && !Failed;
 204 | 
 205 |     /// <summary>
 206 |     /// The pre-pause stage label — captured when the user pauses so
 207 |     /// <see cref="Resume"/> can restore the orchestrator's last reported
 208 |     /// stage text (the orchestrator doesn't emit new progress events
 209 |     /// while paused, so the View would otherwise show the
 210 |     /// «Приостановлено: …» text indefinitely after resume).
 211 |     /// </summary>
 212 |     private string? _prePauseStageLabel;
 213 | 
 214 |     /// <summary>
 215 |     /// Final summary (success message + commit stats, or error text).
 216 |     /// Bound to a read-only text block under the progress bar.
 217 |     /// </summary>
 218 |     public string? FinalSummary
 219 |     {
 220 |         get => _finalSummary;
 221 |         private set => SetProperty(ref _finalSummary, value);
 222 |     }
 223 | 
 224 |     // ─── Pet-agent progress (issue #76) ──────────────────────────────
 225 |     //
 226 |     // When the user opts into pet delegations (WorldBrief screen), the
 227 |     // orchestrator runs each as a separate AI sub-task after the
 228 |     // deterministic committer stage. The progress dialog shows a
 229 |     // collapsible «Pet-агенты» section with one row per delegation, so
 230 |     // the user can see exactly what each sub-agent is doing (and review
 231 |     // the result after the build completes).
 232 |     //
 233 |     // PetProgress is pre-populated at construction time (one entry per
 234 |     // delegation, status="pending"). The orchestrator's progress callback
 235 |     // translates each Stage=="pet" event into an update on the matching
 236 |     // row (matched by the delegation label embedded in the event's Label
 237 |     // field). The section auto-expands when the first delegation starts.
 238 | 
 239 |     /// <summary>
 240 |     /// One row per <c>PetDelegation</c> passed to the orchestrator.
 241 |     /// Pre-populated at construction; updated by the progress callback as
 242 |     /// each delegation moves from pending → running → done/error.
 243 |     /// </summary>
 244 |     public ObservableCollection<PetDelegationProgress> PetProgress { get; } = new();
 245 | 
 246 |     /// <summary>True when there's at least one pet delegation to show.</summary>
 247 |     public bool HasPetProgress => PetProgress.Count > 0;
 248 | 
 249 |     private bool _isPetSectionExpanded;
 250 |     /// <summary>
 251 |     /// Whether the «Pet-агенты» section is expanded. Auto-set to true
 252 |     /// when the first delegation moves to <c>running</c> status. Bound to
 253 |     /// a toggle button so the user can fold/unfold the section manually.
 254 |     /// </summary>
 255 |     public bool IsPetSectionExpanded
 256 |     {
 257 |         get => _isPetSectionExpanded;
 258 |         private set => SetProperty(ref _isPetSectionExpanded, value);
 259 |     }
 260 | 
 261 |     /// <summary>
 262 |     /// Toggle the pet-progress section's expanded state. Bound to the
 263 |     /// section header button (so the user can fold/unfold it manually
 264 |     /// after the auto-expand on first Active event).
 265 |     /// </summary>
 266 |     [RelayCommand]
 267 |     private void TogglePetSection()
 268 |     {
 269 |         IsPetSectionExpanded = !IsPetSectionExpanded;
 270 |     }
 271 | 
 272 |     // ─── Commands ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 273 | 
 274 |     /// <summary>
 275 |     /// Auto-invoked on view load (via Avalonia's Loaded event or
 276 |     /// code-behind). Starts the orchestrator. Can be re-invoked after
 277 |     /// a failure via the «Повторить» button.
 278 |     /// </summary>
 279 |     [RelayCommand(CanExecute = nameof(CanStart))]
 280 |     private async Task StartAsync()
 281 |     {
 282 |         IsBusy = true;
 283 |         ErrorMessage = null;
 284 |         Failed = false;
 285 |         Completed = false;
 286 |         IsPaused = false;
 287 |         _prePauseStageLabel = null;
 288 |         Percent = 0;
 289 |         StageLabel = "Запуск планировщика…";
 290 |         StageDetail = null;
 291 |         FinalSummary = null;
 292 | 
 293 |         _cts = new CancellationTokenSource();
 294 |         try
 295 |         {
 296 |             var profile = _profileStore.GetOrCreate();
 297 |             var settings = _settingsStore.Load();
 298 | 
 299 |             // Validate AI settings — the planner + narrator both need them.
 300 |             if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(settings.Ai.ApiKey))
 301 |             {
 302 |                 Failed = true;
 303 |                 FinalSummary = "Не задан API-ключ. Откройте Профиль → Настройки AI и введите ключ.";
 304 |                 return;
 305 |             }
 306 | 
 307 |             // Build a fresh world + AI client + orchestrator.
 308 |             var world = DefaultWorld.Create(seed: (int)DateTimeOffset.UtcNow.ToUnixTimeSeconds());
 309 |             var ai = new AiClient(settings.Ai);
 310 |             var prompts = ServiceHost.Resolve<PromptLoader>();
 311 |             var tools = new ToolRegistry(world);
 312 | 
 313 |             var orchestrator = new WorldBuilderOrchestrator(
 314 |                 ai, world, prompts, tools, petDelegations: _petDelegations,
 315 |                 aiSettings: settings.Ai);
 316 |             _orchestrator = orchestrator;
 317 | 
 318 |             // If a saved state was loaded into this VM (via
 319 |             // LoadStateForResume), restore it on the orchestrator so the
 320 |             // resumed run skips already-completed stages.
 321 |             if (_pendingLoadedState is not null)
 322 |             {
 323 |                 orchestrator.LoadState(_pendingLoadedState);
 324 |                 _pendingLoadedState = null;
 325 |             }
 326 | 
 327 |             // Progress marshals to UI thread via Avalonia's dispatcher.
 328 |             var progress = new Progress<WorldBuildProgress>(p =>
 329 |             {
 330 |                 Avalonia.Threading.Dispatcher.UIThread.Post(() =>
 331 |                 {
 332 |                     // While paused, ignore orchestrator progress events
 333 |                     // so the «Приостановлено: …» label stays put (the
 334 |                     // orchestrator publishes a Done tick when resuming
 335 |                     // from a paused state, which would otherwise
 336 |                     // overwrite the user-facing pause indicator).
 337 |                     if (IsPaused) return;
 338 | 
 339 |                     Percent = p.Percent;
 340 |                     StageLabel = p.Label;
 341 |                     StageDetail = p.Detail;
 342 | 
 343 |                     // Translate pet-stage events into per-delegation row
 344 |                     // updates (issue #76). The orchestrator emits one
 345 |                     // Active event per delegation at the start, then a
 346 |                     // Done or Error event when it finishes. We match by
 347 |                     // the delegation label embedded in the event's Label
 348 |                     // field (format: "Pet-агент: {label}" for Active,
 349 |                     // "Pet: {label} — готово" / "Pet: {label} — ошибка"
 350 |                     // for Done/Error).
 351 |                     if (p.Stage == "pet")
 352 |                     {
 353 |                         UpdatePetProgress(p);
 354 |                     }
 355 |                 });
 356 |             });
 357 | 
 358 |             _result = await orchestrator.RunAsync(
 359 |                 new WorldPlanRequest { Brief = _brief, GenerationMode = _generationMode },
 360 |                 progress,
 361 |                 _cts.Token);
 362 | 
 363 |             if (_result.Kind == WorldBuilderResultKind.Complete)
 364 |             {
 365 |                 // Persist the built world as a save, with the opening
 366 |                 // narration as the first log entry so the game screen
 367 |                 // shows it immediately.
 368 |                 var title = _result.Plan?.Title ?? "Новый мир";
 369 |                 var meta = _saveManager.CreateSave(title, world, profile.Id);
 370 | 
 371 |                 // Issue #20 (chunked generation): stash the original
 372 |                 // WorldPlan JSON on the save's meta so the travel handler
 373 |                 // can reload it + ask the AI to fill in cold regions
 374 |                 // on-demand. We serialize with the same options the
 375 |                 // orchestrator uses for parsing (camelCase).
 376 |                 if (_result.Plan is not null)
 377 |                 {
 378 |                     var planJson = System.Text.Json.JsonSerializer.Serialize(
 379 |                         _result.Plan,
 380 |                         new System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions
 381 |                         {
 382 |                             PropertyNamingPolicy = System.Text.Json.JsonNamingPolicy.CamelCase,
 383 |                             WriteIndented = false,
 384 |                         });
 385 |                     meta = meta with { OriginalPlanJson = planJson };
 386 |                 }
 387 | 
 388 |                 if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(_result.OpeningNarration))
 389 |                 {
 390 |                     var log = new[]
 391 |                     {
 392 |                         LogEntry.Narrative(_result.OpeningNarration, authorId: null),
 393 |                     };
 394 |                     _saveManager.SaveAll(meta.Id, world, meta, log);
 395 |                 }
 396 |                 else
 397 |                 {
 398 |                     // No narration — still persist the meta update (the
 399 |                     // OriginalPlanJson field was just set on the meta).
 400 |                     _saveManager.SaveAll(meta.Id, world, meta, Array.Empty<LogEntry>());
 401 |                 }
 402 | 
 403 |                 FinalSummary = _result.Summary ?? "Мир готов.";
 404 |                 Completed = true;
 405 |                 Percent = 100;
 406 | 
 407 |                 // Build is complete — clear any saved state file so the
 408 |                 // app doesn't offer to resume a finished build on next
 409 |                 // launch.
 410 |                 try { WorldBuilderStateStore.Delete(); }
 411 |                 catch { /* non-fatal */ }
 412 |             }
 413 |             else if (_result.Kind == WorldBuilderResultKind.Cancelled)
 414 |             {
 415 |                 Failed = true;
 416 |                 FinalSummary = "Генерация отменена: " + (_result.Summary ?? "");
 417 |             }
 418 |             else
 419 |             {
 420 |                 Failed = true;
 421 |                 FinalSummary = "Генерация не удалась: " + (_result.Summary ?? "неизвестная ошибка");
 422 |             }
 423 |         }
 424 |         catch (OperationCanceledException)
 425 |         {
 426 |             Failed = true;
 427 |             FinalSummary = "Генерация отменена.";
 428 |         }
 429 |         catch (Exception ex)
 430 |         {
 431 |             Failed = true;
 432 |             FinalSummary = "Ошибка: " + ex.Message;
 433 |         }
 434 |         finally
 435 |         {
 436 |             IsBusy = false;
 437 |             IsPaused = false;
 438 |             _orchestrator = null;
 439 |             _cts?.Dispose();
 440 |             _cts = null;
 441 |         }
 442 |     }
 443 | 
 444 |     private bool CanStart() => !IsBusy && !Completed;
 445 | 
 446 |     /// <summary>
 447 |     /// Pause the in-flight build (issue #19). The orchestrator blocks at
 448 |     /// the next checkpoint (between stages / sub-stages); the UI label
 449 |     /// switches to «Приостановлено: {stage}» so the user knows what
 450 |     /// stage is waiting. No-op when not running or already paused.
 451 |     /// </summary>
 452 |     [RelayCommand(CanExecute = nameof(CanPause))]
 453 |     private void Pause()
 454 |     {
 455 |         if (_orchestrator is null) return;
 456 |         _prePauseStageLabel = StageLabel;
 457 |         _orchestrator.Pause();
 458 |         IsPaused = true;
 459 |         // Surface the pause state to the user immediately — the
 460 |         // orchestrator won't emit a new progress event until it resumes.
 461 |         StageLabel = $"Приостановлено: {_prePauseStageLabel ?? "текущая стадия"}";
 462 |     }
 463 | 
 464 |     private bool CanPause() => IsBusy && !IsPaused && !Completed && !Failed;
 465 | 
 466 |     /// <summary>
 467 |     /// Resume a paused build (issue #19). The orchestrator's polling
 468 |     /// loop exits within ~100ms and the next stage begins. Restores the
 469 |     /// pre-pause stage label so the user sees the live stage again as
 470 |     /// soon as the next progress event arrives. No-op when not paused.
 471 |     /// </summary>
 472 |     [RelayCommand(CanExecute = nameof(CanResume))]
 473 |     private void Resume()
 474 |     {
 475 |         if (_orchestrator is null) return;
 476 |         _orchestrator.Resume();
 477 |         IsPaused = false;
 478 |         // Restore the pre-pause label; the next progress tick will
 479 |         // overwrite it with the new stage's label.
 480 |         if (_prePauseStageLabel is not null)
 481 |             StageLabel = _prePauseStageLabel;
 482 |         _prePauseStageLabel = null;
 483 |     }
 484 | 
 485 |     private bool CanResume() => IsBusy && IsPaused && !Completed && !Failed;
 486 | 
 487 |     /// <summary>
 488 |     /// Cancel an in-flight build (issue #19). Distinguished from
 489 |     /// <see cref="Pause"/>: cancel terminates the run and the
 490 |     /// orchestrator's saved state is written to
 491 |     /// <c>%APPDATA%/MyGame/worldbuilder-state.json</c> so a future
 492 |     /// session can offer to resume.
 493 |     /// </summary>
 494 |     [RelayCommand(CanExecute = nameof(CanCancel))]
 495 |     private void Cancel()
 496 |     {
 497 |         // Persist the orchestrator's current state to disk BEFORE
 498 |         // triggering the cancellation — once the token fires, the
 499 |         // orchestrator returns Cancelled and its in-memory state
 500 |         // snapshots the LAST completed stage (which is exactly what we
 501 |         // want to resume from). The state file is what
 502 |         // WorldBuilderStateStore.Load() reads on next app launch.
 503 |         if (_orchestrator is not null)
 504 |         {
 505 |             try
 506 |             {
 507 |                 var state = _orchestrator.SaveState();
 508 |                 WorldBuilderStateStore.Save(state, _brief);
 509 |             }
 510 |             catch (Exception ex)
 511 |             {
 512 |                 System.Diagnostics.Trace.WriteLine(
 513 |                     $"[WorldBuildViewModel] failed to save pause state: {ex.Message}");
 514 |             }
 515 |         }
 516 | 
 517 |         try { _cts?.Cancel(); } catch { /* ignore */ }
 518 |     }
 519 | 
 520 |     private bool CanCancel() => IsBusy && _cts is not null;
 521 | 
 522 |     /// <summary>
 523 |     /// After a successful build, navigate to the character-creation
 524 |     /// screen. The CC screen loads the freshly-created save, swaps the
 525 |     /// auto-created «Странник» for the player's chosen character
 526 |     /// (name/race/class/background + class-appropriate starter gear),
 527 |     /// persists, and then navigates into the game itself.
 528 |     /// </summary>
 529 |     [RelayCommand(CanExecute = nameof(CanEnterGame))]
 530 |     private async Task EnterGameAsync()
 531 |     {
 532 |         // The save was created in StartAsync; re-read its id from the
 533 |         // SaveManager. (We could also stash the meta id on this VM, but
 534 |         // the SaveManager already has it as the most recent save.)
 535 |         var saves = _saveManager.ListSaves();
 536 |         if (saves.Count == 0)
 537 |         {
 538 |             Failed = true;
 539 |             FinalSummary = "Не удалось найти созданный сейв.";
 540 |             return;
 541 |         }
 542 |         // Route through character creation instead of jumping straight
 543 |         // into the game. The CC screen will navigate to the game itself
 544 |         // once the player picks a class / name / background.
 545 |         _shell.NavigateToCharacterCreation(saves[0].Id);
 546 |         await Task.CompletedTask;
 547 |     }
 548 | 
 549 |     private bool CanEnterGame() => Completed;
 550 | 
 551 |     /// <summary>Back to the main menu (also used after failure).</summary>
 552 |     [RelayCommand]
 553 |     private void Back() => _shell.NavigateToMenu();
 554 | 
 555 |     // ─── Resume from saved state (issue #19) ─────────────────────────
 556 |     //
 557 |     // When the app launches with a worldbuilder-state.json file on disk
 558 |     // (written by a previous Cancel()), MainMenuViewModel prompts the
 559 |     // user to resume; if they accept, it loads the state file and calls
 560 |     // MainViewModel.NavigateToWorldBuildForResume(state, brief). The
 561 |     // shell constructs this VM with the brief, then calls
 562 |     // LoadStateForResume(state) BEFORE invoking StartCommand. The state
 563 |     // is stashed in _pendingLoadedState and applied to the orchestrator
 564 |     // inside StartAsync (after the orchestrator is constructed).
 565 | 
 566 |     private WorldBuilderState? _pendingLoadedState;
 567 | 
 568 |     /// <summary>
 569 |     /// Stage a saved <see cref="WorldBuilderState"/> to be loaded into
 570 |     /// the orchestrator on the next <see cref="StartAsync"/> call. Used
 571 |     /// by the resume-from-file flow (issue #19): the main menu detects
 572 |     /// a leftover <c>worldbuilder-state.json</c>, asks the user whether
 573 |     /// to resume, and if yes, navigates to this VM with the state. The
 574 |     /// VM then runs <see cref="StartAsync"/> which calls
 575 |     /// <see cref="WorldBuilderOrchestrator.LoadState"/> before
 576 |     /// <see cref="WorldBuilderOrchestrator.RunAsync"/> so the resumed
 577 |     /// run skips already-completed stages.
 578 |     /// </summary>
 579 |     /// <param name="state">Saved state (must not be null).</param>
 580 |     /// <param name="brief">The original world brief (so the resumed
 581 |     /// run's planner call — if planning hasn't completed yet — uses the
 582 |     /// same brief as the original).</param>
 583 |     public void LoadStateForResume(WorldBuilderState state, string brief)
 584 |     {
 585 |         _pendingLoadedState = state ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(state));
 586 |         // The brief is read-only (readonly field set in ctor); we can't
 587 |         // reassign it. The caller is expected to have constructed this
 588 |         // VM with the original brief already (MainMenu reads it from
 589 |         // the state file and passes it to the NavigateToWorldBuildForResume
 590 |         // helper, which forwards it to the ctor). Defensive check: if
 591 |         // the caller passed a different brief, log + ignore — using
 592 |         // the original ctor brief keeps things simple.
 593 |         if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(brief) && brief != _brief)
 594 |         {
 595 |             System.Diagnostics.Trace.WriteLine(
 596 |                 "[WorldBuildViewModel] LoadStateForResume: brief differs from ctor brief — using ctor brief.");
 597 |         }
 598 |     }
 599 | 
 600 |     // ─── Pet-progress helpers (issue #76) ────────────────────────────
 601 | 
 602 |     /// <summary>
 603 |     /// Translate a <c>Stage == "pet"</c> progress event into an update
 604 |     /// on the matching <see cref="PetProgress"/> row. Matched by the
 605 |     /// delegation label embedded in the event's Label field — the
 606 |     /// orchestrator formats these as
 607 |     /// <list type="bullet">
 608 |     ///   <item><c>"Pet-агент: {label}"</c> (Active)</item>
 609 |     ///   <item><c>"Pet: {label} — готово"</c> (Done)</item>
 610 |     ///   <item><c>"Pet: {label} — ошибка"</c> (Error)</item>
 611 |     /// </list>
 612 |     /// so we extract the <c>{label}</c> portion and look it up by
 613 |     /// <see cref="PetDelegationProgress.Label"/>.
 614 |     /// </summary>
 615 |     private void UpdatePetProgress(WorldBuildProgress p)
 616 |     {
 617 |         var label = ExtractPetDelegationLabel(p.Label);
 618 |         if (label is null) return;
 619 |         var row = PetProgress.FirstOrDefault(r => r.Label == label);
 620 |         if (row is null) return;
 621 | 
 622 |         switch (p.Status)
 623 |         {
 624 |             case ProgressStatus.Active:
 625 |                 row.SetStatus("running");
 626 |                 row.Summary = null;
 627 |                 row.Error = null;
 628 |                 // Auto-expand the section on the first Active event so
 629 |                 // the user immediately sees what's running.
 630 |                 if (!IsPetSectionExpanded) IsPetSectionExpanded = true;
 631 |                 break;
 632 | 
 633 |             case ProgressStatus.Done:
 634 |                 row.SetStatus("done");
 635 |                 row.Summary = p.Detail;
 636 |                 row.Error = null;
 637 |                 break;
 638 | 
 639 |             case ProgressStatus.Error:
 640 |                 row.SetStatus("error");
 641 |                 row.Error = p.Detail;
 642 |                 row.Summary = null;
 643 |                 break;
 644 | 
 645 |             case ProgressStatus.Skipped:
 646 |                 // The orchestrator doesn't currently emit Skipped for the
 647 |                 // pet stage (it just runs each delegation or fails it),
 648 |                 // but handle it defensively: mark as done with no
 649 |                 // summary so the row doesn't stay "running" forever.
 650 |                 row.SetStatus("done");
 651 |                 row.Summary = "(пропущено)";
 652 |                 row.Error = null;
 653 |                 break;
 654 |         }
 655 |     }
 656 | 
 657 |     /// <summary>
 658 |     /// Extract the underlying <c>PetDelegation.Label</c> from a
 659 |     /// pet-stage progress event's Label field. Returns null if the input
 660 |     /// doesn't match either of the orchestrator's two label formats.
 661 |     /// </summary>
 662 |     private static string? ExtractPetDelegationLabel(string? eventLabel)
 663 |     {
 664 |         if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(eventLabel)) return null;
 665 |         string? rest = null;
 666 |         if (eventLabel.StartsWith("Pet-агент: ", StringComparison.Ordinal))
 667 |             rest = eventLabel["Pet-агент: ".Length..];
 668 |         else if (eventLabel.StartsWith("Pet: ", StringComparison.Ordinal))
 669 |             rest = eventLabel["Pet: ".Length..];
 670 |         if (rest is null) return null;
 671 |         // Strip the trailing " — готово" / " — ошибка" suffix the Done /
 672 |         // Error events append. Use IndexOf (not Split) so a label
 673 |         // legitimately containing " — " keeps its earlier segment.
 674 |         var dashIdx = rest.IndexOf(" — ", StringComparison.Ordinal);
 675 |         if (dashIdx >= 0) rest = rest[..dashIdx];
 676 |         return rest.Trim();
 677 |     }
 678 | }

--- src/MyGame.Desktop/Views/GameView.axaml.cs (C#, 349 lines, ~3,639 tokens) ---
   1 | using System.Linq;
   2 | using Avalonia.Collections;
   3 | using Avalonia.Controls;
   4 | using Avalonia.Input;
   5 | using Avalonia.Input.Platform;
   6 | using Avalonia.Interactivity;
   7 | using Avalonia.VisualTree;
   8 | using MyGame.Desktop.ViewModels;
   9 | using System.Collections.Specialized;
  10 | using System.ComponentModel;
  11 | 
  12 | namespace MyGame.Desktop.Views;
  13 | /// <summary>
  14 | /// Code-behind for the game view. Handles two UI-only concerns the
  15 | /// XAML can't express cleanly:
  16 | /// <list type="bullet">
  17 | ///   <item>Auto-scrolling the narrative log to the bottom when new
  18 | ///     entries arrive (subscribe to the Log collection's
  19 | ///     <see cref="INotifyCollectionChanged.CollectionChanged"/> event).</item>
  20 | ///   <item>Pressing Enter in the action input submits the action
  21 | ///     (without needing a global keybinding).</item>
  22 | ///   <item>KEYBOARD-SHORTCUTS (issue #51): global keyboard shortcuts
  23 | ///     wired via the UserControl's <c>KeyDown</c> handler — Esc to
  24 | ///     clear input, Ctrl+S to save, Ctrl+L to leave (with confirm),
  25 | ///     Ctrl+Enter to submit + refocus, 1–4 to switch side-panel tabs,
  26 | ///     F1 to toggle a help overlay.</item>
  27 | /// </list>
  28 | /// </summary>
  29 | public partial class GameView : UserControl
  30 | {
  31 |     private GameViewModel? _vm;
  32 |     private bool _autoScroll = true;
  33 | 
  34 |     public GameView()
  35 |     {
  36 |         InitializeComponent();
  37 |         DataContextChanged += OnDataContextChanged;
  38 |         // Track scroll position so the user can scroll up without the
  39 |         // view yanking them back down.
  40 |         NarrativeScroll.ScrollChanged += OnScrollChanged;
  41 |     }
  42 | 
  43 |     private void OnDataContextChanged(object? sender, System.EventArgs e)
  44 |     {
  45 |         if (_vm is not null)
  46 |         {
  47 |             ((INotifyCollectionChanged)_vm.Log).CollectionChanged -= OnLogChanged;
  48 |             _vm.PropertyChanged -= OnVmPropertyChanged;
  49 |         }
  50 |         _vm = DataContext as GameViewModel;
  51 |         if (_vm is not null)
  52 |         {
  53 |             ((INotifyCollectionChanged)_vm.Log).CollectionChanged += OnLogChanged;
  54 |             // Subscribe to VM property changes so we can auto-scroll the
  55 |             // streaming narrative TextBlock as it grows (the streaming
  56 |             // text isn't a Log entry, so the Log CollectionChanged
  57 |             // handler above doesn't fire for it).
  58 |             _vm.PropertyChanged += OnVmPropertyChanged;
  59 |         }
  60 |     }
  61 | 
  62 |     private void OnScrollChanged(object? sender, ScrollChangedEventArgs e)
  63 |     {
  64 |         // If the user scrolled away from the bottom, pause auto-scroll.
  65 |         var sv = (ScrollViewer)sender!;
  66 |         _autoScroll = sv.Offset.Y + sv.Viewport.Height >= sv.Extent.Height - 24;
  67 |     }
  68 | 
  69 |     private void OnLogChanged(object? sender, NotifyCollectionChangedEventArgs e)
  70 |     {
  71 |         if (_autoScroll && e.Action == NotifyCollectionChangedAction.Add)
  72 |         {
  73 |             // Defer to next frame so the new items have actually laid
  74 |             // out before we try to scroll to them.
  75 |             NarrativeScroll.ScrollToEnd();
  76 |         }
  77 |     }
  78 | 
  79 |     /// <summary>
  80 |     /// VM property-change handler. Used to auto-scroll the streaming
  81 |     /// narrative text as it grows (the StreamingNarrativeText property
  82 |     /// isn't backed by the Log collection, so OnLogChanged doesn't fire).
  83 |     /// </summary>
  84 |     private void OnVmPropertyChanged(object? sender, PropertyChangedEventArgs e)
  85 |     {
  86 |         if (e.PropertyName == nameof(GameViewModel.StreamingNarrativeText) && _autoScroll)
  87 |         {
  88 |             // Defer to next frame so the new text has actually laid out.
  89 |             NarrativeScroll.ScrollToEnd();
  90 |         }
  91 |     }
  92 | 
  93 |     /// <summary>
  94 |     /// Enter (without Shift) submits the action; Shift+Enter inserts a
  95 |     /// newline. Escape clears the input.
  96 |     ///
  97 |     /// <para>
  98 |     /// This handler is wired to the action input TextBox's
  99 |     /// <see cref="InputElement.KeyDown"/> event. It runs before the
 100 |     /// UserControl-level <see cref="OnGlobalKeyDown"/> handler
 101 |     /// (KeyDown bubbles from child to parent); when it sets
 102 |     /// <see cref="KeyEventArgs.Handled"/> = true, the global handler
 103 |     /// never sees the event.
 104 |     /// </para>
 105 |     /// </summary>
 106 |     private void OnActionKeyDown(object? sender, KeyEventArgs e)
 107 |     {
 108 |         if (_vm is null) return;
 109 |         if (e.Key == Key.Enter && (e.KeyModifiers & KeyModifiers.Shift) == 0)
 110 |         {
 111 |             // Ctrl+Enter falls through to the global handler (which
 112 |             // also re-focuses the input); plain Enter just submits.
 113 |             if ((e.KeyModifiers & KeyModifiers.Control) == 0 &&
 114 |                 _vm.SubmitActionCommand.CanExecute(null))
 115 |             {
 116 |                 _vm.SubmitActionCommand.Execute(null);
 117 |                 e.Handled = true;
 118 |             }
 119 |         }
 120 |         else if (e.Key == Key.Escape)
 121 |         {
 122 |             if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(_vm.CurrentAction))
 123 |             {
 124 |                 _vm.CurrentAction = string.Empty;
 125 |                 e.Handled = true;
 126 |             }
 127 |         }
 128 |     }
 129 | 
 130 |     // ─── Global keyboard shortcuts (issue #51) ────────────────────────
 131 | 
 132 |     /// <summary>
 133 |     /// Global key handler wired on the UserControl. Routes the
 134 |     /// shortcut keys (Esc, Ctrl+S, Ctrl+L, Ctrl+Enter, 1–4, F1) to
 135 |     /// their actions. The TextBox-scoped <see cref="OnActionKeyDown"/>
 136 |     /// runs first when focus is in the input; this handler picks up
 137 |     /// everything else (and everything the TextBox handler didn't
 138 |     /// mark handled).
 139 |     /// </summary>
 140 |     private void OnGlobalKeyDown(object? sender, KeyEventArgs e)
 141 |     {
 142 |         if (_vm is null) return;
 143 | 
 144 |         // F1 toggles the help overlay first — works regardless of
 145 |         // other overlays being open.
 146 |         if (e.Key == Key.F1)
 147 |         {
 148 |             HelpOverlay.IsVisible = !HelpOverlay.IsVisible;
 149 |             // Opening the help overlay dismisses the leave-confirm
 150 |             // overlay if it was open (the user is exploring shortcuts,
 151 |             // not leaving).
 152 |             if (HelpOverlay.IsVisible) LeaveConfirmOverlay.IsVisible = false;
 153 |             e.Handled = true;
 154 |             return;
 155 |         }
 156 | 
 157 |         // If the help overlay is open, only Esc/F1 close it (F1
 158 |         // handled above). Everything else is swallowed so the user
 159 |         // doesn't accidentally trigger commands while reading.
 160 |         if (HelpOverlay.IsVisible)
 161 |         {
 162 |             if (e.Key == Key.Escape)
 163 |             {
 164 |                 HelpOverlay.IsVisible = false;
 165 |                 e.Handled = true;
 166 |             }
 167 |             return;
 168 |         }
 169 | 
 170 |         // If the leave-confirm overlay is open, Esc cancels and Enter
 171 |         // confirms; other keys are swallowed.
 172 |         if (LeaveConfirmOverlay.IsVisible)
 173 |         {
 174 |             if (e.Key == Key.Escape)
 175 |             {
 176 |                 LeaveConfirmOverlay.IsVisible = false;
 177 |                 e.Handled = true;
 178 |             }
 179 |             else if (e.Key == Key.Enter)
 180 |             {
 181 |                 LeaveConfirmOverlay.IsVisible = false;
 182 |                 if (_vm.LeaveGameCommand.CanExecute(null))
 183 |                     _vm.LeaveGameCommand.Execute(null);
 184 |                 e.Handled = true;
 185 |             }
 186 |             return;
 187 |         }
 188 | 
 189 |         var mods = e.KeyModifiers;
 190 | 
 191 |         // Ctrl+S → save
 192 |         if (e.Key == Key.S && (mods & KeyModifiers.Control) != 0)
 193 |         {
 194 |             if (_vm.SaveCommand.CanExecute(null))
 195 |                 _vm.SaveCommand.Execute(null);
 196 |             e.Handled = true;
 197 |             return;
 198 |         }
 199 | 
 200 |         // Ctrl+L → show leave-confirm overlay
 201 |         if (e.Key == Key.L && (mods & KeyModifiers.Control) != 0)
 202 |         {
 203 |             LeaveConfirmOverlay.IsVisible = true;
 204 |             e.Handled = true;
 205 |             return;
 206 |         }
 207 | 
 208 |         // Ctrl+Enter → submit + refocus input (fast play loop)
 209 |         if (e.Key == Key.Enter && (mods & KeyModifiers.Control) != 0)
 210 |         {
 211 |             if (_vm.SubmitActionCommand.CanExecute(null))
 212 |                 _vm.SubmitActionCommand.Execute(null);
 213 |             // Refocus the input so the user can immediately type the
 214 |             // next action without clicking.
 215 |             ActionInput.Focus();
 216 |             e.Handled = true;
 217 |             return;
 218 |         }
 219 | 
 220 |         // Esc → clear the action input. OnActionKeyDown handles this
 221 |         // when focus is in the input; this branch covers the case
 222 |         // where focus is elsewhere (a button, a list item, …). The
 223 |         // spec says "if the input is already empty, do nothing" — we
 224 |         // don't leave the game on Esc.
 225 |         if (e.Key == Key.Escape)
 226 |         {
 227 |             if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(_vm.CurrentAction))
 228 |             {
 229 |                 _vm.CurrentAction = string.Empty;
 230 |                 e.Handled = true;
 231 |             }
 232 |             return;
 233 |         }
 234 | 
 235 |         // 1, 2, 3, 4 → switch side-panel tabs (Character / Inventory /
 236 |         // Quests / World). Only when focus is NOT in a TextBox so
 237 |         // typing "1" in the action input doesn't switch tabs. Both
 238 |         // the top-row digits (D1..D4) and the numpad digits
 239 |         // (NumPad1..NumPad4) work.
 240 |         if (e.Key is Key.D1 or Key.D2 or Key.D3 or Key.D4
 241 |                 or Key.NumPad1 or Key.NumPad2 or Key.NumPad3 or Key.NumPad4)
 242 |         {
 243 |             if (!IsFocusInTextBox())
 244 |             {
 245 |                 int idx = e.Key switch
 246 |                 {
 247 |                     Key.D1 or Key.NumPad1 => 0,
 248 |                     Key.D2 or Key.NumPad2 => 1,
 249 |                     Key.D3 or Key.NumPad3 => 2,
 250 |                     Key.D4 or Key.NumPad4 => 3,
 251 |                     _ => -1,
 252 |                 };
 253 |                 if (idx >= 0 && idx < SideTabs.ItemCount)
 254 |                 {
 255 |                     SideTabs.SelectedIndex = idx;
 256 |                     e.Handled = true;
 257 |                 }
 258 |             }
 259 |             return;
 260 |         }
 261 |     }
 262 | 
 263 |     /// <summary>
 264 |     /// True when the currently focused element is a TextBox. Used to
 265 |     /// suppress digit-key tab switching while the user is typing into
 266 |     /// the action input or chat.
 267 |     /// </summary>
 268 |     private bool IsFocusInTextBox()
 269 |     {
 270 |         var topLevel = this.FindAncestorOfType<TopLevel>();
 271 |         var focused = topLevel?.FocusManager?.GetFocusedElement();
 272 |         return focused is TextBox;
 273 |     }
 274 | 
 275 |     // ─── Help overlay handlers (issue #51) ────────────────────────────
 276 | 
 277 |     /// <summary>
 278 |     /// Click anywhere on the help overlay's dim backdrop (outside the
 279 |     /// centered panel) dismisses the overlay. Clicks on the inner
 280 |     /// panel don't reach this handler because the inner Border eats
 281 |     /// them (it's a sibling, not a child, of the backdrop — but
 282 |     /// PointerPressed still fires on the backdrop only when the
 283 |     /// pointer is over the backdrop itself).
 284 |     /// </summary>
 285 |     private void OnHelpOverlayClick(object? sender, PointerPressedEventArgs e)
 286 |     {
 287 |         HelpOverlay.IsVisible = false;
 288 |         e.Handled = true;
 289 |     }
 290 | 
 291 |     // ─── Leave-confirm overlay handlers (issue #51) ───────────────────
 292 | 
 293 |     /// <summary>
 294 |     /// Click on the dim backdrop cancels leave (matches the "Отмена"
 295 |     /// button behavior).
 296 |     /// </summary>
 297 |     private void OnLeaveConfirmOverlayClick(object? sender, PointerPressedEventArgs e)
 298 |     {
 299 |         LeaveConfirmOverlay.IsVisible = false;
 300 |         e.Handled = true;
 301 |     }
 302 | 
 303 |     /// <summary>
 304 |     /// "Выйти" button click — the Button's Command is already bound
 305 |     /// to <c>LeaveGameCommand</c>, so this handler just hides the
 306 |     /// overlay. The command itself navigates back to the menu.
 307 |     /// </summary>
 308 |     private void OnLeaveConfirmAccept(object? sender, RoutedEventArgs e)
 309 |     {
 310 |         LeaveConfirmOverlay.IsVisible = false;
 311 |         // Don't mark Handled — let the Command binding execute.
 312 |     }
 313 | 
 314 |     /// <summary>"Отмена" button click — hide the overlay, stay in game.</summary>
 315 |     private void OnLeaveConfirmCancel(object? sender, RoutedEventArgs e)
 316 |     {
 317 |         LeaveConfirmOverlay.IsVisible = false;
 318 |         e.Handled = true;
 319 |     }
 320 | 
 321 |     // ─── Lobby handlers (issue #77) ───────────────────────────────────
 322 | 
 323 |     /// <summary>
 324 |     /// Copy the lobby's share address to the clipboard so the host can
 325 |     /// paste it into a chat / email for friends. Best-effort: if the
 326 |     /// clipboard isn't available (headless, no TopLevel), the click is
 327 |     /// a silent no-op. The address stays visible in the panel regardless.
 328 |     /// </summary>
 329 |     private async void OnCopyAddressClick(object? sender, RoutedEventArgs e)
 330 |     {
 331 |         if (_vm is null) return;
 332 |         var address = _vm.ShareAddress;
 333 |         if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(address)) return;
 334 |         try
 335 |         {
 336 |             var topLevel = TopLevel.GetTopLevel(this);
 337 |             if (topLevel?.Clipboard is { } cb)
 338 |             {
 339 |                 await cb.SetTextAsync(address);
 340 |             }
 341 |         }
 342 |         catch (System.Exception ex)
 343 |         {
 344 |             System.Diagnostics.Trace.WriteLine(
 345 |                 $"[GameView] failed to copy share address: {ex.Message}");
 346 |         }
 347 |     }
 348 | }
