--- src/MyGame.Tests/AI/AiClientWithModelTests.cs (C#, 158 lines, ~1,688 tokens) ---
   1 | using System.Net;
   2 | using System.Net.Http;
   3 | using System.Text;
   4 | using MyGame.Core.AI;
   5 | 
   6 | namespace MyGame.Tests.AI;
   7 | 
   8 | /// <summary>
   9 | /// Unit tests for <see cref="AiClient.WithModel"/> (issue #26). Verifies
  10 | /// that the derived client uses the new model id while sharing the
  11 | /// underlying <see cref="HttpClient"/> (no new socket pool).
  12 | /// </summary>
  13 | public class AiClientWithModelTests
  14 | {
  15 |     [Fact]
  16 |     public void WithModel_ChangesModelOnDerivedClient()
  17 |     {
  18 |         // The derived client's Settings.Model must match the requested
  19 |         // override, not the base client's model.
  20 |         var baseClient = new AiClient(new AiSettings
  21 |         {
  22 |             Model = "gpt-4o-mini",
  23 |             ApiKey = "sk-test",
  24 |         });
  25 | 
  26 |         var derived = baseClient.WithModel("gpt-4o");
  27 | 
  28 |         Assert.Equal("gpt-4o-mini", baseClient.Settings.Model); // unchanged
  29 |         Assert.Equal("gpt-4o",       derived.Settings.Model);   // overridden
  30 |     }
  31 | 
  32 |     [Fact]
  33 |     public void WithModel_NullOrWhitespace_ReturnsSameInstance()
  34 |     {
  35 |         // When the requested model is null/empty/whitespace, WithModel
  36 |         // returns the SAME client instance (no allocation). This makes
  37 |         // the pattern `ai.WithModel(roleOverride ?? ai.Settings.Model)`
  38 |         // a no-op when no override is set.
  39 |         var baseClient = new AiClient(new AiSettings
  40 |         {
  41 |             Model = "gpt-4o-mini",
  42 |             ApiKey = "sk-test",
  43 |         });
  44 | 
  45 |         Assert.Same(baseClient, baseClient.WithModel(null));
  46 |         Assert.Same(baseClient, baseClient.WithModel(""));
  47 |         Assert.Same(baseClient, baseClient.WithModel("   "));
  48 |     }
  49 | 
  50 |     [Fact]
  51 |     public void WithModel_SameModel_ReturnsSameInstance()
  52 |     {
  53 |         // When the requested model matches the base client's model,
  54 |         // WithModel returns the same instance (no allocation). This is
  55 |         // the common case when no override is set: GetModelForRole
  56 |         // returns Model, and WithModel(Model) is a no-op.
  57 |         var baseClient = new AiClient(new AiSettings
  58 |         {
  59 |             Model = "gpt-4o-mini",
  60 |             ApiKey = "sk-test",
  61 |         });
  62 | 
  63 |         Assert.Same(baseClient, baseClient.WithModel("gpt-4o-mini"));
  64 |     }
  65 | 
  66 |     [Fact]
  67 |     public void WithModel_PreservesOtherSettings()
  68 |     {
  69 |         // The derived client inherits ALL other settings from the base
  70 |         // (BaseUrl, ApiKey, Temperature, MaxTokens) — only Model changes.
  71 |         var baseClient = new AiClient(new AiSettings
  72 |         {
  73 |             BaseUrl = "https://api.deepseek.com/v1",
  74 |             ApiKey = "sk-test",
  75 |             Model = "deepseek-chat",
  76 |             Temperature = 0.5,
  77 |             MaxTokens = 1000,
  78 |         });
  79 | 
  80 |         var derived = baseClient.WithModel("deepseek-reasoner");
  81 | 
  82 |         Assert.Equal("https://api.deepseek.com/v1", derived.Settings.BaseUrl);
  83 |         Assert.Equal("sk-test",                     derived.Settings.ApiKey);
  84 |         Assert.Equal(0.5,                           derived.Settings.Temperature);
  85 |         Assert.Equal(1000,                          derived.Settings.MaxTokens);
  86 |         Assert.Equal("deepseek-reasoner",           derived.Settings.Model);
  87 |     }
  88 | 
  89 |     [Fact]
  90 |     public async Task WithModel_DerivedClientSendsOverriddenModelInRequestBody()
  91 |     {
  92 |         // End-to-end: when the derived client makes an HTTP call, the
  93 |         // request body's `model` field must be the overridden value
  94 |         // (not the base client's model). We verify by intercepting the
  95 |         // request with a stub HttpMessageHandler and inspecting the JSON.
  96 |         var handler = new CapturingHandler();
  97 |         var http = new HttpClient(handler)
  98 |         {
  99 |             BaseAddress = new Uri("http://localhost:11434/v1/"),
 100 |         };
 101 |         var baseClient = new AiClient(
 102 |             new AiSettings { Model = "gpt-4o-mini", ApiKey = "sk-test" },
 103 |             http);
 104 | 
 105 |         var derived = baseClient.WithModel("gpt-4o");
 106 |         try
 107 |         {
 108 |             await derived.ChatAsync(new[]
 109 |             {
 110 |                 ChatMessage.System("test"),
 111 |                 ChatMessage.User("hi"),
 112 |             }, CancellationToken.None);
 113 |         }
 114 |         catch (AiException)
 115 |         {
 116 |             // The stub returns a 200 with an empty body, which the
 117 |             // AiClient's parser will reject as a Parse error. We don't
 118 |             // care about the response — we only want to inspect the
 119 |             // request body that was sent.
 120 |         }
 121 | 
 122 |         Assert.NotNull(handler.LastRequestBody);
 123 |         Assert.Contains("\"model\":\"gpt-4o\"", handler.LastRequestBody);
 124 |         Assert.DoesNotContain("\"model\":\"gpt-4o-mini\"", handler.LastRequestBody);
 125 |     }
 126 | 
 127 |     /// <summary>
 128 |     /// Minimal HttpMessageHandler that captures the request body and
 129 |     /// returns a 200 with an empty JSON object body. The body is irrelevant
 130 |     /// for the model-override tests — we only care about what was sent.
 131 |     /// </summary>
 132 |     private sealed class CapturingHandler : HttpMessageHandler
 133 |     {
 134 |         public string? LastRequestBody { get; private set; }
 135 | 
 136 |         protected override Task<HttpResponseMessage> SendAsync(
 137 |             HttpRequestMessage request,
 138 |             CancellationToken cancellationToken)
 139 |         {
 140 |             if (request.Content is HttpContent content)
 141 |             {
 142 |                 LastRequestBody = content.ReadAsStringAsync(cancellationToken)
 143 |                     .GetAwaiter().GetResult();
 144 |             }
 145 |             // Return a 200 with an empty-but-valid JSON object so the
 146 |             // AiClient's parser doesn't blow up before we get to inspect
 147 |             // the captured request body. The parser will still likely
 148 |             // raise a Parse AiException (no choices[0]) — the test
 149 |             // catches that.
 150 |             var resp = new HttpResponseMessage(HttpStatusCode.OK)
 151 |             {
 152 |                 Content = new StringContent("{}", Encoding.UTF8, "application/json"),
 153 |             };
 154 |             return Task.FromResult(resp);
 155 |         }
 156 |     }
 157 | }

--- src/MyGame.Tests/AI/AiSettingsTests.cs (C#, 181 lines, ~1,940 tokens) ---
   1 | using MyGame.Core.AI;
   2 | 
   3 | namespace MyGame.Tests.AI;
   4 | 
   5 | /// <summary>
   6 | /// Unit tests for <see cref="AiSettings"/>'s per-role model overrides
   7 | /// (issue #26). Verifies the fallback chain (role override → main Model)
   8 | /// and that null/whitespace overrides are treated as "not set".
   9 | /// </summary>
  10 | public class AiSettingsTests
  11 | {
  12 |     [Fact]
  13 |     public void GetModelForRole_NoOverrides_FallsBackToMainModel()
  14 |     {
  15 |         // When no role-specific overrides are set, every role returns
  16 |         // the main Model. This is the default behaviour for existing
  17 |         // settings.json files (which don't have the new fields).
  18 |         var s = new AiSettings { Model = "gpt-4o-mini" };
  19 | 
  20 |         Assert.Equal("gpt-4o-mini", s.GetModelForRole(AiRole.Planner));
  21 |         Assert.Equal("gpt-4o-mini", s.GetModelForRole(AiRole.GM));
  22 |         Assert.Equal("gpt-4o-mini", s.GetModelForRole(AiRole.Narrator));
  23 |         Assert.Equal("gpt-4o-mini", s.GetModelForRole(AiRole.Pet));
  24 |     }
  25 | 
  26 |     [Fact]
  27 |     public void GetModelForRole_WithOverride_ReturnsOverrideForThatRole()
  28 |     {
  29 |         // Each role-specific field, when set, overrides the main Model
  30 |         // for that role only — the other roles still fall back.
  31 |         var s = new AiSettings
  32 |         {
  33 |             Model = "gpt-4o-mini",
  34 |             PlannerModel = "gpt-4o",
  35 |             GMModel = "deepseek-chat",
  36 |             NarratorModel = "claude-3-haiku",
  37 |             PetModel = "llama3.1:8b",
  38 |         };
  39 | 
  40 |         Assert.Equal("gpt-4o",         s.GetModelForRole(AiRole.Planner));
  41 |         Assert.Equal("deepseek-chat",  s.GetModelForRole(AiRole.GM));
  42 |         Assert.Equal("claude-3-haiku", s.GetModelForRole(AiRole.Narrator));
  43 |         Assert.Equal("llama3.1:8b",    s.GetModelForRole(AiRole.Pet));
  44 |     }
  45 | 
  46 |     [Fact]
  47 |     public void GetModelForRole_WhitespaceOverride_TreatedAsUnset()
  48 |     {
  49 |         // A whitespace-only override is treated as "not set" — GetModelForRole
  50 |         // falls back to the main Model. This matches how the SettingsViewModel
  51 |         // persists empty text boxes (null) and how the user expects the
  52 |         // "по умолчанию" placeholder to behave.
  53 |         var s = new AiSettings
  54 |         {
  55 |             Model = "gpt-4o-mini",
  56 |             PlannerModel = "   ",
  57 |             GMModel = "",
  58 |             NarratorModel = "\t",
  59 |             PetModel = null,
  60 |         };
  61 | 
  62 |         Assert.Equal("gpt-4o-mini", s.GetModelForRole(AiRole.Planner));
  63 |         Assert.Equal("gpt-4o-mini", s.GetModelForRole(AiRole.GM));
  64 |         Assert.Equal("gpt-4o-mini", s.GetModelForRole(AiRole.Narrator));
  65 |         Assert.Equal("gpt-4o-mini", s.GetModelForRole(AiRole.Pet));
  66 |     }
  67 | 
  68 |     [Fact]
  69 |     public void GetModelForRole_TrimsOverride()
  70 |     {
  71 |         // Leading/trailing whitespace on a non-empty override is trimmed
  72 |         // so the model id sent to the provider is clean. (A model id with
  73 |         // a trailing space would 404 at most providers.)
  74 |         var s = new AiSettings
  75 |         {
  76 |             Model = "gpt-4o-mini",
  77 |             PlannerModel = "  gpt-4o  ",
  78 |         };
  79 | 
  80 |         Assert.Equal("gpt-4o", s.GetModelForRole(AiRole.Planner));
  81 |     }
  82 | 
  83 |     [Fact]
  84 |     public void GetModelForRole_PartialOverrides_OnlySpecifiedRolesOverride()
  85 |     {
  86 |         // A common setup: override only the planner (creative) and the GM
  87 |         // (frequent), leave narrator + pet on the main model.
  88 |         var s = new AiSettings
  89 |         {
  90 |             Model = "gpt-4o-mini",
  91 |             PlannerModel = "gpt-4o",
  92 |             GMModel = "deepseek-chat",
  93 |         };
  94 | 
  95 |         Assert.Equal("gpt-4o",        s.GetModelForRole(AiRole.Planner));
  96 |         Assert.Equal("deepseek-chat", s.GetModelForRole(AiRole.GM));
  97 |         Assert.Equal("gpt-4o-mini",   s.GetModelForRole(AiRole.Narrator));
  98 |         Assert.Equal("gpt-4o-mini",   s.GetModelForRole(AiRole.Pet));
  99 |     }
 100 | 
 101 |     [Fact]
 102 |     public void AiSettings_WithExpression_PreservesRoleOverrides()
 103 |     {
 104 |         // The record's `with` syntax must preserve role-specific fields
 105 |         // when updating other fields (e.g. changing the main Model).
 106 |         // This is the pattern used by AiClient.WithModel + the agent
 107 |         // constructors to derive role-specific clients.
 108 |         var s = new AiSettings
 109 |         {
 110 |             Model = "gpt-4o-mini",
 111 |             PlannerModel = "gpt-4o",
 112 |             GMModel = "deepseek-chat",
 113 |         };
 114 | 
 115 |         var derived = s with { MaxTokens = 500 };
 116 | 
 117 |         Assert.Equal("gpt-4o",        derived.PlannerModel);
 118 |         Assert.Equal("deepseek-chat", derived.GMModel);
 119 |         Assert.Equal("gpt-4o-mini",   derived.Model);
 120 |         Assert.Equal(500,             derived.MaxTokens);
 121 |     }
 122 | 
 123 |     [Fact]
 124 |     public void AiSettings_JsonRoundTrip_PreservesRoleOverrides()
 125 |     {
 126 |         // Serialising to JSON + deserialising back must preserve the
 127 |         // role-specific overrides (System.Text.Json's default behaviour
 128 |         // for init-only record properties — this test guards against a
 129 |         // future [JsonIgnore] accident).
 130 |         var s = new AiSettings
 131 |         {
 132 |             Model = "gpt-4o-mini",
 133 |             ApiKey = "sk-test",
 134 |             PlannerModel = "gpt-4o",
 135 |             GMModel = "deepseek-chat",
 136 |             NarratorModel = "claude-3-haiku",
 137 |             PetModel = "llama3.1:8b",
 138 |         };
 139 | 
 140 |         var json = System.Text.Json.JsonSerializer.Serialize(s);
 141 |         var back = System.Text.Json.JsonSerializer.Deserialize<AiSettings>(json);
 142 | 
 143 |         Assert.NotNull(back);
 144 |         Assert.Equal("gpt-4o",         back!.PlannerModel);
 145 |         Assert.Equal("deepseek-chat",  back.GMModel);
 146 |         Assert.Equal("claude-3-haiku", back.NarratorModel);
 147 |         Assert.Equal("llama3.1:8b",    back.PetModel);
 148 |         Assert.Equal("gpt-4o-mini",    back.Model);
 149 |         Assert.Equal("sk-test",        back.ApiKey);
 150 |     }
 151 | 
 152 |     [Fact]
 153 |     public void AiSettings_OldJsonWithoutOverrides_LoadsWithNulls()
 154 |     {
 155 |         // A settings.json written before issue #26 doesn't have the
 156 |         // role-specific fields. Deserialising it must not throw, and the
 157 |         // role fields default to null (GetModelForRole then falls back
 158 |         // to Model). This is the backward-compatibility guarantee.
 159 |         var oldJson = """
 160 |             {
 161 |               "BaseUrl": "https://api.openai.com/v1",
 162 |               "ApiKey": "sk-test",
 163 |               "Model": "gpt-4o-mini",
 164 |               "Temperature": 0.7,
 165 |               "MaxTokens": 2000
 166 |             }
 167 |             """;
 168 | 
 169 |         var back = System.Text.Json.JsonSerializer.Deserialize<AiSettings>(oldJson);
 170 | 
 171 |         Assert.NotNull(back);
 172 |         Assert.Equal("gpt-4o-mini", back!.Model);
 173 |         Assert.Null(back.PlannerModel);
 174 |         Assert.Null(back.GMModel);
 175 |         Assert.Null(back.NarratorModel);
 176 |         Assert.Null(back.PetModel);
 177 |         // And the fallback chain works as expected.
 178 |         Assert.Equal("gpt-4o-mini", back.GetModelForRole(AiRole.GM));
 179 |     }
 180 | }

--- src/MyGame.Tests/AI/Prompts/PromptLoaderHotReloadTests.cs (C#, 229 lines, ~2,598 tokens) ---
   1 | using MyGame.Core.AI.Prompts;
   2 | 
   3 | namespace MyGame.Tests.AI.Prompts;
   4 | 
   5 | /// <summary>
   6 | /// Unit tests for <see cref="PromptLoader"/>'s hot-reload feature
   7 | /// (issue #82). Verifies that when <see cref="PromptLoader.EnableHotReload"/>
   8 | /// is true, the loader reads from a disk <c>prompts/</c> folder before
   9 | /// falling back to the embedded resource — and that disk reads are
  10 | /// NOT cached (so editing the .md file + re-running Get picks up changes).
  11 | /// </summary>
  12 | public class PromptLoaderHotReloadTests : IDisposable
  13 | {
  14 |     private readonly string _tempDir;
  15 | 
  16 |     public PromptLoaderHotReloadTests()
  17 |     {
  18 |         // Per-test temp dir for the hot-reload folder. Using a fresh dir
  19 |         // per test avoids cross-test interference and doesn't touch the
  20 |         // process working directory.
  21 |         _tempDir = Path.Combine(Path.GetTempPath(), "MyGamePromptTests_" + Guid.NewGuid().ToString("N"));
  22 |         Directory.CreateDirectory(_tempDir);
  23 |     }
  24 | 
  25 |     public void Dispose()
  26 |     {
  27 |         try { Directory.Delete(_tempDir, recursive: true); }
  28 |         catch { /* best-effort */ }
  29 |     }
  30 | 
  31 |     [Fact]
  32 |     public void Get_HotReloadDisabled_FallsBackToEmbeddedResource()
  33 |     {
  34 |         // When EnableHotReload is false, the disk check is skipped
  35 |         // entirely — even if a prompts/ folder exists with the file,
  36 |         // the embedded resource is used. This is the production behaviour.
  37 |         var loader = new PromptLoader(enableHotReload: false)
  38 |         {
  39 |             HotReloadFolderOverride = _tempDir,
  40 |         };
  41 | 
  42 |         // Drop a disk file that would override the embedded system.md.
  43 |         File.WriteAllText(Path.Combine(_tempDir, "system.md"),
  44 |             "DISK OVERRIDE — should NOT be used when hot-reload is off.");
  45 | 
  46 |         var content = loader.Get("system");
  47 |         Assert.DoesNotContain("DISK OVERRIDE", content);
  48 |         // The embedded system.md should be non-empty + contain the
  49 |         // expected placeholder (system.md is a known resource).
  50 |         Assert.False(string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(content));
  51 |     }
  52 | 
  53 |     [Fact]
  54 |     public void Get_HotReloadEnabled_ReadsDiskFileWhenPresent()
  55 |     {
  56 |         // When EnableHotReload is true AND a disk file exists, the disk
  57 |         // content wins over the embedded resource. This is the dev
  58 |         // workflow: edit prompts/system.md on disk → next Get picks it up.
  59 |         var loader = new PromptLoader(enableHotReload: true)
  60 |         {
  61 |             HotReloadFolderOverride = _tempDir,
  62 |         };
  63 | 
  64 |         File.WriteAllText(Path.Combine(_tempDir, "system.md"),
  65 |             "DISK OVERRIDE — hot reload is on, this should win.");
  66 | 
  67 |         var content = loader.Get("system");
  68 |         Assert.Equal("DISK OVERRIDE — hot reload is on, this should win.", content);
  69 |     }
  70 | 
  71 |     [Fact]
  72 |     public void Get_HotReloadEnabled_FallsBackToEmbeddedWhenDiskFileMissing()
  73 |     {
  74 |         // When EnableHotReload is true BUT no disk file exists for the
  75 |         // requested name, the loader falls back to the embedded resource
  76 |         // (current behaviour). This is important: hot-reload is opt-in
  77 |         // per-prompt, not all-or-nothing.
  78 |         var loader = new PromptLoader(enableHotReload: true)
  79 |         {
  80 |             HotReloadFolderOverride = _tempDir,
  81 |         };
  82 | 
  83 |         // No file dropped — the embedded resource is used.
  84 |         var content = loader.Get("system");
  85 |         Assert.False(string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(content));
  86 |         Assert.DoesNotContain("DISK OVERRIDE", content);
  87 |     }
  88 | 
  89 |     [Fact]
  90 |     public void Get_HotReloadEnabled_DiskReadsAreNotCached()
  91 |     {
  92 |         // Disk reads must NOT be cached — editing the .md file + re-running
  93 |         // Get picks up the change immediately. This is the whole point of
  94 |         // hot-reload: the dev shouldn't have to restart the app to see
  95 |         // prompt edits.
  96 |         var loader = new PromptLoader(enableHotReload: true)
  97 |         {
  98 |             HotReloadFolderOverride = _tempDir,
  99 |         };
 100 | 
 101 |         var path = Path.Combine(_tempDir, "system.md");
 102 |         File.WriteAllText(path, "version 1");
 103 |         Assert.Equal("version 1", loader.Get("system"));
 104 | 
 105 |         // Edit the file and re-Get — must see the new content.
 106 |         File.WriteAllText(path, "version 2");
 107 |         Assert.Equal("version 2", loader.Get("system"));
 108 | 
 109 |         // And again — every call re-reads.
 110 |         File.WriteAllText(path, "version 3");
 111 |         Assert.Equal("version 3", loader.Get("system"));
 112 |     }
 113 | 
 114 |     [Fact]
 115 |     public void Get_HotReloadEnabled_DiskReadFailure_FallsBackToEmbedded()
 116 |     {
 117 |         // If the disk file exists but can't be read (e.g. locked by an
 118 |         // editor — we simulate by throwing via a missing-folder edge
 119 |         // case), the loader falls through to the embedded resource
 120 |         // rather than throwing. The dev workflow shouldn't crash the
 121 |         // app because a prompt file is briefly inaccessible.
 122 |         //
 123 |         // We can't easily simulate an IOException on File.ReadAllText
 124 |         // without a real lock, but we CAN test the "folder doesn't
 125 |         // exist" case: HotReloadFolderOverride pointing at a nonexistent
 126 |         // path → File.Exists returns false → fall through to embedded.
 127 |         var loader = new PromptLoader(enableHotReload: true)
 128 |         {
 129 |             HotReloadFolderOverride = Path.Combine(_tempDir, "does_not_exist"),
 130 |         };
 131 | 
 132 |         var content = loader.Get("system");
 133 |         Assert.False(string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(content));
 134 |     }
 135 | 
 136 |     [Fact]
 137 |     public void Get_HotReloadEnabled_UnknownPrompt_ThrowsWithHelpfulMessage()
 138 |     {
 139 |         // When neither disk nor embedded has the prompt, the loader
 140 |         // throws FileNotFoundException with a message listing available
 141 |         // embedded resources (so the dev sees what's there). The
 142 |         // hot-reload-on message also mentions the disk folder.
 143 |         var loader = new PromptLoader(enableHotReload: true)
 144 |         {
 145 |             HotReloadFolderOverride = _tempDir,
 146 |         };
 147 | 
 148 |         var ex = Assert.Throws<FileNotFoundException>(() => loader.Get("this_prompt_does_not_exist"));
 149 |         Assert.Contains("this_prompt_does_not_exist", ex.Message);
 150 |         Assert.Contains("prompts/", ex.Message); // hint about the disk folder
 151 |     }
 152 | 
 153 |     [Fact]
 154 |     public void Exists_HotReloadEnabled_ReturnsTrueForDiskFile()
 155 |     {
 156 |         // Exists must return true when a disk file is present (even if
 157 |         // no embedded resource matches). This lets callers probe for
 158 |         // optional prompts that exist only on disk during dev.
 159 |         var loader = new PromptLoader(enableHotReload: true)
 160 |         {
 161 |             HotReloadFolderOverride = _tempDir,
 162 |         };
 163 | 
 164 |         // No disk file yet → Exists returns false for an unknown name.
 165 |         Assert.False(loader.Exists("nonexistent_prompt_xyz"));
 166 | 
 167 |         // Drop a disk file → Exists returns true.
 168 |         File.WriteAllText(Path.Combine(_tempDir, "nonexistent_prompt_xyz.md"), "content");
 169 |         Assert.True(loader.Exists("nonexistent_prompt_xyz"));
 170 |     }
 171 | 
 172 |     [Fact]
 173 |     public void Exists_HotReloadDisabled_OnlyChecksEmbeddedResources()
 174 |     {
 175 |         // When hot-reload is off, Exists only checks the embedded
 176 |         // resources — disk files are invisible. This is the production
 177 |         // behaviour: the disk folder is a dev-time convenience.
 178 |         var loader = new PromptLoader(enableHotReload: false)
 179 |         {
 180 |             HotReloadFolderOverride = _tempDir,
 181 |         };
 182 | 
 183 |         // Drop a disk file for a prompt that DOESN'T exist as an
 184 |         // embedded resource. Exists must return false.
 185 |         File.WriteAllText(Path.Combine(_tempDir, "disk_only_prompt.md"), "content");
 186 |         Assert.False(loader.Exists("disk_only_prompt"));
 187 | 
 188 |         // But a prompt that DOES exist as an embedded resource still
 189 |         // returns true.
 190 |         Assert.True(loader.Exists("system"));
 191 |     }
 192 | 
 193 |     [Fact]
 194 |     public void Constructor_DefaultHotReloadFlag_MatchesBuildConfiguration()
 195 |     {
 196 |         // The parameterless ctor sets EnableHotReload based on the build
 197 |         // configuration: true in DEBUG, false in RELEASE. We can't test
 198 |         // both configurations from a single test run, but we CAN verify
 199 |         // the flag is set (whatever the default is for THIS build).
 200 |         var loader = new PromptLoader();
 201 | #if DEBUG
 202 |         Assert.True(loader.EnableHotReload);
 203 | #else
 204 |         Assert.False(loader.EnableHotReload);
 205 | #endif
 206 |     }
 207 | 
 208 |     [Fact]
 209 |     public void EnableHotReload_CanBeToggledAtRuntime()
 210 |     {
 211 |         // The EnableHotReload flag is settable at runtime so a host (or
 212 |         // test) can flip the behaviour without rebuilding. This is
 213 |         // useful for a release build with a developer-mode flag.
 214 |         var loader = new PromptLoader(enableHotReload: false)
 215 |         {
 216 |             HotReloadFolderOverride = _tempDir,
 217 |         };
 218 |         Assert.False(loader.EnableHotReload);
 219 | 
 220 |         // Drop a disk file — initially ignored.
 221 |         File.WriteAllText(Path.Combine(_tempDir, "system.md"), "DISK VERSION");
 222 |         Assert.DoesNotContain("DISK VERSION", loader.Get("system"));
 223 | 
 224 |         // Flip the flag — now the disk version is used.
 225 |         loader.EnableHotReload = true;
 226 |         Assert.Equal("DISK VERSION", loader.Get("system"));
 227 |     }
 228 | }

--- src/MyGame.Tests/AI/StubAiClient.cs (C#, 209 lines, ~2,560 tokens) ---
   1 | using System.Runtime.CompilerServices;
   2 | using MyGame.Core.AI;
   3 | 
   4 | namespace MyGame.Tests.AI;
   5 | 
   6 | /// <summary>
   7 | /// Test double for <see cref="IAiClient"/>. Returns canned
   8 | /// <see cref="ChatResponse"/> objects in FIFO order, counts calls, and
   9 | /// never makes a real HTTP request. Used by the multiplayer integration
  10 | /// tests (issue #57) to drive the GameMaster through a deterministic
  11 | /// turn without contacting an LLM provider.
  12 | ///
  13 | /// <para>
  14 | /// <b>Construction:</b> pass either a fixed list of canned responses
  15 | /// (each call dequeues the next; throws when the queue is empty) or a
  16 | /// single response that's returned on every call. The
  17 | /// <see cref="Settings"/> property defaults to a minimal
  18 | /// <see cref="AiSettings"/>; pass your own if the code under test reads
  19 | /// <see cref="IAiClient.Settings"/> (e.g. for role-specific model
  20 | /// derivation).
  21 | /// </para>
  22 | ///
  23 | /// <para>
  24 | /// <b>Streaming behaviour:</b>
  25 | /// <see cref="StreamChatWithToolsAsync"/> yields the canned
  26 | /// <see cref="ChatResponse.Content"/> as a single delta and then writes
  27 | /// the full <see cref="ChatResponse"/> into the supplied
  28 | /// <see cref="StreamChatResult"/>. This terminates the GameMaster's
  29 | /// tool-call loop on iteration 1 (no tool calls → loop exits), so the
  30 | /// stub can be used to drive a complete GM turn with one canned
  31 | /// response.
  32 | /// </para>
  33 | ///
  34 | /// <para>
  35 | /// <see cref="WithModel"/> returns <c>this</c> — model overrides are
  36 | /// meaningless for a stub returning canned responses, and returning the
  37 | /// same instance matches the production <see cref="AiClient.WithModel"/>
  38 | /// no-op-when-same-model contract.
  39 | /// </para>
  40 | /// </summary>
  41 | public sealed class StubAiClient : IAiClient
  42 | {
  43 |     private readonly Queue<ChatResponse> _responses;
  44 |     private readonly AiSettings _settings;
  45 |     private int _chatWithToolsCallCount;
  46 |     private int _chatCallCount;
  47 |     private int _streamChatWithToolsCallCount;
  48 |     private int _streamChatCallCount;
  49 | 
  50 |     /// <summary>
  51 |     /// Create a stub that returns the given responses in order, one per
  52 |     /// call to <see cref="ChatAsync"/> /
  53 |     /// <see cref="ChatWithToolsAsync"/> /
  54 |     /// <see cref="StreamChatWithToolsAsync"/> /
  55 |     /// <see cref="StreamChatAsync"/>. The queue is shared across all
  56 |     /// four methods (each call dequeues the next response regardless of
  57 |     /// which method was called). Throws <see cref="InvalidOperationException"/>
  58 |     /// if a call is made after the queue is empty.
  59 |     /// </summary>
  60 |     /// <param name="responses">Canned responses, returned in FIFO order.</param>
  61 |     /// <param name="settings">Optional settings exposed via
  62 |     /// <see cref="Settings"/>. Defaults to a minimal
  63 |     /// <see cref="AiSettings"/> with a placeholder model id.</param>
  64 |     public StubAiClient(IEnumerable<ChatResponse> responses, AiSettings? settings = null)
  65 |     {
  66 |         _responses = new Queue<ChatResponse>(responses ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(responses)));
  67 |         _settings = settings ?? new AiSettings { Model = "stub-model", ApiKey = "stub-key" };
  68 |     }
  69 | 
  70 |     /// <summary>
  71 |     /// Convenience: create a stub that always returns the same response.
  72 |     /// Equivalent to passing a single-element queue, but the response is
  73 |     /// re-enqueued after each call so the stub never runs out.
  74 |     /// </summary>
  75 |     public StubAiClient(ChatResponse response, AiSettings? settings = null)
  76 |         : this(Enumerable.Repeat(response, 1), settings)
  77 |     {
  78 |         _repeatForever = response;
  79 |     }
  80 | 
  81 |     private readonly ChatResponse? _repeatForever;
  82 | 
  83 |     /// <summary>
  84 |     /// The settings exposed via <see cref="IAiClient.Settings"/>. Tests
  85 |     /// can read this to assert on what the code under test passed in,
  86 |     /// and the code under test can read it for role-specific model
  87 |     /// derivation (returns the stub's settings, which don't affect the
  88 |     /// canned responses).
  89 |     /// </summary>
  90 |     public AiSettings Settings => _settings;
  91 | 
  92 |     /// <summary>Number of times <see cref="ChatWithToolsAsync"/> was called.</summary>
  93 |     public int ChatWithToolsCallCount => Volatile.Read(ref _chatWithToolsCallCount);
  94 | 
  95 |     /// <summary>Number of times <see cref="ChatAsync"/> was called.</summary>
  96 |     public int ChatCallCount => Volatile.Read(ref _chatCallCount);
  97 | 
  98 |     /// <summary>Number of times <see cref="StreamChatWithToolsAsync"/> was called.</summary>
  99 |     public int StreamChatWithToolsCallCount => Volatile.Read(ref _streamChatWithToolsCallCount);
 100 | 
 101 |     /// <summary>Number of times <see cref="StreamChatAsync"/> was called.</summary>
 102 |     public int StreamChatCallCount => Volatile.Read(ref _streamChatCallCount);
 103 | 
 104 |     /// <summary>
 105 |     /// Total calls across all four methods. Useful for asserting "the
 106 |     /// GM made exactly one AI call this turn" without caring which
 107 |     /// overload was used.
 108 |     /// </summary>
 109 |     public int TotalCallCount => ChatWithToolsCallCount + ChatCallCount
 110 |         + StreamChatWithToolsCallCount + StreamChatCallCount;
 111 | 
 112 |     /// <summary>
 113 |     /// Returns <c>this</c>. Model overrides are meaningless for a stub
 114 |     /// returning canned responses, and returning the same instance
 115 |     /// matches the production AiClient.WithModel no-op-when-same-model
 116 |     /// contract.
 117 |     /// </summary>
 118 |     public IAiClient WithModel(string? model) => this;
 119 | 
 120 |     /// <summary>
 121 |     /// Dequeue the next canned response. Throws if the queue is empty
 122 |     /// (unless the stub was constructed via the single-response
 123 |     /// convenience ctor, in which case the response is re-enqueued
 124 |     /// forever).
 125 |     /// </summary>
 126 |     public Task<ChatResponse> ChatAsync(
 127 |         IReadOnlyList<ChatMessage> messages,
 128 |         CancellationToken ct = default)
 129 |     {
 130 |         Interlocked.Increment(ref _chatCallCount);
 131 |         return Task.FromResult(NextResponse());
 132 |     }
 133 | 
 134 |     /// <summary>
 135 |     /// Dequeue the next canned response. The <paramref name="tools"/>
 136 |     /// argument is ignored — the stub doesn't generate tool calls unless
 137 |     /// the canned response carries them.
 138 |     /// </summary>
 139 |     public Task<ChatResponse> ChatWithToolsAsync(
 140 |         IReadOnlyList<ChatMessage> messages,
 141 |         IReadOnlyList<ToolDefinition>? tools,
 142 |         CancellationToken ct = default)
 143 |     {
 144 |         Interlocked.Increment(ref _chatWithToolsCallCount);
 145 |         return Task.FromResult(NextResponse());
 146 |     }
 147 | 
 148 |     /// <summary>
 149 |     /// Yield the canned response's content (if any) as a single delta.
 150 |     /// Token counts and tool calls are NOT surfaced via this streaming
 151 |     /// overload — use <see cref="StreamChatWithToolsAsync"/> for those.
 152 |     /// </summary>
 153 |     public async IAsyncEnumerable<string> StreamChatAsync(
 154 |         IReadOnlyList<ChatMessage> messages,
 155 |         [EnumeratorCancellation] CancellationToken ct = default)
 156 |     {
 157 |         Interlocked.Increment(ref _streamChatCallCount);
 158 |         var response = NextResponse();
 159 |         if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(response.Content))
 160 |             yield return response.Content;
 161 |         await Task.CompletedTask.ConfigureAwait(false);
 162 |     }
 163 | 
 164 |     /// <summary>
 165 |     /// Yield the canned response's content (if any) as a single delta
 166 |     /// and write the full <see cref="ChatResponse"/> (content + tool
 167 |     /// calls + token counts + finish reason) into <paramref name="result"/>.
 168 |     /// The GameMaster's tool-call loop sees the response, processes any
 169 |     /// tool calls, and either continues (if tool calls were present) or
 170 |     /// terminates (if not). A canned response with no tool calls
 171 |     /// terminates the loop on iteration 1.
 172 |     /// </summary>
 173 |     public async IAsyncEnumerable<string> StreamChatWithToolsAsync(
 174 |         IReadOnlyList<ChatMessage> messages,
 175 |         IReadOnlyList<ToolDefinition>? tools,
 176 |         StreamChatResult result,
 177 |         [EnumeratorCancellation] CancellationToken ct = default)
 178 |     {
 179 |         if (result is null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(result));
 180 |         Interlocked.Increment(ref _streamChatWithToolsCallCount);
 181 |         var response = NextResponse();
 182 |         result.Response = response;
 183 |         if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(response.Content))
 184 |             yield return response.Content;
 185 |         await Task.CompletedTask.ConfigureAwait(false);
 186 |     }
 187 | 
 188 |     /// <summary>
 189 |     /// Dequeue the next response. If the stub was constructed via the
 190 |     /// single-response convenience ctor, the response is re-enqueued
 191 |     /// forever (so the stub never runs out). Otherwise throws when the
 192 |     /// queue is empty — that's a test bug (the code under test made
 193 |     /// more AI calls than the test set up canned responses for).
 194 |     /// </summary>
 195 |     private ChatResponse NextResponse()
 196 |     {
 197 |         if (_repeatForever is not null)
 198 |             return _repeatForever;
 199 |         lock (_responses)
 200 |         {
 201 |             if (_responses.Count == 0)
 202 |                 throw new InvalidOperationException(
 203 |                     "StubAiClient response queue is empty. The code under test made " +
 204 |                     "more AI calls than the test supplied canned responses for.");
 205 |             return _responses.Dequeue();
 206 |         }
 207 |     }
 208 | }

--- src/MyGame.Tests/AI/Tools/CombatDeathToolsTests.cs (C#, 376 lines, ~3,985 tokens) ---
   1 | using MyGame.Core.AI.Tools;
   2 | using MyGame.Core.Common;
   3 | using MyGame.Core.World;
   4 | using MyGame.Core.World.Entities;
   5 | 
   6 | // 'World' is both a namespace and a type — alias the type so the
   7 | // compiler doesn't see this as namespace usage when we type a variable
   8 | // as the World type. We can still use the namespace import for other
   9 | // types like DefaultWorld / CombatState.
  10 | using GameWorld = MyGame.Core.World.World;
  11 | 
  12 | namespace MyGame.Tests.AI.Tools;
  13 | 
  14 | /// <summary>
  15 | /// COMBAT-DEATH (issues #88, #63): targeted unit tests for the four
  16 | /// new combat/death tools (start_combat / end_combat / next_turn /
  17 | /// death_save) plus the deal_damage branches they interact with
  18 | /// (player death-saves, NPC death during combat, auto-end combat).
  19 | /// </summary>
  20 | public class CombatDeathToolsTests
  21 | {
  22 |     private static (ToolRegistry reg, GameWorld world) MakeRegistry(long seed = 42)
  23 |     {
  24 |         var world = DefaultWorld.Create(seed: seed);
  25 |         return (new ToolRegistry(world), world);
  26 |     }
  27 | 
  28 |     private static Npc SpawnHostile(GameWorld world, string name, int hp, int dex = 10)
  29 |     {
  30 |         var npc = new Npc
  31 |         {
  32 |             Id = EntityId.NewId(),
  33 |             Name = name,
  34 |             LocationId = world.ActivePlayer!.LocationId,
  35 |             Disposition = "hostile",
  36 |             IsAlive = true,
  37 |             Attributes = new() { ["dex"] = dex },
  38 |             Resources = new() { ["hp"] = hp, ["ac"] = 10 },
  39 |         };
  40 |         world.SpawnNpc(npc);
  41 |         return npc;
  42 |     }
  43 | 
  44 |     [Fact]
  45 |     public async Task StartCombat_InstallsCombatState_WithPlayerPlusHostiles()
  46 |     {
  47 |         var (reg, world) = MakeRegistry();
  48 |         var hostile = SpawnHostile(world, "Гоблин", hp: 7, dex: 14);
  49 | 
  50 |         var result = await reg.ExecuteAsync("c1", "start_combat", "{}");
  51 |         Assert.False(result.IsError);
  52 |         Assert.NotNull(world.Combat);
  53 |         Assert.True(world.Combat!.Active);
  54 |         Assert.Equal(1, world.Combat.Round);
  55 |         Assert.Equal(0, world.Combat.CurrentActorIndex);
  56 | 
  57 |         // Player + 1 hostile = 2 combatants.
  58 |         Assert.Equal(2, world.Combat.TurnOrder.Count);
  59 |         // Both combatants should appear by name in the result.
  60 |         Assert.Contains("Странник", result.Content);
  61 |         Assert.Contains("Гоблин", result.Content);
  62 |         // Initiative should be listed.
  63 |         Assert.Contains("Инициатива", result.Content);
  64 |     }
  65 |

 106 |     public async Task NextTurn_AdvancesActor_WrapsAndIncrementsRound()
 107 |     {
 108 |         var (reg, world) = MakeRegistry();

 110 |         SpawnHostile(world, "Гоблин 2", hp: 7);

 113 |         var combat = world.Combat!;

 115 |         Assert.True(count >= 3); // player + 2 goblins
 116 |

 118 |         var r1 = await reg.ExecuteAsync("c2", "next_turn", "{}");
 119 |         Assert.False(r1.IsError);

 121 |         Assert.Equal(1, combat.Round);
 122 |

 124 |         var r2 = await reg.ExecuteAsync("c3", "next_turn", "{}");
 125 |         Assert.False(r2.IsError);
 126 |         Assert.Equal(2, combat.CurrentActorIndex);
 127 |         Assert.Equal(1, combat.Round);
 128 | 
 129 |         // Third next_turn → wraps to 0, round 2.
 130 |         var r3 = await reg.ExecuteAsync("c4", "next_turn", "{}");
 131 |         Assert.False(r3.IsError);
 132 |         Assert.Equal(0, combat.CurrentActorIndex);
 133 |         Assert.Equal(2, combat.Round);

 222 |     [Fact]
 223 |     public async Task DeathSave_Stabilizes_AfterThreeSuccesses()
 224 |     {
 225 |         // Force the RNG so death_save rolls 10 (success) three times.
 226 |         // The Rng is a PCG32 — we can't directly inject a "10 next", so
 227 |         // we retry until we get three successes OR rig the world so the
 228 |         // player already has 2 successes going in.
 229 |         var (reg, world) = MakeRegistry();
 230 |         var p = world.ActivePlayer!;
 231 |         p.Resources["hp"] = 0;
 232 |         world.Flags ??= new Dictionary<string, object>();
 233 |         world.Flags["deathSaves"] = "2,0"; // 2 successes already.
 234 | 
 235 |         // Loop until we roll a 10+ to confirm stabilization, capping
 236 |         // attempts so the test can't infinite-loop on a bad seed.
 237 |         string lastContent = "";
 238 |         bool stabilized = false;
 239 |         for (int i = 0; i < 200; i++)
 240 |         {
 241 |             var result = await reg.ExecuteAsync("c1", "death_save", "{}");
 242 |             Assert.False(result.IsError);
 243 |             lastContent = result.Content;
 244 |             if (result.Content.Contains("стабилизирован"))
 245 |             {
 246 |                 stabilized = true;
 247 |                 break;
 248 |             }
 249 |             // If we died, the test setup is wrong.
 250 |             Assert.DoesNotContain("Игрок погиб", result.Content);
 251 |         }
 252 |         Assert.True(stabilized, $"Expected to stabilize within 200 rolls. Last: {lastContent}");
 253 |         // Player should still be alive but at 0 HP.
 254 |         Assert.True(p.IsAlive);
 255 |         Assert.Equal(0, p.Resources["hp"]);
 256 |     }
 257 | 
 258 |     [Fact]
 259 |     public async Task DeathSave_NaturalTwenty_RegainsOneHP()
 260 |     {
 261 |         var (reg, world) = MakeRegistry();
 262 |         var p = world.ActivePlayer!;
 263 |         p.Resources["hp"] = 0;
 264 |         world.Flags ??= new Dictionary<string, object>();
 265 |         world.Flags["deathSaves"] = "0,2"; // close to death.
 266 | 
 267 |         bool natural20 = false;
 268 |         for (int i = 0; i < 2000; i++)
 269 |         {
 270 |             // Reset state each iteration so we can keep trying for a
 271 |             // natural 20.
 272 |             p.Resources["hp"] = 0;
 273 |             world.Flags["deathSaves"] = "0,2";
 274 |             p.IsAlive = true;
 275 |             var result = await reg.ExecuteAsync("c1", "death_save", "{}");
 276 |             Assert.False(result.IsError);
 277 |             if (result.Content.Contains("Natural 20"))
 278 |             {
 279 |                 natural20 = true;
 280 |                 // After a nat 20, the player should be at 1 HP and
 281 |                 // conscious (alive), and the deathSaves flag should be
 282 |                 // cleared (no longer needed).
 283 |                 Assert.Equal(1, p.Resources["hp"]);
 284 |                 Assert.True(p.IsAlive);
 285 |                 Assert.False(world.Flags.ContainsKey("deathSaves"));
 286 |                 break;
 287 |             }
 288 |         }
 289 |         Assert.True(natural20, "Expected to roll a natural 20 within 2000 attempts.");
 290 |     }
 291 | 
 292 |     [Fact]
 293 |     public async Task DeathSave_ThreeFailures_KillsPlayer()
 294 |     {
 295 |         // Set the player up at 2 failures, then loop until we either
 296 |         // roll a 1 (2 failures → 4 clamped to 3 → dead) or accumulate
 297 |         // 3 failures through normal rolls.
 298 |         var (reg, world) = MakeRegistry();
 299 |         var p = world.ActivePlayer!;
 300 |         p.Resources["hp"] = 0;
 301 |         world.Flags ??= new Dictionary<string, object>();
 302 |         world.Flags["deathSaves"] = "0,2";
 303 | 
 304 |         bool died = false;
 305 |         for (int i = 0; i < 2000; i++)
 306 |         {
 307 |             if (!p.IsAlive)
 308 |             {
 309 |                 died = true;
 310 |                 break;
 311 |             }
 312 |             // Reset HP / saves for retry if a nat 20 happened.
 313 |             if (p.Resources.TryGetValue("hp", out var hp) && hp > 0)
 314 |             {
 315 |                 p.Resources["hp"] = 0;
 316 |                 world.Flags["deathSaves"] = "0,2";
 317 |             }
 318 |             var result = await reg.ExecuteAsync("c1", "death_save", "{}");
 319 |             Assert.False(result.IsError);
 320 |         }
 321 |         Assert.True(died, "Expected the player to die within 2000 attempts.");
 322 |         Assert.False(p.IsAlive);
 323 |         // The deathSaves flag should be cleared once the player dies
 324 |         // (no longer tracking — they're dead).
 325 |         Assert.False(world.Flags!.ContainsKey("deathSaves"));
 326 |     }

 328 |     [Fact]
 329 |     public async Task GetLocation_MarksDeadNpcsAsDead()
 330 |     {
 331 |         var (reg, world) = MakeRegistry();
 332 |         var goblin = SpawnHostile(world, "Гоблин", hp: 5);
 333 |         // Kill the goblin directly (no combat).
 334 |         goblin.IsAlive = false;
 335 | 
 336 |         var result = await reg.ExecuteAsync("c1", "get_location", "{}");
 337 |         Assert.False(result.IsError);
 338 |         Assert.Contains("Гоблин", result.Content);
 339 |         Assert.Contains("мёртв", result.Content);
 340 |     }

 342 |     [Fact]
 343 |     public void CombatState_SerializesAndRoundTrips()
 344 |     {
 345 |         var world = new GameWorld();
 346 |         var cs = new CombatState
 347 |         {
 348 |             Active = true,
 349 |             Round = 3,
 350 |             TurnOrder = new()
 351 |             {
 352 |                 new Combatant(EntityId.NewId(), "Игрок", 18, true),
 353 |                 new Combatant(EntityId.NewId(), "Гоблин", 12, false),
 354 |             },
 355 |             CurrentActorIndex = 1,
 356 |             StartedAtTurn = 7,
 357 |         };
 358 |         world.Combat = cs;
 359 | 
 360 |         var json = world.ToJson();
 361 |         var restored = GameWorld.FromJson(json, world.Registries);
 362 | 
 363 |         Assert.NotNull(restored.Combat);
 364 |         Assert.True(restored.Combat!.Active);
 365 |         Assert.Equal(3, restored.Combat.Round);
 366 |         Assert.Equal(2, restored.Combat.TurnOrder.Count);
 367 |         Assert.Equal("Игрок", restored.Combat.TurnOrder[0].Name);
 368 |         Assert.Equal("Гоблин", restored.Combat.TurnOrder[1].Name);
 369 |         Assert.Equal(18, restored.Combat.TurnOrder[0].Initiative);
 370 |         Assert.True(restored.Combat.TurnOrder[0].HasActedThisRound);
 371 |         Assert.False(restored.Combat.TurnOrder[1].HasActedThisRound);
 372 |         Assert.Equal(1, restored.Combat.CurrentActorIndex);
 373 |         Assert.Equal(7, restored.Combat.StartedAtTurn);
 374 |     }
 375 | }

--- src/MyGame.Tests/AI/Tools/EngineDepthToolsTests.cs (C#, 558 lines, ~5,558 tokens) ---
   1 | using MyGame.Core.AI.Tools;
   2 | using MyGame.Core.World;
   3 | using MyGame.Core.World.Entities;
   4 | 
   5 | // 'World' is both a namespace and a type — alias the type so the
   6 | // compiler doesn't see this as namespace usage when we type a variable
   7 | // as the World type. Matches the convention in CombatDeathToolsTests.
   8 | using GameWorld = MyGame.Core.World.World;
   9 | 
  10 | namespace MyGame.Tests.AI.Tools;
  11 | 
  12 | /// <summary>
  13 | /// ENGINE-DEPTH (issues #34 / #36 / #43): targeted unit tests for the
  14 | /// five new engine-depth tools — set_weather / get_weather /
  15 | /// adjust_reputation / get_factions / get_lore — plus the
  16 | /// WorldBuilderCommitter.CommitFactions + CommitLore stages. All tools
  17 | /// are optional (no-op on worlds that haven't activated the subsystem),
  18 | /// so these tests cover both the activated and the inactive paths.
  19 | /// </summary>
  20 | public class EngineDepthToolsTests
  21 | {
  22 |     private static (ToolRegistry reg, GameWorld world) MakeRegistry(long seed = 42)
  23 |     {
  24 |         var world = DefaultWorld.Create(seed: seed);
  25 |         return (new ToolRegistry(world), world);
  26 |     }
  27 | 
  28 |     // ─── Weather (issue #34) ──────────────────────────────────────────────
  29 | 
  30 |     [Fact]
  31 |     public async Task SetWeather_SetsCurrentWeatherAndForecast()
  32 |     {
  33 |         var (reg, world) = MakeRegistry();
  34 |         Assert.Null(world.CurrentWeather);
  35 | 
  36 |         var result = await reg.ExecuteAsync("c1", "set_weather",
  37 |             """{"weather":"rain","forecast":"К вечеру гроза"}""");
  38 |         Assert.False(result.IsError);
  39 |         Assert.Equal("rain", world.CurrentWeather);
  40 |         Assert.Equal("К вечеру гроза", world.WeatherForecast);
  41 |         Assert.Contains("Дождь", result.Content);
  42 |         Assert.Contains("К вечеру гроза", result.Content);
  43 |     }
  44 | 
  45 |     [Fact]
  46 |     public async Task SetWeather_NoForecast_Works()
  47 |     {
  48 |         var (reg, world) = MakeRegistry();
  49 |         var result = await reg.ExecuteAsync("c1", "set_weather",
  50 |             """{"weather":"fog"}""");
  51 |         Assert.False(result.IsError);
  52 |         Assert.Equal("fog", world.CurrentWeather);
  53 |         Assert.Null(world.WeatherForecast);
  54 |         Assert.Contains("Туман", result.Content);
  55 |     }
  56 |

  59 |     {

  61 |         var result = await reg.ExecuteAsync("c1", "set_weather",
  62 |             """{"weather":"hurricane"}""");
  63 |         Assert.True(result.IsError);
  64 |         Assert.Null(world.CurrentWeather);
  65 |         Assert.Contains("hurricane", result.Content);
  66 |         // The valid list should be enumerated.
  67 |         Assert.Contains("clear", result.Content);
  68 |         Assert.Contains("rain", result.Content);
  69 |     }

  76 |         Assert.True(result.IsError);
  77 |         Assert.Contains("weather", result.Content);
  78 |     }

  81 |     public async Task SetWeather_NormalizesCase()
  82 |     {
  83 |         // Model may emit "Rain" or "RAIN" — normalize to lowercase.
  84 |         var (reg, world) = MakeRegistry();
  85 |         var result = await reg.ExecuteAsync("c1", "set_weather",
  86 |             """{"weather":"RAIN"}""");
  87 |         Assert.False(result.IsError);
  88 |         Assert.Equal("rain", world.CurrentWeather);
  89 |     }

 101 |     public async Task GetWeather_WhenSet_ReturnsLabelAndForecast()
 102 |     {
 103 |         var (reg, world) = MakeRegistry();
 104 |         world.CurrentWeather = "storm";
 105 |         world.WeatherForecast = "Ураган усиливается";
 106 | 
 107 |         var result = await reg.ExecuteAsync("c1", "get_weather", "{}");
 108 |         Assert.False(result.IsError);

 114 |     [InlineData("clear", "Ясно")]
 115 |     [InlineData("rain", "Дождь")]
 116 |     [InlineData("storm", "Гроза")]
 117 |     [InlineData("fog", "Туман")]
 118 |     [InlineData("snow", "Снег")]
 119 |     [InlineData("overcast", "Облачно")]
 120 |     public async Task SetWeather_AllCanonicalTags_Accepted(string tag, string label)
 121 |     {
 122 |         var (reg, world) = MakeRegistry();
 123 |         var result = await reg.ExecuteAsync("c1", "set_weather",
 124 |             $$"""{"weather":"{{tag}}"}""");
 125 |         Assert.False(result.IsError);
 126 |         Assert.Equal(tag, world.CurrentWeather);
 127 |         Assert.Contains(label, result.Content);

 156 |     [Fact]
 157 |     public async Task AdjustReputation_PositiveDelta_ClampsAndUpdatesAlignment()
 158 |     {
 159 |         var world = MakeWorldWithFactions();
 160 |         var reg = new ToolRegistry(world);
 161 | 
 162 |         var result = await reg.ExecuteAsync("c1", "adjust_reputation",
 163 |             """{"factionName":"Орден Серебряной Чаши","delta":40}""");
 164 |         Assert.False(result.IsError);
 165 |         var f = world.Factions.First(f => f.Name == "Орден Серебряной Чаши");
 166 |         Assert.Equal(40, f.Reputation);
 167 |         Assert.Equal("ally", f.Alignment);
 168 |         Assert.Contains("40", result.Content);
 169 |         Assert.Contains("ally", result.Content);
 170 |     }

 366 |     // ─── WorldBuilderCommitter stages ─────────────────────────────────────
 367 | 
 368 |     [Fact]
 369 |     public void CommitFactions_RegistersAllPlanFactions_NeutralStart()
 370 |     {
 371 |         var world = DefaultWorld.Create(seed: 1);
 372 |         var committer = new WorldBuilderCommitter(world);
 373 |         var plan = new MyGame.Core.AI.WorldPlan
 374 |         {
 375 |             Title = "T", Theme = "t", Setting = "s", Atmosphere = "a", StartingHook = "h",
 376 |             Factions = new()
 377 |             {
 378 |                 new() { Name = "Орден Зари", Description = "Рыцари.", Alignment = "good", Type = "order" },
 379 |                 new() { Name = "Тёмный Культ", Description = "Злодеи.", Alignment = "evil", Type = "cult" },
 380 |             },
 381 |         };
 382 | 
 383 |         var stats = new CommitStats();
 384 |         committer.CommitFactions(plan, stats);
 385 | 
 386 |         Assert.Equal(2, stats.Factions);
 387 |         Assert.Equal(2, world.Factions.Count);
 388 |         // All factions start neutral regardless of plan alignment.
 389 |         Assert.All(world.Factions, f => Assert.Equal("neutral", f.Alignment));
 390 |         Assert.All(world.Factions, f => Assert.Equal(0, f.Reputation));
 391 |         Assert.NotNull(world.Factions.FirstOrDefault(f => f.Name == "Орден Зари"));
 392 |         Assert.NotNull(world.Factions.FirstOrDefault(f => f.Name == "Тёмный Культ"));
 393 |     }
 394 | 
 395 |     [Fact]
 396 |     public void CommitFactions_Idempotent_NoDuplicatesOnRerun()
 397 |     {
 398 |         var world = DefaultWorld.Create(seed: 1);
 399 |         var committer = new WorldBuilderCommitter(world);
 400 |         var plan = new MyGame.Core.AI.WorldPlan
 401 |         {
 402 |             Title = "T", Theme = "t", Setting = "s", Atmosphere = "a", StartingHook = "h",
 403 |             Factions = new()
 404 |             {
 405 |                 new() { Name = "Гильдия Купцов", Description = "Торговцы.", Alignment = "neutral" },
 406 |             },
 407 |         };
 408 | 
 409 |         var stats1 = new CommitStats();
 410 |         committer.CommitFactions(plan, stats1);
 411 |         Assert.Equal(1, stats1.Factions);
 412 |         Assert.Single(world.Factions);
 413 | 
 414 |         // Re-run — should skip the already-registered faction.
 415 |         var stats2 = new CommitStats();
 416 |         committer.CommitFactions(plan, stats2);
 417 |         Assert.Equal(0, stats2.Factions);
 418 |         Assert.Single(world.Factions);
 419 |     }

 439 |     public void CommitLore_PopulatesEntriesFromPlan()
 440 |     {
 441 |         var world = DefaultWorld.Create(seed: 1);
 442 |         var committer = new WorldBuilderCommitter(world);
 443 |         var plan = new MyGame.Core.AI.WorldPlan
 444 |         {
 445 |             Title = "T", Theme = "t", Setting = "s", Atmosphere = "a", StartingHook = "h",
 446 |             Cosmology = new()
 447 |             {
 448 |                 Origin = "Древние боги",
 449 |                 Nature = "Плоский мир",
 450 |                 HigherPowers = "Боги Зари",
 451 |                 CosmicThreats = "Тёмные Лорды",
 452 |             },
 453 |             History = new()
 454 |             {
 455 |                 new() { Name = "Эпоха Туманов", YearsAgo = "300 лет назад",
 456 |                     Events = new() { "Основание деревни" }, Legacy = "Туманы до сих пор." },
 457 |             },
 458 |             MagicSystem = new()
 459 |             {
 460 |                 Type = "Природная",
 461 |                 Source = "Лунный свет",
 462 |                 Wielders = "Жрицы",
 463 |                 Limits = "Только ночью",
 464 |                 PublicAttitude = "Страх",
 465 |             },
 466 |             Cultures = new()
 467 |             {
 468 |                 new() { Name = "Туманники", Race = "human", Customs = "Молятся луне",
 469 |                     AttitudeToOutsiders = "Подозрительно" },
 470 |             },
 471 |             Economy = new()
 472 |             {
 473 |                 Currencies = "Серебро",
 474 |                 TradeRoutes = "Тракт",
 475 |                 KeyGoods = "Меха и травы",
 476 |                 MajorGuilds = "Гильдия Купцов",
 477 |                 Prosperity = "Бедное",
 478 |             },
 479 |             CurrentEvents = "Сейчас идёт война на севере.",
 480 |         };
 481 | 
 482 |         var stats = new CommitStats();
 483 |         committer.CommitLore(plan, stats);
 484 | 
 485 |         Assert.Equal(6, stats.LoreEntries);
 486 |         Assert.NotNull(world.Lore);
 487 |         Assert.Equal(6, world.Lore!.Entries.Count);
 488 |         Assert.NotNull(world.Lore.Get("deities"));
 489 |         Assert.NotNull(world.Lore.Get("history"));
 490 |         Assert.NotNull(world.Lore.Get("magic"));
 491 |         Assert.NotNull(world.Lore.Get("cultures"));
 492 |         Assert.NotNull(world.Lore.Get("economy"));
 493 |         Assert.NotNull(world.Lore.Get("current events"));
 494 |         Assert.Contains("Лунный свет", world.Lore.Get("magic")!.Content);
 495 |         Assert.Contains("война", world.Lore.Get("current events")!.Content);
 496 |     }

 548 |     [Fact]
 549 |     public void GameTime_ToDisplayWithTimeOfDay_AppendsLabel()
 550 |     {
 551 |         var t = new GameTime(3, 14, 0);
 552 |         Assert.Equal("День 3, 14:00 — день", t.ToDisplayWithTimeOfDay());
 553 | 
 554 |         var night = new GameTime(3, 22, 30);
 555 |         Assert.Equal("День 3, 22:30 — ночь", night.ToDisplayWithTimeOfDay());
 556 |     }

--- src/MyGame.Tests/AI/Tools/ToolRegistryTests.cs (C#, 222 lines, ~2,176 tokens) ---
   1 | using MyGame.Core.AI;
   2 | using MyGame.Core.AI.Tools;

   5 | namespace MyGame.Tests.AI.Tools;

   7 | /// <summary>
   8 | /// Unit tests for the ToolRegistry. Covers builtin-tool registration,
   9 | /// unknown-tool errors, the roll_dice happy path, and lenient JSON arg
  10 | /// parsing (the spec's "don't crash on malformed args" requirement).
  11 | /// </summary>
  12 | public class ToolRegistryTests
  13 | {
  14 |     /// <summary>
  15 |     /// The built-in tools the registry registers. Mirrors the list in
  16 |     /// ToolRegistry.RegisterBuiltins (5 MVP + 13 expansion + 4 combat/
  17 |     /// death from COMBAT-DEATH + 2 runtime content authoring + 5
  18 |     /// engine-depth tools from issues #34/#36/#43). If this list drifts
  19 |     /// from the source, the Constructor_RegistersAllBuiltinTools test
  20 |     /// will fail — update both together.
  21 |     /// </summary>
  22 |     private static readonly string[] ExpectedTools =
  23 |     {
  24 |         "roll_dice",
  25 |         "get_player_state",
  26 |         "get_location",
  27 |         "spawn_npc",
  28 |         "advance_time",
  29 |         "move_player",
  30 |         "give_item",
  31 |         "spawn_item_on_ground",
  32 |         "equip_player",
  33 |         "update_quest",
  34 |         "set_flag",
  35 |         "get_world_state",
  36 |         "get_npc_state",
  37 |         "award_xp",
  38 |         "roll_attack",
  39 |         "deal_damage",
  40 |         "apply_status",
  41 |         "create_item_template",
  42 |         // COMBAT-DEATH (issues #88, #63)
  43 |         "start_combat",
  44 |         "end_combat",

  47 |         // Runtime content authoring (let GM invent new entity types mid-game)

  49 |         "create_building_template",
  50 |         // ENGINE-DEPTH (issues #34 / #36 / #43): weather, faction
  51 |         // reputation, lore query. All optional — no-op on worlds that
  52 |         // haven't activated the corresponding subsystem.
  53 |         "set_weather",
  54 |         "get_weather",
  55 |         "adjust_reputation",
  56 |         "get_factions",
  57 |         "get_lore",
  58 |         // Economy + crafting + containers (issues #37, #65, #67)
  59 |         "craft_item",
  60 |         "search_location",
  61 |         "set_market_price",
  62 |         "get_market_price",
  63 |         // Procedural dungeon generation (issue #38)
  64 |         "generate_dungeon",
  65 |     };
  66 | 
  67 |     private static ToolRegistry MakeRegistry()
  68 |     {
  69 |         var world = DefaultWorld.Create(seed: 1);
  70 |         return new ToolRegistry(world);
  71 |     }
  72 | 
  73 |     [Fact]
  74 |     public void Constructor_RegistersAllBuiltinTools()
  75 |     {
  76 |         var reg = MakeRegistry();
  77 |         var names = reg.Definitions.Select(d => d.Name).ToHashSet();
  78 |         foreach (var name in ExpectedTools)
  79 |             Assert.Contains(name, names);
  80 |         Assert.Equal(ExpectedTools.Length, reg.Definitions.Count);
  81 |     }
  82 | 
  83 |     [Fact]
  84 |     public void GetDefinition_ReturnsKnownTool()
  85 |     {
  86 |         var reg = MakeRegistry();
  87 |         var def = reg.GetDefinition("roll_dice");
  88 |         Assert.NotNull(def);
  89 |         Assert.Equal("roll_dice", def!.Name);
  90 |         Assert.False(string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(def.Description));
  91 |     }
  92 | 
  93 |     [Fact]
  94 |     public void GetDefinition_Nonexistent_ReturnsNull()
  95 |     {
  96 |         var reg = MakeRegistry();
  97 |         Assert.Null(reg.GetDefinition("not_a_tool"));
  98 |     }
  99 | 
 100 |     [Fact]
 101 |     public async Task ExecuteAsync_UnknownTool_ReturnsError()
 102 |     {
 103 |         var reg = MakeRegistry();
 104 |         var result = await reg.ExecuteAsync("call_1", "nonexistent_tool", "{}");
 105 |         Assert.True(result.IsError);
 106 |         Assert.Contains("nonexistent_tool", result.Content);
 107 |         Assert.Equal("call_1", result.ToolCallId);
 108 |     }
 109 | 
 110 |     [Fact]
 111 |     public async Task ExecuteAsync_RollDice_ReturnsResult()
 112 |     {
 113 |         var reg = MakeRegistry();
 114 |         var result = await reg.ExecuteAsync("call_1", "roll_dice", """{"expression":"1d20"}""");
 115 |         Assert.False(result.IsError);
 116 |         // The handler formats as "Брошено {expr}: кости [...] ±N = {total}."
 117 |         Assert.Contains("Брошено", result.Content);
 118 |         Assert.Contains("1d20", result.Content);
 119 |     }
 120 | 
 121 |     [Fact]
 122 |     public async Task ExecuteAsync_RollDice_NoArgs_DefaultsTo1d20()
 123 |     {
 124 |         // When `expression` is absent, the handler defaults to "1d20".
 125 |         var reg = MakeRegistry();
 126 |         var result = await reg.ExecuteAsync("call_1", "roll_dice", "{}");
 127 |         Assert.False(result.IsError);
 128 |         Assert.Contains("1d20", result.Content);
 129 |     }
 130 | 
 131 |     [Fact]
 132 |     public async Task ExecuteAsync_MalformedArgs_DoesNotCrash()
 133 |     {
 134 |         // The spec's "don't crash on malformed args" requirement: bad JSON
 135 |         // is treated as an empty object, then the handler runs with
 136 |         // defaults. roll_dice falls back to "1d20" when expression is
 137 |         // missing — so the result must NOT be an error, and the content
 138 |         // should mention 1d20.
 139 |         var reg = MakeRegistry();
 140 |         var result = await reg.ExecuteAsync("call_1", "roll_dice", "{bad json");
 141 |         Assert.False(result.IsError);
 142 |         Assert.Contains("1d20", result.Content);
 143 |     }
 144 |

 147 |     {

 150 |         Assert.False(result.IsError);
 151 |         Assert.Contains("1d20", result.Content);
 152 |     }
 153 |

 156 |     {
 157 |         var reg = MakeRegistry();

 159 |         Assert.False(result.IsError);
 160 |     }

 164 |     {

 167 |         Assert.False(result.IsError);

 169 |         Assert.Contains("Странник", result.Content);
 170 |     }

 176 |         // resolves to null — the handler should either return a
 177 |         // graceful error or some non-error result; the key requirement
 178 |         // is it doesn't throw out of ExecuteAsync. Any well-formed
 179 |         // ToolResult is acceptable here.
 180 |         var reg = MakeRegistry();
 181 |         var result = await reg.ExecuteAsync("call_1", "get_location", """{"locationId":"does_not_exist"}""");
 182 |         // Either IsError=true with a useful message, or IsError=false
 183 |         // with a "not found" string. Both are acceptable; just don't
 184 |         // throw.
 185 |         Assert.NotNull(result);
 186 |         Assert.False(string.IsNullOrEmpty(result.Content));
 187 |     }

 194 |         var reg = MakeRegistry();
 195 |         var result = await reg.ExecuteAsync("my_call_id_42", "roll_dice", "{}");

 197 |     }

 204 |

 206 |         {
 207 |             Name = "test_custom",
 208 |             Description = "A test tool.",
 209 |             ParametersJson = "{}",
 210 |         };
 211 |         reg.Register(def, (args, ct) =>
 212 |             Task.FromResult(ToolResult.Ok("c1", "custom ok")));
 213 | 
 214 |         Assert.Equal(before + 1, reg.Definitions.Count);
 215 |         Assert.NotNull(reg.GetDefinition("test_custom"));
 216 |     }

 220 |         Assert.Throws<ArgumentNullException>(() => new ToolRegistry(null!));
