--- src/MyGame.Tests/AI/Tools/UpdateQuestRewardsTests.cs (C#, 185 lines, ~1,948 tokens) ---
   1 | using System.Text.Json;
   2 | using MyGame.Core.AI.Tools;
   3 | using MyGame.Core.Common;
   4 | using MyGame.Core.World;
   5 | using MyGame.Core.World.Entities;
   6 | 
   7 | namespace MyGame.Tests.AI.Tools;
   8 | 
   9 | /// <summary>
  10 | /// Unit tests for the <c>update_quest</c> tool's issue-#70 behavior:
  11 | /// completing a quest STAGES the reward in
  12 | /// <see cref="Quest.UnclaimedRewards"/> rather than granting it inline.
  13 | /// The player must claim via the Quest panel UI (the GameViewModel
  14 | /// handles the actual grant).
  15 | /// </summary>
  16 | public class UpdateQuestRewardsTests
  17 | {
  18 |     /// <summary>
  19 |     /// A fresh <see cref="Quest"/> has <see cref="Quest.UnclaimedRewards"/>
  20 |     /// = null. Old saves without the field also load with null (System.Text.Json
  21 |     /// defaults missing properties to null for nullable reference types).
  22 |     /// </summary>
  23 |     [Fact]
  24 |     public void Quest_UnclaimedRewards_DefaultsNull()
  25 |     {
  26 |         var q = new Quest();
  27 |         Assert.Null(q.UnclaimedRewards);
  28 |     }
  29 | 
  30 |     /// <summary>
  31 |     /// Calling <c>update_quest</c> with action=complete STAGES the
  32 |     /// reward in UnclaimedRewards (does NOT grant currency / XP / items
  33 |     /// to the player). The tool result text tells the player to go
  34 |     /// claim via the Quest panel.
  35 |     /// </summary>
  36 |     [Fact]
  37 |     public async Task UpdateQuest_Complete_StagesReward_NotGranted()
  38 |     {
  39 |         var world = DefaultWorld.Create(seed: 1);
  40 |         var player = world.ActivePlayer ?? world.Players.First();
  41 |         var currencyBefore = player.Inventory.Currency;
  42 |         var xpBefore = player.Experience ?? 0;
  43 |         var itemsBefore = player.Inventory.Items.Count;
  44 | 
  45 |         // Spawn a quest with a reward, set it Active so completion is
  46 |         // a meaningful transition.
  47 |         var quest = new Quest
  48 |         {
  49 |             Id = EntityId.NewId(),
  50 |             Name = "Тестовый квест",
  51 |             Status = QuestStatus.Active,
  52 |             Reward = new QuestReward
  53 |             {
  54 |                 Currency = 100,
  55 |                 Experience = 50,
  56 |                 Items = new() { "item_potion_health" },
  57 |             },
  58 |         };
  59 |         world.Quests.Add(quest);
  60 | 
  61 |         var reg = new ToolRegistry(world);
  62 |         var args = $$"""{"questId":"{{quest.Id.Value}}","action":"complete"}""";
  63 |         var result = await reg.ExecuteAsync("call_1", "update_quest", args);
  64 | 
  65 |         // The tool result should be the "staged" message — NOT the old
  66 |         // "received N gold + N XP + item" message.
  67 |         Assert.False(result.IsError);
  68 |         Assert.Contains("выполнен", result.Content);
  69 |         Assert.Contains("Награда ожидает получения", result.Content);
  70 | 
  71 |         // Quest status changed to Completed.
  72 |         Assert.Equal(QuestStatus.Completed, quest.Status);
  73 | 
  74 |         // Reward staged in UnclaimedRewards (not granted to player).
  75 |         Assert.NotNull(quest.UnclaimedRewards);
  76 |         Assert.Equal(100, quest.UnclaimedRewards!.Currency);
  77 |         Assert.Equal(50, quest.UnclaimedRewards.Experience);
  78 | 
  79 |         // Player's currency / XP / items UNCHANGED — the reward is
  80 |         // staged, not granted.
  81 |         Assert.Equal(currencyBefore, player.Inventory.Currency);
  82 |         Assert.Equal(xpBefore, player.Experience ?? 0);
  83 |         Assert.Equal(itemsBefore, player.Inventory.Items.Count);
  84 |     }
  85 | 
  86 |     /// <summary>
  87 |     /// Completing a quest with NO reward still stages null in
  88 |     /// UnclaimedRewards (which is the same as the default — there's
  89 |     /// nothing to claim, so the Quest panel won't show the
  90 |     /// «Получить награду» button). This is the correct behavior: a
  91 |     /// rewardless quest just transitions to Completed, no claim step.
  92 |     /// </summary>
  93 |     [Fact]
  94 |     public async Task UpdateQuest_Complete_NoReward_StagesNull()
  95 |     {
  96 |         var world = DefaultWorld.Create(seed: 1);
  97 |         var quest = new Quest
  98 |         {
  99 |             Id = EntityId.NewId(),
 100 |             Name = "Без награды",
 101 |             Status = QuestStatus.Active,
 102 |             Reward = null,
 103 |         };
 104 |         world.Quests.Add(quest);
 105 | 
 106 |         var reg = new ToolRegistry(world);
 107 |         var args = $$"""{"questId":"{{quest.Id.Value}}","action":"complete"}""";
 108 |         var result = await reg.ExecuteAsync("call_1", "update_quest", args);
 109 | 
 110 |         Assert.False(result.IsError);
 111 |         Assert.Equal(QuestStatus.Completed, quest.Status);
 112 |         // Reward was null → UnclaimedRewards is also null (nothing to claim).
 113 |         Assert.Null(quest.UnclaimedRewards);
 114 |     }
 115 | 
 116 |     /// <summary>
 117 |     /// The <see cref="Quest.UnclaimedRewards"/> field round-trips
 118 |     /// through JSON serialization (so saves persist the unclaimed state
 119 |     /// across reloads). When the field is null, it serializes to null
 120 |     /// (or omitted); when set, it round-trips the reward values.
 121 |     /// </summary>
 122 |     [Fact]
 123 |     public void Quest_UnclaimedRewards_JsonRoundTrip()
 124 |     {
 125 |         var q = new Quest
 126 |         {
 127 |             Id = EntityId.NewId(),
 128 |             Name = "Тест",
 129 |             UnclaimedRewards = new QuestReward
 130 |             {
 131 |                 Currency = 250,
 132 |                 Experience = 75,
 133 |                 Items = new() { "item_potion_health", "item_dagger" },
 134 |             },
 135 |         };
 136 | 
 137 |         var opts = new JsonSerializerOptions
 138 |         {
 139 |             PropertyNamingPolicy = JsonNamingPolicy.CamelCase,
 140 |             PropertyNameCaseInsensitive = true,
 141 |         };
 142 |         var json = JsonSerializer.Serialize(q, opts);
 143 |         var restored = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<Quest>(json, opts);
 144 | 
 145 |         Assert.NotNull(restored);
 146 |         Assert.NotNull(restored!.UnclaimedRewards);
 147 |         Assert.Equal(250, restored.UnclaimedRewards!.Currency);
 148 |         Assert.Equal(75, restored.UnclaimedRewards.Experience);
 149 |         Assert.Equal(2, restored.UnclaimedRewards.Items!.Count);
 150 |     }
 151 | 
 152 |     /// <summary>
 153 |     /// An old save (serialized without the UnclaimedRewards field)
 154 |     /// deserializes with UnclaimedRewards = null — backward-compatible
 155 |     /// (no migration needed).
 156 |     /// </summary>
 157 |     [Fact]
 158 |     public void Quest_OldSaveWithoutUnclaimedRewards_LoadsAsNull()
 159 |     {
 160 |         // A quest JSON without the unclaimedRewards field (mimics an
 161 |         // old save written before issue #70).
 162 |         var json = """
 163 |             {
 164 |               "id": "quest_old_1",
 165 |               "name": "Старый квест",
 166 |               "kind": "quest",
 167 |               "status": 2,
 168 |               "reward": {"currency": 50, "experience": 10}
 169 |             }
 170 |             """;
 171 |         var opts = new JsonSerializerOptions
 172 |         {
 173 |             PropertyNamingPolicy = JsonNamingPolicy.CamelCase,
 174 |             PropertyNameCaseInsensitive = true,
 175 |         };
 176 |         var restored = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<Quest>(json, opts);
 177 | 
 178 |         Assert.NotNull(restored);
 179 |         Assert.Null(restored!.UnclaimedRewards);
 180 |         // The reward itself DID load (it's the pre-#70 field).
 181 |         Assert.NotNull(restored.Reward);
 182 |         Assert.Equal(50, restored.Reward!.Currency);
 183 |     }
 184 | }

--- src/MyGame.Tests/Common/EntityIdTests.cs (C#, 186 lines, ~1,708 tokens) ---
   1 | using System.Text.Json;
   2 | using MyGame.Core.Common;
   3 | 
   4 | namespace MyGame.Tests.Common;
   5 | 
   6 | /// <summary>
   7 | /// Unit tests for the cuid-style EntityId. Covers NewId uniqueness,
   8 | /// parse round-trip, ordinal comparison, and JSON serialization.
   9 | /// </summary>
  10 | public class EntityIdTests
  11 | {
  12 |     [Fact]
  13 |     public void NewId_ReturnsNonEmptyString()
  14 |     {
  15 |         var id = EntityId.NewId();
  16 |         Assert.False(string.IsNullOrEmpty(id.ToString()));
  17 |         Assert.True(id.ToString().Length > 0);
  18 |     }
  19 | 
  20 |     [Fact]
  21 |     public void NewId_HasExpectedPrefix()
  22 |     {
  23 |         // The cuid-style format is 'c' + 9-char base36 timestamp +
  24 |         // 12-char base36 random suffix -> 22 chars total.
  25 |         var id = EntityId.NewId().ToString();
  26 |         Assert.StartsWith("c", id);
  27 |         Assert.Equal(22, id.Length);
  28 |     }
  29 | 
  30 |     [Fact]
  31 |     public void NewId_IsUnique()
  32 |     {
  33 |         // 1000 minted ids should all be distinct. The cuid scheme mixes a
  34 |         // millisecond timestamp with 12 base36 random digits — collisions
  35 |         // are astronomically unlikely even in tight loops (where the
  36 |         // timestamp can repeat across iterations).
  37 |         var ids = new HashSet<string>();
  38 |         for (int i = 0; i < 1000; i++)
  39 |         {
  40 |             var s = EntityId.NewId().ToString();
  41 |             Assert.True(ids.Add(s), $"Duplicate id generated at iteration {i}: {s}");
  42 |         }
  43 |     }
  44 | 
  45 |     [Fact]
  46 |     public void TryParse_RoundTrips()
  47 |     {
  48 |         var original = EntityId.NewId();
  49 |         var s = original.ToString();
  50 | 
  51 |         Assert.True(EntityId.TryParse(s, out var parsed));
  52 |         Assert.Equal(original, parsed);
  53 |         Assert.Equal(s, parsed.ToString());
  54 |     }
  55 | 
  56 |     [Fact]
  57 |     public void Parse_RoundTrips()
  58 |     {
  59 |         var original = EntityId.NewId();
  60 |         var s = original.ToString();
  61 |         var parsed = EntityId.Parse(s);
  62 |         Assert.Equal(original, parsed);
  63 |     }
  64 | 
  65 |     [Fact]
  66 |     public void TryParse_RejectsEmptyAndWhitespace()
  67 |     {
  68 |         // TryParse never throws — it returns false for null/empty inputs.
  69 |         // The implementation only guards against null/empty (it does NOT
  70 |         // trim whitespace), so "   " is accepted as a non-empty string.
  71 |         // (Per the impl: any non-empty string is accepted, since EntityId
  72 |         // is just a wrapper around an arbitrary string.)
  73 |         Assert.False(EntityId.TryParse(null, out _));
  74 |         Assert.False(EntityId.TryParse("", out _));
  75 |         Assert.True(EntityId.TryParse("   ", out _));
  76 |     }
  77 | 
  78 |     [Fact]
  79 |     public void TryParse_AcceptsArbitraryNonEmptyString()
  80 |     {
  81 |         // Per the implementation, any non-empty string is accepted — the
  82 |         // EntityId is just a wrapper. So "not-an-id" parses successfully.
  83 |         Assert.True(EntityId.TryParse("not-an-id", out var parsed));
  84 |         Assert.Equal("not-an-id", parsed.ToString());
  85 |     }
  86 | 
  87 |     [Fact]
  88 |     public void Parse_EmptyOrNull_Throws()
  89 |     {
  90 |         Assert.Throws<ArgumentException>(() => EntityId.Parse(null!));
  91 |         Assert.Throws<ArgumentException>(() => EntityId.Parse(""));
  92 |     }
  93 | 
  94 |     [Fact]
  95 |     public void CompareTo_IsConsistentWithToString()
  96 |     {
  97 |         // Mint two ids and compare them via CompareTo — the ordering must
  98 |         // match a plain ordinal string comparison of their .ToString()
  99 |         // values (that's how CompareTo is implemented).
 100 |         var a = EntityId.NewId();
 101 |         var b = EntityId.NewId();
 102 |         // Ensure deterministic ordering: if the random suffix makes a<b
 103 |         // lexically, swap so we have a known "smaller / larger" pair.
 104 |         if (a.CompareTo(b) > 0) (a, b) = (b, a);
 105 | 
 106 |         Assert.Equal(
 107 |             Math.Sign(string.CompareOrdinal(a.ToString(), b.ToString())),
 108 |             Math.Sign(a.CompareTo(b)));
 109 |         Assert.True(a.CompareTo(b) <= 0);
 110 |     }
 111 | 
 112 |     [Fact]
 113 |     public void CompareTo_SortOrderMatchesStringOrder()
 114 |     {
 115 |         var ids = new List<EntityId>();
 116 |         for (int i = 0; i < 50; i++) ids.Add(EntityId.NewId());
 117 | 
 118 |         var byEntityId = ids.OrderBy(x => x, Comparer<EntityId>.Default).ToList();
 119 |         var byString = ids.OrderBy(x => x.ToString(), StringComparer.Ordinal).ToList();
 120 | 
 121 |         Assert.Equal(byString, byEntityId);
 122 |     }
 123 | 
 124 |     [Fact]
 125 |     public void ImplicitStringConversion_Works()
 126 |     {
 127 |         EntityId id = EntityId.NewId();
 128 |         // EntityId -> string
 129 |         string s = id;
 130 |         Assert.Equal(id.ToString(), s);
 131 | 
 132 |         // string -> EntityId
 133 |         EntityId fromString = "test_id";
 134 |         Assert.Equal("test_id", fromString.ToString());
 135 |     }
 136 | 
 137 |     [Fact]
 138 |     public void Equality_IsValueBased()
 139 |     {
 140 |         var a = new EntityId("abc");
 141 |         var b = new EntityId("abc");
 142 |         var c = new EntityId("def");
 143 | 
 144 |         Assert.Equal(a, b);
 145 |         Assert.True(a == b);
 146 |         Assert.True(a != c);
 147 |         Assert.False(a == c);
 148 |     }
 149 | 
 150 |     [Fact]
 151 |     public void Json_SerializesAsBareString()
 152 |     {
 153 |         // The custom JsonConverter should serialize an EntityId as a bare
 154 |         // JSON string, NOT as an object with a "value" property.
 155 |         var id = new EntityId("c1234567890abcdefghijkl");
 156 |         var json = JsonSerializer.Serialize(id);
 157 |         Assert.Equal("\"c1234567890abcdefghijkl\"", json);
 158 |     }
 159 | 
 160 |     [Fact]
 161 |     public void Json_RoundTripsAsBareString()
 162 |     {
 163 |         var id = EntityId.NewId();
 164 |         var json = JsonSerializer.Serialize(id);
 165 |         var back = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<EntityId>(json);
 166 |         Assert.Equal(id, back);
 167 |     }
 168 | 
 169 |     [Fact]
 170 |     public void Json_DeserializesFromBareString()
 171 |     {
 172 |         const string json = "\"my-custom-id\"";
 173 |         var parsed = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<EntityId>(json);
 174 |         Assert.Equal("my-custom-id", parsed.ToString());
 175 |     }
 176 | 
 177 |     [Fact]
 178 |     public void Empty_IsKnownSentinel()
 179 |     {
 180 |         Assert.Equal(string.Empty, EntityId.Empty.ToString());
 181 |         // Empty.Value is a string-typed sentinel; parsing its underlying
 182 |         // string should yield false (empty string is rejected).
 183 |         Assert.False(EntityId.TryParse((string?)EntityId.Empty.Value, out _));
 184 |     }
 185 | }

--- src/MyGame.Tests/Common/RngTests.cs (C#, 221 lines, ~1,862 tokens) ---
   1 | using MyGame.Core.Common;
   2 | 
   3 | namespace MyGame.Tests.Common;
   4 | 
   5 | /// <summary>
   6 | /// Unit tests for the PCG32 deterministic PRNG. Covers the value ranges,
   7 | /// determinism / state round-trip, fork independence, and the
   8 | /// shuffle / pick helpers.
   9 | /// </summary>
  10 | public class RngTests
  11 | {
  12 |     private const int SampleCount = 1000;
  13 | 
  14 |     [Fact]
  15 |     public void Next_ReturnsValueInUnitInterval()
  16 |     {
  17 |         var rng = new Rng(42);
  18 |         for (int i = 0; i < SampleCount; i++)
  19 |         {
  20 |             var v = rng.Next();
  21 |             Assert.InRange(v, 0.0, 1.0);
  22 |             Assert.True(v < 1.0, $"Next() returned >= 1.0: {v}");
  23 |         }
  24 |     }
  25 | 
  26 |     [Fact]
  27 |     public void NextInt_MaxIsExclusive()
  28 |     {
  29 |         var rng = new Rng(7);
  30 |         for (int i = 0; i < SampleCount; i++)
  31 |         {
  32 |             var v = rng.NextInt(10);
  33 |             Assert.InRange(v, 0, 9);
  34 |         }
  35 |     }
  36 | 
  37 |     [Fact]
  38 |     public void NextInt_MinMax_RespectsBounds()
  39 |     {
  40 |         var rng = new Rng(123);
  41 |         for (int i = 0; i < SampleCount; i++)
  42 |         {
  43 |             var v = rng.NextInt(5, 10);
  44 |             // min inclusive, max exclusive (matches System.Random semantics)
  45 |             Assert.InRange(v, 5, 9);
  46 |         }
  47 |     }
  48 | 
  49 |     [Fact]
  50 |     public void NextInt_NonPositiveMax_Throws()
  51 |     {
  52 |         var rng = new Rng(1);
  53 |         Assert.Throws<ArgumentOutOfRangeException>(() => rng.NextInt(0));
  54 |         Assert.Throws<ArgumentOutOfRangeException>(() => rng.NextInt(-5));
  55 |     }
  56 | 
  57 |     [Fact]
  58 |     public void NextInt_MinGreaterThanOrEqualMax_Throws()
  59 |     {
  60 |         var rng = new Rng(1);
  61 |         Assert.Throws<ArgumentOutOfRangeException>(() => rng.NextInt(5, 5));
  62 |         Assert.Throws<ArgumentOutOfRangeException>(() => rng.NextInt(6, 5));
  63 |     }
  64 | 
  65 |     [Fact]
  66 |     public void SameSeed_ProducesIdenticalSequence()
  67 |     {
  68 |         var a = new Rng(987654321L);
  69 |         var b = new Rng(987654321L);
  70 |         for (int i = 0; i < 100; i++)
  71 |             Assert.Equal(a.Next(), b.Next());
  72 |     }
  73 | 
  74 |     [Fact]
  75 |     public void State_RoundTrips()
  76 |     {
  77 |         // Capture state mid-stream and reconstruct; the new Rng must
  78 |         // continue exactly where the original left off.
  79 |         var original = new Rng(42);
  80 |         for (int i = 0; i < 10; i++)
  81 |             original.Next();
  82 | 
  83 |         var snapshot = original.State;
  84 |         var restored = Rng.FromState(snapshot);
  85 | 
  86 |         for (int i = 0; i < 10; i++)
  87 |             Assert.Equal(original.Next(), restored.Next());
  88 |     }
  89 | 
  90 |     [Fact]
  91 |     public void State_Setter_RestoresStream()
  92 |     {
  93 |         var original = new Rng(2024);
  94 |         for (int i = 0; i < 5; i++) original.Next();
  95 |         var snap = original.State;
  96 | 
  97 |         var other = new Rng(0);
  98 |         other.State = snap;
  99 | 
 100 |         for (int i = 0; i < 10; i++)
 101 |             Assert.Equal(original.Next(), other.Next());
 102 |     }
 103 | 
 104 |     [Fact]
 105 |     public void Fork_ProducesIndependentStream()
 106 |     {
 107 |         var parent = new Rng(0xDEADBEEF);
 108 |         var child = parent.Fork();
 109 | 
 110 |         // The fork consumes state from the parent — so the parent's next
 111 |         // output is NOT what it would have been without forking. The child
 112 |         // also produces its own values; assert neither matches a fresh
 113 |         // parent at the same seed.
 114 |         var freshParent = new Rng(0xDEADBEEF);
 115 | 
 116 |         // freshParent didn't fork, so its outputs differ from parent's
 117 |         // (whose state advanced by 2 uint pulls during Fork).
 118 |         var parentNext = parent.Next();
 119 |         var freshNext = freshParent.Next();
 120 |         Assert.NotEqual(parentNext, freshNext);
 121 | 
 122 |         // And the child produces values distinct from both.
 123 |         var childNext = child.Next();
 124 |         Assert.NotEqual(parentNext, childNext);
 125 |         Assert.NotEqual(freshNext, childNext);
 126 |     }
 127 | 
 128 |     [Fact]
 129 |     public void Fork_IsDeterministic()
 130 |     {
 131 |         // Two parents with the same seed must produce identical forks —
 132 |         // the forked stream is a function of the parent's state.
 133 |         var p1 = new Rng(555);
 134 |         var p2 = new Rng(555);
 135 |         var c1 = p1.Fork();
 136 |         var c2 = p2.Fork();
 137 |         for (int i = 0; i < 50; i++)
 138 |             Assert.Equal(c1.Next(), c2.Next());
 139 |     }
 140 | 
 141 |     [Fact]
 142 |     public void Shuffle_PreservesElements()
 143 |     {
 144 |         var rng = new Rng(3);
 145 |         var original = Enumerable.Range(1, 50).ToList();
 146 |         var copy = original.ToList();
 147 |         rng.Shuffle(copy);
 148 | 
 149 |         // Same elements (multiset equality), possibly different order.
 150 |         Assert.Equal(
 151 |             original.OrderBy(x => x),
 152 |             copy.OrderBy(x => x));
 153 | 
 154 |         // Sanity: with a 50-element list and a non-degenerate PRNG, the
 155 |         // shuffle is overwhelmingly likely to move at least one element.
 156 |         // If it didn't, that's a strong signal the shuffle is a no-op.
 157 |         var moved = original.Where((t, i) => copy[i] != t).Count();
 158 |         Assert.True(moved > 0, "Shuffle did not change the list ordering.");
 159 |     }
 160 | 
 161 |     [Fact]
 162 |     public void Shuffle_EmptyAndSingle_DoesNotThrow()
 163 |     {
 164 |         var rng = new Rng(1);
 165 |         var empty = new List<int>();
 166 |         rng.Shuffle(empty);
 167 |         Assert.Empty(empty);
 168 | 
 169 |         var single = new List<string> { "x" };
 170 |         rng.Shuffle(single);
 171 |         Assert.Equal("x", single[0]);
 172 |     }
 173 | 
 174 |     [Fact]
 175 |     public void Shuffle_NullList_Throws()
 176 |     {
 177 |         var rng = new Rng(1);
 178 |         Assert.Throws<ArgumentNullException>(() => rng.Shuffle<int>(null!));
 179 |     }
 180 | 
 181 |     [Fact]
 182 |     public void Pick_ReturnsElementFromList()
 183 |     {
 184 |         var rng = new Rng(99);
 185 |         var list = new List<int> { 10, 20, 30, 40, 50 };
 186 |         for (int i = 0; i < 100; i++)
 187 |         {
 188 |             var picked = rng.Pick(list);
 189 |             Assert.Contains(picked, list);
 190 |         }
 191 |     }
 192 | 
 193 |     [Fact]
 194 |     public void Pick_EmptyList_Throws()
 195 |     {
 196 |         var rng = new Rng(1);
 197 |         Assert.Throws<ArgumentException>(() => rng.Pick(new List<int>()));
 198 |     }
 199 | 
 200 |     [Fact]
 201 |     public void Pick_NullList_Throws()
 202 |     {
 203 |         var rng = new Rng(1);
 204 |         Assert.Throws<ArgumentNullException>(() => rng.Pick<int>(null!));
 205 |     }
 206 | 
 207 |     [Fact]
 208 |     public void Pick_DistributesReasonably()
 209 |     {
 210 |         // Statistical sanity: over many picks from a 2-element list, both
 211 |         // elements should be picked. (Not asserting exact distribution —
 212 |         // that's flaky — just that both show up at all.)
 213 |         var rng = new Rng(4242);
 214 |         var list = new List<int> { 0, 1 };
 215 |         var seen = new bool[2];
 216 |         for (int i = 0; i < 200; i++)
 217 |             seen[rng.Pick(list)] = true;
 218 |         Assert.True(seen[0] && seen[1], "Pick never returned one of the elements.");
 219 |     }
 220 | }

--- src/MyGame.Tests/Multiplayer/HostClientIntegrationTests.cs (C#, 572 lines, ~7,018 tokens) ---
   1 | using System.Diagnostics;
   2 | using System.Net.WebSockets;
   3 | using MyGame.Core.AI;
   4 | using MyGame.Core.AI.Agents;
   5 | using MyGame.Core.AI.Prompts;
   6 | using MyGame.Core.AI.Tools;
   7 | using MyGame.Core.Multiplayer;
   8 | using MyGame.Core.Multiplayer.Protocol;
   9 | using MyGame.Core.World;
  10 | using MyGame.Tests.AI;
  11 | 
  12 | // 'Profile' is both a namespace (MyGame.Core.Profile) and a type (the
  13 | // record inside it). Same alias trick as HostSession.cs — alias the
  14 | // type to a different name to sidestep the namespace/type collision.
  15 | using GameProfile = MyGame.Core.Profile.Profile;
  16 | 
  17 | namespace MyGame.Tests.Multiplayer;
  18 | 
  19 | /// <summary>
  20 | /// Integration tests for the multiplayer host + client protocol (issue #57).
  21 | /// Each test spins up a real <see cref="HostServer"/> (or
  22 | /// <see cref="HostSession"/> for action-resolution tests) on an
  23 | /// OS-assigned port and connects one or more <see cref="GameClient"/>
  24 | /// instances over loopback <c>ws://localhost:&lt;port&gt;/</c>.
  25 | ///
  26 | /// <para>
  27 | /// <b>Only the AI layer is stubbed:</b> a <see cref="StubAiClient"/>
  28 | /// returns canned <see cref="ChatResponse"/> objects so the GM turn
  29 | /// produces deterministic narration without contacting a real LLM
  30 | /// provider. Everything else — the WebSocket transport, the JSON wire
  31 | /// protocol, the <see cref="HostServer"/>'s accept loop, the
  32 | /// <see cref="GameClient"/>'s read loop, the
  33 | /// <see cref="HostSession"/> turn orchestration, the
  34 | /// <see cref="ActionQueue"/> draining — is exercised end-to-end.
  35 | /// </para>
  36 | ///
  37 | /// <para>
  38 | /// <b>Determinism:</b> every wait uses a
  39 | /// <see cref="TaskCompletionSource{TResult}"/> wired up to the relevant
  40 | /// event handler, awaited with a 10-second timeout. No
  41 | /// <c>Thread.Sleep</c> / <c>Task.Delay</c> polling. If the expected
  42 | /// event doesn't fire, the test fails fast rather than hanging the
  43 | /// suite.
  44 | /// </para>
  45 | ///
  46 | /// <para>
  47 | /// <b>Cleanup:</b> each test's host + clients are torn down in a
  48 | /// <c>finally</c> block (or via <see cref="IAsyncDisposable"/>) so
  49 | /// background read loops don't leak across tests. The
  50 | /// <see cref="HostServer"/>'s accept loop is stopped via its shutdown
  51 | /// token; client <see cref="ClientWebSocket"/>s are closed + disposed.
  52 | /// </para>
  53 | /// </summary>
  54 | public sealed class HostClientIntegrationTests : IAsyncDisposable
  55 | {
  56 |     // 10-second timeout for every event wait. Generous enough for a
  57 |     // loopback WebSocket round-trip (typically &lt;5ms) + the
  58 |     // HostServer's ThreadPool-scheduled read loop start-up. Tight
  59 |     // enough to fail a hanging test before the suite's overall
  60 |     // timeout.
  61 |     private static readonly TimeSpan WaitTimeout = TimeSpan.FromSeconds(10);
  62 | 
  63 |     private readonly List<Func<Task>> _cleanupSteps = new();
  64 |     private readonly List<CancellationTokenSource> _ctsToDispose = new();
  65 | 
  66 |     /// <summary>
  67 |     /// Per-test cleanup. Runs all registered cleanup steps in reverse
  68 |     /// order (LIFO — clients before host) so disconnects happen before
  69 |     /// the host stops accepting. Safe to call multiple times.
  70 |     /// </summary>
  71 |     public async ValueTask DisposeAsync()
  72 |     {
  73 |         // Stop clients first so the HostServer's MemberLeft broadcast
  74 |         // doesn't race with the listener shutdown.
  75 |         var exceptions = new List<Exception>();
  76 |         for (int i = _cleanupSteps.Count - 1; i >= 0; i--)
  77 |         {
  78 |             try { await _cleanupSteps[i]().ConfigureAwait(false); }
  79 |             catch (Exception ex) { exceptions.Add(ex); }
  80 |         }
  81 |         _cleanupSteps.Clear();
  82 |         foreach (var cts in _ctsToDispose)
  83 |         {
  84 |             try { cts.Cancel(); cts.Dispose(); } catch { /* ignore */ }
  85 |         }
  86 |         _ctsToDispose.Clear();
  87 | 
  88 |         if (exceptions.Count > 0)
  89 |             throw new AggregateException("Cleanup failed", exceptions);
  90 |     }
  91 | 
  92 |     // ─── Helpers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  93 | 
  94 |     /// <summary>
  95 |     /// Build a fresh <see cref="Profile"/> with the given nickname.
  96 |     /// Each call produces a new ProfileId so two clients with the same
  97 |     /// nickname would still be distinct profiles (the HostServer
  98 |     /// validates nickname uniqueness, not profile id uniqueness).
  99 |     /// </summary>
 100 |     private static GameProfile MakeProfile(string nickname) =>
 101 |         new() { Nickname = nickname };
 102 | 
 103 |     /// <summary>
 104 |     /// Start a fresh <see cref="HostServer"/> on an OS-assigned port
 105 |     /// bound to <c>localhost</c> (single-machine testing — no admin
 106 |     /// privileges needed, no LAN exposure). The shutdown CTS is
 107 |     /// registered for disposal; the server's StopAsync is registered
 108 |     /// for cleanup. Returns the started server + the actual port.
 109 |     /// </summary>
 110 |     private async Task<(HostServer server, int port, CancellationTokenSource cts)> StartHostAsync(
 111 |         GameProfile hostProfile)
 112 |     {
 113 |         var cts = new CancellationTokenSource();
 114 |         _ctsToDispose.Add(cts);
 115 |         var server = new HostServer(
 116 |             hostProfile,
 117 |             requestedPort: 0,
 118 |             shutdownToken: cts.Token,
 119 |             bindHost: "localhost");
 120 |         var port = await server.StartAsync();
 121 |         _cleanupSteps.Add(async () => await server.StopAsync());
 122 |         return (server, port, cts);
 123 |     }
 124 | 
 125 |     /// <summary>
 126 |     /// Connect a <see cref="GameClient"/> to <c>ws://localhost:&lt;port&gt;/</c>
 127 |     /// and await the Welcomed event. The client's DisconnectAsync is
 128 |     /// registered for cleanup. Returns the client + the WelcomeMsg.
 129 |     /// </summary>
 130 |     private async Task<(GameClient client, WelcomeMsg welcome)> ConnectClientAsync(
 131 |         GameProfile profile, int port)
 132 |     {
 133 |         var client = new GameClient(profile);
 134 |         _cleanupSteps.Add(async () =>
 135 |         {
 136 |             try { await client.DisconnectAsync(); }
 137 |             catch { /* ignore — already disconnected */ }
 138 |         });
 139 | 
 140 |         // Wire up a TaskCompletionSource for the Welcomed event BEFORE
 141 |         // calling ConnectAsync so we don't miss the event (ConnectAsync
 142 |         // raises Welcomed inside its handshake wait, but the read loop
 143 |         // starts AFTER ConnectAsync returns — so the race is only
 144 |         // between ConnectAsync returning and our handler wiring).
 145 |         // Belt-and-suspenders: subscribe before ConnectAsync.
 146 |         var welcomedTcs = new TaskCompletionSource<WelcomeMsg>(
 147 |             TaskCreationOptions.RunContinuationsAsynchronously);
 148 |         client.Welcomed += w => welcomedTcs.TrySetResult(w);
 149 | 
 150 |         using var connectCts = new CancellationTokenSource(WaitTimeout);
 151 |         try
 152 |         {
 153 |             await client.ConnectAsync("localhost", port, connectCts.Token);
 154 |         }
 155 |         catch (OperationCanceledException)
 156 |         {
 157 |             throw new TimeoutException(
 158 |                 $"GameClient.ConnectAsync timed out after {WaitTimeout.TotalSeconds}s.");
 159 |         }
 160 | 
 161 |         // ConnectAsync returns AFTER the Welcomed event is raised
 162 |         // (it awaits the WelcomeMsg internally). But the event handler
 163 |         // is invoked synchronously inside ConnectAsync, so by the time
 164 |         // we get here welcomedTcs is already completed. Still, use a
 165 |         // timeout to be defensive against future changes to ConnectAsync.
 166 |         var welcome = await welcomedTcs.Task.WaitAsync(WaitTimeout);
 167 |         return (client, welcome);
 168 |     }
 169 | 
 170 |     /// <summary>
 171 |     /// Build a <see cref="HostSession"/> wired to a <see cref="GameMaster"/>
 172 |     /// that uses a <see cref="StubAiClient"/> returning the given canned
 173 |     /// narration. The session is started (server bound to an
 174 |     /// OS-assigned port on <c>localhost</c>) and ready to accept
 175 |     /// clients. The shutdown CTS + StopAsync are registered for cleanup.
 176 |     /// </summary>
 177 |     private async Task<(HostSession session, int port, StubAiClient ai, CancellationTokenSource cts)>
 178 |         StartHostSessionAsync(GameProfile hostProfile, string cannedNarration)
 179 |     {
 180 |         var world = DefaultWorld.Create(seed: 1);
 181 |         var ai = new StubAiClient(new ChatResponse
 182 |         {
 183 |             Content = cannedNarration,
 184 |             FinishReason = "stop",
 185 |             PromptTokens = 12,
 186 |             CompletionTokens = 34,
 187 |         });
 188 |         var prompts = new PromptLoader(enableHotReload: false);
 189 |         var tools = new ToolRegistry(world);
 190 |         var gm = new GameMaster(ai, world, prompts, tools);
 191 | 
 192 |         var cts = new CancellationTokenSource();
 193 |         _ctsToDispose.Add(cts);
 194 |         var session = new HostSession(
 195 |             hostProfile, world, gm,
 196 |             saveManager: null,
 197 |             saveId: null,
 198 |             requestedPort: 0,
 199 |             shutdownToken: cts.Token,
 200 |             bindHost: "localhost");
 201 |         var port = await session.StartAsync();
 202 |         _cleanupSteps.Add(async () => await session.StopAsync());
 203 |         return (session, port, ai, cts);
 204 |     }
 205 | 
 206 |     /// <summary>
 207 |     /// Wait for a TaskCompletionSource with the suite-wide timeout.
 208 |     /// Throws <see cref="TimeoutException"/> with a helpful message on
 209 |     /// timeout so the test output names the event that didn't fire.
 210 |     /// </summary>
 211 |     private static async Task<T> WaitForAsync<T>(TaskCompletionSource<T> tcs, string eventName)
 212 |     {
 213 |         try
 214 |         {
 215 |             return await tcs.Task.WaitAsync(WaitTimeout);
 216 |         }
 217 |         catch (TimeoutException)
 218 |         {
 219 |             throw new TimeoutException(
 220 |                 $"Timed out after {WaitTimeout.TotalSeconds}s waiting for {eventName}.");
 221 |         }
 222 |     }
 223 | 
 224 |     /// <summary>
 225 |     /// Build a TaskCompletionSource wired to the given event via the
 226 |     /// supplied subscribe/unsubscribe callbacks. The returned tuple
 227 |     /// holds the TCS (to await) + an unsubscribe action (to detach the
 228 |     /// handler after the event fires, so a stale handler doesn't bleed
 229 |     /// into the next test).
 230 |     /// </summary>
 231 |     private static (TaskCompletionSource<T> tcs, Action unsubscribe) WireEvent<T>(
 232 |         Action<Action<T>> subscribe,
 233 |         Action<Action<T>> unsubscribe,
 234 |         string label) where T : notnull
 235 |     {
 236 |         var tcs = new TaskCompletionSource<T>(
 237 |             TaskCreationOptions.RunContinuationsAsynchronously);
 238 |         void Handler(T args) => tcs.TrySetResult(args);
 239 |         subscribe(Handler);
 240 |         return (tcs, () => unsubscribe(Handler));
 241 |     }
 242 | 
 243 |     // ─── Tests ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 244 | 
 245 |     /// <summary>
 246 |     /// Issue #57: a fresh HostServer starts on an OS-assigned port and
 247 |     /// a GameClient completes the Hello → Welcome handshake over
 248 |     /// loopback. The WelcomeMsg must carry the server-assigned
 249 |     /// ConnectionId, the host's nickname in the party snapshot, and
 250 |     /// the Lobby status.
 251 |     /// </summary>
 252 |     [Fact]
 253 |     public async Task HostServer_Starts_And_Client_Connects()
 254 |     {
 255 |         var hostProfile = MakeProfile("Host");
 256 |         var (server, port, _) = await StartHostAsync(hostProfile);
 257 | 
 258 |         var clientProfile = MakeProfile("Alice");
 259 |         var (client, welcome) = await ConnectClientAsync(clientProfile, port);
 260 | 
 261 |         // ConnectionId assigned by the host — non-empty Guid.
 262 |         Assert.NotEqual(Guid.Empty, welcome.ConnectionId);
 263 |         Assert.Equal(MemberRole.Player, welcome.Role);
 264 | 
 265 |         // Party snapshot includes the host (with its nickname) AND the
 266 |         // just-joined client (the host broadcasts MemberJoinedMsg
 267 |         // after WelcomeMsg, but the snapshot the client received in
 268 |         // WelcomeMsg reflects the moment of join — at that point the
 269 |         // host is already a member, and the new client is added to
 270 |         // the roster slightly later via MemberJoinedMsg).
 271 |         Assert.Equal(PartyStatus.Lobby, welcome.Party.Status);
 272 |         Assert.Contains(welcome.Party.Members, m => m.Nickname == "Host" && m.Role == MemberRole.Host);
 273 | 
 274 |         // The GameClient exposes the assigned ConnectionId.
 275 |         Assert.Equal(welcome.ConnectionId, client.ConnectionId);
 276 |     }
 277 | 
 278 |     /// <summary>
 279 |     /// Issue #57: when a second client joins, the first client
 280 |     /// receives a <see cref="MemberJoinedMsg"/> carrying the new
 281 |     /// member's nickname + Player role.
 282 |     /// </summary>
 283 |     [Fact]
 284 |     public async Task HostServer_Client_Join_Broadcasts_MemberJoined()
 285 |     {
 286 |         var hostProfile = MakeProfile("Host");
 287 |         var (server, port, _) = await StartHostAsync(hostProfile);
 288 | 
 289 |         var (client1, _) = await ConnectClientAsync(MakeProfile("Alice"), port);
 290 | 
 291 |         // Wire up the MemberJoined listener BEFORE client2 connects
 292 |         // so we don't miss the broadcast.
 293 |         var (joinedTcs, unsub) = WireEvent<MemberJoinedMsg>(
 294 |             h => client1.MemberJoined += h,
 295 |             h => client1.MemberJoined -= h,
 296 |             "MemberJoined");
 297 | 
 298 |         try
 299 |         {
 300 |             var (client2, _) = await ConnectClientAsync(MakeProfile("Bob"), port);
 301 | 
 302 |             var joined = await WaitForAsync(joinedTcs, "client1.MemberJoined");
 303 | 
 304 |             // The joined member is client2 ("Bob"), a Player.
 305 |             Assert.Equal("Bob", joined.Member.Nickname);
 306 |             Assert.Equal(MemberRole.Player, joined.Member.Role);
 307 |             Assert.NotEqual(Guid.Empty, joined.Member.ConnectionId);
 308 |         }
 309 |         finally
 310 |         {
 311 |             unsub();
 312 |         }
 313 |     }
 314 | 
 315 |     /// <summary>
 316 |     /// Issue #57: when the host sends a lobby chat message via
 317 |     /// <see cref="HostServer.BroadcastAsync(NetMessage, CancellationToken)"/>,
 318 |     /// every connected client receives the <see cref="ChatMsg"/> via
 319 |     /// their <see cref="GameClient.ChatReceived"/> event.
 320 |     /// </summary>
 321 |     [Fact]
 322 |     public async Task HostServer_Chat_Relayed_To_All_Clients()
 323 |     {
 324 |         var hostProfile = MakeProfile("Host");
 325 |         var (server, port, cts) = await StartHostAsync(hostProfile);
 326 | 
 327 |         var (client1, _) = await ConnectClientAsync(MakeProfile("Alice"), port);
 328 |         var (client2, _) = await ConnectClientAsync(MakeProfile("Bob"), port);
 329 | 
 330 |         // Wire up chat listeners BEFORE broadcasting so we don't miss
 331 |         // the message. Use TaskCompletionSource per client.
 332 |         var chat1Tcs = new TaskCompletionSource<ChatMsg>(
 333 |             TaskCreationOptions.RunContinuationsAsynchronously);
 334 |         var chat2Tcs = new TaskCompletionSource<ChatMsg>(
 335 |             TaskCreationOptions.RunContinuationsAsynchronously);
 336 |         void Handler1(ChatMsg c) => chat1Tcs.TrySetResult(c);
 337 |         void Handler2(ChatMsg c) => chat2Tcs.TrySetResult(c);
 338 |         client1.ChatReceived += Handler1;
 339 |         client2.ChatReceived += Handler2;
 340 | 
 341 |         try
 342 |         {
 343 |             // Broadcast a chat on behalf of the host. The HostServer
 344 |             // relays to all connected clients.
 345 |             var chatMsg = new ChatMsg
 346 |             {
 347 |                 FromId = server.HostConnectionId,
 348 |                 FromNickname = "Host",
 349 |                 Text = "hello party",
 350 |                 Ts = DateTimeOffset.UtcNow,
 351 |             };
 352 |             await server.BroadcastAsync(chatMsg, cts.Token);
 353 | 
 354 |             // Both clients should receive the chat.
 355 |             var received1 = await WaitForAsync(chat1Tcs, "client1.ChatReceived");
 356 |             var received2 = await WaitForAsync(chat2Tcs, "client2.ChatReceived");
 357 | 
 358 |             Assert.Equal("hello party", received1.Text);
 359 |             Assert.Equal("Host", received1.FromNickname);
 360 |             Assert.Equal("hello party", received2.Text);
 361 |             Assert.Equal("Host", received2.FromNickname);
 362 |         }
 363 |         finally
 364 |         {
 365 |             client1.ChatReceived -= Handler1;
 366 |             client2.ChatReceived -= Handler2;
 367 |         }
 368 |     }
 369 | 
 370 |     /// <summary>
 371 |     /// Issue #57: a client submits an action via
 372 |     /// <see cref="ClientSession.SubmitActionAsync"/>; the host's
 373 |     /// <see cref="HostSession.ProcessNextTurnAsync"/> drains the queue,
 374 |     /// calls the GameMaster (which uses the
 375 |     /// <see cref="StubAiClient"/> to return canned narration), and
 376 |     /// broadcasts <see cref="NarrativeFinalMsg"/> +
 377 |     /// <see cref="StateUpdateMsg"/> to all clients.
 378 |     ///
 379 |     /// <para>
 380 |     /// Verifies the full pipeline: client-side action submission →
 381 |     /// HostServer ActionQueued broadcast → HostSession drain + GM turn
 382 |     /// → NarrativeDelta + NarrativeFinal + StateUpdate + TurnEnd
 383 |     /// broadcasts back to the client.
 384 |     /// </para>
 385 |     /// </summary>
 386 |     [Fact]
 387 |     public async Task HostServer_Action_Queued_And_Resolved()
 388 |     {
 389 |         const string cannedNarration = "Ты осмотрелся. Таверна гудит тихой беседой.";
 390 |         var hostProfile = MakeProfile("Host");
 391 |         var (session, port, stubAi, cts) = await StartHostSessionAsync(hostProfile, cannedNarration);
 392 | 
 393 |         // Connect a client. Use the ClientSession wrapper for action
 394 |         // submission convenience.
 395 |         var clientProfile = MakeProfile("Alice");
 396 |         var clientSession = new ClientSession(clientProfile);
 397 |         _cleanupSteps.Add(async () =>
 398 |         {
 399 |             try { await clientSession.DisconnectAsync(); }
 400 |             catch { /* ignore */ }
 401 |         });
 402 | 
 403 |         var welcomedTcs = new TaskCompletionSource<WelcomeMsg>(
 404 |             TaskCreationOptions.RunContinuationsAsynchronously);
 405 |         clientSession.Welcomed += w => welcomedTcs.TrySetResult(w);
 406 | 
 407 |         using var connectCts = new CancellationTokenSource(WaitTimeout);
 408 |         await clientSession.ConnectAsync("localhost", port, connectCts.Token);
 409 |         await welcomedTcs.Task.WaitAsync(WaitTimeout);
 410 | 
 411 |         // Wire up listeners for the narrative + state-update events.
 412 |         var narrativeFinalTcs = new TaskCompletionSource<NarrativeFinalMsg>(
 413 |             TaskCreationOptions.RunContinuationsAsynchronously);
 414 |         var stateUpdateTcs = new TaskCompletionSource<StateUpdateMsg>(
 415 |             TaskCreationOptions.RunContinuationsAsynchronously);
 416 |         var turnEndTcs = new TaskCompletionSource<TurnEndMsg>(
 417 |             TaskCreationOptions.RunContinuationsAsynchronously);
 418 |         void OnFinal(NarrativeFinalMsg m) => narrativeFinalTcs.TrySetResult(m);
 419 |         void OnState(StateUpdateMsg m) => stateUpdateTcs.TrySetResult(m);
 420 |         void OnTurnEnd(TurnEndMsg m) => turnEndTcs.TrySetResult(m);
 421 |         clientSession.NarrativeFinal += OnFinal;
 422 |         clientSession.StateUpdate += OnState;
 423 |         clientSession.TurnEnd += OnTurnEnd;
 424 | 
 425 |         try
 426 |         {
 427 |             // Submit an action via the client session. The host's
 428 |             // HostServer relays ActionQueuedMsg back; the host's
 429 |             // batching window (issue #12) then fires
 430 |             // ProcessNextTurnAsync. With a single ready non-spectator
 431 |             // member (the host — the client is Pending), the window
 432 |             // fires IMMEDIATELY (no 5-second countdown). The GM turn
 433 |             // runs, broadcasting NarrativeDelta + NarrativeFinal +
 434 |             // StateUpdate + TurnEnd back to the client.
 435 |             //
 436 |             // We don't call ProcessNextTurnAsync from the test — we
 437 |             // let the batching window do it. To verify the turn ran,
 438 |             // we wait for the client's NarrativeFinal event. If the
 439 |             // action never reached the host (network failure), the
 440 |             // wait times out after 10s and the test fails clearly.
 441 |             await clientSession.SubmitActionAsync("осмотреться", cts.Token);
 442 | 
 443 |             // The client should now receive NarrativeFinal + StateUpdate +
 444 |             // TurnEnd. Wait for each (10s timeout each).
 445 |             var final = await WaitForAsync(narrativeFinalTcs, "NarrativeFinal");
 446 |             var stateUpdate = await WaitForAsync(stateUpdateTcs, "StateUpdate");
 447 |             var turnEnd = await WaitForAsync(turnEndTcs, "TurnEnd");
 448 | 
 449 |             // The canned narration is what the GM produced.
 450 |             Assert.Equal(cannedNarration, final.FullText);
 451 |             // Tool events should be empty (the stub returned no tool calls).
 452 |             Assert.Empty(final.ToolEvents);
 453 |             // Token counts match the canned response.
 454 |             Assert.Equal(12, final.PromptTokens);
 455 |             Assert.Equal(34, final.CompletionTokens);
 456 |             // Turn should match across all three broadcast messages.
 457 |             Assert.Equal(turnEnd.TurnNumber, final.Turn);
 458 |             Assert.Equal(turnEnd.TurnNumber, stateUpdate.Turn);
 459 |             Assert.True(turnEnd.TurnNumber > 0, "Turn number should be > 0 after a GM turn.");
 460 |             // State update carries a non-empty world JSON snapshot.
 461 |             Assert.False(string.IsNullOrEmpty(stateUpdate.WorldJson));
 462 |             // The stub made exactly one streaming call (the GM loop
 463 |             // exits on iteration 1 because the canned response has no
 464 |             // tool calls).
 465 |             Assert.Equal(1, stubAi.StreamChatWithToolsCallCount);
 466 |             Assert.Equal(0, stubAi.ChatCallCount);
 467 |         }
 468 |         finally
 469 |         {
 470 |             clientSession.NarrativeFinal -= OnFinal;
 471 |             clientSession.StateUpdate -= OnState;
 472 |             clientSession.TurnEnd -= OnTurnEnd;
 473 |         }
 474 |     }
 475 | 
 476 |     /// <summary>
 477 |     /// Issue #57: when a client disconnects (graceful close), the
 478 |     /// host raises <see cref="HostServer.MemberLeft"/> and broadcasts
 479 |     /// <see cref="MemberLeftMsg"/> to remaining clients.
 480 |     /// </summary>
 481 |     [Fact]
 482 |     public async Task HostServer_Client_Disconnect_Broadcasts_MemberLeft()
 483 |     {
 484 |         var hostProfile = MakeProfile("Host");
 485 |         var (server, port, cts) = await StartHostAsync(hostProfile);
 486 | 
 487 |         var (client1, _) = await ConnectClientAsync(MakeProfile("Alice"), port);
 488 |         var (client2, _) = await ConnectClientAsync(MakeProfile("Bob"), port);
 489 | 
 490 |         // Wire up the MemberLeft listener on client1 BEFORE client2
 491 |         // disconnects so we don't miss the broadcast.
 492 |         var (leftTcs, unsub) = WireEvent<MemberLeftMsg>(
 493 |             h => client1.MemberLeft += h,
 494 |             h => client1.MemberLeft -= h,
 495 |             "MemberLeft");
 496 | 
 497 |         // Also wire up the host-side MemberLeft event.
 498 |         var hostLeftTcs = new TaskCompletionSource<MemberInfo>(
 499 |             TaskCreationOptions.RunContinuationsAsynchronously);
 500 |         void HostHandler(MemberInfo m) => hostLeftTcs.TrySetResult(m);
 501 |         server.MemberLeft += HostHandler;
 502 | 
 503 |         try
 504 |         {
 505 |             // Graceful disconnect from client2.
 506 |             await client2.DisconnectAsync();
 507 | 
 508 |             // The host should raise MemberLeft for client2.
 509 |             var hostLeft = await WaitForAsync(hostLeftTcs, "HostServer.MemberLeft");
 510 |             Assert.Equal("Bob", hostLeft.Nickname);
 511 | 
 512 |             // The remaining client1 should receive a MemberLeftMsg.
 513 |             var left = await WaitForAsync(leftTcs, "client1.MemberLeft");
 514 |             Assert.Equal(client2.ConnectionId, left.ConnectionId);
 515 |         }
 516 |         finally
 517 |         {
 518 |             server.MemberLeft -= HostHandler;
 519 |             unsub();
 520 |         }
 521 |     }
 522 | 
 523 |     /// <summary>
 524 |     /// Issue #57: when the host calls
 525 |     /// <see cref="HostServer.KickAsync"/>, the kicked client receives a
 526 |     /// <see cref="KickedMsg"/> and its connection is closed (its
 527 |     /// <see cref="GameClient.Disconnected"/> event fires with
 528 |     /// <see cref="DisconnectedInfo.Intentional"/> = true).
 529 |     /// </summary>
 530 |     [Fact]
 531 |     public async Task HostServer_Kick_Removes_Client()
 532 |     {
 533 |         var hostProfile = MakeProfile("Host");
 534 |         var (server, port, cts) = await StartHostAsync(hostProfile);
 535 | 
 536 |         var (client, welcome) = await ConnectClientAsync(MakeProfile("Alice"), port);
 537 | 
 538 |         // Wire up Kicked + Disconnected listeners BEFORE the kick so
 539 |         // we don't miss either event.
 540 |         var kickedTcs = new TaskCompletionSource<KickedMsg>(
 541 |             TaskCreationOptions.RunContinuationsAsynchronously);
 542 |         var disconnectedTcs = new TaskCompletionSource<DisconnectedInfo>(
 543 |             TaskCreationOptions.RunContinuationsAsynchronously);
 544 |         void OnKicked(KickedMsg k) => kickedTcs.TrySetResult(k);
 545 |         void OnDisconnected(DisconnectedInfo info) => disconnectedTcs.TrySetResult(info);
 546 |         client.Kicked += OnKicked;
 547 |         client.Disconnected += OnDisconnected;
 548 | 
 549 |         try
 550 |         {
 551 |             // Host kicks the client.
 552 |             await server.KickAsync(welcome.ConnectionId, "test kick", cts.Token);
 553 | 
 554 |             // The client should receive KickedMsg.
 555 |             var kicked = await WaitForAsync(kickedTcs, "Kicked");
 556 |             Assert.Equal("test kick", kicked.Reason);
 557 | 
 558 |             // The client should disconnect (intentional — the GameClient
 559 |             // sets _intentionalDisconnect = true on Kicked).
 560 |             var disconnected = await WaitForAsync(disconnectedTcs, "Disconnected");
 561 |             Assert.True(disconnected.Intentional,
 562 |                 "Kick should mark the disconnect as intentional so the UI " +
 563 |                 "suppresses the reconnect overlay.");
 564 |         }
 565 |         finally
 566 |         {
 567 |             client.Kicked -= OnKicked;
 568 |             client.Disconnected -= OnDisconnected;
 569 |         }
 570 |     }
 571 | }

--- src/MyGame.Tests/Rules/CheckTests.cs (C#, 161 lines, ~1,619 tokens) ---
   1 | using MyGame.Core.Common;
   2 | using MyGame.Core.Rules;
   3 | 
   4 | namespace MyGame.Tests.Rules;
   5 | 
   6 | /// <summary>
   7 | /// Unit tests for the d20 check resolver. Covers crit-success / crit-failure
   8 | /// bands, success-by-meeting-DC, and explicit-failure. The Rng can't be
   9 | /// easily mocked (it's a sealed class), so each test finds a seed whose
  10 | /// first d20 roll produces the value needed for the scenario.
  11 | /// </summary>
  12 | public class CheckTests
  13 | {
  14 |     /// <summary>
  15 |     /// Find a seed such that the FIRST call to D20.Roll(rng, 20) returns
  16 |     /// <paramref name="targetRoll"/>. The returned Rng is fresh (no rolls
  17 |     /// consumed) so the test can hand it straight to
  18 |     /// <see cref="Check.RollCheck"/> which will draw that same first roll.
  19 |     /// </summary>
  20 |     private static Rng FindRngWithFirstRoll(int targetRoll)
  21 |     {
  22 |         // Each d20 face has a 1/20 = 5% probability, so a hit shows up
  23 |         // within ~20 seeds on average. Cap the search at 100k seeds for
  24 |         // safety; if we ever blow through that we'd want to know.
  25 |         for (long seed = 0; seed < 100_000; seed++)
  26 |         {
  27 |             // Probe a throwaway copy so we don't consume the candidate.
  28 |             if (D20.Roll(new Rng(seed), 20) == targetRoll)
  29 |                 return new Rng(seed);
  30 |         }
  31 |         throw new InvalidOperationException(
  32 |             $"Could not find a seed producing a first-roll d20 of {targetRoll}.");
  33 |     }
  34 | 
  35 |     [Fact]
  36 |     public void RollCheck_Natural20_IsCriticalSuccess()
  37 |     {
  38 |         var rng = FindRngWithFirstRoll(20);
  39 |         var result = Check.RollCheck(rng, modifier: 0, dc: 25);
  40 | 
  41 |         Assert.Equal(20, result.Roll);
  42 |         Assert.True(result.CriticalSuccess);
  43 |         Assert.False(result.CriticalFailure);
  44 |         // A natural 20 is always a success regardless of DC.
  45 |         Assert.True(result.Success);
  46 |     }
  47 | 
  48 |     [Fact]
  49 |     public void RollCheck_Natural20_SucceedsEvenAgainstImpossibleDC()
  50 |     {
  51 |         // Crit success ignores DC entirely. DC 100 would normally be
  52 |         // unreachable, but a natural 20 still wins.
  53 |         var rng = FindRngWithFirstRoll(20);
  54 |         var result = Check.RollCheck(rng, modifier: 0, dc: 100);
  55 | 
  56 |         Assert.True(result.CriticalSuccess);
  57 |         Assert.True(result.Success);
  58 |     }
  59 | 
  60 |     [Fact]
  61 |     public void RollCheck_Natural1_IsCriticalFailure()
  62 |     {
  63 |         var rng = FindRngWithFirstRoll(1);
  64 |         var result = Check.RollCheck(rng, modifier: 50, dc: 5);
  65 | 
  66 |         Assert.Equal(1, result.Roll);
  67 |         Assert.True(result.CriticalFailure);
  68 |         Assert.False(result.CriticalSuccess);
  69 |         // A natural 1 is always a failure even if the modifier would
  70 |         // otherwise push Total over the DC.
  71 |         Assert.False(result.Success);
  72 |     }
  73 | 
  74 |     [Fact]
  75 |     public void RollCheck_Natural1_FailsEvenWithHugeModifier()
  76 |     {
  77 |         var rng = FindRngWithFirstRoll(1);
  78 |         // Total = 1 + 1000 = 1001 >> dc 5, but nat 1 still fails.
  79 |         var result = Check.RollCheck(rng, modifier: 1000, dc: 5);
  80 |         Assert.False(result.Success);
  81 |         Assert.True(result.CriticalFailure);
  82 |     }
  83 | 
  84 |     [Fact]
  85 |     public void RollCheck_TotalMeetsDC_IsSuccess()
  86 |     {
  87 |         // Roll 10, modifier 5, dc 15 → Total 15 = dc → success, no crit.
  88 |         var rng = FindRngWithFirstRoll(10);
  89 |         var result = Check.RollCheck(rng, modifier: 5, dc: 15);
  90 | 
  91 |         Assert.Equal(10, result.Roll);
  92 |         Assert.Equal(5, result.Modifier);
  93 |         Assert.Equal(15, result.Total);
  94 |         Assert.True(result.Success);
  95 |         Assert.False(result.CriticalSuccess);
  96 |         Assert.False(result.CriticalFailure);
  97 |     }
  98 | 
  99 |     [Fact]
 100 |     public void RollCheck_TotalExceedsDC_IsSuccess()
 101 |     {
 102 |         var rng = FindRngWithFirstRoll(15);
 103 |         var result = Check.RollCheck(rng, modifier: 3, dc: 10);
 104 | 
 105 |         Assert.Equal(15, result.Roll);
 106 |         Assert.Equal(3, result.Modifier);
 107 |         Assert.Equal(18, result.Total);
 108 |         Assert.True(result.Success);
 109 |         Assert.False(result.CriticalSuccess);
 110 |         Assert.False(result.CriticalFailure);
 111 |     }
 112 | 
 113 |     [Fact]
 114 |     public void RollCheck_TotalBelowDC_IsFailure()
 115 |     {
 116 |         var rng = FindRngWithFirstRoll(5);
 117 |         var result = Check.RollCheck(rng, modifier: 0, dc: 15);
 118 | 
 119 |         Assert.Equal(5, result.Roll);
 120 |         Assert.Equal(0, result.Modifier);
 121 |         Assert.Equal(5, result.Total);
 122 |         Assert.False(result.Success);
 123 |         Assert.False(result.CriticalSuccess);
 124 |         Assert.False(result.CriticalFailure);
 125 |     }
 126 | 
 127 |     [Fact]
 128 |     public void RollCheck_EnumDcOverload_MatchesIntDc()
 129 |     {
 130 |         var rng = FindRngWithFirstRoll(12);
 131 |         var viaEnum = Check.RollCheck(rng, modifier: 5, DifficultyClass.Hard);
 132 |         // Hard = 15. Total = 12 + 5 = 17 → success.
 133 |         Assert.True(viaEnum.Success);
 134 |         Assert.Equal(15, (int)DifficultyClass.Hard);
 135 |     }
 136 | 
 137 |     [Fact]
 138 |     public void RollCheck_NullRng_Throws() =>
 139 |         Assert.Throws<ArgumentNullException>(
 140 |             () => Check.RollCheck(null!, modifier: 0, dc: 10));
 141 | 
 142 |     [Fact]
 143 |     public void RollCheck_AllFacesProduceValidResult()
 144 |     {
 145 |         // Sanity: for every possible d20 face (1..20), the resolver should
 146 |         // produce a well-formed CheckResult (no exceptions, Total = roll +
 147 |         // modifier, modifier echoed back).
 148 |         for (int face = 1; face <= 20; face++)
 149 |         {
 150 |             var rng = FindRngWithFirstRoll(face);
 151 |             var r = Check.RollCheck(rng, modifier: 4, dc: 12);
 152 |             Assert.Equal(face, r.Roll);
 153 |             Assert.Equal(4, r.Modifier);
 154 |             Assert.Equal(face + 4, r.Total);
 155 |             // Crit bands only on 1 and 20.
 156 |             Assert.Equal(face == 20, r.CriticalSuccess);
 157 |             Assert.Equal(face == 1, r.CriticalFailure);
 158 |         }
 159 |     }
 160 | }

--- src/MyGame.Tests/Rules/D20Tests.cs (C#, 177 lines, ~1,464 tokens) ---
   1 | using MyGame.Core.Common;
   2 | using MyGame.Core.Rules;
   3 | 
   4 | namespace MyGame.Tests.Rules;
   5 | 
   6 | /// <summary>
   7 | /// Unit tests for the D20 dice primitives. Covers Roll value ranges,
   8 | /// the default-sides convenience, RollStat bounds, and a statistical
   9 | /// sanity check on advantage vs disadvantage.
  10 | /// </summary>
  11 | public class D20Tests
  12 | {
  13 |     private const int SampleCount = 1000;
  14 | 
  15 |     [Fact]
  16 |     public void Roll_ReturnsValueInRange()
  17 |     {
  18 |         var rng = new Rng(42);
  19 |         for (int i = 0; i < SampleCount; i++)
  20 |         {
  21 |             var v = D20.Roll(rng, 20);
  22 |             Assert.InRange(v, 1, 20);
  23 |         }
  24 |     }
  25 | 
  26 |     [Fact]
  27 |     public void Roll_DefaultSidesIs20()
  28 |     {
  29 |         var rng = new Rng(7);
  30 |         for (int i = 0; i < SampleCount; i++)
  31 |         {
  32 |             // No `sides` arg → defaults to 20. Result must be in [1, 20].
  33 |             var v = D20.Roll(rng);
  34 |             Assert.InRange(v, 1, 20);
  35 |         }
  36 |     }
  37 | 
  38 |     [Fact]
  39 |     public void Roll_CustomSidesRespected()
  40 |     {
  41 |         var rng = new Rng(13);
  42 |         for (int i = 0; i < SampleCount; i++)
  43 |         {
  44 |             var v = D20.Roll(rng, 6);
  45 |             Assert.InRange(v, 1, 6);
  46 |         }
  47 |     }
  48 | 
  49 |     [Fact]
  50 |     public void Roll_ZeroOrNegativeSides_ReturnsZero()
  51 |     {
  52 |         var rng = new Rng(1);
  53 |         Assert.Equal(0, D20.Roll(rng, 0));
  54 |         Assert.Equal(0, D20.Roll(rng, -5));
  55 |     }
  56 | 
  57 |     [Fact]
  58 |     public void Roll_NullRng_Throws()
  59 |     {
  60 |         Assert.Throws<ArgumentNullException>(() => D20.Roll(null!));
  61 |     }
  62 | 
  63 |     [Fact]
  64 |     public void RollWithModifier_AddsModifier()
  65 |     {
  66 |         var rngA = new Rng(99);
  67 |         var rngB = new Rng(99);
  68 |         // Same seed → same base roll; RollWithModifier adds the modifier.
  69 |         var raw = D20.Roll(rngA, 20);
  70 |         Assert.Equal(raw + 5, D20.RollWithModifier(rngB, 20, 5));
  71 |         Assert.Equal(raw - 3, D20.RollWithModifier(new Rng(99), 20, -3));
  72 |     }
  73 | 
  74 |     [Fact]
  75 |     public void RollStat_ReturnsValueInRange()
  76 |     {
  77 |         var rng = new Rng(2024);
  78 |         for (int i = 0; i < 100; i++)
  79 |         {
  80 |             var v = D20.RollStat(rng);
  81 |             // 4d6-drop-lowest: min 3 (four 1s) — max 18 (three 6s).
  82 |             Assert.InRange(v, 3, 18);
  83 |         }
  84 |     }
  85 | 
  86 |     [Fact]
  87 |     public void RollStat_DropsLowest_AtLeastThree()
  88 |     {
  89 |         // Hard to test the drop-lowest mechanic directly without
  90 |         // intercepting the inner rolls. Sanity check: every result is
  91 |         // >= 3 (3d6 floor) and <= 18 (3d6 ceiling). A result of exactly
  92 |         // 3 is possible only if all four dice rolled 1 — rare but legal.
  93 |         var rng = new Rng(55);
  94 |         for (int i = 0; i < 500; i++)
  95 |         {
  96 |             var v = D20.RollStat(rng);
  97 |             Assert.True(v >= 3, $"RollStat returned {v} < 3");
  98 |             Assert.True(v <= 18, $"RollStat returned {v} > 18");
  99 |         }
 100 |     }
 101 | 
 102 |     [Fact]
 103 |     public void RollStat_DistributionFavorsMiddle()
 104 |     {
 105 |         // 4d6-drop-lowest has a bell-ish curve centered near 13. Over
 106 |         // a large sample the mean should be in [10, 15] — far from the
 107 |         // extremes. (Not asserting exact statistics — just sanity.)
 108 |         var rng = new Rng(12345);
 109 |         double sum = 0;
 110 |         const int N = 2000;
 111 |         for (int i = 0; i < N; i++)
 112 |             sum += D20.RollStat(rng);
 113 |         var mean = sum / N;
 114 |         Assert.InRange(mean, 10.0, 15.0);
 115 |     }
 116 | 
 117 |     [Fact]
 118 |     public void RollStat_NullRng_Throws()
 119 |     {
 120 |         Assert.Throws<ArgumentNullException>(() => D20.RollStat(null!));
 121 |     }
 122 | 
 123 |     [Fact]
 124 |     public void Advantage_ReturnsValueInRange()
 125 |     {
 126 |         var rng = new Rng(11);
 127 |         for (int i = 0; i < SampleCount; i++)
 128 |         {
 129 |             var v = D20.Advantage(rng, 20);
 130 |             Assert.InRange(v, 1, 20);
 131 |         }
 132 |     }
 133 | 
 134 |     [Fact]
 135 |     public void Disadvantage_ReturnsValueInRange()
 136 |     {
 137 |         var rng = new Rng(22);
 138 |         for (int i = 0; i < SampleCount; i++)
 139 |         {
 140 |             var v = D20.Disadvantage(rng, 20);
 141 |             Assert.InRange(v, 1, 20);
 142 |         }
 143 |     }
 144 | 
 145 |     [Fact]
 146 |     public void Advantage_GreaterOrEqualToDisadvantage_OnAverage()
 147 |     {
 148 |         // Statistical sanity: over many rolls, advantage's average should
 149 |         // be >= disadvantage's average. Using a fixed seed for determinism
 150 |         // — the property holds robustly for any non-degenerate PRNG with
 151 |         // far more samples than 100 pairs, so a single seed suffices.
 152 |         var rngAdv = new Rng(31337);
 153 |         var rngDis = new Rng(31337);
 154 | 
 155 |         const int Pairs = 500;
 156 |         double advSum = 0, disSum = 0;
 157 |         for (int i = 0; i < Pairs; i++)
 158 |         {
 159 |             advSum += D20.Advantage(rngAdv, 20);
 160 |             disSum += D20.Disadvantage(rngDis, 20);
 161 |         }
 162 | 
 163 |         var advAvg = advSum / Pairs;
 164 |         var disAvg = disSum / Pairs;
 165 |         Assert.True(advAvg >= disAvg,
 166 |             $"Advantage average {advAvg} should be >= Disadvantage average {disAvg}");
 167 |     }
 168 | 
 169 |     [Fact]
 170 |     public void Advantage_NullRng_Throws() =>
 171 |         Assert.Throws<ArgumentNullException>(() => D20.Advantage(null!));
 172 | 
 173 |     [Fact]
 174 |     public void Disadvantage_NullRng_Throws() =>
 175 |         Assert.Throws<ArgumentNullException>(() => D20.Disadvantage(null!));
 176 | }

--- src/MyGame.Tests/Saves/BackupSavesTests.cs (C#, 367 lines, ~4,075 tokens) ---
   1 | using System.Globalization;
   2 | using System.IO;
   3 | using MyGame.Core.Common;
   4 | using MyGame.Core.Saves;
   5 | using MyGame.Core.World;
   6 | using MyGame.Core.World.Content;
   7 | using MyGame.Core.World.Entities;
   8 | 
   9 | // 'World' is both a namespace (MyGame.Core.World) and a type
  10 | // (MyGame.Core.World.World). Alias to GameWorld to disambiguate, matching
  11 | // the convention used by SaveManager.cs.
  12 | using GameWorld = MyGame.Core.World.World;
  13 | 
  14 | namespace MyGame.Tests.Saves;
  15 | 
  16 | /// <summary>
  17 | /// Unit tests for the auto-rotating backup system (issue #81). Covers:
  18 | /// <list type="bullet">
  19 | ///   <item>SaveAll creates a backup of the previous version.</item>
  20 | ///   <item>ListBackups returns timestamps (newest-first).</item>
  21 | ///   <item>RestoreBackup restores the correct version.</item>
  22 | ///   <item>Old backups are auto-deleted (keep 5 most recent).</item>
  23 | ///   <item>Backups older than 30 days are pruned regardless of count.</item>
  24 | ///   <item>First SaveAll on a brand-new save does NOT create a backup.</item>
  25 | /// </list>
  26 | /// </summary>
  27 | public class BackupSavesTests : IDisposable
  28 | {
  29 |     private readonly string _tempDir;
  30 |     private readonly SaveManager _manager;
  31 |     private readonly ContentRegistry _registries;
  32 | 
  33 |     public BackupSavesTests()
  34 |     {
  35 |         _tempDir = Path.Combine(Path.GetTempPath(), "MyGameBackupTests_" + Guid.NewGuid().ToString("N"));
  36 |         Directory.CreateDirectory(_tempDir);
  37 |         _registries = ContentRegistry.LoadDefault();
  38 |         _manager = new SaveManager(savesDirectory: _tempDir, registries: _registries);
  39 |     }
  40 | 
  41 |     public void Dispose()
  42 |     {
  43 |         try { Directory.Delete(_tempDir, recursive: true); }
  44 |         catch { /* best-effort */ }
  45 |     }
  46 | 
  47 |     private static GameWorld MakeWorld() => DefaultWorld.Create(seed: 42);
  48 | 
  49 |     // ─── First-save no backup ─────────────────────────────────────────
  50 | 
  51 |     [Fact]
  52 |     public void FirstSave_CreatesNoBackup()
  53 |     {
  54 |         // A brand-new save has nothing to back up — the SaveAll that
  55 |         // CreateSave triggers should NOT create a backups/ folder.
  56 |         var world = MakeWorld();
  57 |         var meta = _manager.CreateSave("Fresh", world);
  58 | 
  59 |         var backupsRoot = Path.Combine(_tempDir, meta.Id, "backups");
  60 |         Assert.False(Directory.Exists(backupsRoot),
  61 |             "First save should not create a backups/ directory");
  62 |         Assert.Empty(_manager.ListBackups(meta.Id));
  63 |     }
  64 | 
  65 |     // ─── Save creates a backup of previous version ───────────────────
  66 | 
  67 |     [Fact]
  68 |     public void SecondSave_CreatesBackupOfPreviousVersion()
  69 |     {
  70 |         // Save #1: turn=1.
  71 |         var world = MakeWorld();
  72 |         world.Turn = 1;
  73 |         var meta = _manager.CreateSave("BK", world);
  74 | 
  75 |         // Sleep a bit so the next backup timestamp differs (the
  76 |         // timestamp format has millisecond resolution, but on a fast
  77 |         // machine two saves within the same ms would collide and get a
  78 |         // _N suffix — sleeping avoids that for the test's sake).
  79 |         Thread.Sleep(20);
  80 | 
  81 |         // Save #2: turn=2. This should back up the turn=1 state.
  82 |         world.Turn = 2;
  83 |         _manager.SaveAll(meta.Id, world, meta, Array.Empty<LogEntry>());
  84 | 
  85 |         var backups = _manager.ListBackups(meta.Id);
  86 |         Assert.Single(backups);
  87 |         var backup = backups[0];
  88 | 
  89 |         // The backup directory should exist and contain meta.json from
  90 |         // the turn=1 state.
  91 |         var backupDir = Path.Combine(_tempDir, meta.Id, "backups", backup.Timestamp);
  92 |         Assert.True(Directory.Exists(backupDir));
  93 |         Assert.True(File.Exists(Path.Combine(backupDir, "meta.json")));
  94 |         Assert.True(File.Exists(Path.Combine(backupDir, "world.json")));
  95 | 
  96 |         // The backup's meta should reflect turn=1 (the state BEFORE
  97 |         // save #2). Read it directly to bypass the manager's caching.
  98 |         var backupMetaJson = File.ReadAllText(Path.Combine(backupDir, "meta.json"));
  99 |         var backupMeta = System.Text.Json.JsonSerializer.Deserialize<SaveMeta>(
 100 |             backupMetaJson, WorldJson.Options);
 101 |         Assert.NotNull(backupMeta);
 102 |         Assert.Equal(1, backupMeta!.Turn);
 103 |     }
 104 | 
 105 |     // ─── ListBackups ordering ─────────────────────────────────────────
 106 | 
 107 |     [Fact]
 108 |     public void ListBackups_ReturnsNewestFirst()
 109 |     {
 110 |         var world = MakeWorld();
 111 |         var meta = _manager.CreateSave("Order", world);
 112 | 
 113 |         // Make 3 saves (turns 1, 2, 3) — each creates a backup of the
 114 |         // previous. So after the third save we have 2 backups (turn=1
 115 |         // and turn=2). Wait — actually the first SaveAll creates a
 116 |         // backup of the CreateSave state (turn=0); let me bump turns
 117 |         // explicitly so the assertions are clear.
 118 |         for (int i = 1; i <= 3; i++)
 119 |         {
 120 |             Thread.Sleep(20);
 121 |             world.Turn = i;
 122 |             _manager.SaveAll(meta.Id, world, meta, Array.Empty<LogEntry>());
 123 |         }
 124 | 
 125 |         // After 3 SaveAll calls, we have 3 backups (of turn=0, 1, 2).
 126 |         var backups = _manager.ListBackups(meta.Id);
 127 |         Assert.Equal(3, backups.Count);
 128 | 
 129 |         // Newest-first: backup[0] should have a later timestamp than
 130 |         // backup[1], which should be later than backup[2].
 131 |         Assert.True(backups[0].CreatedAt > backups[1].CreatedAt);
 132 |         Assert.True(backups[1].CreatedAt > backups[2].CreatedAt);
 133 |     }
 134 | 
 135 |     [Fact]
 136 |     public void ListBackups_EmptyForNonexistentSave()
 137 |     {
 138 |         Assert.Empty(_manager.ListBackups("save_" + new string('a', 32)));
 139 |     }
 140 | 
 141 |     [Fact]
 142 |     public void ListBackups_EmptyForInvalidSaveId()
 143 |     {
 144 |         Assert.Empty(_manager.ListBackups("not_a_real_save_id"));
 145 |     }
 146 | 
 147 |     // ─── Restore ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 148 | 
 149 |     [Fact]
 150 |     public void RestoreBackup_RestoresCorrectVersion()
 151 |     {
 152 |         var world = MakeWorld();
 153 |         world.Turn = 100;
 154 |         var meta = _manager.CreateSave("Restore", world);
 155 | 
 156 |         // Save with turn=200 → backup of turn=100 is created.
 157 |         Thread.Sleep(20);
 158 |         world.Turn = 200;
 159 |         _manager.SaveAll(meta.Id, world, meta, Array.Empty<LogEntry>());
 160 | 
 161 |         // Verify current state is turn=200.
 162 |         var loaded = _manager.LoadAll(meta.Id);
 163 |         Assert.Equal(200, loaded!.Value.world.Turn);
 164 | 
 165 |         // Restore the turn=100 backup.
 166 |         var backups = _manager.ListBackups(meta.Id);
 167 |         Assert.Single(backups);
 168 |         var restored = _manager.RestoreBackup(meta.Id, backups[0].Timestamp);
 169 |         Assert.True(restored);
 170 | 
 171 |         // The current save should now be turn=100 again.
 172 |         loaded = _manager.LoadAll(meta.Id);
 173 |         Assert.NotNull(loaded);
 174 |         Assert.Equal(100, loaded!.Value.world.Turn);
 175 |     }
 176 |

 178 |     public void RestoreBackup_NonexistentBackup_ReturnsFalse()
 179 |     {
 180 |         var world = MakeWorld();
 181 |         var meta = _manager.CreateSave("RB2", world);
 182 | 
 183 |         Assert.False(_manager.RestoreBackup(meta.Id, "nonexistent_backup"));
 184 |     }
