 186 |     [Fact]
 187 |     public void RestoreBackup_NonexistentSave_ReturnsFalse()
 188 |     {
 189 |         Assert.False(_manager.RestoreBackup("save_" + new string('a', 32), "anything"));
 190 |     }
 191 | 
 192 |     [Fact]
 193 |     public void RestoreBackup_RejectsPathTraversal()
 194 |     {
 195 |         var world = MakeWorld();
 196 |         var meta = _manager.CreateSave("RB3", world);
 197 | 
 198 |         // The timestamp must not contain '..' or slashes — the restore
 199 |         // path is backups/{ts}/, so a malicious ts like "../foo" would
 200 |         // escape the save dir. The manager should reject it.
 201 |         Assert.False(_manager.RestoreBackup(meta.Id, ".."));
 202 |         Assert.False(_manager.RestoreBackup(meta.Id, "../escape"));
 203 |         Assert.False(_manager.RestoreBackup(meta.Id, "foo/bar"));
 204 |         Assert.False(_manager.RestoreBackup(meta.Id, "foo\\bar"));
 205 |     }
 206 | 
 207 |     // ─── Auto-cleanup (keep 5) ────────────────────────────────────────
 208 | 
 209 |     [Fact]
 210 |     public void AutoCleanup_KeepsOnlyFiveMostRecentBackups()
 211 |     {
 212 |         var world = MakeWorld();
 213 |         var meta = _manager.CreateSave("Prune", world);
 214 | 
 215 |         // Make 10 saves — each SaveAll backs up the previous state.
 216 |         // After the 10th save, we should have at most 5 backups
 217 |         // (the most recent 5). Save #1's backup gets pruned.
 218 |         for (int i = 1; i <= 10; i++)
 219 |         {
 220 |             Thread.Sleep(20);
 221 |             world.Turn = i;
 222 |             _manager.SaveAll(meta.Id, world, meta, Array.Empty<LogEntry>());
 223 |         }
 224 | 
 225 |         var backups = _manager.ListBackups(meta.Id);
 226 |         Assert.True(backups.Count <= SaveManager.MaxBackupsPerSave,
 227 |             $"Expected at most {SaveManager.MaxBackupsPerSave} backups, got {backups.Count}");
 228 |         Assert.Equal(SaveManager.MaxBackupsPerSave, backups.Count);
 229 |     }
 230 | 
 231 |     [Fact]
 232 |     public void AutoCleanup_DeletesOldBackupsBeyondFive()
 233 |     {
 234 |         // Verify the OLDEST backups are the ones pruned (not random ones).
 235 |         var world = MakeWorld();
 236 |         var meta = _manager.CreateSave("Prune2", world);
 237 | 
 238 |         var backupTimestamps = new List<string>();
 239 |         for (int i = 1; i <= 7; i++)
 240 |         {
 241 |             Thread.Sleep(20);
 242 |             world.Turn = i;
 243 |             _manager.SaveAll(meta.Id, world, meta, Array.Empty<LogEntry>());
 244 |             // After each save, record the current backups.
 245 |             backupTimestamps = _manager.ListBackups(meta.Id)
 246 |                 .Select(b => b.Timestamp).ToList();
 247 |         }
 248 | 
 249 |         // After 7 SaveAll calls, only 5 backups survive.
 250 |         var finalBackups = _manager.ListBackups(meta.Id);
 251 |         Assert.Equal(5, finalBackups.Count);
 252 | 
 253 |         // The newest 5 of the original 7 should be the survivors. The
 254 |         // oldest 2 should be gone.
 255 |         var survivors = finalBackups.Select(b => b.Timestamp).ToHashSet();
 256 |         Assert.Equal(5, survivors.Count);
 257 | 
 258 |         // The 5 newest timestamps should be in survivors. The 2 oldest
 259 |         // should not. (We sort the recorded timestamps newest-first;
 260 |         // the first 5 are the survivors.)
 261 |         var sortedTimestamps = backupTimestamps
 262 |             .OrderByDescending(t => t, StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
 263 |             .ToList();
 264 |         foreach (var ts in sortedTimestamps.Take(5))
 265 |             Assert.Contains(ts, survivors);
 266 |         foreach (var ts in sortedTimestamps.Skip(5))
 267 |             Assert.DoesNotContain(ts, survivors);
 268 |     }
 269 | 
 270 |     // ─── 30-day age rule ──────────────────────────────────────────────
 271 | 
 272 |     [Fact]
 273 |     public void AutoCleanup_DeletesBackupsOlderThan30Days()
 274 |     {
 275 |         // Manually craft a save dir with backups that have timestamps
 276 |         // 31 and 10 days ago, then trigger another save. The 31-day-old
 277 |         // backup should be pruned; the 10-day-old should survive (even
 278 |         // if we have fewer than 5 backups).
 279 |         var world = MakeWorld();
 280 |         var meta = _manager.CreateSave("Age", world);
 281 |         var saveDir = Path.Combine(_tempDir, meta.Id);
 282 |         var backupsRoot = Path.Combine(saveDir, "backups");
 283 |         Directory.CreateDirectory(backupsRoot);
 284 | 
 285 |         var oldTs = DateTimeOffset.UtcNow.AddDays(-31).ToString("yyyyMMddHHmmssfff", CultureInfo.InvariantCulture);
 286 |         var newTs = DateTimeOffset.UtcNow.AddDays(-10).ToString("yyyyMMddHHmmssfff", CultureInfo.InvariantCulture);
 287 |         foreach (var ts in new[] { oldTs, newTs })
 288 |         {
 289 |             var dir = Path.Combine(backupsRoot, ts);
 290 |             Directory.CreateDirectory(dir);
 291 |             File.Copy(Path.Combine(saveDir, "meta.json"), Path.Combine(dir, "meta.json"));
 292 |             File.Copy(Path.Combine(saveDir, "world.json"), Path.Combine(dir, "world.json"));
 293 |         }
 294 | 
 295 |         // Sanity: we have 2 backups before the next save.
 296 |         Assert.Equal(2, _manager.ListBackups(meta.Id).Count);
 297 | 
 298 |         // Trigger another save — PruneBackups should delete the 31-day
 299 |         // backup (it's beyond the 30-day cutoff AND not in the top 5 —
 300 |         // both rules would allow it, but the age rule is the binding
 301 |         // one here).
 302 |         Thread.Sleep(20);
 303 |         world.Turn = 99;
 304 |         _manager.SaveAll(meta.Id, world, meta, Array.Empty<LogEntry>());
 305 | 
 306 |         var remaining = _manager.ListBackups(meta.Id);
 307 |         // We had 2 (old + new), then SaveAll added 1 more (the
 308 |         // pre-save state). The 31-day-old one should be pruned.
 309 |         // Net: 2 backups (the 10-day-old + the just-created one).
 310 |         var timestamps = remaining.Select(b => b.Timestamp).ToHashSet();
 311 |         Assert.DoesNotContain(oldTs, timestamps);
 312 |         Assert.Contains(newTs, timestamps);
 313 |     }
 314 | 
 315 |     // ─── Backups survive a regular SaveAll ────────────────────────────
 316 | 
 317 |     [Fact]
 318 |     public void SaveAll_DoesNotDeleteBackupsSubdir()
 319 |     {
 320 |         // The backups/ subfolder is INSIDE the save dir. SaveAll must
 321 |         // not delete it (only the top-level meta/world/log/state files
 322 |         // are overwritten).
 323 |         var world = MakeWorld();
 324 |         var meta = _manager.CreateSave("Keep", world);
 325 | 
 326 |         Thread.Sleep(20);
 327 |         world.Turn = 1;
 328 |         _manager.SaveAll(meta.Id, world, meta, Array.Empty<LogEntry>());
 329 | 
 330 |         var backupsRoot = Path.Combine(_tempDir, meta.Id, "backups");
 331 |         Assert.True(Directory.Exists(backupsRoot));
 332 |         Assert.NotEmpty(_manager.ListBackups(meta.Id));
 333 |     }
 334 | 
 335 |     // ─── Backups work with compression too ───────────────────────────
 336 | 
 337 |     [Fact]
 338 |     public void Backup_Works_WithCompressionEnabled()
 339 |     {
 340 |         // The backup system should work the same whether the save is
 341 |         // compressed or not. The backup just copies whatever files are
 342 |         // there.
 343 |         _manager.CompressSaves = true;
 344 |         var world = MakeWorld();
 345 |         world.Turn = 5;
 346 |         var meta = _manager.CreateSave("C", world);
 347 | 
 348 |         Thread.Sleep(20);
 349 |         world.Turn = 6;
 350 |         _manager.SaveAll(meta.Id, world, meta, Array.Empty<LogEntry>());
 351 | 
 352 |         var backups = _manager.ListBackups(meta.Id);
 353 |         Assert.Single(backups);
 354 | 
 355 |         // The backup should contain world.json.gz (the compressed
 356 |         // variant) since that's what was on disk before save #2.
 357 |         var backupDir = Path.Combine(_tempDir, meta.Id, "backups", backups[0].Timestamp);
 358 |         Assert.True(File.Exists(Path.Combine(backupDir, "world.json.gz")));
 359 |         Assert.False(File.Exists(Path.Combine(backupDir, "world.json")));
 360 | 
 361 |         // Restore should bring back the turn=5 state.
 362 |         Assert.True(_manager.RestoreBackup(meta.Id, backups[0].Timestamp));
 363 |         var loaded = _manager.LoadAll(meta.Id);
 364 |         Assert.Equal(5, loaded!.Value.world.Turn);
 365 |     }
 366 | }

--- src/MyGame.Tests/Saves/SaveCompressionTests.cs (C#, 283 lines, ~3,191 tokens) ---
   1 | using System.IO;
   2 | using System.IO.Compression;
   3 | using System.Text;
   4 | using MyGame.Core.Common;
   5 | using MyGame.Core.Saves;
   6 | using MyGame.Core.World;
   7 | using MyGame.Core.World.Content;
   8 | using MyGame.Core.World.Entities;
   9 | 
  10 | // 'World' is both a namespace (MyGame.Core.World) and a type
  11 | // (MyGame.Core.World.World). Alias to GameWorld to disambiguate, matching
  12 | // the convention used by SaveManager.cs.
  13 | using GameWorld = MyGame.Core.World.World;
  14 | 
  15 | namespace MyGame.Tests.Saves;
  16 | 
  17 | /// <summary>
  18 | /// Unit tests for save-file gzip compression (issue #80). Covers:
  19 | /// <list type="bullet">
  20 | ///   <item>Compressed save round-trips (save → load → verify world matches).</item>
  21 | ///   <item>Uncompressed save still loads (backward compat with old saves).</item>
  22 | ///   <item>Compressed save is smaller than uncompressed for the same world.</item>
  23 | ///   <item>Toggling CompressSaves between writes switches the on-disk layout
  24 | ///     and the next load still works (forward + backward compat).</item>
  25 | ///   <item>meta.json stays uncompressed (ListSaves fast-path).</item>
  26 | /// </list>
  27 | /// </summary>
  28 | public class SaveCompressionTests : IDisposable
  29 | {
  30 |     private readonly string _tempDir;
  31 |     private readonly SaveManager _manager;
  32 |     private readonly ContentRegistry _registries;
  33 | 
  34 |     public SaveCompressionTests()
  35 |     {
  36 |         _tempDir = Path.Combine(Path.GetTempPath(), "MyGameCompressTests_" + Guid.NewGuid().ToString("N"));
  37 |         Directory.CreateDirectory(_tempDir);
  38 |         _registries = ContentRegistry.LoadDefault();
  39 |         _manager = new SaveManager(savesDirectory: _tempDir, registries: _registries);
  40 |     }
  41 | 
  42 |     public void Dispose()
  43 |     {
  44 |         try { Directory.Delete(_tempDir, recursive: true); }
  45 |         catch { /* best-effort */ }
  46 |     }
  47 | 
  48 |     private static GameWorld MakeWorld() => DefaultWorld.Create(seed: 42);
  49 | 
  50 |     // ─── Round-trip ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  51 | 
  52 |     [Fact]
  53 |     public void Compressed_Save_RoundTripsWorld()
  54 |     {
  55 |         // Default CompressSaves=false (matches the SaveManager ctor
  56 |         // default used by the other test suites). Toggle it ON for
  57 |         // this test to exercise the gzip path.
  58 |         _manager.CompressSaves = true;
  59 | 
  60 |         var world = MakeWorld();
  61 |         // Mutate the world so we can verify the loaded copy isn't a
  62 |         // fresh DefaultWorld (e.g. the turn counter, an item).
  63 |         world.Turn = 13;
  64 |         world.Items.Add(new() { Id = EntityId.NewId(), Name = "compressed-test-item" });
  65 | 
  66 |         var meta = _manager.CreateSave("Compressed", world);
  67 | 
  68 |         // Verify the on-disk layout: world.json.gz exists, world.json
  69 |         // (plain) does NOT.
  70 |         var saveDir = Path.Combine(_tempDir, meta.Id);
  71 |         Assert.True(File.Exists(Path.Combine(saveDir, "world.json.gz")),
  72 |             "world.json.gz should exist when CompressSaves=true");
  73 |         Assert.False(File.Exists(Path.Combine(saveDir, "world.json")),
  74 |             "world.json (plain) should NOT exist when CompressSaves=true");
  75 |         Assert.True(File.Exists(Path.Combine(saveDir, "log.json.gz")));
  76 |         Assert.True(File.Exists(Path.Combine(saveDir, "state.json.gz")));
  77 |         // meta.json stays uncompressed (ListSaves fast-path).
  78 |         Assert.True(File.Exists(Path.Combine(saveDir, "meta.json")));
  79 |         Assert.False(File.Exists(Path.Combine(saveDir, "meta.json.gz")));
  80 | 
  81 |         // Round-trip: load the compressed save back.
  82 |         var loaded = _manager.LoadAll(meta.Id);
  83 |         Assert.NotNull(loaded);
  84 |         Assert.Equal(13, loaded!.Value.world.Turn);
  85 |         Assert.Contains(loaded.Value.world.Items,
  86 |             i => i.Name == "compressed-test-item");
  87 |     }
  88 | 
  89 |     [Fact]
  90 |     public void Compressed_LoadWorld_RoundTripsRng()
  91 |     {
  92 |         _manager.CompressSaves = true;
  93 | 
  94 |         var world = MakeWorld();
  95 |         for (int i = 0; i < 5; i++) world.Rng.Next();
  96 |         world.RngState = world.Rng.State;
  97 |         var stateBefore = world.Rng.State;
  98 | 
  99 |         var meta = _manager.CreateSave("RNG", world);
 100 |         var loaded = _manager.LoadWorld(meta.Id);
 101 |         Assert.NotNull(loaded);
 102 |         Assert.Equal(stateBefore, loaded!.RngState);
 103 | 
 104 |         // Continuing the stream should produce the same outputs.
 105 |         for (int i = 0; i < 5; i++)
 106 |             Assert.Equal(world.Rng.Next(), loaded.Rng.Next());
 107 |     }
 108 | 
 109 |     // ─── Backward compatibility ───────────────────────────────────────
 110 | 
 111 |     [Fact]
 112 |     public void Uncompressed_Save_Still_Loads_WhenCompressFlagTrue()
 113 |     {
 114 |         // Write an UNCOMPRESSED save (CompressSaves=false), then turn
 115 |         // the flag on and verify the save still loads (backward compat:
 116 |         // the loader probes for .gz first, falls back to plain .json).
 117 |         _manager.CompressSaves = false;
 118 |         var world = MakeWorld();
 119 |         world.Turn = 7;
 120 |         var meta = _manager.CreateSave("Plain", world);
 121 | 
 122 |         var saveDir = Path.Combine(_tempDir, meta.Id);
 123 |         Assert.True(File.Exists(Path.Combine(saveDir, "world.json")));
 124 |         Assert.False(File.Exists(Path.Combine(saveDir, "world.json.gz")));
 125 | 
 126 |         // Now flip the flag ON and load. The save should still load
 127 |         // because TryReadText falls back to plain .json.
 128 |         _manager.CompressSaves = true;
 129 |         var loaded = _manager.LoadAll(meta.Id);
 130 |         Assert.NotNull(loaded);
 131 |         Assert.Equal(7, loaded!.Value.world.Turn);
 132 |     }
 133 | 
 134 |     [Fact]
 135 |     public void Compressed_Save_Still_Loads_WhenCompressFlagFalse()
 136 |     {
 137 |         // Write a COMPRESSED save, then turn the flag OFF and verify
 138 |         // the save still loads (forward compat: a save written with
 139 |         // compression ON loads fine when the manager later has
 140 |         // compression OFF).
 141 |         _manager.CompressSaves = true;
 142 |         var world = MakeWorld();
 143 |         world.Turn = 21;
 144 |         var meta = _manager.CreateSave("Compressed", world);
 145 | 
 146 |         _manager.CompressSaves = false;
 147 |         var loaded = _manager.LoadAll(meta.Id);
 148 |         Assert.NotNull(loaded);
 149 |         Assert.Equal(21, loaded!.Value.world.Turn);
 150 |     }
 151 | 
 152 |     // ─── Toggle mid-session ───────────────────────────────────────────
 153 | 
 154 |     [Fact]
 155 |     public void ToggleCompression_BetweenSaves_SwitchesLayoutAndKeepsLoadable()
 156 |     {
 157 |         // First save: uncompressed.
 158 |         _manager.CompressSaves = false;
 159 |         var world = MakeWorld();
 160 |         world.Turn = 1;
 161 |         var meta = _manager.CreateSave("Toggle", world);
 162 | 
 163 |         var saveDir = Path.Combine(_tempDir, meta.Id);
 164 |         Assert.True(File.Exists(Path.Combine(saveDir, "world.json")));
 165 | 
 166 |         // Second save: compressed — should DELETE the plain world.json
 167 |         // and write world.json.gz.
 168 |         _manager.CompressSaves = true;
 169 |         world.Turn = 2;
 170 |         _manager.SaveAll(meta.Id, world, meta, Array.Empty<LogEntry>());
 171 | 
 172 |         Assert.True(File.Exists(Path.Combine(saveDir, "world.json.gz")));
 173 |         Assert.False(File.Exists(Path.Combine(saveDir, "world.json")),
 174 |             "stale plain world.json should be deleted after a compressed save");
 175 | 
 176 |         var loaded = _manager.LoadAll(meta.Id);
 177 |         Assert.NotNull(loaded);
 178 |         Assert.Equal(2, loaded!.Value.world.Turn);
 179 | 
 180 |         // Third save: uncompressed again — should delete the .gz and
 181 |         // write plain .json.
 182 |         _manager.CompressSaves = false;
 183 |         world.Turn = 3;
 184 |         _manager.SaveAll(meta.Id, world, meta, Array.Empty<LogEntry>());
 185 | 
 186 |         Assert.True(File.Exists(Path.Combine(saveDir, "world.json")));
 187 |         Assert.False(File.Exists(Path.Combine(saveDir, "world.json.gz")),
 188 |             "stale world.json.gz should be deleted after an uncompressed save");
 189 | 
 190 |         loaded = _manager.LoadAll(meta.Id);
 191 |         Assert.NotNull(loaded);
 192 |         Assert.Equal(3, loaded!.Value.world.Turn);
 193 |     }
 194 | 
 195 |     // ─── Size reduction ───────────────────────────────────────────────
 196 | 
 197 |     [Fact]
 198 |     public void Compressed_Save_IsSmaller_ThanUncompressed()
 199 |     {
 200 |         // Build the same world twice: once compressed, once uncompressed.
 201 |         // The compressed world.json.gz must be smaller than the plain
 202 |         // world.json. (DefaultWorld has a fair amount of text — names,
 203 |         // descriptions — so gzip should shrink it meaningfully.)
 204 |         var world = MakeWorld();
 205 | 
 206 |         // Compressed save.
 207 |         _manager.CompressSaves = true;
 208 |         var compressedMeta = _manager.CreateSave("C", world);
 209 |         var compressedWorldPath = Path.Combine(_tempDir, compressedMeta.Id, "world.json.gz");
 210 |         var compressedSize = new FileInfo(compressedWorldPath).Length;
 211 | 
 212 |         // Uncompressed save.
 213 |         _manager.CompressSaves = false;
 214 |         var plainMeta = _manager.CreateSave("P", world);
 215 |         var plainWorldPath = Path.Combine(_tempDir, plainMeta.Id, "world.json");
 216 |         var plainSize = new FileInfo(plainWorldPath).Length;
 217 | 
 218 |         // The compressed size should be strictly smaller. (Gzip on a
 219 |         // non-trivial JSON blob will always beat raw UTF-8 here —
 220 |         // DefaultWorld has lots of repeated structure.)
 221 |         Assert.True(compressedSize < plainSize,
 222 |             $"compressed world.json.gz ({compressedSize} B) should be smaller than " +
 223 |             $"plain world.json ({plainSize} B)");
 224 |     }
 225 | 
 226 |     // ─── Magic bytes / file integrity ─────────────────────────────────
 227 | 
 228 |     [Fact]
 229 |     public void Compressed_File_HasGzipMagicBytes()
 230 |     {
 231 |         // Gzip files start with the magic bytes 1F 8B. This is a sanity
 232 |         // check that we're writing real gzip (not just renaming a plain
 233 |         // file with a .gz extension).
 234 |         _manager.CompressSaves = true;
 235 |         var world = MakeWorld();
 236 |         var meta = _manager.CreateSave("Magic", world);
 237 | 
 238 |         var gzPath = Path.Combine(_tempDir, meta.Id, "world.json.gz");
 239 |         var bytes = File.ReadAllBytes(gzPath);
 240 |         Assert.True(bytes.Length >= 2, "world.json.gz should not be empty");
 241 |         Assert.Equal(0x1F, bytes[0]);
 242 |         Assert.Equal(0x8B, bytes[1]);
 243 |     }
 244 | 
 245 |     [Fact]
 246 |     public void Compressed_File_DecompressesToValidJson()
 247 |     {
 248 |         // Manually decompress world.json.gz and verify the result is
 249 |         // the same JSON the World.ToJson() produced. This catches any
 250 |         // encoding bug (e.g. wrong codepage, truncated stream).
 251 |         _manager.CompressSaves = true;
 252 |         var world = MakeWorld();
 253 |         var expectedJson = world.ToJson();
 254 |         var meta = _manager.CreateSave("Manual", world);
 255 | 
 256 |         var gzPath = Path.Combine(_tempDir, meta.Id, "world.json.gz");
 257 |         string decompressed;
 258 |         using (var fs = File.OpenRead(gzPath))
 259 |         using (var gz = new GZipStream(fs, CompressionMode.Decompress))
 260 |         using (var reader = new StreamReader(gz, Encoding.UTF8))
 261 |         {
 262 |             decompressed = reader.ReadToEnd();
 263 |         }
 264 | 
 265 |         Assert.Equal(expectedJson, decompressed);
 266 |     }
 267 | 
 268 |     // ─── ListSaves still works with compressed saves ─────────────────
 269 | 
 270 |     [Fact]
 271 |     public void ListSaves_ReturnsCompressedSaves()
 272 |     {
 273 |         // ListSaves reads meta.json (always uncompressed) — should work
 274 |         // the same with compression ON.
 275 |         _manager.CompressSaves = true;
 276 |         var world = MakeWorld();
 277 |         var meta = _manager.CreateSave("Listed", world);
 278 | 
 279 |         var saves = _manager.ListSaves();
 280 |         Assert.Contains(saves, s => s.Id == meta.Id && s.Name == "Listed");
 281 |     }
 282 | }

  13 | /// <summary>

  24 |     public SaveManagerTests()
  25 |     {
  26 |         // Per-test temp dir under the system temp path. The SaveManager
  27 |         // ctor accepts an explicit savesDirectory so we don't have to
  28 |         // monkey-patch ProfileStore.DefaultProfileDirectory.

  36 |     {

  38 |         catch { /* best-effort cleanup; tests shouldn't fail on teardown */ }
  39 |     }

 147 |         Thread.Sleep(50);

 149 | 
 150 |         var saves = _manager.ListSaves();
 151 |         Assert.Equal(3, saves.Count);
 152 |         // Most recently played first.
 153 |         Assert.Equal(c.Id, saves[0].Id);
 154 |         Assert.Equal(b.Id, saves[1].Id);
 155 |         Assert.Equal(a.Id, saves[2].Id);
 156 |     }

 164 |

 166 |         Assert.True(deleted);

 196 |     [Fact]
 197 |     public void LoadWorld_RoundTripsRngState()
 198 |     {
 199 |         var world = MakeWorld();
 200 |         // Advance RNG past the construction state, then sync RngState so
 201 |         // the save captures the post-roll position. (World.RngState is the
 202 |         // serialized snapshot of the live Rng's state, but the live Rng
 203 |         // advances independently — the property isn't auto-synced.)
 204 |         for (int i = 0; i < 5; i++) world.Rng.Next();
 205 |         world.RngState = world.Rng.State;
 206 |         var stateBeforeSave = world.Rng.State;
 207 | 
 208 |         var meta = _manager.CreateSave("RNG Test", world);
 209 |         var loaded = _manager.LoadWorld(meta.Id);
 210 |         Assert.NotNull(loaded);
 211 |         Assert.Equal(stateBeforeSave, loaded!.RngState);
 212 | 
 213 |         // Continuing the stream should produce the same outputs.
 214 |         for (int i = 0; i < 5; i++)
 215 |             Assert.Equal(world.Rng.Next(), loaded.Rng.Next());
 216 |     }
 217 | 
 218 |     [Fact]
 219 |     public void SaveAll_UpdatesExistingSave()
 220 |     {
 221 |         var world = MakeWorld();
 222 |         var meta = _manager.CreateSave("Update Me", world);
 223 | 
 224 |         // Mutate the world: bump turn counter, add an item.
 225 |         world.Turn = 7;
 226 |         world.Items.Add(new() { Id = EntityId.NewId(), Name = "new item" });
 227 | 
 228 |         _manager.SaveAll(meta.Id, world, meta, Array.Empty<LogEntry>());
 229 | 
 230 |         var reloaded = _manager.LoadAll(meta.Id);
 231 |         Assert.NotNull(reloaded);
 232 |         Assert.Equal(7, reloaded.Value.world.Turn);
 233 |         Assert.Single(reloaded.Value.world.Items);
 234 |     }
 235 | 
 236 |     [Fact]
 237 |     public void NewSaveId_IsWellFormed()
 238 |     {
 239 |         var id = SaveManager.NewSaveId();
 240 |         Assert.StartsWith("save_", id);
 241 |         Assert.Equal(37, id.Length);
 242 |         // Hex chars after "save_" prefix.
 243 |         foreach (var c in id.Substring(5))
 244 |             Assert.True((c >= '0' && c <= '9') || (c >= 'a' && c <= 'f'));
 245 |     }
 246 | }

   1 | using System.Text.Json;
   2 | using MyGame.Core.Saves;
   3 | using MyGame.Core.World;
   4 | using MyGame.Core.World.Content;
   5 | 
   6 | // 'World' is both a namespace (MyGame.Core.World) and a type
   7 | // (MyGame.Core.World.World). Alias to GameWorld to disambiguate, matching
   8 | // the convention used by SaveManager.cs.
   9 | using GameWorld = MyGame.Core.World.World;
  10 | 
  11 | namespace MyGame.Tests.Saves;
  12 | 
  13 | /// <summary>
  14 | /// Unit tests for the <see cref="SaveMeta.HistorySummary"/> field (issue #25).
  15 | /// Verifies that the summary round-trips through JSON (save/load) and
  16 | /// that old saves without the field load with null (backward compat).
  17 | /// </summary>
  18 | public class SaveMetaHistorySummaryTests
  19 | {
  20 |     private static string TempDir()
  21 |     {
  22 |         var dir = Path.Combine(Path.GetTempPath(), "MyGameSaveMetaTests_" + Guid.NewGuid().ToString("N"));
  23 |         Directory.CreateDirectory(dir);
  24 |         return dir;
  25 |     }
  26 | 
  27 |     [Fact]
  28 |     public void HistorySummary_DefaultsToNull()
  29 |     {
  30 |         // A fresh SaveMeta has HistorySummary = null (no summarization
  31 |         // has happened yet on a new save).
  32 |         var meta = new SaveMeta { Id = "save_test", Name = "test" };
  33 |         Assert.Null(meta.HistorySummary);
  34 |     }
  35 | 
  36 |     [Fact]
  37 |     public void HistorySummary_RoundTripsThroughJson()
  38 |     {
  39 |         // Serialising a meta with a non-null HistorySummary + deserialising
  40 |         // back must preserve the summary text exactly. This is the
  41 |         // save/load round-trip the GameViewModel relies on to persist
  42 |         // the GM's context across reloads.
  43 |         var meta = new SaveMeta
  44 |         {
  45 |             Id = "save_test",
  46 |             Name = "test",
  47 |             HistorySummary = "Игрок встретил торговца Арвина в Деревне, купил зелье, отправился в Тёмный лес.",
  48 |         };
  49 | 
  50 |         var json = JsonSerializer.Serialize(meta, WorldJson.Options);
  51 |         var back = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<SaveMeta>(json, WorldJson.Options);
  52 | 
  53 |         Assert.NotNull(back);
  54 |         Assert.Equal(meta.HistorySummary, back!.HistorySummary);
  55 |     }
  56 | 
  57 |     [Fact]
  58 |     public void HistorySummary_NullRoundTripsAsNull()
  59 |     {
  60 |         // A null HistorySummary must round-trip as null (not as empty
  61 |         // string, not as undefined). The GM treats null as "no summary
  62 |         // yet" and starts fresh summarization when the history grows.
  63 |         var meta = new SaveMeta
  64 |         {
  65 |             Id = "save_test",
  66 |             Name = "test",
  67 |             HistorySummary = null,
  68 |         };
  69 | 
  70 |         var json = JsonSerializer.Serialize(meta, WorldJson.Options);
  71 |         var back = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<SaveMeta>(json, WorldJson.Options);
  72 | 
  73 |         Assert.NotNull(back);
  74 |         Assert.Null(back!.HistorySummary);
  75 |     }
  76 | 
  77 |     [Fact]
  78 |     public void HistorySummary_OldSaveWithoutField_LoadsAsNull()
  79 |     {
  80 |         // A settings.json written before issue #25 doesn't have the
  81 |         // HistorySummary field. Deserialising it must not throw, and the
  82 |         // field defaults to null. This is the backward-compatibility
  83 |         // guarantee — old saves load fine, the GM just starts with no
  84 |         // summary and will create one when the history grows.
  85 |         var oldJson = """
  86 |             {
  87 |               "Id": "save_test",
  88 |               "Name": "old save",
  89 |               "OwnerId": "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000",
  90 |               "CreatedAt": "2024-01-01T00:00:00+00:00",
  91 |               "UpdatedAt": "2024-01-01T00:00:00+00:00",
  92 |               "PlaytimeMs": 0,
  93 |               "Turn": 0,
  94 |               "BuildStatus": 2,
  95 |               "EngineVersion": "0.1.0",
  96 |               "StorageVersion": 2,
  97 |               "SessionPromptTokens": 1000,
  98 |               "SessionCompletionTokens": 500
  99 |             }
 100 |             """;
 101 | 
 102 |         var back = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<SaveMeta>(oldJson, WorldJson.Options);
 103 | 
 104 |         Assert.NotNull(back);
 105 |         Assert.Null(back!.HistorySummary);
 106 |         // Other fields load as expected.
 107 |         Assert.Equal("save_test", back.Id);
 108 |         Assert.Equal(1000, back.SessionPromptTokens);
 109 |     }
 110 | 
 111 |     [Fact]
 112 |     public void SaveManager_SaveAll_LoadAll_RoundTripsHistorySummary()
 113 |     {
 114 |         // End-to-end: a SaveManager.SaveAll that persists a meta with
 115 |         // HistorySummary set, followed by a LoadAll, must return the
 116 |         // same summary text. This is the actual save/load path the
 117 |         // GameViewModel uses.
 118 |         var tempDir = TempDir();
 119 |         try
 120 |         {
 121 |             var manager = new SaveManager(savesDirectory: tempDir, registries: ContentRegistry.LoadDefault());
 122 |             var world = DefaultWorld.Create(seed: 1);
 123 |             var meta = manager.CreateSave("summary test", world);
 124 |             // Attach a summary as the GameViewModel would after a GM
 125 |             // summarization pass.
 126 |             meta = meta with
 127 |             {
 128 |                 HistorySummary = "Игрок исследовал руины, нашёл артефакт, сразился с гоблином.",
 129 |                 SessionPromptTokens = 1234,
 130 |                 SessionCompletionTokens = 567,
 131 |             };
 132 |             manager.SaveAll(meta.Id, world, meta, Array.Empty<LogEntry>());
 133 | 
 134 |             var loaded = manager.LoadAll(meta.Id);
 135 |             Assert.NotNull(loaded);
 136 |             Assert.Equal("Игрок исследовал руины, нашёл артефакт, сразился с гоблином.",
 137 |                 loaded.Value.meta.HistorySummary);
 138 |             Assert.Equal(1234, loaded.Value.meta.SessionPromptTokens);
 139 |             Assert.Equal(567,  loaded.Value.meta.SessionCompletionTokens);
 140 |         }
 141 |         finally
 142 |         {
 143 |             try { Directory.Delete(tempDir, recursive: true); } catch { }
 144 |         }
 145 |     }
 146 | 
 147 |     [Fact]
 148 |     public void SaveManager_OldSave_LoadsAsNullSummary()
 149 |     {
 150 |         // End-to-end: a meta.json written WITHOUT the HistorySummary

 152 |         // must not throw on the missing field.

 164 |                 {
 165 |                   "Id": "{{meta.Id}}",
 166 |                   "Name": "old style save",
 167 |                   "OwnerId": "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000",
 168 |                   "CreatedAt": "2024-01-01T00:00:00+00:00",
 169 |                   "UpdatedAt": "2024-01-01T00:00:00+00:00",
 170 |                   "PlaytimeMs": 0,
 171 |                   "Turn": 0,
 172 |                   "BuildStatus": 2,
 173 |                   "EngineVersion": "0.1.0",
 174 |                   "StorageVersion": 2,
 175 |                   "SessionPromptTokens": 0,
 176 |                   "SessionCompletionTokens": 0
 177 |                 }
 178 |                 """;
 179 |             File.WriteAllText(metaPath, oldStyleJson);
 180 |

 183 |             Assert.Null(loaded.Value.meta.HistorySummary);
 184 |         }
 185 |         finally
 186 |         {
 187 |             try { Directory.Delete(tempDir, recursive: true); } catch { }

 189 |     }

--- src/MyGame.Tests/Saves/SaveMigratorTests.cs (C#, 249 lines, ~2,471 tokens) ---
   1 | using MyGame.Core.Common;
   2 | using MyGame.Core.Saves;
   3 | using MyGame.Core.World;
   4 | using MyGame.Core.World.Content;
   5 | using MyGame.Core.World.Entities;
   6 | 
   7 | // 'World' is both a namespace (MyGame.Core.World) and a type
   8 | // (MyGame.Core.World.World). Alias to GameWorld to disambiguate, matching
   9 | // the convention used by SaveManager.cs.
  10 | using GameWorld = MyGame.Core.World.World;
  11 | 
  12 | namespace MyGame.Tests.Saves;
  13 | 
  14 | /// <summary>
  15 | /// Unit tests for the save-file migration pipeline. Covers the v1→v2
  16 | /// step (Item.Weight backfill from template) and the no-op fast-path
  17 | /// when the save is already at the current version.
  18 | /// </summary>
  19 | public class SaveMigratorTests
  20 | {
  21 |     /// <summary>
  22 |     /// Build a minimal world wired up with the default content registry.
  23 |     /// The migrator needs the registry to look up item templates during
  24 |     /// the v1→v2 backfill.
  25 |     /// </summary>
  26 |     private static GameWorld MakeWorld()
  27 |     {
  28 |         var registries = ContentRegistry.LoadDefault();
  29 |         var world = new GameWorld
  30 |         {
  31 |             Rng = new Rng(1),
  32 |             Registries = registries,
  33 |         };
  34 |         return world;
  35 |     }
  36 | 
  37 |     /// <summary>
  38 |     /// Make an item instance that looks like a v1 save: Weight=0 (the
  39 |     /// default before v2), with a known TemplateId. The migrator should
  40 |     /// backfill Weight from the template on load.
  41 |     /// </summary>
  42 |     private static Item MakeV1Item(string templateId, string name = "v1 item")
  43 |     {
  44 |         var item = new Item
  45 |         {
  46 |             Id = EntityId.NewId(),
  47 |             TemplateId = templateId,
  48 |             Name = name,
  49 |             Quantity = 1,
  50 |             Weight = 0, // pre-v2 default — this is what we're backfilling
  51 |         };
  52 |         return item;
  53 |     }
  54 | 
  55 |     [Fact]
  56 |     public void Migrate_v1_to_v2_BackfillsItemWeight()
  57 |     {
  58 |         var world = MakeWorld();
  59 |         // wpn_shortsword has weight 2 in data.json.
  60 |         var item = MakeV1Item("wpn_shortsword", "Короткий меч");
  61 |         world.Items.Add(item);
  62 | 
  63 |         // Sanity: before migration, Weight is 0.
  64 |         Assert.Equal(0, item.Weight);
  65 | 
  66 |         SaveMigrator.MigrateWorld(world, fromVersion: 1);
  67 | 
  68 |         // After migration, Weight should match the template's weight.
  69 |         var tpl = world.Registries.Items.Get("wpn_shortsword");
  70 |         Assert.NotNull(tpl);
  71 |         Assert.Equal(tpl!.Weight, item.Weight);
  72 |         Assert.Equal(2, item.Weight); // wpn_shortsword is 2 lb in data.json
  73 |     }
  74 | 
  75 |     [Fact]
  76 |     public void Migrate_v1_to_v2_BackfillsInventoryAndEquippedItems()
  77 |     {
  78 |         // The v1→v2 step also targets characters' carried inventory +
  79 |         // equipped slots, not just loose ground items. Build a player
  80 |         // with a v1 short sword equipped and a v1 health potion in the
  81 |         // inventory, then verify both get backfilled.
  82 |         var world = MakeWorld();
  83 | 
  84 |         var loc = new Location { Id = EntityId.NewId(), Name = "loc" };
  85 |         world.AddLocation(loc);
  86 | 
  87 |         var player = new Player
  88 |         {
  89 |             Id = EntityId.NewId(),
  90 |             Name = "Tester",
  91 |             LocationId = loc.Id,
  92 |         };
  93 |         var sword = MakeV1Item("wpn_shortsword", "Короткий меч");
  94 |         var armor = MakeV1Item("arm_leather", "Кожаный доспех");
  95 |         player.Equipped["weapon"] = sword;
  96 |         player.Equipped["armor"] = armor;
  97 |         player.Inventory.Items.Add(MakeV1Item("cns_health_potion", "Зелье лечения"));
  98 |         world.SpawnPlayer(player);
  99 | 
 100 |         SaveMigrator.MigrateWorld(world, fromVersion: 1);
 101 | 
 102 |         var swordTpl = world.Registries.Items.Get("wpn_shortsword")!;
 103 |         var armorTpl = world.Registries.Items.Get("arm_leather")!;
 104 |         var potionTpl = world.Registries.Items.Get("cns_health_potion")!;
 105 | 
 106 |         Assert.Equal(swordTpl.Weight, sword.Weight);    // 2
 107 |         Assert.Equal(armorTpl.Weight, armor.Weight);    // 10
 108 |         Assert.Equal(potionTpl.Weight,
 109 |             player.Inventory.Items[0].Weight);          // 0.5
 110 |     }
 111 | 
 112 |     [Fact]
 113 |     public void Migrate_v1_to_v2_UnknownTemplate_LeavesZero()
 114 |     {
 115 |         // An item with a TemplateId that doesn't exist in the registry
 116 |         // can't be backfilled — the migrator leaves Weight at 0 (the
 117 |         // pre-v2 default) rather than throwing or guessing.
 118 |         var world = MakeWorld();
 119 |         var item = MakeV1Item("this_template_does_not_exist", "мусор");
 120 |         world.Items.Add(item);
 121 | 
 122 |         SaveMigrator.MigrateWorld(world, fromVersion: 1);
 123 | 
 124 |         Assert.Equal(0, item.Weight);
 125 |     }
 126 | 
 127 |     [Fact]
 128 |     public void Migrate_v1_to_v2_NoTemplateId_LeavesZero()
 129 |     {
 130 |         // Hand-spawned items (no TemplateId) can't be backfilled either.
 131 |         var world = MakeWorld();
 132 |         var item = new Item
 133 |         {
 134 |             Id = EntityId.NewId(),
 135 |             TemplateId = null,
 136 |             Name = "Безымянный предмет",
 137 |             Weight = 0,
 138 |         };
 139 |         world.Items.Add(item);
 140 | 
 141 |         SaveMigrator.MigrateWorld(world, fromVersion: 1);
 142 | 
 143 |         Assert.Equal(0, item.Weight);
 144 |     }
 145 | 
 146 |     [Fact]
 147 |     public void Migrate_AlreadyCurrent_NoOp()
 148 |     {
 149 |         // A world already at the current version should be returned
 150 |         // unchanged. The migrator must not re-touch items that already
 151 |         // have a non-zero Weight (idempotence).
 152 |         var world = MakeWorld();
 153 |         var tpl = world.Registries.Items.Get("wpn_shortsword")!;
 154 |         var item = new Item
 155 |         {
 156 |             Id = EntityId.NewId(),
 157 |             TemplateId = "wpn_shortsword",
 158 |             Name = "Уже обновлённый меч",
 159 |             Weight = tpl.Weight, // already at template weight (v2 state)
 160 |         };
 161 |         world.Items.Add(item);
 162 |         var weightBefore = item.Weight;
 163 | 
 164 |         SaveMigrator.MigrateWorld(world, fromVersion: SaveMigrator.CurrentStorageVersion);
 165 | 
 166 |         Assert.Equal(weightBefore, item.Weight); // unchanged
 167 |     }
 168 | 
 169 |     [Fact]
 170 |     public void Migrate_FromOlderThanCurrent_RunsAllSteps()
 171 |     {
 172 |         // fromVersion=1 with CurrentStorageVersion=2 runs exactly one
 173 |         // step (v1→v2). The loop bound is CurrentStorageVersion, so a
 174 |         // future v3 migration would extend this — for now we just
 175 |         // verify the v1→v2 step ran (Weight backfilled) and the world
 176 |         // is returned (not null).
 177 |         var world = MakeWorld();
 178 |         var item = MakeV1Item("wpn_club", "Дубина");
 179 |         world.Items.Add(item);
 180 | 
 181 |         var returned = SaveMigrator.MigrateWorld(world, fromVersion: 1);
 182 | 
 183 |         Assert.Same(world, returned); // mutated in place AND returned
 184 |         var tpl = world.Registries.Items.Get("wpn_club")!;
 185 |         Assert.Equal(tpl.Weight, item.Weight); // backfilled
 186 |     }
 187 | 
 188 |     [Fact]
 189 |     public void MigrateMeta_BumpsStorageVersionAndEngineVersion()
 190 |     {
 191 |         // A v1 meta with an older EngineVersion should be bumped to the
 192 |         // current StorageVersion + EngineVersion by MigrateMeta.
 193 |         var oldMeta = new SaveMeta
 194 |         {
 195 |             Id = "save_test",
 196 |             Name = "old",
 197 |             StorageVersion = 1,
 198 |             EngineVersion = "0.1.0", // older than Core.Common.Version.Current
 199 |         };
 200 | 
 201 |         var migrated = SaveMigrator.MigrateMeta(oldMeta, fromVersion: 1);
 202 | 
 203 |         Assert.Equal(SaveMigrator.CurrentStorageVersion, migrated.StorageVersion);
 204 |         Assert.Equal(MyGame.Core.Common.Version.Current, migrated.EngineVersion);
 205 |     }
 206 | 
 207 |     [Fact]
 208 |     public void MigrateMeta_AlreadyCurrent_StillIdempotent()
 209 |     {
 210 |         // A meta already at the current version should remain at the
 211 |         // current version (the bump is a no-op; the EngineVersion
 212 |         // refresh is also idempotent).
 213 |         var current = new SaveMeta
 214 |         {
 215 |             Id = "save_test",
 216 |             Name = "current",
 217 |             StorageVersion = SaveMigrator.CurrentStorageVersion,
 218 |             EngineVersion = MyGame.Core.Common.Version.Current,
 219 |         };
 220 | 
 221 |         var migrated = SaveMigrator.MigrateMeta(current, fromVersion: SaveMigrator.CurrentStorageVersion);
 222 | 
 223 |         Assert.Equal(SaveMigrator.CurrentStorageVersion, migrated.StorageVersion);
 224 |         Assert.Equal(MyGame.Core.Common.Version.Current, migrated.EngineVersion);
 225 |     }
 226 | 
 227 |     [Fact]
 228 |     public void MigrateWorld_NullWorld_Throws()
 229 |     {
 230 |         Assert.Throws<ArgumentNullException>(() =>
 231 |             SaveMigrator.MigrateWorld(null!, fromVersion: 1));
 232 |     }
 233 | 
 234 |     [Fact]
 235 |     public void MigrateMeta_NullMeta_Throws()
 236 |     {
 237 |         Assert.Throws<ArgumentNullException>(() =>
 238 |             SaveMigrator.MigrateMeta(null!, fromVersion: 1));
 239 |     }
 240 | 
 241 |     [Fact]
 242 |     public void CurrentStorageVersion_IsAtLeast2()
 243 |     {
 244 |         // The Item.Weight backfill (v1→v2) is the trigger for bumping
 245 |         // the version. This test guards against accidental rollback.
 246 |         Assert.True(SaveMigrator.CurrentStorageVersion >= 2);
 247 |     }
 248 | }

--- src/MyGame.Tests/Saves/SaveSchemaMigrationTests.cs (C#, 161 lines, ~1,734 tokens) ---
   1 | using System.IO;
   2 | using System.Text.Json;
   3 | using MyGame.Core.Common;
   4 | using MyGame.Core.Saves;
   5 | using MyGame.Core.World;
   6 | using MyGame.Core.World.Content;
   7 | using MyGame.Core.World.Entities;
   8 | using Xunit;
   9 | 
  10 | using GameWorld = MyGame.Core.World.World;
  11 | 
  12 | namespace MyGame.Tests.Saves;
  13 | 
  14 | /// <summary>
  15 | /// Tests that saves from older schema versions migrate correctly to the
  16 | /// current version. Uses in-memory fixture saves (no on-disk fixture
  17 | /// files needed — we construct old-format saves programmatically).
  18 | /// Issue #72.
  19 | /// </summary>
  20 | public class SaveSchemaMigrationTests
  21 | {
  22 |     private static string MakeTempDir()
  23 |     {
  24 |         var dir = Path.Combine(Path.GetTempPath(), "PathstoneMigrationTests_" + Guid.NewGuid().ToString("N"));
  25 |         Directory.CreateDirectory(dir);
  26 |         return dir;
  27 |     }
  28 | 
  29 |     [Fact]
  30 |     public void Migrate_v1_Save_Loads_On_Current_Version()
  31 |     {
  32 |         var dir = MakeTempDir();
  33 |         try
  34 |         {
  35 |             var saveId = SaveManager.NewSaveId();
  36 |             var saveDir = Path.Combine(dir, saveId);
  37 |             Directory.CreateDirectory(saveDir);
  38 | 
  39 |             // Construct a v1 SaveMeta (StorageVersion=1, no Item.Weight field).
  40 |             var oldMeta = new SaveMeta
  41 |             {
  42 |                 Id = saveId,
  43 |                 Name = "Migration Test World",
  44 |                 OwnerId = Guid.NewGuid(),
  45 |                 StorageVersion = 1, // old version
  46 |                 EngineVersion = "0.1.0",
  47 |                 CreatedAt = DateTimeOffset.UtcNow,
  48 |                 UpdatedAt = DateTimeOffset.UtcNow,
  49 |             };
  50 | 
  51 |             // Construct a v1 World (items without Weight — simulates old save).
  52 |             var registries = ContentRegistry.LoadDefault();
  53 |             var world = new GameWorld { Seed = 42, Rng = new Rng(42), Registries = registries };
  54 |             var loc = new Location { Id = EntityId.NewId(), Name = "Test Loc", Terrain = "plains" };
  55 |             world.AddLocation(loc);
  56 | 
  57 |             // Add an item WITHOUT Weight (simulates old save format).
  58 |             var swordTpl = registries.Items.Get("wpn_shortsword")!;
  59 |             var oldItem = new Item { Id = EntityId.NewId(), Name = swordTpl.Name, TemplateId = swordTpl.Id, Weight = 0 };
  60 |             world.Items.Add(oldItem);
  61 |             loc.Items.Add(oldItem.Id);
  62 | 
  63 |             // Write the save in v1 format.
  64 |             File.WriteAllText(Path.Combine(saveDir, "meta.json"),
  65 |                 JsonSerializer.Serialize(oldMeta, WorldJson.Options));
  66 |             File.WriteAllText(Path.Combine(saveDir, "world.json"), world.ToJson());
  67 |             File.WriteAllText(Path.Combine(saveDir, "log.json"), "[]");
  68 |             File.WriteAllText(Path.Combine(saveDir, "state.json"),
  69 |                 JsonSerializer.Serialize(new { turn = 0, activePlayerId = (string?)null }, WorldJson.Options));
  70 | 
  71 |             // Load via SaveManager — should auto-migrate.
  72 |             var sm = new SaveManager(dir) { CompressSaves = false };
  73 |             var loaded = sm.LoadAll(saveId);
  74 | 
  75 |             Assert.NotNull(loaded);
  76 |             Assert.Equal(SaveMigrator.CurrentStorageVersion, loaded.Value.meta.StorageVersion);
  77 | 
  78 |             // The item's Weight should be backfilled from the template.
  79 |             var migratedItem = loaded.Value.world.Items.FirstOrDefault(i => i.TemplateId == "wpn_shortsword");
  80 |             Assert.NotNull(migratedItem);
  81 |             Assert.Equal(swordTpl.Weight, migratedItem!.Weight);
  82 |         }
  83 |         finally
  84 |         {
  85 |             if (Directory.Exists(dir)) Directory.Delete(dir, true);
  86 |         }
  87 |     }
  88 | 
  89 |     [Fact]
  90 |     public void Current_Version_Save_Does_Not_Migrate()
  91 |     {
  92 |         var dir = MakeTempDir();
  93 |         try
  94 |         {
  95 |             var sm = new SaveManager(dir) { CompressSaves = false };
  96 |             var registries = ContentRegistry.LoadDefault();
  97 |             var world = DefaultWorld.Create(seed: 99);
  98 |             world.Registries = registries;
  99 |             var meta = sm.CreateSave("No Migration Test", world, Guid.NewGuid());
 100 | 
 101 |             // Load — should be current version already.
 102 |             var loaded = sm.LoadAll(meta.Id);
 103 |             Assert.NotNull(loaded);
 104 |             Assert.Equal(SaveMigrator.CurrentStorageVersion, loaded.Value.meta.StorageVersion);
 105 |         }
 106 |         finally
 107 |         {
 108 |             if (Directory.Exists(dir)) Directory.Delete(dir, true);
 109 |         }
 110 |     }
 111 | 
 112 |     [Fact]
 113 |     public void Migration_Preserves_World_State()
 114 |     {
 115 |         var dir = MakeTempDir();
 116 |         try
 117 |         {
 118 |             var saveId = SaveManager.NewSaveId();
 119 |             var saveDir = Path.Combine(dir, saveId);
 120 |             Directory.CreateDirectory(saveDir);
 121 | 
 122 |             var registries = ContentRegistry.LoadDefault();
 123 |             var world = DefaultWorld.Create(seed: 7);
 124 |             world.Registries = registries;
 125 |             world.Turn = 42;
 126 |             world.Clock = new GameTime(5, 14, 30);
 127 | 
 128 |             var oldMeta = new SaveMeta
 129 |             {
 130 |                 Id = saveId,
 131 |                 Name = "Preserve Test",
 132 |                 OwnerId = Guid.NewGuid(),
 133 |                 StorageVersion = 1,
 134 |                 EngineVersion = "0.1.0",
 135 |                 CreatedAt = DateTimeOffset.UtcNow,
 136 |                 UpdatedAt = DateTimeOffset.UtcNow,
 137 |                 Turn = 42,
 138 |             };
 139 | 
 140 |             File.WriteAllText(Path.Combine(saveDir, "meta.json"),
 141 |                 JsonSerializer.Serialize(oldMeta, WorldJson.Options));
 142 |             File.WriteAllText(Path.Combine(saveDir, "world.json"), world.ToJson());
 143 |             File.WriteAllText(Path.Combine(saveDir, "log.json"), "[]");
 144 |             File.WriteAllText(Path.Combine(saveDir, "state.json"),
 145 |                 JsonSerializer.Serialize(new { turn = 42 }, WorldJson.Options));
 146 | 
 147 |             var sm = new SaveManager(dir) { CompressSaves = false };
 148 |             var loaded = sm.LoadAll(saveId);
 149 | 
 150 |             Assert.NotNull(loaded);
 151 |             Assert.Equal(42, loaded.Value.world.Turn);
 152 |             Assert.Equal(5, loaded.Value.world.Clock.Day);
 153 |             Assert.Equal(14, loaded.Value.world.Clock.Hour);
 154 |         }
 155 |         finally
 156 |         {
 157 |             if (Directory.Exists(dir)) Directory.Delete(dir, true);
 158 |         }
 159 |     }
 160 | }

--- src/MyGame.Tests/Saves/SaveSharingTests.cs (C#, 469 lines, ~5,448 tokens) ---
   1 | using System.IO;
   2 | using System.IO.Compression;
   3 | using System.Text;
   4 | using System.Text.Json;
   5 | using MyGame.Core.Common;
   6 | using MyGame.Core.Saves;
   7 | using MyGame.Core.World;
   8 | using MyGame.Core.World.Content;
   9 | 
  10 | // 'World' is both a namespace (MyGame.Core.World) and a type
  11 | // (MyGame.Core.World.World). Alias to GameWorld to disambiguate, matching
  12 | // the convention used by SaveManager.cs.
  13 | using GameWorld = MyGame.Core.World.World;
  14 | 
  15 | namespace MyGame.Tests.Saves;
  16 | 
  17 | /// <summary>
  18 | /// Unit tests for the save sharing system (issue #33 —
  19 | /// <c>.pathstone-world</c> export/import). Covers:
  20 | /// <list type="bullet">
  21 | ///   <item>ExportSave creates a valid zip with a manifest.json.</item>
  22 | ///   <item>ImportSave extracts the zip and creates a new saveId.</item>
  23 | ///   <item>Export → Import round-trip: world data matches.</item>
  24 | ///   <item>Importing the same archive twice produces two saves (no collision).</item>
  25 | ///   <item>Export preserves compression (world.json.gz stays .gz in the zip).</item>
  26 | ///   <item>Export works even when the save has no manifest (re-export of imported save).</item>
  27 | /// </list>
  28 | /// </summary>
  29 | public class SaveSharingTests : IDisposable
  30 | {
  31 |     private readonly string _tempDir;
  32 |     private readonly SaveManager _manager;
  33 |     private readonly ContentRegistry _registries;
  34 | 
  35 |     public SaveSharingTests()
  36 |     {
  37 |         _tempDir = Path.Combine(Path.GetTempPath(), "MyGameShareTests_" + Guid.NewGuid().ToString("N"));
  38 |         Directory.CreateDirectory(_tempDir);
  39 |         _registries = ContentRegistry.LoadDefault();
  40 |         _manager = new SaveManager(savesDirectory: _tempDir, registries: _registries);
  41 |     }
  42 | 
  43 |     public void Dispose()
  44 |     {
  45 |         try { Directory.Delete(_tempDir, recursive: true); }
  46 |         catch { /* best-effort */ }
  47 |     }
  48 | 
  49 |     private static GameWorld MakeWorld() => DefaultWorld.Create(seed: 42);
  50 | 
  51 |     /// <summary>
  52 |     /// Open a .pathstone-world archive as a zip and return the list of
  53 |     /// entry names (for assertions about archive contents).
  54 |     /// </summary>
  55 |     private static List<string> ListZipEntries(string archivePath)
  56 |     {
  57 |         using var fs = File.OpenRead(archivePath);
  58 |         using var zip = new ZipArchive(fs, ZipArchiveMode.Read);
  59 |         return zip.Entries.Select(e => e.FullName).ToList();
  60 |     }
  61 | 
  62 |     /// <summary>
  63 |     /// Read a single text entry from a zip archive (UTF-8). Returns null
  64 |     /// if the entry doesn't exist.
  65 |     /// </summary>
  66 |     private static string? ReadZipEntry(string archivePath, string entryName)
  67 |     {
  68 |         using var fs = File.OpenRead(archivePath);
  69 |         using var zip = new ZipArchive(fs, ZipArchiveMode.Read);
  70 |         var entry = zip.GetEntry(entryName);
  71 |         if (entry is null) return null;
  72 |         using var es = entry.Open();
  73 |         using var reader = new StreamReader(es, Encoding.UTF8);
  74 |         return reader.ReadToEnd();
  75 |     }
  76 | 
  77 |     // ─── Export ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  78 | 
  79 |     [Fact]
  80 |     public void Export_CreatesValidZipWithManifest()
  81 |     {
  82 |         var world = MakeWorld();
  83 |         world.Turn = 7;
  84 |         var meta = _manager.CreateSave("Exportable", world, ownerId: Guid.NewGuid());
  85 | 
  86 |         var exportPath = Path.Combine(_tempDir, "export1.pathstone-world");
  87 |         var ok = _manager.ExportSave(meta.Id, exportPath);
  88 |         Assert.True(ok);
  89 |         Assert.True(File.Exists(exportPath));
  90 | 
  91 |         // The zip should contain manifest.json + the 4 save files.
  92 |         var entries = ListZipEntries(exportPath);
  93 |         Assert.Contains("manifest.json", entries);
  94 |         Assert.Contains("meta.json", entries);
  95 |         Assert.Contains("world.json", entries);
  96 |         Assert.Contains("log.json", entries);
  97 |         Assert.Contains("state.json", entries);
  98 | 
  99 |         // The manifest should have the expected fields.
 100 |         var manifestJson = ReadZipEntry(exportPath, "manifest.json");
 101 |         Assert.NotNull(manifestJson);
 102 |         var manifest = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<SaveManifest>(manifestJson!, WorldJson.Options);
 103 |         Assert.NotNull(manifest);
 104 |         Assert.Equal("Exportable", manifest!.SaveName);
 105 |         Assert.Equal(meta.OwnerId, manifest.OwnerProfileId);
 106 |         Assert.Equal(MyGame.Core.Common.Version.Current, manifest.EngineVersion);
 107 |         Assert.True(manifest.ExportTimestamp <= DateTimeOffset.UtcNow);
 108 |         Assert.True(manifest.ExportTimestamp > DateTimeOffset.UtcNow.AddMinutes(-1));
 109 |     }
 110 | 
 111 |     [Fact]
 112 |     public void Export_PreservesCompressionWhenEnabled()
 113 |     {
 114 |         // When the save is compressed, the zip should contain
 115 |         // world.json.gz (not world.json). The exporter copies files
 116 |         // verbatim — it doesn't re-compress or decompress.
 117 |         _manager.CompressSaves = true;
 118 |         var world = MakeWorld();
 119 |         var meta = _manager.CreateSave("Compressed", world);
 120 | 
 121 |         var exportPath = Path.Combine(_tempDir, "export2.pathstone-world");
 122 |         Assert.True(_manager.ExportSave(meta.Id, exportPath));
 123 | 
 124 |         var entries = ListZipEntries(exportPath);
 125 |         Assert.Contains("world.json.gz", entries);
 126 |         Assert.DoesNotContain("world.json", entries);
 127 |         // meta.json stays uncompressed even with compression on.
 128 |         Assert.Contains("meta.json", entries);
 129 |     }
 130 | 
 131 |     [Fact]
 132 |     public void Export_NonexistentSave_ReturnsFalse()
 133 |     {
 134 |         var exportPath = Path.Combine(_tempDir, "nope.pathstone-world");
 135 |         Assert.False(_manager.ExportSave("save_" + new string('a', 32), exportPath));
 136 |         Assert.False(File.Exists(exportPath));
 137 |     }
 138 | 
 139 |     [Fact]
 140 |     public void Export_InvalidSaveId_ReturnsFalse()
 141 |     {
 142 |         var exportPath = Path.Combine(_tempDir, "nope2.pathstone-world");
 143 |         Assert.False(_manager.ExportSave("not_a_real_save_id", exportPath));
 144 |     }
 145 | 
 146 |     [Fact]
 147 |     public void Export_AtomicWrite_DoesNotLeavePartialFileOnFailure()
 148 |     {
 149 |         // Exporting to a path whose directory doesn't exist should
 150 |         // fail cleanly without leaving a .pathstone-world file (it may
 151 |         // leave a .tmp file, but the final file should not exist).
 152 |         var world = MakeWorld();
 153 |         var meta = _manager.CreateSave("Atomic", world);
 154 | 
 155 |         var badPath = Path.Combine(_tempDir, "nonexistent_subdir", "out.pathstone-world");
 156 |         Assert.False(_manager.ExportSave(meta.Id, badPath));
 157 |         Assert.False(File.Exists(badPath));
 158 |     }
 159 | 
 160 |     // ─── Import ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 161 | 
 162 |     [Fact]
 163 |     public void Import_ExtractsArchiveAndCreatesNewSave()
 164 |     {
 165 |         var world = MakeWorld();
 166 |         world.Turn = 42;
 167 |         var originalMeta = _manager.CreateSave("Original", world);
 168 | 
 169 |         var exportPath = Path.Combine(_tempDir, "imp1.pathstone-world");
 170 |         Assert.True(_manager.ExportSave(originalMeta.Id, exportPath));
 171 | 
 172 |         var newSaveId = _manager.ImportSave(exportPath);
 173 |         Assert.NotNull(newSaveId);
 174 |         Assert.NotEqual(originalMeta.Id, newSaveId);
 175 |         Assert.StartsWith("save_", newSaveId);
 176 | 
 177 |         // The imported save should appear in ListSaves.
 178 |         var saves = _manager.ListSaves();
 179 |         Assert.Contains(saves, s => s.Id == newSaveId);
 180 | 
 181 |         // The imported save's meta should have the new saveId (not the
 182 |         // original) — the importer rewrites the Id to match the new
 183 |         // save directory.
 184 |         var importedMeta = _manager.LoadMeta(newSaveId!);
 185 |         Assert.NotNull(importedMeta);
 186 |         Assert.Equal(newSaveId, importedMeta!.Id);
 187 |         Assert.Equal("Original", importedMeta.Name);
 188 | 
 189 |         // The manifest sidecar should exist in the save dir with the
 190 |         // import timestamp set.
 191 |         var manifestPath = Path.Combine(_tempDir, newSaveId!, "manifest.json");
 192 |         Assert.True(File.Exists(manifestPath));
 193 |         var manifestJson = File.ReadAllText(manifestPath);
 194 |         var manifest = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<SaveManifest>(manifestJson, WorldJson.Options);
 195 |         Assert.NotNull(manifest);
 196 |         Assert.NotNull(manifest!.ImportTimestamp);
 197 |     }
 198 | 
 199 |     [Fact]
 200 |     public void Import_SameArchiveTwice_CreatesTwoSeparateSaves()
 201 |     {
 202 |         // The whole point of generating a new saveId on import is so
 203 |         // importing the same archive twice doesn't collide. Both
 204 |         // imports should succeed and produce distinct saveIds.
 205 |         var world = MakeWorld();
 206 |         var meta = _manager.CreateSave("Shared", world);
 207 | 
 208 |         var exportPath = Path.Combine(_tempDir, "imp2.pathstone-world");
 209 |         _manager.ExportSave(meta.Id, exportPath);
 210 | 
 211 |         var id1 = _manager.ImportSave(exportPath);
 212 |         var id2 = _manager.ImportSave(exportPath);
 213 | 
 214 |         Assert.NotNull(id1);
 215 |         Assert.NotNull(id2);
 216 |         Assert.NotEqual(id1, id2);
 217 | 
 218 |         var saves = _manager.ListSaves();
 219 |         Assert.Contains(saves, s => s.Id == id1);
 220 |         Assert.Contains(saves, s => s.Id == id2);
 221 |     }
 222 | 
 223 |     // ─── Round-trip ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
 224 | 
 225 |     [Fact]
 226 |     public void ExportImportRoundTrip_WorldDataMatches()
 227 |     {
 228 |         // The canonical test: build a world, save it, export it, import
 229 |         // it as a new save, load the imported save, and verify the
 230 |         // world matches the original.
 231 |         var world = MakeWorld();
 232 |         // Mutate to make the comparison non-trivial.
 233 |         world.Turn = 77;
 234 |         world.Items.Add(new()
 235 |         {
 236 |             Id = EntityId.NewId(),
 237 |             Name = "shared-item",
 238 |         });
 239 |         var meta = _manager.CreateSave("RoundTrip", world);
 240 | 
 241 |         var exportPath = Path.Combine(_tempDir, "rt.pathstone-world");
 242 |         Assert.True(_manager.ExportSave(meta.Id, exportPath));
 243 | 
 244 |         var importedId = _manager.ImportSave(exportPath);
 245 |         Assert.NotNull(importedId);
 246 | 
 247 |         var loaded = _manager.LoadAll(importedId!);
 248 |         Assert.NotNull(loaded);
 249 | 
 250 |         Assert.Equal(world.Turn, loaded!.Value.world.Turn);
 251 |         Assert.Equal(world.Players.Count, loaded.Value.world.Players.Count);
 252 |         Assert.Equal(world.Npcs.Count, loaded.Value.world.Npcs.Count);
 253 |         Assert.Equal(world.Locations.Count, loaded.Value.world.Locations.Count);
 254 |         Assert.Equal(world.Items.Count, loaded.Value.world.Items.Count);
 255 |         Assert.Contains(loaded.Value.world.Items, i => i.Name == "shared-item");
 256 | 
 257 |         // RNG state should also round-trip (continuing the stream on
 258 |         // the imported save should produce the same outputs as the
 259 |         // original).
 260 |         for (int i = 0; i < 5; i++)
 261 |             Assert.Equal(world.Rng.Next(), loaded.Value.world.Rng.Next());
 262 |     }
 263 | 
 264 |     [Fact]
 265 |     public void ExportImportRoundTrip_Works_WithCompression()
 266 |     {
 267 |         // Compression should be transparent to export/import — the
 268 |         // compressed save's world.json.gz is copied verbatim into the
 269 |         // zip, extracted verbatim on import, and the loaded world
 270 |         // matches.
 271 |         _manager.CompressSaves = true;
 272 |         var world = MakeWorld();
 273 |         world.Turn = 99;
 274 |         var meta = _manager.CreateSave("CRT", world);
 275 | 
 276 |         var exportPath = Path.Combine(_tempDir, "crt.pathstone-world");
 277 |         Assert.True(_manager.ExportSave(meta.Id, exportPath));
 278 | 
 279 |         // The archive should contain world.json.gz (compression
 280 |         // preserved). On import, the .gz is extracted verbatim — the
 281 |         // SaveManager's reader auto-detects it.
 282 |         var entries = ListZipEntries(exportPath);
 283 |         Assert.Contains("world.json.gz", entries);
 284 | 
 285 |         var importedId = _manager.ImportSave(exportPath);
 286 |         Assert.NotNull(importedId);
 287 | 
 288 |         var loaded = _manager.LoadAll(importedId!);
 289 |         Assert.NotNull(loaded);
 290 |         Assert.Equal(99, loaded!.Value.world.Turn);
 291 |     }
 292 | 
 293 |     // ─── Edge cases ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
 294 | 
 295 |     [Fact]
 296 |     public void Import_NonexistentFile_ReturnsNull()
 297 |     {
 298 |         Assert.Null(_manager.ImportSave(Path.Combine(_tempDir, "does_not_exist.pathstone-world")));
 299 |     }

 301 |     [Fact]
 302 |     public void Import_EmptyPath_ReturnsNull()
 303 |     {
 304 |         Assert.Null(_manager.ImportSave(""));
 305 |         Assert.Null(_manager.ImportSave(null!));
 306 |     }
 307 | 
 308 |     [Fact]
 309 |     public void Import_DoesNotImportManifestAsMetaFile()
 310 |     {
 311 |         // The importer writes manifest.json to the save dir as a
 312 |         // sidecar. ListSaves should NOT confuse it for a second save
 313 |         // (it scans immediate subdirs of saves/ for meta.json, and the
 314 |         // save dir's manifest.json is INSIDE the save dir — not a
 315 |         // sibling). Verify: after import, the save count is exactly 1
 316 |         // (the imported save), not 2.
 317 |         var world = MakeWorld();
 318 |         var meta = _manager.CreateSave("ToShare", world);
 319 |         var exportPath = Path.Combine(_tempDir, "edge.pathstone-world");
 320 |         _manager.ExportSave(meta.Id, exportPath);
 321 | 
 322 |         // Fresh manager in a fresh dir so we can count saves cleanly.
 323 |         var freshDir = Path.Combine(Path.GetTempPath(), "MyGameShareFresh_" + Guid.NewGuid().ToString("N"));
 324 |         Directory.CreateDirectory(freshDir);
 325 |         try
 326 |         {
 327 |             var freshManager = new SaveManager(savesDirectory: freshDir, registries: _registries);
 328 |             var importedId = freshManager.ImportSave(exportPath);
 329 |             Assert.NotNull(importedId);
 330 | 
 331 |             var saves = freshManager.ListSaves();
 332 |             Assert.Single(saves);
 333 |             Assert.Equal(importedId, saves[0].Id);
 334 |         }
 335 |         finally
 336 |         {
 337 |             try { Directory.Delete(freshDir, recursive: true); } catch { }
 338 |         }
 339 |     }
 340 | 
 341 |     [Fact]
 342 |     public void Import_RejectsZipSlipEntries()
 343 |     {
 344 |         // Construct a malicious zip that contains an entry with a
 345 |         // path-traversal name (../../etc/passwd). The importer should
 346 |         // sanitize the name and either skip the entry or extract it
 347 |         // with a flat name — but in NO case should it write outside
 348 |         // the save dir.
 349 |         var maliciousZipPath = Path.Combine(_tempDir, "malicious.pathstone-world");
 350 |         using (var fs = File.Create(maliciousZipPath))
 351 |         using (var zip = new ZipArchive(fs, ZipArchiveMode.Create))
 352 |         {
 353 |             // A legitimate meta.json so the imported save has a valid meta.
 354 |             var meta = new SaveMeta
 355 |             {
 356 |                 Id = "save_" + new string('a', 32),
 357 |                 Name = "Malicious",
 358 |             };
 359 |             var metaJson = JsonSerializer.Serialize(meta, WorldJson.Options);
 360 |             var metaEntry = zip.CreateEntry("meta.json");
 361 |             using (var es = metaEntry.Open())
 362 |             {
 363 |                 var bytes = Encoding.UTF8.GetBytes(metaJson);
 364 |                 es.Write(bytes, 0, bytes.Length);
 365 |             }
 366 | 
 367 |             // A malicious entry that tries to escape via "..". Note:
 368 |             // entry.Name strips directory components on most platforms,
 369 |             // so we put the traversal in FullName and use a flat name
 370 |             // via CreateEntry(name) — but ZipArchiveEntry.Name is the
 371 |             // part after the last separator, so a "../escape.txt" name
 372 |             // would have Name "escape.txt" (sanitized to flat). Test
 373 |             // both shapes.
 374 |             var bad1 = zip.CreateEntry("../escape.txt");
 375 |             using (var es = bad1.Open())
 376 |             {
 377 |                 var bytes = Encoding.UTF8.GetBytes("should not escape");
 378 |                 es.Write(bytes, 0, bytes.Length);
 379 |             }
 380 |         }
 381 | 
 382 |         var importedId = _manager.ImportSave(maliciousZipPath);
 383 |         Assert.NotNull(importedId);
 384 | 
 385 |         // Verify no "escape.txt" was written OUTSIDE the save dir.
 386 |         var escapePath = Path.Combine(_tempDir, "escape.txt");
 387 |         Assert.False(File.Exists(escapePath),
 388 |             "Importer should not write files outside the save dir (zip-slip)");
 389 | 
 390 |         // The save dir should have meta.json (legit) and possibly an
 391 |         // escape.txt INSIDE the save dir (sanitized flat name). The
 392 |         // import succeeded; the malicious entry was sanitized.
 393 |         var saveDir = Path.Combine(_tempDir, importedId!);
 394 |         Assert.True(File.Exists(Path.Combine(saveDir, "meta.json")));
 395 |     }
 396 | 
 397 |     [Fact]
 398 |     public void Import_ArchiveWithoutManifest_StillImports()
 399 |     {
 400 |         // An archive without a manifest.json (e.g. hand-zipped save
 401 |         // dir) should still import — the manifest is optional. The
 402 |         // importer just won't have export metadata.
 403 |         var world = MakeWorld();
 404 |         var meta = _manager.CreateSave("NoManifest", world);
 405 | 
 406 |         var exportPath = Path.Combine(_tempDir, "nomanifest.pathstone-world");
 407 |         // Build the archive manually without a manifest entry.
 408 |         using (var fs = File.Create(exportPath))
 409 |         using (var zip = new ZipArchive(fs, ZipArchiveMode.Create))
 410 |         {
 411 |             var saveDir = Path.Combine(_tempDir, meta.Id);
 412 |             foreach (var file in Directory.EnumerateFiles(saveDir, "*", SearchOption.TopDirectoryOnly))
 413 |             {
 414 |                 var name = Path.GetFileName(file);
 415 |                 if (name.EndsWith(".tmp", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)) continue;
 416 |                 var entry = zip.CreateEntry(name);
 417 |                 using var es = entry.Open();
 418 |                 using var src = File.OpenRead(file);
 419 |                 src.CopyTo(es);
 420 |             }
 421 |         }
 422 | 
 423 |         var importedId = _manager.ImportSave(exportPath);
 424 |         Assert.NotNull(importedId);
 425 | 
 426 |         var loaded = _manager.LoadAll(importedId!);
 427 |         Assert.NotNull(loaded);
 428 |         Assert.Equal("NoManifest", loaded!.Value.meta.Name);
 429 | 
 430 |         // The importer should write a manifest.json sidecar (with
 431 |         // ImportTimestamp set) even when the archive had no manifest —
 432 |         // the importer synthesizes one.
 433 |         var manifestPath = Path.Combine(_tempDir, importedId!, "manifest.json");
 434 |         Assert.True(File.Exists(manifestPath));
 435 |     }
 436 | 
 437 |     // ─── Re-export of an imported save ────────────────────────────────
 438 | 
 439 |     [Fact]
 440 |     public void ReExport_ImportedSave_ProducesFreshExportManifest()
 441 |     {
 442 |         // Importing a save writes a manifest.json sidecar to the save
 443 |         // dir (with ImportTimestamp). Re-exporting that save should
 444 |         // produce a fresh EXPORT manifest (NOT copy the save dir's
 445 |         // import manifest) — the exporter synthesizes a new manifest
 446 |         // from the save's current meta.
 447 |         var world = MakeWorld();
 448 |         var meta = _manager.CreateSave("ReExport", world);
 449 |         var exportPath1 = Path.Combine(_tempDir, "re1.pathstone-world");
 450 |         _manager.ExportSave(meta.Id, exportPath1);
 451 | 
 452 |         var importedId = _manager.ImportSave(exportPath1);
 453 |         Assert.NotNull(importedId);
 454 | 
 455 |         // Re-export the imported save.
 456 |         var exportPath2 = Path.Combine(_tempDir, "re2.pathstone-world");
 457 |         Assert.True(_manager.ExportSave(importedId!, exportPath2));
 458 | 
 459 |         // The re-export's manifest should be a fresh EXPORT manifest
 460 |         // (ImportTimestamp null, ExportTimestamp set to now-ish).
 461 |         var manifestJson = ReadZipEntry(exportPath2, "manifest.json");
 462 |         Assert.NotNull(manifestJson);
 463 |         var manifest = JsonSerializer.Deserialize<SaveManifest>(manifestJson!, WorldJson.Options);
 464 |         Assert.NotNull(manifest);
 465 |         Assert.Null(manifest!.ImportTimestamp);
 466 |         Assert.True(manifest.ExportTimestamp > DateTimeOffset.UtcNow.AddMinutes(-1));
 467 |     }
 468 | }

   6 | /// <summary>

 122 |         {

 124 |         };

 126 |         {
 127 |             Id = "x", Name = "Second", Description = "", Category = "misc", Rarity = "common",
 128 |         };
 129 |         reg.Items.Register(tpl1);
 130 |         reg.Items.Register(tpl2);
 131 |         var got = reg.Items.Get("x");

--- src/MyGame.Tests/World/WorldTests.cs (C#, 259 lines, ~2,800 tokens) ---
   1 | using System.Text.Json;
   2 | using MyGame.Core.Common;
   3 | using MyGame.Core.World;
   4 | using MyGame.Core.World.Content;
   5 | using MyGame.Core.World.Entities;
   6 | 
   7 | // 'World' is both a namespace (MyGame.Core.World) and a type
   8 | // (MyGame.Core.World.World). The test file lives in MyGame.Tests.World,
   9 | // so a bare `World` resolves to the test namespace. Alias the type to
  10 | // GameWorld (matching the convention used by SaveManager.cs).
  11 | using GameWorld = MyGame.Core.World.World;
  12 | 
  13 | namespace MyGame.Tests.World;
  14 | 
  15 | /// <summary>
  16 | /// Unit tests for the World aggregate root. Covers ToJson/FromJson
  17 | /// round-tripping, RNG state preservation across save/load, player
  18 | /// spawning, and FindEntity lookups across collections.
  19 | /// </summary>
  20 | public class WorldTests
  21 | {
  22 |     [Fact]
  23 |     public void ToJson_FromJson_RoundTrips()
  24 |     {
  25 |         // DefaultWorld is the canonical seed-built world — it exercises
  26 |         // every entity collection, the calendar, the ruleset, and the
  27 |         // RNG state. If a round-trip is stable on it, we're confident
  28 |         // the schema is fully reversible.
  29 |         var world = DefaultWorld.Create(seed: 42);
  30 |         var registries = world.Registries;
  31 | 
  32 |         var json1 = world.ToJson();
  33 |         var restored = GameWorld.FromJson(json1, registries);
  34 |         var json2 = restored.ToJson();
  35 | 
  36 |         Assert.Equal(json1, json2);
  37 |     }
  38 | 
  39 |     [Fact]
  40 |     public void ToJson_FromJson_PreservesEntityCounts()
  41 |     {
  42 |         var world = DefaultWorld.Create(seed: 7);
  43 |         var json = world.ToJson();
  44 |         var restored = GameWorld.FromJson(json, world.Registries);
  45 | 
  46 |         Assert.Equal(world.Players.Count, restored.Players.Count);
  47 |         Assert.Equal(world.Npcs.Count, restored.Npcs.Count);
  48 |         Assert.Equal(world.Items.Count, restored.Items.Count);
  49 |         Assert.Equal(world.Buildings.Count, restored.Buildings.Count);
  50 |         Assert.Equal(world.Locations.Count, restored.Locations.Count);
  51 |         Assert.Equal(world.Quests.Count, restored.Quests.Count);
  52 |     }
  53 | 
  54 |     [Fact]
  55 |     public void ToJson_FromJson_PreservesSeedAndClock()
  56 |     {
  57 |         var world = DefaultWorld.Create(seed: 1234);
  58 |         var restored = GameWorld.FromJson(world.ToJson(), world.Registries);
  59 | 
  60 |         Assert.Equal(world.Seed, restored.Seed);
  61 |         Assert.Equal(world.Clock, restored.Clock);
  62 |         Assert.Equal(world.Turn, restored.Turn);
  63 |         Assert.Equal(world.Ruleset.Id, restored.Ruleset.Id);
  64 |     }
  65 | 
  66 |     [Fact]
  67 |     public void RngState_RoundTripsThroughJson()
  68 |     {
  69 |         // Roll the RNG a few times, snapshot state, round-trip through
  70 |         // JSON, then roll a few more — the restored world's RNG must
  71 |         // produce the same sequence as the original.
  72 |         //
  73 |         // Note: World.RngState is the SERIALIZED snapshot of the live
  74 |         // Rng's state, but the live Rng advances independently — the
  75 |         // property isn't auto-synced. To capture the post-roll state
  76 |         // into the snapshot, we explicitly copy it back before ToJson.
  77 |         // (The SaveManager does the same thing internally when saving.)
  78 |         var world = DefaultWorld.Create(seed: 999);
  79 |         for (int i = 0; i < 5; i++)
  80 |             world.Rng.Next();
  81 |         world.RngState = world.Rng.State;
  82 | 
  83 |         var stateBeforeSave = world.Rng.State;
  84 |         var restored = GameWorld.FromJson(world.ToJson(), world.Registries);
  85 |         Assert.Equal(stateBeforeSave, restored.RngState);
  86 | 
  87 |         // Both RNGs should now produce identical next-5 outputs.
  88 |         for (int i = 0; i < 5; i++)
  89 |             Assert.Equal(world.Rng.Next(), restored.Rng.Next());
  90 |     }
  91 | 
  92 |     [Fact]
  93 |     public void FromJson_EmptyString_Throws() =>
  94 |         Assert.Throws<ArgumentException>(() => GameWorld.FromJson("", new ContentRegistry()));
  95 | 
  96 |     [Fact]
  97 |     public void FromJson_NullRegistries_Throws()
  98 |     {
  99 |         var world = DefaultWorld.Create(seed: 1);
 100 |         var json = world.ToJson();
 101 |         Assert.Throws<ArgumentNullException>(() => GameWorld.FromJson(json, null!));
 102 |     }
 103 | 
 104 |     [Fact]
 105 |     public void SpawnPlayer_SetsActivePlayer()
 106 |     {
 107 |         var world = new GameWorld { Rng = new Rng(1) };
 108 |         Assert.Null(world.ActivePlayerId);
 109 |         Assert.Null(world.ActivePlayer);
 110 | 
 111 |         var loc = new Location { Id = EntityId.NewId(), Name = "Старт" };
 112 |         world.AddLocation(loc);
 113 | 
 114 |         var player = new Player
 115 |         {
 116 |             Id = EntityId.NewId(),
 117 |             Name = "Герой",
 118 |             LocationId = loc.Id,
 119 |         };
 120 |         world.SpawnPlayer(player);
 121 | 
 122 |         Assert.Equal(player.Id, world.ActivePlayerId);
 123 |         Assert.NotNull(world.ActivePlayer);
 124 |         Assert.Equal(player.Id, world.ActivePlayer!.Id);
 125 |         Assert.Single(world.Players);
 126 |     }

 128 |     [Fact]
 129 |     public void SpawnPlayer_FirstPlayerBecomesActive_SecondDoesNotReplace()
 130 |     {
 131 |         var world = new GameWorld { Rng = new Rng(1) };
 132 |         var loc = new Location { Id = EntityId.NewId(), Name = "loc" };
 133 | 
 134 |         var p1 = new Player { Id = EntityId.NewId(), Name = "p1", LocationId = loc.Id };
 135 |         var p2 = new Player { Id = EntityId.NewId(), Name = "p2", LocationId = loc.Id };
 136 | 
 137 |         world.SpawnPlayer(p1);
 138 |         world.SpawnPlayer(p2);
 139 | 
 140 |         // Active player should remain the first one spawned.
 141 |         Assert.Equal(p1.Id, world.ActivePlayerId);
 142 |         Assert.Equal(2, world.Players.Count);
 143 |     }
